facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on November 11, 2022 that FREYR Battery will invest $2.57 billion into Georgia’s sustainable technology ecosystem.
The investment will create 723 new jobs over the next seven years at a manufacturing facility in Coweta County.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that job creators and innovators from around the world bring operations to Georgia because they know they will have success there, welcoming FREYR as the latest company to bring transformational investments and opportunity to communities.
FREYR is dedicated to supporting a domestic supply chain for renewable power sources.
The company’s battery cells can be used for stationary energy storage (ESS), electric mobility, and additional applications.

FREYR’s Co-Founder and Chief Executive Officer Tom Einar Jensen stated the company is deeply committed to decarbonizing the transportation and energy sectors with U.S. partners and expects meaningful investments to spur job creation and eventual development of localized, decarbonized supply chains in the U.S.
The new facility will be located at the Bridgeport Industrial Park site, which has been zoned for a mix of heavy and light industrial use since 2014.
The company will be hiring for technicians, operators, engineers, executive, and administrative roles.
The facility, to be called Giga America, will have a planned first phase production of approximately 34 gigawatt hours.
The facility will use the U.S.-based 24M Technologies SemiSolidTM platform to simplify the production process and increase manufacturing efficiency.
FREYR is currently evaluating clean power supply solutions for the new facility, including the potential use of renewable energy from solar installations.

Coweta County Development Authority Chair John Daviston stated the authority is extremely pleased to welcome FREYR Battery to the community and excited about the opportunities and addition of quality and highly-skilled jobs that will provide a significant stimulus to the local economy.

Metro Atlanta Chamber President and CEO Katie Kirkpatrick stated metro Atlanta has quickly become a hub for battery cell research and production, and FREYR Battery will benefit from the vibrant research ecosystem and diversity of talent in the region.

Project Manager John Soper represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with Coweta County Development Authority, the Metro Atlanta Chamber, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated companies like FREYR are driving a global revolution based on sustainable technology and solutions, and that electrification is changing how entire cities are powered while the state develops a battery ecosystem with manufacturers, recyclers, and customers to support home and commercial storage.

Georgia’s prime location, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and business-friendly climate has made it an attractive location for a diverse array of rapidly developing industries focused on creating a sustainable future.
Georgia is cultivating a vertically integrated supply chain that will help companies increase efficiencies by reducing reliance on imported materials.
FREYR Battery aims to provide industrial scale clean battery solutions to reduce global emissions and is listed on the New York Stock Exchange.
FREYR’s mission is to produce green battery cells to accelerate the decarbonization of energy and transportation systems globally.
FREYR has commenced building the first of its planned factories in Mo i Rana, Norway.
FREYR announced potential development of industrial scale battery cell production in Vaasa, Finland, and the United States.
FREYR intends to install 50 GWh of battery cell capacity by 2025, 100 GWh annual capacity by 2028, and 200 GWh of annual capacity by 2030.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Battery Manufacturer to Invest $2.57B, Create Over 700 Jobs in Coweta County | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

entity_name: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Recent State Updates
canonical_name: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Recent State Updates
facts: Beginning Jan. 1, 2026, Kansas annual registration fee is $165 for EVs, $100 for PHEVs, and $70 for HEVs.
Electric motorcycles in Kansas have an annual registration fee of $30 starting Jan. 1, 2026.
Beginning Jan. 1, 2026, Kansas annual registration fee for trucks and truck tractors under 12,000 pounds gross weight is $125 for HEVs or PHEVs and $200 for EVs.

Massachusetts requires certain commercial buildings with new parking spaces to provide a minimum number of EV-ready spaces based on total space count, ranging from 1 space for 2–6 total spaces to 10% (not more than 16) for 41 or more total spaces.
An EV-ready space in Massachusetts is defined as a designated parking space with a dedicated branch circuit for EV chargers.
North Dakota state policy under the 25x’25 initiative prioritizes agricultural, forestry, and other natural resources as sources of fuel.

North Dakota Industrial Commission (NDIC) programs, including the Renewable Energy Program and Clean Sustainable Energy Authority (CSEA), provide matching grants and financial assistance for low-emission technology research, demonstration, development, or commercialization.

South Carolina defines a low-speed vehicle as a four-wheeled motor vehicle capable of reaching speeds of at least 20 mph but not greater than 25 mph with a gross vehicle weight rating under 3,000 pounds that meets CFR Title 49 section 571.500 safety standards.
Low-speed vehicles in South Carolina may only operate on secondary highways with posted speed limits up to 35 mph but may cross higher-speed highways at intersections.

Golf cart owners in South Carolina must obtain a DMV permit decal and registration by providing proof of ownership, liability insurance, and a $5 fee, replacing the decal every 5 years or after an address change.
Operators of golf carts in South Carolina must be at least 16 years old, hold a valid driver’s license, carry required documentation, and passengers under 12 must wear fastened safety belts.
An entity operating an electric vehicle charger in South Carolina is not considered a public utility, and authorized utilities may charge for infrastructure supporting these stations.
Utilities offering direct-current-fast-charging services in South Carolina must provide fair and nondiscriminatory rates to all operators.
Tennessee defines low-speed vehicles as four-wheeled electric vehicles achieving speeds of at least 20 mph but not more than 25 mph, excluding golf carts.
Tennessee defines medium-speed vehicles as four-wheeled electric or gasoline vehicles with a maximum speed of more than 30 mph but not more than 35 mph.
Low- and medium-speed vehicles in Tennessee are classified as utility terrain vehicles and are prohibited on interstate highways or any road with a posted speed limit greater than 45 mph.
Texas allows motor vehicles equipped with automated driving systems to operate regardless of whether a human driver is physically present, preempting local discriminatory rules.
Automated commercial motor vehicles in Texas require TxDMV authorization and an emergency responder interaction plan filed with the Department of Public Safety for commercial transport.
By Dec. 1, 2024, the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation adopted standards for EV charger connectors or plugs to ensure wide compatibility with as many EVs as practicable.
EV charging providers in Texas must disclose on the charger display the fee calculation method, current rate, and applicable surcharges, and provide itemized receipts upon request.
After Dec. 1, 2024, publicly available EV chargers funded through public grants or state rebate programs in Texas must meet additional TDLR standards.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Recent State Updates

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Joint Venture
canonical_name: LGES-Hyundai Metaplant America Battery Cell Joint Venture
facts: August 31, 2023: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution will invest an additional $2 billion in their battery cell manufacturing joint venture at the Metaplant in Bryan County.
The additional investment raises the joint venture's total investment value to more than $4.3 billion.
The expansion will create another 400 new jobs.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that in a single year, they broke ground on the largest project in state history, landed multiple suppliers across the state for Hyundai's Metaplant, and welcomed LGES to Bryan County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated Georgia is being built as the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Governor Brian Kemp noted major investments ultimately go to hardworking Georgians in the form of paychecks, improved schools and infrastructure.
Governor Brian Kemp thanked Hyundai Motor Group and LGES for recognizing Georgia as the No. 1 state for business.

José Muñoz, president and global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and president and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, stated the incremental investment reflects a continued commitment to create a more sustainable future powered by American workers.

José Muñoz stated Hyundai is proud to partner with LGES and grateful for support from Governor Kemp and Georgia communities to drive operations as a global leader in the electrified mobility industry.

In May 2023, Hyundai Motor Group and LGES signed a memorandum of understanding establishing LGES as the partner for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s onsite battery cell manufacturing joint venture.

With this announcement, the EV manufacturing facility and the battery joint venture represent an estimated $7.59 billion in investment and will create 8,500 new jobs for the region over the next eight years.

Dong-Myung Kim, president and head of the Advanced Automotive Battery Division of LG Energy Solution, stated the investment underscores dedication to driving America’s EV transition while bolstering the local economy through quality jobs.
Dong-Myung Kim thanked support from the State of Georgia for bringing top-quality products and operational experience to grow with communities.
The 30 GWh facility will be able to support the production of 300,000 units of EVs annually at full operations.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant, then supply them to Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.

Carter Infinger, Chairman of the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, stated the announcement will bring 400 additional well-paying jobs for those in the Savannah region.
Carter Infinger noted continued investment by Hyundai Motor Group and announced suppliers like LG Energy Solution is truly remarkable and transformative for the region.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, Georgia Power, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Governor Kemp set a goal of Georgia becoming the top location for EV manufacturing in the United States, and the state is in a great position to claim that distinction.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted focus on building an ecosystem supporting the entire EV supply chain continues industry-leading success, bringing jobs home to Georgians.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson credited state and community partners like the Savannah-area JDA, utility providers, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start for creating a collaborative, business-friendly environment.
Since 2018, Georgia has pursued job creation along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in more than $25 billion in investments and the creation of over 30,000 jobs.
Battery related projects have accounted for at least $11 billion in investment.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise with about 250,000 employees worldwide and mobility brands including Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
LG Energy Solution is a split-off from LG Chem and a leading global manufacturer of lithium-ion batteries for electric vehicles, mobility, IT, and energy storage systems.
LG Energy Solution has 30 years of experience in battery technology and extensive R&D, holding over 25,000 patents as the top battery-related patent holder in the world.

LG Energy Solution's global network spans North America, Europe, Asia, and Australia, including battery manufacturing facilities established through joint ventures with General Motors, Stellantis N.V., Hyundai Motor Group, and Honda Motor Co., Ltd.
LG Energy Solution aims to achieve carbon neutral operations by 2050.

links.context: LG Energy Solution news and innovations

source_title: Gov. Kemp: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution to Invest Additional $2B in Bryan County | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on January 30, 2023, that SK Battery America (SKBA) has exceeded its hiring goal of 2,600 employees two years ahead of schedule for its two lithium-ion battery manufacturing facilities in Commerce, Georgia.
The company met its initial hiring goal at the end of 2022.
SKBA is expanding its workforce to approximately 3,000 over the next year to meet growing demand for electric vehicles.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that SK Battery America and the State of Georgia share a commitment to reaching the next milestone and thanked SKBA for going above and beyond in delivering on their commitments.
SK Battery America announced its first project in Georgia in 2018, with an initial planned investment of $1.6 billion.
Since 2019, the company has invested $2.6 billion to build two battery manufacturing facilities in Jackson County.
The facilities supply batteries for electric vehicles including the Ford F-150 Lightning and the Volkswagen ID.4.
SKBA started mass production in early 2022.
SK Battery America CEO Timothy Jeong credited the state’s Quick Start program for helping attract and train workers with speed and scale.
More than 300,000 new electric vehicles a year will be on American roads because of the work being done at the Georgia facilities.
SKBA has worked with Georgia Quick Start, the state’s top-ranked job training program through the Technical College System of Georgia, since announcing its first project in 2018.
In July 2022, SKBA announced a partnership with the Georgia National Guard’s Work for Warriors Georgia program to focus on hiring veterans, servicemembers, retirees, reservists, and their families.
The company has held numerous career fairs, visited schools, and held community events in recent months to attract talent.
To attract the highest-quality workforce, SKBA has consistently improved wages, benefits, and its overall work environment.
Tom Crow, Chairman of the Jackson County Board of Commissioners, expressed appreciation for SKBA’s commitment to providing high quality jobs and its substantial financial investment in the county.
The company is still hiring for jobs in production, quality, utility, logistics, ERT, maintenance, and other areas.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson praised SKBA’s relentless pursuit of hiring Georgians for high-skilled, high-paying jobs and its commitment to meeting standing commitments and aggressive hiring needs.
SK On is the parent company of SK Battery America.
SK Battery America produces one of the world’s safest, fastest charging and longest-lasting batteries, currently installed in the Ford F-150 Lightning and Volkswagen ID.4.
The two facilities will have a combined capacity to make 22 GWh of battery cells a year.
This production capacity is enough to power the equivalent of about 300,000 EVs annually.
Since 2019, the company has invested a total of $2.6 billion to build EV battery plants in Commerce, Georgia, creating 2,600 high-skilled jobs.
Governor Kemp and General Carden previously announced SK Battery America becoming a ‘Work for Warriors’ partner.
SK Innovation announced an additional investment of $940 million for a Jackson County expansion that will create 600 jobs.
SK Innovation announced it would begin construction on its second Georgia battery facility in July.
source_title: SK Battery America Exceeds Hiring Goal, On Track to Reach 3,000 Worker | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Seohan Auto Georgia will create at least 180 new jobs and invest more than $72 million in a new manufacturing facility in Liberty County.
The announcement was made on April 11, 2023, in Atlanta.
Seohan Auto Georgia Corporation is a Tier-1 automotive parts supplier for Hyundai Motor Group America.
The company is based in South Korea and has production facilities around the world, including multiple facilities in the United States.
Jung Kee Koo serves as the CEO of Seohan Auto Georgia Corporation.
Seohan's new facility in Georgia is scheduled to begin production in late 2024.
The new manufacturing facility will be located at the Dunwoody Court Site in the Tradeport East Business Center, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified site.
The facility will produce H/shafts, axles, and brake systems for OEMs, including Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Allen Brown is the Chairman of Liberty County Development Authority.
Alyce Thornhill serves as the Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team.
The project was developed in partnership with the Liberty County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Power.
Pat Wilson is the GDEcD Commissioner.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground on October 25 in neighboring Bryan County for its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Offsite suppliers connected to Hyundai's project were predicted to invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around the state.
Projects announced to date have already exceeded this prediction by nearly $1 billion.
Since 2020, more than 35 EV-related projects have announced more than $21 billion in investment and 27,800 jobs in Georgia.
Seohan Auto Georgia Corporation is a new subsidiary of Korea Movenex Co., Ltd.
Korea Movenex Co., Ltd. was established in 1974 and employs more than 2,800 employees globally.

Governor Brian Kemp stated: “The Hyundai Metaplant is and will continue to be an economic driver for the entire region, creating more jobs for hardworking Georgians in communities like Liberty County.”
Jung Kee Koo stated: “We believe Georgia will be the center of the EV industry, and will be a new frontier for Seohan's future with limitless opportunities and potential.”

Allen Brown stated: “From our negotiations with Seohan Auto Georgia we’ve learned it and its affiliated enterprises in South Korea, the United States, and around the world truly are responsible, thorough, and progressive organizations that are striving to advance innovative technology.”
Pat Wilson stated: “The ongoing transition to e-mobility and sustainable technology creates a window of opportunity for companies and states that remain on the forefront...”

links.context: Press contact email for Governor Kemp's office
Deputy Press Secretary email for Governor Kemp's office
Communications Manager email for Georgia Department of Economic Development

source_title: Seohan Auto Georgia Announces 180 New Jobs in Liberty County | Georgia

facts: Manufacturing in Georgia outpaced the Southeast in manufacturing GDP and job growth over the last decade, according to the 2025 JobsEQ report.
Georgia leads in advanced manufacturing sectors including electric mobility, aerospace, advanced materials, and automation.
Companies benefit from Georgia’s investments in workforce development, robust logistics infrastructure, and development-ready sites.
Business Facilities continues to rank Georgia in the top five for aerospace and defense manufacturers.
In fiscal year 2025, manufacturing accounted for 46% of new jobs announced by GDEcD.
Georgia Tech ranks No. 1 among public universities for undergraduate engineering programs according to U.S. News & World Report.
Georgia supports manufacturers at every stage of growth from concept to full-scale production and ongoing process improvement.

Through services unique to the state, companies connect with leading university researchers, prototyping and fabrication shops, contract manufacturers, and more to identify opportunities to enhance efficiency and bring products to market.

Qualified manufacturers are invited to join the Georgia Made® program, a promotional state branding and marketing opportunity that naturally enables stronger alliances with GDEcD’s business support teams.
source_title: Manufacturing in Georgia | Georgia

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group and SK On EV Battery Facility
canonical_name: SK On / Hyundai Motor Group Bartow County EV Battery Facility
facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) and SK On have selected a site in Bartow County for a new electric vehicle (EV) battery manufacturing facility.
The new facility will supply Hyundai Motor Group’s plants in the U.S.
The project is considered one of the largest economic development projects in Georgia state history.
Stakeholders estimate the project will create more than 3,500 new jobs.
The project involves approximately $4-5 billion of investment in Bartow County.
Hyundai Motor Group and SK On recently signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) regarding the partnership for a new EV battery facility in the U.S.
Details of the partnership are still in development.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Hyundai Motor Group and SK On are valued partners and key players in Georgia's ever-growing automotive industry.
Governor Kemp noted his administration has been focused on bringing jobs and opportunity to communities across the state that may have been overlooked in the past.
Governor Kemp said SK and HMG share the goal of bringing jobs and opportunity and are proud they are choosing to invest further in Georgia, the No. 1 state for business.
SK On was established in 2021 as the lithium-ion battery subsidiary branch of SK Innovation.
SK On currently employs more than 2,000 Georgians at its SK Battery America facility in Commerce.
In July, SK Battery America announced it would partner with the Work for Warriors Georgia program to hire veterans, servicemembers, and their families at its Georgia operations.
The new facility's development is subject to execution of relevant agreements and HMG and SK On's final board decisions.
The new facility will be located at Bartow Centre, a zoned manufacturing and industrial site located on Highway 411.
The project is aiming to begin operations in 2025.
Bartow County Sole Commissioner Steve Taylor expressed pleasure with the decision of Hyundai Motor Group and SK On.
Commissioner Taylor thanked the owners of the former Bartow Centre Industrial property and noted they pursued the extraordinary economic development project together.
Commissioner Taylor stated the project will have regional benefit for job seekers in the area and establishes Bartow in Georgia’s EV ecosystem in a significant way.
Commissioner Taylor cited Bartow’s strong history and talent-base built upon advanced manufacturing, the automotive industry, and innovation as reasons the battery partnership should thrive.
Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was pursued in partnership with Georgia EMC, Development Authority of Bartow County, the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated HMG and SK have been pioneering partners for Georgia for decades as major drivers for Georgia’s automotive renaissance.
Commissioner Wilson noted HMG was the first Korean manufacturer to locate in the state.

Commissioner Wilson said supporting cooperation across Georgia's growing EV ecosystem is creating a fully integrated supply chain for automotive OEMs while connecting battery manufacturers with recyclers to close the loop on battery manufacturing.
Commissioner Wilson expressed excitement for the jobs of the future this will create for Bartow County and northwest Georgia.

Georgia’s prime location, major ports system, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and pro-business climate have made it an attractive location for rapidly developing industries focused on creating a sustainable future.
Building on assets that make the automotive industry successful, Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric transportation industry.
Excluding this announcement, EV-related projects announced in Georgia since 2020 total approximately $17 billion in investment and more than 22,800 new jobs.
Hyundai Motor Group separately announced in May its plans to invest $5.54 billion in Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, an EV manufacturing facility in Bryan County.
Hyundai Motor Group broke ground on the Bryan County facility in October with Governor Kemp, other state leaders, and local and federal officials.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise that has created a value chain based on mobility, steel, construction, logistics, finance, IT, and service.
Hyundai Motor Group has about 250,000 employees worldwide.
Hyundai Motor Group's mobility brands include Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
SK On is a global leading electric vehicle (EV) battery developer, manufacturer, and solutions provider.
SK On's mission is to make the world a cleaner and more convenient place as an electrification linchpin.
SK On was launched as an independent company in 2021 after SK Innovation, South Korea’s largest energy company, decided to split off its battery business.
SK On aims to become a world leader in the clean energy industry by leveraging its global production base and R&D capabilities, as well as its production and quality management know-how.
SK On is headquartered in Seoul, South Korea.
SK On has a worldwide presence with battery plants currently operating or in construction across the United States, Europe, and Asia.

links.context: More information about Hyundai Motor Group

source_title: Gov. Kemp: Hyundai Motor Group and SK On To Build EV Battery Facility in Bartow County | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Over the last seven years, Georgia has seen over 219,700 new jobs and more than 118 billion dollars in investment from projects worked by the state economic development team.
64 percent of those jobs and 75 percent of those investment dollars are going to communities outside the metro-Atlanta region.
Georgia has maintained its streak as the number one state for business for an unprecedented 12 years in a row.
Since 2019, Georgia has welcomed the three largest economic development projects in state history.
The Hyundai Metaplant is cited as an example of a large-scale project representing a generational opportunity for Georgia families and communities.
Over the last seven years, the state has invested more money toward education and workforce training programs than ever before in its history.
The state has fully funded Quality Basic Education (QBE) seven times and added an additional 368 million dollars to programs and initiatives that directly prepare workers for good jobs.
Georgia is proposing a further 20-basis point reduction in the state’s personal and corporate income tax rate, bringing it to just 4.99 percent.
From the 2024 budget through the proposed Amended 2026 budget, 4.1 billion dollars in capital projects have been funded through cash instead of debt.
The state has invested over 6 billion dollars in water, sewer, and transportation projects across Georgia.
The state has put 2 billion additional dollars toward higher education than when the governor first took office.
This year’s budget proposals include 40.7 million dollars for healthcare education facilities and medical workforce training programs.
The state has spent over 26 million dollars for medical residency slots, adding 897 positions for a grand total of 2,523 residency slots as of this fiscal year.
This year’s budget adds another 2.1 million dollars to expand medical residency slots.
The governor’s amended budget proposal invests 325 million dollars in the University System of Georgia’s DREAMS Scholarship program, the first need-based scholarship of its kind in state history.

The Georgia MATCH program, launched in 2023, has seen a 5.9 percent rise in University System of Georgia (USG) enrollment and a 7.1 percent increase in Technical College System of Georgia (TCSG) enrollment over its first two years.
Tuition costs at Georgia public universities have increased by just five percent between 2019 and the current academic year, compared to roughly 19 percent at the University of California system.
The HOPE Scholarship has been returned to 100 percent of its promise while tuition costs were held in check across the Board of Regents system.
Georgia is recognized as one of the top states in the country for four-year public university affordability according to National Center for Education Statistics data.

La Regina, an Italian food production company with a facility in Alma, Georgia, employs 110 workers and moves goods through the Port of Savannah, serving as an example of economic development revitalizing former facilities.
source_title: Governor Kemp's 2026 State of the State Address | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: On August 29, 2023, Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Daesol Ausys Georgia will establish a new manufacturing facility in West Point.
The new facility will create more than 140 new jobs and represent $72 million in investment in Harris County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right and is excited for Daesol Ausys to join other innovators in Harris County.
Georgia's automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians, according to Governor Kemp.
Daesol Ausys was established in Korea in 2017 and specializes in designing and manufacturing motor and electric vehicle interior components and accessories.
The company is a key supplier for Kia Georgia, Hyundai Motor Group, and General Motors.
Min Ho Kwon, CEO of Daesol Ausys, expressed excitement about the project at the Northwest Harris Business Park and the partnership with the State of Georgia and Harris County.
Daesol Ausys’ new facility will produce luggage boards and luggage covers.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in December 2024.
The company will be hiring for positions in production, purchasing, quality control, maintenance, human resources, and management.

Robert Grant, Chairman of the Harris County Commission, noted that Daesol Ausys’ decision represents a commitment to Harris County and highlighted the relationship with sister companies Daehan Solutions and Daesol Materials.
Craig Greenhaw, Chairman of the Harris County Development Authority, welcomed Daesol Ausys as a new corporate citizen bringing jobs and capital investment into the region.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at GDEcD’s Global Commerce team, represented the state in partnership with the Harris County Commission, Harris County Development Authority, City of West Point, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that economic development is driven by the local level and praised community partners for bringing Daesol Ausys to West Georgia.
Since 2018, Georgia's pursuit of the entire EV supply chain has created more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in Georgia's automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to fiscal year 2021.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in fiscal year 2023.
Daesol Ausys Georgia is a leading manufacturer of automotive components and has a long history as one of the top suppliers to Kia and Hyundai worldwide.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Automotive Supplier Daesol Ausys Georgia to Invest $72 Million in Harris County | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on June 07, 2023 that Woory Industrial Co., Ltd. will establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
The facility will create more than 130 new jobs and an estimated $18 million in investment in Dublin-Laurens County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated the company is helping Georgia on the path to becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Woory Industrial Company was established in 1989 and specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components and systems for the global automotive industry.
The company is concentrating on the development of new products for electric vehicles (EV) and hydrogen-fueled cars.
Jungwoo Kim, CEO of Woory Industrial Company, said the transition to electric vehicles is the future and the U.S. EV market is the key market among all automotive markets.
Over the past 10 years, Woory has successfully grown into a specialized EV component company through continuous innovation.
The new facility will be located at 404 Kellam Road in Dublin and will be the company’s first U.S. manufacturing location.
The facility will produce electrical heaters, control units, and actuators for EV and internal combustion carmakers in North America.
Operations are expected to begin in November 2023.
Unlike internal combustion engine vehicles, electric vehicles do not have a heating source, so heaters for EVs are required for heating the cabin and maintaining battery temperature.
Woory's pioneering development of electric heaters over the last 10 years has led the company to become a leader in this growing market segment.
Woory will be hiring for positions in personnel from all fields necessary for an automotive parts manufacturing plant.

Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight said Woory’s commitment to place an existing manufacturing building back into service helps promote more infill development while creating new career paths for the community.

Dr. Fred Williams, Chair of the Board of Directors of the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, noted that Dublin and Laurens County has a rapidly growing automotive sector thanks to its geographic location on Interstate 16 and access to a skilled and trained workforce.
The partnership with area education systems and training providers will ensure training programs are in place to help fill Woory's talent pipeline for years to come.

Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that projects like Woory are creating jobs and opportunities in rural communities and that Georgia has invested for decades to match competitive projects with communities and sites prepared for development.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, the state has pursued job creation opportunities along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in over $22.7 billion in investment and the creation of more than 28,400 jobs.
Woory started operating a small factory producing fuel senders in Suwon, South Korea almost 35 years ago.
Woory has grown into an international enterprise with production plants around the world employing over 1,300 people.
Woory has been nominated as a supplier for Tesla, Rivian, Lucid, and Canoo.
Woory is currently supplying the HV heater system used to heat the cabins of EVs.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Woory to Open EV Parts Manufacturing, R&D Facility in Laurens County | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: The electric vehicle market is projected to grow by more than 40 percent in the next few years.
Korea-based energy company SK Innovation needed to build a plant in North America to serve its growing list of customers like Ford and Volkswagen.
SK Battery America (SKBA) was formed to pursue the project.
Georgia was selected as the site because it is the No. 1 state in the U.S. for doing business.
Georgia’s infrastructure and supportive local and state governments were essential to the site selection process.
Workforce training partner Georgia Quick Start was key to sealing the deal.
SKBA spent $2.6 billion to build two facilities.
The two facilities will manufacture lithium-ion batteries.
The project will create nearly 2,600 direct new jobs.
SKBA’s President Hwang Jun-ho stated that the Quick Start training was really crucial.
This facility is SKBA’s first plant in the United States.
With full support from Quick Start, SKBA can meet production timelines without any obstacles.
SK Battery America is headquartered in Seoul, South Korea.
SK Battery America is a global leading manufacturer for electric vehicles.
SK Battery America aims to produce the safest, fastest-charging, and longest-lasting batteries to create a cleaner and more sustainable future across the United States.
Lanier Technical College is listed as a partner on the page.
Timothy (Jun Yong) Jeong serves as the CEO of SK Battery America.
source_title: Sk battery - Georgia Quick Start

facts: Georgia Quick Start is a discretionary incentive that provides customized, company-specific workforce training at no cost to companies investing in and creating jobs in Georgia.
In 1967, Georgia’s leadership passed legislation to create Georgia Quick Start as a first-of-its-kind organization to provide free customized workforce training to attract new industry to the state.
The program's success exceeded expectations and it remains the No. 1-ranked program of its type, serving as a model for other states.
Georgia Quick Start is considered the state’s most effective discretionary incentive and plays a key role in decisions by companies large and small to create more and better jobs for Georgians.
The organization's headquarters and Northern Operations Regional Office are located at 75 5th Street, NW, Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30308.
An Eastern Operations Regional Office and Advanced Manufacturing Training Center are located at 1500 Pine Meadow Drive, Savannah, GA 31322.
A Bioscience Training Center is located at 320 Shire Parkway, Social Circle, GA 30025.
A Western Operations Regional Office and Kia Georgia Training Center are located at 900 Kia Boulevard, West Point, GA 31833.
The Quick Start Cartersville Training Complex is located at 315 Etowah Drive, Cartersville, GA 30125.
The Quick Start Hyundai Mobility Training Center of GA is located at 700 Genesis Drive, Ellabell, GA 31308.
source_title: About Us - Georgia Quick Start

facts: Georgia Quick Start hosted a training plan signing on January 27, 2026 at 11 a.m. to commemorate its partnership with stow Group and Georgia Northwestern Technical College.
The Belgium-based company is investing $36 million to build a new industrial storage and automation solution facility outside of Adairsville, Georgia.
stow plans to hire 200 employees for production roles as well as logistics, administrative and management roles.
Georgia Quick Start, the state’s top-ranked workforce training program, will be providing customized training for stow.
Training will include several hands-on labs such as a Safety Evaluation Room and Torquing Room.
Scott McMurray, Deputy Commissioner at Georgia Quick Start, stated that stow’s decision to come to Georgia is a strong vote of confidence in the state's workforce.
The training scope includes courses on the stow manufacturing process, upright operations, beam operations, bracing operations, logistics and warehousing operations, and assembly.
Four customized courses will be complete eLearning courses delivered via a computer lab.
Michael Morgenroth, General Plant Manager for stow Manufacturing in Adairsville, noted that Quick Start will be an invaluable partner as they prepare to bring the facility online.
The training program involves three distinct partners: stow, Georgia Northwestern Technical College, and Georgia Quick Start, each responsible for successful program completion.

Quick Start will deliver training during the expansion, while Georgia Northwestern Technical College will serve as stow’s long-term partner for ongoing training and providing a pipeline of qualified graduates.

Heidi Popham, President of Georgia Northwestern Technical College, expressed pride in partnering to support stow Group’s decision to locate in Gordon County and help ensure a skilled workforce across northwest Georgia.

The Development Authority of Gordon County, Gordon County Chamber, Georgia Power, and the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) worked with Georgia Northwestern Technical College and Georgia Quick Start to qualify stow Group for training support as operations are upgraded.
Training is scheduled to begin in March at the Quick Start Cartersville Training Complex.
source_title: stow Agreement Signing - Georgia Quick Start

facts: Go-Station announced a new DC fast electric vehicle (EV) charging site for passenger cars and heavy-duty trucks at the Port Fueling Center located at 5712 Augusta Road, adjacent to the Port of Savannah.
Go-Station will break ground on the new EV charging site in the coming months following the January 4, 2022 announcement.
Andrew Hisey, Go-Station's president and CEO, stated the fueling center's EV charging site will address the growing demand of EVs traveling in the area.
Hisey noted the site will also address the rapidly expanding population of EV light, medium, and heavy-duty trucks hitting the roads soon.
Hisey described the project as a critical EV charging infrastructure development that will enable private and commercial vehicles to complete EV journeys not possible today.
The Port of Savannah is home to the largest single-terminal container facility in North America, comprised of two modern, deep-water terminals: Garden City Terminal and Ocean Terminal.
Garden City Terminal is the fourth busiest container handling facility in the United States, encompassing more than 1,500 acres.
Garden City Terminal moved in excess of 5 million TEU's in the last year.
The Port Fuel Center opened in January 2022 as an innovative, state-of-the-art food and fueling facility for drayage and freight operators in and around the Port of Savannah.
The center features live digital monitors displaying up-to-the-minute stacking at each of the port interchange gates.
The center provides overnight parking for up to 80 trucks.
The center includes EV shuttle service, a medical facility, an onsite truck repair facility, outdoor handwashing stations, showers, and trucker paperwork drop boxes.
The center offers fresh and prepared foods and a Wendy's restaurant with drive-thru access.
The center features 16 gasoline pumps, 10 DEF diesel lanes, and 3 fuel lanes for Compressed Natural Gas.

Sean Register, Managing Director of the Fuel Center, stated the Savannah Port Fueling Center is a prime example of their commitment to economic expansion, job growth, a more sustainable future, and customers.

Register noted they are partnering with Go-Station to make a significant contribution to Savannah and EV transportation with a center near the largest and fastest-growing container terminal in America.
Go-Station offers a complete range of EV charging services, including turn-key charging solutions.
Go-Station serves verticals including multi-family developments, workplaces, parking facilities, healthcare, retail centers, fueling stations, and fleet operators transitioning to electrification.
source_title: Go-Station Announces a New Electric Vehicle Car and

facts: SK Battery America aims to produce the most advanced EV batteries in the U.S.
SK Battery America is the flagship entity of SK On.
SK Battery America is a leader in the electric vehicle (EV) battery manufacturing sector.
SKBA is at the forefront of advancing high nickel technology.
SKBA has a dedicated team of over 2,600 employees.
SKBA provides high-energy density battery cells to top automotive brands.
SKBA's North American expansion is marked by strategic joint partnerships with industry giants such as Ford and Hyundai.
source_title: About Us < Introduction < Company < SK battery America

facts: Absolics, Inc., a subsidiary of SKC Co. Ltd., broke ground on November 1, 2022, on a planned $600 million manufacturing facility in Covington, Ga.
The investment increases total planned investment plans to more than $600 million, up from the $473 million originally announced in October 2021.
SKC and Absolics raised the investment projections based on the need for more advanced manufacturing equipment to meet anticipated demand.
The facility will create more than 400 high-skilled jobs in Georgia.
Phase 1 is planned for completion by the end of 2023, representing a $240 million investment expected to create 140 jobs and focus on small-volume manufacturing.
Mass production at the facility is expected to start in the second quarter of 2024.
Phase 2 is expected to be an additional $360 million investment that creates another 270 jobs and ramps up to high-volume manufacturing, anticipated to be completed over the next 3-5 years.
The Georgia site will manufacture a glass substrate, which is a thin layer of glass on which processing and memory chips can be mounted together to create the brains of a computing system.
The glass substrate reduces the space required for a multi-chip package, allowing more chips to be packed into a single device.
The material is considered a breakthrough because it can significantly increase the performance and energy efficiency of chipsets.
SKC and Absolics originally developed the technology as part of a research consortium with the Georgia Institute of Technology.
Absolics is expected to be the first in the world to mass produce the glass substrate material.
The groundbreaking ceremony was attended by officials including Senator Jon Ossoff and Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson.

Dr. Woncheol Park, President and Chief Executive Officer of SKC, stated that Absolics will be an integral part of the semiconductor ecosystem in the U.S. and expressed pleasure at building a factory to create hundreds of jobs impacting Covington and Georgia.

Senator Jon Ossoff stated he is working every day to bring jobs and business to Georgia, noting the plant will create Georgia jobs, support U.S. national security, and bring advanced manufacturing to the state.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted the development as an opportunity to grow the semiconductor industry in Georgia and the U.S., noting it came about through research at Georgia Tech and praising the long-term relationship between Georgia and SK Group.
Absolics was created last year by SKC, a majority owner of the new business.
The inaugural product will be a thin piece of glass, approximately 3-by-3 inches, used to package different semiconductor components together.
Processing, memory, and logic chips can be mounted next to each other on the glass to create the core of a computing system.
The new glass material replaces the standard silicon imposer on a plastic substrate, simplifying manufacturing and reducing package thickness by almost half.
Multi-layer ceramic capacitors (MLCC) can be embedded in the glass substrate, freeing more surface space for chips.
Absolics’ product allows larger processors and up to four times more chips to be mounted in the same space compared to standard packaging.
The energy efficiency of the glass substrate reduces overall power consumption by up to 50 percent, driving down costs and environmental impact at scale in large data centers.
Absolics expects Phase 1 to have an annual production capacity of 12,000 m2.
Phase 2 is expected to ramp up to an annual production capacity of 72,000 m2.

The investment and facility are part of SK Group’s broader commitment to increase U.S. investments and assets to more than $50 billion by the end of 2025, focusing on clean energy, semiconductors, and life sciences.
Earlier in 2022, SK Siltron CSS opened a new facility in Michigan as part of a $300 million investment to increase manufacturing capacity for semiconductor wafers used in EV components.
SK On is investing $2.6 billion in a site in Commerce, Ga., to make batteries for electric vehicles.
SKC was founded in 1976 and established a presence in Georgia in 1996, growing into one of the largest manufacturers of polyester films (PET film) in North America.
SKC’s Semiconductor Material Business develops and manufactures semiconductors and specialty materials, including chemical mechanical polishing (CMP) pads and slurry.
SKC is securing future growth drivers by producing copper foil, an essential material for electric vehicle (EV) battery anodes.
SK Group, South Korea’s second-largest conglomerate, drives innovations in semiconductors, sustainable energy, telecommunications, and life sciences.
SK Group is based in Seoul and invests globally with a commitment to reducing greenhouse gas emissions and increasing renewable energy use.
SK companies combined have $139 billion in global annual revenue and employ more than 100,000 people worldwide.

SK companies are investing billions of dollars in expanding their U.S. presence through operations or partnerships in hydrogen energy, fuel cells, EV battery manufacturing, energy storage, pharmaceuticals, semiconductors, and advanced materials.
source_title: Absolics Breaks Ground On Planned $600M Investment | News | SK

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on July 12, 2023 that Kia will invest more than $200 million at its West Point plant.
The investment will create nearly 200 new jobs at the West Point plant.
The funding accommodates assembly of the company’s all-electric 2024 EV9 three-row SUV.
EV9 assembly is expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
The EV9 will become the fifth model assembled at Kia Georgia, joining the Telluride, Sorento and Sportage SUVs, and the K5 mid-size sedan.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia’s longstanding partnership with Kia has led to generational job creation and growth for the West Point area.
Governor Kemp noted the project will help propel the state toward its goal of becoming the e-mobility capital.
Currently, more than 40 percent of Kia vehicles sold in the U.S. are manufactured in West Point.
The addition of the EV9 to the lineup will further increase the facility’s output.
The EV9 is the brand’s first three-row EV SUV and the first Kia EV to be assembled in North America.
The EV9 serves as the flagship vehicle for Kia’s global Plan S strategy focused on sustainable mobility leadership.
Sean Yoon, President & CEO of Kia Motors North America and Kia Motors America, stated the EV9 has the potential to be another change catalyst for Kia.
Sean Yoon described the EV9 as the most innovative vehicle Kia has ever built and a standout in the EV market.
Kia Georgia is a certified Georgia Made manufacturer that will utilize services from Georgia Quick Start, the nation’s top-ranked workforce development program.
Georgia Quick Start operates the Kia Georgia Training Center to provide comprehensive, customized training for new employees on the new assembly line.
Stuart Countess, President & CEO of Kia Georgia, stated the team looks forward to beginning production of its first EV.
Stuart Countess highlighted partnerships with the Governor’s Office, the Department of Economic Development, Georgia QuickStart, the City of West Point, and Troup County.
City of West Point Mayor Steve Tramell stated Kia has been an outstanding corporate citizen and community partner for seventeen years.
Mayor Tramell noted Kia has improved livelihoods in West Point through employment opportunities and economic growth.
Amanda Fields, Senior Regional Project Manager, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team on the project.

The project was executed in partnership with the West Point Development Authority, the City of West Point, Troup County, Georgia Center of Innovation’s Manufacturing and Energy teams, and Georgia Quick Start.
Pat Wilson, Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner, stated Kia has exceeded expectations since being recruited by Georgia in 2006.
Commissioner Wilson thanked Kia and partners for meeting the needs of today and tomorrow’s leading industries.
To date, Kia has invested more than $1.9 billion in the state of Georgia.
Kia Georgia is responsible for more than 14,000 plant and supplier jobs in the region.
Governor Kemp and state lawmakers recognized January 31 as “Kia Day” earlier this year to commend Kia’s vehicle manufacturing and impact.
The 2006 announcement of a state-of-the-art vehicle assembly facility in West Point was one of the biggest single foreign investments in Georgia’s history.
From Georgia, Kia ships vehicles to sales markets globally, including the United States, Canada, Mexico, the Caribbean, Middle East and Pacific nations.
Since 2020, Georgia has pursued job creation along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in more than $22.7 billion in investment.
This state-wide supply chain effort has created over 28,400 jobs since 2020.
Kia Georgia, Inc. is the first manufacturing site in North America for Seoul-based Kia Corporation.
Kia Georgia has an annual capacity of 340,000 units and is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Georgia.
The facility began mass production on November 16, 2009.
Kia America is headquartered in Irvine, California.
Kia offers a range of gasoline, hybrid, plug-in hybrid and electrified vehicles sold through over 750 dealers in the U.S.
The K5, Sportage, Sorento, and Telluride (excludes HEV and PHEV models) are assembled in the United States from U.S. and globally sourced parts.
The EV9 is expected to be assembled in the United States from U.S. and globally sourced parts.

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) LLC
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America LLC
facts: Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung, along with Governor Brian P. Kemp, company officials, and state and local leaders officially broke ground on Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) LLC’s new manufacturing plant at the Bryan County Mega site.

The celebration included a morning groundbreaking on site followed by a future technology expo at Enmarket Arena in Savannah where the company highlighted “advanced technology and extraordinary human achievement” with exhibits for hundreds of community attendees.
A $5.54 billion investment by Hyundai Motor Group and its affiliate suppliers was announced five months ago.
The project includes plans to produce a diverse range of innovative Hyundai, Genesis and Kia electric vehicles (EVs).
The project includes plans to build a new battery manufacturing facility.
HMGMA will create more than 8,100 jobs over the next few years.
The company aims to establish a stable supply chain for EV battery and other EV components in the U.S. market.
HMGMA’s new smart factory is expected to begin commercial production in the first half of 2025.

Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung stated that Hyundai and the people of Georgia share qualities including respect for histories, ingenuity, creativity, and determination to make the world better for the next generation.
Euisun Chung noted that Hyundai's EVs are currently recognized as best in class.
Euisun Chung expressed determination to be the global leader in electrification, safety, quality, and sustainability through this partnership.
Euisun Chung added that with Metaplant America, Hyundai Motor Group will evolve beyond an automaker to become the world’s leading mobility solutions provider.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that more Georgians are working than ever before with record jobs and investment coming to all parts of the state.
Governor Kemp highlighted award-winning workforce development programs and infrastructure as drivers for Georgia's economy reaching new heights.
Governor Kemp said the partnership with Hyundai Motor Group and the groundbreaking exemplify unprecedented success.
Governor Kemp expressed a long-term commitment to improving lives and livelihoods, looking forward to seeing the facility's impact on the community and state.
source_title: Hyundai Metaplant America EV Plant | Tim Moran Hyundai

facts: Georgia Quick Start was ranked the No. 1 workforce training program in Area Development magazine’s 2024 survey of site location consultants.
This marks the 15th consecutive year that Georgia Quick Start has won the top spot in the Workforce Training Programs category.

In its inaugural 2010 survey, Area Development magazine asked leading site location consultants to identify the best state for workforce training programs, and Georgia Quick Start won the No. 1 spot for Georgia.
The program maintained its top ranking annually from 2011 through 2024.
The September 2024 survey cites Georgia Quick Start as the best customized workforce training program in the U.S.

Area Development editors stated that states excelling in this category invest heavily in customized training programs that meet specific business and industry needs, crediting Georgia's leadership to the highly acclaimed Georgia Quick Start program.

Georgia Quick Start operates within the Technical College System of Georgia and offers customized workforce training to qualified companies as a discretionary incentive to support investment and job creation in the state.

Area Development’s annual state rankings are considered the gold standard for assessing state appeal and are the only ranking based on surveys of site location professionals who directly influence investment and job creation decisions.
Georgia ranked No. 1 overall as the Best State for Doing Business for the 11th consecutive year in this survey.

Magazine editor Andy Greiner attributed Georgia's top overall ranking to its robust pro-business environment and consistent performance across categories, noting it remains a perennial favorite for businesses expanding or relocating.
Greiner highlighted Quick Start’s pivotal role in establishing the state's reputation, stating it is a key factor in Georgia’s sustained success.
According to Greiner, Georgia Quick Start has been pivotal in attracting companies requiring tech-savvy workers with specific skills and bolstering the state’s competitive labor market.
source_title: Press Release - Georgia Quick Start

facts: Joon Georgia, Inc., an automotive parts manufacturer, will create 630 new jobs and invest $317 million in Bulloch County.
Plans for the company’s new manufacturing facility mark the first confirmed supplier for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America in Bryan County.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the project on November 7, 2022.
Joon Georgia is part of Ajin USA, a current supplier for multiple Hyundai Motor Group facilities.
The company produces a variety of automotive body parts and electronics for original equipment manufacturers.
Jung Ho Sea serves as the CEO of Ajin USA.
Joon Georgia will create approximately 630 new jobs with competitive benefits in Bulloch County and the State of Georgia.
Joon Georgia’s new facility will be located at Bruce Yawn Commerce Park, previously known as the Southern Gateway Commerce Park, near Statesboro.
The site is situated in close proximity to the Port of Savannah.
Operations at the Bruce Yawn Commerce Park are expected to begin in mid-2024.
The company will begin hiring for the facility in mid-2023.
Billy Allen serves as the Chair of the Development Authority of Bulloch County.
Georgia Southern University and Ogeechee Technical College will provide critical workforce support to help Joon Georgia succeed.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this project.
The project was executed in partnership with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Quick Start, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Power.
Workforce development for the new facility will be supported by Quick Start, which is ranked as the top workforce training program in the nation.

Quick Start partners with the Technical College System of Georgia to assess workers, train new employees on unique processes, and develop customized job-specific training using current techniques and media.
Pat Wilson serves as the GDEcD Commissioner.
Since 2020, more than 30 EV-related projects have contributed over $13.3 billion in investment and announced more than 18,100 jobs in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground on October 25 in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Non-affiliated suppliers connected to the Hyundai project are predicted to invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around the state.

facts: Georgia has received more clean energy manufacturing investment since passage of the Inflation Reduction Act than any other state in the nation.
In January of 2023, Senator Ossoff helped announce a historic $2.5 billion investment by Qcells to significantly expand solar manufacturing.
The Qcells investment was made possible by Senator Ossoff’s Solar Energy Manufacturing for America Act.
The Qcells investment marks the largest clean energy manufacturing investment in American history.

In May, following his third economic delegation to the Republic of Korea, Senator Ossoff announced a partnership between LG and Hyundai to build an electric vehicle battery plant as part of the new Hyundai MetaPlant in Bryan County.
This summer, Senator Ossoff joined local leaders to celebrate the groundbreaking of a new manufacturing facility in Bainbridge that will manufacture key components for electric vehicles.
The Bainbridge EV component facility is made possible by a $117 million bipartisan infrastructure law grant.
Grants were delivered to help communities eliminate railway-highway crossings, improving safety and strengthening supply chains.
Senator Ossoff secured a historic $8 million investment to begin planning a passenger rail line between Atlanta and Savannah.
Three new bipartisan infrastructure law grants were secured to accelerate planning of passenger rail between Atlanta-Savannah, Atlanta-Charlotte, and Atlanta-Nashville.
Senator Ossoff set a goal to establish Georgia as the leading State in advanced energy innovation and manufacturing.
Legislation passed under Senator Ossoff has made Georgia the top state in the nation for advanced energy manufacturing and investment.
Senator Ossoff is championing the bipartisan JOBS Act to help students access and afford job training programs for short-term skills and workforce development.

The JOBS Act would ensure students in Georgia enrolled in high-quality short-term skills and job training programs have access to Pell Grants, which currently may only be used for two- and four-year colleges.
source_title: Three-Year Report – U.S. Senator for Georgia Jon Ossoff

facts: A U.S. electric vehicle supplier is ditching plans to build a new facility in Georgia.
The Biden administration tentatively backed the project for a $671 million loan.
Aspen Aerogels CEO Don Young told investors on a call Thursday that the company will “cease construction” of the plant near Statesboro, roughly 60 miles west of Savannah.
The Massachusetts-based company planned to produce EV insulation and fire protection materials at the facility.
The planned Georgia facility would have complemented production at an existing facility in Rhode Island.
The Biden administration tentatively approved Aspen Aerogels for the Department of Energy loan in October.
source_title: EV supplier halts construction of Georgia plant - E&E News by POLITICO

facts: Provides safe, responsible, and efficient battery recycling services in Georgia.
Recovers valuable materials including lead, lithium, and nickel from scrap batteries.
Offers competitive payouts for recycled batteries while preventing hazardous waste from entering landfills.
Serves auto repair shops, industrial businesses, electricians, contractors, and homeowners.
Accepts automotive batteries such as lead-acid car batteries, truck and heavy equipment batteries, and marine/RV batteries.
Accepts industrial and commercial batteries including forklift and UPS backup batteries, and heavy-duty industrial batteries.
Accepts rechargeable and specialty batteries including lithium-ion batteries used in electric vehicles, laptops, and power tools.
Accepts nickel-cadmium (NiCd) and nickel-metal hydride (NiMH) batteries commonly found in consumer electronics.

Provides safety guidelines for battery recycling, advising against disposal in trash, recommending storage in cool/dry places, taping terminals to prevent accidental discharge, and following state and federal guidelines for safe processing.
Operates two drop-off facilities located in Atlanta and Gainesville, GA.
Accepts both single batteries and bulk loads with no minimum amount requirement.
Processes scrap batteries through a drop-off system that requires no appointment, followed by staff weighing and inspection, and immediate payment based on current scrap battery market rates.
source_title: Battery Recycling Services in Georgia | Olympus Recycling

canonical_name: Senate Bill 26 (Georgia Electric Vehicle Future Act)
facts: During the latest state legislative session, the Georgia House passed a measure that proponents say will “advance” the state’s electric vehicle industry.

State officials have given millions of dollars in taxpayer-funded incentives to various EV projects, including $1.5 billion for a Rivian Automotive electric vehicle assembly plant in Morgan and Newton counties.
Senate Bill 26, the Georgia Electric Vehicle Future Act, permits the Georgia Department of Economic Development to launch a statewide electric vehicle manufacturing program.
The agency would focus on developing, marketing and promoting investments and job creation for Georgia’s EV industry.

State Rep. Chuck Martin (R-Alpharetta) said in a constituent newsletter that the state economic development agency would work with state agencies, including the Georgia Department of Transportation, to deploy “the appropriate infrastructure,” including EV charging stations.
The legislation would establish the Georgia Electric Vehicle Manufacturing Commission.
The commission would be comprised of private sector and public officials.
The commission would serve as an oversight body for the state’s EV initiatives.
The commission would work to develop policy recommendations for the General Assembly to consider.
The commission would coordinate with higher educational systems to determine workforce needs and help train future workers for this industry.
The Georgia Electric Vehicle Future Act would promote public-private partnerships to develop forward-looking EV solutions.
The act aims to support this job-producing industry and strengthen Georgia’s position in EV manufacturing and innovation.
Last year, the Federal Highway Administration signed off on Georgia’s National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Plan.
The approval opens the door to millions in federal taxpayer dollars for the state.
With this approval, the Georgia Department of Transportation began planning how the state will spend roughly $130 million to pay for electric vehicle charging infrastructure over the next five years.
The state House and state Senate passed different versions of the bill.
The state Senate’s version would have allowed development authorities and community improvement districts to hold meetings and public hearings via teleconference.
The Senate did not agree to the House’s substituted version before the end of the session.
The bill will be pending when the state Senate resumes its session next year.
source_title: Georgia could develop statewide electric vehicle manufacturing program • The Georgia Virtue

entity_name: Upstate SC Industrial & Automotive Supply Chain Corridor
canonical_name: Upstate South Carolina Industrial Manufacturing Zone
facts: Commercial roofing in Greenville SC is shaped by one of the deepest industrial building inventories in the Southeast.

The Greenville-Anderson-Mauldin MSA has 1 million+ residents, combined with Spartanburg County (330,000+) and the broader Upstate SC region representing one of the most concentrated industrial manufacturing zones in the Eastern US.
BMW Manufacturing Spartanburg (Greer SC) is the largest BMW plant in the world by output, spanning more than 8 million sqft of plant building.
The plant produces X3, X4, X5, X6, X7, and XM models.
A Tier-1 supplier ring around BMW spans 50+ miles and includes facilities from Adient, BorgWarner, Bosch, Continental, Denso, Faurecia, Magna, Mahle, Plastic Omnium, Thyssenkrupp, and ZF.
This supplier density creates specific discipline requirements including BMW facility standards, JIT production sequencing, and OEM-spec compliance.
The Upstate region hosts Michelin North America HQ in Greenville with multiple tire plants across the region.

Other major industrial presences include GE Power, GE Aviation, Bosch Rexroth, Milliken textile technology, Fluor engineering construction, Lockheed Martin supplier base, 3M specialty manufacturing, ScanSource, and Denny's.
Major medical centers in the area include Prisma Health (formerly Greenville Health System) and Bon Secours St. Francis.
SC commercial contractor licensing (SC LLR) is required for work in the region.

City of Greenville, city of Spartanburg, Greenville County Building Services, Spartanburg County Building Services, and other Upstate counties permit directly with relatively fast plan review taking 10-20 business days for routine commercial projects.
BMW-adjacent Tier-1 work adds BMW corporate qualification requirements on top of local code.
Federal-funded projects under IRA or similar programs require Davis-Bacon wage workflow and certified payroll reporting.
Upstate SC commercial roofing pricing tracks similar to comparable NC metro pricing given the shared labor market and supply chain.
TPO mechanically-attached 60-mil reroof runs $8.50-12 per sqft for routine commercial, while BMW Tier-1 supplier work runs $11-15 per sqft for OEM-spec compliance.
I-85 large-format distribution warehouse reroof runs toward the low end at $8-10.50 per sqft.
Silicone coating systems extend roof life at approximately 50% of full reroof cost for buildings with remaining service life.
Automotive and EV supplier plants require strict OEM-spec discipline.

Installed costs run $8–15 per square foot across Upstate SC, with BMW Tier-1 supplier and medical facility work running higher for compliance, and I-85 distribution warehouses running lowest due to scale.
Upstate SC work adapts to three dominant site types: BMW-adjacent Tier-1 supplier plants, I-85 distribution warehouse, and downtown/institutional commercial.
Licensed roofing professionals are deployed on-site within 48 hours of initial RFQ.
BMW Tier-1 supplier work requires coordination with facility engineering and BMW corporate spec documentation.

Detailed bids are delivered within 48 hours of assessment, including system specs (TPO typical; fully-adhered for BMW paint-shop-adjacent or clean-assembly; standing seam for architectural) and insulation build-up per SC energy code.
Permits must be pulled from applicable municipal or county building services before start, with SC LLR commercial contractor license filed on application.
For BMW Tier-1 plants, tear-off is sequenced to production schedules with paint-shop debris control for paint-shop-adjacent work.
Distribution warehouse sequencing occurs around receiving/shipping operations windows.
Medical facility work requires coordination with facility infection control and rooftop HVAC scheduling.
BMW-adjacent work uses fully-adhered attachment in paint-shop-adjacent or clean-assembly zones, and mechanically-attached in general assembly.
Manufacturer non-dollar-limit warranties are registered for 15-30 years depending on the system.
OEM supplier projects require facility spec compliance documentation for corporate records.
The Upstate SC commercial roof inventory is defined by the BMW Plant Spartanburg ripple that reshaped the region's industrial building stock since 1994.

Over 30+ years of Tier-1 supplier facility construction produced a deep inventory of 100K-500K sqft purpose-built automotive supplier plants across Greenville, Spartanburg, Cherokee, and Laurens counties.
These buildings feature standardized roof profiles, typically mechanically-attached TPO 60-mil over steel deck, with EPDM ballast systems on older supplier facilities.
Original 1990s-2000s builds are now entering full reroof cycles, with increased bid activity on this cohort over the last 3 years.

Michelin North America headquarters in Greenville and multiple tire manufacturing plants (Greenville, Lexington, Sandy Springs SC) carry specific tire-curing-room HVAC spec and process-equipment penetration detailing.
Curing rooms operate at elevated temperature with significant moisture output, causing accelerated deterioration of roof systems above curing operations from thermal and moisture exposure.
Michelin corporate facility standards drive spec choice for its facilities.
The I-85 corridor distribution warehouse boom from Spartanburg south to Anderson represents one of the densest e-commerce fulfillment zones in the SE.
This zone serves both metro Atlanta (2 hours south) and Charlotte (90 minutes north) as overflow capacity.
Amazon, Walmart, FedEx, UPS, and regional 3PLs have all expanded heavily in the corridor.
Typical distribution warehouse footprints run 300K-1M+ sqft.
Cold storage and refrigerated distribution facilities serving the East Coast food distribution cold chain require specific vapor drive and insulation requirements.

Downtown Greenville has experienced significant urban revitalization over the last 20 years, driving new Class A mid-rise office, boutique hotel, and mixed-use development around Main Street, Peace Center, Falls Park, and Liberty Bridge.
Roof inventory in downtown includes newer flat-roof TPO and EPDM systems (2010-present) alongside historic preservation work on adaptive-reuse projects.
Greenville Historic Preservation Commission review applies to downtown historic buildings.

Prisma Health operates one of the largest hospital systems in SC with major campuses in Greenville and across the Upstate, requiring coordination with facility infection control and FGI Guidelines compliance for occupied patient areas.
Pharmacy compounding, research, and GMP-adjacent portions of medical facilities require pharma/biotech roofing discipline.

Clemson University research buildings carry institutional spec requirements including vibration control near sensitive equipment and air-quality monitoring during tear-off near research ventilation systems.
Anderson and Pickens County manufacturing includes Bosch Rexroth, 3M, and a diversified industrial base requiring conventional industrial commercial roofing discipline.

BMW Plant Spartanburg enforces documented corporate facility standards; any roofing scope on BMW-owned property or managed expansions runs through BMW's facility engineering and corporate spec documentation.
JIT production sequencing is the critical constraint on BMW-adjacent facility work, as Tier-1 suppliers cannot afford production disruption.
Roof work on Tier-1 supplier plants runs around production schedules, with tear-off sequenced to shift changes, weekends, or planned maintenance windows.
Paint-shop-adjacent or clean-assembly-area work requires fully-adhered TPO attachment to eliminate fastener debris risk, while general assembly areas typically use mechanically-attached 60-mil TPO.
Federal-funded projects under IRA, CHIPS Act, or related programs require Davis-Bacon wage workflow and certified payroll reporting.
Work near hospital air intakes cannot generate airborne particulates that could enter patient areas.
