facts: Rivian has paused construction of its $5 billion manufacturing facility near Social Circle, Georgia.
Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe announced the pause at the company’s R2 reveal event on Thursday.
The decision to halt construction at the Stanton Springs North site will reduce the amount of capital needed for Rivian to launch and deliver its R2 model to the market.
Instead of Georgia, R2 production will start at Rivian's facility in Normal, Illinois.
The Normal, Illinois plant's capacity is being expanded to 215,000 units a year.
Production of the R2 model at the Illinois facility is expected to begin in the first half of 2026.
RJ Scaringe stated that the Georgia site remains core to scaling across all vehicles between the R2, R3, and R3X models.
RJ Scaringe expressed excitement about leveraging the Illinois facility's team and skills to bring the R2 to market as quickly as possible.
Rivian estimates total savings of over $2.25 billion compared to initial plans to launch the first R2 production line at the Stanton Springs North facility.
The expected savings will come from capital expenditures, product development investment, and supplier sourcing opportunities.
Rivian has been cutting costs over the last year to conserve cash.
Last month, Rivian announced plans to reduce its salaried workforce by 10%.
In February 2023, Rivian laid off 6% of its workforce.
Rivian's stock is down over 40% since the start of the year.
source_title: Rivian halts construction on $5B Georgia EV facility | Manufacturing Dive

canonical_name: Rivian EV Manufacturing Plant in Stanton Springs, Georgia
facts: Clayco has been selected by Rivian to build an advanced manufacturing plant in Stanton Springs, Georgia.
Rivian aims to develop an expansive 1,800-acre complex dedicated to sustainable transportation.
The facility is located in the Stanton Springs area, situated 30 minutes east of metro Atlanta along I-20.
The site is part of a high-tech and bio-pharma industrial park that includes existing residents Takeda, Facebook’s Newton Data Center, and the Georgia Bioscience Training Center.
Upon full completion, the plant will have an annual capacity of 400,000 electric vehicle units.
The facility is positioned at the Stanton Springs North site, approximately 40 minutes from Atlanta across Morgan and Walton Counties.
The State of Georgia and the Joint Development Authority (JDA) of Jasper, Morgan, Newton & Walton Counties collaborated with local partners to prepare the site for Rivian’s establishment.
Earthwork for enabling works was almost 95% complete as of October 2023.
Plant construction is expected to commence in early 2024.
Clayco will work alongside Jacobs, who will serve as Rivian’s engineer of record.
Skidmore, Owings & Merrill (SOM) will act as the owner’s design architect for the project.
The project is backed by an Economic Development Agreement inked in 2022 between the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) and the Joint Development Authority (JDA).
Rivian’s $5 billion Georgia project aims to create 7,500 jobs at Stanton Springs North.
The average wage for the created jobs is projected to be $56,000.
source_title: Clayco to build Rivian’s new EV manufacturing Plant in Georgia - Construction Front | Commercial Intelligence for Infrastructure & Energy

entity_name: US Federal EV Policy & Supply Chain Impact
canonical_name: US Executive Order on Unleashing American Energy and EV Policy
facts: US President Donald Trump took aim at electric vehicles as part of his flurry of first-day actions.
Trump’s executive order on “Unleashing American Energy” included steps to ensure a level playing field for gasoline-powered motors.
The executive order halted federal funding to build new EV charging stations.
The executive order referenced the possible elimination of a federal tax credit for EV purchases.
The executive order referenced the renouncement of a US waiver that allows California to set stricter requirements on cars.
During his inaugural address, Trump said the moves would “end” the “Green New Deal.”
Trump ridiculed Biden-backed incentives for EV sales during his inaugural address.

Policy experts are skeptical Trump will junk all Biden-era EV programs because significant federal funding has gone toward projects in Republican congressional districts where thousands of jobs are expected to be created.
Shares of EV makers like Rivian and EV charging companies such as EVgo fell sharply on Tuesday following the policy announcement.
Tesla, which is led by close Trump ally Elon Musk, also saw its shares fall.
Kathy Harris, director for clean vehicles of the Natural Resources Defense Council (NRDC), called Trump’s policy a sop for “fat-cat oil executives.”
Harris noted that EVs are better for the environment and can save consumers money on gasoline.
Many of Trump’s executive orders are expected to face legal challenges, including possible outcomes for the EV measures.
Harris stated, “This is not the end of this story,” warning that if the administration tries to cut corners or ignore the law, they will end up in court.
The Alliance for Automotive Innovation reiterated its criticism of California’s car regulations in a statement that did not address other elements in Trump’s executive order.

John Bozzella, president of the Alliance for Automotive Innovation, said, “The country should have a single, national standard to reduce carbon in transportation,” and added, “We can’t have regulations that push the industry too far ahead of the customer.”
Automakers are pausing some EV investments due to slowing growth, even as sales of emission-free vehicles climb to new levels in the United States.
In 2024, EV sales in the country reached 1.3 million, up 7.3 percent from the prior year, according to Cox Automotive’s Kelley Blue Book.
Kelley Blue Book pointed to a meaningful rise in EVs at different price levels.
GM and Ford have scaled back some EV investments in recent months, pointing to slowing demand growth.
A Ford executive warned in November that a glut of EVs across showrooms will lead to “incredible pressure” on prices in 2025.
During the presidential campaign, Trump claimed Democrat Kamala Harris wanted to force EVs on consumers.
Harris said she favored consumer choice.
The Biden administration’s fuel economy rules required automakers to market fleets with sharply lower carbon dioxide emissions to address climate change.
Laws such as the $400 billion Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 included a slew of lending and tax credit programs to boost EVs.
Programs under the IRA and the 2021 infrastructure law are in various stages of implementation.
Monday’s executive order directs officials implementing the IRA to undertake a 90-day review to ensure that spending does not unfairly favor EVs “by rendering other types of vehicles unaffordable.”
Policy experts see no meaningful chance that the new administration will try to claw back US funds that have already been spent.
It remains unclear whether Trump will seek to block other projects still moving through the pipeline.
Nearly half of the $5 billion set aside for new EV chargers has been allocated to states under the infrastructure law, according to a memo from NRDC.
The NRDC stated that the 2021 infrastructure law’s “embedded safeguards… should ensure continuity for infrastructure investments.”
The NRDC noted that while the incoming administration could try drastic measures, those will face real-world and legal hurdles.
In November, the Energy Department advanced projects to provide Rivian a $6.6 billion federal loan to build an EV manufacturing facility in Stanton Springs North, Georgia.
Construction on the Georgia plant is expected to begin in 2026, according to Rivian.
The Energy Department also advanced a $7.5 billion loan to StarPlus Energy to finance lithium-ion battery cell manufacturing plants in Kokomo, Indiana, under a Stellantis-Samsung joint venture.
Neither Rivian nor Stellantis responded to AFP queries on the implications of the new Trump policy for their projects.
source_title: As Trump takes aim at EVs, how far will rollback go?

facts: Rivian finalized a $6.6 billion loan from the US Department of Energy (DOE) to construct an electric vehicle manufacturing facility in Stanton Springs North, near the City of Social Circle, Georgia.
The 9-million-square-foot manufacturing plant will be located about an hour from downtown Atlanta.
The facility will create over 7,500 jobs.
The plant will produce Rivian’s R2 SUV and R3 crossover.
Construction of the manufacturing facility is expected to start in 2026.
Vehicle production at the facility is expected to begin by 2028.
The facility will feature advanced manufacturing techniques and sustainable design.
The $6.6 billion loan will be divided into two construction phases: the first phase will amount to $3.4 billion, and the second phase to $2.6 billion.
The remainder of the $6.6 billion loan is capitalized interest.
Rivian Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe stated that the loan will help accelerate the launch of the Georgia plant for the R2 and R3 models, providing thousands of jobs in the state.
RJ Scaringe added that additional capacity for mass market products is key to U.S. leadership in the electric vehicle industry.
Rivian and the DOE worked together closely for over two years to finalize the loan.
The Rivian R2 is an all-electric, 5-seat SUV with an estimated range of 300 miles.
Rivian sells its vehicles directly to consumer and commercial customers.
Rivian's first manufacturing facility is located in Normal, Illinois.
Rivian's headquarters in Irvine, California, manages its engineering and design, electronics, software, propulsion, battery systems, supply chain, and logistics.
source_title: Rivian and DOE Finalize $6.6 Billion Loan for Georgia EV Manufacturing Plant

facts: Rivian broke ground on a $5 billion electric vehicle plant in Social Circle, Georgia.
The facility is located east of Atlanta.
The groundbreaking occurred on a Tuesday.
Rivian is a California-based company that is currently money-losing.
President Donald Trump successfully pushed to roll back electric vehicle tax credits.
Starting Sept 30, buyers will no longer qualify for savings of up to $7,500 per car.
CEO RJ Scaringe stated Rivian will sell its R2 sport utility vehicles because it is a superior vehicle, not for environmental or tax incentive reasons.
Scaringe described the R2 as an incredible five-passenger offroad machine that happens to be electric.
The Georgia plant was first announced in 2021.
The Georgia plant is key to Rivian's profitability goals.
Rivian currently makes the high-end R1T pickup truck and R1S SUV in Normal, Illinois.
Rivian also manufactures delivery vans for Amazon and others.
R1T prices start at $71,000.
The Illinois plant will begin making the smaller R2 next year.
R2 prices will start at $45,000.
An expanded Illinois plant will be able to assemble 215,000 vehicles yearly.
If the R2 is a hit and Rivian successfully produces an even smaller R3, it will need more capacity.
The Georgia operation will be able to make 200,000 vehicles yearly starting in 2028.
Scaringe called the Georgia operation the foundation for growth.
Rivian plans another 200,000 in capacity in phase two.
Phase two volume would spread fixed costs over many more vehicles.
Rivian expects to deliver between 40,000 and 46,000 vehicles this year.
Vehicle deliveries are down from 52,000 last year.
The company is limiting production in part to launch 2026 models.
Alex Oyler, North American director of auto research firm SBD Automotive, said it is do-or-die time for Rivian.
Oyler noted that Tesla showed the key to profitability is scale.
Oyler stated you cannot scale if your cheapest vehicle is $70,000.
Oyler said they need the plant online to achieve a level of scale for the R2 and ultimately R3.
Sales growth for electric vehicles in the United States rose only 1.5% in 2025's first half, according to Cox Automotive.
Tesla accounted for almost 45% of U.S. electric vehicle sales in that period, according to Cox.
General Motors' slice of American EV sales has climbed to 13%.
Rivian had a 3% share in the first half of the year, behind Tesla and six traditional automakers.
Excluding Tesla, Rivian is the most successful startup automaker.
Rivian initially tapped a largely unfilled niche: demand for electric pickups and SUVs.
Competition now includes Ford’s F-150 Lightning and the electric Chevrolet Silverado.
After an initial public offering in 2021, Rivian shares have fallen by more than 80%.
Automaker shares overall have outpaced the broader stock market.
Rivian lost $1.66 billion in 2025’s first half.
Stellantis canceled Ram’s electric truck program last week.
Ford has delayed production at a new Tennessee plant.
General Motors abandoned plans to build electric vehicles in suburban Detroit.
Sam Fiorani, vice president at AutoForecast Solutions, said slowing EV growth and competition do not play in Rivian's favor.
Fiorani noted there still is an EV market out there.
Georgia has pledged $1.5 billion of incentives to Rivian in exchange for 7,500 company jobs.
The 7,500 jobs will pay at least $56,000 a year on average.
Rivian cannot benefit from most incentives unless it meets employment goals.
Georgia is already spending $175 million to buy and grade land and improve roads.
Georgia Republican Gov. Brian Kemp wants to make Georgia the electric mobility capital of America.
Kemp acknowledged the road to get here has not been smooth.
Kemp voiced confidence that Rivian can deliver an innovation revolution benefitting Georgians.
Tesla has thousands of employees in California and Texas.
Two separate EV makers that hoped to assemble vehicles in a former GM plant in Lordstown, Ohio, went bankrupt.
Georgia’s Hyundai complex near Savannah is faring better, with production underway.
A battery plant at the Hyundai complex has been delayed by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement arresting 475 people on site.
The arrests included more than 300 South Koreans.
Rivian was supposed to be making trucks by now at the 2,000-acre (800-hectare) site near Social Circle.
The site is about 45 miles (70 kilometers) east of Atlanta.
As the company burned through cash in 2024, it paused construction.
German automaker Volkswagen agreed to invest $5.8 billion in Rivian in exchange for software and electrical technology.
Then-President Joe Biden’s administration agreed to loan Rivian $6.6 billion to build the Georgia plant.
Scaringe said Rivian has built a very close relationship with the U.S. Department of Energy.
Scaringe said the company’s goals align with some of Trump’s big ones, including U.S. manufacturing, U.S. technology, U.S. technology that supports global business, and leading in all those areas.
Kemp said he has urged Energy Secretary Chris Wright to back Rivian.
Rivian faces opposition from some residents who say the plant is an inappropriate neighbor to farms and will pollute groundwater.
Resident Eddie Clay, who lives less than a mile away, questioned why the biggest project in Georgia has to go next door.
Clay says his well water turned mud-choked after excavation at the Rivian site.
Challenges include tariffs costing $2,000 per vehicle.
The Trump administration ending a tax-credit program will cost the company $140 million in revenue this year.
Long-term threats come from low-priced, cutting-edge Chinese EVs.
Scaringe said the start of construction shows Rivian is working through its challenges.
Scaringe stated these are not kinds of things you start without having clear sight to be able to fully finish and launch.
source_title: ‘Do Or Die’ | News, Sports, Jobs - Post Journal

facts: The Department of Energy announced Jan. 16 that it has closed a $6.57 billion loan to electric vehicle manufacturer Rivian for an electric vehicle plant that previously had been put on pause.
The loan will support the construction of the 9 million-square-foot manufacturing facility near the city of Social Circle, Georgia, near Atlanta.
The project anticipates creating 2,000 full-time jobs through construction, according to the announcement.
Rising costs caused the firm to pause construction of the plant in the spring of 2024.
The EV manufacturer selected Chicago-based Clayco as the general contractor.
When work first paused, Irvine, California-based Rivian said Clayco would maintain a “presence” at the jobsite and that the company would continue performing limited site development activities.
Rivian anticipates that construction will begin in 2026 with production of the company’s R2 SUVs and R3 crossover vehicles starting in 2028.
The firm said it has already begun hiring for construction and management roles, with multiple open positions statewide, and it anticipates ramping up recruitment.
The loan — which includes $5.975 billion of principal and $592 million of capitalized interest — will be split across two phases of the Georgia facility.
The loan is conditional, and exemplary of former President Joe Biden’s agenda to invest in projects related to renewable energy.

President Donald Trump made moves on his first day in office limiting the adoption of some kinds of renewable energy and related products, including electric vehicles, which could slow or dampen construction of these types of projects.
Trump also ordered agencies to pause disbursement of Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act and Inflation Reduction Act funds for EV charging stations.
source_title: Georgia Rivian plant to resume construction | WardsAuto

facts: Six Mexican nationals were brought to the U.S. under false pretenses, with engineering job offers from a staffing agency serving as smoke screens for low-paid assembly-line work in Georgia factories.
The visa program used was the Trade NAFTA (TN) visa, intended for high-skilled jobs but lacking oversight and linked to Southeast auto sector worker exploitation.
The company that hired the Mexican nationals and supported their TN visa applications is a Newnan staffing agency, GB2G, Inc., also known as Allswell.
Workers say Allswell hired them to work for several different auto parts manufacturers in the West Point area that supply the Kia auto plant there.

Allswell has not been charged with any wrongdoing and claims it provides engineering staffing solutions while being in full compliance with all laws, citing federal on-site inspections that found no violations.

Only one of the six interviewed Mexican professionals filed a formal complaint against the company; others preferred to move on rather than initiate lengthy legal processes due to uncertain immigration status.
During his first night in Georgia, worker Luis was promised housing but instead shared a house floor with seven other TN workers.
The six Mexican nationals arrived between December 2020 and May 2021, during a pandemic-driven labor shortage that made low-wage jobs harder to fill with U.S. workers.
Shelly Anand, executive director of the Sur Legal Collaborative, noted employers are seeking more compliant labor forces in high-turnover industries during this economic moment.

Ben Botts, legal director at Centro de los Derechos del Migrante (CDM), highlighted that TN visa fraud allegations in the West Point auto sector are notable given the industry's visibility as a model for rural economic development.

In April, a class action lawsuit filed in Atlanta federal court claimed a Gwinnett-based labor recruiter used the TN visa program to hire dozens of Mexican engineers who performed manual labor in Luverne, Alabama.

The lead plaintiff in the Alabama case is Jaime Obregon Acosta, a Mexican national with a bachelor’s in mechanical engineering and a master’s in business administration, alleging horrendously long hours and hourly wages a fraction of U.S. citizens'.
Over 40 workers hired through the visa program have joined the Alabama class action lawsuit.
Civil rights litigator Daniel Werner stated he frequently encounters visa misclassification cases aimed at lowering labor costs.
Mexican engineers interviewed by the AJC provided documentation showing they worked at three parts makers supplying automaker Kia in West Point: Hyundai Mobis, Hyundai Glovis, and Mando.
Allswell's website lists Hyundai Mobis, Hyundai Glovis, and Mando as part of its client roster; all are South Korean auto parts manufacturers with facilities in the West Point area.
Hyundai Mobis and Mando did not respond to requests for comment.
The head of Glovis Georgia’s HR department stated her company does not work with Allswell, suggesting another subsidiary, Glovis America, may have received the workers.
Kia Georgia stated it is committed to equal employment opportunities and expects the same principles from its business partners.
TN visa status is only for Canadian or Mexican nationals intending temporary U.S. work, requiring a job offer under 60 NAFTA professions that typically require at least a bachelor’s degree.
In fiscal year 2021, the U.S. issued 24,904 TN visas, up from 16,119 four years prior, with Mexico accounting for 98.8% of total visas issued to TN workers and dependents that year.
The State Department operates the TN program for Mexican applicants and claims to work aggressively to ensure visa adjudication integrity through extensive vetting.

Unlike the H-2A farmworker program, which has labor protections and investigations by the U.S. Labor Department’s Wage and Hour division, TN workers lack equivalent labor protections and reporting mechanisms.
Because a TN visa is tied to the sponsoring employer, quitting for a better job in the U.S. can be difficult, leaving workers stuck.
In fall 2020, Heber Zapata saw a LinkedIn posting from Georgia labor recruitment agency SPJ Connect for U.S.-based engineering roles and moved nearly 2,000 miles to take it.
Zapata was offered a $44,000 salary as a "production engineer" with benefits exceeding his Mexican earnings, supported by an Allswell letter describing the job as professional and specialized.
The AJC reviewed six TN visa support letters showing five men were hired at Hyundai Mobis and one at Mando, with offered salaries ranging from $40,000 to $44,000 a year.

Zapata worked overnight shifts (7 p.m. to 7 a.m.) on the assembly line at Hyundai Mobis, earning roughly $580 a week (just over $30,000 annually), compared to American workers performing similar work who earned higher hourly wages.

Zapata filed reports with LaGrange Police Department (July 24, 2021 incident report), the National Human Trafficking Hotline, and U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) but received no follow-up action.
Eight months after arriving in West Point, Zapata left Allswell in August 2021 and applied for a visa granting legal status to human trafficking victims; his allegations remain under investigation.
Labor recruiter SPJ Connect continues advertising vacancies via social media videos pitching TN visa benefits to Mexican university students.

During an April 8 visit to Universidad Tecnológica de Huejotzingo, SPJ Connect executive Yadira Leos told students the company has roughly 500 monthly vacancies and emphasized professional growth in the U.S.

SPJ Connect colleague Elizabeth Cantú stated in a deleted video that all recruited jobs are in the U.S. automotive industry and "Everyone goes to the production line the first year" regardless of job title.

Allswell is registered in Coweta County and provides workers to Georgia-based automotive manufacturers; it should not be confused with All’s Well, a healthcare staffing firm in California, South Carolina, Nevada, Florida, and Pennsylvania.
SPJ Connect is based in Hogansville with offices in Mexico and served as the first point of contact for the interviewed workers.
Hyundai Mobis in West Point builds chassis, bumpers, and cockpit modules for nearby Kia; a 2018 Select Georgia report identified it as a top auto employer with nearly 850 workers.

SPJ Connect and Allswell present as separate entities but share the same registered agent address (124 Big Horn Drive in Newnan) per Georgia Secretary of State records, with Youngjin Lee listed as CEO of SPJ Connect and secretary of Allswell.
LaGrange city officials boosted recruitment efforts; in July 2021, two Economic Development Office officers participated in a SPJ Connect recruiting trip to a Mexican university.
Three months prior, a consortium of Mexican technical colleges signed a collaboration agreement with SPJ Connect for graduate placement, hosted by the LaGrange Economic Development Office.
LaGrange Mayor James Thornton stated industries must recruit internationally when local hiring falls short but emphasized creating jobs for residents as a primary economic development goal.
Tension exists on assembly lines regarding foreign workers; Augusto noted that plants with Mexican workers know locals earn much more, and some locals told the migrants to "Get out of here."

Jose, an industrial engineering graduate, worked as a production supervisor in Tijuana earning roughly $850 per week before accepting a 2020 production engineer position at Hyundai Mobis on a TN visa.
The AJC story originated from April 2022 reporting on the Alabama class action suit, which prompted six former Allswell employees to contact the newspaper with similar experiences and documentation.
source_title: Engineers from Mexico say they were misled into manual labor in Georgia factories

facts: FREYR Battery is a publicly traded Luxembourg and Norway-based company emerging as a leader in clean, high-density battery cell manufacturing.
Tom Jensen serves as the CEO and Cofounder of FREYR Battery.
FREYR is committing some $2.6 Billion to a facility envisioned for Coweta County in Georgia.
FREYR's Giga Arctic facility will be powered by 100% renewable energy.
FREYR's core operational mantras are speed, scale, and sustainability.
Tom Jensen focuses on scalable industrial systems that can be replicated rapidly in many jurisdictions to reduce CO2 emissions.
The Inflation Reduction Act launched by the US administration is cited as a poster child example for accelerating the energy transition.
The IRA incentive program accelerated FREYR's deployment plans in the US, shifting focus from Norway to the US market.

FREYR originally planned to expand further in Norway before replicating its ambitions elsewhere but changed course due to the IRA and strong interest from customers, technology providers, supply chain partners, regulators, and local/state officials.
FREYR plans to develop over 40 gigawatt hours of battery cell capacity by 2025.
FREYR aims to position itself as one of Europe’s largest cell suppliers.
Norway is the leading adopter of electric vehicles on the planet, with close to 90% of all cars sold there being electric.
The Norwegian government removed all taxes on electric vehicles to drive adoption.
Norway launched a national battery strategy earlier this year (relative to February 2023).
FREYR is establishing the full battery value chain in Norway, including its Giga Arctic site located on the Arctic Circle.
FREYR's production technology was selected from 24M Technologies, a MIT spinoff founded by material science professor Yet-Ming Chiang.
FREYR's new production process reduces facility footprint per gigawatt hour by more than 80% compared to conventional solutions.
The new process consumes more than 60% less energy in the production process itself by eliminating solvent recovery steps.
The simplified process requires less than half as many people per installed capacity compared to conventional plants.
FREYR can use existing raw materials including nickel, manganese, cobalt oxide cathodes, lithium-ion phosphate cathodes, graphite-based anodes, and silicone in anodes.
The first gigawatt-hour scale production line, labeled the customer qualification plant, is expected to be operational in Q1 2024.
Giga Arctic will feature eight of these gigawatt-scale production lines.
FREYR's technology offers a 50% lower estimated capital expenditure per gigawatt hour compared to plants currently in operation or under construction.
The technology is expected to generate anticipated 200% higher production per plant employed compared to industry averages.
Q CELLS announced a $2.5 billion expansion of its solar panel manufacturing facilities in Georgia, creating 2,500 new jobs.

Georgia's attractiveness for clean tech includes relatively low energy costs, a huge logistics hub with rail and air, an influential network of technical colleges, favorable right-to-work laws, and state/local government support.
FREYR is counting on developing and expanding a local supply chain in Georgia as part of its Giga America project.
Tom Jensen previously worked at Norsk Hydro in the oil/gas and aluminum sectors before focusing on sustainability and batteries.
FREYR Battery is publicly traded on the New York Stock Exchange.

canonical_name: 8 Companies Announce U.S. Investments in LFP Battery Production
facts: Chinese power battery companies have secured six spots in the global installation TOP10 list, with a market share exceeding 60%.

From January to July, lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries significantly led ternary batteries in production and installation volumes, both with percentages exceeding 60%, reaching as high as 68.1% of total installation volume.
New energy vehicles equipped with LFP batteries are now spread globally, with BYD and Tesla being the most notable among global new energy vehicle giants.
Car companies from Japan and South Korea, such as Toyota and Hyundai, have begun using LFP batteries.
Overseas markets are accelerating adoption of LFP battery technologies, with U.S. interest and investment rising due to the Inflation Reduction Act.
Eight companies announced plans to build LFP battery and material factories in the U.S., with six specifying investment amounts totaling over 841 billion RMB.
Tesla and Rivian are considering similar investments, with Tesla leaning towards collaborations with Ford and CATL.
BYD Executive Vice President Li Ke revealed in December that the company is looking to establish a dedicated battery production base in the U.S.
On February 14, Ford and CATL jointly announced the construction of a lithium iron phosphate (LFP) battery factory in Marshall, Michigan.
CATL will offer construction and operational services and license its battery technology for the Ford project.
The Ford-CATL factory investment is projected at $3.5 billion (equivalent to ¥255.2 billion RMB).
The Ford-CATL factory has an initial design capacity of about 35GWh per year.
The Ford-CATL factory will supply battery packs for approximately 400,000 Ford electric vehicles.
Ford is considering expanding the Marshall, Michigan factory's capacity.
On March 24, LG Energy Solution officially declared its intention to invest ₩7.2 trillion in a large battery manufacturing complex in Arizona with a total capacity of 43.3GWh.
LG Energy Solution's Arizona investment includes ₩4.2 trillion for a cylindrical battery factory in North America with an average annual capacity of 27GWh, with production beginning in 2025.
LG Energy Solution will use ₩3 trillion (equivalent to ¥163.22 billion RMB) to establish a battery production plant specifically for ESS (Energy Storage Systems).
The LG Energy Solution ESS plant will be the world's first ESS-only production factory upon completion.
LG Energy Solution is developing a cobalt-free LFP battery to outperform CATL by 20% in energy density and aims to supply Tesla.
During an April 28 media event, Guoxuan High-tech Deputy General Manager Chen Ruilin revealed plans to establish two U.S. production bases, one for batteries and the other for battery materials.
Reports from last October indicated Gotion, a subsidiary of Guoxuan, received approval to build a battery materials factory near Big Rapids, Michigan with an estimated investment of $2.364 billion.
In June, Gotion received federal approval to continue its Michigan construction.
The location for Guoxuan's battery factory will be determined soon.
In June 2022, Guoxuan announced a 60GWh production line equipment tender that included locations in the U.S.
On December 6, 2022, American Battery Factory (ABF) announced its plan to construct an LFP super-factory in Tucson, Arizona with an estimated investment of $1.2 billion (¥87.49 billion RMB).
In November 2022, Norwegian battery startup FREYR Battery, listed in New York, announced the purchase of land in Coweta County, Georgia to build the Giga America battery factory.
The FREYR Battery Giga America factory has a phase one capacity of about 34GWh and an initial capital estimate of $1.7 billion.
In October 2022, OUR NEXT ENERGY (ONE) revealed a $1.6 billion investment plan (¥116.67 billion RMB) to build the ONE Circle LFP battery factory in Van Buren Township, Michigan.
Production at the ONE Circle LFP battery factory is set to start in 2024, aiming for a 20 GWh annual capacity by 2027.

In October 2022, specialty mineral company ICL Group announced a $400 million investment plan (¥29.17 billion RMB) to establish an LFP cathode active material factory in St. Louis, starting operations in 2024.
U.S. battery tech developer Kore Power plans to set up the KOREPlex factory in Arizona, producing both EV batteries and storage systems using NMC and LFP technologies.
Investment and capacity details for the Kore Power KOREPlex factory remain undisclosed.
source_title: $84.1 Billion LFP Battery Project Lands in the U.S - MANLY

facts: Freyr Battery has officially abandoned its plans for a $2.6 billion battery manufacturing facility in Coweta County, Georgia.
The project was announced in 2022 under the name Giga America.
The Georgia facility was expected to create over 700 jobs.
The first phase of the Georgia facility was expected to deliver 34 GWh of battery capacity.
Freyr is now focusing on solar module manufacturing in Texas after acquiring a 5 GW solar facility from Trina Solar for $340 million.
Freyr selected Austin, Texas, to relocate its headquarters and sold the 368-acre Georgia property.
The company stated that the announcement coincides with the ongoing ramp of production at FREYR’s G1 solar module facility in Wilmer, Texas.
The G1 solar module facility in Wilmer, Texas, already employs more than 1,000 people across the Wilmer and Dallas communities.

Daniel Barcelo, FREYR’s Chairman of the Board and Chief Executive Officer, stated that as the company moves forward with its strategy to build an integrated American solar + storage manufacturing network, it intends to bring more than 1,000 new American jobs to the Texas economy.

Owner James Hill holds a Georgia Master Electrical Contractor license, the highest level of electrical licensing issued by the state.
James Hill personally oversees every Newnan installation from the initial site visit through the final walkthrough.
The company is a Generac Authorized Dealer.
The company is fully insured.
Holds a 4.9-star rating from over 30 verified Atlanta customers on Google.
Operates without commission-driven sales reps.
Charges no hidden fees for installations.
Avoids surprise add-ons on all projects.
Installs whole-home Generac standby generators for residential customers in Newnan to maintain power during outages of any length.
Sizes every residential generator to fit the specific home.
Runs new gas lines for generator installations.
Installs automatic transfer switches for all systems.
Provides operational walkthroughs for all installed equipment.
Handles all Newnan home generator installations in-house, ranging from small essential-circuit systems to full whole-house backup.
Installs commercial Generac standby generator systems for restaurants, retail stores, medical practices, professional offices, and manufacturing facilities across Newnan.
Sizes commercial generator systems to fit specific business operations.
Handles all permitting for commercial installations in-house.

Verifies panel capacity is sufficient for EV charging loads.
Configures breaker setups to safely handle vehicle electrical loads.
Service area covers Downtown Newnan, Summergrove, Lake Redwine, White Oak, Sharpsburg, and surrounding Coweta County neighborhoods for generator and EV charger installations.
Serves almost every street in the metro Atlanta area.

Adopts a no-high-pressure-sales approach for all customer interactions.

entity_name: Top 10 Electric UTV Manufacturers in Poland
facts: The electric ATV and UTV industry is expected to reach a market size of approximately USD 3,683 million by 2030, with a market valuation of USD 1,409 million by 2024.
Electric UTVs are rapidly replacing traditional fuel models due to core advantages including low noise, zero emissions, and low operating costs.
Electric UTVs are widely used in agriculture, forestry, industrial transportation, recreation, and outdoor adventure.
Poland is experiencing rising demand for electric UTVs with huge market potential as an important agricultural country and industrial manufacturing powerhouse in Europe.
Polaris Inc. is headquartered in Medina, Minnesota, and is a leading global manufacturer of off-road vehicles including ATVs, UTVs, motorcycles, and snowmobiles.
Polaris reported revenues approaching $8.6 billion in 2022.
Polaris product lines include RZR, RANGER, and THE GENERAL, supported by more than 3,000 dealerships globally and the industry’s highest annual sales volume.
Polaris introduced the Ranger XP Kinetic model featuring a 110-horsepower electric motor that delivers instant torque and zero-emission driving.
The Polaris Pro XD Kinetic model offers approximately 60 percent lower scheduled maintenance costs than comparable gas-powered vehicles.
Polaris has over 70 years in the industry with global brand awareness second to none and a distribution network covering major markets worldwide.
Polaris’s all-electric RANGER model is regarded as the “gold standard” of electric UTVs and has won numerous industry awards.
Polaris electric models face high pricing and entry thresholds that pressure small and medium-sized users.
Polaris’s European after-sales service network is insufficient compared to North America, requiring improved local service response speed in Poland.
Can-Am is operated by the Canadian BRP Group, which holds a major share of the off-road vehicle market alongside Polaris, Honda, Yamaha, and Kawasaki.
BRP was founded in 1942 and is headquartered in Valcourt, Quebec, Canada.
BRP’s Can-Am brand launched Defender and Maven series models equipped with hybrid and all-electric systems for electric UTVs.
BRP introduced the Segway Super Villain SX20 Hybrid, a revolutionary Side-by-Side product combining a turbocharged engine and powerful electric motor.
Can-Am has deep roots in the European market with a well-established sales and after-sales network, making it highly competitive in Poland.
Can-Am products feature avant-garde design and strong performance, highly sought after by young consumers.
Can-Am maintains a stable system of authorized dealers in Poland and other European countries with strong service guarantees.
Can-Am holds a first-mover advantage in oil-electric hybrid technology to satisfy users with different needs.
BRP’s R&D pace for pure electric models lags behind major competitors, slowing its electrification transformation progress.
Can-Am faces high dependence on imported core components, creating supply chain stability challenges under special circumstances.
John Deere was founded in 1837 and is headquartered in Moline, Illinois, USA.
John Deere is the world’s largest manufacturer of agricultural machinery and the second largest manufacturer of construction machinery.
John Deere’s Gator UTV series covers the Work series, medium-sized Crossover Gator series, full-size Gator XUV Crossover series, and electric UTVs.
John Deere emphasizes durability, power, and practical functionality over cool appearance in its product design.
The John Deere Gator Series is widely recognized and trusted by farmers and professional organizations in Poland for reliable agricultural performance.
John Deere products feature industrial-grade components designed for long service life and low total cost of ownership.
John Deere enjoys high local recognition in Poland due to the country’s status as an important European agricultural powerhouse.
John Deere pricing is on the high side, targeting professional agricultural customers with limited acceptance by individual recreational users.
John Deere’s motorized product line is conservative, and its innovation pace lags behind competing brands focused on off-road recreation.
Club Car is headquartered in Augusta, Georgia, and is a leading manufacturer of electric and gasoline-powered vehicles specializing in golf carts, utility vehicles, and low-speed vehicles.
Club Car’s Carryall line serves commercial and recreational applications alongside competitors Textron Specialized Vehicles and Toro.
Club Car electric models are positioned mainly in the high-end, heavy-duty segment.
Club Car dominates enclosed or semi-enclosed scenarios such as industrial sites, airports, resorts, and golf courses with a high reputation in the European commercial UTV market.
Club Car has a well-established global service network with dealers across Europe and strong local after-sales support in Poland.
Club Car has a long history of electrification with early starts in electric vehicle models, deep technology accumulation, and mature products.
Club Car products have relatively limited off-road performance and adaptability to extreme terrain due to their commercial positioning.
Consumer perception of Club Car is biased towards “slow and practical,” making it less attractive to performance-focused off-road players.
CFMOTO was founded in 1989 and is headquartered in Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China.
CFMOTO has established a deep strategic partnership with the world-renowned brand KTM for technology development and brand enhancement.
In 2024, CFMOTO launched the CFORCE EV110 electric ATV designed for teenagers, featuring an equivalent of 110cc motor, maximum speed of 28 mph, and a range of 40 miles.
CFMOTO products have been exported to more than 100 countries and regions with authorized distribution networks in Poland and the entire European market.
CFMOTO offers outstanding price/performance ratios providing mainstream European and American product performance at reasonable prices.
CFMOTO products comply with relevant EU regulations and certification standards, facing no compliance barriers to enter the Polish market.
CFMOTO’s high-end brand image is still under construction, with some European consumers limiting brand premium recognition of Chinese brands.
CFMOTO has a gap in localized after-sales service systems in Europe compared to top European and American brands.
Hisun Motors was founded in 1988 and prioritizes innovation and quality in its diversified lineup of work and recreational UTVs.
In 2024, Hisun established a North American production base in McKinney, Texas, specializing in final assembly of UTVs for the North American market.

Hisun’s Sector E1 is a competitive low-priced electric UTV featuring a standard roof, windshield, winch, 26-inch tires, 14-inch aluminum wheels, mirrors, and a tilting cargo bed carrying up to 500 pounds.
Hisun provides OEM production of UTVs for large retailers and possesses mature large-scale manufacturing capability.
Hisun faces low brand awareness in Poland and Europe with room for improvement in consumer recognition.
Hisun EV range and performance parameters still have room for improvement compared to top brands.
Segway-Ninebot is headquartered in Beijing, China, with R&D and production centers in many countries worldwide.
Segway-Ninebot successfully entered the electric UTV track with its Villain series of electric off-road vehicles.
The Segway Super Villain SX20 hybrid model uses a composite power structure of a turbocharged engine and powerful electric motor, described by R&D as “probably the most rugged model ever.”
Segway is rapidly gaining traction in the European market, attracting technology enthusiasts and a new generation of off-roaders.
Segway has significant advantages in in-vehicle systems, intelligent connectivity, and digital driving experience meeting young user needs.
Segway’s hybrid system effectively solves pure electric range anxiety, offering more flexible usage scenarios.
Segway’s internationalized brand image strongly attracts millennials and Generation Z consumer groups.
Segway is a late entrant to the electric UTV segment, with accumulation and recognition among professional off-road users still in a growth stage.
The dealer and after-sales network for Segway’s high-end hybrid models in the Polish market requires further development.
American Landmaster is headquartered in Indiana, USA, and has been engaged in multi-purpose off-road vehicles for decades.
Landmaster launched the AMP 4×4 all-electric UTV series driven by 48V lithium-ion batteries with a maximum range of up to 50 miles.
The Landmaster AMP XL version offers more torque and a longer cargo bed, while the fenceless RVR version has a top speed of 15 mph ideal for business park interiors.
American Landmaster is ranked by industry authorities as one of the top players in the global electric ATV and UTV market.
Landmaster products feature utilitarian designs fitting farms, ranches, and construction industries with easy maneuverability.
Landmaster offers a reasonable price range balancing performance and cost for small and medium-sized users with limited budgets.
Landmaster has limited international awareness and almost no systematic sales and service channels established in the Polish and European markets.
Landmaster’s relatively conservative product design limits appeal to European consumers interested in appearance and personalization.
Volcon Inc. is a pioneering all-electric off-road powersports company that will unveil its HF1 electric UTV in September 2024 and launch its first production run.
The Volcon HF1 features a three-seat, single-row design combining advanced technology with robust off-road performance for enthusiasts and commercial operators.
The HF1 will be the fastest accelerating model in the single-cabin electric UTV category.
Volcon’s core strategy is to fully embrace pure electric technology across motorcycles, UTVs, and other categories as cutting-edge exploration of electrified transformation.
Volcon faces challenges in scaling up mass production and supply chain maturity as a relatively young brand.
Volcon’s brand penetration and dealer network layout in Europe, especially Poland, are still in the initial stage.
Lvcci & Kingwoo specializes in electric UTV series for agriculture, ranching, forestry, industrial transportation, construction, and hunting.
The Lvcci & Kingwoo industrial park is located in Henan Province, China, founded in 2014 with over 10 years of production experience.
The company operates under a dual-brand system including Henan Province Lvcci Electric Vehicle Manufacturing Co. and is one of the few suppliers legally registered for European roads.
Flagship models RUE725 and RUE725X passed EEC/COC certification, fully meeting strict requirements for legal operation on European roads.
Every component of the RUE725 series, including windshields, lamps, horns, mirrors, tires, and seat belts, obtained EU E-Mark certification.
This certification advantage significantly reduces compliance risks and entry costs for importers in the Polish market.
Lvcci & Kingwoo electric UTVs underwent rigorous 30° incline platform tests where constant torque from powerful electric motors maintained stable climbing processes.
The company offers strong customization capabilities including body color, seat count, and power configurations supported by a technical team with 10 years of manufacturing experience.
Lvcci & Kingwoo provides competitive pricing offering close to or same-level performance as European and US brands at more competitive factory prices.
The company offers Li-Ion battery upgrade options over standard AGM batteries, providing longer single-use range and a service life three to four times longer.
Lvcci & Kingwoo has established a perfect after-sales service system for its products.
Brand recognition and marketing efforts for Lvcci & Kingwoo in Poland and Europe still have room for improvement compared to established European and American manufacturers.
Chinese manufacturers like Lvcci & Kingwoo need time to accumulate high-end brand perception among some European consumers, limiting brand premium space without localized promotion.
source_title: Top 10 Electric UTV Manufacturers in Poland | Brand Comparison & Buying Guide

facts: U.S. Battery Manufacturing Co. was cited by OSHA for exposing employees to lead exceeding permissible exposure limits at its Augusta, Georgia facility.
The company was also cited for exposing employees to arsenic above the action level.
OSHA cited the manufacturer for failing to record on OSHA's 300 log that medically removed employees from exposure.
The company failed to conduct required training.
The company failed to implement a medical surveillance program.
The facility was cited for having unguarded machinery.
The facility was cited for failing to properly guard equipment.
OSHA proposed $115,594 in penalties against U.S. Battery Manufacturing Co.
The investigation was initiated under OSHA's Regional Emphasis Program on Lead.

The company has 15 business days from receipt of the citations and proposed penalties to comply, request an informal conference with OSHA’s area director, or contest the findings before the Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission.
OSHA Atlanta-East Area Director William Fulcher stated that elevated lead levels can cause debilitating and permanent health issues.
Under OSHA's lead standard, employers must minimize workers' exposure using engineering controls, safe work practices, and protective clothing and equipment.

The Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 holds employers responsible for providing safe and healthful workplaces, with OSHA setting and enforcing standards while providing training, education, and assistance.
LG Energy Solution Vertech and Qcells announced a multi-year commitment to install American-made storage products on Qcells development projects across the United States.
The agreement includes 5 GWh of lithium-ion energy storage systems for utility-scale projects, along with lifecycle services from LG Energy Solution Vertech.
In 2024, Qcells entered into battery purchase agreements with LG Energy Solution Vertech totaling 4.8 GWh of BESS projects developed across the United States.

The new 5-GWh partnership secures energy storage systems for Qcells that meet domestic content requirements because LG Energy Solution’s battery energy storage products are made in Michigan and Qcells’ solar panels are made in Georgia.

Chris Hodrick, CEO of Qcells EPC, stated that the collaboration reflects a shared commitment to building clean energy by creating American jobs, strengthening supply chains, and delivering reliable, affordable power for communities across the country.
The energy storage systems included in the commitment are built using LG Energy Solution containerized lithium-ion battery solutions with LFP cells manufactured at U.S. facilities.
LG Energy Solution is manufacturing up to 16.5 GWh of battery energy storage cells in Holland, Michigan.
LG Energy Solution's production is set to ramp up to around 50 GWh at sites across North America by the end of 2026.

Jaehong Park, CEO and President at LG Energy Solution Vertech, stated that being a long-term partner to customers is extremely important and expressed commitment to supporting projects throughout their lifecycles to enable long-term success of energy storage programs.
Qcells operates the largest silicon solar panel manufacturing facility in the United States, based in Dalton, Georgia.
The company is building a second factory in Cartersville, Georgia, where it will manufacture ingots, wafers, cells, and finished panels.
Once fully operational, Qcells’ annual solar panel production capacity in the US will reach 8.4 GW.
source_title: Qcells US construction arm signs 5-GWh BESS supply deal with LG Energy Solution

facts: Qcells is a South Korean solar manufacturer with facilities in the U.S. and Malaysia.
Qcells has begun manufacturing solar cells at its Cartersville, Georgia facility.
Manufacturing solar cells allows Qcells to produce the major components of a solar module all under one roof at the Cartersville site.
Qcells expects to reach full production at its Cartersville facility by Q3 2026.
At full capacity, the Cartersville facility will produce an estimated 3.3 GW each of ingots, wafers, and cells annually.
The Cartersville facility will also produce 3.5 GW of solar modules per year at full capacity.
Qcells operates a separate manufacturing facility in Dalton, Georgia.
Qcells' Dalton facility tripled its module capacity to 5.1 GW in late 2023.
Combined, Qcells' two Georgia facilities can produce 8.6 GW of solar modules annually at full capacity.
The combined annual output of the two Georgia facilities equals approximately 47,000 panels.
Andy Park, Global CEO of Qcells, stated that producing the first solar cells at Cartersville is a milestone for Qcells and American manufacturing.
Andy Park noted that a dependable domestic supply chain creates thousands of good-paying jobs and gives customers greater certainty on price, supply, tariffs, and product trust.
By the end of 2026, Qcells expects to employ a combined workforce of nearly 4,000 people across its two Georgia sites.
The addition of 3.3 GW of cell capacity to the U.S. solar supply chain coincides with record-high electricity demand and solar projects rushing to meet tax credit eligibility deadlines.
Jade Jones, senior director of solar products manufacturing and supply chain for SEIA, described the current state of U.S. solar manufacturing as 'an exciting story' during a recent webinar.
Jones noted that solar manufacturing is taking place in 43 states and Puerto Rico.
Jones stated that for some solar products, domestic capacity is sufficient to meet domestic demand.
Jones observed that while there are upstream gaps, upstream manufacturing buildout typically follows downstream buildout.
According to SEIA, U.S. solar module manufacturing grew from 8 GW prior to federal manufacturing tax credits to 69.9 GW as of June 2026, an increase of over 750%.
In 2024, U.S. solar cell manufacturing was onshored for the first time since 2019.
SEIA reports that about 30 GW of solar manufacturing is currently under construction in the U.S.
While U.S. solar module manufacturing has grown steadily since 2018, ingot, wafer, and cell manufacturing has only recently begun to rise.

Scott Moskowitz, vice president of market strategy and industrial affairs at Qcells, stated in a press conference last year that the company invested over $3 billion to build out its entire solar supply chain on U.S. soil.
Qcells has expanded beyond vertically integrated solar manufacturing into recycling and energy storage.
Qcells' first recycling operation is located at the Cartersville facility and is expected to recycle approximately 250 MW of solar panels annually, or about 500,000 panels per year.
Qcells launched a new business division called 'Qcells New Homes' to provide builders with turnkey solutions for integrating solar and battery storage into new construction projects.
Moskowitz told pv magazine USA that with storage, EPC, and solar recycling in its portfolio, Qcells is building the clean energy infrastructure the country needs.
source_title: Qcells begins production of solar cells in Cartersville, Georgia - pv magazine USA

facts: Offers public and private charging solutions capable of charging electric vehicles anywhere, anytime.
Provides an interconnected network that is easily accessible and bookable via smartphone.
Maintains a widespread network of more than 70,000 charging points around the world.
Ensures reliable and accessible service for worry-free charging wherever users are.
Public EV charging services are offered by various operators or Mobility Service Providers (MSPs).
Each provider allows customers to view a map of their EV charging stations on their website or dedicated app.
The Enel map shows the charging infrastructure of MSPs with whom Enel has interoperability agreements, along with their status.
Interoperability agreements allow Enel's customers to access a network of hundreds of thousands of charging points through an Enel service subscription.
The Enel On Your Way app lets users manage and monitor charging activity while locating stations via an interactive map.
Users can find nearby charging stations and check availability in real time via smartphone or car infotainment screen.
Charging station maps provide visual clues to show station status, such as in use, reserved, under maintenance, or available.
Operational charging points appear green on the Enel On Your Way app map.
Clicking an operational point allows users to check availability of various charging points and supported socket or connector types.
The Enel On Your Way app is at the heart of an ecosystem of products and services for monitoring and managing charging sessions with a simple tap.
Offers dedicated, flexible, and customizable solutions for corporate clients that enable monitoring of electric vehicles and charging stations and simplify corporate fleet management.
source_title: Electric charging stations Map | Enel Group

facts: Saft is a leading lithium-ion battery developer and manufacturer.
Haskell combined manufacturing, administrative, and support areas into a smart, sustainable structure for the facility.
The facility produces 4,800,000 cells annually.
The facility includes a two-story administrative office.
The facility includes clean/dry rooms class 1,000 (ISO Class 6), certified with -40ºC and -27ºC return dew points.
The facility includes coating ovens, formation area (charging) and open circuit voltage testing area, final assembly area.
The facility includes electrical, chillers and air compressor rooms, maintenance and support areas, and shipping and receiving docks.
The Saft plant’s sustainable design elements earned LEED silver certification.
The facility features photovoltaic electricity generation.
The facility features stormwater management.
The facility features a reduced heat island effect.
The facility features reduced parking.
The facility uses captured rainwater.
The facility features fundamental refrigerant management.
The facility features high-efficiency HVAC equipment.
LEED Silver certification was achieved with 37 points under v2.2.
Water conservation is achieved through captured rainwater and low-flow fixtures, allowing a 100% reduction in irrigation.
Energy conservation reduced energy use by 18%.
Alternative transportation features include bicycle racks, showers, lockers, changing facilities, and preferred parking for high efficiency vehicles.
26% of building materials came from recycled sources.
Nearly 41% of building materials were produced regionally.
Many operations materials are recycled on-site.
The photovoltaic system is the largest private photovoltaic installation in the Southeast at 1-MW (mega-watt) of power.
The manufacturing facility size is 235,200 SF.
Haskell team members worked closely with process and commissioning subject matter experts to understand lithium-ion construction needs.
Haskell challenged Saft and Jacksonville city officials to ensure safe and viable structures were produced.
source_title: Saft America Battery Manufacturing Facility | Haskell

facts: Develops advanced all-solid-state lithium battery solutions using a patented glass electrolyte separator for higher energy density and safety.
Based in Atlanta, Georgia.
Drives innovation for diverse applications.
source_title: Solid-State Battery Companies - Battery-Tech Network

facts: Tesla laid off hundreds of employees.
EnviroSpark, an Atlanta-based EV charging company, is bringing on new talent.
The layoffs at Tesla have created a new hiring opportunity for EnviroSpark.
source_title: Tesla layoffs give EnviroSpark, Atlanta EV charging company, new opportunity - Atlanta Business Chronicle

facts: Duracell Manufacturing, LLC announced it will invest $25 million to expand its existing battery component manufacturing operations in Cleveland, Tennessee.
The expansion project will create 25 new jobs as a result of the project.
The expansion will support Duracell’s LaGrange, Georgia, site by providing strategic battery components supply and allowing the company to meet and exceed its growing North American battery demand.
The Cleveland plant opened in 1961 and has been producing batteries under the Duracell brand name since 1964.
Tennessee Gov. Bill Lee, Department of Economic and Community Development Commissioner Stuart McWhorter and Duracell Manufacturing officials announced the expansion.
Stefaan Boterberg, president, Duracell Manufacturing, LLC stated the site adds a new important role of supporting the greater North American supply chain.
Stefaan Boterberg noted the company is excited to invest in and leverage the skill sets of current and new Duracell team members.
Cleveland Mayor Kevin Brooks confirmed the $25 million expansion is located at the local Duracell plant on Mouse Creek Road.
Cleveland Mayor Kevin Brooks highlighted Duracell’s community support through the “Power Forward” relief program and supporting local organizations.
Bradley County Mayor D. Gary Davis stated the $25 million investment and new jobs underscore Duracell’s commitment to improving its production capacity.
Bradley County Mayor D. Gary Davis emphasized the project solidifies a partnership with the community for decades to come.

TVA senior vice president of economic development John Bradley confirmed TVA and Cleveland Utilities are partnering with the City of Cleveland, Bradley County, Cleveland-Bradley Chamber of Commerce and TNECD to help support Duracell.
Tennessee State Sen. Adam Lowe (R-Calhoun) expressed appreciation for the work of state and local officials who helped facilitate the investment.
Tennessee State Rep. Kevin Raper (R-Cleveland) stated the expansion will further strengthen the economy and bring more quality jobs to the area.
Since 2019, TNECD has supported more than 40 economic development projects in the Southeast Tennessee region.
These TNECD-supported projects have resulted in approximately 7,800 job commitments and $3.2 billion in capital investment.
source_title: Governor Lee, Commissioner McWhorter Announce Duracell Manufacturing, LLC to Expand Manufacturing Operations in Bradley County - TNECD

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) LLC
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) LLC
facts: Hyundai Motor Group’s Executive Chair Euisun Chung and Georgia’s Governor Brian P. Kemp recently broke ground on Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) LLC’s new EV and battery plant at the Bryan County Mega site.
A future technology expo highlighting “advanced technology and extraordinary human achievement” was held at the Enmarket Arena in Savannah following the groundbreaking ceremony.
Five months ago, Hyundai and its affiliated suppliers announced a $5.54 billion investment.
The investment includes plans to build a new battery production plant.
The investment also covers plans to produce a variety of cutting-edge Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia electric cars (EVs).
HMGMA is expected to add more than 8,100 jobs over the coming years.
HMGMA is working to build a reliable supply chain for EV batteries and other EV parts for the American market.
Commercial production at HMGMA’s new smart factory is anticipated to start in the first half of 2025.
Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung stated that Hyundai and Georgians share qualities including respect for history, ingenuity, creativity, and determination.
Chung noted that Hyundai's EVs are recognized as best in class and expressed determination to be a global leader in electrification, safety, quality, and sustainability through this partnership.
Chung stated that the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America will help the company evolve beyond an automaker to become the world’s leading mobility solutions provider.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated that record jobs and investment are coming to all parts of Georgia alongside award-winning workforce development programs and infrastructure.
Kemp described the partnership with Hyundai Motor Group and the groundbreaking as exemplifying unprecedented economic success for the state.
Kemp expressed a long-term commitment to improving lives and livelihoods and anticipated the facility's impact on both the local community and the state.
source_title: Hyundai’s EV & Battery manufacturing plant to open 8,100 jobs in Georgia

facts: Auto parts maker Joon Georgia Inc. announced they will build a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County.
The project will create more than 600 jobs.
The company is investing $317 million into the county.
Joon Georgia Inc. is the first supplier for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America in Bryan County.
The facility will manufacture the frame for the vehicles at the Hyundai EV plant in Black Creek.
Joon Georgia Inc. is part of Ajin USA, who is already a supplier for several Hyundai Motor Group facilities.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated that less than two weeks after the groundbreaking of Hyundai Motor Group’s innovative facility, they are already seeing regional impact.
Gov. Brian Kemp noted Joon Georgia will be the first of many companies drawn to Georgia to support the Metaplant, creating jobs and opportunity for generations of hardworking Georgians.
The facility will be located seven miles outside Statesboro in the Bruce Yawn Commerce Park.
The unit is currently planned for 866,000 square feet.
The square footage could change as the facility is still in the design phase.
Savannah Joint Development Authority President and CEO Trip Tollison said this announcement marks the first of many supplier announcements to come as Hyundai Motor Group moves into the market.
The Savannah JDA consists of Bryan, Bulloch, Chatham and Effingham counties.
Benjy Thompson is the CEO of the Bulloch County Development Authority.
Thompson described the site as the city’s “industrial jewel” and noted it took years to develop.
Joon Georgia Inc. reached out to Thompson in June.
Thompson stated they put resources on the property at the interstate to make it marketable.
Thompson mentioned spending a significant amount of money making their entire site pad ready.
Thompson expects more suppliers to build facilities in Bulloch County but could not provide further details.
The hiring process is expected to begin mid-2023.
Operations at the facility are expected to start in 2024.
Thompson noted their workforce and proximity away from the plant worked in their favor.
Thompson stated the cost of labor in Bulloch County is probably a little less than the Savannah metro area.
Thompson explained they can draw the workforce to the west, whereas to the east of Savannah is the ocean.
Ajin USA’s CEO Jung Ho Sea said they are eager to partner with the county and state and are committed to being the “best employer” for the community.

Sea stated they are excited to be one of the first Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America automotive suppliers to build an automotive parts manufacturing facility in Bulloch County and for their first plant in Georgia.
Sea expressed a desire to be the best corporate citizen and give back to the community of Bulloch County and the city of Statesboro.
source_title: Hyundai's first supplier has arrived: Joon Georgia Inc. to build facility in Bulloch County - AOL

facts: Hyundai Motor Group is making history with the grand opening of Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
The facility is located in Georgia.
It is the largest economic development project in the state’s history.
Construction was completed just two and a half years after breaking ground.
The grand opening event celebrated Hyundai’s dedication to innovation, sustainability, and job creation in the U.S.
Through a $21 billion investment, Hyundai aims to drive manufacturing growth.
Hyundai plans to create 14,000 full-time jobs by 2028.
The economic ripple effect is expected to generate over 100,000 direct and indirect jobs across related industries.
This further solidifies Hyundai’s role in shaping the future of mobility in America.
The facility focuses on electric vehicle manufacturing with advanced technology and cutting-edge production methods.
Hyundai Motor Group’s new facility represents a major step forward in the company’s expansion and commitment to sustainability.
source_title: Hyundai's new Georgia plant set to revolutionize U.S. electric vehicle production

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America is Hyundai Motor Group's new electric vehicle plant in Georgia.
The plant will begin production later this year.
Production start will allow Hyundai to leverage Inflation Reduction Act benefits to better compete in the US EV market.
Hyundai announced that the inaugural model produced at the Georgia plant will be the 2025 Ioniq 5.
The launch of the inaugural model is set for the fourth quarter.
This announcement marks Hyundai's first official statement on the operational timeline and specific production plans for the new facility.
The plant will manufacture all trims of the Ioniq 5 except the high-performance Ioniq 5 N.
Industry expectations pinpoint October as the likely start date for production.
HMGMA is located in Bryan County, Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group began constructing the dedicated EV plant in October 2022.
The project was initially slated for completion in the first half of next year.
The project timeline was accelerated by about three months to align with the Biden administration's IRA incentives.
The IRA incentives provide substantial benefits for domestically produced electric vehicles.
Once fully operational, HMGMA is expected to significantly boost Hyundai's market share in the US.
Production of the 2025 Ioniq 5 in the US will make it eligible for the full $7,500 IRA subsidy.
Currently, Hyundai does not qualify for the US government's IRA subsidy of up to $7,500 for electric vehicles.
To compensate for not qualifying for the IRA subsidy, Hyundai offers $7,500 in cash incentives to customers purchasing the 2024 Ioniq 5.
According to the Korea Automobile & Mobility Association, Hyundai and Kia collectively captured an 11.2 percent share of the US EV market from January to May this year.
Hyundai and Kia ranked second behind Tesla in the US EV market with sales of 48,838 units out of 437,246 total electric passenger vehicles sold.
Beyond the Ioniq 5, Hyundai Motor Group plans to produce six electric vehicles under the Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis brands at the HMGMA facility.
The company intends to manufacture hybrid models at the plant due to growing demand for hybrid vehicles in the US.
Hyundai Motor Group has invested $7.59 billion in the HMGMA project.
The project is creating 8,500 jobs.
source_title: Hyundai to start Georgia EV plant, unlocking IRA benefits - The Korea Herald

entity_name: Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia
canonical_name: Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia
facts: Governor Brian Kemp and Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America President and CEO Tony Heo joined state and local leaders on November 5 to celebrate the grand opening of the Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia.
The training center is an 89,000-square-foot state-of-the-art facility designed to train the next generation of electric vehicle (EV) manufacturing professionals.
It is strategically located adjacent to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) in Bryan County.
This marks the first training center in Georgia geared toward EVs and sets a new standard for workforce development.
HMGMA has announced an expansion that will include hybrid vehicles, and the facility is designed to accommodate training for those vehicles in the future.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that the Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia will give thousands of people over the years the knowledge they need to benefit from this generational project.
José Muñoz, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, said the center represents a commitment to investing in people as they continue to create high-paying American jobs.
As HMGMA expands production at one of the most advanced facilities in the world, it needs a workforce equipped with skills to build next-generation vehicles with next-generation production processes.
Hyundai's partnership with Georgia Quick Start ensures Meta Pros have access to world-class training that will help them succeed and help Georgia lead in advanced manufacturing.
A special thank you was given to Governor Kemp, Commissioner Pat Wilson, and the economic development team in Georgia for their ongoing support.
Hyundai's project in Georgia will create approximately 8,500 jobs by 2031.
At full capacity, the new facility can train up to 824 individuals at a time, providing customized, immersive education tailored to Hyundai's advanced EV manufacturing processes.
The center is operated by Georgia Quick Start, the State's renowned workforce development program and part of the Technical College System of Georgia (TCSG).
The center will prepare employees for roles at HMGMA and affiliate companies.
Greg Dozier, Commissioner of TCSG, said the center reflects Georgia's commitment to innovation, accessibility, and long-term economic opportunity.
Quick Start's training professionals collaborated with HMGMA to design and build world-class infrastructure that closely mirrors the processes trainees will encounter at the Metaplant.
The facility includes a STEP Line (Safe Transition for Efficient Production) where trainees build timing, awareness, and coordination skills in a controlled, digital environment.

It includes an IMPACT Line (Immersive Manufacturing Process and Conveyor Training), a full-length, moving production line with 53 integrated assembly activities and a built-in quality check system replicating real-world EV production.
It features a High Voltage Battery and Safety Lab for hands-on learning with high-voltage systems critical to EV and hybrid vehicle performance.
It includes a VR Experience Lab, a simulated environment for safety and hazard identification.
It has an Automation & Mechatronics Lab focused on programmable logic controllers (PLCs), sensors, motion systems, and industrial automation.
Quick Start Deputy Commissioner Scott McMurray said subject matter experts partnered directly with HMGMA to learn the skills its Meta Pros will need when they hit the production floor.

Additional facilities include a 5,764-square-foot multipurpose "Momentum Room," six flexible classrooms, a robotics lab, welding lab, paint lab, and a robotic dog that supports advanced automation training.
The training center stands as a core pillar of Georgia's broader strategy to prepare and train the state's workforce for success in advanced manufacturing.
This launch reaffirms Georgia's leadership in the global mobility and EV manufacturing sectors while delivering unmatched value to communities across the state.
Tony Heo said the grand opening marks a proud milestone for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, the State of Georgia, the Technical College System, and Georgia Quick Start.
Heo stated the training center gives confidence that trained talent will be needed to shape the future of mobility and American manufacturing in Southeast Georgia.
Georgia Quick Start is a program within TCSG that offers customized workforce training to qualified companies as a discretionary incentive to support investment and job creation in the state.
Georgia Quick Start is the oldest workforce training program in the United States, as well as the most awarded by site selection consultants.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) is Hyundai Motor Group's first dedicated Electrified Vehicle mass-production plant located in Bryan County, GA.
The HMGMA plant boasts a highly connected, automated, and flexible manufacturing system that organically connects all elements of the EV ecosystem to realize customer value.
The Georgia facility will become an intelligent manufacturing plant where all processes of production are optimized utilizing AI and data.
The innovative manufacturing system helps create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers.
source_title: Georgia Hosts Grand Opening for Hyundai Mobility Training Center

facts: Rivian Automotive has broken ground on its long-planned electric-vehicle factory in Georgia.
The project is valued at $5 billion.
The project is supported by one of the state’s largest incentive packages and a multibillion-dollar federal loan designed to anchor EV manufacturing in the Southeast.
The company and state leaders held a groundbreaking ceremony on Sept. 16 at the Stanton Springs North site.
The site is located about 45 miles east of Atlanta.
The ceremony marked the official start of site development.
Construction is expected to mobilize in 2026.
Vehicle production is expected to begin in 2028, according to the automaker.
The facility will be completed in two phases.
The facility will span roughly 9 million sq ft.
The production capacity aims to top 400,000 vehicles annually.
Rivian says its R2 and R3 crossover models will anchor production at the plant.
Rivian founder and CEO RJ Scaringe said at the groundbreaking that they are excited to build their next plant in Georgia and inspired by the state’s innovative spirit.
Scaringe stated the Georgia facility will support global expansion and provide the scale necessary to get millions of future drivers in all-electric vehicles in the United States and overseas.
Georgia officials estimate the combined value of state and local incentives at about $1.5 billion.
The Economic Development Agreement was signed by Rivian and the state in May 2022.
The agreement outlines statutory tax credits, property tax abatements, workforce training, land and infrastructure improvements, and discretionary “speed-to-market” spending.
Georgia Dept. of Economic Development (GDED) Commissioner Pat Wilson said approximately one-quarter of the total incentive package comes in the form of state incentives.
Wilson added that the final 20% of the estimated value is delivered through site work such as wetlands mitigation, a 500-acre pad ready for development, and road improvements.
The state legislature appropriated $112.6 million for land acquisition and site preparation.
Site preparation includes a new interchange on Interstate 20, a rail spur, and a Georgia Quick Start training center.
Rivian must invest $5 billion to qualify for the full incentives.
Rivian must employ 7,500 workers at an average salary of $56,000 by the end of 2028 to qualify for the full incentives.
The agreement requires Rivian to maintain those employment and investment levels through 2047.
If Rivian’s performance drops below 80% of the commitments, clawbacks require pro-rata repayment of incentives.
The deal includes a payments-in-lieu-of-taxes schedule beginning with $1.5 million annually from 2023 through 2028.
Payments will rise to $12 million in year seven.
Estimated revenues from the schedule are more than $900 million over 25 years to benefit local schools and governments.
The U.S. Dept. of Energy Loan Programs Office announced in January it had closed on a loan of up to $6.57 billion to Rivian New Horizon LLC.
Rivian New Horizon LLC is the entity building the Georgia plant.
