entity_name: Federal Clean Vehicle and Charging Infrastructure Tax Credits
canonical_name: Federal Clean Vehicle and Charging Infrastructure Tax Credits
facts: Federal tax credits were available to consumers, fleets, businesses, and tax-exempt entities that acquired new, used, and commercial clean vehicles before September 30, 2025.
Credits were also available for alternative fuel refueling infrastructure placed in service before June 30, 2026.
As of July 5, 2025, clean vehicle tax credits were available for vehicles acquired before September 30, 2025.
Taxpayers purchasing an eligible vehicle may qualify for a tax credit of up to $7,500 for qualified new vehicles and up to $4,000 for qualified pre-owned vehicles.
Eligibility for the clean vehicle tax credit is based on income and vehicle requirements.
Buyers of pre-owned clean vehicles must obtain a copy of the vehicle history report during the purchase and keep it for their records.
Starting Jan. 1, 2024, purchasers may lower a vehicle’s purchase price by transferring the clean vehicle tax credit to a registered dealer at the time of sale.
The credit transfer mechanism is available at over 9,500 registered dealers rather than waiting to claim it on next year’s tax return.
Commercial fleets and tax-exempt organizations buying qualified commercial clean vehicles may qualify for a credit per vehicle.
The maximum commercial credit is $7,500 for vehicles with gross vehicle weight ratings under 14,000 pounds and $40,000 for all other vehicles.

The commercial credit equals the lesser of: 15% of basis for plug-in hybrid vehicles or 30% of basis for full battery or fuel cell EVs, or the incremental cost depending on the year placed in service.
There is no limit on the number of commercial clean vehicle credits an organization can claim.

IRS Notices 2023-9 and 2024-5 created a safe harbor allowing eligible entities to identify tax credit values for commercial clean vehicles acquired in 2023 and 2024 using a U.S. Department of Energy incremental cost analysis.
The form to claim the commercial clean vehicle tax credit was under development at the time of publication.
For the first time, tax-exempt entities can qualify to receive direct payment (elective pay) for a federal clean vehicle tax credit if they meet requirements and register with the IRS.
Dealers and sellers must register with the IRS using the Energy Credits Online tool and report clean vehicle sales to be eligible for credits.
Eligible clean vehicles include all-electric and plug-in hybrid EVs under 14,000 pounds GVWR with at least 7 kilowatt hours battery capacity, as well as fuel cell EVs.
Dealers can identify specific vehicle eligibility by entering the VIN into the dealer portal webpage.
Dealers must provide purchasers with a copy of the accepted seller report (time of sale report) documenting vehicle eligibility.
IRS Revenue Procedure 2024-26 Section 6 provides rules for updating seller reports in cases of errors, cancelled sales, or vehicle returns.
Transferring the tax credit at sale allows customers to immediately save up to $7,500 per new vehicle and up to $4,000 per pre-owned vehicle.
Eligible customers transfer their credit to the dealer so the dealer provides a reduced purchase price.
Dealers submit transfer information electronically via the Energy Credits Online tool and receive an advance payment for the credit value.
The IRS expects to issue advance payments to dealers within 72 hours of when the customer elects to transfer the credit.
EV manufacturers must enter a written agreement with the IRS and report information for each vehicle to qualify for clean vehicle tax credits.
Manufacturers can submit documentation demonstrating compliance for upfront review by the IRS per Revenue Procedure 2024-26.
Dealers cannot sell vehicles for a clean vehicle credit until qualified manufacturers have submitted the VIN and the vehicle is determined eligible.
Fuel cell EVs are not required to be made by a qualified manufacturer, but doing so makes it easier for consumers to claim credits.
As of July 5, 2025, the alternative fuel infrastructure tax credit is available for property placed in service by June 30, 2026.
Home EV charging equipment purchases may be eligible for a 30% tax credit up to $1,000 covering the port, essential components/parts, and installation labor.
A credit in the same amount is available for energy storage associated with the home charger.
Home charging eligibility requires the installation location to be in an eligible census tract.

Businesses, fleets, or tax-exempt entities purchasing EV charging equipment may be eligible for a 6% tax credit of the cost starting Jan. 1, 2023, with a maximum of $100,000 per single item of property.

A single item of property is defined as each charging port or energy storage property, and the credit also applies to fueling dispensers and storage for hydrogen, natural gas, propane, E85, or B20 biodiesel blends.
Projects meeting prevailing wage and apprenticeship requirements may be eligible for a 30% tax credit instead of 6%.
Tax-exempt entities can qualify to receive full payment for the federal alternative fuel infrastructure tax credit via direct pay starting Jan. 1, 2023.
Tax-exempt entities may obtain the credit indirectly by having sellers or installers treated as the taxpayer and disclosing the amount to negotiate a lower upfront cost.
Tax-exempt entities seeking technical assistance for EV and zero emission vehicle infrastructure can refer to the Joint Office of Energy and Transportation Technical Assistance resources.
The page notes that summaries are not formal IRS guidance and taxpayers should consult professionals regarding eligibility.

facts: Honda Motor Co., Ltd. has resolved to acquire an additional 21% equity interest in Astemo, Ltd. from Hitachi, Ltd.
The acquisition will make Astemo a consolidated subsidiary of Honda.

Astemo was formed in January 2021 through the management integration of Hitachi Automotive Systems, Ltd., and three Honda subsidiaries: Keihin Corporation, Showa Corporation, and Nissin Kogyo Co., Ltd.
In October 2023, JIC Capital, Ltd. joined as a new joint partner, establishing a shareholding structure of 40% for Honda, 40% for Hitachi, and 20% for JICC.
Astemo is leveraging strong software development capabilities and accelerating investment in advanced technology areas to achieve sustainable growth as a global mega supplier.

The automotive industry is expected to continue an accelerated shift toward software-defined vehicles (SDVs), where vehicle value relies more on software technologies for autonomous driving and over-the-air updates than primarily hardware technologies.
Honda aims to enhance its SDV development capabilities and cost competitiveness by leading Astemo's transformation toward high-speed, high-efficiency AI and software technology development.
Post-acquisition, the shareholding structure will change to 61% for Honda, 19% for Hitachi, and 20% for JICC.
Honda plans to maintain consideration for an IPO of Astemo while leading its growth as a global supplier.
Astemo's business includes the development, manufacture, sales, and services of automotive components, transportation related components, industrial machines, and systems.
Astemo is located at Shin-Otemachi Building, 2-1, Otemachi 2-chome, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo.
Kohei Takeuchi serves as the Representative Director, President & CEO of Astemo.
Astemo's stated capital was 129.1 billion JPY as of March 31, 2025.
Astemo was established on July 1, 2009.
Prior to the transaction, Honda held 40% voting rights, equivalent to 425,916 shares of Astemo's common stock.
Astemo's consolidated sales revenue was 1,920,067 million yen for the fiscal year ended March 2023, 2,190,332 million yen for March 2024, and 2,186,537 million yen for March 2025.
Astemo's consolidated operating profit was 7,320 million yen for the fiscal year ended March 2023, 67,293 million yen for March 2024, and 67,400 million yen for March 2025.

Hitachi, Ltd. is the counterparty to the share acquisition and develops, produces, sells, and provides services for products related to digital systems & services, energy, mobility, and connective industries.
Hitachi's stated capital was 464,384 million yen as of March 31, 2025.
The total acquisition price is 152.3 billion yen, determined through analysis by independent financial advisor Nomura Securities Co., Ltd. and subsequent negotiations.
Honda will acquire 223,605 shares, representing 223,605 voting rights units.
Post-acquisition, Honda will hold 61% voting rights, equivalent to 649,521 shares of Astemo.
The agreement was signed on December 16, 2025.
The share transfer execution date is scheduled for the first quarter of the fiscal year ending March 31, 2027, subject to competition law procedures.
Toshihiro Mibe serves as Director, President and Representative Executive Officer of Honda Motor Co., Ltd.
Koji Ito is the General Manager of the Finance Division at Honda.
Masao Kawaguchi is the Head of Accounting and Finance Unit at Honda.
source_title: Form 6-K

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Office of Isotope R&D and Production (IRP), within the Office of Science, announced the successful establishment of domestic production capability for Iridium-192 at Oak Ridge National Laboratory on June 25, 2026.

Just over two years after announcing a joint product development agreement with QSA Global Inc., the collaboration produced the first domestically manufactured, high specific activity Ir-192 discs and is now in routine production.
Irradiated Ir-192 discs were shipped to QSA Global who fabricated them into commercial sealed sources for use in various industries across the U.S.

Establishing domestic production of critical isotopes is central to stabilizing the U.S. isotope market and securing long-term supply chain resilience for critical sectors such as manufacturing and energy systems.
The establishment of Ir-192 production marks a major milestone in reversing decades of foreign dependence and advancing U.S. leadership in isotope capability.
Ir-192 is indispensable for enabling non-destructive testing of structural integrity in metal components such as castings, forgings, and welds, in a process called industrial gamma radiography.
Structural integrity testing using Ir-192 is foundational to sectors including shipbuilding, automotive manufacturing, aerospace, and energy.
The radioisotope also supports on-site inspection of oil and gas pipelines, ensuring the safe and efficient operation of critical energy infrastructure and reinforcing national defense readiness.
This milestone represents the successful execution of a strategic effort launched to address a critical supply gap.
For more than two decades, the United States relied on foreign sources for Ir-192 following the cessation of domestic production.
The 2023 agreement between DOE IRP and QSA Global was designed to reverse that dependency.

The partnership leverages ORNL’s High Flux Isotope Reactor (a DOE Office of Science User Facility) and radioisotope production expertise alongside QSA Global’s manufacturing and commercialization capabilities.
Together, the partners rapidly advanced target design, irradiation processes, and transportation protocols to enable deployment for high-priority domestic applications.
Christopher Landers, Director, DOE IRP, stated that the achievement demonstrates the power of coordinated federal leadership and industry partnership to deliver critical capabilities at speed.

Landers noted that by restoring a domestic supply of Ir-192, they are strengthening U.S. supply chain resilience, reducing reliance on foreign sources, and ensuring industrial and energy sectors have access to the materials they need to operate safely and competitively.
The establishment directly advances IRP’s mission to foster a reliable, secure, and innovative domestic isotope supply through strategic partnerships and enhanced production capabilities.

It reflects a broader commitment to stabilizing the U.S. isotope market and securing long-term domestic independence in supply, key steps toward positioning the United States as the global leader in isotope science and production.
Jake Bourn, General Manager at QSA Global, Inc., stated that securing a domestic supply of Ir-192 is critical for their industry and nation.
Bourn highlighted that at a time of international supply volatility, their collaboration with the U.S. DOE and ORNL empowers QSA Global to deliver with unmatched reliability.
Bourn expressed honor in bridging the gap between government innovation and frontline technicians working to protect America's energy infrastructure.
Through continued collaboration with industry and the National Laboratory system, DOE IRP remains committed to strengthening the nation’s isotope enterprise.
The initiative ensures that critical materials like Ir-192 are available when and where needed to support economic competitiveness, infrastructure resilience, and national security.
source_title: DOE Strengthens Domestic Supply Chain for American Energy and Manufacturing | Department of Energy

facts: The Hollingsworth Companies completed a 108,960 SF facility at SouthPoint Business Park in Huntsville, AL.
The company leased and expanded a 174,000 SF facility to 195,000 SF at SouthPoint Business Park in Huntsville, AL.
SouthPoint Business Park in Huntsville has grown to 12 buildings with over 2,000,000 SF under roof and is fully leased.
Tenants in the park include automotive suppliers, defense contractors, building materials companies, and marine and beverage distributors.
Brooks Kracke, President and CEO of the North Alabama Industrial Development Association, stated that a balanced mix of industries in the park has provided stability to the regional labor market.
Brooks Kracke noted that the park mirrors regional growth over the last ten years fueled by major projects from Defense Contractors, Automotive Suppliers, and support industries.

Joe Hollingsworth, CEO of The Hollingsworth Companies, stated he is very bullish on the North Alabama market and believes American manufacturing will continue to prosper with the Southeastern United States leading growth.
Joe Hollingsworth thanked the community for investing time, effort, and money as partners in making the park successful, predicting the next 10 years will be the best economic period of their lives.
SouthPoint Industrial Park is located adjacent to both I-65 and I-565, providing access to markets from Nashville TN and Louisville KY to the north and Birmingham and Montgomery AL to the south.
The corridor surrounding SouthPoint Industrial Park is highly favored by companies that serve the Southern Automotive market.
North Alabama is home to the Army Contracting Command, NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center, and the Missile and Space Intelligence Center.
Recently opened manufacturing sites in the North Alabama area include Mazda/Toyota, Polaris, GE Aviation, Dynetics, Blue Origin, and Space X.
The surrounding area has a population of almost 700,000 that includes a well seasoned, high tech work force, posturing it for advanced manufacturing growth and distribution.
The Hollingsworth Companies is the largest non-urban Industrial Real Estate developer and construction firm in the Southeastern United States.
The Hollingsworth Companies manages 125 tenants across 18 million square feet of industrial space spread over 17 states.
The Hollingsworth Companies has facilities located in Alabama, Georgia, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Virginia.

source_title: The Hollingsworth Companies Continues Industrial Expansion

facts: Eastman Chemical Company's headquarters and largest manufacturing facility are located in Kingsport, Tennessee.
Eastman expects its methanolysis manufacturing plant in Kingsport, Tennessee to begin commissioning and startup activities by early summer 2023.
Approximately 75 percent of the required feedstock for the Kingsport, Tennessee methanolysis manufacturing plant is being procured under contract or in active negotiation.

Solar-absorbing Saflex™ PVB interlayer solutions, including the Saflex™ S series, Saflex™ Solar Connect, and XIR™ Solar Control Technology, are ideal for electric vehicle ("EV") glass in cabin-forward designs and larger sunroofs to reduce air-conditioning loads and maximize EV driving range.

Eastman's Saflex™ Horizon is a next-generation polyvinyl butyral ("PVB") interlayer product that supports advanced Head-up Displays ("HUD") systems with longer virtual image distance, expanded field of view, and augmented reality features.
Eastman produces performance films innovations for automotive window films and paint protection films.
Saflex™ PVB and HUD acoustic interlayers in the automotive sector are essential to ensure passenger safety and enable the adoption of digital technologies within the cabin.
Georgia DOT provides transportation permits to contractors, consultants, truck operators and businesses.
Georgia’s National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Program (NEVI) is derived from the 2021 Bipartisan Infrastructure Law enacted as the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA).
The Connected Vehicle (CV) Program includes a Vehicle to Everything (V2X) Roadmap Program that aims to link road users, vehicles, and infrastructure.
The Carbon Reduction Program (CRP) provides funding to states for projects that result in transportation emission reductions.

GDOT permit programs include Access Management, Outdoor Advertising, Oversize Permits, Utility Permits, Vegetation Management, GPAS, Movie & TV Production, LOGO Sign Program, Memorial Markers, Red Light Running Photo, Automated Traffic Enforcement Safety Device (ATESD), and Automated License Plate Readers (ALPR).

facts: Honda Motor Co., Ltd. completed procedures on January 13, 2023, to establish a joint venture with LG Energy Solution for EV battery production in the U.S., as previously announced on August 29, 2022.
The new joint venture will operate under the tentative name L-H Battery Company, Inc.
The JV is located in Jeffersonville, Ohio, U.S.A., approximately 40 miles southwest of Columbus in Fayette County.
Initial capital for the JV is $210 million USD, with a plan for incremental investment to about $3.5 billion USD.
Ownership split is 51% LG Energy Solution and 49% Honda Motor Co., Ltd.
The JV will begin construction of a new battery plant early in 2023, with a goal of completion by the end of 2024.
Mass production of advanced lithium-ion battery cells is scheduled to start by the end of 2025.
The new plant aims for an annual production capacity of approximately 40GWh.
LG Energy Solution and Honda have committed to creating 2,200 jobs at the new production facility.
The companies’ overall investment related to the JV is projected to reach $4.4 billion.
All batteries produced by the JV will be supplied exclusively to Honda plants in North America to power battery-electric vehicles sold in North America.
Robert H. Lee serves as Director and CEO of the JV, currently holding the title of Executive Vice President and North America Regional Group Leader at LGES.
Rick Riggle serves as Director and COO of the JV, previously Plant Lead of Honda's Anna Engine Plant.
LGES board members appointed to the JV include Hyung Sik Kim, Se Joong Oh, and Jae Wook Jung.
Honda board members appointed to the JV include Kensuke Oe and Arata Ichinose.
Robert H. Lee stated that LG Energy Solution possesses financial stability, quality, competitiveness, and production capacity with global operational expertise to ensure the JV's success.
Lee added that combining both companies' expertise will provide top-quality batteries for Honda EVs in North America and create high-value jobs in Ohio.

Rick Riggle noted that establishing the JV in Ohio is a major step toward an electrified future and expressed excitement about partnering with LGES and building community relationships in Fayette County.
The JV is accounted for using the equity method as an affiliate of Honda and is not anticipated to materially impact Honda's consolidated financial results for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2023.
LG Energy Solution is a split-off from LG Chem and holds over 25,000 battery-related patents globally.
LGES aims to achieve carbon neutral operations by 2050 and maintains battery manufacturing JV facilities with General Motors, Stellantis N.V., Hyundai Motor Group, and Honda.
Honda Motor Co., Ltd. delivers over 30 million products annually across automobiles, motorcycles, power products, and aviation lines.
Honda operates more than 60 manufacturing plants in 27 countries, employing approximately 220,000 associates globally.
Honda aims to achieve carbon neutrality for all products and corporate activities by 2050, alongside zero traffic collision fatalities involving its vehicles.

facts: In January 2025, the U.S. Department of Energy’s Loan Programs Office announced the closing of a $6.57 billion loan to Rivian New Horizon, LLC.
The loan consists of $5.975 billion of principal and $592 million of capitalized interest.
The loan finances the development and construction of Project Horizon, an electric vehicle manufacturing facility located in Stanton Springs North, near the City of Social Circle, Georgia.
The project expects to support up to 2,000 full-time jobs through construction.
The project expects to support 7,500 operations jobs by 2030.
The loan supports construction of a nine million square foot facility.
The facility will manufacture up to 400,000 mass-market electric sport utility vehicles (SUVs) and crossover vehicles.
At full capacity, the EVs manufactured at the facility are expected to yield an annual fuel consumption savings of approximately 146 million gallons of petroleum.
As outlined in its Community Benefit Plan, Rivian intends to hire at least a quarter of its employees at the Stanton Springs facility from the local community.
Rivian is working alongside the local technical college system to create training programs to attract, train, and retain the required workforce.

This includes working with the State of Georgia’s QuickStart technical training incentive program, which provides customized workforce training to companies making job-creating investments in the state.
Rivian currently has approximately twenty apprentices local to Georgia who have completed their initial round of education.
These apprentices are currently receiving job training at Rivian's Illinois plant.
Upon completion of the program, the apprentices will return to Georgia to work in the facility once it is operational.
Rivian is exploring expanding participation in this program to other career paths.
The project owners are Rivian New Horizon, LLC.
The facility location is listed as Stanton Springs North, Georgia.
The loan program is ATVM.
The loan type is a Direct Loan.
The guaranteed loan amount is $6.57 billion.
The issuance date is January 2025.
The permanent U.S. jobs supported are estimated at 7,500.
The loan amount is approximate and includes principal and any capitalized interest as of January 2025.
The job support estimate was made at the time of closing.
source_title: Project Horizon | Department of Energy

facts: Project Horizon will manufacture mid-sized electric SUVs in Georgia that are price competitive with gas-powered equivalents.
The Department of Energy’s Loan Programs Office (LPO) announced the closing of a $6.57 billion loan to Rivian New Horizon, LLC on January 16, 2025.
The loan consists of $5.975 billion of principal and $592 million of capitalized interest.
The loan finances the development and construction of Project Horizon, an electric vehicle (EV) manufacturing facility located in Stanton Springs North, near the City of Social Circle, Georgia.
The project is owned by a subsidiary of Rivian Automotive, Inc.
The project is supported by an equity investment from Rivian Automotive, Inc.
Rivian submitted its application to LPO in October 2022.
The loan will support construction of a nine million square foot facility.
The facility will manufacture up to 400,000 mass-market electric sport utility vehicles (SUVs) and crossover vehicles annually.
The U.S. EV market saw a 12% increase in sales in the final quarter of 2024.
At its Georgia facility, Rivian will produce its all-electric midsize platform (MSP) with its R2 and R3 models as the first variants in production.
Rivian currently produces its three-row electric SUV, the R1S, and its electric pickup truck, the R1T, at its Normal, Illinois, facility.
Rivian also produces commercial vans at its Normal, Illinois, facility.
At full capacity, the EVs manufactured at the Georgia facility are expected to yield an annual fuel savings of approximately 146 million gallons of petroleum.
The loan is made available through LPO’s Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing (ATVM) Loan Program.
The ATVM Loan Program provides loans to support U.S. manufacturing of advanced technology vehicles, qualifying components, and materials that improve fuel economy.
This loan marks the first ATVM loan under the Biden-Harris Administration to produce advanced technology vehicles (ATVs), rather than ATV components.
The project expects to support up to 2,000 full-time jobs through construction.
The project expects to support 7,500 operations jobs through 2030.
Nearly 16 million jobs have been created since President Biden and Vice President Harris took office.
LPO borrowers are required to develop and ultimately implement a comprehensive Community Benefits Plan (CBP).
CBPs ensure borrowers meaningfully engage with communities and labor groups to create good-paying jobs and improve the well-being of residents and workers.
As outlined in its CBP, Rivian intends to hire at least a quarter of its employees at the Stanton Springs facility from the local community.

This investment supports the Biden-Harris Administration’s Justice40 Initiative, which sets a goal that 40% of the overall benefits of certain federal investment in climate, clean energy, and other areas flow to disadvantaged communities.
Rivian is working alongside the local technical college system to create training programs to attract, train, and retain the required workforce.

This includes working with the State of Georgia’s QuickStart technical training incentive program, which provides customized workforce training to companies making job-creating investments in the state.
Rivian has hired approximately twenty fellows local to Georgia who have completed their initial round of education.
These fellows are currently receiving job training at Rivian's Illinois plant.
Upon completion of the program, the team will return to Georgia to work in the facility once it is operational.
Rivian is exploring expanding participation in this program to other career paths.
Across all LPO’s programs, DOE has attracted 182 applications for projects across the country totaling over $278.9 billion in requested loans and loan guarantees as of December 2024.
source_title: DOE Announces $6.57 Billion Loan to Rivian to Support the Construction of EV Manufacturing Facility in Georgia | Department of Energy

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America is the key pillar of the Group's $12.6 billion investment in Georgia.
It is the largest economic development project in Georgia's history.
The vehicle assembly and battery plant will produce up to 500,000 electric and hybrid vehicles annually for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis brands.
Hyundai Motor Group commits to an additional $21 billion investment from 2025 to 2028 to drive U.S. manufacturing growth.
Since entering the U.S., Hyundai Motor Group has invested $20.5 billion in the U.S., creating directly and indirectly more than 570,000 American jobs.
HMGMA hosted its Grand Opening celebration on March 26, 2025.
The ceremony marks the completion of the project just two and a half years after breaking ground.
Euisun Chung serves as Executive Chair of Hyundai Motor Group.
Governor Brian P. Kemp attended the grand opening event.
U.S. Representative Buddy Carter attended the grand opening event.
Jaehoon Chang serves as Vice Chair of Hyundai Motor Group.
José Muñoz is President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company.
Ho Sung Song is President and CEO of Kia Corporation.
Governor Kemp highlighted collaborations between HMGMA and Georgia's universities and technical colleges, including Georgia Quick Start's on-site training facility.
HMG entered a landmark agreement with the State of Georgia in May 2022 to establish the facility in Ellabell, Georgia.
The site is located 20 miles outside of historic Savannah.
HMGMA was officially formed by June 2022.
Groundbreaking occurred within four months of the company's formation.
On October 3, 2024, the first vehicle – a Hyundai IONIQ 5 – rolled off the assembly line.
Hyundai's IONIQ 9 three-row electric SUV is now in production at HMGMA.
The first Kia model to be built at the facility will join production in 2026.
The plant initially has capacity to produce 300,000 vehicles annually.
Investment in Georgia related to HMGMA and joint battery ventures with LG Energy Solution and SK On totals $12.6 billion.
The project includes a total of 8,500 jobs at HMGMA by 2031.
Total investments in Georgia are expected to create nearly 40,000 direct and indirect jobs.
Expected $4.6 billion in individual earnings every year according to the Center for Automotive Research.
As of the end of 2024, more than $2.5 billion in capital investment and approximately 6,900 jobs have been announced by HMGMA suppliers in 12 counties across the state.
Supplier investments more than double the additional $1 billion investment originally anticipated.
The Group announced a significant investment of $21 billion in the U.S. from 2025 to 2028.
$9 billion will be invested to establish an annual production capacity in the U.S. of 1.2 million vehicles.
The Group plans to expand HMGMA's annual production capacity to a total of 500,000 units, an increase of 200,000 units from the original plan.
Existing U.S. production sites – Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama and Kia Autoland Georgia – will also see improvements in production facilities.
$6 billion will be allocated to enhance the Group's parts and logistics business.
The Group is establishing an Electric Arc Furnace-based integrated steel mill in the U.S. through affiliate Hyundai Steel, with an annual steel production capacity of 2.7 million tons.
The mill will supply high-quality automotive steel plates to HMGMA when commercial steel production begins in 2029.

The Group will invest an additional $6 billion to drive innovation and expand strategic partnerships with U.S. companies in autonomous driving, robotics, artificial intelligence (AI), and advanced air mobility (AAM).
Through the $21 billion commitment, the Group anticipates creating 14,000 new direct full-time jobs in the U.S. by 2028.
The overall economic impact is expected to generate more than 100,000 direct and indirect job opportunities across related industries.
HMGMA's architecture blends advanced technology with design elements reflecting Hyundai's brand identity and the natural beauty of the Savannah area.
HMGMA integrated proof-of-concept manufacturing technologies from Hyundai Motor Group Innovation Center Singapore (HMGICS).
All processes including order collection, procurement, logistics, and production are optimized using AI and real-time data.
Cutting-edge robotics and vision systems ensure high-quality vehicle production.
Automation in HMGMA's assembly shop is nearly double that of the Group's existing facilities.
The facility uses automated guided vehicles for logistics, Boston Dynamic's Spot for exterior quality inspection in the weld shop, and parking robots in the assembly shop.
Oscar Kwon serves as CEO of HMGMA.
Employees at the plant are known as "Meta Pros".
A solar parking lot at the front of the site will generate 5.2 megawatts of energy, accounting for roughly five percent of the Metaplant's total power consumption.
Solar panels are installed above 1,878 parking spaces to provide shade and produce electricity.
A 41-acre ecological park with trails, running tracks, sports fields, and picnic areas is under construction in front of the Metaplant for employees.
HMGMA deployed Hyundai Motor XCIENT heavy-duty hydrogen fuel-cell electric trucks for clean logistics operations.
A total of 21 XCIENT trucks are currently in operation at the facility.
These trucks represent more than a third of the Glovis America truck fleet at HMGMA.
The trucks transport vehicle parts from suppliers across the region to the 2,906-acre Megasite where HMGMA is located on a daily basis.
Since 2022, Hyundai and Genesis have donated nearly $4.5 million to local organizations in Georgia through social responsibility initiatives.
Kia's Autoland Georgia factory in West Point has provided over $22 million in support of Georgia communities since 2009.
Kia's support includes more than $6 million for educational programs such as the THINC College & Career Academy and SAE's "A World in Motion."
HMGMA is Hyundai Motor Group's first dedicated electrified vehicle mass-production plant located in Bryan County, GA.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America Celebrates Grand Opening, Powering U.S. Economic Growth

facts: Georgia Tech and Hyundai Motor Group signed a memorandum of understanding on Sept. 19, 2023.
The partnership will focus on sustainable mobility, hydrogen economy, workforce development, and making cities smarter and more sustainable.
Hyundai Motor Group is investing more than $12 billion in EV manufacturing and battery production in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group is investing $12.6 billion in new dedicated electric vehicle and battery manufacturing facilities in Georgia.
The investment includes the $7.59 billion Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) site in Bryan County, Georgia.
The HMGMA site will create more than 8,500 direct jobs.
The investment also includes a $5 billion battery manufacturing facility joint venture with SK On in Bartow County, Georgia.
The SK On joint venture facility will employ approximately 3,500 people.
The close proximity of these facilities to Georgia Tech was an important consideration in choosing the locations.
Hyundai's economic impact in Georgia will be tens of billions of dollars in the years to come.
Hyundai's investments in Georgia are expected to help create nearly 40,000 jobs.
A recent study by the Center for Automotive Research reveals that Hyundai Motor's automotive operations contributed $20.1 billion and 190,000 jobs to the U.S. economy in 2021 alone.
According to the study, Hyundai's investments in Georgia are expected to create nearly 40,000 jobs and $4.6 billion in individual earnings every year.
The Kia Georgia assembly plant began vehicle production in 2009.
The Kia plant was established under the leadership of Honorary Chairman Mong-Koo Chung and Executive Chair Euisun Chung.
University System of Georgia Chancellor Sonny Perdue was Governor of Georgia at the time of the Kia plant opening and attended today's ceremony.

Georgia Tech President Ángel Cabrera stated that Hyundai is a global brand synonymous with quality, innovation, and a commitment to advancing technology, and that the alignment of values between the organizations has become obvious.

Jay Chang, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, stated that Georgia is like a second home to Hyundai and that the partnership includes research, hydrogen economy, workforce development, and smart cities cooperation.
Jay Chang noted that this partnership is one of the reasons Hyundai chose Georgia for its EV investments.

José Muñoz, President and Global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and President and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, stated that Hyundai's investments will create tens of thousands of jobs and bring tens of billions of dollars in economic impact for decades to come.
José Muñoz noted that proximity to institutions like Georgia Tech was one of the many reasons Hyundai selected Georgia for its new EV manufacturing facility.
The partnership envisions zero emissions hydrogen-powered vehicles on campus, advanced air mobility shuttling to Hartsfield International Airport, and hands-free autonomous vehicles on I-75 and I-85.

Under the research and innovation element, Hyundai will collaborate with Georgia Tech on joint R&D programs focused on sustainable mobility, including battery technology and expanding the hydrogen economy.
Under workforce development, Georgia Tech and Hyundai will create technical training and leadership development programming for Hyundai employees.
The partnership will initiate engagement activities to stimulate interest in STEM degrees among students.
Under student engagement, Hyundai will actively participate in career recruitment events at Georgia Tech and create learning programs for undergrad and graduate students.
Hyundai's partnership includes field-naming recognition at Bobby Dodd Stadium, which is now known as Bobby Dodd Stadium at Hyundai Field.
The partnership provides a vital pathway for Georgia Tech to expand its stature as a leading research entity in automotive electrification through world-class hydrogen research.
The partnership offers an opportunity for Georgia Tech to contribute to Georgia's commitment as a hub for EV production.
The partnership will generate new revenue for Georgia Tech Athletics, providing resources for student-athletes and teams.
Georgia Tech conducts more than $1 billion in research annually for government, industry, and society.
Hyundai Motor Group has about 250,000 employees worldwide and its mobility brands include Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
source_title: Georgia Tech and Hyundai Motor Group Sign MoU for Future Mobility Collaboration

entity_name: Alternative Fuels Data Center Vehicle Registration Counts
canonical_name: Alternative Fuels Data Center (AFDC) Vehicle Registration Counts by State
facts: The page provides approximate light-duty vehicle registration counts derived by the National Laboratory of the Rockies with data from Experian Information Solutions.
Counts are rounded to the closest 100 vehicles and reflect the total number of light-duty registered vehicles through the selected year (2021).
Fuel types are based on vehicle identification numbers (VINs), which do not reflect aftermarket conversions to use different fuels or power sources.
Georgia had approximately 34,000 Electric (EV) vehicle registrations in 2021.
Georgia had approximately 13,600 Plug-In Hybrid Electric (PHEV) vehicle registrations in 2021.
Georgia had approximately 128,200 Hybrid Electric (HEV) vehicle registrations in 2021.
Georgia had approximately 70,200 Biodiesel vehicle registrations in 2021.
Georgia had approximately 745,200 Ethanol/Flex (E85) vehicle registrations in 2021.
Georgia had approximately 700 Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) vehicle registrations in 2021.
Georgia had approximately 300 Propane vehicle registrations in 2021.
Georgia had 0 Hydrogen and 0 Methanol vehicle registrations in 2021.
Georgia had approximately 8,123,100 Gasoline vehicle registrations in 2021.
Georgia had approximately 203,200 Diesel vehicle registrations in 2021.
Georgia had approximately 81,200 vehicles with unknown fuel types registered in 2021.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Vehicle Registration Counts by State

entity_name: Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle (FCEV) Tax Exemption
canonical_name: Washington State FCEV Tax Exemption Program
facts: Passenger vehicles, light-duty trucks, and medium-duty passenger FCEVs are exempt from state sales and use taxes.
The exemption includes used FCEVs and the first 650 purchases or leases of new FCEVs.
The maximum value amount eligible for the tax exemption for new vehicles is 50% of the cost, with no cap.
For used vehicles, the exemption is the lesser of $16,000 or the fair market value of the vehicle at the time of leasing.
The FCEV exemption may not be combined with the Retail Sales and Use Tax Exemption.
The exemption is set to expire on June 30, 2029, or in the month following the sale or lease of the first 650 new vehicles qualifying for this exemption, whichever comes first.
Caps apply to both vehicle sales and leases, with leases continuing to receive the exemption after the expiration date if they were initiated before the expiration.
The incentive applies in Washington State jurisdiction.
The policy was enacted on July 2, 2021.
The exemption references Revised Code of Washington 82.08.993 and 82.12.817.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle (FCEV) Tax Exemption

facts: The purpose of the Funding Opportunity Announcement is to support continued innovation in photovoltaic cell and module technologies.
It is anticipated that the PVRD funding opportunity will award $20,000,000 in Federal funding.
The funding will support 30 to 35 research projects advancing photovoltaic cell and module performance toward and beyond the $0.06/kWh 2020 SunShot goal.

Successful applicants must demonstrate a convincing ability to improve power conversion efficiency, fielded energy output, service lifetime, or manufacturability of commercial and emerging PV technologies.
The program includes multiyear research projects in Topic 2 and Topic 3.
Topic 1 is dedicated to small, single-year projects intended to demonstrate potential for expanded work, reviewed and selected on an accelerated schedule.
This funding program represents one aspect of the SunShot Initiative’s efforts to encourage widespread deployment of PV systems by achieving cost competitiveness with traditional energy sources.
Applicants are encouraged to form teams that leverage individual member strengths and demonstrate project timeliness and relevance to the present status of the industry.
A second upcoming PVRD webinar will cover Full Applications, the award negotiation process, and DOE's active program management processes.
The webinar is scheduled for Tuesday December 8 at 2:00 PM ET.

links.context: Registration link for the second upcoming PVRD webinar

source_title: DOE: CMEI eXCHANGE: Funding Opportunities

facts: Kia Georgia operates an automotive manufacturing facility located in West Point, GA.
The company is currently advertising four full-time on-site job openings in West Point, GA.
A Die Maintenance Team Member position is available for a night shift with one opening and included benefits.
The Die Maintenance Team Member role requires performing work to meet production and quality needs in an automotive manufacturing environment.
The Die Maintenance Team Member role involves performing maintenance on automotive transfer dies to improve dimensional and surface quality of stamping panels.
The Die Maintenance Team Member role supports production in the mass production of automobile panels.
The Die Maintenance Team Member role includes performing corrective and predictive maintenance tasks.
A Technical Development Specialist position is available for a day shift with one opening and included benefits.

The Technical Development Specialist role is responsible for developing and implementing engineering solutions and training programs that support technical operations, enhance equipment functionality, and improve production efficiency.
The Technical Development Specialist role requires conducting technical evaluations to identify equipment optimization opportunities.
The Technical Development Specialist role involves analyzing and troubleshooting production and training issues.
A Maintenance Engineering - PLC Night Shift position is available for a night shift with one opening.
The Maintenance Engineering - PLC Night Shift role is responsible for assisting group leaders and team members with repetitive and chronic equipment problems.
The Maintenance Engineering - PLC Night Shift role involves implementing and maintaining maintenance documents.
A Maintenance Engineering Specialist position is available for a day shift with one opening and included benefits.

The Maintenance Engineering Specialist role shares responsibilities with the PLC Night Shift role regarding assisting group leaders with shift-to-shift follow-up activities and establishing and implementing maintenance plans.

Preferred skills across the listed positions include critical thinking, judgment and decision making, active learning, classroom delivery, maintenance, PLC, PLC Allen Bradley, electrical work, SAP, AutoCAD, and large capital projects.
source_title: Kia Georgia | Manufacturing Georgia

entity_name: Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Credit (48C) Program
facts: The Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Credit (48C) was established by the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 and expanded with a $10 billion investment under the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022.
At least $4 billion of the total $10 billion will be allocated to projects in designated 48C energy communities, which includes communities with closed coal mines or coal plants.
48C provides an investment tax credit of up to 30% of qualified investments for certified qualifying advanced energy projects that meet prevailing wage and apprenticeship requirements.

On May 29, 2024, the IRS released allocations for the first round of the 48C program, with approximately $1.5 billion of the $4 billion in tax credit allocations supporting projects in designated 48C energy communities.

On January 10, 2025, the IRS released allocations for the second round of the 48C program, with roughly $2.5 billion of the $6 billion in tax credit allocations supporting projects in designated 48C energy communities.

ArcelorMittal Calvert LLC received a $280,500,000 credit allocation to build an advanced manufacturing facility in Calvert, Alabama, to produce high-quality non-grain oriented electrical steel (NOES) essential for electric vehicle motors and other clean energy technologies.
The ArcelorMittal Calvert project aims to fill a critical gap in the domestic supply chain, reduce dependency on imports, and support decarbonization of the automotive sector.

Epsilon Advanced Materials Inc. received a $115,500,000 credit allocation to establish a 30,000 MTPA (Phase 1) manufacturing facility in Leland, North Carolina, to produce sustainable, high performance synthetic graphite anode material for lithium-ion batteries.

e-VAC Magnetics LLC received a $111,990,000 credit allocation to advance its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Sumter, South Carolina, which is projected to create 300 new jobs with operations beginning in the fall of 2025.
e-VAC Magnetics produces rare earth neodymium-iron-boron (NdFeB) magnets that are critical to automotive, defense, industrial, and renewable energy applications.

LANXESS Corporation received a $1,124,095 credit allocation to expand its Charleston, South Carolina facility's phosphorus trichloride (PCl3) production capabilities to manufacture battery-grade PCl3, essential for producing a critical battery component in electric vehicle batteries and energy storage systems.

The LANXESS expansion will enhance existing workforce roles and support local workforce development through partnerships prioritizing prevailing wages, apprenticeship programs, and community engagement.

Moment Energy Inc. received a $6,000,000 credit allocation to build the first large-scale U.S. manufacturing facility in Vance, Alabama, to process Giga-Watt hours of electric vehicle (EV) batteries for stationary storage applications.

The Moment Energy facility will repurpose retired EV batteries with 80% life remaining after their initial use in EVs, ensuring battery materials are kept in the U.S. supply chain before being recycled domestically.
Moment Energy's initiative creates hundreds of well-paying jobs in the renewable industry and builds upon the success of its 15,000-square-foot concept facility certified by UL Solutions to UL 1974.

Mobis North America electrified Powertrain, LLC received a $57,666,890 credit allocation to construct three different electric vehicle component part plants in Richmond Hill, Georgia, which will provide parts for the production of Hyundai and Kia electric vehicles.

The awarded Mobis plant in Richmond Hill will specifically produce Power Electric Systems (PE System), integral to EV production, with a positive environmental and community impact to the United States economy.

NOVONIX Anode Materials LLC received a $103,063,889 credit allocation to fully equip its recently purchased and retrofitted industrial facility in Chattanooga, Tennessee, to produce high-performance synthetic graphite anode material at a capacity of 20,000 tonnes per annum.

The NOVONIX project marks the first commercially active large scale synthetic graphite facility dedicated to battery anode material in North America, significantly expanding U.S. production and reducing reliance on China for this key battery material.

Eaton Corporation received three credit allocations ($16,278,300 for Nacogdoches, TX; $9,000,000 for El Paso, TX; and $1,326,060 for Waukesha, WI) to expand production capacity of voltage regulators, low-voltage switchgear, circuit breakers, panelboards, and three-phase transformers.
Eaton's expanded equipment provides vital functionality to support electric grid resilience, renewable energy projects, and electric vehicle (EV) charging stations across the nation.
source_title: Applicant Self-Disclosed 48C Projects | Department of Energy

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy released details for 35 projects across 20 states that voluntarily shared they received a total of $1.93 billion in allocations of the Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Credit (48C).

The 48C credit is an allocated tax credit funded by President Biden’s Investing in America agenda through the Inflation Reduction Act to accelerate clean energy manufacturing and recycling and reduce greenhouse gas emissions at industrial facilities.

The announced projects address critical needs across grid components, electric vehicle components and chargers, solar components, clean steel, critical materials processing and recycling, and other clean energy products.
Seven of the announced projects are located in traditional energy communities, which include communities with closed coal mines or coal plants.
Projects receiving a 48C tax credit must meet prevailing wage and registered apprenticeship requirements to receive the full 30 percent investment tax credit.

U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm stated the administration is executing an industrial strategy prioritizing rebuilding the domestic manufacturing sector and creating good-paying jobs across the country.

U.S. Secretary of the Treasury Janet Yellen stated President Biden’s economic agenda ensures all communities benefit from clean energy growth by driving innovation and investment, strengthening energy security, and lowering energy costs for American families and small businesses.

John Podesta, Senior Advisor to the President for International Climate Policy, stated the announcement represents a direct, targeted investment in energy communities and manufacturing across the nation.

The Inflation Reduction Act expanded the Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Credit (48C) to provide an additional credit allocation of $10 billion, with $4 billion set aside for projects in designated energy communities.

On March 29, 2024, the IRS allocated approximately $4 billion of 48C credits for over 100 projects across 35 states, with approximately $1.5 billion allocated to projects in designated energy communities.
The 48C(e) program can release names and allocation amounts only after project certification, a process that can take up to two years.
As of April 18, 2024, 35 projects voluntarily self-disclosed information to the DOE to share publicly ahead of certification.

Highland Materials, Inc. in Surgoinsville, TN, located in an Energy Community, will initially produce 16,000 Metric Tons per year of solar grade polysilicon at less than standard cost with a 90% reduction in carbon emissions.
At full capacity, the Highland Materials project will produce 20,000 MT/year, equivalent to 11 GW of solar cells.

Highland Materials is working closely with Northeast State Community College to develop and implement workforce development including DOL certified apprenticeship programs and community support programs.
The Highland Materials project received a credit amount of $255.6 Million.
Mobis North America electrified Powertrain, LLC (MNAe) in Richmond Hill, GA, will construct three different electric vehicle component plants to provide parts for Hyundai and Kia electric vehicles.
The awarded MNAe plant in Richmond Hill will specifically produce Power Electric Systems (PE System), which are integral to the production of electric vehicles.
The MNAe project received a credit amount of $57.6 Million.
Eaton Corporation in Waukesha, WI, will increase manufacturing capacity for three-phase transformers used for utility power grids, data centers, and large commercial and industrial applications.
Eaton's equipment will provide critical support for electric grid resilience, renewable energy projects, and electric vehicle charging stations.
Eaton works closely with Waukesha County Technical College to create education and training programs for manufacturing careers, including coil winding.
The Eaton project received a credit amount of $1.33 Million.

Prysmian in Williamsport, PA, will reequip and expand an existing facility to increase manufacturing capacity of advanced transmission conductors, enabling production of thousands of metric tons per year.
Prysmian's conductors are expected to supply the Grain Belt Express project, a long-distance high-voltage direct-current transmission line delivering clean electricity to the Midwest.
The Prysmian project received a credit amount of $3.89 Million.
ArcelorMittal Calvert LLC in Calvert, AL, will build a new advanced manufacturing facility in an Energy Community to produce high-quality non-grain oriented electrical steel (NOES).
NOES is a critical material for production of electric motors used to power battery electric vehicles, plug-in hybrid electric vehicles, hybrid vehicles, and other clean energy technologies.
ArcelorMittal Calvert will partner with local educational and training institutions to develop tailored workforce development programs.
The ArcelorMittal Calvert project received a credit amount of $280.5 Million.

Nth Cycle Inc. in Fairfield, OH, will provide the nation’s first large-scale production of Mixed Hydroxide Precipitate (MHP) using recycled and scrap materials from Li-ion batteries, spent catalysts, and alloy scraps and slags.
The Nth Cycle process will provide critical nickel and cobalt materials for EV battery production.
The Nth Cycle project received a credit amount of $7.22 Million.

Middlesex County Utilities Authority in Sayreville, NJ, will increase the efficiency of a solid waste handling process of wastewater treatment through a new Advanced Anaerobic Digestion Facility, increasing renewable energy use and reducing emissions by approximately 23%.

The Middlesex County Utilities Authority construction contracts will include project labor agreements, support apprenticeships, and engage with businesses owned by minorities, women, veterans, and local small business owners.
The Middlesex County Utilities Authority project received a credit amount of $40.5 Million.
Topsoe SOEC Production US Inc. in Chester, VA, will significantly expand U.S. electrolyzer manufacturing capacity to meet growing demand for green hydrogen.
Electrolyzers are key for decarbonizing energy-intensive industries like steel, mining, and long-distance transportation, which account for approximately 30% of global greenhouse gas emissions.
The Topsoe SOEC Production US Inc. project received a credit amount of $135.9 Million.

Projects are partnering with training and education organizations, such as community colleges and career technical centers, to develop a skilled workforce for the clean energy and manufacturing sectors.
Community benefit agreements, project labor agreements, and impact agreements identifying specific benefits for workers and local communities are being utilized across the portfolio.
The U.S. Department of Treasury and Internal Revenue Service anticipate issuing guidance for the second round of the 48C program in the coming weeks.
A submission window for required concept papers for the second round is anticipated to open by summer.
Companies may submit an opt-in form to the DOE to be publicly listed as recipients of the 48C program.
The Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Credit (48C) is managed by the DOE’s Office of Manufacturing and Energy Supply Chains.
source_title: Biden-Harris Administration Announces First Projects Receiving Clean Energy Manufacturing Investments in America's Industrial and Energy Communities | Department of Energy

facts: EVs that are subject to the annual EV registration fee are exempt from the Alternative Fuels Tax in Pennsylvania.

The Pennsylvania Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) AFV Program offers rebates to assist eligible residents with the cost of purchasing or leasing new or qualifying pre-owned alternative fuel vehicles, including all-electric vehicles (EVs), plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs), compressed natural gas (CNG) vehicles, electric motorcycles, and propane vehicles.
Applicants for the DEP AFV rebate program must meet income eligibility requirements and the eligible AFV purchase price cannot exceed $45,000.
Rebate amounts under the DEP AFV program are $3,000 for new or pre-owned EVs, $1,500 for new or pre-owned PHEVs, and $500 for new or pre-owned CNG, propane, or electric motorcycles.
An additional rebate of $1,000 is available for all applicants that meet the low-income requirement as defined by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services.
DEP AFV rebate applications must be received within six months of vehicle purchase and are awarded on a first-come, first-served basis.
The Alternative Fuels Incentive Grant (AFIG) Program provides financial assistance for innovative, advanced fuel and vehicle technology projects in Pennsylvania.
AFIG program selection favors projects that result in product commercialization and the expansion of Pennsylvania companies.

Eligible AFIG applicants include school districts, municipal authorities, political subdivisions, non-profits, corporations, limited liability companies, or partnerships incorporated or registered in the Commonwealth.

AFIG grants can cover incremental cost expenses for retrofitting vehicles to operate on alternative fuels, purchasing alternative fuel vehicles, installing fleet- or home-refueling equipment, or performing research, training, development, and demonstration of new applications or next-phase technology related to alternative fuel vehicles.
The Pennsylvania Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) administers the Pennsylvania State Clean Diesel Grant Program for diesel emission reduction projects.

DEP Clean Diesel grants are funded by Pennsylvania’s portion of the Volkswagen Environmental Mitigation Trust and the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency’s Diesel Emission Reduction Act (DERA) Program.

The DEP offers Heavy-Duty Emission Reduction Grants to repower or replace ferries, tugboats, and freight switcher locomotives with any new U.S. EPA or California Air and Resource Board-certified diesel, alternative fuel, or all-electric equivalent.
The DEP provides Off-Road Electric Equipment Grants for the replacement of airport ground support equipment, forklifts, and port cargo handling equipment with an all-electric equivalent.
All funds for the DEP Off-Road Electric Equipment Grants have been awarded.

Pennsylvania’s NEVI Formula Program requires the Pennsylvania Department of Transportation (PennDOT) to submit an annually updated EV Infrastructure Deployment Plan for fiscal years 2022 through 2026.
The submitted NEVI plans must describe how the state intends to distribute funds, outline physical and cybersecurity strategies, and include a Community Engagement Outcomes Report.
Alternative fuels used to propel vehicles of any kind on public highways in Pennsylvania are taxed at a rate determined on a gasoline gallon equivalent basis.
EV owners in Pennsylvania must pay an annual fee of $200 in 2025 and $250 in 2026, while plug-in hybrid electric vehicle owners must pay an annual fee equal to 25% of the EV fee.

Pennsylvania signed a memorandum of understanding with multiple other states to support the deployment of medium- and heavy-duty zero emission vehicles (MHD ZEVs) through a Multi-State ZEV Task Force.
In July 2022, the Multi-State ZEV Task Force published an action plan that includes limiting all new MHD vehicle sales in signatory states to ZEVs by 2050.
The MHD ZEV deployment action plan aims to accelerate ZEV deployment and ensure it benefits disadvantaged communities.
A Neighborhood Electric Vehicle (NEV) is defined as a four-wheeled electric vehicle that has a maximum speed greater than 20 miles per hour but not more than 25 mph.

NEVs must comply with safety standards established in Title 49 of the U.S. Code of Federal Regulations, section 571.500, and drivers must maintain a defined level of insurance coverage for all registered vehicles.
In Pennsylvania, a person, corporation, or entity that owns or operates an EV charging station open to the public is not defined as a public utility.
Each electric distribution company in Pennsylvania must address third-party owned and operated EV charging stations in its tariff.
Pennsylvania state agencies must replace 25% of their passenger car fleets with EVs by 2025.
State agencies must collectively reduce all energy consumption by 3% annually, achieving a 21% reduction from a 2017 baseline by 2025.
The Governor’s Green Government Council was reestablished to work with state agencies to oversee the development and implementation of procedures to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and energy usage.

facts: Georgia achieved an unprecedented milestone: a full decade of business excellence, earning the rank of No. 1 state for business from Area Development magazine for 10 consecutive years.
No other state in the nation has achieved this benchmark.
The announcement was made by Governor Brian P. Kemp in conjunction with the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
Area Development’s 2023 Top States for Doing Business rankings are based on scores from approximately 50 leading site consulting firms across the U.S. in 14 categories.
Georgia placed in the top 10 for all 14 categories, earning the No. 1 spot in seven classifications and claiming the overall No. 1 ranking.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that people who help businesses choose where to make long-term investments have repeatedly said Georgia is the best state in the nation for opportunity.
The administration has brought record-breaking jobs and investments to all four corners of the state, especially to rural Georgia.

Dennis Shea, Publisher and President of Area Development, congratulated Governor Kemp, the Georgia Legislature, Commissioner Pat Wilson, and the GDEcD team for earning the No. 1 ranking for an impressive and unprecedented 10th consecutive year.

Area Development Staff Editor Steve Kaelble noted that Georgia is uniformly at or near the top of most lists making a state a great place to do business, having carefully assembled building blocks that location decision-makers look for.
Georgia earned a No. 1 ranking for the 14th year in a row for its workforce development program, Georgia Quick Start.
Georgia also earned a No. 1 ranking for competitive labor market.
Area Development noted that Georgia sits atop the list of states with the best workforce training opportunities, centered on the state’s Quick Start program.
The Quick Start program is offered through the Technical College System of Georgia and delivers customized training targeted at new and expanding businesses.
Companies in manufacturing, biotechnology, information technology, and other sectors need tech-savvy workers with specific skills, and this program has helped attract those businesses to Georgia.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones stated that the recognition is a testament of the administration, the Georgia General Assembly, and business communities working together.

Jones highlighted Georgia’s affordable cost of doing business, lower rates through a versatile energy mix, and support from hardworking Georgians as factors keeping the state moving in the right direction.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns stated that Georgia leads in logistics due to the nation’s fastest growing container port, the world’s busiest airport, and a robust road and rail network.
Burns noted that Georgia will continue to invest strategically in infrastructure to support the economy and keep the state the envy of the nation.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that success requires planning and implementation over years, supported by successive Governors, local leadership, and the Georgia General Assembly.
Wilson credited the partnership approach to economic development for allowing state and local teams to be responsive to business.

Wilson noted that the University System and Technical College System of Georgia have been ahead of the curve in preparing Georgians for workforce needs of tomorrow, giving Georgia a competitive advantage.
Area Development is considered the leading executive magazine covering corporate site selection and relocation, founded in 1965.

GDEcD is described as the state’s sales and marketing arm and lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging expansion of existing industry, locating new markets, and promoting economic development.

Deputy press contact for Governor Kemp's office

source_title: Georgia No. 1 State for Business for 10th Consecutive Year | Georgia

facts: Georgia Foreign-Trade Zone, Inc., grantee of FTZ 26, submitted a notification of proposed production activity to the FTZ Board on behalf of Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC), operator of Subzone 26L.
SMAC operates facilities located in Rome, Jonesboro, and Cartersville, Georgia.
The notification conforming to FTZ Board regulations was received on May 21, 2014.
SMAC already has authority to produce all-terrain vehicles (ATVs) and related components including carriers, footrests, fuel tanks, grips/handle bars, frames, and rear box assemblies.
The current request would add certain foreign components to the scope of authority for production under FTZ procedures.
Production under FTZ procedures could exempt SMAC from customs duty payments on foreign status components used in export production.
For domestic sales, SMAC can choose the duty rate during customs entry procedures that applies to ATVs and related components (2.5%) for the noted foreign status inputs.
Customs duties could possibly be deferred or reduced on foreign status production equipment.

Components sourced from abroad include plastic resins, chip plates, rubber hoses with/without fittings, rubber v-belts, tires and tubes, steel tubing, runners/banners, printed books/manuals/brochures, chain cam/oil pump drives, fasteners of steel and aluminum, aluminum gaskets, mountings and brackets, fenders, oil strainers, crankshaft bearings and assemblies, bearing housings, metal gaskets, stainless o-rings, batteries, spark plugs, starter motors, capacitive discharge ignition (CDI) units, magnetos, compact disc sets, copper and paper gaskets, gear shift assemblies, light bulbs, KD parts of ATVs, gear/fork shafts, therma-coupler nozzles, speedometers, and battery plates.
The duty rate for the sourced components ranges from free to 9.0%.
Public comment on the proposed activity was invited until July 22, 2014.
Andrew McGilvray serves as the Executive Secretary of the FTZ Board.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on May 22, 2025, that Mercedes-Benz will establish Atlanta as its North American headquarters by centralizing and uniting key corporate functions.
The company will move up to 500 jobs to the existing Mercedes-Benz facility known as “1MB” in Fulton County.
The company will make a multi-million dollar investment in a future state-of-the-art Research & Development (R&D) facility located near the “1MB” facility.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia continues to lead in the future of mobility and technical innovation, attracting world-class companies like Mercedes-Benz that are driving the automotive industry forward.

Governor Brian P. Kemp noted that Mercedes-Benz is a job creator with close ties to Georgia that is doubling down on its choice and growing its presence in what he called the best state for business and opportunity.
Mercedes-Benz opened its “1MB” facility in 2018 in Sandy Springs.
The “1MB” facility currently supports approximately 800 jobs in Georgia.

Jason Hoff, CEO of Mercedes-Benz North America, thanked the State of Georgia for its support in deepening Mercedes-Benz’s roots in the Atlanta area as the company brings more talented team members to the city.
Jason Hoff stated that bringing teams closer together will enable Mercedes-Benz to be more agile, increase speed to market, and ensure the best customer experience.
The “1MB” facility located in Sandy Springs will house existing sales teams, financial services teams, and corporate functions.
The new state-of-the-art Research & Development hub will be located near Sandy Springs.
The company anticipates that the move to metro Atlanta will be completed by August 2026.

Sandy Springs Mayor Rusty Paul expressed excitement over Mercedes-Benz expanding in Sandy Springs, noting they have been outstanding corporate partners since establishing their headquarters there in 2018.
Mayor Rusty Paul stated that the expansion highlights the success of the city’s recent infrastructure and capital investments which are now clearly paying dividends.

Fulton County Board of Commissioners Chairman Robb Pitts stated that Mercedes-Benz reaffirming its commitment by investing and growing in Fulton County is a testament to the strength and vitality of their community.

Chairman Robb Pitts noted that this proves Fulton County continues to be a destination for corporate solutions, providing major companies an accessible, vibrant, and growing community for their business to thrive.

Metro Atlanta Chamber President & CEO Katie Kirkpatrick stated the expansion is a testament to both Mercedes-Benz’s commitment to excellence and metro Atlanta's strength as a hub for innovation and talent.
Katie Kirkpatrick noted that when the 1MB facility opened in 2018, it quickly became an integral part of the business landscape, driving economic growth and elevating the region’s global presence.
Assistant Director of Statewide Projects Elizabeth McLean represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the City of Sandy Springs, Select Fulton, Metro Atlanta Chamber, and Georgia Power.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that since the strategic decision to relocate Mercedes-Benz USA to Sandy Springs in 2018, they have watched Mercedes-Benz become an integral part of their business community.

Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that this expansion and commitment to R&D in the metro Atlanta area will further strengthen the company’s long-term success and highlights talent and collaborative partnerships fostered by the University System of Georgia.
Mercedes-Benz AG is part of the Mercedes-Benz Group AG, which has a total of around 175,000 employees worldwide.
Mercedes-Benz AG is responsible for the global business of Mercedes-Benz Cars and Mercedes-Benz Vans.
Ola Källenius serves as Chairman of the Board of Management of Mercedes-Benz AG.
The company focuses on the development, production, and sales of passenger cars, vans, and vehicle-related services.
The company aspires to be the leader in the fields of electric mobility and vehicle software.
The product portfolio comprises the Mercedes-Benz brand with Mercedes AMG, Mercedes Maybach, and G-Class with their all-electric models as well as products of the smart brand.
Mercedes-Benz AG is one of the world’s largest manufacturers of high-end passenger cars.

Communications Manager for Georgia Department of Economic Development

source_title: Mercedes-Benz Establishing North American Headquarters, new Research & | Georgia

facts: Is a partner in the Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative.
Has committed to a 20% reduction in energy intensity by 2026 from an FY2014 baseline.
Operates 9 facilities.
Has achieved a 2% cumulative reduction in energy intensity versus its baseline.
Has committed to a 20% reduction in water use intensity from a 2014 baseline by 2028.
Has achieved a -5% cumulative reduction in water use intensity versus its baseline.
