facts: Georgia achieved another record year for economic development investments and job creation during fiscal year 2022.
FY22 investments and job creation were 94 percent and 53 percent above prior economic development records, respectively.
Investments in expansions and new locations totaled more than $21.2 billion between July 1, 2021, and June 30, 2022.
51,132 jobs were created in the state through 358 projects supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce team during FY22.
Excluding Rivian and Hyundai Motor Group, Georgia's FY22 jobs and investments totaled 35,532 jobs and $10.7 billion.
Rivian and Hyundai Motor Group were two of 12 automotive projects that created a total of nearly 16,000 new jobs across the state.
Eighty-five percent of FY22 investments and more than 30,000 new jobs came from outside the 10-county metro Atlanta region.
Companies choosing to expand in Georgia accounted for 74 percent of total projects, while new locations created 33,846 jobs across the state.
Advanced manufacturing added more than 10,000 new jobs in the state during FY22.
Both advanced manufacturing and automotive industries include manufacturers and suppliers for the electric vehicle (EV) industry.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s (HMGMA) development includes EV battery manufacturing along with affiliate suppliers on-site.
Component recyclers Aurubis and Ascend Elements have joined HMGMA as part of Georgia’s efforts to build a sustainable, reliable supply chain for its electric mobility ecosystem.

Lt. Governor Geoff Duncan stated that Georgia is working towards its shared goal to be the technology capital of the East Coast and continues to attract critical investments necessary to achieve that goal.

Speaker David Ralston credited strong public-private partnerships, lowering the tax burden on businesses and families, and incentivizing workforce development and investment for creating the nation’s best environment for business.

Access to Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport (the busiest and most efficient airport in the world), the Port of Savannah (the single largest and fastest-growing container terminal in America), and a robust network of highways and rail lines support Georgia's logistics and distribution industry.
Foreign direct investment (FDI) accounted for over $8 billion in investments created in fiscal year 2022.
The top five foreign direct investment sources were South Korea, Germany, Japan, France, and the Netherlands.
Korea-based projects represented the highest job creation and investment from a single country, with Germany producing the second most jobs and investments after Korea.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson led economic development missions to the United Kingdom, South Korea, and Germany in FY22.
Meetings were held with economic development drivers like Hyundai Motor Group and SK On during these missions.
Discussions regarding new opportunities for development occurred with the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia and the Liverpool City Region Freeport.
GDEcD offers 'Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development' (GRAD) certification to recognize pre-qualified industrial sites where due diligence is completed prior to a prospect visit.
Through GRAD, Georgia has certified more than 60 industrial sites as ready for fast-track industrial projects.
Georgia’s Quick Start program provides specialized training tailored specifically to business needs and has developed this workforce training capability for 55 years.
Since Governor Kemp took office, more than 119,963 new jobs and over $42.9 billion in investment have been announced in communities across the state.

Seventy-seven percent of state economic development projects, 81 percent of investments, and 57 percent of jobs since Kemp took office have gone to communities outside the 10-county metro Atlanta area.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Georgia Shatters Investment and Job Records in FY22 | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Atlanta-based Vernay Laboratories Inc. celebrated its 75th anniversary in 2021.
The company is the world’s leading designer and manufacturer of critical flow-control components for medical, automotive, and other markets.
Founder Sergius Vernet invented a wax-filled thermostat to control internal combustion engine temperature in the 1930s while working at Antioch College in Yellow Springs, Ohio.
Vernay Laboratories Inc. was founded in 1946 by Sergius Vernet, adopting a phonetic spelling for easier pronunciation.
The company's early thermostats were utilized in military airplanes and tanks during World War II.
In 1944, the U.S. Army awarded Vernay’s plant the Army-Navy “E” Award for excellence in war production, making it one of the smallest companies to receive the honor.
Sergius Vernet began molding rubber components himself after customers struggled to find suppliers capable of meeting critical thermostat part requirements.
Vernay Labs currently employs over 800 people globally.
The privately held firm operates five plants worldwide located in the U.S., the Netherlands, Italy, and China.
Vernay manufactures more than 1 billion precision molded elastomeric components per year.
The company utilizes an extensive library of custom formulated elastomeric materials and compounds for its products.
Founder Sergius Vernet was known in the mid-20th century for hiring African Americans, women, and Japanese Americans into key roles to promote diversity and social justice.
Sergius Vernet and his wife Suzanne created the philanthropic Vernay Foundation to fund civic institutions in Yellow Springs.
At the time of his death in 1968 at age 69, Sergius Vernet held more than 100 patents for his inventions.
Vernay Laboratories has earned 81 patents on product geometries and processes throughout its history.
Bob Ferguson serves as vice president of global research and development and is a 16-year company veteran.

Over the past dozen years, Vernay has hired key executives with significant global experience, increased automation, adopted Industry 4.0 protocols, and developed a unified product development approach while continuing to custom formulate its own material compounds.
Ed Urquhart was named CEO in 2009.
The company focuses on co-development with customers, assisting in concept generation and fluid/gas flow design for their applications.
Vernay is currently expanding clean-room space in the U.S. and Italy to serve the medical devices sector.
The company was designated an “essential supplier” by the U.S. and Italian governments during the COVID-19 pandemic for providing critical ventilator and respirator components.
Andy Woodward has served as global vice president of sales and marketing since 2010.
Vernay Laboratories formulates its own compounds to address specific applications, drawing from a library of over 33,000 elastomeric materials and compound formulations.
source_title: Celebrating 75 Years of Vernay Labs

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the third round of grants from the Rural Site Development Initiative (RSDI) within the OneGeorgia Authority on January 30, 2026.
The latest RSDI allotment includes $4.3 million for rural site development projects.
The total investment committed to rural site development projects since the program’s launch in FY 2025 is up to $21 million.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia is committed to bringing good-paying jobs to every part of the state and views the RSDI as a tool to bring opportunity to every zip code.
Governor Kemp credited the partnership of the General Assembly and state agency leadership for the initiative's generational impact on rural communities.

RSDI grants are awarded for either site improvement or to assist with costs of seeking Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certification on approved projects critical to economic development.
State funding for these grants is matched by participating communities at various levels based on program criteria.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones emphasized that workforce development and economic growth are essential for rural communities where new opportunities are emerging daily.

Speaker Jon Burns stated that OneGeorgia’s Rural Site Development grants represent the state’s commitment to uplifting rural communities, strengthening economic competitiveness, and creating pathways to prosperity in every corner of Georgia.

Department of Community Affairs Commissioner Christopher Nunn said helping keep Georgia’s communities ready for investment is essential for their long-term success and that DCA works alongside communities to ensure their vision of the future.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that development-ready sites are a must-have to be competitive in key industries such as logistics and manufacturing, and that partnering through GRAD certification and RSDI programs allows marketing every corner of the state for opportunities.
The OneGeorgia Authority scores and votes on eligible grant awards for the Rural Site Development Initiative.
Camden County Joint Development Authority in coastal Georgia was awarded $2 million for site readiness improvements, including construction of an entry road, at the Coastal Georgia Commerce Park.
The total project costs for the Camden County site readiness improvements are $7.3 million.
Toombs County Development Authority in south Georgia was awarded $2 million for site readiness improvements at the 599-acre, GRAD-certified One Toombs Industrial Park.
The total project costs for the Toombs County improvements are $2.7 million.
Brantley County Development Authority in southeast Georgia was awarded $45,000 to certify the 103-acre Satilla Industrial Park.
The total project costs for the Brantley County certification are $49,500.
Candler County Industrial Authority in south Georgia was awarded $103,500 to renew certification for and expand the Metter-Candler I-16 Industrial Park.
The total project costs for the Candler County expansion and renewal are $115,000.
Rome-Floyd Development Authority in northwest Georgia was awarded $20,625 to certify the 200-acre Enterprise Corner Industrial Park site.
The total project costs for the Rome-Floyd certification are $27,500.
Waycross-Ware County Development Authority in southeast Georgia was awarded $161,500 to certify the 391-acre Waycross and Ware County Airport Industrial Park.
The total project costs for the Waycross-Ware County certification are $188,900.
Eligible activities for grant funds include site studies, land grading, and other essential community infrastructure efforts supporting site development.
Grantees demonstrated substantive plans for industrial site development while meeting OneGeorgia Authority eligibility requirements.
Use of funds is subject to all applicable state laws and regulations, as well as policies and requirements of the OneGeorgia Authority and Department of Community Affairs.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm and lead agency for attracting new business investment and encouraging industry expansion.
GDEcD is responsible for locating new markets for Georgia products, promoting tourism, and planning and mobilizing state resources for economic development.

The Georgia Department of Community Affairs (DCA) partners with communities to build strong neighborhoods, commercial and industrial areas through community and economic development, local government assistance, and housing initiatives.
DCA uses state and federal resources to spur private job creation, implement planning, develop downtowns, generate affordable housing solutions, and promote volunteerism.

Georgia Department of Community Affairs general information

source_title: Gov. Kemp Announces Third Round of Rural Site Development Grants | Georgia

facts: The US DOE SuperTruck 3 initiative seeks to reduce medium/heavy truck greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by 75% on a lifecycle basis.
The initiative aims to reduce the total cost of truck ownership.
SuperTruck 3 runs from 2023 to 2027 and targets Class 4-6 and Class 7/8 trucks.
Participants in SuperTruck 3 are concentrating on electric and fuel cell applications for medium- and heavy-duty (MD/HD) trucks.
The first SuperTruck initiative was launched in 2009 with four industry participants successfully demonstrating new efficient technologies for Class 8 trucks, many of which were commercialized.
SuperTruck 2 ran from 2016 to 2023 and had five industry participants succeed with increasing Class 8 truck freight efficiency and engine brake thermal efficiency.
The initiative is ongoing as of June 10, 2024.
Information provided by Siddiq Khan of the U.S. Department of Energy Vehicle Technologies Office in 2024.
source_title: FOTW #1346, June 10, 2024: US DOE SuperTruck 3 Initiative Seeks to Reduce Medium/Heavy Truck Greenhouse Gas Emissions by 75% | Department of Energy

facts: The lithium-ion battery market was valued at $36.7 billion in 2019.
The market is projected to reach $129.3 billion by 2027.
The market will grow at a CAGR of 18% from 2020 to 2027.
Market growth is driven by increased use of electric and hybrid vehicles.
Product demand is expected to rise across the electrical & electronics industry due to smartphone and laptop penetration.
Lithium-ion batteries are widely used in tablets, laptops, mobile phones, PCs, and cameras due to prolonged service life and high energy density.
Smartphones, tablets, and laptop/PCs are the major segments that use lithium-ion batteries.
Battery backup is considered an important feature consumers enquire about before buying tablets, mobile phones, or laptops/PCs.
Li-ion batteries provide enhanced battery life and are majorly preferred in smartphone manufacturing.
The cost of a lithium-ion battery is 805.3 USD/KWh according to Power Tech Systems.
The cost of a lead-acid battery is 172.6 USD/KWh.
Lithium-ion battery prices have declined by about 15% per year over the past five years due to increased production volume.
High prices compared to conventional batteries limit market growth and slow adoption rates across different applications.
The report covers components including cathode, anode, electrolytic solution, foils, binders, and separators.
The cathode segment was the highest contributor to the market.
The electrolytic solution segment is predicted to grow at the fastest rate during the forecast period.
The global market is segmented by end-use industry into electrical & electronics, automotive, and industrial sectors.
The electrical & electronics segment was the highest contributor to the market.
The automotive segment is estimated to grow with a CAGR of 19.1% in the near future.
The automotive end-use segment includes cars, buses, trucks, scooters & bikes, trains & aircrafts, cranes & forklift, mining equipment, and smart grid & renewable energy storage.
The market is analyzed across North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and LAMEA regions.
Asia-Pacific is expected to dominate the market due to demand from consumer electronics and increased EVs in China, Japan, and India.
Lithium-ion batteries are extensively used in the automotive sector in North America and Europe.
Asia-Pacific and LAMEA regions are expected to grow rapidly due to surge in adoption of these batteries in smartphones, tablets, and laptops/PCs.

Key players operating in the global industry include Automotive Energy Supply Corporation, Panasonic Corporation, Samsung SDI Co. Ltd., LG Chem Power (LGCPI), LITEC Co., Ltd., A123 Systems, LLC., Toshiba Corporation, Hitachi Chemical Co., Ltd., China BAK Battery Co. Ltd., and GS Yuasa International Ltd.
Key players are adopting strategic moves such as business agreements, expansion plans, mergers & acquisitions, and joint ventures to enhance market position.
In September 2017, Maruti Suzuki India Ltd. collaborated with Toshiba and Denso to start a lithium-ion battery production facility in India, with Toshiba responsible for cell modules.
The North America lithium-ion battery market is projected to grow at a rapid CAGR of nearly 18.7% during the projected period.
Asia-Pacific is anticipated to dominate the global lithium-ion battery market during the entire forecast period.
Asia-Pacific and North America collectively accounted for around 72.8% of the lithium-ion battery market share in 2019, with Asia-Pacific constituting around 39.9%.
Mexico is expected to witness a CAGR of 19.9% during the forecast period.
The U.S. is expected to witness a CAGR of 18.5% during the forecast period.

Company profiles in the report include CBAK Energy Technology, Inc., GS Yuasa International Ltd., Hitachi, Ltd., LG Chem, Lithium Werks B.V., Panasonic Corporation, Renault Group, Samsung SDI Co. Ltd., Toshiba Corporation, and Wanxiang Group Corporation.
source_title: Lithium-ion Battery Market Worth $129 Billion by 2027 - Breakdown by Component, End-use Industry and Region - ResearchAndMarkets.com

facts: HMGMA hosted its Grand Opening celebration on March 26, 2025.
The ceremony marks the completion of the largest economic development project in Georgia’s history.
Groundbreaking occurred two and a half years prior to the grand opening.
Euisun Chung serves as Executive Chair of Hyundai Motor Group.
Euisun Chung stated HMGMA represents advanced manufacturing capabilities, commitment to innovation, and investment in relationships with partners and communities in Georgia.
Euisun Chung noted the community's rich history of craftsmanship and manufacturing, and that the talented workforce at HMGMA is building the future of mobility with America, in America.

Attendees at the grand opening included Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung, Governor Brian P. Kemp, U.S. Representative Buddy Carter, Hyundai Motor Group Vice Chair Jaehoon Chang, Hyundai Motor Company President and CEO José Muñoz, Kia Corporation President and CEO Ho Sung Song, company officials, state and local leaders, HMGMA Meta Pro employees, and other distinguished guests.
Governor Brian P. Kemp called HMGMA a once-in-a-generation opportunity made possible by commitment to working with job creators for decades.

Governor Kemp highlighted collaborations between HMGMA and Georgia’s universities and technical colleges, including Georgia Quick Start’s on-site training facility, preparing Georgians for high-quality jobs.
Governor Kemp noted award-winning infrastructure, such as ports connecting companies to markets around the nation and globe.
Governor Kemp highlighted the plant's positive impact on the state’s economy, role in advancing innovative manufacturing, and cultivating a skilled labor pool in Georgia.
HMG entered a landmark agreement with the State of Georgia in May 2022 to establish an electric and hybrid vehicle production and battery manufacturing facility in Ellabell, Georgia.
The facility is located 20 miles outside of historic Savannah.
HMGMA was officially formed by June 2022.
Groundbreaking occurred within four months of the company's formation.
On October 3, 2024, less than two years after breaking ground, the first vehicle rolled off the assembly line.
The first vehicle produced was a Hyundai IONIQ 5.
Hyundai’s IONIQ 9 three-row electric SUV is now in production at HMGMA.
The first Kia model to be built at the facility will join production in 2026.
The plant manufactures electric vehicles for Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia.
The plant is equipped to produce hybrid vehicles.
HMGMA initially has a capacity to produce 300,000 vehicles annually.
The Group's investment in Georgia related to HMGMA and joint battery ventures with LG Energy Solution and SK On is $12.6 billion.
This represents the largest single investment in the state's history.
The investment includes a total of 8,500 jobs at HMGMA by 2031.
The Group’s total investments in Georgia are expected to create nearly 40,000 direct and indirect jobs.
These investments are expected to generate $4.6 billion in individual earnings every year, according to the Center for Automotive Research.
As of July 2024, more than $2.5 billion in capital investment has been announced by HMGMA suppliers in 12 counties across the state.
Approximately 6,900 jobs have been announced by HMGMA suppliers as of July 2024.
This supplier investment more than doubles the originally anticipated $1 billion investment.
To date, Hyundai Motor Group has invested more than $20.5 billion in the U.S.
These investments have created or supported over 570,000 jobs nationwide.
Earlier this week relative to March 26, 2025, the Group announced a significant investment of $21 billion in the U.S. from 2025 to 2028.
$9 billion will be invested to establish an annual production capacity in the U.S. of 1.2 million vehicles.
The Group plans to expand HMGMA's annual production capacity to a total of 500,000 units.
This expansion is an increase of 200,000 units from the original plan.
Existing U.S. production sites – Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama and Kia Autoland Georgia – will also see improvements in production facilities.
A total of $6 billion will be allocated to enhance the Group’s parts and logistics business.
Another $6 billion will fund establishing an Electric Arc Furnace-based integrated steel mill in the U.S. through its affiliate, Hyundai Steel.
The steel mill will have an annual steel production capacity of 2.7 million tons.
The mill will supply high-quality automotive steel plates to HMGMA when commercial steel production begins in 2029.
The Group will invest an additional $6 billion to drive innovation and expand strategic partnerships with U.S. companies.
Partnerships will focus on autonomous driving, robotics, artificial intelligence (AI), and advanced air mobility (AAM).
Through the $21 billion commitment, the Group anticipates creating 14,000 new direct full-time jobs in the U.S. by 2028.
The overall economic impact is expected to generate more than 100,000 direct and indirect job opportunities across related industries.
HMGMA’s architecture blends advanced technology with design elements reflecting Hyundai’s brand identity and the natural beauty of the Savannah area.
HMGMA integrated proof-of-concept manufacturing technologies and systems from Hyundai Motor Group Innovation Center Singapore (HMGICS).
These systems were scaled to accommodate larger production volume at the Georgia facility.
All HMGMA’s processes – order collection, procurement, logistics and production – are optimized using AI and real-time data.
Cutting-edge robotics and vision systems ensure high-quality vehicle production.
These innovative manufacturing systems help create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers, improving efficiency.
HMGMA expands its mixed-model production capability and integrates various robot processes to enhance automation.
Automation includes automated guided vehicles for an optimized logistics system.
Boston Dynamic’s Spot is used for exterior quality inspection in the weld shop.
Parking robots are used in the assembly shop.
Automation in HMGMA's assembly shop is nearly double that of the Group’s existing facilities.
Oscar Kwon serves as CEO of HMGMA.
Oscar Kwon stated it's a historic moment that their state-of-the-art smart plant is officially open.
Oscar Kwon emphasized creating a comfortable and welcoming work environment for Meta Pros (HMGMA employees) alongside an energy-efficient and technologically advanced plant.
Oscar Kwon noted it is essential for Meta Pros to be happy, comfortable and safe for long-lasting careers at HMGMA.
All work zones, from office to production areas, have been designed to encourage communication and build team collaboration.
Designs feature open layouts with ample daylight and indoor landscaping.
Production areas, such as those in the assembly building, have team centers with skylights delivering natural light at the center of the building.
These team centers can be used for employee breaks and meetings.
The 3,050-foot-long front of the HMGMA building’s exterior is lit in two parallel lines inspired by a stream.
This lighting outlines an area where interstate travelers can view vehicles as they travel by conveyor from paint to general assembly.
A solar parking lot located at the front of the site will generate 5.2 megawatts of energy.
This accounts for roughly five percent of the Metaplant’s total power consumption.
The solar panels are installed above 1,878 parking spaces.
The panels provide shade for vehicles while simultaneously producing electricity.
A 41-acre ecological park will feature prominently in front of the Metaplant.
The park includes trails, running tracks, sports fields, and picnic areas.
The park is designed for Meta Pros to enjoy before or after work.
Construction of the park is ongoing.
HMGMA has deployed Hyundai Motor XCIENT heavy-duty hydrogen fuel-cell electric trucks for clean logistics operations.
A total of 21 XCIENT trucks are currently in operation.
These trucks represent more than a third of the Glovis America truck fleet at HMGMA.
The trucks transport vehicle parts from suppliers across the region to the 2,906-acre Megasite where HMGMA is located on a daily basis.
This initiative represents a significant step forward in HMGMA’s leadership efforts to reduce its carbon footprint and promote sustainable practices in daily logistics operations.
Hyundai Motor has supported several non-profit organizations impacting education, safety, and sustainability under its global vision of smart mobility solutions and sustainable innovation.
Since 2022, Hyundai and Genesis, along with various social responsibility initiatives and nonprofit organizations, have donated nearly $4.5 million to local organizations in Georgia.
Kia's Autoland Georgia factory in West Point has provided over $22 million in support of Georgia communities since 2009.
This includes more than $6 million to support educational programs such as the THINC College & Career Academy, and SAE’s “A World in Motion.”
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America Celebrates Grand Opening, Powering US Economic Growth | Press Releases & Insights | Worldwide | HTWO

facts: In 2024, investments through DOE’s Office of Manufacturing and Energy Supply Chains (MESC) helped companies create or retain nearly 50,000 jobs.
In September, DOE announced more than $2.8 billion to support affordable clean power in the Midwest through new nuclear energy facilities that will create more than 2,000 good-paying union jobs.

The Industrial Training and Assessment Center (ITAC) Program invested $100 million in workforce development, funding over 200 projects to help ensure America has the skilled labor force necessary to sustain recent manufacturing momentum.
In May, DOE announced the first faculty cohort for the Faculty-Applied Clean Energy Sciences (FACES) Program to help teachers inspire STEM students to pursue careers in clean energy.

In 2024, DOE’s Grid Deployment Office (GDO) announced more than $5.4 billion in grid improvement grants and competitive awards to strengthen and expand the nation’s grid, covering all 50 states, 264 tribes, 5 territories, and the District of Columbia.
DOE deployed $900 million in funding to revitalize the nuclear sector and add more carbon-free energy to the grid.

DOE announced $30 million in funding to deploy AI software solutions to accelerate the energy interconnection application process and created a resource hub for datacenter owners and operators to secure clean, reliable energy solutions.
In August, DOE dedicated its new Grid Storage Launchpad facility to strengthen the resilience of the grid to withstand future tests and challenges.
In 2024, MESC deployed over $12 billion to transform energy supply chains, enabling the construction of 80+ manufacturing facilities across 31 states.

In April, DOE announced $75 million for a project to develop a Critical Minerals Supply Chain Research Facility, funded by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to strengthen domestic supply chains and reduce reliance on unreliable foreign sources.
DOE announced more than $850 million for America's rural and remote communities through the Energy Improvements in Rural or Remote Areas Program.
DOE launched the Arctic Energy Ambassadors program to facilitate energy projects in Alaska.
DOE directed $190 million through the Renew America’s Schools Program to support school districts across the country in implementing energy upgrades.
In October, DOE announced $518 million to support 23 selected projects that will strengthen the nation’s infrastructure for permanent, safe storage of carbon pollution.
DOE announced nearly $85 million to accelerate manufacturing electric heat pumps, helping to reduce energy consumption and harmful greenhouse gas emissions.

In December, DOE announced $850 million for 40+ projects as part of the joint EPA-DOE Methane Emissions Reduction Program (MERP), providing a total of $1.36 billion to support monitoring and mitigation of methane emissions from the oil and natural gas sector.

DOE’s Office of Clean Energy and Demonstrations announced up to $6 billion for 30+ projects as part of the Industrial Demonstrations Program (IDP) to help accelerate the commercial-scale demonstration of emerging industrial decarbonization technologies, strengthening domestic manufacturing.

Thanks to the Inflation Reduction Act, DOE rolled out the $8.8 billion home energy rebates program in 10 states this year, helping American households save up to $14,000 on residential energy efficiency and electrification upgrades.
DOE launched tools for Americans to save money on home appliances and electric vehicles, including the Tax Credit Product Lookup Tool and the Local Fuel Savings Tool.
The Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP) served approximately 38,000 homes this year, reducing energy costs for low-income households by increasing the energy efficiency of their homes.

In 2024, the Assisting Federal Facilities with Energy Conservation Technologies (AFFECT) grant program announced more than $250 million in funding to leverage public-private partnerships to reduce pollution, create jobs, and cut costs for taxpayers.

For the first time, the Loan Programs Office published full Community Benefit Plans (CBPs) for projects that have reached financial close, ensuring tangible benefits for communities of color and low-income communities.

Through the $5 million Regional Energy Democracy Initiatives (REDI) program, DOE’s Office of Energy Justice and Equity (EJE) is providing capacity building and technical assistance for communities in Texas and Louisiana to maximize benefits from clean energy investments.

The Office of Indian Energy announced $25 million to support clean energy technology deployment on tribal lands and delivered $9 million to Tribal Colleges and Universities (TCUs) to advance clean energy projects and bolster food sovereignty initiatives on their campuses.

Key Federal programs supported by the Joint Office of Energy and Transportation have spurred private sector investment to grow the national electric vehicle (EV) charging network to more than 205,000 public EV chargers, with nearly 1,000 new public chargers turned on every week.

Thanks to the National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Program and $2.5 billion Charging and Fueling Infrastructure (CFI) Program, there are 259 federally funded public charging ports operational across 15 states, with projects for 24,800 federally funded charging ports underway across the country.
DOE announced millions of dollars in investments in innovative clean energy technology, including solar energy, geothermal, and offshore wind.
DOE commissioned El Capitan, the fastest supercomputer in the world, to support clean energy innovation.
DOE embedded energy innovators at national labs to develop next-generation technologies.

In 2024, DOE supported research and development opportunities, surpassing $1 billion in total funding to advance nuclear energy research and training and announcing over $200 million for small business research and development grants.
Over the last four years, DOE committed more than half a billion dollars in Tribal energy investments for clean energy generation and deployment, electrification and grid resilience.

In September 2024, the Loan Programs Office closed on their first loan to finance the development of a solar-plus-long-duration-energy-storage microgrid on the lands of the Viejas Band of the Kumeyaay Indians on the Viejas Reservation near Alpine, California for $72.8 million.

In August 2024, the Biden-Harris Administration announced the government’s first-ever purchase of carbon pollution-free electricity from a Tribal majority-owned business under the Indian Energy Purchase Preference provisions of the Energy Policy Act of 2005, led by the General Services Administration and supported through interagency collaboration with DOE.

In February 2024, more than 700 Tribal Leaders, staff, and partners attended the DOE Tribal Clean Energy Summit on the homelands of the Pechanga Band of Luiseño Indians in Temecula, California to build networks, exchange ideas, and explore clean energy solutions that support Tribal energy sovereignty.
source_title: DOE's Top Clean Energy Accomplishments in 2024 | Department of Energy

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on March 10, 2026, that Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. has officially decided to move its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The relocation decision follows a meeting between Governor Kemp and Yamaha executives in October of last year at the Southeast U.S./Japan (SEUS-Japan) annual gathering in Tokyo.
Yamaha is relocating out of California following roughly 50 years of operation there.
The relocation of the Yamaha Motor Corp. U.S. headquarters will begin this year and is expected to continue through 2028.
Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. manufactures ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, and other motorized products.
The company currently employs over 2,300 Georgians across various facilities in the state.
The 1.3-million-square-foot Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America factory in Newnan opened in 1986 and currently employs roughly 2,000 Georgians.
Yamaha relocated its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999.
The company expanded the Kennesaw facility with a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
In 2019, the company moved its motorsports operations to Georgia to benefit from close proximity to the existing facilities.
Governor Brian Kemp called Georgia the "No. 1 state for business" and welcomed Yamaha's American headquarters as a testament to what the state offers job creators.
Governor Kemp addressed other California-based companies looking for a better home, stating he would give them plenty of reasons to keep Georgia on their mind.
Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. is a world-leading producer of motorcycles, marine products, power products, and robotics.
The company was founded in 1955 and is headquartered in Iwata, Shizuoka, Japan.
The company operates under the corporate mission of being a "Kando Creating Company," aiming to offer new excitement and a more fulfilling life for people all over the world.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Yamaha to Move HQ to Georgia | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: The Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) of 2022 (Public Law 117-169) supports alternative fuel vehicle and infrastructure technologies through tax credits, grant programs, and loan programs.

Section 13201 provides an Alternative Fuel Excise Tax Credit reinstated through December 31, 2024, offering $0.50 per gallon for alternative fuels including natural gas, liquefied hydrogen, propane, P-Series fuel, Fischer-Tropsch coal-derived liquid fuel, and biomass-derived compressed or liquefied gas.

For propane and natural gas sold after December 31, 2015, the Alternative Fuel Excise Tax Credit is calculated based on gasoline gallon equivalent (GGE) or diesel gallon equivalent (DGE), where one GGE equals 5.75 lbs of propane and 5.66 lbs of compressed natural gas, and one DGE equals 6.06 lbs of liquefied natural gas.

Eligible entities for the Alternative Fuel Excise Tax Credit must be liable for reporting and paying federal excise tax on qualified fuel sales or use, with tax-exempt state and local governments qualifying if they dispense qualified fuel from on-site stations, and all eligible entities must register with the IRS.

Section 13201 provides a Biodiesel Mixture Excise Tax Credit extended through December 31, 2024, granting registered IRS blenders $1.00 per gallon of pure biodiesel, agri-biodiesel, or renewable diesel blended with petroleum diesel to produce a mixture containing at least 0.1% diesel fuel.

To claim the Biodiesel Mixture Excise Tax Credit, claims must include a certificate from a registered producer or importer identifying the product, specifying its biodiesel/agri-biodiesel/renewable diesel content, confirming EPA registration, and confirming ASTM Standard D6751 compliance.

Renewable diesel is defined as liquid biomass-derived fuel meeting EPA registration and ASTM Standards D975 or D396, explicitly excluding fuels derived from co-processing biomass with non-biomass feedstocks.

Section 13201 provides a Biodiesel Income Tax Credit extended through December 31, 2024, granting taxpayers $1.00 per gallon of pure, unblended biodiesel (B100) delivered into vehicle tanks or used as on-road fuel in their trade or business.

For retail-sold biodiesel, only the seller who placed it into the vehicle tank is eligible for the Biodiesel Income Tax Credit, which applies against income tax liability and requires certificates confirming EPA registration and ASTM D6751 compliance.

Section 13401 amended the Qualified Plug-in Electric Drive Motor Vehicle Credit (IRC 30D) to create the Clean Vehicle Credit, adding a North America final assembly requirement effective August 17, 2022.

Vehicles placed in service on or after January 1, 2023, face additional requirements, and credit amounts depend on meeting new critical minerals and battery components requirements for vehicles placed in service after April 17, 2023.
Taxpayers purchasing eligible vehicles may qualify for a Clean Vehicle Credit of up to $7,500.

Section 50142 authorizes the DOE Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Loan Program to offer direct loans covering up to 30% of costs for re-equipping, expanding, or establishing U.S. manufacturing facilities for qualified advanced technology vehicles, components, or alternative fuel infrastructure.

Qualified ATVs are light-, medium-, and heavy-duty ultra-efficient vehicles meeting federal emission standards, emitting low or zero exhaust, and achieving at least 75 miles per gallon on gasoline or diesel as hybrids or fully electric vehicles.

Section 13201 provides an Alternative Fuel Mixture Excise Tax Credit reinstated through December 31, 2024, granting registered IRS blenders $0.50 per gallon of alternative fuel blends containing at least 0.1% gasoline, diesel, or kerosene.
Qualified alternative fuels for the mixture credit include liquefied hydrogen, P-Series fuel, Fischer-Tropsch coal-derived liquid fuel, and biomass-derived liquid fuel.

Section 13404 provides an Alternative Fuel Infrastructure Tax Credit of 30% of cost (not to exceed $30,000) for eligible fueling equipment installed through December 31, 2022, excluding permitting and inspection fees.

Starting January 1, 2023, the Alternative Fuel Infrastructure Tax Credit increases to 30% of cost or 6% for depreciable property (not to exceed $100,000), requiring installation in eligible census tracts and compliance with apprenticeship and prevailing wage requirements.
Consumers purchasing qualified residential fueling equipment between January 1, 2023, and December 31, 2032, may receive a tax credit of up to $1,000.
Section 13402 provides a Pre-Owned EV and FCEV Tax Credit of up to $4,000 beginning January 1, 2023, for vehicles at least two model years prior to purchase with a price not exceeding $25,000.

Individuals claiming the pre-owned vehicle credit must have gross annual income below specified thresholds and may only claim one credit per vehicle and no more than one pre-owned vehicle credit in a three-year period.

Section 13403 provides a Commercial EV and FCEV Tax Credit beginning January 1, 2023, requiring vehicles under 14,000 lbs GVWR to have at least 7 kWh battery capacity and those above 14,000 lbs to have at least 15 kWh.

The commercial vehicle credit amount equals the lesser of specified thresholds, with maximum credits capped at $7,500 for vehicles under 14,000 lbs and $40,000 for vehicles above 14,000 lbs, and businesses cannot combine it with the Clean Vehicle Tax Credit.

Section 60101 mandates the EPA to create a Heavy-Duty Zero Emission Vehicle and Infrastructure Grant program by February 12, 2023, covering up to 100% of eligible project costs for state, municipal, tribal governments, and non-profit school transportation associations.
Additional grant funding under Section 60101 is available for projects located in nonattainment communities.

Section 50142 provides a 30% tax credit for qualified advanced energy project investments to reequip, expand, or establish manufacturing facilities for light-, medium-, and heavy-duty EVs, FCEVs, EV charging stations, and hydrogen fueling stations.
Credits under the EV and FCEV Manufacturing Tax Credit cannot be allocated to projects in census tracts where similar projects have previously received allocations.

Section 60102 mandates the EPA to establish a competitive Clean Ports grant program for purchasing or installing zero emission port equipment, including EVs and FCEVs, with eligible applicants including port authorities, government entities, air pollution control agencies, and private port operators.
Additional funding under the Clean Ports program is available for projects in nonattainment communities.

Section 13203 provides a Sustainable Aviation Fuel Tax Credit of $1.25 per gallon for producers whose SAF reduces greenhouse gas emissions by at least 50%, with an additional $0.01 per gallon for each percent reduction exceeding 50% up to $0.50 per gallon.
Eligible SAF producers must register with the IRS, and additional terms and conditions apply to the credit.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Inflation Reduction Act of 2022

facts: The Georgia Department of Community Affairs (DCA) partners with communities to create a climate of success for Georgia's families and businesses through community and economic development, local government assistance, and safe and affordable housing.
Using state and federal resources, DCA helps communities spur private job creation, implement planning, develop downtowns, generate affordable housing solutions, and promote volunteerism.
DCA coordinates a variety of economic incentives and tools, tax credits, and financing plans.
DCA works with and supports the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) in their mission to promote economic development in Georgia.

DCA economic development finance programs include OneGeorgia Authority funds and programs, the Regional Economic Business Assistance program, the Georgia Business Expansion and Support Act income tax credit programs and many others.

In April 2012, the Georgia General Assembly passed and Governor Nathan Deal signed legislation updating the state's Open Records Act to promote economic competitiveness, provide clarity on the responsibilities of state and local governments and increase transparency for residents of the state.
The revised code keeps the state's sensitive information regarding economic activities confidential while ensuring that a citizen's personal information is protected.

Pursuant to O.C.G.A. 50-18-72 (a)(46), the Georgia Department of Economic Development will comply with updated public notice requirements for the use of state funds and economic development incentive programs.
For projects where GDEcD committed OneGeorgia Authority or REBA funds, the department must post a notice of the binding commitment on its website along with records documenting the commitment.
The department must also publish notice of the project and participating parties in the legal organ of each county where the economic development project is located.
Notice will be given on the webpage no later than five (5) business days after GDEcD secures a binding commitment with a qualified economic development project.
Notices will reside on the page for a period of 30 days.
An economic development project pertains to a plan or proposal to locate or expand a business that includes an expenditure of over $25,000,000 by the company.
An economic development project pertains to a plan or proposal to locate or expand a business that includes hiring of 50 or more employees by the company.
An economic development project pertains to a plan or proposal to locate or expand a business that includes commitment of state funds from OneGeorgia Authority or REBA.
A binding agreement between GDEcD and Dongwon Autopart Technology Georgia, Emanuel LLC was entered into on May 19, 2026.
The Dongwon Autopart Technology Georgia project is located in Swainsboro, Emanuel County.
An EDGE Grant in the amount of $600,000 will be awarded to Dongwon Autopart Technology Georgia, Emanuel LLC through the Emanuel County Development Authority.
A binding agreement between GDEcD and Irving Consumer Products, Inc. was entered into on May 26, 2026.
The Irving Consumer Products project is located in Macon, Bibb County.
An EDGE Grant in the amount of $750,000 will be awarded to Irving Consumer Products, Inc. through the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority.
A binding agreement between GDEcD and Unified Defense Holdings, LLC d/b/a Prince Service & Manufacturing was entered into on May 28, 2026.
The Unified Defense Holdings project is located in Macon, Bibb County.
An EDGE Grant in the amount of $3,000,000 will be awarded to Unified Defense Holdings, LLC d/b/a Prince Service & Manufacturing through the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Procter & Gamble Distributing, LLC is planning to invest $205 million in a new distribution facility in Jackson, Georgia.
The new facility will create 350 new jobs in Butts County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated his administration has been laser-focused on creating jobs and opportunities in rural Georgia.
Procter & Gamble Company is headquartered in Cincinnati, Ohio, and specializes in personal healthcare and hygiene products.
P&G currently operates a manufacturing facility in Albany and a distribution center outside of Atlanta.
Ignacio Arranz, Senior Vice President of P&G Physical Distribution Innovation, stated the company is expanding its Georgia footprint to better meet product demand.
The new distribution center will be located at 950 Logistics Parkway in Jackson, Georgia.
The state-of-the-art, automated facility will exceed 1 million square feet upon completion.
Butts County Board of Commissioners’ Chair Joe Brown welcomed P&G as a corporate citizen to the county.
Metro Atlanta Chamber President and CEO Katie Kirkpatrick noted the announcement bolsters Georgia’s reputation and leadership in the supply chain sector.
Griff Lynch, Executive Director of the Georgia Ports Authority, highlighted Georgia’s logistics network ability to reach major domestic and global markets efficiently.
Bob Kosek, Director of Industrial Projects, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team on the project.
The project was executed in partnership with the Development Authority of Butts County, Metro Atlanta Chamber, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Georgia’s logistics infrastructure includes highways connected to 80% of the U.S. market by less than two days of driving.
The announcement was made on May 13, 2022, in Atlanta.

facts: Adient is a global leader in automotive seating.
Adient has approximately 75,000 employees in 33 countries.
Adient operates 208 manufacturing/assembly plants worldwide.
Adient produces and delivers automotive seating for all major OEMs.
Adient's product range spans from complete seating systems to individual components.
Adient's expertise covers every step of the automotive seat-making process, from research and design to engineering and manufacturing.
Adient supplies its products into more than 20 million vehicles every year.
Q3 2022 GAAP net loss was $(30)M with diluted EPS of $(0.32).
Q3 2022 adjusted diluted EPS was $0.08.
Q3 2022 adjusted EBITDA was $143M, up $25M year-over-year (up $18M when adjusting for portfolio actions executed in FY21).
Adient held strong cash and cash equivalents of $892M at June 30, 2022.
Total liquidity was approximately $1.7B at June 30, 2022.
Gross debt totaled approximately $2.7B and net debt totaled approximately $1.8B at June 30, 2022.
Douglas Del Grosso serves as Adient's president and chief executive officer.
Jeff Stafeil serves as Adient's executive vice president and chief financial officer.
Adient cites the Ukraine conflict and widespread COVID lockdowns in China as impacting regional/global economies, supply chains, and vehicle production.
Adient identifies semiconductor availability, energy and commodity prices (particularly steel), and raw material access as key supply chain risks.

entity_name: $1.3 Billion Electric Vehicle Charging and Alternative Fueling Infrastructure Funding Opportunity
canonical_name: Charging and Fueling Infrastructure (CFI) Discretionary Grant Program
facts: The Joint Office of Energy and Transportation opened applications for a historic $1.3 billion funding opportunity for electric vehicle charging and alternative fueling infrastructure, including hydrogen fueling infrastructure.
The funding targets deployment in urban and rural communities as well as along designated highways, interstates, and major roadways.
The funding opportunity is made possible by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act's Charging and Fueling Infrastructure (CFI) Discretionary Grant Program.
Additional funds are sourced from the National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Formula Program set aside for strategic grants to states and local governments.
The CFI program is supported by unifying guidance and technical assistance from the Joint Office of Energy and Transportation.
The CFI program is administered by the Federal Highway Administration.
Eligible applicants include state, regional, Tribal, and local government entities.
The CFI program is divided into two distinct grant funding categories: Community Charging and Fueling Grants and Alternative Fuel Corridor Grants.
The program requires that 50% of the funding over five years be made available for both communities and corridors.

Community Charging and Fueling Grants will strategically deploy publicly accessible EV charging infrastructure and hydrogen, propane, and natural gas fueling infrastructure in urban and rural communities.

Alternative Fuel Corridor Grants will strategically deploy publicly accessible EV charging infrastructure and hydrogen, propane, and natural gas fueling infrastructure along designated alternative fuel corridors.
Eligible applicants also include metropolitan planning organizations, port authorities, Indian Tribes, and U.S. territories.
Applications are due by August 28, 2024.
source_title: Applications Open for $1.3 Billion in Funding to Expand National Electric Vehicle Charging Network and Alternative Fueling Infrastructure | Department of Energy

facts: On February 28th, Archer closed on the local incentive transaction for the project that, along with a Georgia REBA grant, totals ~33% of the planned capital investment.
On March 2nd, Archer began construction on Phase 1 of the project, which is the build-out of a ~350,000 square-foot facility capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
The Covington facility will be capable of being expanded by an additional 800,000 square feet, which is estimated to support long-term production targets of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Archer and Stellantis are set to join forces to begin manufacturing Archer’s Midnight eVTOL aircraft in 2024.
In January, Archer announced an expanded relationship with Stellantis, one of the world’s leading automakers and owner of brands like Jeep®, Ram and Maserati.
Under the partnership, Stellantis will contribute advanced manufacturing technology and expertise, experienced personnel, and capital.
Archer will bring its team of eVTOL, electric powertrain, and certification experts to the partnership.
The collaboration is intended to enable the rapid scaling of aircraft production to meet Archer’s commercialization plans.
The partnership allows Archer to avoid hundreds of millions of dollars in spending during the manufacturing ramp-up phase.
The goal is for Stellantis to mass produce Archer’s eVTOL aircraft as its exclusive contract manufacturer.

Adam Goldstein, Archer’s Founder and CEO, stated that scale is key to unlocking the promise of the eVTOL industry and expressed thrill over support from the State of Georgia and Newton County for the first-of-its-kind facility.
Archer plans to hold a ceremonial event in April in Covington, GA to mark the construction milestone.
The facility is strategically located adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport.
Archer is designing and developing electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.
Archer's team is based in Santa Clara, CA.
source_title: Archer Closes Incentive Transaction; Begins Construction on First of its Kind High-Volume eVTOL Manufacturing Facility in Covington, GA

facts: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) Rural Energy Savings Program provides loans for cost-effective energy efficiency measures in rural areas.
Loans may be used to finance consumer-owned EV chargers and the infrastructure necessary to supply EV chargers.
Eligible borrowers include utilities; energy efficiency service providers; and state, local, and tribal governments.
The program is referenced under Public Law 113-79, Public Law 115-334, and 7 U.S. Code 8107a.
The jurisdiction for the program is Federal.
The program type is classified as Incentives.
The administering agency is the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
The program was enacted on Feb 7, 2014.
The program was amended on Dec 20, 2018.
The technologies supported by the program include EVs and PHEVs.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Rural Electric Vehicle (EV) Infrastructure Loans

entity_name: DOE AMO CMEI eXCHANGE Funding Opportunity
facts: The submission deadline for Full Applications has been extended to 09/12/2019 at 5 PM ET.
The expected submission deadline for Replies to Reviewer Comments has been extended to 10/23/219 at 5 PM ET.
Applicants should reference updated Modification 0002 of the Funding Opportunity Announcement for changes.
The submission deadline for Full Applications has been extended to 09/05/2019, 5:00pm ET.
Applicants should reference updated Modification 0001 of the Funding Opportunity Announcement for additional changes.

This funding opportunity announcement supports federal investment in innovative, early-stage advanced manufacturing applied research and development projects focusing on specific high-impact manufacturing technology, materials, and process challenges.

The topics are aimed at foundational energy-related advanced manufacturing technologies that impact areas relevant to manufacturing processes where energy costs are a determinant of competitive manufacturing and broadly applicable platform technologies.

Competitively selected projects will focus on developing next-generation manufacturing material, information, and process technologies that improve energy efficiency in energy intensive and energy dependent processes.
The Advanced Manufacturing Office intends to fund high-impact, early to mid-stage research in specific topic areas.
Topic 1 covers Innovations for the Manufacture of Advanced Materials.
Subtopic 1.1 focuses on accelerating the manufacturing process design and development cycle for advanced energy conversion and storage materials.
Subtopic 1.2 covers innovative manufacturing processes for battery energy storage.
Subtopic 1.3 covers materials and manufacturing process development of nanocrystalline metal alloys.
Subtopic 1.4 covers process-informed science, design, and engineering of materials and devices operating in harsh service conditions.
Topic 2 covers Lower Thermal Budget processes for industrial efficiency and productivity.
Subtopic 2.1 covers advances in industrial and process drying.
Subtopic 2.2 covers thermal process intensification.
Topic 3 covers connected, flexible and efficient manufacturing facilities and energy systems.
Subtopic 3.1 covers medium-voltage power conditioning systems to enable grid-dispatchable and resilient manufacturing facilities.
Subtopic 3.2 covers high power to heat ratio, high efficiency combined heat and power.
Subtopic 3.3 covers verification and validation of combined heat and power and district energy.
Applicants should reference the DE-FOA-0001980 AMO 2019 FAQ Log for questions and answers pertaining to this funding opportunity announcement.
EERE Exchange is designed to enforce the deadlines specified in this funding opportunity announcement.
The Apply and Submit buttons will automatically disable at the defined submission deadlines.
Applicants experiencing technical difficulties with a submission prior to the deadline should contact the EERE Exchange helpdesk for assistance.
The EERE Exchange helpdesk and system administrators will assist applicants in resolving issues.

Applicants experiencing severe technical difficulties resulting in late submissions should contact the EERE Exchange helpdesk for assistance, including finalizing submission on behalf of and with the applicant concurrence.
Network traffic is at its heaviest during the final hours and minutes prior to submittal deadline.
Applicants who experience technical difficulties during the final hours or minutes and are unsuccessful in uploading documents will not be able to use this process.
source_title: DOE: CMEI eXCHANGE: Funding Opportunities

facts: The New Manufacturing Facilities Property Credit is a tax incentive for a manufacturer who has operated a manufacturing facility in Georgia for at least 3 years and who spends $800 million on a new manufacturing facility in the state.
Eligible manufacturers must have a number of full-time employees equal or exceed 1,800, though these jobs do not have to be new jobs to Georgia.
An application must be filed which a panel must approve to receive the benefit.
The benefit awarded is a credit against taxes equal to 6 percent of the cost of all qualified investment property purchased or acquired.
The total credit allowed under the program is $50 million.
The credit offsets income tax liability and any excess may be used to offset withholding taxes.
There is a 15-year carry forward of any unused tax credit.
For taxable years beginning on or after January 1, 2025, any credits generated but not used may be carried forward for 10 years.
There are different provisions available for certain high-impact aerospace defense projects.
The tax credit code for this incentive is 120.
source_title: New Manufacturing Facilities Property Credit | Department of Revenue

Import and export with ease in Georgia by tapping into an extensive transportation network anchored by partners like the Georgia Ports Authority and Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport (ATL).
Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport (ATL) is the most traveled and most efficient airport in the world.
Georgia products can reach 80% of Americans within a 2-day drive.
There are 4,600+ miles of rail and two Class 1 railroads, CSX and Norfolk Southern, creating the Southeast's most extensive rail system.
560,000+ tons of cargo moved annually via ATL.
The Port of Savannah has approximately 10,000 feet of contiguous berth space.
The Port of Savannah is America's single largest container terminal.
Georgia's diverse talent and regions provide what businesses need to succeed across various industries.
Select Georgia publishes annual industry publications covering top industries from aerospace and automotive manufacturing to life sciences and information technology.

facts: The Continental facility occupies approximately 89 acres of property in Culpeper, Virginia.
Continental has been manufacturing brake and suspension components for the automobile industry at this site since 1972.
The facility is bounded by Lovers Lane to the north, with US Highway 29 located north of Lovers Lane followed by a manufacturing facility and several residential neighborhoods.
Areas to the west consist of light industrial facilities, commercial facilities, and agricultural land.
A heavy rail line bounds the facility to the east, followed by agricultural land, a business park, and residential properties.
Agricultural land adjoins the facility grounds to the south.
Primary contaminants in groundwater include tetrachloroethylene, trichloroethylene and associated degradation compounds.
Secondary contaminants in groundwater include chromium.
No unacceptable risks to human health and the environment from soil have been identified during environmental investigations.
No impacts to air, surface water, or sediments were identified during environmental investigations.
On October 10, 2000, a Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA) Corrective Action Permit was issued to investigate Solid Waste Management Units (SWMUs).
In 2002, Continental began a RCRA Facility Investigation (RFI) and in 2003 began cleanup activities implemented via Interim Measures.
In October 2008, Continental received final DEQ approval for their RFI Report.
On September 16, 2004, DEQ EPA determined that contamination in groundwater and human exposures risk from contaminants were under control.
Continental submitted a final Corrective Measures Study (CMS) to the Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) in December 2012 evaluating potential corrective measures for groundwater.
The CMS presented results of a pilot test study evaluating the effectiveness of in-situ chemical oxidation using permanganate.
Based on the results, in situ chemical oxidation was selected as the proposed remedy and the CMS was approved by DEQ on May 13, 2013.
Continental Automotive Systems completed a class II permit modification to their Hazardous Waste Management Permit for Site-Wide Corrective Action on September 10, 2014.
The permit modification included selection of the facility’s final corrective action remedy and was followed by a 60-day public comment period between June 17 and August 20, 2014.
No comments were received during the public comment period, so the proposed remedy was selected as the final remedy for the site.

Continental’s final remedy consists of in situ chemical oxidation to reduce contaminants in groundwater, ongoing groundwater monitoring for remedial effectiveness, and institutional controls required by their permit.
The facility is currently implementing the final remedy.

On June 27, 2019, the Virginia Department of Environmental Quality (VDEQ) conducted a Long Term Stewardship assessment and determined that remedy institutional and engineering controls have been fully implemented with no control deficiencies identified.

Groundwater from beneath the property cannot be used for any purposes other than environmental testing unless it is demonstrated that such use does not pose an unacceptable risk to human health and the environment.
Residential land use is prohibited within the boundary of the former pretreatment lagoon and sludge drying beds (SWMUs Five and Six).
The facility must inspect and maintain the final cover for the former pretreatment lagoon (SWMU Six).

A vapor mitigation or control system must be installed in any new structure intended for occupation within 100 feet of contaminated groundwater unless it is demonstrated that it is not necessary to protect human health.
source_title: EPA RCRA ID: VAD030341077 | US EPA

The expansion involves a $14 million investment.
The investment will create 10 new jobs.
Denkai America, Inc. has been operating for more than 40 years.
The company is a leading manufacturer of high-quality electrodeposited copper foils for printed circuit board, industrial and energy storage applications.
The facility is located at 29 Battleship Extension Road in Camden.
The expansion will increase the company’s capability and capacity to meet growing demand for anticipated business growth into the energy storage market segment.
The project includes the modernization of the current manufacturing facility.
The project includes the construction of additional factory space.
Electrodeposited copper foil is a key component in lithium ion batteries which power electric vehicles.
Denkai America, Inc. is positioned to participate in the rapidly growing vehicle electrification market.
The expansion is expected to be completed by October 2022.
Denkai America, Inc. directs interested job applicants to visit the company’s careers webpage.
Denkai America, Inc. Chief Operating Officer Michael Coll stated the company is excited to revitalize its manufacturing facility and prepare for growth into new markets.
On behalf of CEO Dr. Nakajima, Michael Coll extended gratitude to the business development teams from the state of South Carolina and Kershaw County.
Michael Coll noted the investment is necessary for ensuring continued prosperity and the ability to create additional high-quality jobs in Camden.
Governor Henry McMaster congratulated Denkai America, Inc. on its latest expansion in Kershaw County and praised South Carolina’s workforce excellence in the manufacturing sector.
Secretary of Commerce Bobby Hitt stated the expansion is proof that manufacturing continues to attract jobs and capital investment in South Carolina.
Bobby Hitt noted the expansion will benefit Kershaw County and the state for years to come, highlighting that South Carolina is “Just right” for making quality products.
Kershaw County Council Chairman Julian Burns welcomed the expansion and jobs announcement, calling it great news for home-team high performers and proof that the community competes globally.

Central SC Alliance Chairman W. Keller Kissam congratulated Denkai America, Inc. and partners in Kershaw County, noting the expansion amplifies the company’s portfolio and career advancement opportunities for workers.
source_title: Denkai America, Inc. expanding operations in Kershaw County | S.C. Governor Henry McMaster

entity_name: U.S.-Japan Strategic Partnership under Genesis Mission
canonical_name: United States and Japan Strategic Partnership under President Trump’s Genesis Mission
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy and Japan’s Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology and Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry announced an historic $1 billion strategic partnership making Japan the first international partner in President Trump’s Genesis Mission.

Under the partnership, eleven joint scientific teams will unite twelve DOE National Laboratories, one DOE Office of Science User Facility, and twelve leading Japanese research institutions to advance breakthroughs in quantum information science, fusion energy, biotechnology, advanced materials, particle physics, and autonomous laboratory systems.

DOE Under Secretary for Science and Genesis Mission Lead Dr. Darío Gil stated that the partnership brings together two of the world’s great scientific powers to accelerate discovery and unlock breakthroughs that will shape the future.

MEXT Vice-Minister for Policy Coordination Dr. Yasuyoshi Kakita noted that under Japan’s Seventh Basic Plan for Science, Technology and Innovation, they are expanding investments in science and technology, recognizing AI and computing resources as essential to both research excellence and industrial competitiveness.

METI Vice Minister for International Affairs Mr. Takehiko Matsuo stated that Japan and the United States have built a complementary partnership that leverages each other’s strengths and has driven innovation in advanced fields.

Mr. Takehiko Matsuo further noted that Japan is strengthening its industrial base and expanding investments under the ‘Semiconductor and Digital Industry Strategy Initiatives’ and the ‘Budgetary Framework for Strengthening AI and Semiconductors’.

The collaboration builds on the U.S.-Japan Technology Prosperity Deal signed in 2025 and establishes a long-term framework for collaboration across government, academia, industry, philanthropic organizations, and research institutions in both countries.

Early projects include planned partnerships among RIKEN, the University of Tokyo, the National Institute for Materials Science (NIMS), and DOE National Laboratories to develop the next generation of autonomous laboratories powered by AI and robotics.

Additional planned collaborations involving KEK, the High Energy Accelerator Research Organization, RIKEN, J-PARC, the Japan Proton Accelerator Research Complex, DOE National Laboratories, and DOE user facilities will advance particle accelerator technologies.

The joint teams will have access to world class computing infrastructure—including the DOE’s high performance systems and Japan’s Fugaku—enabling unprecedented capabilities for AI driven research and scientific discovery.

Building on a joint Statement of Intent signed in January 2026, DOE and MEXT announced their plan to invest a combined $1 billion over five years—$500 million from each nation—to advance AI science and technology challenges and expand computing infrastructure needed to support next-generation research, subject to the availability of future appropriations.

The partnership advances the Genesis Mission’s goal to double the productivity and impact of American science and engineering within a decade by harnessing AI, advanced computing, and deep international collaboration to accelerate discovery.
source_title: United States and Japan Announce Historic $1 Billion Partnership Under President Trump’s Genesis Mission | Department of Energy

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), in cooperation with Glovis America, deployed Hyundai XCIENT heavy-duty hydrogen fuel-cell electric trucks for clean logistics operations in Ellabell, Ga.
Initially, a total of 21 XCIENT trucks will be in operation at the facility.

Oscar Kwon, HMGMA CEO, stated that the company is committed to creating sustainable transportation solutions and that the zero-emission hydrogen fuel-cell trucks offer a cleaner alternative to traditional freight logistics methods.
The deployment represents a significant step in HMGMA’s efforts to reduce its carbon footprint and actively promote sustainable practices in daily logistics operations.
The Hyundai XCIENT Class 8 heavy-duty trucks will transport vehicle parts from HMGMA suppliers across the region to the Megasite on a daily basis.
During the initial rollout, the XCIENT trucks will transport parts between suppliers and the on-site Consolidation Center before expanding to a broader network of suppliers.
The 21 XCIENT vehicles will represent more than one-third of Glovis America’s truck fleet at HMGMA.

Earlier this year at CES, Hyundai Motor Group announced HTWO, its new hydrogen value chain business brand encompassing businesses and affiliates across production, storage, transportation, and utilization.

Jim Park, SVP of commercial vehicle and hydrogen business development at Hyundai Motor North America, stated that HMGMA’s deployment of XCIENT for logistics is one of the main initiatives of HTWO and delivers on their strategy to showcase a closed-loop hydrogen logistics ecosystem.
HTWO Logistics, a joint venture between Hyundai Motor Company and Glovis America, will install a mobile hydrogen refueling station at the Megasite for efficient refueling.
A hydrogen production and refueling station is being developed for the Megasite, with details to be announced at a later date.
HMGMA is Hyundai Motor Group’s first dedicated electric vehicle mass-production plant, located in Bryan County, Ga.
The Georgia facility will feature a highly connected, automated, and flexible manufacturing system that organically connects all elements of the EV ecosystem.
All production processes at the facility—including order collection, procurement, logistics, and production—will be optimized utilizing AI and data.
The innovative manufacturing system aims to create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise with approximately 250,000 employees worldwide and mobility brands including Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
