facts: Norma Precision Ammunition, a subsidiary of RUAG Ammotec, established its U.S. headquarters, manufacturing site, and warehousing and distribution operations in Chatham County, Georgia.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the relocation on April 11, 2022.
The company is currently hiring for advanced manufacturing, distribution, management, and sales opportunities.
RUAG Ammotec operates in 12 different countries.
RUAG Ammotec develops and produces high-end ammunition for sports shooters, special operations forces, and peace officers worldwide.
The company serves key markets in the military, law enforcement, hunting, and sporting goods sectors.
In 2021, Norma Precision Ammunition imported over 400 containers of ammunition from RUAG Ammotec factories in Europe.
In 2021, the company delivered over 30 million cartridges of ammunition made in the U.S.
Norma Precision Ammunition has been manufacturing in the United States for over 10 years under the name RUAG Ammotec, USA.
The four years prior to the announcement were marked by steady growth.
Christoph Eisenhardt serves as President and CEO of RUAG Ammotec.
Paul Lemke serves as President of Norma Precision Ammunition.
The company's facility is located at 141 Prosperity Drive in Garden City, Georgia.
Trip Tollison serves as President and CEO of the Savannah Economic Development Authority.
Griff Lynch serves as Executive Director of the Georgia Ports Authority.
Pat Wilson serves as Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
According to National Shooting Sports Foundation data, Georgia has become one of the country's top 10 states for firearm manufacturing.
Georgia's GDP was $59.5 billion in 2020.
The Georgia Department of Labor estimated there were 392,508 manufacturing jobs in the state as of the third quarter of 2021.
RUAG Ammotec markets its product offerings under the Norma and Swiss P brands in the U.S.
The company brings Swiss, German, Hungarian and Swedish high-tech offerings to the U.S.
source_title: Norma Precision Ammunition Relocates its U.S. Headquarters to Georgia | Georgia

entity_name: Department of Energy OCED Industrial Demonstrations Program
facts: Federal Cost Share up to $61.7 million awarded to Roanoke Cement Company, LLC in Troutville, VA.
Roanoke Cement Company (RCC) leads the Calcined Clay Production for Limestone Calcined Clay Cement project.
RCC plans to demonstrate utilizing widely available clay types to minimize clinker use.
Clinker is identified as the most carbon-intensive component in cement production.
The project validates the market for calcined clays in a region with high demand.
Project anticipates reducing carbon intensity by approximately 83% compared to products with clinker.
Federal Cost Share up to $189 million awarded to Brimstone Commercial, LLC at TBD location.
Brimstone Commercial, LLC leads the Deeply Decarbonized Cement Project.
Project plans to construct a first-of-a-kind commercial-scale demonstration plant.
Plant will annually produce an estimated 103,000 metric tons of decarbonized OPC, supplementary cementitious materials, and smelter grade alumina.
Smelter grade alumina is identified as a critical mineral.
Project avoids more than 77,000 metric tons of carbon dioxide emissions per year using calcium silicate rocks and alternative industrial production methods.
Project builds on previously supported Advanced Research Projects Agency–Energy (ARPA-E) research pathways.
Project expects to reduce technical risks and demonstrate sufficient demand for future industrial-scale decarbonized facilities.
Federal Cost Share up to $86.9 million awarded to Sublime Systems in Holyoke, MA.
Sublime Systems leads the First Commercial Electrochemical Cement Manufacturing project.
Plans to build a new ultra-low-carbon cement manufacturing facility in Holyoke, Massachusetts.
Method replaces carbon-intensive limestone with abundant calcium silicate-based feedstock.
Produces industry-standard cement electrochemically instead of using high heat.
Builds on previously supported ARPA-E research pathways.
Aims to strengthen American supply chains for low-carbon products and catalyze industry-wide change.
Federal Cost Share up to $500 million awarded to National Cement Company of California, Inc. in Lebec, CA.
National Cement Company of California, Inc. (NCC-CA) leads the Lebec Net Zero Project.
Project plans to produce carbon-neutral cement at the Lebec, CA cement plant.

Deploys three strategies: using locally sourced biomass from agricultural byproducts like pistachio shells to replace fossil fuel reliance; producing LC3 using calcined clay; and capturing/sequestering remaining CO2 emissions estimated at 950,000 metric tons each year.
Aims to demonstrate how decarbonization levers drive emissions of existing U.S. cement production facilities to net-zero.
Federal Cost Share up to $500 million awarded to Heidelberg Materials US, Inc. in Mitchell, Indiana.
Heidelberg Materials leads the Mitchell Cement Plant Decarbonization Project.
Plans to construct and operate an integrated carbon capture, transport, and storage system at a newly modernized plant in Mitchell, Indiana.
Captures at least 95% of carbon dioxide from one of the largest cement plants in the nation.
Stores captured CO2 in a geologic formation beneath the plant property.
Expects to prevent two million tons of carbon dioxide per year from entering the atmosphere.
Demonstrates a pathway to decarbonize existing U.S. cement plants.
Builds on ongoing OCED awarded front-end engineering and design study and sequestration site development.
Represents one of the first carbon capture and storage projects for cement facilities in the nation.
DOE selection for award negotiations is not a commitment to issue an award or provide funding.
DOE and each selectee will negotiate a cooperative agreement, with funding provided only after negotiations complete and Contracting Officer executes the agreement.
DOE may cancel award negotiations and rescind selection before a funding agreement is executed.
Federal Cost Share up to $215.6 million awarded to Summit Materials, Inc. for the Low-Carbon Calcined Clay Cement Demonstration project.
Project locations include Port Deposit, Maryland; McIntyre, Georgia; Elmendorf, Texas; and Sulphur Springs, Texas.
Summit Materials leads the Low-Carbon Calcined Clay Cement Demonstration project.
Plans to construct four new calcination facilities in Maryland, Georgia, and Texas.
Demonstrates viability of displacing high-emitting limestone-based cement with a clay-based product in multiple geographies.
Range of sites builds case for replicability given diverse clay sources and availability of cementitious products across the U.S.
Scale offers potential for substantial emissions reductions, preventing 1.1 million metric tons of carbon emissions per year.
Addresses 2% of the U.S. 2030 projected demand for cement.
Expects to generate more than 4,000 direct, indirect, and induced jobs across the four sites during the project lifetime.
Summit plans to negotiate a Community Workforce Agreement or Project Labor Agreement for construction phases at each site.
Community engagement goals and strategies may evolve at each location to respond to community needs.
Plans to provide tailored workforce training investments, including manufacturing training certifications, engineering programs, and partnerships with local workforce and educational institutions.
source_title: Industrial Demonstrations Program Selected and Awarded Projects: Cement and Concrete | Department of Energy

facts: A Korean auto supplier will invest $10 million in Georgia.
The company plans to hire 285 people northeast of Atlanta.
The facility will manufacture parts that will accompany electric vehicle batteries.
SK Innovation will produce the accompanying electric vehicle batteries nearby.
source_title: Korean auto supplier to add 285 jobs near Georgia battery plant and more business news | Chattanooga Times Free Press

facts: Three businesses are bringing new locations to Right to Work Georgia.
The companies investing in Right to Work Georgia include Brightmark, Duckyang, and IC Biomedical.
These three businesses will create new jobs all over the state of Georgia.
source_title: Area Development Magazine Archives - Page 47 of 59 - National Right To Work Committee

facts: Duckyang recently announced it will invest $10 million in opening its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Jackson County.
Duckyang is an electric mobility parts supplier of automotive battery modules and energy storage systems to SK Battery America (SKBA).
Duckyang's new 230,000 sq. ft. facility will be located at 284 Broadway Ave. in Braselton.
The facility will create 285 jobs in the area.
Governor Brian Kemp made an announcement regarding the project on May 20.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Duckyang is creating high-paying, advanced automotive manufacturing jobs for the hardworking Georgians of Jackson County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia is home to the best workforce training programs in the nation and has remained the number one state for business for the past eight years.
Duckyang is headquartered in Ulsan, Korea.
Duckyang supplies cockpit modules to customers around the world including Hyundai, Kia, Renault Samsung Motors, Genesis and Mobis.
Cockpit modules are integrated auto parts comprised of more than 130 sub-components found near the driver’s seat of a vehicle.
Company leaders say that opening a facility in Georgia will allow Duckyang to better serve several of its automotive customers within the region.
Dong-in Son is the CEO of Duckyang Ind. Co. Ltd.

Dong-in Son stated that entering the U.S. electric vehicle battery market with SKBA will allow cooperation with many other automotive companies, including Hyundai and Kia Motors, that already have a manufacturing base in the U.S.

It is expected that Duckyang will have business expansion opportunities to supply not only electric vehicle parts but also interior parts, including the cockpit module, door trim panel module, and other flagship parts to global automakers based in the United States.
The company will be hiring for positions in production, maintenance, logistics and warehouse management.

Braselton Town Manager Jennifer Scott stated that the Town of Braselton looks forward to welcoming Duckyang to their community and is delighted to see a new manufacturing facility located in their town.
John Scott is the Director of Economic Development for the Jackson County Area Chamber of Commerce.

John Scott stated that as the automotive industry continues to move toward electric, great companies like Duckyang are helping Jackson County and the State of Georgia secure a place in the industry’s future.
Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce division on this project.
The GDEcD worked in partnership with the Jackson County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Quick Start and Georgia EMC on this project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia is focused on growing the entire electric mobility supply chain.
Pat Wilson noted that strong relationships with SK Battery America and other Korean companies, such as Duckyang, are helping grow the supply chain.
Pat Wilson highlighted that this project is an example of how they help companies bring their suppliers to the state in order to secure their local supply of needed parts.
source_title: Korean auto manufacturer to open facility in Braselton | News | mainstreetnews.com

facts: Duckyang Ind. Co., Ltd. was established in 1977.
The company's headquarters and main plant are located in Ulsan, Korea, which is considered the main industrial city of Korea.
Its second and third plants are located in Gyeongju and Yesan, Korea.
Duckyang’s annual sales amount is approximately $11 billion.
The company started its business by supplying crash pads, a plastic item covering the front automotive part on the dashboard for Hyundai Motor Company’s first-ever car, the Pony.
The Pony was the original model leading up to Hyundai Motor’s latest electric car, the IONIQ 5.
Duckyang currently manufactures cockpit modules, door trims, and crash pads as automotive interior components.
The cockpit module is the company’s best-selling item.
Cockpit modules are assembled to accommodate audio, air conditioner, airbag, etc., for a total of approximately 130 items in the front automotive parts around the driver’s seat.
This assembly process is known as ‘Modulization.’
Duckyang supplies these items for most of Hyundai Motor’s models manufactured in its Ulsan Plant, Korea.
Duckyang contributes to the Korean automotive export industry by supplying interior parts to exported Hyundai Motor models, known as indirect export.
The company developed components for EVs due to paradigm shifts in the auto industry from ICE cars to EVs.
These EV components include BMA (Battery Module Assembly) and ESS (Energy Storage System).
Regarding BMA, Duckyang was awarded as an R&D national organization hosted by KIAT (Korea Institute for Advancement of Technology).
Duckyang’s 2020 exports increased by 4% compared to the 2019 export amount despite challenging conditions caused by COVID-19.
Increasing demand for EVs is expected to further increase export amounts.
Duckyang has a separate R&D department with facilities located in Suwon and Gyeongju, Korea.
Through R&D on cockpit modules, Duckyang successfully focused on decreasing weight while ensuring top-class quality.
Duckyang received the 2021 National Quality Competitiveness Excellent Corporation award from the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy for eight consecutive years.
The company is establishing Duckyang America in the state of Georgia, USA, with SK Innovation for BMA and ESS business.
source_title: Automotive Interior Components – Korean-Machinery.com

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Duckyang, an electric mobility parts supplier of automotive battery modules and energy storage systems to SK Battery America (SKBA), will invest $10 million in opening its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Jackson County.
The new facility will create 285 jobs in Braselton, Georgia.
Governor Kemp stated: "It's great to see Duckyang create high-paying, advanced automotive manufacturing jobs for the hardworking Georgians of Jackson County."
Georgia has remained the number one state for business for the past eight years and is home to the best workforce training programs in the nation.
Duckyang is headquartered in Ulsan, Korea.
Duckyang supplies cockpit modules to customers around the world including Hyundai, Kia, Renault Samsung Motors, Genesis, and Mobis.
Cockpit modules are integrated auto parts comprised of more than 130 subcomponents found near the driver's seat of a vehicle.
By opening a facility in Georgia, Duckyang will be able to better serve several of its automotive customers within the region.

Dong-in Son, CEO of Duckyang Ind. Co. Ltd., stated: "By entering the U.S. electric vehicle battery market with SKBA, we will have the opportunity to cooperate with many other automotive companies, including Hyundai and Kia Motors, that already have a manufacturing base in the U.S."

Dong-in Son added: "It is expected that we will have business expansion opportunities to supply not only electric vehicle parts but also interior parts, including the cockpit module, door trim panel module, and other flagship parts to global automakers based in the United States."
Duckyang's new facility will be 230,000 square feet.
The facility will be located at 984 Broadway Avenue in Braselton.
The company will be hiring for positions in production, maintenance, logistics, and warehouse management.

Town Manager and Clerk of Braselton Jennifer Scott stated: "The Town of Braselton looks forward to welcoming Duckyang to our community and is delighted to see a new manufacturing facility located in our town."

John Scott, Director of Economic Development for the Jackson County Area Chamber of Commerce, stated: "As the automotive industry continues to move toward electric, great companies like Duckyang are helping Jackson County and the State of Georgia secure a place in the industry's future. We're very excited to welcome Duckyang to our community!"

Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development's (GDEcD) Global Commerce division on this project in partnership with the Jackson County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated: "In Georgia, we're focused on growing the entire electric mobility supply chain. Our strong relationships with SK Battery America and other Korean companies, such as Duckyang, are helping us do just that. This is also a great example of how we help our companies bring their suppliers to the state in order to secure their local supply of needed parts."

Pat Wilson added: "I appreciate our partners in Jackson County for their support of these projects and look forward to seeing the new opportunities this will create locally and for the entire U.S. mobility sector."
The State of Georgia's strong international relationships helped facilitate more than $1.3 billion in investment and more than 2,700 jobs from South Korean companies during 2020 alone.
Georgia remains a national leader in advanced manufacturing and continues to attract business from all over the world.
Duckyang Industrial was established in July 1977 and is the first company to develop and produce automobile cockpit modules in Korea.
Duckyang solidified its position in the automobile business through a strategic alliance with Visteon, an American global automotive supplier that was spun off from Ford in 1999.
Duckyang is continuously promoting research and development on new technologies to play a role as a leader in the global automotive market.
Duckyang plans to expand their production capabilities for interior automotive parts in addition to electronics in the future.

facts: Enchem America INC. is a subsidiary of South Korean-based Enchem Co., Ltd.
Enchem America INC. specializes in manufacturing electrolytes and high-functionality additives for rechargeable batteries.
Enchem America INC. was founded to develop essential components for electric vehicles and energy storage systems.
Enchem America INC. is headquartered in Commerce, Georgia.
Enchem America INC. has expanded operations to Tennessee.
The expansion to Tennessee aims to optimize supply chain management and meet market demand.
source_title: Enchem America INC. - Battery-Tech Network

facts: South Korean battery materials maker Enchem has scrapped its plan to invest $143 million to build a plant in the US.
The cancellation is due to a protracted downturn in the world’s third-largest electric vehicle market.
Enchem, the world’s No. 4 electrolyte manufacturer, canceled its plan to spend 200 billion won ($142.7 million) to construct a production base in Tennessee.
The planned Tennessee facility was intended to supply electrolytes to BlueOval SK LLC.
BlueOval SK LLC is a joint venture between South Korea’s battery maker SK On Co. and US automaker Ford Motor Co.
Supply deals for the Tennessee plant, which were expected to be signed last year, were repeatedly delayed.
Enchem’s decision coincides with Ford's EV business remaining sluggish, having lost $12 billion in total, including $2.2 billion in the first half of 2025.
The US administration eliminated the tax credit benefit of up to $7,500 for EV buyers starting this month.
US President Donald Trump’s policies are boosting internal combustion engine vehicles while hurting the EV industry.
Ford Chief Executive Officer Jim Farley stated that the elimination and softening of emissions rules will sharply curtail EV demand in the US.
Farley predicted the share of zero-emission vehicles, currently around 10% of the domestic market, could fall by half.
These policy changes are expected to affect not only the EV industry but also the battery sector.
BlueOval SK's total capacity is likely to rise to 127 gigawatt-hours (GWh) by next year.
BlueOval SK is predicted to underutilize its facilities due to the market conditions.
An industry source in Seoul stated that all companies involved in the local EV value chain with Ford are now revising their strategies.
Instead of proceeding with the Tennessee project, Enchem plans to focus its investments on its Georgia facility.
Enchem's Georgia facility supplies electrolytes to LG Energy Solution Ltd.
Enchem also operates an Indiana plant that serves Samsung SDI Co.
source_title: Korea’s battery materials maker Enchem scraps $143 mn US plant plan - KED Global

facts: Governor Brian Kemp announced Korean manufacturer Enchem Ltd. will build two new manufacturing plants in Jackson County.
The project will create more than 300 jobs.
The project involves an investment of $61.35 million.
Enchem is a Korean technology leader specializing in the development and manufacturing of electrolytes for rechargeable batteries and electrostatic double-layer capacitors (ELDCs).
Enchem is the first announced supplier for SK Innovation.
SK Innovation broke ground in Jackson County in March 2019 on its $1.67 billion battery manufacturing plant.
Enchem was founded in 2012.
Since its founding, Enchem has developed multiple new electrolytes and functional additives to improve and extend battery life.
The company continues to increase its sales to major rechargeable battery and EDLC manufacturers worldwide.
Enchem America CEO Oh Jung Kang said the company is excited to build their factory in Georgia.
Oh Jung Kang believes the new plant will surely benefit from its infrastructure, transportation, and excellent weather.
Governor Kemp said building on the success of SK Innovation in Jackson County, Enchem is exactly the type of quality project that Georgia hoped to attract when visiting Korea last June.
The announcement was made on a Wednesday.
Governor Kemp added that as the market for electric vehicles and batteries continues its rapid growth, it’s great to see the industry building a hub here in Georgia.
Jackson County Board of Commissioners Chairman Tom Crow said they are excited to welcome Enchem to Jackson County.
In fiscal year 2019, South Korea was a leader in both foreign direct investment and jobs in Georgia, investing $1.87 billion and creating 2,507 jobs.
source_title: Korean Manufacturer to Create More Than 300 Jobs in Jackson County - 92.1 WLHR

facts: Tennessee acknowledged $2 million in subsidies for Enchem America.
Enchem America plans to spend $152.5 to create electrolyte manufacturing operations in Brownsville.
The electrolytes are used in electric vehicle batteries.
The facility will be located at the I-40 Advantage Industrial Park.
The I-40 Advantage Industrial Park recently received a separate $1 million grant for access road construction and sewer infrastructure.
This subsidy joins the $884 million Ford Blue Oval City electric truck subsidy.
Tennessee has given $78 million in subsidies to the Ultium Cells plant in Spring Hill.
Ultium plans to employ a workforce of 1,700 in Maury County.
Magna announced plans to build three manufacturing facilities in Tennessee to supply Blue Oval City.
Two of Magna's planned facilities will be at the West Tennessee Megasite adjoining Ford.
The third Magna facility will be located in Lawrenceburg.
Subsidies for Magna have not yet been released.
The State Funding Board canceled its July meeting where it would normally approve those subsidies.
Tennessee gave 6K Energy Tennessee a $1.75 million incentive for its battery material manufacturing plant in Jackson.
6K Energy Tennessee received a $50 million federal Department of Energy grant for the $166 million plant.
Tennessee’s Department of Community and Economic Development continues to tout the subsidies as being necessary to bring the electric vehicle facilities to Tennessee.
Academic researchers question whether those subsidies actually impact the economy.
Research has shown that the subsidies benefit politicians in both political donations and votes more than they impact the economy.

University of Texas professor Nathan Jensen previously told The Center Square the main reason to give incentives to vehicle plants is because they tend to have lots of high-paying jobs and deep supply chains for other local companies that manufacture vehicle components.
It is not clear there are any benefits to giving subsidies for EV manufacturing.
States do things such as pay for infrastructure as megasites are built, and then also hand out subsidies when companies agree to occupy the megasite.
University of Texas professor Nathan Jensen stated that with EVs, these are not established companies sometimes and they have much shorter supply chains because EVs are simpler devices.
source_title: Enchem America to receive $2 million in incentives from Tennessee - Tennessee - The Black Chronicle

facts: Georgia has recruited Enchem, a South Korean company that makes parts for batteries used in electric cars.
Enchem will build two facilities in Jackson County to make electrolytes used in rechargeable batteries.
Gov. Brian Kemp’s office announced the project on Wednesday.
State officials describe the project as a $61 million investment.

facts: Enchem Co., Ltd. announced a strategic partnership with Elementium Materials, Inc. to accelerate commercial deployment of advanced battery electrolytes.
The collaboration combines state-of-the-art electrolyte manufacturing capabilities with breakthrough electrolyte innovation to advance next-gen EV batteries.
Elementium has developed industry-leading electrolyte technology that unlocks higher energy, higher power, and longer-lasting batteries with improved safety characteristics.

Elementium's formulations are engineered to address key performance and reliability challenges in lithium-ion systems, including fast charging limitations, durability under demanding operating conditions, and energy density constraints.
Enchem is a global electrolyte manufacturer with decades of experience in the development, qualification, and large-scale production of lithium-ion battery electrolytes.
The company focuses on high-voltage and high-capacity battery performance, safety, and stability.
Enchem operates major manufacturing facilities in Korea, China, Europe, and the United States.
With a global production capacity of approximately 600,000 tons per year, Enchem provides the manufacturing scale necessary to support rapid adoption across major automotive and battery cell markets.
Through this partnership, Elementium's advanced electrolyte technologies are being produced using Enchem's established global manufacturing and supply infrastructure.
Enchem has recently begun customer delivery of pilot-scale production samples of Elementium's electrolyte, marking an important milestone toward commercialization.

In parallel, Elementium and Enchem have begun negotiating a non-exclusive license of Elementium's technology to accelerate the development and commercialization of products for emerging electric vehicle applications.
This exciting partnership is expected to focus on joint validation programs with automotive OEMs and battery cell manufacturers, followed by phased commercial deployment.

Enchem will leverage its global footprint to enable localized blending and delivery of Elementium's next-generation electrolytes worldwide, supporting regional supply chains and improving responsiveness to customer manufacturing needs across North America, Europe, and Asia.

By combining Elementium's materials innovation with Enchem's manufacturing prowess and global footprint, the companies aim to deliver differentiated electrolyte solutions capable of supporting higher-performance electric vehicles while strengthening reliability and safety standards of next-generation batteries.

links.context: Enchem Co., Ltd. US Sales contact email

source_title: Enchem and Elementium Announce Strategic Partnership Accelerating Commercial Deployment of Advanced Battery Electrolytes - The Detroit Free Press

entity_name: Tennessee Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) from South Korea
canonical_name: Tennessee Department of Economic and Community Development (TNECD)
facts: Tennessee is home to nearly 1,050 foreign-based establishments that employ roughly 165,000 people across the state.

Much of Tennessee's FDI growth is attributed to the Tennessee Department of Economic and Community Development’s (TNECD) well-earned reputation for building relationships, understanding cultures, and solving problems.
Karel Abboud serves as global director of FDI and trade for TNECD.
TNECD employs a team of five in-country global FDI directors who recruit across 12 countries in Europe and Asia.
Since 2016, TNECD has landed nearly 20 projects by Korean-owned companies to create 4,600 new jobs and invest over $4.9 billion in capital.
South Korea ranks among the top 10 countries for FDI in Tennessee, much of which is invested in automotive operations.
According to fDi Markets, an online Financial Times product, Tennessee ranks second in the nation for job commitments from Korean companies over the last five years and fifth for capital investment.
Heejung Shin serves as TNECD’s in-country FDI representative in South Korea.
Shin states that key factors for building a long-term relationship with Koreans are sincerity, honesty, and consistency.
Shin's networking extends to lawyers, accountants, architects, and various association members in both South Korea and the U.S. to help companies expand successfully.
Shin emphasizes that Korean companies prioritize unwavering local-level support, such as permitting assistance during construction and ongoing operational support, over incentives alone.

Clarksville has become a thriving manufacturing cluster anchored by several South Korean companies including Hankook, LG Electronics, Shinhung Global, Hankook & Company, LG Chem, and Dongwha Electrolyte.
Hankook Tire located in Clarksville in 2013 and later assisted LG Electronics during its site search in 2015/2016, influencing LGE's decision to locate there as well.
Wonbae Kim is the COO and senior director of Dongwha Electrolyte.
Kim praises Shin for her agile approach, prompt follow-up, and commitment to consistency and transparency in client communication.
Korean companies have a strong presence in Tennessee's manufacturing sectors including automotive, electronics, chemicals, electric vehicles (EV), food, and biotechnology.
In 2022, LG Chem announced a $3.2 billion cathode materials plant in Montgomery County for EV batteries, creating 1,000 jobs.
In 2023, Dongwha Electrolyte USA announced a $70 million facility in Clarksville to produce electrolytes for EV lithium batteries, creating 68 jobs.
In 2023, Enchem America announced a $152 million electrolyte manufacturing plant in Brownsville, creating 190 new jobs.
In 2023, Hanon Systems announced a $170 million manufacturing facility in Loudon to produce thermal solutions for the EV industry, creating 600 jobs.
In 2024, Unitech North America announced a $30 million first U.S. facility in Morrison to produce thermal adhesives and battery compression pads for EV batteries, creating 75 jobs.
source_title: Tennessee Tops for FDI from Korea - Area Development

facts: Enchem America Inc. has announced plans to build a new electrolyte manufacturing facility in Brownsville, Tennessee.
The $152.5 million facility will be the company’s second manufacturing plant in the U.S.
The facility is expected to create 190 new jobs for the area.
The facility will be used to manufacture electrolytes for electric vehicle (EV) batteries.

Jung Kang Oh, CEO and founder of Enchem Co. Ltd., thanked Haywood County, the City of Brownsville, and all professionals that assisted Enchem in its journey as it expands its presence in North America.

Jung Kang Oh stated that the growth of the EV market in recent years has been truly remarkable and indicated that indications suggest this trend will continue at an accelerated pace over the coming decade.
Jung Kang Oh expressed confidence that Enchem is well positioned to further capitalize on the growth of the EV market.
Jung Kang Oh stated that Enchem looks forward to exploring new avenues of innovation and partnership opportunities with Haywood County and the City of Brownsville.
source_title: Electrolyte manufacturer to build $152.5 million facility in Tennessee | Plant Services

facts: F & P Georgia Manufacturing Inc. is a Tier 1 automotive parts supplier.
The company is based in Rome, Georgia.
A data breach affected approximately 12,132 individuals in the United States, including 22 Indiana residents.
An unauthorized actor gained access to the company's computer network between June 24, 2025, and July 2, 2025.
The unauthorized actor copied or viewed certain files during the breach.
Exposed personal information included Social Security numbers and driver's license information.
The breach was reported to the Indiana Attorney General and the Massachusetts Office of Consumer Affairs and Business Regulation starting on April 20, 2026.
Consumer notifications regarding the breach began on April 20, 2026.
Affected individuals are offered 12 months of free credit monitoring and identity restoration services.
The monitoring services include single-bureau credit monitoring, a credit report, and a credit score.
Enrolled individuals will receive same-day alerts when changes occur to their credit file.
Affected individuals can enroll by visiting Cyberscout's activation page and entering a unique code from their notification letter.
Enrollment must be completed within 90 days of the notification letter date.
A dedicated assistance line is available for additional questions.
The company's mailing address is 88 Enterprise Drive, Acworth, GA 30101.
source_title: F & P Georgia Data Breach Affects 12,132 Individuals

facts: F&P Georgia is a tier one automotive parts supplier located in Rome, Georgia, USA.
Its parent company is Ftech Inc., with its head office located in Saitama prefecture in Japan.
In North America, it operates as one of five subsidiaries.
Two North American subsidiaries are located in Ontario, Canada, in Tottenham and Stratford.
Two North American subsidiaries are located in the USA, in Troy, Ohio and Rome, Georgia.
Two North American subsidiaries are located in Mexico, in Queretaro and Irapuato.
Ftech currently has over 20 facilities throughout the globe.
The company is a world class, tier one automotive supplier with a global presence in the suspension component market.
Its goal is to produce high quality parts at competitive pricing and deliver them when and where customers require, without fail.
Safety and Environment are its number one priorities.
It relies on associate creativity, expertise, and teamwork to meet and exceed goals.
Customer satisfaction is supported through continuous improvement, perpetual training of associates, a never ending pursuit of new technologies, and a daily challenge to existing processes.
source_title: F & P Georgia Manufacturing Inc - Company Profile, Product Range, Locations & Major Industrial Contacts

The company employs approximately 20+ people.
Yuichi Fukuda is listed as a key contact for the facility.
Operating hours are Monday through Friday 24 hours, Saturday 6:00 AM to 2:30 AM, and closed on Sunday.

facts: Georgia’s pro-business environment is consistently recognized for its supply of development-ready sites, workforce development programs and strong logistics infrastructure.
Corporations have found Georgia’s low corporate tax rate and access to quality talent to be just a few of the benefits to establishing headquarters in the state.
Georgia smashed previous economic records – including for foreign direct investment – in fiscal year 2022, cementing itself as a leader in development across many thriving industries.

Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that from July 1, 2021, through June 30, 2022, investments and job creation were up again – reaching 94 percent and 53 percent above prior economic development records, respectively.
More than 51,000 jobs were created in Georgia from July 1, 2021, through June 30, 2022.
$21.2 billion was invested in expansions and new locations in Georgia during the period of July 1, 2021, through June 30, 2022.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development runs the “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) Certification Program to offer businesses a portfolio of pre-qualified industrial sites with completed due diligence.
Companies including Amazon, Facebook/Meta, Starbucks and Qcells have benefited from choosing GRAD Certified properties in Georgia.
Georgia has a labor force of 5.3 million strong.
Georgia Quick Start is the nation’s number one workforce training program and has trained over one million employees for companies expanding or locating in the state.

The Georgia Quick Start program began more than 55 years ago and provides tailored training for specific industries or companies in conjunction with the University System of Georgia and the Technical College System of Georgia.
Underemployed workers in other fields can undergo upskilling training through the Georgia Quick Start program.
More than 132,000 degrees and certificates are awarded to graduates from public and private universities in Georgia each year.
According to U.S. News & World Report, Georgia is home to two of the nation’s top 20 public universities: the Georgia Institute of Technology and the University of Georgia.
Georgia is home to two of the nation’s top five HBCUs: Spelman College and Morehouse College.
Georgia Tech graduates the most female and underrepresented minority engineers of any college in the nation.
Georgia’s HOPE Scholarship is the first merit-based scholarship in the U.S., providing tuition assistance to recent high school graduates and those pursuing higher education later in their careers.
Georgia has the fifth-largest military population in the country, with almost 700,000 former servicemembers and over 101,000 military retirees currently living in the state.
In April 2022, Governor Kemp created the first military retirement income tax exemption in state history to retain retiring military servicemembers in Georgia's workforce.
The state’s corporate tax rate sits at 5.75 percent, and new and expanding companies can earn Job Tax Credits for creating new jobs in Georgia.
Atlanta ranks third among U.S. cities for the number of Fortune 500 headquarters locations.
Georgia is home to 18 of the country’s top 500 companies and 33 of the top 1,000.
Georgia continues to invest in targeted industries including advanced manufacturing, electric vehicles and logistics.
In July 2022, copper foil manufacturer Denkai America announced it will locate a new manufacturing facility and North American headquarters in Augusta.
Denkai America’s materials are used in the lithium-ion batteries that power electric vehicles.
Denkai America plans to invest a total of $430 million over the next five years.
Denkai America plans to create 250 new jobs over the next five years.
Georgia serves as the North American headquarters for many advanced manufacturing companies due to its strong logistics network and skilled workforce.
Georgia outpaces the rest of the nation in 10-year GDP growth, particularly in machinery, electrical equipment and components, and fabricated metals manufacturing.
Success in several manufacturing sectors resulted in a GDP of $64.9 billion for Georgia in 2021.
The Georgia Department of Labor estimated there were 411,746 manufacturing jobs in the state in the second quarter of 2022.

Georgia has welcomed recent investments in advanced manufacturing industries including electric vehicle batteries, electric vertical aircraft, high-end ammunition, clothing, furniture and building materials.
Manufacturers can access the Georgia Center of Innovation’s expert manufacturing team at no cost to assist with market expansion, product development, and strategic partnerships.
Georgia has been an established automotive manufacturing center since 1909, when the first automobile was assembled in the state.
In 2021, more than 4,000 EVs were sold in Georgia, representing a total annual growth of 20 percent.
As of September 2022, more than 42,500 electric vehicles were registered in Georgia.
Dozens of EV-related projects have popped up all over the state since 2020.
Rivian is planning to invest over $5 billion into EV manufacturing in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group is planning to invest over $5 billion into EV manufacturing in Georgia.
SK Battery America is investing at least $2.6 billion in two battery manufacturing facilities in Georgia.
Korean lithium-ion battery recycler SungEel HiTech announced its first U.S. recycling facility in Georgia.
Joon Georgia, an automotive parts manufacturer, was announced as the first confirmed supplier for the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Georgia provides a seamless system of connected ocean, rail, truck and air transportation to support burgeoning industries like EVs.
Atlanta’s Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport is known as the busiest and most efficient airport in the world.
Georgia’s three inland ports provide regions across the state with direct paths to the Port of Savannah and Brunswick.
The state has 5,000 miles of rail, creating the most extensive rail system and largest intermodal hub in the Southeast.
source_title: Georgia: Corporate Headquarters, Development-Ready Sites Foster Continued Wave of Development | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Pratt & Whitney is investing $200 million to expand its manufacturing presence in Columbus, Georgia.
The investment aims to bolster production capacity for commercial and military engine programs.
The expansion centers on adding a seventh isothermal forging press at the Columbus Forge facility.
Operations for the new forging press are slated to begin in 2028.
The new press is projected to increase the output of critical engine components, specifically rotating compressor and turbine disks, by 30 percent.
Increased capacity will directly support production of the geared turbofan engine and the F135 engine powering the Lockheed Martin F-35 Lightning II fighter.
Shane Eddy, president of Pratt & Whitney, stated that the company has invested more than $1 billion in its Columbus facility since 2008.
Eddy noted the latest investment will increase output of critical parts for growing military and commercial engine programs and underscores a commitment to ramp industrial capacity.
Pratt & Whitney recently completed a $70 million, 81,000-square-foot expansion of its Columbus Engine Center.

The Columbus Engine Center expansion focused on enhancing maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) operations for geared turbofan (GTF) engines using advanced equipment aligned with Industry 4.0 strategy.
The Columbus Engine Center expansion increased the facility’s annual capacity by over 25 percent and added critical overhaul volume to the GTF MRO network.
A previously announced $206 million initiative in 2023 also focused on expanding GTF engine manufacturing and overhaul operations.
The 2023 investment included facility expansions, new equipment, and an anticipated creation of 400 new jobs by 2028.
Pratt & Whitney’s Columbus site is located approximately 90 miles south of Atlanta.
The Columbus site comprises both the Columbus Engine Center and Columbus Forge operating on the same campus.
The Columbus facility supports GTF engines for the Airbus A320neo, Airbus A220, and Embraer E-Jets E2.
The Columbus facility also supports engines for the Boeing C-17 and McDonnell Douglas F-15/Lockheed Martin F-16.
source_title: Pratt & Whitney Invests $200M to Expand Georgia Manufacturing & Boost Engine Production - News Usa Today

facts: Kia Georgia announced the start of 2027 Telluride production in West Point.
The milestone marks the plant's 5 millionth vehicle built since mass production began on November 16, 2009.
The 2027 Telluride is the first hybrid electric vehicle assembled in the state of Georgia.
It also marks Kia's first hybrid model produced in the United States.
The West Point facility is located on approximately 2,200 acres with an annual production capacity of up to 350,000 units.
The plant currently builds the Telluride, Sorento, and Sportage (excluding their HEV and PHEV versions).
The plant also builds the all-electric EV6 and EV9.
Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp attended the ceremony underscoring the event's regional significance.
Since its debut in 2019, the Telluride has been exclusively Georgia-built and has become a flagship SUV for the brand in North America.
In certain years, U.S. sales of the Telluride have exceeded 90,000 to 100,000 units.
The launch of hybrid production reflects Kia's global electrification strategy and expansion of its electrified vehicle lineup.
Localizing hybrid assembly in the United States signals a shift of key technologies closer to one of Kia's most important markets.
Reaching the five-million-vehicle milestone took less than two decades, highlighting sustained demand for Georgia-built vehicles.
Company leadership attributes this achievement to the plant's workforce and its ability to produce gasoline-powered models, electric vehicles, and now hybrids within the same facility.
The start of 2027 Telluride hybrid production marks the beginning of Kia's hybrid manufacturing era in the United States.
source_title: Kia Georgia Starts 2027 Telluride Hybrid | REVBUZZ

facts: The rise of electric vehicles (EVs) and lithium-ion battery manufacturing is reshaping the economic landscape of the U.S. South.
Attracting a majority of investments are the states of Georgia, South Carolina, North Carolina, Kentucky, and Tennessee.
Between 2018 and 2024 alone, the South attracted $428 billion in clean energy investments.

With billions in investments flowing into the region, the South is rapidly transforming into a powerhouse for advanced battery production, electric vehicle assembly, and the broader e-mobility supply chain.
The now-paused Inflation Reduction Act and Bipartisan Infrastructure Law fueled the capital flow of clean energy investments.
State-level policies also played an outsized role in attracting battery & EV manufacturers into the region.

On top of the federally-funded National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) program, Battery Belt states are expanding fast-charging networks along priority interstates and highways to ease range concerns.
Georgia partnered with EVgo and Tesla’s Supercharger network to expand high-speed charging corridors.
This infrastructure isn’t just for consumer EVs, but is also crucial for electric pickups and heavy-duty vehicles that rely on high-powered charging for efficiency.
Readily available industrial mega-sites provided factories with close proximity to railways, ports, more affordable utilities, and a skilled workforce.
Established logistical networks, abundant land, and lucrative financial incentives also contributed to major companies, such as Toyota and Nissan, to migrate South.
More than a manufacturing hub, the Battery Belt represents the future of mobility and transportation.
The trifecta of manufacturing, use, and end-of-life solutions for the modern automotive industry co-existing in the same region will ensure synergies for all players in the Battery Belt.
To ensure long-term sustainability for the EV industry, a mix of advanced technology investments, supply chain resiliency, and continuity in clean energy policies would be critical.
source_title: NEU Battery Materials

facts: Georgia achieved a record fiscal year for 2022 in economic development investments and job creation.
Governor Brian P. Kemp and GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson announced that Georgia achieved another record year for economic development investments and job creation during fiscal year 2022.
Investments and job creation were 94 percent and 53 percent above prior economic development records, respectively.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated his administration prioritized bringing jobs and investment to every corner of the state and highlighted Georgia’s business-friendly environment, superior logistics network, top-ranked workforce training program, and pursuit of being the best state for Americans to live, work, and raise a family.
Investments in expansions and new locations totaled more than $21.2 billion.
51,132 jobs were created in the state through 358 projects supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce team between July 1, 2021, and June 30, 2022.
Excluding the two largest projects in state history – Rivian and Hyundai Motor Group – Georgia jobs and investments for the year totaled 35,532 jobs and $10.7 billion, respectively.
Rivian and Hyundai Motor Group were two of 12 automotive projects that created a total of nearly 16,000 new jobs across the state.
Eighty-five percent of the investments and more than 30,000 new jobs came from outside the 10-county metro Atlanta region.
Companies choosing to expand in Georgia accounted for 74 percent of total projects.
New locations created 33,846 jobs across the state.
Advanced manufacturing added more than 10,000 new jobs in the state.
Both advanced manufacturing and automotive industries include manufacturers and suppliers for the electric vehicle (EV) industry.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s (HMGMA) development also includes EV battery manufacturing along with affiliate suppliers on-site.
Component recyclers Aurubis and Ascend Elements have joined HMGMA as part of Georgia’s efforts to build a sustainable, reliable supply chain for its electric mobility ecosystem.

Lt. Governor Geoff Duncan expressed gratitude for prioritizing high-quality jobs and strengthening Georgia’s workforce to cement its reputation as the best state for business and achieve the goal of being the technology capital of the East Coast.
Speaker David Ralston attributed Georgia’s success to strong public-private partnerships, lowering the tax burden on businesses, and incentivizing workforce development and investment.

The logistics and distribution industry remains an economic engine for Georgia due to access to Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport, the Port of Savannah, and a robust network of highways and rail lines.
Foreign direct investment (FDI) accounted for over $8 billion in investments created in fiscal year 2022.
The top five foreign direct investment sources were South Korea, Germany, Japan, France, and the Netherlands.
Korea-based projects represented the highest job creation and investment from a single country, with Germany producing the second most jobs and investments after Korea.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson led economic development missions to the United Kingdom, South Korea, and Germany in FY22.
Meetings were held with economic development drivers like Hyundai Motor Group and SK On to discuss new opportunities for development.
Discussions occurred regarding new opportunities for development with the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia and the Liverpool City Region Freeport.
GDEcD offers “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) certification, which recognizes pre-qualified industrial sites for which due diligence has been completed prior to a prospect visit.
Georgia has certified more than 60 industrial sites as ready for fast-track industrial projects through partnerships with property owners, development authorities, or economic development offices.
Georgia’s Quick Start program provides specialized workforce training tailored specifically to business needs and has operated for 55 years.
Since Governor Brian P. Kemp took office, more than 119,963 new jobs and over $42.9 billion in investment have been announced in communities across the state.

Seventy-seven percent of state economic development projects, 81 percent of investments, and 57 percent of jobs since Kemp took office have gone to communities outside the 10-county metro Atlanta area.

links.context: Details of state-announced economic development projects

source_title: Georgia achieves economic record for 2022

canonical_name: Georgia Israel Business Alliance (GIBA) / Conexx Ecosystem
facts: Cumulative Georgia-Israel merchandise trade tracked by GDEcD since 1996 totals $5.4B.
Georgia exported $374M in manufacturing goods to Israel in 2024.
More than 50 Israeli-affiliated companies operate in Georgia, with Atlanta ranking as a top-five U.S. center for Israeli company activity.
Georgia ranks 10th among U.S. states in exports to Israel.
Georgia firms have received approximately $615M in U.S. Foreign Military Financing dollars routed to IDF contracts since 1996.
Conexx operated for 33 years (1992-2025), facilitating over $1B in completed transactions and serving 140+ Israeli companies before voting to wind down in late 2024.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development opened its formal Israel office in 1994, one of the longest-running U.S. state economic-development presences in Israel, currently led by Sima Amir.

In 2018, Southern Company signed an MoU with the Israel Innovation Authority establishing a joint R&D funding program covering vehicle automation, advanced materials, sustainable energy, public safety, smart homes, and distributed energy.
The Year 1 contribution for the Southern Company-Israel Innovation Authority joint R&D program was $1M, matched by the IIA.

Atlanta-headquartered Coca-Cola operated The Bridge startup accelerator from Tel Aviv alongside a sister BridgeCommunity program in Atlanta, serving as a consistent corporate gateway for Israeli technology into the enterprise ecosystem.
Israel ranks first globally on the Anthropic AI Usage Index at 4.9x the global baseline, per the April 2026 5W & Louder study "Claude in Israel."

The Georgia Israel Business Alliance (GIBA) launched in 2025 as the institutional successor to Conexx, incubated by Atlanta's Zalik Foundation, with a program calendar including trade missions, networking events, and sector-specific partnerships across defense, energy, cyber, and innovation.

Hartsfield-Jackson Airport is consistently the busiest passenger airport in the world, positioning Atlanta as the de facto capital of the U.S. Southeast for finance, media, logistics, and Fortune 500 corporate presence.

Governor Kemp has been a consistent public advocate for Georgia-Israel ties, with predecessors Barnes, Perdue, and Deal all leading economic missions to Israel, establishing a bipartisan and durable relationship across administrations.
David Zalik chairs the Georgia Solidarity Network and was the driving force behind Delta's restoration of the ATL-TLV route, while his foundation incubated GIBA.

Delta Air Lines announced in October 2025 plans to resume three-times-weekly nonstop Atlanta-Tel Aviv flights on the Airbus A350-900 with a launch date of April 15, 2026, though service was suspended through August 4-5, 2026 due to regional conflict escalation.

Governor Kemp publicly stated regarding Delta's flight restoration: "This flight is important to so many in our state, further strengthening the cultural and economic ties between Georgia and Israel."
Atlanta's enterprise customer base includes Truist, Aflac, Equifax, Home Depot, and UPS, forming a deep market for Israeli innovation in cybersecurity, fintech, logistics, and AI.

Ronn Torossian, Founder and Chairman of 5W, noted that Israeli founders increasingly recognize Atlanta as a welcoming, well-built U.S. base with thirty years of formal economic representation and serious philanthropic backing.

A comprehensive USIBA-style economic-impact study for Georgia has not yet been published, though triangulation against Florida's data suggests Georgia operates in the same tier as states reporting up to $19.5B in total output.
The 2018 Southern Company-Israel Innovation Authority R&D framework remains active, continuing to support collaborative projects in energy and advanced technology.

Conexx originally operated as the American-Israel Chamber of Commerce Southeast Division before evolving into a primary business organization connecting Israel with Georgia and the broader Southeast over three decades.
source_title: Israeli Tech in Georgia: 33 Years, 50+ Companies, $5.4B in Bilateral Trade | 5W Research Report 2026

facts: F&P Georgia announced a $22 million expansion to its Rome facility due to contracts with automakers Honda and Tesla.
F&P’s facility is located on 51 acres of the Floyd County Industrial Park in Rome.
F&P operates as a tier one automotive supplier.
The Rome plant expansion follows a series of previous expansions at the company’s facility.
International automotive parts company Hwashin plans to open a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Laurens County.
The Hwashin facility will create around 460 jobs.
The project brings more than $176 million investment to Laurens County.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp’s Office announced the Hwashin details via a news release.
The Georgia Environmental Finance Authority (GEFA) and a coalition of partners announced a $250 million grant from the Department of Energy on Oct. 18.
The funding supports the Grid Resilience and Innovation Partnerships (GRIP) program.
The GRIP program aims to enhance the resiliency and flexibility of the electric grid.
GRIP is a product of the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law signed by President Biden in Nov. 2021.
The program seeks to ensure the reliability of the power sector’s infrastructure so all communities have access to affordable, reliable, clean electricity.
Qcells operates a solar panel manufacturing facility in Dalton, Georgia.
Qcells donated a total of $64,000 to local chapters of the Boys & Girls Clubs in Georgia.
The Dalton and Cartersville locations each received $32,000 from the donation.

Gov. Brian Kemp urged Georgia’s congressional delegation to support a request by the Georgia Ports Authority (GPA) for a study of the economic and environmental impacts of another deepening of Savannah Harbor.
The ports authority is asking Congress to approve the study as part of the Water Resources Development Act federal lawmakers are due to consider next year.
source_title: Georgia Trend Daily - Nov. 1, 2023 - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: The Georgia Manufacturing Alliance (GMA) partnered with UK-based Trans-Global Events to host the Greener Manufacturing Show on June 8-9, 2022.
The event took place at the Cobb Galleria in Atlanta, GA, and is described as North America's leading event for environmental and sustainable manufacturing solutions.

The Greener Manufacturing Show brought together more than 400 sustainability leaders from various industrial sectors to explore industry innovations, market trends, regulatory updates, and optimizing manufacturing processes.
The event showcased the latest R&D in green materials, green chemicals, and greener manufacturing facilities of the future.

Three dedicated conference tracks targeted sustainability priority areas for manufacturing companies, including innovations in sustainable materials, new greener chemicals, targeting net-zero operations, and tackling waste plastic and other valuable resources.
Delegates had access to all three conferences as well as post-conference proceedings.
Several Georgia Manufacturing Alliance members exhibited at the event.
Stuart Countess, President & Chief Executive Officer of Kia Georgia, Inc., spoke on June 8 at 9:25 am.

The event targeted sustainability-focused companies looking to design and manufacture products from more sustainable materials, limit and eliminate toxic chemicals, and reduce the environmental impact of industrial processes.

Jason Moss, CEO of the Georgia Manufacturing Alliance, stated they were excited to co-host the event to showcase sustainability success stories in the Georgia manufacturing community and allow industry leaders to network and learn best practices.
The exhibit hall was free but required registration, while conference tickets were required for educational sessions.
Conference attendees gained access to more than 80 speakers and all three conference sessions alongside the co-located Plastic Waste Free World conference.
GMA members were eligible for special conference pricing.

The Georgia Manufacturing Alliance is a membership-based industry organization founded in 2008 to support Georgia's manufacturing community through monthly plant tours, educational sessions, tradeshows, and networking opportunities.

source_title: The Georgia Manufacturing Alliance partners with The Greener Manufacturing Show in Atlanta June 8-9 2022 - Manufacturing News Today - EIN Presswire

facts: The global automotive metal stamping market was valued at $116.79 billion in 2024 and is expected to reach $176.26 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 7.1%.

Automotive metal stamping uses dies, presses, and specialized machinery to shape and cut metal sheets into precise parts like body panels, brackets, and structural components for the automotive industry.
Global electric vehicle sales exceeded 11 million in 2022, with a forecasted 35% growth in 2023 to reach 14 million units.

The development of high-strength alloys, including advanced high-strength steels (AHSS) and aluminum-based composites, is enhancing automotive metal stamping by providing stronger, lighter, and more durable materials for vehicle components.
Raw material price fluctuations for steel, aluminum, and copper significantly impact production costs and supply chain stability in the automotive metal stamping industry.

Rising demand for lightweight vehicles to improve fuel efficiency and reduce carbon emissions is driving automakers to use stamped aluminum, high-strength steel, and advanced alloys for strong yet lightweight components.

The automotive metal stamping industry faces challenges from a limited availability of skilled labor needed to operate, maintain, and optimize advanced technologies like automation, robotics, and precision stamping.
The COVID-19 pandemic disrupted global supply chains for the industry, causing shortages in raw materials like steel and aluminum, factory closures, labor shortages, and production delays.

Hot stamping is a key innovation that involves heating steel sheets to high temperatures before pressing them into precise shapes, resulting in lightweight, high-strength components ideal for automotive applications.

The commercial vehicles segment is expected to have the highest CAGR due to growing demand for heavy-duty, fuel-efficient, and safer vehicles requiring optimized metal stamping techniques and materials like AHSS and aluminum alloys.

North America is poised to hold the largest market share as increasing EV demand shifts automakers toward lighter, more durable materials for specialized parts such as battery enclosures, chassis components, and electric motor housings.

Electric vehicles require specialized parts like battery enclosures, chassis components, and electric motor housings that are often produced through advanced metal stamping processes to be lightweight yet strong.
The shift towards EVs encourages innovation in manufacturing techniques, fostering advancements in automation and precision stamping within the region.

Asia Pacific is estimated to witness the highest CAGR as countries like China, India, and Southeast Asian nations ramp up automotive manufacturing capabilities and demand for high-quality, precision metal stamped components increases.

Key players in the automotive metal stamping market include BASF SE, Calsonic Kansei Corporation, Hella GmbH & Co. KGaA, Magna International Inc, Martinrea International Inc, Nippon Steel Corporation, Schaeffler Group, Shiloh Industries, Inc, Tata AutoComp Systems Ltd, and Toyota Boshoku Corporation.
In January 2024, GSC Steel Stamping LLC acquired the assets of Dixien LLC to maintain its legacy in automotive parts stamping.

In September 2022, General Motors Co. announced a $491 million investment to boost production of steel and aluminum stamped parts for future products, including electric vehicles, at its Marion metal stamping facility in Indiana, United States.

facts: Honda Motor Co., Ltd. has announced plans to consolidate Astemo, Ltd. as a fully owned subsidiary by the end of the third quarter of fiscal years 2026/2027.
The move follows Honda’s agreement to acquire an additional 21% stake in Astemo from Hitachi, Ltd.
This acquisition increases Honda's ownership in Astemo from 40% to 61%.
The remaining shares will be held by JIC Capital, Ltd. at 20%, and Hitachi at 19%.
The transaction is expected to be finalized during the first quarter of the fiscal year beginning April 1, 2026, pending regulatory approvals.
Astemo was formed in 2021 through the integration of four companies.
Astemo has been a key player in automotive technology, combining hardware and software capabilities.
The company has been actively developing solutions for software-defined vehicles (SDVs), a trend expected to reshape the automotive industry.
Honda emphasized that Astemo’s growth is critical to enhancing its SDV development capabilities and maintaining cost competitiveness in a rapidly evolving market.
Under the new ownership structure, Astemo aims to accelerate its transformation into a global supplier of integrated mobility solutions.
Astemo's focus areas include electrification, AI, and cloud-based technologies.
The company remains open to an initial public offering (IPO) in the future.

facts: Astemo Americas is undergoing significant changes, introducing new mobility technologies, expanding its North American footprint, and implementing brand updates.
The company is part of the global Astemo Group, a major automotive supplier.
Astemo is accelerating its investments in advanced mobility to solidify its leadership in an industry increasingly defined by software.
The company is introducing next-generation technologies in autonomous driving, electrification, chassis, suspension, steering, and braking to its North American customers.
Astemo plans to expand its headquarters in Farmington Hills, Michigan, to support its growth.
Key innovations include an AI-based multi-camera system for AD/ADAS, an AI-powered full suspension system, and new “by-wire” steering and braking systems.
The company was formerly known as Hitachi Astemo Americas and has officially been renamed Astemo Americas to align with its global parent company.
Astemo is strategically positioning itself as a leader in electrification and mobility intelligence.
The company is increasing its investment in key areas such as electrification, advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), autonomous driving, and advanced chassis technology.
This summer, Astemo will showcase a wide range of new mobility solutions to its major OEM customers across the Americas through a series of presentations, displays, and vehicle test drives.
The company has announced plans to move its Farmington Hills, Michigan, technical center and headquarters to a new, larger location in the near future.
Astemo is previewing several cutting-edge systems for automakers that are designed for future vehicle models.
The company has developed automated driving solutions that use a multi-camera system with proprietary AI-based stereo vision.
This technology generates a 360-degree point cloud that is denser than conventional LiDAR and includes color and brightness information.
A new full suspension system utilizes AI-powered control logic and deep-learning algorithms to improve ride and handling at a lower cost than comparable systems.
The suspension system is equipped with a “preview” camera that can anticipate and prepare for bumps and potholes in the road.
Astemo’s latest steer-by-wire technology replaces the traditional steering wheel with a handheld device, representing a key step toward autonomous driving.
The company is making its North American debut with the “Smart Brake,” an electro-mechanical brake-by-wire system that reduces the lag time between driver input and brake response.
By eliminating the traditional hydraulic connection, the Smart Brake simplifies vehicle assembly, helps extend battery range, and reduces maintenance by removing the need for brake fluid.
The name Astemo is derived from “Advanced Sustainable Technologies for Mobility,” highlighting the company’s commitment to developing cutting-edge and sustainable solutions.
The company's new branding message is “Astemo, Mobility Beyond – From the Core of Mobility, Freedom.”
Astemo operates as a joint venture between Hitachi, Ltd., Honda Motor Co., and JIC Capital, Ltd.
The company employs 80,000 people worldwide.
Over 14,000 of Astemo's employees are located in the Americas.

facts: Lithium-ion batteries provide higher charging efficiency and no memory effect, giving them an advantage over nickel-hydrogen batteries.

The batteries were adopted as the battery for eco-friendly cars due to distinct features for better charging efficiency than the Nickel metal hydride battery and memory-effect-free performance.
In actual application for vehicles, many cells need to be combined as a module to form a battery pack with additional control circuit to monitor voltage or temperature for safety use.
Batteries require reliability under severe conditions and high safety level in case of crash or accident.

facts: Foresight Autonomous Holdings Ltd. announced signing an agreement with Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. to test and evaluate autonomous-driving vision systems (ADAS).
Hitachi Astemo is a global Tier One supplier of engine management, electric powertrain and integrated vehicle-control systems for the auto industry.
The paid joint proof of concept project with Hitachi Astemo calls for evaluating and testing simulated and real-life autonomous driving scenarios.
The program pairs Foresight QuadSight® and Mono2Stereo™ technologies with existing Hitachi Astemo camera systems to improve distance measurement and object detection.
This project follows the successful evaluation of a QuadSight vision system prototype reported by Foresight in March 2021.

Foresight’s proprietary Mono2Stereo software-based solution uses overlapping views of existing cameras with different fields of vision to create a three-dimensional stereo-vision system for improved object detection.

Haim Siboni, CEO of Foresight, stated that being chosen by Hitachi Astemo underlines the importance of stereoscopic technology in enhancing ADAS systems, amplifying performance, distance accuracy, and active safety features.
Foresight's solutions can be deployed in vehicles equipped with Level 2 and Level 2-plus autonomy systems without requiring additional hardware and design changes.
Haim Siboni believes teaming up with Hitachi Astemo could potentially lead to further collaboration with other automotive OEMs.

Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain, and integrated vehicle control for major automotive manufacturers worldwide while adhering to ISO/TS:16949:2009 industry standards.
Foresight Autonomous Holdings Ltd. is a technology company developing smart multi-spectral vision software solutions and cellular-based applications.

Through wholly owned subsidiaries Foresight Automotive Ltd., Foresight Changzhou Automotive Ltd. and Eye-Net Mobile Ltd., Foresight develops “in-line-of-sight” vision systems and “beyond-line-of-sight” accident-prevention solutions.

Foresight’s vision solutions include modules of automatic calibration and dense three-dimensional (3D) point cloud applicable to automotive, defense, autonomous vehicles and heavy industrial equipment markets.
Eye-Net Mobile’s cellular-based solution suite provides real-time pre-collision alerts to enhance road safety using AI technology and advanced analytics.

facts: Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. and Light have begun "proof of concept" development of a forward-facing perception system for use with advanced driver assistance systems.
The two companies will begin to field test the new multi-view, camera-based sensing technology later in July 2021.
Light's Clarity™ systems integrate seamlessly into Hitachi Astemo's advanced vehicle assistance systems (ADAS) to work directly with other ADAS and ECU (engine control unit) applications.
John Nunneley serves as senior vice president of Design Engineering at Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc.
Hitachi Astemo Americas aims to create the next generation of leading ADAS and autonomous driving solutions in collaboration with Light.
Clarity is an industry-first multi-view perception platform using cameras designed to see any 3D structures in the road from 10 centimeters to 1,000 meters away.
The Clarity platform provides three times the range and 20x the detail of best-in-class LiDAR.

Clarity outperforms existing sensing solutions by seamlessly combining visual information from cameras with measured depth so that longer distances and greater detail can be measured rather than only inferred by machine learning.

Benefits of the technology include safer automatic emergency braking, evasive steering control for a wider variety of objects, adaptive suspension adjustment for road hazards such as potholes, and smoother adaptive cruise-control and lane-keeping systems.
Dave Grannan serves as Light's co-founder and CEO.
Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain, and integrated vehicle control for major automotive manufacturers worldwide.
Hitachi Astemo adheres to ISO/TS:16949:2009 industry standards.
Light is pioneering real-time 3D perception by combining computational imaging with multi-camera calibration to provide accurate depth at both near and far distances in real-time.

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose Company will invest $6.1 million in its operation in Floyd County, Virginia.
The company will expand its existing facility to add manufacturing capacity for a product currently only manufactured in the U.K.
The expansion is expected to create 17 new jobs.
Virginia successfully competed against Georgia and Massachusetts to secure this project.
Governor Bob McDonnell announced the investment on November 14, 2013.
Hollingsworth & Vose has been a major employer in Floyd County for nearly 40 years, operating there since 1974.
Jim Cheng, Virginia Secretary of Commerce and Trade, stated that Hollingsworth & Vose is a global leader selling products in more than 80 countries.
Val Hollingsworth, President and CEO, said the company is pleased to continue growing and investing in Floyd, Virginia.

Mike Clark, H&V Division President for High Efficiency and Specialty Filtration, noted the expansion will provide capacity to support increasing North American demand for high performance synthetic filter media.
The new state-of-the-art production line will produce H&V's patented Technostat electret filter media, which provides high efficiency filtration with very low pressure drop.
Hollingsworth & Vose is a global leader in the supply of technically advanced engine, high efficiency and liquid filtration media, battery separator materials, and industrial nonwovens.
The company operates manufacturing sites and research centers across the Americas, Europe, and Asia.
The Virginia Economic Development Partnership worked with Floyd County and the New River Valley Economic Development Alliance to secure the project for Virginia.
Governor McDonnell approved a $100,000 grant from the Governor’s Opportunity Fund to assist with the project.
The Virginia Tobacco Indemnification and Community Revitalization Commission approved $65,000 in Tobacco Region Opportunity Funds for the project.
Through the Virginia Jobs Investment Program, the Virginia Department of Business Assistance will provide funding and services to support recruitment and training activities.
Floyd County Board of Supervisors Chairman Case C. Clinger expressed delight at the company's continued investment and job creation in the region.

Delegate Terry Kilgore, Chairman of the Virginia Tobacco Indemnification and Community Revitalization Commission, highlighted the additional job creation and investment as a testament to the county's efforts to compete for new business.

The Honda Talon Sport Side-by-Side is manufactured in Timmonsville, SC.
The Honda Pioneer Side-by-Side is manufactured in Timmonsville, SC.
The Honda FourTrax ATV is manufactured in Swepsonville, NC.
Honda Snow Blowers are manufactured in Swepsonville, NC.
Honda Mini-Tillers are manufactured in Swepsonville, NC.
Honda V-6 Auto Engines are manufactured in Lincoln, AL and Anna, OH.
Honda L4 Auto Engines are manufactured in Anna, OH.
The Honda 2-Motor Hybrid System is manufactured in Russells Point, OH.
Honda Auto Transmissions are manufactured in Tallapoosa, GA.
Honda Auto Gear Sets are manufactured in Russells Point, OH.
The HondaJet Elite II Aircraft is manufactured in Greensboro, NC.
Honda Aero Aircraft Engines are manufactured in Burlington, NC.
Honda has been building powersports products in America for more than 45 years.
North Carolina is the exclusive production source for Honda ATVs in North America and South Carolina is the exclusive production source for Honda side-by-side vehicles globally.
In 2024, Honda produced 116,800 power equipment units in the U.S.
In 2024, Honda produced 37,000 powersports products in the U.S.
The global headquarters for the Honda Aircraft Company is located in Greensboro, North Carolina, where associates have produced light duty jets since 2006.
Honda Aircraft Company’s headquarters house design, engineering, and production facilities in a single, integrated campus.

Honda is reimagining its approach to advanced auto production with flexible manufacturing that leverages human-friendly and sustainable practices to build gas, hybrid, and electric vehicles on the same production lines.
Last year, Honda invested over $1 billion to retool three major auto plants in Ohio to serve as the foundation for Honda’s flexible manufacturing approach globally.
source_title: U.S. Manufacturing - Honda in America | Driving Investment,

facts: Honda provided a first look at future EV models planned for 2026 with the Saloon and Space-Hub concepts at CES in Las Vegas.
Both the Saloon and Space-Hub concepts are envisioned for Honda's 0 Series (Zero Series) platform.
At least some of these upcoming models will be U.S.-made and will arrive in the U.S. before Japan, China, and Europe.
The vehicles will feature a not-yet-detailed Motion Management System for ride and handling.
Honda's future EVs will emphasize efficiency and use lightweight, energy-dense battery packs.
Batteries to be used in later versions of the 0 Series platform will charge from 15-80% in just 10-15 minutes.
The batteries will lose no more than 10% of original capacity (and range) after 10 years of use.

The 0 Series platform consists of multiple layers including an EV propulsion platform, an E&E electrical and electronic architecture, a software platform, an application layer, a sensing suite, and Honda Connect telematics and data/software delivery.
Initial 0 Series production models will start in the mid-large segment, with concepts appearing to be around 190 inches long.
Honda declined to quote dimensions for the concepts but plans to introduce different-sized EV and propulsion platforms over time to span from small to large vehicles.
A production model based on the Saloon concept will launch in 2026, though Honda has not yet decided on a specific production volume.
There will be multiple production Honda EVs based on the 0 Series building blocks.
Honda has committed to 40 gigawatt-hours annually with LG as part of a joint-venture battery-supply agreement, enough to produce 350,000 to 400,000 vehicles.
