The 0 Series lineup will not use existing Honda brand names like Accord, Civic, or CR-V.
Honda does not expect an affordable small EV much before 2030, as adding EV versions of legacy models proved more difficult than anticipated.
The Afeela electric sedan, set to be sold by Sony-Honda in 2026, will use the 0 Series underpinnings as common building blocks but will include additional components to enhance vehicle value.
Honda's technical approach for the 0 Series emphasizes thin, light, and wise design, starting with reducing battery height at the bottom of the vehicle by about 10%.
Creative director Daisuke Tsutamori stated that reducing battery height frees up design choices on the way to production.
Honda developed the Zero series with a target of 300 miles or more as the optimal range for the U.S. market production models.
General manager Koji Nakano confirmed the 300-mile range target was chosen combined with a thin battery form factor to minimize losses and optimize efficiency.
Honda will not use carbon fiber or carbon-fiber-reinforced plastic for these vehicles, despite BMW's past use of it in the i3.
Nakano could not reveal whether the vehicles will be made from steel, aluminum, or other materials.
Production models will be developed with bidirectional charging capability in mind.
The Space-Hub concept appeared to feature a mobile power pack charging between the front and rear seats, potentially tying into Honda's Motocompacto electric scooter.
Honda is working on SAE Level 3 city-driving capability for the 0 Series, aiming to deploy it over-the-air in the late 2020s.
Honda aims to make steer-by-wire systems and assisted driving features more widely available and affordable.
The steer-by-wire system will allow for assisted driving plus increased packaging flexibility.
Aerodynamics is a key part of efficiency and real-world range, with Honda focusing on the coefficient of drag in modeling the Saloon concept's shape.
Honda plans to bring an EV based closely on the Saloon model to production, though it would not reveal drag numbers as they are still concepts.

The 0 Series design emphasizes simple, clean aesthetics mixed with elements from earlier Honda designs like the first-generation Civic front end, which houses radar, lidar, and other sensors instead of a grille.
Neither concept vehicle features a rear window, a design choice Honda would like to make to expand rear passenger space, pending market-by-market regulatory checks as models evolve toward production.
source_title: 12 things to know about next-gen Honda EVs arriving in 2026

canonical_name: Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC (HDMA)
facts: Honda has a clear vision for the future in 2030 focused on innovation and reaching future-focused goals.
Honda strives to realize “the joy and freedom of mobility” by developing new technologies and an innovative approach to achieve a “zero environmental footprint.”
The position participates in Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC’s (HDMA) Engineering Development Program (EDP).
The EDP has been a critical program for developing entry-level engineers over the last 25 years.
The program is a one to two-year duration where participants rotate through various departments and roles throughout HDMA.
Successful graduates of the EDP will return to a “Home” department with experience across manufacturing and business operations facets.
Openings are available in multiple locations, including Georgia: Tallapoosa.
Other program locations include Ohio (Anna, East Liberty, Marysville, Raymond, Russells Point), Alabama (Lincoln), and Indiana (Greensburg).

Manufacturing Engineering roles lead, create, and implement technical activities in manufacturing operations, monitoring and managing equipment and processes to ensure optimal performance while minimizing operating expenses.

Equipment/Tooling Design Engineering roles design manufacturing equipment including stamping, hardware & software, and die & mold to ensure efficiency and stability of new models and technology introduction across HDMA North American facilities.

Quality Engineering roles interact with Manufacturing Departments, Support Departments, Suppliers, and R&D for root cause analysis and countermeasures, and perform car testing during new model trial stages to verify design intent matches vehicle performance.
Quality Engineering roles are available in Ohio & Georgia.

Materials Development Engineer roles focus on the research and development of resins, fabrics, and other non-metal materials within the Organics Group, seeking materials or chemical engineering majors.

Safety Engineer roles apply scientific and practical knowledge of Safety compliance to innovate, design, and improve systems, processes, equipment, and tools to align with daily operations, new models, business plans, and innovation targets.

Data Analytics Engineer roles review multiple data sources to evaluate, summarize, and draw conclusions identifying trends for countermeasure activity, analyzing data covering quality, delivery, safety, and cost within manufacturing, design, and test environments.
Applicants must have a bachelor’s degree from an ABET or ATMAE accredited program in Mechanical, Electrical, Industrial, or other equivalent engineering fields.
Candidates require less than 2 years of engineering work experience, with 3 months co-op/internship experience in a relevant environment preferred.
Sponsorship for employment visa status is unavailable for these positions, including F-1 CPT/OPT, H-1B, and TN visas.
Compensation includes a competitive base salary based on variables like geographic location and work experience, paid overtime, and regional bonuses when applicable.

Benefits include industry-leading plans (Medical, Dental, Vision, Rx), paid time off for vacation/holidays/shutdowns, company-paid short-term and long-term disability, and a 401K plan with company match plus additional contribution.
Relocation assistance is available if eligible.
Career growth offerings include advancement opportunities, career mobility, education reimbursement for continued learning, and training and development programs.

Additional offerings include tuition assistance & student loan repayment, lifestyle accounts, childcare reimbursement accounts, elder care support, wellbeing programs, community service and engagement programs, product programs, and free drinks onsite.

Honda is an equal opportunity employer that considers qualified applicants without regard to race, color, creed, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected factor.
source_title: Engineering Development Program – 2026 Graduates – Center for Career and Professional Development | Mercer University

facts: King’s Hawaiian has announced its investment of US$54 m to expand its plant in Oakwood, Georgia.
A new manufacturing line is set to be installed at the 150,000-square-foot Oakwood South Industrial Park facility.
The project is expected to generate 135 new jobs.
Hiring will focus on roles in management, maintenance, food safety, and quality control.
Operations for the new production line are scheduled to begin in the second quarter of 2026.
Plans include introducing additional flavors of King’s Hawaiian Pretzel Bites.
King’s Hawaiian was established in 1950 by the Taira family in Hilo, Hawaii.
The company is now a member of the Irresistible Foods Group (IFG), alongside brands like Grillo’s Pickles.
Since expanding operations to Georgia in 2010, King’s Hawaiian has reportedly created over 800 jobs statewide.
The details of the investment were shared last week through an official announcement from Georgia Governor Brian Kemp.
CEO Mark Taira stated that the expansion represents a major milestone in their journey and that Georgia has been essential to their success for 15 years.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that King’s Hawaiian has proven to be an incredible partner in creating quality jobs in northeast Georgia over nearly 15 years.
Governor Brian Kemp added that expansions like these are an important part of economic development work, helping create further opportunities in growing communities.
King’s Hawaiian’s latest investment reinforces the state’s reputation as a hub for innovation, workforce development, and community-driven growth.
The expansion allows the company to scale its production and deepen its legacy in the state of Georgia, also known as the Peach State.
source_title: King's Hawaiian commits US$54M to Georgia expansion  - Milling Middle East & Africa Magazine - No.1 Grains Industry Magazine & Website for Africa & the Middle East

facts: Honda announced plans to combine all of its automobile manufacturing facilities in the U.S. related to frame, engine, transmission, and related engineering and purchasing operations into one new company, to be named Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC (HDMA).
The new HDMA company will integrate the automobile product development operations of Honda R&D Americas, LLC (HRA).
The unification of Honda’s manufacturing operations and merger with auto product development will be effective April 1, 2021.
Shinji Aoyama serves as chief officer of North American Regional Operations of Honda Motor Co., Ltd., and president & CEO of American Honda.

Automobile product design and certain market research functions currently part of the HRA Los Angeles Center will be integrated into the Regional Operations of American Honda Motor Co., Inc. based in Torrance, Calif.
HDMA will centralize key business functions to become more efficient and eliminate redundancies.
HDMA will execute new product development with increased speed and accuracy.
HDMA will improve manufacturing characteristics with a focus on quality, cost competitiveness and the ability to deliver vehicles that appeal to customers in a timely manner.

This restructuring mirrors an announcement in February 2020 by Honda Motor Co., Ltd. in Japan integrating the automobile development function of Honda R&D Co., Ltd. and key elements of Honda Engineering Co., Ltd. into parent company Honda Motor’s Automobile Operations.

Auto-related Honda companies to be wholly or partially consolidated into HDMA include Honda of America Mfg., Honda Manufacturing of Alabama, Honda Manufacturing of Indiana, Honda Transmission Manufacturing of America, Honda Precision Parts of Georgia, Honda Engineering North America, Honda R&D Americas, and Honda Accessory America.
American Honda announced plans to streamline its Auto Sales and Parts & Service Field operations to enable a more agile response to local market needs.
In January 2021, Honda and Acura zone offices will be restructured to improve communication between dealers, zone management and National Sales and Parts & Service management.
The sales restructuring includes four new Regional Manager positions created to provide oversight of Honda Sales, Acura Sales and Parts, Service & Technical field operations.
The new Regional Managers will become the key link between the company’s National and Field operations, ensuring better alignment among all staff who call on Honda and Acura dealers.
Decision making authority will be pushed down to local staff to more quickly address dealer and customer needs.

Honda companies in the U.S. responsible for Powersports business, including motorcycle, ATV and side-by-side products, began restructuring earlier in 2020 to unite the functions of sales, manufacturing, product development and purchasing.
Honda of South Carolina Mfg., responsible for powersports manufacturing in the U.S., will formally become part of the Powersports business unit within American Honda.
This follows a similar reorganization in Japan in 2019 when the Motorcycle Operations of Honda Motor and the Motorcycle center of Honda R&D were merged into one organization.
Honda established operations in North America in 1959.

The company employs more than 40,000 associates in the region engaged in the development, manufacturing, sales and service support of Honda and Acura automobiles, Honda power equipment, Honda powersports products, the HondaJet advanced light jet and GE Honda HF120 turbofan engines.
Honda operates 19 major manufacturing plants in North America, working with more than 740 suppliers in the region to produce a diverse range of Honda products.
In 2019, more than 90 percent of the Honda and Acura automobiles sold in the U.S. were produced in North America, using domestic and globally sourced parts.
Honda operates 14 major research and development centers in North America which jointly fully design, develop and engineer many of the products the company makes in North America.
The Hyundai Metaplant is the newest fully EV and battery manufacturing facility for Hyundai Motor Group.
Currently under construction, the facility will include 5 buildings that stretch across approximately 4 million SF on a 3,000 acre site in Savannah, GA.
Atlas Collaborative is the Architect of Record.
The square footage is listed as 4,002,000.
The project status is listed as BUILT.
source_title: Hyundai Metaplant - Atlas Collaborative

facts: Honda is in the midst of completing more than $1 billion in new investments in Ohio this year, upped from an initially announced $700 million.
The investment upgrades notably include installing six “giga presses” (6,000-ton high-pressure die cast machines) for megacasting materials.
Honda is establishing a new “cell” manufacturing system for its upcoming electric vehicle battery cases.
The company’s emerging EV hub in Ohio will serve as the flagship for Honda’s global manufacturing operations.
Honda’s Marysville Auto Plant will be capable of producing traditional vehicles, hybrids and EVs on the same assembly line.

Mike Fischer, North American lead for Honda’s battery-electric vehicle projects, stated that the Ohio EV hub is establishing the global standard for EV production for people, technology, and processes.
The Ohio investments were initially announced in October 2022 as part of the Biden administration’s push to on-shore manufacturing.
Honda produced more than 1 million vehicles at five U.S. assembly plants in 2024, with about 64% sold in the U.S. and the remainder exported.
Once completed, Honda will be able to produce roughly 220,000 vehicles annually at its Marysville plant, located in central Ohio outside of Columbus.
The 4 million-square-foot Marysville facility currently produces several Honda and Acura vehicles and is expected to join production of an all-electric Acura RSX crossover later this year.
The all-electric Acura RSX crossover will be the first EV produced by Honda in the U.S.
The new Acura crossover will be followed by the Honda 0 SUV and Honda 0 Saloon EV prototypes debuted at CES in Las Vegas.

Aluminum battery packs for the new EVs will be produced at Honda’s nearby engine complex in Anna, Ohio, which is the company’s largest engine facility globally and spans more than 2.8-million-square feet.
Tim Stroh, EV battery case project leader, stated that the goal is to roll out the large aluminum production technology for all Honda products and factories across the world.
The massive gigacasting machines use enormous pressure to form parts, theoretically slashing per-unit manufacturing costs by eliminating the welding of dozens of body parts to cast one single module.

Once cast, battery packs will be shipped from Anna to Marysville and other plants to have battery cells from Honda’s joint venture operations with LG Energy Solution installed before final EV assembly.
To combine battery cells and packs in Marysville, Honda is installing nearly 60 flexible manufacturing “cells” or zones for battery assembly that run parallel to the main line to prevent slowdowns.

Bob Schwyn, senior vice president of Honda Development and Manufacturing of America, referred to the transition to electric vehicles as Honda’s “second founding” and stated they are using the opportunity to reimagine their approach to manufacturing.

Despite slower-than-expected EV adoption in the U.S., Honda maintains its previously announced goal of achieving zero environmental impact by 2050 through carbon neutrality, clean energy, and resource circulation.
Honda’s goals also include exclusively selling zero-emissions vehicles by 2040.

The more than $1 billion investments include new manufacturing processes to lower emissions and waste, including using a special form of structural aluminum for EV battery packs that can be recycled and reused.

Schwyn stated the company is implementing strategies to recapture products at end-of-life and recycle or reuse 100% of materials, especially finite materials for EV batteries, to essentially make new Hondas out of old Hondas.
source_title: Honda's new EV production revolution begins with $1 billion in Ohio

facts: Honda’s Jeffersonville plant has begun production of lithium batteries for electric grid and storage purposes, not for EV batteries, the company said Wednesday, July 1.
L-H Battery Co. Inc. said it has launched lithium-ion battery cell mass production.
Construction of the plant began more than three years ago.
The plant is located west of Ohio 729 and south of Interstate 71.
The plant spans more than 2 million square feet.
The plant has more than 1,000 employees.
L-H Battery stated it will remain flexible with its products and production as the battery cell market evolves.
The plant was originally envisioned to begin mass production to supply battery cells for electric vehicles.
The plant pivoted to support energy storage systems and future production of hybrid-electric vehicle battery cells in North America.
A spokeswoman for L-H Battery referred questions about EV battery production start dates to Honda.
LG Energy Solution will sell its stake in the joint venture EV battery production site in Fayette County to Honda Development and Manufacturing of America for $2.85 billion.
The sale was reported in a Korean regulatory filing in December 2025.
The first battery cells produced at L-H Battery will be packaged into energy storage systems by LG Energy Solution Vertech.
LG Energy Solution Vertech is the U.S. energy storage division of LG Energy Solution.

The energy storage systems are delivered to customers for grid-scale and other technology applications, including residential homes, commercial and industrial operations, and large-scale utility grids.
These systems capture and store electricity created by traditional energy sources like oil and gas as well as renewables like wind and solar.
L-H Battery Chief Executive Chahun Ku said such systems are an important part of L-H Battery’s future, along with production of battery cells for application in hybrid-electric vehicles.

Rick Riggle, chief operating officer at L-H Battery, said it has been nearly four years since the establishment of L-H Battery and expressed gratification over progress from completing construction to establishing a new workforce and delivering economic benefits for Fayette County.
Global lithium-ion battery deployment in 2025 was six times that of 2020, according to the International Energy Agency as reported by Reuters.
source_title: Honda battery plant has started full-time production | Local | daytondailynews.com

facts: Georgia’s economic development strategy has been driven by consistency, collaboration, and competitiveness for more than a decade.
The state maintains a steady approach anchored by disciplined fiscal policy, targeted infrastructure and workforce investment, and a focus on business climate benefits.
Pratt & Whitney announced a $200 million expansion in Columbus, following a prior expansion that added 400 new jobs and 81,000 square feet to its Columbus Engine Center.
Georgia has been ranked the No. 1 state for business for 12 consecutive years.
State leaders approved legislation reducing Georgia’s corporate income tax rate to 4.99 percent, retroactive to January 1, 2026.
The tax legislation establishes a structured pathway for annual reductions of 0.125 percent until the rate reaches 3.99 percent.
Existing incentives include the Job Tax Credit and other performance-based incentives.
Georgia Power’s Integrated Resource Plan supports meeting projected growth of 8,500 megawatts by 2030.
Utilities are diversifying the energy mix with investments in solar capacity, storage, and Plant Vogtle, the first new nuclear plant to open in the United States in three decades.
UCB announced a $2 billion investment in a new manufacturing facility in Georgia, creating 330 jobs.
Unified Legacy announced a $125 million investment in Macon-Bibb County, which will create 500 new jobs.
Qcells is expanding its solar manufacturing footprint in northwest Georgia, strengthening the state’s role in the clean energy supply chain.
Salesforce maintains a significant presence in Atlanta, with Site Lead Tanya Counter highlighting local talent and deepening investment.
Glytec CEO Patrick Cua cited favorable business environment, global connectivity, and high quality of living as reasons for selecting Georgia.
Duracell located its global research and development headquarters in Atlanta.
HK USA CEO Michael Holley credited workforce partners including Georgia Quick Start, the Technical College System of Georgia, and the Choose Columbus retention program.
Georgia Quick Start has been recognized by Area Development magazine as the No. 1 workforce training program for 14 consecutive years.

The General Assembly passed HB 1302 (Education and Workforce Strategy Act) to strengthen coordination across agencies, align workforce planning with employer demand, and expand apprenticeship pathways.
Georgia’s infrastructure includes the Ports of Savannah and Brunswick, Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport, and an extensive interstate and rail system.
A Georgia Tech study released in April found that the Port of Savannah saves shippers more than $1,000 per container to Atlanta, Memphis, and Nashville compared to West Coast gateways.
Independent research by Georgia Tech’s Supply Chain and Logistics Institute estimated shipping through Savannah offers a 32 percent cost savings over the West Coast.
The GRAD (Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development) program and RSDI (Rural Site Development Initiative) prepare industrial sites in advance to reduce development timelines.
Preciball USA utilized RSDI funding on a GRAD-certified site in coastal Georgia, where local authorities pre-built a spec building that shaved months off the development timeline.
Atlanta’s airport connects the state to more than 80 international and 160 domestic destinations via nonstop service.
source_title: Georgia Builds on Record Momentum While Advancing Long-Term Growth - Expansion Solutions

facts: Georgia Gov. Brian P. Kemp announced that DAS Corp. (DAS), an automotive parts company, will establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter.
The new facility will create 300 new jobs.
The project will generate more than $35 million in investment in Candler County.
The initiative will also involve collaborative indirect investments.
DAS was established in Korea in 1987 and specializes in designing and manufacturing seating systems, safety seating components, and seating structures.
DAS is a key supplier for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) and Hyundai Motor Group.
The new DAS manufacturing facility will be located in the Candler County Industrial Park in Metter.
Operations at the new facility are projected to begin in the latter half of 2024.
Over the course of the next five years, DAS will recruit for assembly technician roles.
DAS intends to establish collaborations with local employment services as part of its hiring process.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant resulted in over $2 billion in investment last fiscal year alone, helping Georgia achieve a third straight year of record-breaking economic growth.

Governor Brian Kemp noted that Georgia's logistics assets, including a reliable network of rail lines and highways, connect companies like DAS to key business partners statewide and to global markets.

Sean Kim, Chief Operating Officer at DAS, said the strategic location near prominent cities like Savannah and Macon facilitated convenient access and played a pivotal role in the decision-making process.
Sean Kim added that DAS deemed Metter an ideal location in anticipation of its role as a global electric vehicle production hub due to its close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.

Metter Mayor Ed Boyd stated that the City of Metter is proud to welcome DAS and expects a transformative impact on the community, stimulating investment in housing, creating other business opportunities, and improving socioeconomic wellbeing.
Glyn Thrift, Chairman of the Candler County Board of Commissioners, thanked DAS for choosing Candler County as its new home and stated they will provide a boost to spur more economic development.
Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, expressed pride in supporting the growing automotive manufacturing presence in the state and driving economic activity and jobs.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team, represented the agency on this competitive project.
The GDEcD project was executed in partnership with the Candler County Industrial Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Electric Cities of Georgia, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia is well-positioned for the arrival of Hyundai’s supplier network due to years of consistent investment in site preparation, workforce solutions, and connectivity.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson described DAS as another exciting addition to Georgia’s automotive community and congratulated the company along with partners in the City of Metter and Candler County.
source_title: GA: Auto Parts Manufacturer DAS to Establish Facility in Metter, Creating 300 Jobs in Candler County | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Honda is postponing its planned investments to build an electric vehicle supply chain in Canada by two years amid tariffs and slower than expected electric vehicle demand in North America, CEO Toshihiro Mibe said during the company’s earnings call on May 13.
Honda last April announced plans to invest up to $11 billion to establish an EV production hub in Canada.
The Canadian EV production hub included a factory with an annual output of 240,000 EVs a year.
The hub included a 36 gigawatt hour battery factory in Alliston, Ontario, developed with joint venture partner Posco Future M Co.
The original plans for Honda’s Canada EV hub included all aspects of manufacturing and the procurement of raw materials for battery production.
The hub plans included a cathode active material and precursor processing and separator plant with joint venture partner Asahi Kasei.
Honda is consulting with the Canadian government and Ontario Province regarding the postponement, which has received their consent.
CEO Toshihiro Mibe stated during the earnings call: “We have to observe what is happening and ultimately make the decision.”
The revised plans come as Honda seeks to shift more vehicle production to the U.S. to avoid new U.S. duties on imported vehicles.
New U.S. duties are expected to significantly impact Honda's profits in the upcoming fiscal year.
Honda’s FY 2026 forecast called for its operating profit to fall by nearly 59%, or over 700 billion yen ($4.8 billion).
Honda's margins are expected to decline by over half.
CEO Toshihiro Mibe stated: “In the mid-term, if the tariff measures are to be in place for a long time, then we will have to increase our production capacity in [the] United States.”
Honda has already begun studies on increasing U.S. production capacity.
Honda will reevaluate its Canada investment plans based on market conditions as they unfold.
CEO Toshihiro Mibe stated: “If the conditions were to change, at that point in time we have to revisit this.”
The postponed Canadian investment would have been Honda's largest ever investment in the country.
Among tariff mitigation plans, Honda is moving production of the Honda Civic hybrid from Japan to its U.S. plant in Indiana.
Production of the CR-V compact SUV will be moved to Ohio or Indiana due to tariffs.
The CR-V was originally planned to continue production in Ontario.
As of 2023, the Canada factory was the global lead plant for manufacturing the CR-V and North America lead plant for the CR-V hybrid, according to Honda.
Honda did not specify which U.S. plant will assemble the CR-V, as plans are still being finalized.
Honda’s postponed investments in Canada have drawn criticism from Ontario Premier Doug Ford, who last month expressed optimism that Honda would maintain production in the country.
Doug Ford said a media report that Honda planned to shift more vehicle production to the U.S. were “not accurate at all.”
Doug Ford stated: “We’re going to keep Honda here, and I’ll do everything I can to protect the people and their jobs.”
In a press conference on May 13 after the plans were made public, Doug Ford said he would hold Honda and other automakers “accountable” for pulling some vehicle production out of Canada.
Before tariffs were levied, Honda planned to ramp up EV production in the U.S.
Over the past year, Honda has been retooling three facilities in Ohio to create an EV production hub.
Honda stated that each of its North America production facilities will have a “critical role” in its electrified future.
Honda revised its EV production plans in Ohio since last year.
In February, Honda announced plans to produce a mix of ICE, hybrid and fully electric models on the same production lines.
The mixed production lines allow for enough flexibility to adapt to changes in market demand in North America.
source_title: Honda postpones $11B EV production investment in Canada | ESG Dive

facts: The Georgia Manufacturing Investment Tax Credit is a financial incentive aimed at supporting businesses in the manufacturing and telecommunications sectors.
By making capital improvements more affordable, this state credit encourages companies to expand and upgrade facilities, promoting economic growth and job creation throughout the state.
The credit can offset up to 50% of a company’s Georgia corporate income tax liability.
Any unused portion can be carried forward for up to 10 years.
Introduced in 1994, the Georgia Manufacturing Investment Tax Credit was established to make it more affordable for businesses to expand and modernize their operations.
Initially, the program required a minimum investment of $50,000 to qualify for the credit.
Significant updates in 2020 included increasing the minimum investment threshold to $100,000 for new or existing facilities.
Provisions were added to benefit businesses in economically distressed rural counties, allowing excess credits to offset withholding taxes.
Qualified investment expenses include land acquisition, improvements, buildings, and the purchase of machinery and equipment to be used in these facilities.
The tax credit amount ranges from 1% to 8% of the qualified capital investments, depending on the geographic location and the type of investment made.

Those focused on pollution control, recycling equipment, or converting a defense plant can earn higher credits (3% to 8%), while general manufacturing or telecommunications equipment investments earn lower credits (1% to 5%).
For facilities located in tier 1 or tier 2 rural counties, excess credits may be used to offset withholding taxes after first applying to 50% of income tax liability.
Preapproval from the Georgia Department of Revenue (GA DOR) is required to utilize this provision.
Companies that increase their import/export activity through Georgia ports by 10% can qualify for a Port Activity Tax Credit Bonus, which increases the credit up to 5%.
The total amount of tax credits that can be used against payroll withholding taxes is capped at $1 million per company per year and $10 million for all companies statewide.
To claim the credit, taxpayers must submit Form IT-APP to the Georgia Department of Revenue (GA DOR) and a detailed project plan and capital expenditure budget.
The credit can be claimed on the appropriate tax return only after approval from the GA DOR.
Once approved, the company will receive an approval letter from the GA DOR.
All forms must be submitted within 30 days of project completion.
Companies cannot claim the job tax credit or the optional investment tax credit for the same project.
A manufacturing company operating in a tier 2 rural county invested $200,000 in new manufacturing equipment and pollution control devices in 2023.
The company earned a tax credit of 6% of the $200,000 investment, totaling $12,000.
They used the credit to offset 50% of their state corporate income tax liability for 2023, carrying forward the remaining unused credit to offset future tax liabilities.
Claiming the credit is complicated, so it’s important to consult with a qualified tax advisor who can determine eligibility and guide you through the process.
source_title: GA Manufacturing Investment Tax Credit | Atlanta CPA Firm

facts: Honda is spending $1 billion to revamp its Ohio manufacturing plants.
The plant revamp will allow Honda to build gasoline, hybrid, and electric vehicles on the same assembly line.
This flexibility allows the company to quickly adapt to changing market demand.
Mike Fischer serves as chief engineer of Honda development and manufacturing of America.
Fischer describes the initiative as a "huge deal" for the company and calls it the "second founding of Honda."
The initiative aims to reimagine manufacturing and set a new footprint for the manufacturing business model in Ohio.
This sets the business model for how Honda will operate in North America and ultimately deploy it globally.
Fischer states the initiative is gaining incredible traction internally and as products hit the market later this fall.
The shift represents a paradigm change from solely internal combustion engines to a new way of making vehicles.
Facilities are designed to be flexible to adapt to changing customer tastes and market demands.
This approach allows Honda to maintain efficiency and keep operations full, which equals good value for customers in price positioning.
Ohio was chosen because the majority of Honda's customer base is in North America.
Ohio serves as an EV hub with heavily located R&D facilities, manufacturing, purchasing, supply base, and supply chain.
The business model focuses on flexibility to utilize existing facilities: Marysville Auto Plant, East Liberty Auto Plant, and Engine Plant.
Honda's business model sets a goal to build EV vehicles at 100% positioning towards 2040.
The flexible line allows shifting production between internal combustion, hybrid, and EV vehicles based on customer demand and value proposition.
The shift will require workforce changes, specifically for battery case manufacturing and battery assembly operations.
Honda projects adding about 300 additional associates to its Ohio manufacturing operations.
Honda expects to debut the new electric Acura RSX later this year (2025), which will be built in Ohio.
Several new all-electric vehicle models with the Honda nameplate are planned thereafter, also to be built in Ohio.
Honda has a goal of producing only zero-emission vehicles by 2040.
source_title: Honda has $1B plan to make gas, hybrid, electric cars on same Ohio assembly line | WYSO

facts: Tim Leopold is 44 years old.
He is leading the conversion of Honda’s Marysville Auto Plant from production of five- to six-speed automatic transmissions.

The auto industry is undergoing a massive transformation as it mobilizes to electrify its gasoline-powered fleets, and Leopold is at the center of that shift at Honda Development and Manufacturing of America.
Honda is building an electric vehicle and battery hub in Ohio.
Marysville Auto Plant is Honda’s oldest plant in North America.
Marysville will be the first of Honda’s existing factories to build EVs.
The first vehicle planned at Marysville is a compact crossover from Acura in late 2025 that will use the automaker’s in-house EV architecture.
The team is reconfiguring lines to “greatly reduce” consumption of resources that the plant requires to operate as part of Honda’s goal to reach 100 percent carbon neutrality by 2050.
Tim Leopold stated: “We’re redefining not only our company, but how we operate within our company, and to be in a big role at our flagship plant in Marysville only comes once in a career.”
The team is working around real-time assembly of the Honda Accord, plus the Acura TLX and Integra, which are all exclusively built at Marysville.
Tim Leopold stated: “The goal is to make the conversion without having to completely shut down and retool the plant, which is a significant challenge.”
After completing two separate internship programs with Honda while studying at Ohio University, he was offered a full-time position with the automaker before even graduating.
For about a decade, he was part of an “expert support team” for innovation projects throughout North America.

Tim Leopold stated: “I’ve always been a firm believer that I learn through every interaction — both good and bad. Each of those projects, whether they were successful or not, it helped groom me to who I’ve become.”
In 2010, he went to Honda’s transmission plant in Tallapoosa, Ga., to lead the conversion of assembly from five-speed to six-speed transmissions.

The Georgia facility’s smaller size exposed him to many facets of the manufacturing business, and his role eventually evolved from leading the initial project to overseeing the whole manufacturing operation.
Tim Leopold stated: “It was a huge growth opportunity for me, and a great experience.”
In 2017, under his leadership, the plant moved from assembly of six-speed transmissions to 10-speeds.
Unlike the six-speed transmission, it was not just a “transfer of technology” from Japan.

Tim Leopold stated: “This was the first opportunity to design not only the product, but the equipment, the quality and the market approach, and to pull all of the manufacturing experts together on one team and launch it from North America.”

Tim Leopold stated: “I’ve had the privilege of being a part of so many different projects of varying scales, some smaller and some extremely large, like with Marysville. But I’ve found value in each one of them.”
source_title: 2024 Rising Star: Tim Leopold - Automotive News

facts: Hyundai Motor Group is a South Korean chaebol headquartered in Seoul, South Korea.
The group was formed in 1998 through the purchase of 51% of Kia by Hyundai Motor Company.
As of 2011, Hyundai owns 33.7% of Kia Motors.
In June 2021, Hyundai Motor, Hyundai Mobis, Hyundai Glovis, and Executive Chair Chung Eui-sun collectively purchased an 80% stake in Boston Dynamics from SoftBank Group.

On May 22, 2022, the group announced an additional $5 billion investment in the United States by 2025 to strengthen collaboration with U.S. firms in urban air mobility, autonomous driving, artificial intelligence, and robotics.
The May 2022 investment announcement occurred during a visit to South Korea by former President Joe Biden.
On December 10, 2024, Hyundai allowed customers to purchase Hyundai cars on Amazon for pickup at local dealerships.
Hyundai Motor Company and Kia are the largest and second-largest car manufacturers in South Korea, respectively.
The group sold 8.01 million vehicles in 2015, falling short of its sales target.
In 2017, the group sold 7.25 million vehicles, marking the lowest sales in five years at that time.
According to OICA, Hyundai Motor Group was the world's third-largest vehicle manufacturer by production volume in 2017, behind Toyota and Volkswagen Group.
On June 17, 2024, Hyundai Motor India announced the commencement of an IPO process to list on the Bombay Stock Exchange.
In 2024, Hyundai Motor Group announced plans to invest a record $16.7 billion in South Korea, focusing on green technologies and future mobility.
Chairman Chung Eui-sun announced an investment of 31 trillion won in the U.S. over four years, prompting a response from Trump that he would exempt the company from tariffs.
Affiliates include Ionna (a joint venture), Motional, Supernal, Hyundai AutoEver, and Hyundai Glovis.
On September 7, 2021, Hyundai held the Hydrogen Wave global online forum to outline its hydrogen business vision and Hydrogen Vision 2040.
The group unveiled the Vision FK, a high-performance hydrogen fuel cell vehicle developed in collaboration with Limak.

The Vision FK utilizes second-generation fuel cell stacks averaging 85kW with a maximum of 95kW, along with two 2kg hydrogen fuel tanks and a PE System comprising motor drives, a decelerator, inverter, and battery.
The total output of the rear-wheel motor drives on the Vision FK exceeds 500kW.
In December 2021, Hyundai suspended development of its Genesis hydrogen cars and possibly other hydrogen vehicles.
Key executives include Chung Mong-koo (honorary chairman), Chung Eui-sun (Executive Chairman & CEO), and Chang Jae-hoon (Vice Chairman).
The group reported production output of 3,890,726 vehicles in 2021 and had 292,575 employees as of 2021.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group - Wikipedia

facts: Hyundai Motor Group announced plans to open a new plant near Savannah, Ga., a few years ago.
The plant was originally planned to exclusively make electric vehicles.

In 2022, José Muñoz, then CEO of Hyundai Motor North America, stated the project responded to 'the clarion call of [the Biden] administration to hasten the adoption of new electric vehicles and reduce carbon emissions.'
The plant's grand opening occurred last week (relative to March 31, 2025).
Hyundai changed its plans over the last few years from a dedicated EV plant to also manufacturing hybrid vehicles.

José Muñoz, now CEO of the entire global company, said at the opening that initially everyone thought 'the world is going to be fully electric,' but they realized 'the pace of electrification was not going to be as fast as everybody thought.'
EV sales grew slower than companies had anticipated, while hybrid vehicle sales took off.
Hyundai executives decided quickly to produce hybrid vehicles at the Georgia plant in response to market trends.
The industrywide trend of slowing EV plans and focusing on hybrids is also seen in companies such as Ford, GM, and Volvo.
Under President Trump, regulations pushing automakers to make EVs are being rewritten, tax breaks for consumers buying them are at risk, and funds for EV chargers are frozen.

Georgia Sen. Jon Ossoff warned during a Zoom call with reporters that 'The Trump administration's war on EVs poses a serious threat to EV sales, to the electric vehicle industry and the EV market, and therefore to the state of Georgia.'
Hyundai executives maintain that government policy never drove their decision to expand into hybrids.

José Muñoz stated, 'Building plants on the assumption of incentives is not a good decision,' and noted that if plans change, he would not say, 'Hey, we invested $12.6 billion, so I made a mistake, I'm sorry?' No.
Hyundai's total investment in the Georgia plant is $12.6 billion.
While Hyundai's shift toward hybrids started before Trump took office, the timing coincides with federal policy changes.
The expansion plans were in place long before President Trump announced a new 25% tariff on imported cars.
Kia COO Steve Center noted that the timing of the tariff announcement was 'convenient' for Hyundai's hybrid shift.
Kia is part of Hyundai Motor Group and will also build vehicles at the new plant.
Automakers emphasize that EVs are quiet, fun to drive, and cheap to maintain, and fear ceding an entire market segment to Chinese companies if they stop investing in EVs.
source_title: Hyundai’s all-electric auto plant expands into hybrids : NPR

facts: Hyundai Motor and Georgia Tech announced a multidecade partnership to work together in research and applications to support the future of sustainable mobility, hydrogen economy and workforce development in August 2023.
About a year and a half later, Georgia Tech President Angel Cabrera underlined that the US university’s collaboration with Hyundai has been a win-win situation for both sides.
In an interview in Seoul on Jan. 23, Cabrera stated that Hyundai has become synonymous with technology for Georgia Tech students.

Hyundai Motor has decided to invest $12.6 billion in constructing a new electric vehicle-dedicated manufacturing plant and battery production sites in Georgia in partnerships with Korean battery firms LG Energy Solution and SK On.
Hyundai is already actively recruiting students, and Georgia Tech students want to work for innovative companies.
Cabrera noted that Hyundai's advantage lies in its exciting sense of purpose and project for the future, which appeals to students who see companies doing things differently.

Cabrera dismissed concerns that the pledge by the administration of US President Donald Trump to remove government subsidies for electric vehicles may hinder the growth of the EV sector toward which Hyundai continues to move.

Cabrera stated he does not think the new administration is anti-EV, but rather challenges subsidies, believing the market should develop the technology and that the future of mobility will be electric.

Cabrera bought the Ioniq 5, Hyundai’s award-winning EV, for himself seven months ago as his first pure electric vehicle and expressed confidence that customers will not want to go back to internal combustion engine-powered cars once they experience an EV.

Georgia Tech is exploring a co-location of Hyundai staff inside of its facilities to do research there, with currently about 10 ongoing joint research projects between the school and the automaker in areas such as EV and battery technologies.

The Georgia Tech president underscored that his team visited not only Hyundai’s research sites, but also joint labs with top Korean schools such as Seoul National University and Hanyang University to better understand some of the big questions that Hyundai is facing.
According to Cabrera, his delegation also met with officials from Samsung, Doosan and Hanwha during their visit to Korea to discuss various topics.
Georgia Tech has collaborated with Samsung and Doosan in research areas of software technology and hydrogen turbines.
Hanwha is building a large solar panel production site in Dalton, Georgia, about 145 kilometers away from Atlanta, which is the home of Georgia Tech.

As Georgia Tech’s mission to develop leaders who advance technology and improve the human condition aligns with Hyundai's "Progress for Humanity" vision, the automaker and school always strive for innovation.
Cabrera emphasized that Hyundai is not just building traditional cars but is always trying to figure out how to build the new technology of vehicles.
About 10 years ago, Georgia Tech started offering master’s programs online despite people thinking top universities cannot do that online without hurting the brand.

With now three online master's courses available for computer science, data analytics and cybersecurity, there are about 20,000 students enrolled in remote classes to make up the largest master's programs in the world.
Cabrera, who was in Atlanta as a student in the 1990s before leaving and returning as the president of Georgia Tech in 2019, touted the growth of Hyundai's brand in the US.
Cabrera stated that you now see Hyundai cars everywhere and it has become really one of the biggest brands right now in the US.
source_title: ‘Hyundai has become synonymous with technology’ - The Korea Herald

facts: Even after a Sept. 4 immigration raid that threw a wrench into its operations, Hyundai Motor Group said it would invest $2.7 billion more in Georgia.
The investment clarifies plans to create thousands more jobs at its Metaplant near Savannah.
Hyundai announced earlier this year that it would basically double down on the $20.5 billion the conglomerate has already invested in the United States.
The announcement had not specified how its Georgia car facilities would be affected.
The timing of the new investment was announced at a global investor conference held for the first time in the U.S.
Executives said the commitment to Georgia remains solid despite the immigration snafu.
The raid has played out on television screens across Asia with horror stories shared by returned detainees, shaking investor confidence in the U.S.
In March, Hyundai said it would put $9 billion more into U.S. automotive manufacturing.
This boosts domestic production capacity to 1.2 million units.
It is part of a $21 billion U.S. investment plan that also included a steel mill in Louisiana.
In August, as Korean President Lee Jae-myung visited the White House, Hyundai added $5 billion more to the overall total.
The latest Georgia investment will create 3,000 “direct and indirect jobs” on and around the site.
Tami Overby is a Korea trade expert at DGA Group.
Hyundai Motor Chairman Chung Eui-sun was noticeably absent during the parade of business leaders traveling with President Lee to the White House in August.
His absence may be a sign of discontent over 25 percent auto tariffs imposed in April, days after he joined President Trump to announce the U.S. investment plan.
Hyundai’s U.S. subsidiaries spent $1 million to be present at President Trump’s inauguration.
This was the first time Tami Overby remembers seeing Korean companies getting involved in American politics.
Tami Overby stated she is not sure Americans realized Chairman Chung might be unhappy and understands why.
Hyundai clarified it would raise production capacity in Georgia to 500,000 units per year in the second phase of its plant over the next three years.
CEO Jose Muñoz sees this ramp-up as being driven mainly by hybrids.
This is a shift from initial plans for the plant to operate as a pure-electric factory.
With the repeal of incentives and flagging demand for EVs, Hyundai is leaning more heavily into hybrids.
More than 18 new hybrid models will be introduced globally by 2030.
10 hybrid and EV models are set to be made at Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America by 2030.
By 2030, 60 percent of vehicles sold globally will be electrified, a category that includes hybrids and battery-electric vehicles.
New models will include a mid-size pickup truck for the U.S.
Extended-range EVs launching in 2027 will achieve 600 miles of range by combining smaller “in-house” batteries with conventional motors.
The goal is to eliminate range anxiety.
Plans for next-generation batteries in the U.S. could hinge on Hyundai getting its joint-venture battery factory with LG up and running.
That $4.3 billion site was targeted in the ICE raid and has been delayed for at least two months.
Tami Overby said restoring confidence will involve reassuring investors that the raid was a “one-time mistake.”
Mr. Kemp posted a brief statement on X welcoming Hyundai’s new commitment to Georgia.
His statement read: “We look forward to working with them on their continued growth and success for years to come!”
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson shared a statement on LinkedIn celebrating Hyundai's continuing historic investments in its state-of-the-art Georgia facility.

His statement read: “Today is a day to celebrate – Hyundai is continuing its historic investments in its state-of-the-art Georgia facility. From the beginning, Hyundai and our state and local partners have made this partnership a mission to maximize the possibilities for the future.”
source_title: Hyundai to Invest $2.7B in Georgia, Showing Commitment Despite Immigration Raid - Global Atlanta

entity_name: Hyundai-LG Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
facts: The new battery cell joint venture will be located in Bryan County, Savannah, Georgia, adjacent to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America which is currently under construction.
The joint venture involves a total investment of over USD 4.3 billion (KRW 5.7 trillion).
Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution will each hold a 50 percent stake in the joint venture.
Battery production at the new facility is aimed to start at the end of 2025 at the earliest.
The joint venture has an annual production capacity of 30 GWh, capable of supporting the production of 300,000 EVs annually.
Construction of the joint venture facility is scheduled to start in the second half of 2023.
Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution announced the EV battery cell manufacturing joint venture on May 26, 2023.
The parties signed a memorandum of understanding to produce EV batteries in the U.S. and accelerate electrification efforts in North America.
The signing ceremony took place at LG Energy Solution’s headquarters in Seoul on May 26.
Jaehoon Chang, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, attended the signing ceremony.
Youngsoo Kwon, CEO of LG Energy Solution, attended the signing ceremony.
Hyundai Motor Group stated it is focusing on electrification efforts to secure a leadership position in the global auto industry.
Jaehoon Chang stated the group will create a strong foundation to lead the global EV transition through establishing the new battery cell plant with LG Energy Solution.
Youngsoo Kwon stated that LG Energy Solution will commit its best efforts to offering ultimate sustainable energy solutions by advancing product competitiveness and global operational expertise.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant and supply them to the Group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
The new facility aims to create a stable regional battery supply and allow the group to respond quickly to soaring U.S. EV demand.
With this joint venture, LG Energy Solution now has seven battery plants operating or under construction in the U.S.
LG Energy Solution is concentrating most of its resources on expanding production capacity in the U.S. to expedite the clean energy transition.
Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution have previously supplied EV batteries for vehicles including the Elantra Hybrid, Kona Electric, and IONIQ 6.
The Hyundai Elantra Hybrid, an LPi hybrid vehicle introduced in 2009, was the group’s first electrified model.
In 2021, Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution started construction of a battery cell joint venture in Indonesia set to begin production in the first half of 2024.
This facility marks the second battery manufacturing joint venture between the group and LG Energy Solution as they aim to strengthen their long-time partnership in electrification.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution to Establish Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture in the U.S. - HMGMA

facts: Hyundai Motor Group has partnered with Google to bring Google Maps data to its infotainment system ahead of adopting Android Automotive.
The partnership will deliver information from Google Maps Platform to vehicles from Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
Rollout will begin with Kia vehicles in North America before expanding globally to Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis models.
Integration utilizes the Google Maps Places API to enhance navigation accuracy with details including business hours, contact information, ratings, and reviews.
Drivers of new Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis vehicles will gain access to fresh information about 250 million places around the world from Google Maps Platform.
This infotainment rollout is separate from Hyundai's previously announced plans to adopt Android Automotive starting in 2026.
source_title: Google Maps data is coming to Hyundai and Kia vehicles

facts: Hyundai Motor Group will invest $5.54 billion in Georgia at its first fully dedicated electric vehicle and battery manufacturing facility.
The investment is for opening a state-of-the-art U.S. smart factory at the Bryan County Megasite.
Non-affiliated Hyundai Motor Group suppliers will invest approximately another $1 billion in the project.
The project will deliver approximately 8,100 new jobs to Georgia’s coastal region.
Governor Brian P. Kemp and Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung announced the investment at a signing ceremony on May 20, 2022.
Jay Chang (President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company) and José Muñoz (President and COO of Hyundai Motor Company) attended the announcement.
The Bryan County Megasite is 2,923 acres in size.
The megasite was purchased jointly by the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (JDA) and the State less than a year ago.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated the project is the largest economic development project in Georgia's history.
Hyundai Motor Group expects to begin construction on the new facility in January 2023.
Full production is expected in the first half of 2025.
The facility will have an annual capacity of 300,000 units.
Wages throughout the project will be well-paying advanced manufacturing positions competitive in the local market.
Hyundai Motor Group cited speed-to-market, workforce, and the state’s ability to meet the company’s carbon neutrality standards as reasons for choosing Georgia.
Georgia is home to an existing network of Hyundai subsidiaries and suppliers.
The Bryan County Megasite was developed by the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor JDA over several years in partnership with the state.
Industrial utilities are adjacent to the site, and extensive due diligence reports have been completed.
Hyundai Motor Group plans to produce a diverse range of innovative full electric vehicles at the new Georgia highly connected, automated, and flexible EV smart factory.
Details of production models will be shared at later dates.
Through the battery manufacturing facility, Hyundai Motor Group aims to establish a stable supply chain for EV battery and other EV components in the U.S. market.
When it nears planned 2025 start of operations, Hyundai Motor Group will seek local vendors and suppliers.
Carter Infinger is the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority Board of Directors Chairman.
Bryan County is the fastest-growing county in Georgia.
The strategic purchase of the Bryan County Megasite in 2021 was the largest in state history.
The site is adjacent to Interstate 16 with immediate access from I-95 and I-16 to 250 major metro areas.
The site is less than 30 miles from the Port of Savannah, the single-largest and fastest-growing container terminal in the U.S.
Two Class I rail facilities on-site are provided by Georgia Central Railway.
Russell McMurry is the Georgia Department of Transportation Commissioner.
Griff Lynch is the Georgia Ports Authority Executive Director.
Georgia is the Southeastern leader for EV registrations per 1,000 registered automobiles.
Georgia offers more EV charging outlets per capita than anywhere else in the Southeast.
The Georgia Electric Mobility and Innovation Alliance is a public-private-nonprofit initiative focused on growing the electric mobility ecosystem in the state.
Taylor Walden is the Project Manager for the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
Yoonie Kim is the Director of Korean Investment at GDEcD.
The Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor JDA includes Bryan County, Bulloch County, Chatham County, and Effingham County.
Georgia Quick Start and Georgia Power are involved in the project support.
Pat Wilson is the Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner.

Hyundai Motor Group plans to implement advanced intelligent manufacturing technologies currently under test at its innovation hub, including intelligent A.I.-equipped manufacturing and data-driven processes.
The plant will help support Hyundai Motor Group’s efforts to achieve its carbon neutrality target through the active use of renewable energy for the facility.
Since 2020, Georgia has announced more than 20 EV-related projects that will invest more than $13.54 billion and create nearly 18,000 jobs in the state.
Information related to inquiries about project RFIs, RFPs listing, and supply chain outreach will be available online when it becomes available.

links.context: Project RFIs, RFPs listing, and supply chain outreach

source_title: Hyundai Motor Group to Invest $5.54 Billion in Electric Vehicle Manufacturing Facility | Development Authority of Bryan County

facts: Hyundai previously announced that once the plant becomes fully operational, it will employ more than 8,000 workers.
A South Korean charter plane departed for the United States on Wednesday to repatriate Korean workers detained last week during an immigration raid at the Hyundai Megasite in Bryan County.
Homeland Security Investigations said a total of 475 people were detained in the operation at the HL-GA Battery Company construction site.
Most of the detained individuals are Korean nationals.

According to the Savannah Economic Development Authority, the detained individuals were not full-time, permanent employees but were instead working temporary construction and installation jobs at the site.
The raid has raised broader questions about local employment versus foreign employment at the Hyundai Metaplant and its affiliates.
SEDA told WJCL 22 News that Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) and its current onsite affiliates have created 2,824 jobs to date.
Of the 1,364 positions specifically at Hyundai, just over 73% are held by workers from the greater Savannah area.
Hyundai said 99% of the future workforce will be Americans.
HMGMA told WJCL 22 News it currently has 49 expatriates.
At one point, Hyundai had 56 expatriates.
The number of expats at the site will continue to drop until they hit a maintenance number.
HMGMA said it will always maintain an expat presence to assist in communication with Hyundai’s main office in Korea, which is the same as any other foreign-based company.
source_title: Questions arise about how many Georgians work at Hyundai megasite - WJCL

canonical_name: Hyundai–LG Energy Solution Joint Venture Battery Plant
facts: Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) announced the arrest of 475 illegal migrants during a major immigration enforcement raid on Thursday at a Hyundai electric car battery factory in Georgia.

HSI Atlanta Special Agent in Charge Steven Schrank noted that while the raid was at a Hyundai facility, not all the migrants worked for the parent company and some worked for subcontractors at the site.

Steven Schrank stated during a news conference on Friday: 'We are sending a clear and unequivocal message that those who exploit our workforce, undermine our economy and violate federal laws will be held accountable.'

Several other agencies, including U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), took part in the raid at the Hyundai–LG Energy Solution battery plant construction site in Ellabell, which is located about 20 miles outside Savannah.
The raid was the largest single site operation HSI has carried out and follows major raids at other workplaces over the last few months under the Trump administration.

The facility, a $4.3 billion to $7.6 billion Hyundai–LGES joint venture, is slated to supply batteries to Hyundai’s nearby Savannah EV plant and stands as the largest single industrial investment in Georgia’s history.
Construction at the site, which stretches over 3,000 acres, has been suspended, but there was no interruption at the adjacent EV manufacturing facility.
The South Korean foreign ministry expressed 'concern and regret' over the raid and sent a counselor and embassy officials to the location.

Lee Jae-woong, a spokesperson for South Korea’s foreign ministry, said on Friday: 'Our companies’ economic activities and our people’s rights should not be infringed unfairly in the US legal enforcement process.'
Georgia State Patrol troopers blocked roads to the Hyundai site, and the Georgia Department of Public Safety confirmed they were dispatched to assist federal authorities.
The raid comes after South Korea agreed to $350 billion worth of investments in the U.S. as part of a trade deal struck in July.
LGES told the Financial Times that the battery plant under construction is scheduled to begin operations at the end of this year.

LGES told Fox News Digital: 'We are closely monitoring the situation and gathering all relevant details. Our top priority is always ensuring the safety and well-being of our employees and partners. We will fully cooperate with the relevant authorities.'
Hyundai said it is aware of the raid and believes that none of its workers were detained.

Hyundai told Fox News Digital: 'As of today, it is our understanding that none of those detained is directly employed by Hyundai Motor Company. We prioritize the safety and well-being of everyone working at the site and comply with all laws and regulations wherever we operate.'

The Department of Homeland Security said ICE HSI and other law enforcement offices 'executed a judicial search warrant as part of an ongoing criminal investigation into allegations of unlawful employment practices and other serious federal crimes.'

DHS told Fox News Digital: 'This operation underscores our commitment to protecting jobs for Georgians, ensuring a level playing field for businesses that comply with the law, safeguarding the integrity of our economy, and protecting workers from exploitation.'

DHS stated the investigation is focused on ensuring accountability for those who violate the law and upholding the rule of law, requiring collaboration with the U.S. Department of Labor Office of Inspector General, FBI, DEA, Customs and Border Protection, ATF, IRS, and the Georgia State Patrol.
The department noted that each agency contributed specialized expertise that was critical to the success of this lengthy investigation.

Video posted to social media Thursday showed workers in yellow safety vests lined up as a man wearing a face mask and tactical vest with HSI letters announced they have a search warrant for the whole site.
The man in the video stated: 'We need construction to cease immediately. We need all work to end on the site right now.'
source_title: Immigration raid targets Hyundai EV plant construction site in Georgia | Fox News

facts: Hyundai announced plans to bring 3,000 new jobs to Southeast Georgia with $2.7B expansion.
On Thursday, the company announced its roadmap to 2030, which includes plans for new electric vehicles, some region-specific, and a major manufacturing expansion.

Just weeks after an immigration operation at Hyundai's megasite in Bryan County garnered international headlines, Hyundai has announced a major expansion, scheduled to bring thousands more jobs to Southeast Georgia.
Hyundai is investing $2.7 billion to expand its Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) in Ellabell.
The expansion will add 200,000 units of production capacity by 2028.
The expansion will create 3,000 additional jobs.
Globally, the company says it plans to increase production by 1.2 million units by 2030.
Significant contributions to the global production increase will come from facilities in India, Saudi Arabia, and Vietnam.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp released a statement on X Thursday congratulating @HMGnewsroom on the announcement.
Gov. Kemp's statement reinforced Hyundai's commitment to this project and the state, expressing anticipation for their continued growth and success for years to come.
source_title: Georgia: Hyundai announces plans to create 3,000 new jobs - WJCL

facts: Hyundai Motor has missed out on the United States tax incentives for its new $7.6-billion manufacturing plant -- Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America -- in Bryan County, Georgia, but said the credit did not affect its investment in the plant.

According to industry sources Monday, Hyundai Motor did not make the list of companies that will receive tax incentives in the second round allocations of the Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Credit, or 48C, which is an allocated tax credit funded by President Joe Biden’s Investing in America agenda through the Inflation Reduction Act.
The Biden administration has assigned a total of $10 billion to the 48C program.
Selected companies could receive up to a 30 percent tax credit for their investments on American soil.
A local report, citing unnamed industry sources, estimated that Hyundai Motor could have received up to 480 billion won ($344 million) in tax incentives.

Although Hyundai Motor was not able to receive additional tax incentives under the 48C program, the Georgia plant’s construction has been smooth sailing as the Korean automaker already began early-stage production of electric vehicles at HMGMA in October.
Hyundai Motor is expected to start full operation of the new plant in the first quarter of next year.
A Hyundai Motor official stated, “It’s true that (we) applied for (the 48C program) but we did not expect to receive benefits, as those tax incentives were made for parts makers not automakers.”
The official added that the 48C program was not in consideration when Hyundai Motor invested in HMGMA.
The US Department of Energy announced a list of 35 projects across 20 states selected for a total of $1.93 billion in the first round allocations of the 48C program in April this year.
The projects were categorized into four areas: clean energy and clean vehicle manufacturing, critical minerals and materials, grid components and modernization, and industrial decarbonization.

Mobis North America electrified Powertrain, a US subsidiary of Hyundai Motor Group’s auto parts maker Hyundai Mobis, was awarded $57.6 million in tax credits as the company was set to construct three different EV component plants that will provide parts for the production of Hyundai and Kia electric vehicles.
source_title: Hyundai Motor plays down missed IRA incentives for Georgia plant

facts: Thomas & Hutton provided engineering design services for the Hyundai Metaplant America electric automotive manufacturing facility at the Bryan County Megasite.

Thomas & Hutton's services included site selection, general consulting, due diligence assistance, site master planning, project management, design, permitting, and construction phase services for the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Joint Development Authority for the Hyundai campus, including mass grading.
The planned Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant will include five buildings totaling approximately 4 million square feet.

Thomas & Hutton’s design services for Haeahn/Hyundai Motor Group include onsite road, site, water, sewer, and storm drainage infrastructure; mass grading plans; and wetland permitting to support the approximately 3,000-acre site located in the southeast quadrant of U.S. Highway 280 and Interstate 16 in Bryan County, Georgia.
The Hyundai Metaplant is Hyundai Motor Group’s first dedicated fully EV and battery manufacturing facility in the United States.
The Hyundai Metaplant is the largest economic development deal in the state of Georgia’s history.
Thomas & Hutton began working with the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Joint Development Authority (JDA) in 2019 on planning, survey, design, and permitting.
Thomas & Hutton assisted with grant writing and funding support for regional water and wastewater improvements to service the Megasite and the region at large.
source_title: Bryan County Hyundai Metaplant America Project | Thomas & Hutton

facts: Hyundai Motor Group has grown to become one of the global top three automakers in the U.S. auto market.
A CNBC broadcast report analyzed how Hyundai Motor Group closed the gap with leading electric vehicle companies like Tesla in the EV market.
Hyundai Motor Group continues to invest in robotics, autonomous driving, and future air mobility areas that competitors are giving up.
In January, Hyundai Motor Group ranked fourth in the U.S. electric vehicle market, following General Motors, Toyota, and Ford.
Hyundai Motor Group's electric vehicles feature high-level EV technology and lead the market through innovative technology such as the E-GMP-based ultra-fast charging system.

Guidehouse Insights analyst Sam Abuelsamid stated that Hyundai Motor Group’s EV is recognized as a leading vehicle and its E-GMP-based electric automobiles and future models are recognized for product competitiveness.
AutoPacific senior analyst Ed Kim evaluated that Hyundai’s Ioniq 5 is perceived as a superior product compared to Toyota’s equivalent model, the bZ4X, by general standards.
In 1986, Hyundai Motor started its challenge as a car manufacturer in the U.S. by selling finished cars through the Excel model, a successor to the Pony designed by Giorgetto Giugiaro.
Hyundai Motor quickly grew in the U.S. by achieving synergy based on the credibility of Asian brands built by Toyota.
In 1998, Hyundai Motor faced difficulties after being criticized for not having the same quality as Japanese brands.
Hyundai Motor Group executed a “fast follower” strategy, borrowing best practices from earlier U.S. market entrants like Toyota and Honda.
Through proactive efforts to regain quality reliability, Hyundai Motor Group reached a level comparable to competitors from the 2000s.
Yonsei University professor Lee Moo Won stated that the quality level of Hyundai Motor Group in the early 2000s rose to a level similar to that of the U.S. Big 3 and Japanese automakers.
Ed Kim emphasized that Hyundai Motor Group’s sincerity about quality has been conveyed to consumers through a radical warranty policy, and it is no longer a manufacturer that makes cheap cars.

Hyundai Motor Group faces various difficulties in the U.S. market such as recalls and vehicle theft but is receiving continuous praise for improved vehicle quality and actively responding to vehicle theft issues.
Hyundai Motor Group has shown innovative models such as Nexo and N Vision 74 by continuously developing hydrogen fuel cell technology.

Hyundai Motor Group is constantly challenging and innovating in autonomous driving and online vehicle sales, quickly responding to changes and pioneering the market as a leader in the automotive industry.
source_title: Hyundai vs. Tesla: The Electric Car Showdown Heating Up in the U.S.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group launched a new joint battery cell manufacturing facility with LG Energy Solution in Indonesia.
The facility is set to supply cells for Hyundai's upcoming Kona Electric.
This makes Hyundai the first automaker in Indonesia with a fully localized production ecosystem for electric vehicles, spanning from battery cells to finished vehicles.
Hyundai Motor Group celebrated the opening of the joint HLI Green Power plant in Indonesia's Karawang New Industry City on Wednesday.
The event was attended by Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Chung Euisun, LG Energy Solution CEO Kim Dong-myung, and Indonesian President Joko Widodo.
The event marked the beginning of mass production for the new Kona Electric in Hyundai’s existing Indonesian car plant.

Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Chung Euisun stated that the opening marks a major step forward in collaboration with Indonesia and is a key milestone in developing a sustainable and robust EV ecosystem.
The facility is a key part of Hyundai's strategy to build a strong electric vehicle ecosystem in Southeast Asia.
Hyundai has already made large investments in EV infrastructure in Korea and is now expanding these efforts to Indonesia.
The goal is to create a comprehensive local EV ecosystem that includes sourcing raw materials, producing batteries and vehicles, supporting charging networks, and recycling batteries.
HLI Green Power, a joint venture between Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution, started in September 2021.
The plant began producing battery cells in the second quarter of this year after trial production in late 2022.
The plant covers 320,000 square meters.
The facility can produce 10 gigawatt-hours of battery cells a year.
This capacity is sufficient to power over 150,000 EVs.
The cells are high-performance nickel, cobalt, manganese and aluminum lithium-ion cells.
These cells enhance power output and reduce chemical instability.
The cells are intended for use in Hyundai and Kia models including the new Kona Electric.
The new Kona Electric is scheduled for launch in Indonesia on July 17.
The Kona Electric is the second electric vehicle to be manufactured at Hyundai's Indonesian facility, following the Ioniq 5.
Hyundai aims to establish Kona Electric as a flagship model in Indonesia's expanding electric vehicle market.
Indonesia is advancing its EV industry to achieve carbon neutrality by 2060.
Indonesia targets an annual production of 600,000 EVs by 2030.
The Indonesian government plans to convert over 10,000 fleet vehicles to EVs each year from 2021 to 2030, totaling more than 130,000 vehicles.
Currently, EV demand in Indonesia is low, with only 18,000 units sold in 2023.
This represents about 2 percent of the total automotive market.
Hyundai projects this share will reach double digits by 2030.
The projected growth is spurred by local initiatives and competition from Chinese EV manufacturers.
source_title: Hyundai Motor completes EV ecosystem in Indonesia - THE INVESTOR

facts: Hyundai Motor Group released a promotional video titled “Ballet Parking: when robots dance to park your car.” featuring robots parking a Kia EV3.
The promotional video was first released in October of last year, with viewership surging since April and continuing to climb.
The video has been shared by over 48 global media platforms, communities and social media channels, accumulating combined views exceeding 5.8 million.
Viewer reactions include comments such as “This is truly impressive technology” and “I can’t believe these tiny robots are moving cars.”
The 40-second YouTube Shorts clip includes a “5x” marker indicating the parking sequence is shown at five times the actual speed.
A caption on the video states: “This is a dramatized video and may differ from reality.”
Hyundai unveiled a commercialized version of its robotic parking technology in June last year at the Factory Seoul building in Seongsu-dong, eastern Seoul.
The robotic parking device consists of two flat, rectangular robots that slide under a vehicle, secure the wheels and body using robotic arms, and transport it freely.
These parking robots are currently used in vehicle manufacturing at Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America and Hyundai Motor Group Innovation Center Singapore.
The spinning movements and speed of the robots in the promotional video were created using computer graphics and AI rather than being real-life footage.
Hyundai stated that combining actual technologies with AI enhanced realism and engagement, likely boosting the video’s promotional impact.
A related article headline notes that Hyundai's Georgia plant will use Boston Dynamics' Atlas humanoid robot from October.
source_title: 'This is truly impressive': Robots park EV3 in new viral Hyundai ad

facts: After years of planning and construction, Hyundai Motor Group celebrated the official grand opening of its $12.6 billion Metaplant in March.
Hyundai announced it is increasing production from 300,000 to 500,000 units a year on the Bryan County site.
The production increase will bring an additional 14,000 new jobs by 2028.
The first vehicle to be produced at the plant is the 2025 Hyundai IONIQ 5.

SOPO US Inc., a company from the Czech Republic that makes coils, stators and rotors for motors in the electric vehicle industry, is planning a $2.3 million facility in Flowery Branch that will create 25 jobs.
