facts: Kia's Georgia plant reached the 5 million production mark as of February 24 local time.
The milestone vehicle was the 2027 model-year all-new Telluride hybrid, marking Kia's first hybrid vehicle produced in Georgia.
A ceremony celebrating the milestone was held with Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp, local government officials, community leaders, and employees attending.
The Georgia operation began production in 2009 and has grown into a major local employer and a core manufacturing base for the auto industry in the U.S. Southeast.

The Telluride built at the West Point plant is a strategic model developed around North American consumer demand and U.S. driving conditions, which Kia calls a symbol of the brand's growth in the U.S. market.

The first-generation Telluride won major awards in 2020, including North American Car of the Year, World Car of the Year, and MotorTrend SUV of the Year, helping raise the brand's profile in North America.

Yoon Seung-kyu, head of Kia’s North America regional headquarters and CEO of its U.S. sales unit, stated that Kia Georgia is the only plant in the world that produces the Telluride and that this production start carries meaning beyond the rollout of a new vehicle.

Gov. Brian Kemp said the Telluride has been produced only in Georgia since its 2019 launch and has become one of Kia's most successful models, adding that the achievement is the result of a close partnership with the community and the state government.

Stuart Countess, head of Kia’s Georgia manufacturing unit, said the 5 million milestone reflects employees' dedication and teamwork, noting that the plant's flexibility and technical capabilities were proven again as it adds hybrid production alongside internal combustion and electric vehicles.
source_title: Kia Georgia Plant Tops 5 Million Vehicles, Starts Production of 2027 Telluride Hybrid | Aju Press

entity_name: Hyundai Mobis EV Battery Module Plant
canonical_name: Hyundai Mobis EV Battery Module Plant
facts: A $205 million EV battery module plant is planned for Montgomery.
The facility will supply more than 200,000 EV batteries annually to Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama in Montgomery and the Kia Georgia plant.
The facility will be 450,000 square feet.
The facility is being built for Hyundai Mobis.
Gov. Kay Ivey announced that the project would create 400 jobs.

Gov. Kay Ivey stated that Alabama’s auto manufacturing sector is evolving rapidly to capitalize on the EV revolution sweeping the industry, and that this new Hyundai Mobis battery plant represents another milestone in that transition.
Gov. Kay Ivey expressed excitement about the company’s new investment and what it represents for the next chapter of automaking in Alabama.

H.S. Oh, vice president of the electric powertrain business for Hyundai Mobis, stated the new Alabama EV battery assembly facility will play a critical role in the company’s plans to expand its production network around the world.
H.S. Oh noted that as the EV market continues to grow, having strong production capacity will be key to allowing Hyundai Mobis to see continued growth in the market.
H.S. Oh stated he believes the new facility in Montgomery will be an important step in that process and expressed excitement for the project to be underway.
Construction on the new facility is expected to begin as early as December.
The facility is located at the Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama campus off I-65 in Montgomery.
Production of EV battery assemblies is projected to begin in 2024.
Earlier this year, Hyundai announced plans to begin EV production at its Montgomery manufacturing plant through a $300 million investment that will create 200 jobs.
Hyundai’s Alabama workforce will produce the all-electric Genesis GV70 luxury SUV and a hybrid electric version of the Santa Fe SUV.

facts: Kia Georgia officially launched a hail-protection solar canopy at its West Point plant on April 24, 2026.
The solar canopy generates 10 MW of power.
The generated power accounts for about 10% of the plant's total power use.
The installation creates Georgia’s largest behind-the-meter solar array.

facts: Auto plant announcement for Bryan County megasite is scheduled for Friday.
Automaker to build assembly facility on the Bryan County megasite in the largest economic development deal in Savannah history.
Story updated to reflect a May 19 announcement by Hyundai about plans for its electric vehicle fleet.
Savannah is the manufacturing home to industry-leading original equipment makers Gulfstream, JCB and Mitsubishi Power.
Automaker Hyundai Motors joins the ranks of manufacturers in Savannah.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp will announce plans by Hyundai Motors, which manufactures the Hyundai, Kia and Genesis brands, to build an assembly plant at the Bryan County megasite.
The governor's office called a 3 p.m. Friday press conference at the megasite property.
Details on the deal are scarce because many local and state officials involved are prohibited from speaking about it due to non-disclosure agreements.
According to reporting from the Atlanta Journal-Constitution, the factory will build electric vehicles and employ as many as 8.500 workers.
The South Korean automaker has been building vehicles in Georgia since 2009, when a Kia factory opened in West Point along Interstate 85 near the Georgia-Alabama border.
The West Point plant employs 2,700 workers and operates 24 hours a day to produce 340,000 gas-powered automobiles a year.
Hyundai Motors currently owns 10% market share in the U.S. between its Hyundai, Kia and Genesis brands.
The automaker currently produces two all-electric models: the Hyundai Ioniq 5 and the Kia EV6.
Earlier this year, Hyundai announced plans to add 18 new EV models to its vehicle portfolio by the end of the decade.
Hyundai Motors will be the second automaker to announce plans to build an EV factory in Georgia in the last six months.
Rivian signed a deal in December to build a 7,000-employee assembly plant along Interstate 20 east of Atlanta near the town of Rutledge.
Rivian is a well-financed startup that has targeted the truck and van sectors in the same way Tesla has sedans and SUVs.

State and local economic development leaders have been courting automakers to the Savannah area for 20 years, first marketing the Pooler megasite (now known as the Chatham County Development Center) to Chrysler to build Sprinter vans in 2002.
Other automakers consistently cited issues with the megasite's size of 1,600 acres, not all of it developable.
The megasite is located off Dean Forest Road near the I-16/I-95 interchange.
Property owner Georgia Ports Authority became amenable to dividing the site after manufacturers cited size issues, and Mitsubishi claimed a portion in 2009.
Amazon and Celadon, a paper recycler, are currently building facilities on the remainder of the Chatham County Development Center.

Feedback from manufacturers led Savannah Economic Development Authority officials to combine forces with agencies in Bulloch, Bryan and Effingham counties to form the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (JDA).
The JDA worked to identify other locations for a megasite along I-16.
The JDA identified a 2,000-acre site along I-16 in Bryan County.

The Bryan County property is located just off the U.S. 280 exit, behind the truck weight station and across the highway from the Bryan County Interstate Center, home to small manufacturing centers such as Daniel Defense.
The Bryan County property is located 23 miles west of downtown Savannah.
Several automakers showed interest in the site, including Volvo and Mercedes-Benz, but ultimately chose to build elsewhere.
An issue with the Bryan County location was that it was comprised of several parcels possessed by three different owners, complicating negotiations.
The State of Georgia solved the ownership issue in 2021 by buying out all owners and assembling a 2,284-acre site at a cost of $61 million.
The sale was financed by Amazon's purchase of a portion of the Pooler megasite.
At the time the sale closed, officials said they did not have a potential tenant in mind but predicted it would not be vacant long term.
Word leaked last November that Rivian had looked at Bryan County before settling on the Rutledge site to build its factory.
Hyundai currently builds cars at its Kia plant on the western edge of Georgia, and many of those vehicles and hundreds of parts touch Georgia's coast.
Kia is one of the Georgia Ports Authority's best customers, utilizing its terminals in Savannah and Brunswick.
The Georgia Ports Authority will certainly be a major shipper of the EVs to be built in Bryan County.
The Bryan County site is less than 30 miles from the Garden City terminal.
The Jimmy Deloach Parkway connector is scheduled to be completed well before the Kia plant will open.
The parkway will connect the Georgia Ports Authority gates to I-16 in west Chatham County, taking port truck traffic off of Dean Forest Road and I-95.
According to the GPA 2021 annual report, wheeled cargo such as cars, trucks, buses and construction equipment grew significantly in 2021 with 668,166 units crossing all GPA docks.
The Port of Brunswick handled the majority of the growth, moving 650,000 units of vehicles and heavy machinery in 2021.
Brunswick is the second busiest port for vehicles in the U.S. with an annual capacity of 900,000.
Officials will not disclose details of the Hyundai deal until after Friday's announcement, but the incentives package is expected to be substantial.
State and local governments are providing Rivian $1.5 billion in tax breaks and other incentives to open the plant in Rutledge.
Those Rivian incentives come with a "claw back" provision should Rivian not meet investment and employment commitments.
Savannah has a history of using incentives to lure large-scale employers.
Mitsubishi's initial lease on the Pooler megasite was for $1 a year, and the company was granted a tax abatement through the end of 2022.
The building and land are to be conveyed to Mitsubishi at year's end, at which point the company becomes a taxpaying corporate citizen.
Amazon's 2021 deal was structured differently than previous incentives.
Amazon bought the site outright but will receive tax abatements and infrastructure improvements, including new roads.
Amazon will pay Chatham County school taxes.
Bryan is the state's fastest growing county according to 2020 census data, and the Hyundai Motors plant should further accelerate the growth.
The metro Savannah unemployment rate is currently 3.2%, just off record lows.
The site is located in Bryan County's northern corner, in close proximity to Statesboro, Rincon and Hinesville as well as Savannah.
The plant is expected to lead to population increases throughout the region.
The area boasts several workforce development programs.
In 2019, the state opened a Quick Start training facility at the Georgia Advanced Manufacturing Training Center in western Chatham County.
The region's technical colleges Savannah Tech, Ogeechee Tech and Southeastern Tech offer training programs.
Local four-year universities Georgia Southern and Savannah State also offer workforce training programs.
source_title: Announcement on Kia plant to be built on Bryan County megasite

canonical_name: Kia Georgia West Point Assembly Plant
facts: Kia Georgia has started production with its West Point assembly plant in Georgia for its 2025 Kia EV9 electric vehicle (EV) SUV.
This marked a historical milestone as the first EV assembled in the West Point assembly plant.
Governor of Georgia Brian Kemp, James Watson (Kia Georgia’s Vice President of Production), and SeungKyu (Sean) Yoon (President and CEO for Kia North America and Kia America) attended the celebration.

Governor Brian Kemp praised the milestone, stating he wants to congratulate the Kia Georgia team members and thank them for choosing the Peach State yet again as they work to become the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Previously, the Kia Georgia’s West Point assembly plant only had the Telluride, Sorento, and Sportage models.
The addition of the EV9 to Kia Georgia’s assembly roster came with an investment of over $200 million.
This investment led to creating around 200 new jobs at the plant.
The new EV9 production in Georgia was aimed to be eligible for the $7,500 EV tax credit, leveraging the incentive with local production for further appeal.

The EV9 has won several accolades including 2024 World Car of the Year and 2024 World Electric Car at the New York International Auto Show, as well as ‘Best of the Best at the Red Dot Award: Product Design 2024.

SeungKyu Yoon stated that having achieved prestigious accolades such as World Car of the Year, World EV of the Year, and North American Utility Vehicle of the Year, the EV9 will be in excellent hands with the team members at Kia Georgia.
Following production start, the company anticipates increased success for the EV9 in the U.S. market.
Recent reports track the EV9 to exceed the 50,000-unit milestone within its first year of launch due to high demand in global EV markets.
source_title: Kia Georgia Makes History with 2025 EV9 SUV Assembly | EV.com

facts: Kia Mall of Georgia operates a dealership showroom in Buford, GA.
The dealership serves customers from Cumming, Suwanee, Lawrenceville, Dacula, Sugar Hill, Gainesville, and throughout North Georgia.
Inventory includes 2025, 2026, and 2027 Kia models across SUV, sedan, van, and hatchback body styles.
Electric vehicle options available include the 2026 Kia Niro EV SUV, 2025 Kia EV6 SUV, and 2026 Kia EV9 SUV.

Hybrid and plug-in hybrid options include the 2026 Kia Sportage Hybrid SUV, 2026 Kia Sorento Hybrid SUV, 2026 Kia Carnival Hybrid Van, 2027 Kia Telluride Hybrid SUV, 2026 Kia Sportage Plug-In Hybrid SUV, and 2026 Kia Sorento Plug-In Hybrid SUV.
Customers have three primary payment options: cash purchase, finance purchase, and lease option.
Cash purchases allow immediate vehicle ownership with no monthly payments, and manufacturer cash incentives may apply to reduce the overall purchase amount.
Finance purchases allow customers to spread payments over time while building equity, with special finance incentive rates available for Lawrenceville residents.

facts: Kia Corp plans to produce hybrid vehicles at Hyundai Motor's new U.S. factory starting in mid-2026 to meet growing demand.
Kia CEO Song Ho-sung announced the plan without disclosing specific vehicle models.
Hyundai is the world's No. 3 automaker when including Kia Motors, in which it owns a 35% stake.
Hyundai broke ground for an all-electric car factory in Georgia in 2022 under the Biden administration, which promoted EV-friendly policies.
Hyundai shifted strategy to add hybrid cars to the factory's production lines due to cooling demand for pure electric cars and the Trump administration threatening to end EV subsidies.
Kia vehicles will represent 40% of the Georgia facility's total production.
Kia CEO Song Ho-sung made these remarks on the sidelines of an opening ceremony for the factory on Wednesday.
Hyundai plans to expand the factory's annual production capacity to 500,000 vehicles from an originally planned 300,000.
The expansion will bring Hyundai and Kia's combined total U.S. production capacity to 1.2 million vehicles per year.
The upgraded plan is part of $21 billion in investments announced by President Trump and Hyundai chairman Euisun Chung at the White House earlier this week.
President Trump announced 25% import tariffs on cars and auto parts on Wednesday, a move that may hurt companies, especially Asian automakers which are among the largest vehicle exporters to the U.S.
South Korea is the world's largest exporter of vehicles to the United States after Mexico, followed by Japan, according to S&P data.
Kia Motors currently produces most of its hybrid cars, including the Sportage hybrid crossover, in South Korea.
The new U.S. factory will help achieve Kia's target of boosting U.S. sales to 1.25 million vehicles a year, up from 850,000 currently.
source_title: Kia to produce hybrid cars at Hyundai's Georgia factory from next year

facts: Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc. (KMMG) celebrated its one millionth U.S.-built vehicle rolling off the assembly line on July 11, 2013.
The one millionth vehicle produced was a snow white pearl 2014 Sorento SXL.
KMMG represents an initial investment of $1 billion.
Mass production of the Sorento CUV began at KMMG on November 16, 2009.
Together with on-site and local suppliers, the plant has created more than 11,000 jobs in West Point and the surrounding region.
A $100 million expansion completed in early 2012 increased KMMG's annual production capacity to 360,000 vehicles.
The Optima mid-size sedan was added to the KMMG assembly line in 2011.
The Optima has been Kia’s best-selling vehicle in the U.S. for 19 consecutive months.
Byung Mo Ahn serves as Group President and CEO for Kia Motors America and Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia.
KMMG employs more than 3,000 team members.
Byung Mo Ahn stated that building one million vehicles in less than four years is a tremendous achievement for the company's team members.
Byung Mo Ahn noted that team members' commitment to world-class quality has driven Kia's unprecedented sales and market share growth in the U.S. over the last few years.
The one millionth Sorento will be allocated to one of Kia Motors America's more than 765 dealerships nationwide.
KMMG is the first manufacturing site in North America for Seoul, Korea-based Kia Motors Corporation.
KMMG is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Georgia.
KMMG manufactures both the Sorento CUV and the Optima mid-size sedan.
The Sorento CUV and Optima mid-size sedan are two of Kia's top-selling models in the U.S.

facts: RACEMARK was founded in 1964 by Bob Bailey, a Porsche of America team world-class race car driver.
Mark Donohue partnered with Bob Bailey and joined RACEMARK in 1972.
The company's mission is to create innovative and sustainable automotive floor products that protect and enhance car interiors.
RACEMARK's vision is to be the global leader in sustainable automotive floor protection and design by reducing environmental impact, innovating products and processes, and expanding into new markets.
Core company values include Respect, Integrity, Initiative, Creativity, Leadership, and Sustainability.
In 1973, RACEMARK became the first company in its industry to create a custom-fit carpet car mat.
The company is recognized as the reference point for innovative and stylish car mats in the automotive market.
High material standards, competitive manufacturing processes, and dedication to quality result in optimized product life cycle cost.
Capabilities include high volume production as well as premium and luxury designs for customized products supporting a wide range of vehicle segments.
RACEMARK International includes manufacturing plants located in the United States, Germany, Hungary, and Switzerland.
The company maintains a strong presence in North America and Europe while following international market trends of the highest quality brands in the automotive and textile industries.
RACEMARK delivers features and product enhancements to achieve “best in class” parts for the global automotive industry.
The company has expanded globally to supply just-in-time delivery to customer assembly plants, enabling the shortest lead-times possible at a very competitive price.
Manufacturing plants are dedicated to producing automobile floor mats for premier brands in the automotive industry.
RACEMARK products are installed in the most luxurious automobiles in the world.
In 1970, founder Bob Bailey placed 2nd in Class racing for the Porsche Team of America at the Sebring 12-Hour Grand Prix alongside Mario Andretti and Steve McQueen.

The company produces high-quality products supported by high-performance marketing.
Racemark's core values include innovative features, technical excellence, and environmental responsibility.
Racemark is a privately held OEM supplier to the global automotive industry.
The company was founded in 1972.
Racemark originally served only the automotive aftermarket before evolving to an Automotive OEM supplier due to its high quality and innovative products.
Racemark's products are positioned at the premium end of the market.

All major decisions at Racemark are made with the involvement of a cross-functional team.

These attributes have enabled Racemark to maintain exclusive customer relationships for over 25 years.

Racemark operates within the Automotive industry.

facts: On May 4, 2021, Superior Essex concluded an agreement to become the eighth corporate partner of The Copper Mark.
The partnership aligns with Superior Essex's corporate sustainability goals and its recently announced Vision 2030 for the Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire brand.
The initiative aims to responsibly source raw materials used in its magnet wire products.
Superior Essex Communications, a leading global manufacturer of communication cables, has been a leader in sustainability since 2011.
The company is working to significantly reduce the impact of its products by seeking responsibly sourced materials in collaboration with its copper suppliers.
The Copper Mark is an assurance framework used to demonstrate responsible production practices in the copper industry and the industry's contribution to UN Sustainable Development Goals.
The framework evaluates copper producers against criteria covering 32 different environmental, social, and governance problem areas associated with mineral and metal sourcing.
The Copper Mark aims to establish good management practices derived from voluntary sustainability standards commonly used in mineral and metal supply chains.

Scott Wilde, Global Vice President of Strategic Sourcing at Superior Essex, stated that aligning responsible material sourcing is key to achieving the company's goals and meeting commercial partner needs.
CEO Brian Kim recently launched commitments for Vision 2030, framing the partnership as a challenge to be forward-looking with green production and improving products from the source.
The commitment demonstrates how procurement and sustainability efforts can work together within and outside the organization.
Many commercial partners have changed their sustainability values, ensuring Superior Essex can be part of their solutions.
Being one of the first to adopt The Copper Mark highlights the company's belief in reducing the impact of magnet wire and communication wiring.

The Copper Mark evaluates management practices of copper mines, smelters, and refineries across categories including human rights, working conditions, community, environment, and general governance (transparency, disclosure, corporate integrity).
Company processes are measured against standards defined in a risk readiness assessment created by the Responsible Minerals Initiative, which comprises over 380 cross-industry companies.
Michèle Brülhart, Executive Director of The Copper Mark, stated that Superior Essex's commitment to sustainability and responsible copper production is key to the partnership.
Annie Bevan, Global Head of Sustainability for Superior Essex, noted that having all parties present a joint statement shows the power of Vision 2030, The Copper Mark, and Superior Essex's future.
Superior Essex is the flagship brand of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, and Essex Brownell.
The company has over 3,000 employees across 11 countries on three continents.
Superior Essex is the leading global supplier of magnet wire and is at the forefront of applying communication fiber and Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
The company's products are critical for creating disruptive technological advances in the electrification of automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex is committed to pioneering smart building technology while setting the pace for sustainability.

The Copper Mark offers a comprehensive system addressing 32 environmental, social, and governance issues related to responsible copper production, using the Responsible Minerals Initiative's risk readiness assessment.
Initially developed and funded by the International Copper Association (ICA), the Copper Mark operates as an independent entity headquartered in the UK.
Superior Essex's corporate address is listed as 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
source_title: Superior Essex Continues Commitment To Sustainability, Partners With Copper Mark - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: LS Group’s U.S. subsidiary, Essex Solutions, announced on January 6 that it has successfully raised $200 million (approximately KRW 290 billion) in a pre-IPO funding round.
The investment was led by the Mirae Asset–KCGI Consortium through a third-party allocation of newly issued shares.
This investment gives the Mirae Asset-KCGI Consortium a 20% stake in Essex Solutions, valuing the company at approximately $1 billion (KRW 1.45 trillion) pre-IPO.
The pre-IPO, directly managed by LS Group, attracted competitive bids from top investment firms, including Bain Capital, Goldman Sachs, and IMM.
Originally founded in 1930 as Essex Wire Corporation, a U.S. wire manufacturer, the company rebranded to Superior Essex (SPSX) in 1954 after acquiring a telecommunications wire business.
In 2008, LS Group acquired SPSX for approximately $770 million (KRW 1 trillion).
The company turned profitable in 2016, enabling steady R&D investments to prepare for the electric vehicle era.
In 2020, SPSX formed a joint venture, Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire (EFMW), with Japan’s Furukawa Electric to strengthen its position in the global magnet wire market.
This partnership created synergies in technology, production capacity, networks, and branding.
In April 2024, SPSX acquired Furukawa Electric’s entire stake in EFMW, vertically integrating its magnet wire operations and rebranding as Essex Solutions.
This transformation positioned Essex Solutions as the world’s leading magnet wire company, with strong market presence in North America, Europe, and Asia.
Essex Solutions focuses on two key product categories: ‘specialized magnet wire for electric and hybrid vehicles’ and ‘specialized magnet wire for large-capacity transformers’.
Its EV magnet wire, a critical material for electric motor drives, requires advanced technology to withstand high voltages, an area where Essex Solutions holds a global leadership position.
Thanks to its technological edge, Essex Solutions supplies its products to the world’s leading EV manufacturers and major global automakers known for their rigorous quality standards.
Outside China, the company holds the top market share in North America, the most active EV production region.
It also operates production facilities across North America, South America, Europe, and Asia, enabling it to respond flexibly to regional trade protection policies.
Essex Solutions aims to capture 70% of the North American EV magnet wire market and over 50% of the European market within the next five years.
LS Group plans to complete its selection of underwriters by early 2024 and proceed with a full-scale IPO by 2025.
While initially considering a KOSPI listing, the group is now exploring a Nasdaq IPO due to strong interest and support from U.S. investment banks.

An LS representative stated, “The successful pre-IPO of Essex Solutions highlights the market’s recognition of its future potential in the electric vehicle and power supercycle era. The company will continue to lead the magnet wire industry through sustained R&D investment and its unmatched technological advantage.”
source_title: LS Group’s Essex Solutions Raises $200M, Strengthening Global EV Magnet Wire Market - WOWTALE

facts: Qcells, a unit of Hanwha Solutions, plans to invest more than $2.5 billion to build factories in Georgia.
The company will hire 2,500 new employees for its Georgia operations.
A new factory in Cartersville will employ 2,000 people.
Construction on the Cartersville factory is set to start within weeks of the announcement.
Production at the Cartersville factory is expected to begin before the end of 2024.
Qcells announced a third expansion phase for its existing Dalton plant.
The Dalton plant is currently the largest manufacturer of solar panels in the Western Hemisphere.
Qcells will add nearly 500 jobs at the Dalton facility.
Total employment at the Dalton plant will exceed 1,500 once all expansions are complete.
Qcells CEO Justin Lee stated the company aims to expand low-carbon solar investments and lead the industry toward fully American-made clean energy solutions.
Republican Georgia Governor Brian Kemp celebrated the expansion as an example of Georgia being the No. 1 state for business.
Kemp cited Georgia's robust logistics infrastructure, top-ranked workforce training program, and collaborative approach as providing a business-friendly environment.
Democratic President Joe Biden described the announcement as a win for workers, consumers, and the climate.
Biden stated the project would provide good jobs, reduce American reliance on other countries for solar components, lower solar panel costs, and help lower carbon emissions.
The Cartersville plant fulfills one of the aims of the climate change and health care bill that Biden signed in August.
The bill includes provisions from Georgia Senators Jon Ossoff and Raphael Warnock allowing companies making solar panel components to claim tax credits.
The Cartersville plant will assemble solar panels, silicon ingots and wafers, as well as solar cells.
Qcells will source polysilicon from an REC Silicon plant in Moses Lake, Washington.
Hanwha purchased a 21% stake in REC last year, whose shares are listed in Norway.
Qcells currently produces solar modules with an annual capacity of 1.7 gigawatts at its Dalton plant.
Following expansions, total module production will reach 8.4 gigawatts, equivalent to approximately 10,000 solar panels.
The expanded capacity will be split between 5.1 gigawatts in Dalton and 3.3 gigawatts in Cartersville.
Senator Jon Ossoff stated his goal remains to make Georgia the world leader in advanced energy production.
Qcells currently employs 750 workers at its existing Dalton factory.
Last year, Qcells announced a second expansion phase for Dalton valued at $171 million, which included hiring 470 additional workers.
Senators Warnock and Ossoff along with Biden administration officials stated that Biden’s strategy is enhancing the nation’s manufacturing base as part of the clean energy transition.
Biden clean energy adviser John Podesta projected that by 2027, the Qcells expansion will supply about 30% of total U.S. demand for solar panels.
Podesta described the deal as President Biden’s vision come to life.
The Biden administration states its policies have driven $300 billion in private investment across sectors like semiconductors, clean energy, EVs, and batteries.
Nearly $25 billion of that private investment has been directed to Georgia.
Georgia's investments include two electric vehicle plants valued at over $5 billion each, plus a battery plant announced for Cartersville in December valued at over $4 billion.
Hyundai Motor Group is constructing one of the $5 billion-plus EV plants in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group is partnering with South Korean firm SK Group to build the Cartersville battery plant.
Georgia economic development officials have particularly promoted electric vehicles and batteries.

State Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Qcells solidified Georgia’s place as a leader in renewable energy and sustainable technology when it opened the largest solar panel manufacturing facility in the Western Hemisphere in 2019.
National Climate Adviser Ali Zaidi stated U.S. solar panel manufacturing is on track to exceed 33.5 gigawatts annually by 2024, more than quadrupling the 7 gigawatts at the start of Biden's term.
Zaidi noted this output would be sufficient to power clean solar energy for about 5 million homes per year.
The exact total incentive package from state and local governments was not immediately disclosed.
Qcells may qualify for over $65 million in state income tax credits, calculated at $5,250 per job over five years.
Eligibility for the tax credits requires workers to earn at least $31,300 annually.
Local officials reported that starting wages for Dalton workers are $17 per hour.
source_title: Solar power company plans $2.5B Georgia expansion, hiring 2,500 new workers | FOX 5 Atlanta

facts: Superior Essex Communications announced continued investment in production capacity and equipment at its Brownwood, Texas facility.
Total investments for the facility expansion amount to $73 million.
The expansion will create 170 new manufacturing jobs in the United States.
The project aims to support nationwide broadband deployment to provide high-speed internet and 5G services.
Production facilities will be improved with an additional 90,000 square feet built next to the current Brownwood plant.
The facility's total production area will expand to over 500,000 square feet.
New jobs will be created and some existing roles will transition from old OSP copper cable manufacturing to fiber optic cable manufacturing.
Environmental practices for the expansion include PoE lighting, use of reusable equipment, and exploration of solar and battery energy sources for the facility.
Brad Johnson, President of Superior Essex Communications, stated the improvements are an investment in US manufacturing and American communities, supporting 'Build America, Buy America' products.

The company supports government initiatives to increase US high-speed internet access, including the White House's Internet for All program and the NTIA's Broadband Equity, Access and Deployment (BEAD) program.
Superior Essex Communications is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.

Pollution Control hosted a meeting and tour of its facility for Superior Essex, one of its major distributors.

facts: Superior Essex is rebranding its magnet wire business to Essex Solutions as part of the next phase in its rebranding efforts.
The brand name change follows the company's recent buyout of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire.
Under the new name, Essex Solutions will encompass all current Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire locations worldwide.
Essex Energy in Italy will now be included in the Essex Solutions organization, further reinforcing the company’s global reach and capabilities.
Related businesses Lacroix + Kress, IVA Insulations, and Hi-Wire will join the Essex Solutions organization while continuing to operate under their existing brand identities.
Daniel Choi serves as President and CEO of Superior Essex.
During the transition period, customers and stakeholders may see materials bearing either the existing Essex Furukawa or the new Essex Solutions name.
All name changes are expected to be completed by April 30, 2025.
Essex Solutions will unveil a new logo in the near future to reflect its evolution and commitment to innovation and excellence.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Solutions, Superior Essex Communications, IVA, Lacroix + Kress, Hi-Wire, and Essex Brownell.
Superior Essex has over 3,000 employees across 11 countries on three continents.
Superior Essex is a leading global provider of magnet wire and is at the forefront of communications fiber application as well as Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
The company is instrumental in creating disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex proactively shapes the xEV automotive industry, as well as power, electrification, and renewable energy markets to pave the way for future technologies.

facts: On September 19, 2024, Essex Brownell announced the expansion of its McDonough, GA distribution center.
The expansion reinforces Essex Brownell's position as a southeastern regional hub.
The company renewed its lease at the facility in May 2023 to execute its regional support strategy.
The McDonough distribution center expanded from 25,000 square feet to 133,000 square feet.
The increased space enables better service for customers in the metro Atlanta and southeastern region.
The expansion provides additional space for increased inventory storage.
The facility will increase its hazardous material (HazMat) storage capacity.
A new 40-foot by 40-foot HazMat room is currently under construction and expected to be completed in fall 2024.
Essex Brownell has doubled its racking capacity, allowing for a significant increase in inventory storage.
The expansion includes an increased supply of PureNet, LS Electric, and LS Cable product lines.

Rebecca Hunnicutt, Vice President of Operations & Customer Service at Essex Brownell, stated that their investment in the McDonough facility has yielded better-than-expected results and given them greater confidence for this second expansion.
Hunnicutt added that adding additional space will further enhance the service provided to customers in the region.

Hunnicutt also stated, "We are excited about the opportunities brought by the expanded McDonough distribution center and look forward to continuing to serve our customers with the highest levels of quality and efficiency."
Essex Brownell remains dedicated to meeting growing customer needs and delivering exceptional products and services.
The expansion aligns with Essex Brownell's commitment to providing exceptional service and support to southeastern customers.
By increasing storage capacity and diversifying its product lines, the company aims to solidify its position as a primary supplier in the electrical and industrial sectors.
The facility also increases the company's commitment to safety and compliance.

facts: Superior Essex announced an expanded partnership with Waybridge, a raw material supply chain operating system.
The announcement continues Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire's Vision 2030 initiative through agile digitization of manufacturing plants in Europe, North America, and Malaysia.
Superior Essex is an early adopter of the Waybridge platform, utilizing its technology at two North American magnet wire manufacturing plants (in the U.S. and Mexico) since August 2021.
The new expansion will integrate Waybridge into nine new plants located in Canada, Germany, Italy, Serbia, and the U.S.
Scott Wilde of NP Strategic Sourcing emphasized making innovation part of sourcing and holistic sustainability, describing the Waybridge partnership as both a technical extension and an innovation.
Scott Wilde stated that aligning operations and predicting supply chain issues like stockouts before they impact customers is necessary to deliver optimal experiences for customers and suppliers.

Wilde noted that partnering with Waybridge completely changed how the company views full inventory and end-to-end workflow processes, enabling access to critical forecasting information to address issues proactively.
Inefficient supply chains and stockouts can cost companies more than direct sales losses by increasing logistics costs, damaging brand image, and hindering future demand.
Stockout causes can be traced to increased demand, delivery delays, suppliers, human error, and unexpected inaccurate inventory data across multiple points in the supply chain.

Waybridge's Stock & Flow feature directly addresses inventory challenges by aggregating data from delivery forecasts, real-time delivery locations, predicted ETAs, and production schedules to offer unmatched forecasting capabilities.
In 2021 alone, Waybridge enabled Superior Essex to avoid four raw material stockouts that would have directly impacted customers.
Through the interconnected Waybridge platform, Superior Essex can reduce company-wide Excel usage in procurement by 90% and cut email volume around procurement and tracking by 50%.
Scott Evans, CEO and co-founder of Waybridge, praised Superior Essex as a forward-thinker in the commodity space for standardizing on a common platform to benefit customers and suppliers.
Kyle McCarter, Chief Product Officer at Waybridge, highlighted that Superior Essex's product feedback has allowed Waybridge's product to continuously evolve, benefiting all customers.
Superior Essex now requires all North American materials to be managed through Waybridge and plans to expand the technology to Asian plants in the near future.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, and Essex Brownell.
The company has over 3,000 employees across 11 countries on three continents.
Superior Essex is a leading global magnet wire provider at the forefront of fiber communication applications and Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
The company plays a role in creating disruptive technological advancements in automotive electrification, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex is committed to pioneering smart building technology and advancing sustainability.
Superior Essex's headquarters address is 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
source_title: Superior Essex, Essex Furukawa Partner With Waybridge On Agile Digitization Technology - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp. (SMAC) opened its only U.S. manufacturing facility in Rome, Georgia, in 2002 and began producing the Eiger series of all-terrain vehicles (ATVs).
In February 2011, SMAC celebrated the 10th anniversary of its Rome, Georgia, plant, reporting $1.4 billion in sales over the past decade.
Suzuki Motor Corp. and Intelligent Energy of Loughborough in the UK announced a joint venture to accelerate the commercialization of zero-emission vehicles using hydrogen-powered fuel cells.
Suzuki manufactures automobiles, engines, motorcycles, ATVs, outboard marine engines, wheelchairs, and small internal combustion engines.
Suzuki has over 45,000 employees.
Suzuki operates 35 production facilities in 23 countries and 133 distributors in 192 countries.
In 2016, Suzuki was the eleventh biggest automaker by production worldwide.
Suzuki's production output reached 3,900,000 units in 2021.
In November 2012, American Suzuki Motor Corp. filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection and announced plans to discontinue building automobiles for the U.S. market.
source_title: Suzuki - Wikipedia

facts: Suzuki Manufacturing Of America Corporation is located at 1520 Technology Pkwy Nw in Rome, GA 30165.
The company has been in the business of manufacturing All Terrain Vehicles (ATV) since 2001.
The Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation operates an ATV factory in Rome, Georgia.
An ATV's frame starts as straight pipes that hydraulic presses bend into correct shapes while robotic plasma cutters trim them to appropriate lengths.
Each frame piece takes about six hours to become part of the final ATV.
Pipes of various shapes and sizes form the left, right, top, and bottom of the ATV frame, while smaller components known as 'kiddie parts' combine to create the 'mother part'.
Human welders are still required for tasks in extremely tight spaces that robot arms cannot reach.
Welders use specialized masks with light-sensitive windows that automatically darken when welding begins to shield eyes from sparks and intense light.
After frames are completely assembled and welded, an automated system cleans, paints, and bakes them.
Workers pre-assemble components by attaching handlebars and other necessary parts before sending them to the assembly line.
The Rome facility manufactures red and black fenders using an injection-molding machine.
The injection-molding machine draws small resin granules through a hose, melts them, and molds them into the required shape.
The injection-molding machine applies 3,300 tons of pressure to form the molten resin.
Some black fenders receive a camouflage coating at a different location.
After leaving the conveyor belt, workers trim fenders and apply a plastic coating to protect them as they cure.
Stickers and logos are applied to fenders at another station before they are sent to the assembly line.
Different fender designs require specific molds that are incredibly heavy.
Moving the heavy fender molds requires a 25-ton crane.
Workers use pneumatic and battery-powered tools on the assembly line to install the engine, shock absorbers, fenders, and tires.
During the visit, an ATV took 102 seconds to pass through each station, resulting in one ATV completed every 102 seconds.
As of July 2006, the time per station was reduced to 89 seconds.
The Rome plant produces around 310 ATVs daily, or roughly 80,000 per year.
The final two assembly line stations are for inspection and packaging.
A seesaw-like mechanism lifts each ATV off the assembly pedestal and sets it on its wheels.
Workers fill the gas tank and test the ATV's performance on a drum tester where drums rotate beneath the wheels to allow high speeds without moving forward.
Employees drain the gasoline after the performance test.
The factory uses between 200 and 300 gallons of gasoline each week for these tests.
Each completed ATV is placed on a wooden pallet, covered with a box, and secured with industrial staple guns before a forklift transports it directly to a shipping truck.
The Rome plant does not store completed vehicles.
Over 250 people work at the Rome factory.
The entire manufacturing process, including engine and part production at other facilities, involves more than 300 individuals.
When adding staff who design ATVs, plan manufacturing processes, and provide support, it takes over 350 people to produce a single ATV.
Alan Horne and the team at Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation provided support for the article.
source_title: Explore the ATV manufacturing process with an exclusive factory tour.

facts: Headquartered in Rome, Georgia, Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC) has been producing ATVs in the United States since 2002.
For 22 years, the SMAC facility has served the US and Canadian markets, as well as 25 other countries around the world.
Prior to setting up shop in the US, ATVs for the North American market were sourced all the way from Japan.

Masami Haga, President of SMO and SMAC, explained that North America represents the largest ATV market in the world and noted it is important that these products are built here by skilled American workers.
SMO works with multiple US-based suppliers in providing parts and components for Suzuki’s ATVs.
The Suzuki KingQuad 750 had the honor of being the 500,000th ATV to be produced in SMAC's facility.
Suzuki’s 500,000th ATV is the KingQuad 750 AXi, the brand’s flagship model and one of the best-selling ATVs in the world.
Suzuki credits its KingQuad range as the “inventor of the four-wheel ATV.”
The KingQuad line has been under continuous production for 20 years.
Suzuki pioneered its first four-wheeled ATV all the way back in 1982 with the LT125.
Suzuki’s current ATV lineup consists of a wide selection of four-wheelers, ranging from kid-specific models to 400cc to 750cc KingQuads.
Suzuki has a network of over 700 dealers, making its ATVs and powersports vehicles accessible to a wide audience.
The 500,000th ATV produced will not be sold but will be put up on display at the company's headquarters as a testament to the team's dedication to engineering excellence.
source_title: Suzuki Has Made Half-a-Million ATVs at Its US Facility

facts: Japanese supplier of auto parts to Honda and Nissan in the Southeast U.S. is set to embark on its sixth expansion over two decades in Rome, Ga.
The latest expansion from F&P Georgia will create 15 jobs on $22.8 million in investment.
Much of the $22.8 million investment will go toward a massive new stamping press and robotic equipment.
F&P Georgia is one of five locations under the North American arm of F-Tech Inc.
The company opened its Rome plant in 2001 with an initial investment employing 70 people making sub frames, suspension parts and pedal assemblies.
F&P now employs more than 400 people thanks to a gradual expansion.
The company previously invested another $31 million in 2012 during a prior expansion.
Rome Floyd Chamber of Commerce President Jeanne Krueger noted that most new local jobs are created when companies grow their existing footprints rather than setting up new plants.
The chamber has placed a particular focus on the Greater Rome Existing Industries Association (GREIA), a council of companies that meets monthly to trade insights and best practices.
GREIA addresses workforce and infrastructure challenges, especially amid the COVID-19 pandemic.

Rome sees itself as having core advantages for manufacturing, including being home to four colleges and sitting close to interstates and a new inland port that has helped lure manufacturing investment.
F&P prepares to make front and rear subframes for a new original equipment manufacturer, perhaps the joint Toyota-Mazda plant in Huntsville, Ala., that is set to begin production next year.
F&P Senior Manager Nat Massey credited Rome and Floyd County as a great home for F&P and thanked the support of the people, government and Development Authority of Rome and Floyd County.

Al Hodge, the Rome-Floyd Chamber CEO at the time of the investment, was approached via phone by the Georgia Department of Economic Development, which had given the project the code name 'Trojan Horse'.
Hodge was told there was 'something good in the belly of that horse, because this one has the potential to for expansion at least once, if not twice'.
Al Hodge now serves as chair of the Japan-America Society of Georgia while continuing his economic development consultancy.
Hodge maintained ties with Yuichi Fukuda, who led the expansion into Rome and eventually became president and CEO of F-Tech, the family-owned parent company.
F-Tech Inc. is based in Japan's Saitama prefecture and was founded in the 1950s.
Hodge met Fukuda last May in Japan during a Japan Foundation-backed exchange trip.
Rome and Floyd County are also home to all-terrain vehicle manufacturer Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp., which has expanded at least once since its initial investment.

Rome and Floyd County are also home to Neaton Auto Products Manufacturing Inc., a subsidiary of Nihon Plast Co. Ltd. that makes interior trim products for the automotive sector and has expanded at least once since its initial investment.
source_title: Japanese Auto Supplier to Make Sixth Expansion in Rome - Global Atlanta

facts: Suzuki Motor’s Indian unit is set to deliver its first EV to Toyota Motor by spring 2025.
The SUV-style EV will be exclusively manufactured at Maruti Suzuki’s Gujarat facility in India.
This marks the first time Suzuki and Toyota have collaborated on an electric vehicle.
The new model has been co-developed by Suzuki, Toyota, and Daihatsu.
The SUV will carry a 60 kWh battery pack, although specific range details have yet to be disclosed.
The SUV will join Toyota’s lineup as part of its plan to launch 10 battery-powered models by 2026.
Suzuki has yet to offer EVs globally but has been selling hybrid models in India manufactured by Toyota’s Indian unit.
Maruti Suzuki’s Gujarat facility is set to add a new production line capable of producing 250,000 EV units annually.
The partnership aims to supply the vehicle worldwide, including markets in Japan and Europe.
The collaboration extends Suzuki and Toyota’s long history of working together on traditional vehicles to battery-electric SUVs.
Toyota has reported more than 108,000 battery-powered EV sales worldwide this year so far, comprising 1.5% of its global sales, including its premium Lexus brand.
This partnership aligns with Toyota’s strategic push to boost its presence in the EV sector, particularly the fast-growing SUV segment.
source_title: Toyota's first EV to come from Suzuki India as alliance expands to BEVs: Details - Times of India

facts: Suzuki Motor Corporation announced that it has entered into a business transfer agreement with Kanadevia Corporation to acquire Kanadevia’s all-solid-state lithium-ion battery business.
The acquisition is effective July 1, 2026.
Execution of the acquisition is subject to the fulfillment of customary closing conditions.
Kanadevia began the development of all-solid-state lithium-ion batteries in 2006.

Through its proprietary dry manufacturing process, Kanadevia’s all-solid-state lithium-ion batteries offer high safety, including the absence of liquid leakage, excellent environmental resistance, and operation across a wide temperature range.
These characteristics make them particularly strong for specialized applications such as in aerospace or in high-temperature, vacuum conditions.
Through this Business Acquisition, Suzuki will inherit and further develop the all-solid-state lithium-ion battery technologies cultivated by Kanadevia.
The acquired business includes technology development, design, and sales of all-solid-state lithium-ion batteries.
Financial performance, assets and liabilities of the acquired business are not disclosed.
Acquisition price is not disclosed.
Payment method for the acquisition is cash.
source_title: Notice regarding Acquisition of All-solid-state Lithium-ion Battery Business from Kanadevia Corporation | GLOBAL NEWS | Global Suzuki

facts: Suzuki Motor USA (SMO) announced it has built its 500,000th All-Terrain Vehicle (ATV) at its U.S.-based manufacturing facility in Rome, Georgia.
The milestone vehicle was assembled at Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC), the company’s dedicated ATV manufacturing plant.
The 500,000th model built in the United States was achieved 22 years after the first Suzuki ATV rolled off the facility’s assembly line.
Prior to the U.S. facility being opened in 2002, adult-sized Suzuki ATVs were manufactured in Japan.
The 500,000th model built in the United States was Suzuki’s KingQuad 750AXi, the brand’s flagship ATV.
Masami Haga, President of SMO and SMAC, stated pride in building a half million ATVs in the United States.
Haga noted that North America represents the largest ATV market in the world and emphasized the importance of building these products with skilled American workers.
Haga acknowledged the many United States-based suppliers providing components to Suzuki for inclusion in its ATV products.
Suzuki pioneered the first four-wheel ATV in 1982 and has been a leader in the category for over four decades.
In the United States, Suzuki ATVs are sold through the Suzuki network of over 700 franchised dealers.
Suzuki ATVs built in the United States are also distributed globally in nearly 25 countries.
All-Terrain Vehicles are used for a variety of outdoor recreational and industrial purposes including trail riding, hunting, agriculture, and light construction.
Suzuki ATVs are valued by the market for their high quality, especially their reliability and durability.
Suzuki plans to display the 500,000th unit at its SMAC headquarters, where it will serve as ongoing testimony to the Suzuki team’s dedication to manufacturing and engineering excellence.
Suzuki’s United States sales and distribution headquarters are in Brea, California.
SMAC is a subsidiary of SMO.
source_title: Suzuki Reaches 500,000th All-Terrain Vehicle Milestone At U.S. Production Facility | Sugar Producer Magazine

facts: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp. is located in Rome, Ga., about 60 miles northwest of Atlanta.
The manufacturing site occupies 35 acres.
The facility contains a 100,000-square-foot manufacturing building.
The company employs about 150 people at the Georgia location.
Suzuki remains a mostly Japan-based manufacturer but is beginning to see the effects of other Asian imports to the U.S.
The company is paying attention to Chinese and Taiwanese manufacturers that have begun distributing in the U.S. over the last five or six years.
Competitors are offering lower prices, though whether they can offer equivalent performance, service, and durability remains an open question.
Suzuki's strategy involves providing affordable products with high performance and supporting dealers to stimulate sales and service users.
Glenn Hansen serves as communications manager for Suzuki.
Suzuki pioneered innovations such as four-wheel suspension, rack and pinion steering, and front-wheel drive.
The company was originally founded in 1920 to manufacture textile looms before transitioning to motorized transportation.
In 1952, Suzuki started making motorized bicycles powered by two-stroke engines.
Suzuki first entered the U.S. market in 1963 with its motorcycles.
Twenty years after entering the U.S. market, Suzuki combined two-wheeling and four-wheeling innovations to make the first “quad” all-terrain vehicle (ATV) in the U.S.
source_title: Grounded Success - Industry Today - Leader in Manufacturing & Industry News

facts: Suzuki Motor USA is celebrating 20 years of manufacturing KingQuad ATVs at the Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp. (SMAC) facility.
KingQuad ATVs first rolled off the assembly line in Rome, Georgia, in May of 2002.
KingQuad ATVs have continued to be built exclusively in the U.S. ever since their initial production.
Suzuki has been building the award-winning KingQuad ATVs at a multimillion dollar, 35-acre manufacturing facility since 2002.
The SMAC facility is located in the center of the biggest ATV market in the world.
The facility's location allows access to real-world rider feedback for product refinement and timely delivery of KingQuad ATVs to Suzuki dealers worldwide.
Suzuki led the industry with the creation of the first ever four-wheel ATV in 1983.
“This is an extremely important milestone for Suzuki,” said Kerry Graeber, vice president, division manager MC/ATV sales and marketing.
The performance, toughness and durability of Suzuki ATVs represent the commitment, hard work and dedication of the people who build them in the Rome, Georgia, facility.
The SMAC facility is a key to the continued success of Suzuki’s global ATV business.
Suzuki KingQuad ATVs built at the SMAC facility are shipped to U.S. Suzuki dealers and nearly 30 countries worldwide.
source_title: Suzuki Celebrates 20 Years of KingQuad ATV Manufacturing

facts: GMA is a membership-based industry organization founded in 2008 to support Georgia’s manufacturing community.
GMA provides monthly plant tours, educational sessions, tradeshows, and unique networking opportunities designed to help make profitable business connections for its members.
Jason Moss serves as the founder and CEO of GMA.
Moss has spearheaded the successful drive to expand Georgia’s manufacturing sector.
In 2021, Moss introduced The Manufacturing Mastermind initiative aimed at aiding manufacturing executives with navigating the complexities of the evolving industry landscape.
The Manufacturing Mastermind program is described as an effective mixture of problem-solving, experience-sharing, and partnership-building, in which real challenges are met with real solutions.
Moss authored the book “Manufacturing Success in Georgia – an illustrated history.”
GMA provides opportunities to strengthen leadership and networking among the state’s manufacturing community.
Moss will share how companies have been transformed by taking advantage of the unique education and networking opportunities GMA provides its members.
The American Made 2023 MFG Conference is scheduled for Nov. 10 at the Westshore Grand Hotel in Tampa Bay, Florida.
The conference serves industrial, commercial, and military metal fabricators, product manufacturers, parts manufacturers, machine shops, and textile manufacturers.
GMA's outreach distribution channels include Automotive Industry, Business & Economy, Manufacturing, Shipping, Storage & Logistics, and Textiles & Fabric Industry.
Moss stated that speaking at the conference is an honor and described it as an excellent gathering for informing and inspiring manufacturing professionals about the U.S. sector's future.
source_title: Georgia Manufacturing Alliance founder Jason Moss to speak at American Made MFG 2023 Conference this month - Shipping & Logistics Industry Today - EIN Presswire

facts: The global thermal ceramics market was valued at $3.8 billion in 2019.
The market is anticipated to generate $5.5 billion by 2027.
The market is projected to experience growth at a CAGR of 4.7% from 2020 to 2027.
Rise in demand from several end-use industries and emergence of technical ceramics as a substitute to metal drive the growth of the global technical ceramics market.
High energy costs for manufacturing technical ceramics impede the growth to some extent.
Development in electronics & semiconductor manufacturing in APAC and rapid growth of ceramics in healthcare industry are expected to usher in a number of opportunities in the industry.
The oxide segment accounted for more than three-fifths of the global technical ceramics market revenue in 2018.
High demand for alumina in electronics and electrical, automotive, energy and utility, and other industries drives the oxide segment.
The non-oxide segment would cite the fastest CAGR of 6.5% during the forecast period.
These ceramics are increasingly used in grinding wheels application as an excellent abrasive material.
The monolithic ceramics segment held the major share in 2018, generating more than two-thirds of the global technical ceramics market.
Demand for monolithic ceramics was on the rise on account of drastic changes in the sales of consumer electronics such as laptops, smartphones, television, cameras etc.
The ceramic matrix composites segment would showcase the fastest CAGR of 6.8% throughout the study period.

Ceramic matrix composites exhibit properties such as relatively higher strength at elevated temperature, good corrosion and erosion behaviors, higher stiffness and toughness, high corrosion resistant power, and others.
Asia-Pacific contributed to more than one-fourth of the global technical ceramics market share in 2018.
North America would register the fastest CAGR of 6.7% till 2026.
Rise in adoption of technical ceramics in medical, and military & defense industries in the region has fueled the market growth.

Key industry frontrunners include Albemarle Corporation, Saint-Gobain S.A., KYOCERA Corporation, KCC CORPORATION, Rauschert GmbH, McDanel Advanced Ceramic Technologies, Morgan Advanced Materials plc and 3M, NGK Spark Plug Co., Ltd., and CoorsTek Inc.

Leading market players include Mitsubishi Chemical Holdings Corporation, PPG Industries Inc., DuPont, Solvay, SABIC, Sumitomo Bakelite Co. Ltd., Toray Industries Inc., Röchling, and Hexcel Corporation.
source_title: Thermal Ceramics Market Statistics, Size Will Hit $5.5 Million By 2027 | Growth With Recent Trends & Demand

facts: In 2023, the global thermal ceramics market is expected to be worth US$4.6 billion.
Thermal ceramics demand is expected to grow at a 5.1% CAGR to a market value of US$7.6 billion by the end of 2033.
The Thermal Ceramics market has grown significantly in recent years, owing to rising industrial sectors and the demand for better thermal insulation solutions.
Thermal ceramics have become indispensable for high-temperature applications across industries such as manufacturing, energy, and aerospace due to excellent insulation properties.
The surge in renewable energy sources like solar and wind power has propelled the demand for thermal ceramics used in the insulation of renewable energy equipment.
The automotive industry’s evolution towards electric vehicles (EVs) has driven demand for thermal ceramics to maintain battery efficiency and longevity.
Development of advanced, eco-friendly thermal ceramics presents opportunities as sustainability gains prominence across industries.
The construction industry offers a significant growth avenue as thermal ceramics find applications in building insulation, contributing to energy efficiency and reduced heating and cooling costs.
The United States is poised to witness remarkable growth in the thermal ceramics market, with a projected value of US$1.9 billion by the year 2033.
Over the assessment period from 2023 to 2033, there is an anticipated robust demand for thermal ceramics in the US, expected to grow at a steady CAGR of 5.4%.

Particularly, the electric vehicle (EV) sector is expected to drive significant demand for thermal ceramics in the US, as they are crucial for mitigating thermal conductivity issues in automobile batteries.
The increasing popularity of electric cars in the United States is expected to have a positive impact on the market’s growth trajectory.
Key players like Morgan Thermal Ceramics are incorporating advanced fiber techniques, especially tailored for use in lithium-ion battery packs of electric vehicles.
The United States market is anticipated to witness substantial absolute dollar growth, with an estimated increase of US$794.3 million during the forecast period.
The automotive sector’s increasing adoption of thermal ceramics is driving market growth, as these materials are utilized in various automobile components like circuit carriers and passenger cells.
The rising popularity of electric vehicles (e-vehicles) is further boosting demand for thermal ceramics for applications in charging units, electric motors, and batteries.
Their use in enhancing fuel efficiency, motor management, and electronic component sensors is also on the rise.
The growing demand for lightweight materials in automotive construction, combined with their integration into lighting systems, is contributing to global demand.
Thermal ceramics’ cost-effective thermal energy storage capability is driving their use in photovoltaic cells and solar systems, supported by research and the development of demonstration plants.
These ceramics enhance voltaic cell reliability and are ideal for outdoor applications due to their high mechanical strength and stability.
The Thermal Ceramics market’s value chain comprises several key stakeholders, including raw material suppliers, manufacturers, distributors, and end-users.
Raw materials such as refractory materials, insulating fibers, and binders are sourced from various suppliers and serve as the foundation for thermal ceramics.
Manufacturers play a pivotal role in the value chain by transforming these raw materials into thermal ceramic products with specific characteristics.
These products are distributed through a network of distributors and retailers, reaching end-users across different industries.
End-users encompass a wide range of sectors, including industrial manufacturing, energy generation, aerospace, and construction.
End-users incorporate thermal ceramics into their processes and products to enhance thermal insulation and temperature control.

Prominent suppliers in the thermal ceramics industry are prioritizing the development of safer and more user-friendly components to enhance brand loyalty and attract a broader base of loyal customers.
Leading players are investing in advanced manufacturing technologies to uphold product quality and optimize their supply chain management systems.
Key manufacturers of thermal ceramics are expanding their global presence through various means such as collaborations, mergers and acquisitions, and product launches.

Luyang Energy-Saving Materials Co Ltd made a significant move in June 2021 by shipping 36 containers of luyangwool ceramic fiber blanket products to Vietnam for applications requiring exceptional fire resistance and heat preservation.

Morgan Thermal Ceramics entered the automotive industry in January 2020, unveiling systems for passive and active thermal management aimed at maintaining vehicle battery pack temperatures at optimal levels.

Market segmentation includes types (Thermal Ceramic Fibers, Insulation Bricks), end uses (Mining & Metal Processing, Chemicals & Petrochemicals, Manufacturing, Others), and regions (North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Latin America, Middle East & Africa).

The company offers injection molding, insert molding, pad printing, heat transfer decorating, ultrasonic, vibratory and hot plate welding, manual and automated assembly, and more.
The company caters to automotive, industrial, lawn and garden, and other sectors.

facts: Advantage Capital announced a $2.75 million investment in Thomson-based plastic injection molder Thomson Plastics, Inc. on March 7, 2022.
The funding is provided in connection with the Georgia Agribusiness and Rural Jobs Act (GARJA).
The investment will provide working capital necessary to execute on growth opportunities and continue expanding business in Thomson.
CEO Steve Dyer stated the investment comes at an opportune time in the company’s growth trajectory and provides flexibility for growth in the large tonnage molding market.
Thomson Plastics designs, manufactures, and assembles custom plastic injection molded products for large industrial customers.
The company specializes in high tonnage molding using specially designed models, gas assisted technology, and injection molding presses.
Thomson Plastics currently employs 167 people in the surrounding community.
This marks Advantage Capital’s seventh investment under the GARJA program.
Advantage Capital has now invested more than $20 million in small businesses across Georgia through the GARJA program.
Principal Tim Perusek noted that each GARJA investment offers a customized financing solution positioned for future growth and job expansion in local communities.
The GARJA program was first passed in Georgia in 2017 to provide access to capital for small businesses and stimulate economic expansion in rural parts of the state.
State Senator Max Burns stated that the program’s goal was to drive capital into areas of the state that have traditionally been overlooked.
The plastic injection molding industry is expected to grow to $7.2 billion in the next five years.
Industry revenue was projected to rise 6.5% in 2021 alone as the economy rebounds from the COVID-19 pandemic.
Thomson Plastics currently operates a 180,000 square foot facility in Thomson dedicated to manufacturing, assembly, and warehousing of plastic products.
Advantage Capital has invested more than $3.8 billion in companies from diverse industry sectors since 1992.
Advantage Capital has helped support more than 50,000 jobs through its financing activities.
Joe Stosberg is the Communications Specialist at Advantage Capital.
source_title: Advantage Capital Invests $2.75 Million in Thomson Plastics, Bringing Economic Growth to Rural Georgia | Advantage Capital

facts: Thomson Plastics opened its newest facility in Howell, Michigan, in January 2014.
The Howell, Michigan facility spans 50,000 square feet and is centrally located between Flint, Lansing, and the Greater Detroit area.
The plant initially opened with seven presses ranging from 400 tons to 800 tons capacity, along with an experienced technical staff to support existing and new customers.
Thomson Plastics has plans for additional investments in presses and related support equipment at the Howell facility.
The company will produce assembled components at the new location.
Thomson Plastics utilizes lean manufacturing principles and a customer-focused approach for molding and assembly product requirements.
Additional services provided include Product Design, Tooling Design and Management, Just-In-Time Delivery, Value Analysis/Value Engineering (VA/VE), and Customer Site Representative Services.
The company utilizes gas assist, in mold decoration, pad printing, heat staking, and hot stamping technologies.
All molding and other operations are performed with the benefit of AIAG APQP guidelines.
Thomson Plastics engaged Ann Arbor SPARK to assist in applying for local incentives.
Thomson Plastics coordinated with Michigan Works! on workforce development programs.

Paul Krutko, president and CEO of SPARK (Ann Arbor’s engine for economic development), stated that attracting an out-of-state company to Howell speaks volumes about the community's assets to a growing manufacturer like Thomson Plastics.
Thomson Plastics was established in 1975 and develops processes that integrate design complexity with steady and reliable production.
The company's facility in Thomson, Georgia, has over 200,000 square feet dedicated to manufacturing, assembly, and warehousing.
With the addition of the Howell, Michigan location, Thomson Plastics' two plastic injection molding locations have a combined press capability ranging from 165 tons to 3,150 tons.
Thomson Plastics, Inc. is a portfolio company of Altus Capital Partners I, L.P., an investment firm specializing in middle market manufacturing companies based in the U.S.
source_title: Thomson Plastics, Inc. Announces the Opening of Howell Michigan Plastic Injection Molding Facility

facts: Thomson Plastics Inc. (TPI) has announced the installation of a spotting press in Thomson, GA to strengthen the company’s preventive maintenance program and provide increased support to its injection molding customers.

The investment allows TPI to internally increase performance capability for tool maintenance and repair, enhancing overall product quality and consistency while minimizing production downtime and customer costs.
Though located at TPI’s Georgia headquarters, the company’s facilities in Lexington, NC, Duncan, SC, and Howell, MI, will be able to utilize the spotting press capabilities to serve their customers.

As the premier large-tonnage injection molding firm in the Southeast since 1995, TPI continues to invest in the equipment and people needed to exceed customer expectations for quality, delivery, and price.

The company is currently in the second year of fulfilling its five-year strategic plan to invest in and to refresh its fleet of injection molding equipment across all four facilities by the close of 2026.
Thomson Plastics is one of only a handful of companies that can utilize as much as 4,400 tons to turn plastic pellets into parts.
TPI has capabilities up to 4400 tons of clamping force and has numerous machines at multiple locations to complete this.
The company utilizes robotics to give time off and keep production efficient.
source_title: Altus Capital Partners | News
