facts: TI Fluid Systems (LSE:TIFS) announced it will provide a new generation of plastic fuel tank design capable of meeting stringent pressure demands within hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs).
The plastic fuel tank recently launched in volume production with Volkswagen China on the Passat and Magotan Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle (PHEV) models.
There is a planned adoption of this technology across a wider range of global platforms.

The innovative technology represents the culmination of extensive design and collaborative development, including rigorous testing and vehicle evaluations by VW and TIFS development teams across China and Europe.
Traditionally, conventional fuel tanks operate at atmospheric pressure.
The new generation HEV models are expected to resist cyclic pressure up to 400 mbar when in certain full electric driving modes.
To resist in-tank pressure, the new design solution introduces over-molded ball pins with snap-fit column structures that use TIFS's patented split parison blow molding manufacturing process.
TIFS confirmed that the technology release provides a lightweight plastic solution for HEVs that overcomes the disadvantages of competitor internal tank welded structures.

Bill Kozyra, President and CEO, stated that TIFS's strategic initiatives to address new growth within the electrification mega-trend include supporting all vehicle types with innovative product solutions, including global HEV platforms.
The innovative plastic fuel tank technology allows OEMs to tune the HEV performance driving cycle for optimum EV efficiency and performance.
TI Fluid Systems is a leading global manufacturer of fluid storage, carrying and delivery systems primarily for the light duty automotive market.
TI Fluid Systems has nearly 100 years of automotive fluid systems experience.
TI Fluid Systems has manufacturing facilities in 108 locations across 28 countries serving all major global OEMs.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems Launches Innovative HEV Plastic Fuel Tank Technology With Volkswagen

facts: ABC Technologies agreed to buy British car parts firm TI Fluid Systems in a £1bn deal.
ABC is owned by investment giant Apollo.
ABC offered 200p for each TI Fluid Systems share – representing a 53% premium to its closing share price on August 21, before ABC’s initial bid.
The deal places TI Fluid System's market capital at £1.04bn.
TI Fluid Systems has its roots in Oxford.
TI Fluid Systems is known for making thermal management gear and fuel systems for vehicles including battery-electric, hybrid and internal combustion engine cars.
TI Fluid Systems supplies its products around the world including the US, Canada, Europe, to China and India.
ABC sees the deal as a way helping it to grow internationally.
Tim Cobbold is TI Fluid Systems' chair.

Tim Cobbold said: "The combination will result in a larger, more diversified business with a broader range of products and customers, better positioned to navigate the current challenges facing the automotive industry and deliver sustainable long-term growth."
source_title: TI Fluid Systems agrees £1bn takeover by Canadian rival ABC Technologies  - The Mirror

facts: TI Fluid Systems plc announced the release of its Annual Report and Accounts 2023, Sustainability Report 2023, and Notice of the Annual General Meeting 2024 on 4 April 2024.
The AGM is scheduled for 9 am on Tuesday 14 May 2024 at Latham & Watkins (London) LLP offices in London.
Shareholder registration for the AGM will be available from 8.30 am.
The Board intends to recommend a final dividend of 4.53 Euro cents per share, amounting to €23.2 million.
Subject to shareholder approval at the AGM on 14 May 2024, the final dividend will be paid on 21 June 2024 to shareholders on the register at the close of business on 24 May 2024.
The dividend will be converted to Sterling at a fixed rate on the payment day.
TI Fluid Systems is a global innovator of thermal and fluid system solutions for current and developing vehicle architectures.
The company serves all major automotive manufacturers with more than 100 years of automotive supply experience.
TI Fluid Systems operates across 27 countries.
Management is pivoting to electrification as part of the 'Taking-the-Turn' strategy.
Technological agility to develop and adapt product offerings to meet EV requirements of customers is critical.
Vehicle electrification impacts human resource management, requiring sufficient skilled personnel for product design and development.
The company faces risks from geopolitical tensions, supply chain challenges, and evolving electrification trends.
Inflationary pressures across operations impact cost increases, recovery through pricing, and efficiency.
Climate change drives technology and product-development risk as vehicle electrification continues across the industry.
The company is transitioning to lower-carbon, more efficient manufacturing operations to address physical risks to facilities.
Increasing regulations on plastic use and sustainable supply chains are areas of focus for the sustainability strategy.
Potential delays in regulatory changes mandating vehicle electrification may extend timelines for return on new technology investments but expand opportunities for conventional ICE vehicles.
Management is monitoring the potential impact of generative artificial intelligence on the labour force.
The Group holds 97% of the shares in Bundy India Ltd.
During the year, the Group purchased €4.3 million worth of goods from Gentherm Incorporated (which acquired supplier Alfmeier Precision SE).
Bain Capital, a significant shareholder, charged the company €0.02 million for passed-through costs from a third-party provider.
Hans Dieltjens serves as Chief Executive Officer and President.
Alexander De Bock serves as Chief Financial Officer.
The responsibility statement was approved by the Board of Directors on 11 March 2024.
source_title: Annual Report and Accounts and AGM – Company Announcement - FT.com

facts: ABC Technologies announced a recommended all-cash offer to acquire the entire issued and to be issued ordinary share capital of TI Fluid Systems plc.
The acquisition will be executed by ABC Technologies Acquisitions Limited.
TI Fluid Systems is a London-listed, market-leading global manufacturer of thermal and fluid system solutions for the full range of current and developing vehicle architectures.
TI Fluid Systems operates across 27 countries and serves all major automotive manufacturers.
The combined entity would create an industry leader with an extensive global product portfolio and financial strength to support long-term growth objectives.
The transaction provides access to a broader and more diversified range of customers, including some of the largest and most recognizable automotive OEMs and Tier One suppliers worldwide.

Terry Campbell, President and CEO of ABC Technologies, stated the transaction is a transformative strategic opportunity that unlocks value for all stakeholders and provides a platform for further growth.
ABC Technologies is majority-owned by funds managed by affiliates of Apollo Global Management, Inc.
Shareholders of TI Fluid Systems will be entitled to receive 200 pence per share under the terms of the transaction.
The deal values TI Fluid Systems at an enterprise value of approximately £1,831 million.
The acquisition is expected to complete in H1 2025, subject to shareholder and other relevant legal and regulatory approvals.
Lazard acted as lead financial advisor to ABC Technologies.
Citi, TD Securities, and Scotiabank also acted as financial advisers for the transaction.
Kirkland & Ellis International acted as legal advisor to ABC Technologies.
Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison acted as legal advisor for regulatory and financing matters.
source_title: ABC Technologies Announces Recommended Offer To Acquire TI Fluid Systems - ABC Technologies

facts: TI Fluid Systems is an automotive fluid technology company.
The company announced three executive-level appointments on Tuesday.
Hans Dieltjens will assume the role of president and chief executive officer on 1 October.
Dieltjens will succeed William Kozyra, who will retire on 1 October and step down from the board of directors.
The company stated Dieltjens would assume leadership at a "strategically-important time" as it accelerates its electric vehicle strategy.
TI Fluid Systems is reinforcing its position as a provider of thermal solutions for the automotive industry.
Hans Dieltjens holds a master's degree in electromechanical engineering with 25 years of automotive experience in Asia, Europe and the Americas.
Dieltjens joined TI Fluid Systems in 1996, gaining commercial and operational experience through divisional leadership positions.

Among his successes, Dieltjens led the global fuel tank and delivery systems division in developing and manufacturing products to enhance vehicle performance and safety while exceeding strict emissions regulations to preserve the environment.
Earlier in the year, Dieltjens was appointed chief operating officer in preparation for his succession to president and CEO.
TI Fluid Systems is expanding its executive committee with the appointment of Stephanie Jett as chief commercial officer.
Johannes Helmich was appointed chief technology officer.
Mark Sullivan was appointed chief operating officer.
Stephanie Jett will join the company on 1 October, based at corporate headquarters in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
Jett brings 20 years of automotive industry experience, having worked in senior commercial roles at Valeo, Visteon, Metaldyne, and most recently as global senior vice-president of sales for Autoliv.
Jett will be responsible for sales and commercial activities, including an increased focus on the company's electric vehicle product portfolio.
Johannes Helmich joined the company on 1 September as chief technology officer, based at its technology center in Rastatt, Germany.

Helmich will play a "crucial role" in designing and developing TI's product portfolio, with a specific focus on accelerating electric vehicle product development as part of the 'Fluid Evolution' strategy.
Helmich has more than 25 years of engineering experience, having held high-level executive positions at international tier-1 automotive suppliers such as Magna, Brose, Bosch, and Valeo.
Mark Sullivan is based in Auburn Hills, Michigan, and joined the company earlier in the year from Plastic Omnium Group.
At Plastic Omnium Group, Sullivan held various positions including global president of its fuel tank business Inergy and its plastics exteriors business.
Sullivan has more than 35 years in the automotive industry, having lived and worked in the US, France and Germany.
TI's chair Manfred Wennemer welcomed the new executives and congratulated Hans on his promotion to chief executive officer.
Wennemer stated the additions will strengthen the company's ability to execute its electric vehicle strategy and deliver on near-term objectives.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems makes several executive appointments  - Sharecast.com

facts: TI Automotive has opened its new $30 million state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in northeast Georgia to produce automotive fuel-tank systems.
The plant initially will manufacture blow-molded plastic fuel tanks and filler pipes for vehicles built in nearby Southern states by BMW, DaimlerChrysler and Hyundai.
The plant initially will employ 100 people.
The company has booked business to produce more than 480,000 automotive fuel-tank systems annually.
The facility will begin pilot production in September of this year.
Brian Lindsay, TI Automotive commercial managing director for Global Fuel Systems, said the company expects to reach full production of fuel-system components for the three automakers in 2006.
The 145,000-square-foot plant makes TI Automotive the first manufacturer to build in the Gateway High Tech Industrial Park.
The industrial park was developed through the Joint Development Authority of Hart, Franklin and Stephens counties in part through a State of Georgia Edge Grant funded through the OneGeorgia agency.
A majority of the jobs will be filled later this year and early in 2006.
Training for many of the facility’s high-tech operators is being provided by the state’s Quick Start program through North Georgia Technical College.
TI Automotive will lease the Gateway Park site from the tri-county Joint Development Authority.
Incentives provided through the State of Georgia include an Edge Grant of $650,000 funded through the OneGeorgia agency.
The state incentives also include job tax credits totaling $3,000 per employee per year during the facility’s first five years of operation.
TI Automotive can effectively spread out the state income tax credits over a 10-year period.

Manouchehr Kambakhsh, TI Automotive vice president of Global Advanced Engineering, said the all-new facility will use an innovative blow-molded filler-pipe process, which is a first for a North American automotive supplier.
The facility will have two blow-molding press lines with an annual capacity to produce a million fuel tanks.
The Hartwell plant will be the first TI Automotive facility to incorporate the assembly of fuel tank components, filler pipes and tanks under one roof.
source_title: TI Automotive Opens State-of-the-Art Fuel Tank Plant in Georgia - AftermarketNews

facts: The Georgia Center of Innovation is a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
The Georgia Center of Innovation hosted hundreds of attendees at the 2025 Georgia Logistics Summit.
Experts at the summit offered insights on the intersection of technology and logistics, updates on infrastructure investments, and how the state is preparing the future workforce to support growth.
Established in 2009 as the first state-led event of its kind, the Georgia Logistics Summit is one of the Southeast’s key logistics and supply chain events.
The economic impact of Georgia’s transportation and logistics industry was $107 billion in 2023, according to an economic impact study by the University of Georgia’s Selig Center for Economic Growth.
These industries supported more than 578,000 Georgia jobs, or one in nine jobs in the state.
From 2010 to 2023, transportation and logistics jobs in Georgia grew by 68%, outpacing the national growth rate of 52%.
In 2023 and 2024 alone, new logistics and distribution sector investments, including cold storage and ecommerce fulfillment centers, totaled $3.8 billion and created over 9,000 new jobs.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia’s unmatched global connectivity is a driving force behind economic success.

Decades of strategic investment in logistics and supply chain infrastructure, from ports and rail lines to highways and air cargo capabilities, have led to record-breaking economic investments and trade.

The Logistics Summit brings together private sector, government, and education leaders to learn from experts, exchange best practices, and explore opportunities in the rapidly evolving logistics landscape.
Georgia Department of Transportation Commissioner Russell R. McMurry highlighted Georgia’s strategic investments and how GDOT is leveraging technology to improve freight flow.
Governor Brian P. Kemp and support from the General Assembly allocated $1 billion to the newly created Georgia Freight Program over the past two years.

Additional investments in transportation infrastructure are advancing the timing for key planned transportation projects that will maintain and improve Georgia’s interstate highway system, roads, and bridges.
Georgia’s multimodal transportation network carried nearly half a billion tons of freight in 2019, valued at $673 trillion.
Projections show that freight volume is expected to nearly double to 900 million in tonnage and freight value to more than double today’s value by 2050.
Working with partners that include the Center of Innovation and the Georgia Institute of Technology, GDOT is focusing on enhancing safety and efficiency.
GDOT projects include adding 50% more truck parking and installing fiber internet on Georgia interstates.
Georgia Ports Authority Vice President of Operations Susan Gardner provided updates on strategic investments to expand capacity at the ports.
Georgia Ports Authority (GPA) is leveraging live data to improve safety, track vessel productivity and containers, and eliminate congestion.
Gardner emphasized building a technological culture and prioritizing hiring creative employees, as well as harnessing data insights to boost efficiency.
GPA is investing in $4.5 billion in improvements over the next decade as part of its port master plan to expand cargo handling capabilities and support future supply chain requirements.

This year’s feature panel, “AI and Beyond: Embracing Digital Transformation in Logistics,” included leadership from The Home Depot, Havertys Furniture, and TOTO USA, as well as research perspectives from the Georgia Institute of Technology.
Panelists highlighted the ways digital technologies are reshaping supply chains, including a three- to five-year outlook for the industry.
Center of Innovation Executive Director David Nuckolls stated that digital technologies are reshaping supply chains in various ways and Georgia is working to stay ahead of the curve.
The Center of Innovation and its specialized logistics team work alongside this dynamic industry, helping to position businesses for growth.

Educating the needed talent was the focus of the event’s final panel, with University System of Georgia Chancellor Sonny Perdue and Technical College System of Georgia Commissioner Greg Dozier providing updates on how the state’s post-secondary institutions are developing a globally competitive workforce.

Discussion focused on how these institutions are ensuring the skills they are teaching match the jobs logistics companies are looking for, including creative problem-solving and effective use of new AI and automation tools.

The breadth of Georgia’s technical college programs was discussed, including the High Demand Career Initiatives program and a pilot program called “Dual Achievement” that enrolls students who withdrew from high school in a technical college program.
The “Dual Achievement” program enables students to earn a high school diploma alongside a technical college certificate, diploma, or degree.

Panelists focused on the importance of helping students get where they want to go faster, upskilling the existing workforce, and how connections between industry leaders and educators can help foster greater outcomes.
The Center of Innovation’s Logistics Industry Advisory Board recognized the winners of the inaugural Future Innovators in Supply Chain competition.
The competition invited college students to create videos about supply chain careers, reflecting the Center’s commitment to developing future logistics leaders.
Led by professor Parisa Pooyan, student team “The Masters of Logistinomics” from Kennesaw State University won first prize and a $3,000 grant for the university.
Eli Hampton, Angeline Harris, Joe Johnson, and Dana Pazhouhesh created the winning video.
Participants in the 2025 Georgia Logistics Summit also included leaders from S&P Global Market Intelligence, Boost Phase Ventures, and M.D. Livingstone Consulting.
The Center of Innovation helps Georgia businesses find inspired solutions to challenges and opportunities by connecting them with experts, external partners, and independent mentors.
The Center encourages collaboration across six key industries: Aerospace, AgTech, Energy Technology, Information Technology, Logistics, and Manufacturing.

GDEcD is the state’s sales and marketing arm and lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging expansion of existing industry and small businesses, and locating new markets for Georgia products.

Members of the Georgia Center of Innovation – Logistics Advisory Board gathered at the summit, where each received a personal note of recognition from Governor Brian Kemp for three years of dedicated service.
Their collective efforts have advanced freight and logistics across Georgia, strengthening collaboration between industry, government, and academia to drive economic growth.
Chris Gaffney of Georgia Tech’s Supply Chain and Logistics Institute addressed the summit, reflecting on the power of public-private collaboration.

links.context: Summit information and next year's plans

source_title: Georgia Center of Innovation Hosts 2025 Logistics Summit | Supply Chain and Logistics Institute

facts: British car parts maker TI Fluid Systems has approved a £1bn takeover offer from Canadian firm ABC Technologies.
Under the terms of the transaction, TI's investors will receive 200p per share.
The acquisition represents a 47.3 per cent premium to TI's closing share price of 135.8p on 12 September.
ABC Technologies makes vehicle components including defrosters, door panels, and fuel tanks for some of the world's largest automakers.
ABC made two unsolicited bids earlier this summer worth 165p and 176p per share, which TI initially turned down as too low.
The City's Takeover Panel extended the deadline for ABC to make a 'put up or shut up' offer to 5pm on the day of publication.
Both companies expect the acquisition to be finalised in the first half of 2025, subject to approval from TI shareholders representing at least three-quarters of votes.
Tim Cobbold, chair of TI Fluid Systems, stated the enlarged business would be better positioned to navigate current automotive industry challenges and deliver sustainable long-term growth.
He added that the offer represents an attractive premium and provides shareholders with certainty of a cash consideration reflecting TI's fundamental strengths.
While officially founded in 2000, TI's roots trace back to the Bundy Corporation, established in 1922, which supplied fuel lines for the Ford Model T.

Dan Coatsworth, investment analyst at AJ Bell, noted that TI Fluid struggled to generate investor excitement and traded on valuations below eight times 12-month forward earnings since 2022, making it vulnerable to a bid.
TI Fluid Systems shares were up 1.8 per cent at 192.6p on late Friday afternoon following the announcement.
source_title: British car parts maker TI Fluid Systems approves £1bn takeover offer from Canadian firm | This is Money

facts: Announced annual results for the twelve months ended 31 March, 2026.
Revenue amounted to HK$5,609 million.
Gross profit amounted to HK$1,375 million with a gross margin of 24.5%.
Net profit was adjusted to HK$49.5 million with a net profit margin of 0.9%.
Total assets reached HK$13.3 billion, representing a year-on-year increase of 11.2%.
Maintained a net cash position of HK$1.32 billion.
Operates three core business segments: die-casting for metal blank forming, injection molding for plastic part forming, and CNC machining centre for high-precision finishing.
Die-casting machinery business revenue was HK$3,663 million, accounting for 65.3% of total revenue, with automotive vehicles industries as the primary revenue source.
Injection molding segment generated revenue of HK$1,798 million (32.1% of total) and achieved a three-year compound annual growth rate of 38.7%.
Revenue from the toy industry within injection molding grew over 55% for a second consecutive year, contributing 16.7% to revenue.
CNC machining centre business revenue was HK$148 million (2.6% of total).
R&D expenses reached HK$318 million during the year, a year-on-year increase of 24.6%.
Commercialised the world’s first 16,000T ultra-large intelligent die-casting unit.
Continues to secure and deliver multiple 9000T-class ultra-large die casting machines.
Developed 6,000T–16,000T ultra-large intelligent die-casting cells integrating digital twin technology with the LK-NET cloud die-casting management system.
Solutions are fully compatible with mass production requirements for full vehicle body structural components of automobile manufacturers.
Ranked first globally in cumulative number of ultra-large die-casting machines delivered as at the end of the year.
TPI semi-solid magnesium alloy forming technology covers small precision parts to ultra-large structural parts and supports modular transformation of traditional die-casting equipment.
Modular transformation enables a 50% reduction in energy consumption and a 20% increase in product toughness.
Secured an order from a leading OEM for its self-developed 5,000-tonne TPI semi-solid forming module to produce inner tailgate panels for new energy vehicles.
Integrating nine separate sheet metal parts into one component achieves a 45% reduction in weight and increases material utilisation to 70%.
Orders for TPI magnesium alloy equipment exceeded RMB100 million in shipment value.
Revenue from the related magnesium alloy business increased by 291.1% in the second half of the financial year compared with the first half.
Developed zirconium-based amorphous alloy die casting equipment integrating vacuum melting, automatic solid material feeding, and full-process automation.
Equipment can be used for large-scale production of foldable screen hinges and medical implants.
Low-pressure die-casting machines feature innovative inert gas pressurisation and waste heat recovery technologies, reducing overall energy consumption by 30%.
Low-pressure machines are widely used in manufacturing automotive wheels and motor end covers.
Horizontal extrusion casting machine meets production requirements for high-performance components such as automotive steering knuckles, subframes, and battery end plates.
Developed an integrated intelligent machining system with a local data processing rate exceeding 90% and over 95% accuracy in predicting machining abnormalities.
Completed engineering validation on five-axis gantry machining centres and post-processing equipment for large die-cast components.
Overcame technical challenges in the high-precision forming process for hydraulic valves, enhancing in-house capabilities in core component supply.
Developed intelligent die-casting cells using digital twin technology for intelligent prediction of process parameters, remote equipment diagnostics, and digitalised management/control.
Laid out new productivity tracks including energy storage, AI computing power, humanoid robots, and specialised casting to diversify customer structure.
Revenue from Europe surged by 64.4% year-on-year due to robust demand for new energy equipment and high-end manufacturing.
Secured a high-end equipment order from a well-known European vehicle enterprise.
Revenue from other emerging markets and the Asia-Pacific region increased by 76.9% year-on-year.
Localised sales and service network covers Thailand, Vietnam, and Malaysia.
Revenue from the North American market declined year-on-year.
Mr. Liu Zhuo Ming serves as Chief Executive Officer of L.K. Technology Holdings Limited.
Owns Idra, a world-class precision machinery brand that developed the world’s first Giga Press ultra-large die-casting equipment.
Established sales and service companies in the United States and India to capture overseas markets.
Operates a casting factory in Fuxin, China, for producing cast iron/steel components.
Maintains 15 manufacturing bases and 60 sales and service centers worldwide.
source_title: L.K. Technology Announces 2025/2026 Annual Results - Asia Business Newswire

facts: On April 15, 2025, ABC Technologies announced the successful acquisition of TI Fluid Systems.
Details of the acquisition remain undisclosed.
The combined entity will be rebranded as TI Automotive.
TI Fluid Systems was founded in 1929 and is a global leader in fluid storage and delivery technology for the automotive industry.
TI Fluid Systems has over 25,600 employees across 98 manufacturing locations in 28 countries.
TI Fluid Systems develops fluid carrying systems, powertrain products, thermal management solutions, and tank systems.
ABC Technologies is a Canadian-based leader in highly engineered components.
The acquisition integrates TI Fluid Systems' advanced fluid management technologies with ABC Technologies' engineering expertise.
A representative from ABC Technologies stated the acquisition allows them to leverage combined capabilities to better serve customers in an industry demanding constant innovation.
The merger is poised to reshape automotive supply chain dynamics as electric and hybrid vehicles gain traction.
Advanced thermal management and fluid systems are becoming critical for electric and hybrid vehicles.
The unified company aims to enhance its competitive edge and set new industry standards for efficiency and sustainability.
The acquisition underscores a trend in the automotive industry where collaboration is key to navigating future complexities.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems Acquired by ABC Technologies | M&A News | Signalbase

facts: TI Fluid Systems is an automotive fluid system manufacturer.
The company updated the market on its trading for the third quarter ended 30 September on Thursday.
Group revenue was ahead 0.9% year-on-year at reported rates despite a softer global light vehicle production market background.
Revenue decreased 1.0% at constant currency.
The FTSE 250 firm said this represented a “solid outperformance” of global light vehicle production in the third quarter of 2.2%.
Revenue for the nine months ended 30 September totalled €2.52bn (£2.17bn), despite a 5.9% decline in global light vehicle production.
On a constant currency basis, revenue was 3.9%, but still outperformed global light vehicle production by 2.0%.
By region, on a constant currency basis, Europe and Africa revenue was up 2.8% and Asia Pacific revenue was flat year-on-year.
The rate of decline in North America narrowed to 7.7% from 8.1% in the first half, but was still impacted by mix and last year's strong comparable period.
By division, FCS revenue fell 4.8% for the quarter, and showed a “solid improvement” against the first half of the year.
FTDS revenue increased 4.4% in the quarter, benefitting from business launches including tooling revenue.

For the first nine months of the year in Europe and Africa, revenue decreased 2.0% at constant currency and outperformed light vehicle production volume in that region by 4.5%, primarily driven by strong tooling revenue in FTDS in advance of expected new business launches.
Asia Pacific revenue was down 3.2% at constant currency and outperformed light vehicle production volume in that region by 3.8%.

Outperformance was primarily through continued new business success with fuel tanks in FTDS, particularly in China, where the group is benefiting from automotive megatrends of reduced evaporative emissions and fuel efficiency.
In North America, revenue decreased 8.0% at constant currency, or 5.8% below light vehicle production volume in that region.
Revenue was impacted by lower launch activity in North America compared with the prior period as well as mix with the group's lower exposure to SUVs and light truck programmes in this region.
On a segmental basis for the first nine months, FCS revenue decreased 8.0% at constant currency and was impacted by lower production in China and mix in North America.
FTDS revenue grew 2.0% at constant currency, and the board said the company continued to benefit from new business wins in Asia Pacific, as well as increased tooling revenue in Europe.
The 2019 outlook for the group, provided in the half-year results announcement on 8 August, remains unchanged.
At 0821 GMT, shares in TI Fluid Systems were up 6.95% at 215.5p.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems outperforms global light vehicle market in third quarter  - Sharecast.com

facts: Hyundai Motor Group set out to build the largest electrified vehicle manufacturing plant in the United States.
The facility is the only one to combine EV and battery production at the same campus.
The project operated on an ambitious two-year schedule.
The site is a 3,000-acre coastal location near Savannah, Georgia.
Subsurface conditions included loose sands, soft clays, and a shallow groundwater table.
Site conditions raised concerns about earthquake-related soil liquefaction, long-term settlement under heavy structural loads, and stability during deep excavations.
The project scale involved fourteen buildings totaling 7.6 million square feet designed and contracted separately.
Engineering teams worked across the U.S. and Korea.
Every geotechnical recommendation had to balance safety, constructability, and cost control without slowing progress on one of the fastest large-scale industrial developments ever attempted.
Terracon partnered closely with Hyundai, contractors, and design teams to reduce risk while keeping the project moving.
The geotechnical approach emphasized rigorous site characterization, data-driven design, and close collaboration in the field.
Comprehensive site characterization required a robust geotechnical investigation to develop accurate soil profiles, settlement predictions, and seismic site classifications.
Investigation methods included seismic cone penetration testing (SCPT), multichannel analysis of surface waves (MASW), extensive lab testing, and 435 soil borings.
Refined seismic analysis justified a lower liquefaction risk for the site.
All buildings were supported on piles extending below liquefiable layers, eliminating the need for costly ground improvement programs or specialized structural designs and saving both time and money.
Different structures required customized foundation solutions.
Deep foundations, primarily auger-cast piles, were selected to control settlement and address liquefaction risks.

An innovative hybrid foundation solution used displacement-type cast-in-place rigid inclusions (CGEs) in the upper sands combined with deeper auger-cast rigid inclusions for slab support to reduce pile lengths and optimize costs while maintaining performance.
The team overcame significant challenges including site drainage limitations, groundwater control, and extreme weather events such as Hurricane Helene.
Foundation strategies were adapted, such as using driven steel piles for excavation support, to maintain schedule and safety.
Terracon’s proprietary Compass data-sharing platform kept stakeholders aligned, improved communication across international teams, and helped avoid costly delays.
Less than two years after groundbreaking, the first vehicle rolled off the assembly line.
The project was completed six months ahead of schedule.
Terracon won state and national American Council of Engineering Companies (ACEC) awards for the project's innovation, collaboration, and engineering excellence.

Key collaborators acknowledged included Hyundai Motor Group, Berkel & Company Contractors, Inc., Keller North America, structural and civil design teams, Georgia’s Department of Business Development, the Savannah Economic Development Authority, the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, and Terracon management with dozens of engineers and technicians.
Deep borings were performed using 10 drill rigs.
source_title: Hyundai Metaplant Sets Benchmarks for Speed, Geotechnical Engineering - Terracon

facts: Vanguard has added i3 Product Development to its Battery Technology Partner program, expanding engineering and integration support for OEMs developing battery-powered commercial equipment.
The partnership combines Vanguard’s commercial-grade lithium-ion battery systems with i3 Product Development’s engineering and systems integration capabilities.
The companies said the collaboration is intended to help OEMs move electrified equipment programs from concept toward production.
Vanguard’s Battery Technology Partner program supports small- and mid-sized OEMs working with scalable battery systems.
The program gives manufacturers access to Vanguard battery technology alongside engineering support for integrating battery power into equipment platforms.

i3 Product Development, a Helios Technologies company based in Sun Prairie, Wisconsin, provides mechanical and electrical engineering, embedded systems, software development, industrial design, and prototyping services.
The partnership is focused on off-highway and commercial equipment applications, including turf care, construction, material handling, and utility vehicles.
source_title: Battery integration program supports off-highway electrification - EV Engineering & Infrastructure

entity_name: US Federal EV Tax Incentive Proposal
canonical_name: US Federal Electric Vehicle Tax Credit Proposal
facts: President Biden has issued an executive order requiring the US government to purchase mostly electric vehicles for its massive fleet of vehicles.
President Biden pledged to put federal muscle to work expanding the supply of public charging equipment for EVs.
The Biden proposal aims to keep the existing $7,500 federal tax credit in place and revive it for brands that no longer qualify, including Tesla and GM.
The proposal adds two new tax credits: $2,500 for electric cars manufactured in the US and another $2,500 for electric cars built in the US by union workers.
Under the Biden plan, buyers could qualify for a federal tax credit of up to $12,500 if they choose to buy an electric car.
The existing tax credit is only available on the first 200,000 eligible electric vehicles sold in America by any manufacturer.
Tesla and General Motors have long since passed the 200,000 vehicle threshold, meaning their customers currently get no benefit from the federal tax credit.
US senators proposed a non-binding resolution to limit the tax credit to families earning less than $100,000 a year and cut it off for any vehicle costing more than $40,000.
Without reviving the extension for Tesla and GM buyers, only three EVs would be eligible under the proposed limits: the Hyundai Ioniq, the base Hyundai Kona EV with no options, and the Nissan Leaf.
The base-level Chevy Bolt would be eligible except General Motors has already run through its allotment of EV tax credits.
The base Tesla Model 3 starts at $39,990 but is not eligible because it falls under the exhausted manufacturer cap.
Under NAFTA or its successor, vehicles assembled in Mexico and Canada using parts sourced from multiple foreign countries qualify as “American made.”

Honda issued a statement saying production associates in Alabama, Georgia, Indiana, and Ohio deserve fair treatment and should not be penalized for their choice of workplace regarding the union provision.
Toyota North America stated that the policy would unfairly discriminate against American autoworkers based on their choice of whether to unionize.
Autos Drive America, an industry trade group representing foreign automakers, noted that half of all vehicles manufactured in the U.S. are built by Americans who have chosen not to join a union.
All federal EV incentives are now part of the budget reconciliation process within Biden’s $3.5 trillion infrastructure and social justice plan.
A 2019 study by researchers at the University of California Davis found that Black and Latino car buyers account for 41% of gas-powered vehicle purchases but only 12% of electric vehicle purchases.
source_title: US EV Tax Incentive Debate Heats Up In Congress - CleanTechnica

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development announced on Tuesday, December 14th, 2021, that German multinational liner shipping company Hapag-Lloyd will invest $18 million in its new North American headquarters.
The new North American headquarters will be located in Dunwoody, Georgia.
The investment and new headquarters will create an additional 250 jobs in the metro Atlanta region.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that logistics is an innate part of Georgia’s economic prowess, with the state’s economy connected to the movement of goods and people via sea, rail, air, and road.
Hapag-Lloyd is based in Hamburg, Germany, and operates a fleet of over 250 modern container ships with a total transport capacity of 1.8 million TEUs.
Hapag-Lloyd currently employs more than 500 Georgians across the state.
Once the North American headquarters project is complete, Hapag-Lloyd will employ more than 750 people in Georgia.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson met with company leadership in Hamburg during a November economic development mission to Germany.

Uffe Ostergaard, President of Hapag-Lloyd’s North American region, stated the new headquarters will bring the team together under one roof to better serve customers and expand career pathways for staff.
The company will open its new 125,000-square-foot headquarters at Three Ravinia in Dunwoody by summer 2022.
Hapag-Lloyd will be hiring for careers in finance, human resources, IT services, and more.

Dunwoody Mayor Lynn Deutsch welcomed the company to Dunwoody and appreciated the longstanding partnership with the State of Georgia and DeKalb County as they continue to grow the Dunwoody Perimeter Market.

Dorian DeBarr, President of Decide DeKalb, stated that DeKalb has a skilled workforce, is easily accessible to major highways and transportation hubs, and is a business-friendly community for those looking to settle or expand.
GDEcD’s Director of Corporate Solutions and Cybersecurity in Global Commerce Kristi Brigman represented the Department on this project.
The project was completed in partnership with GDEcD’s Europe office, Decide DeKalb, the Dunwoody Development Authority, and Georgia Power.
source_title: Multinational Liner Shipping Company Hapag-Lloyd Selects Georgia for North American Headquarters - Valdosta CEO

facts: TN GEORGIA, INC., a U.S. subsidiary of Tsubaki Nakashima, obtained Nadcap certification for Non-Destructive Testing (NDT) at its Cumming plant in November 2025.
The certification applies to NDT processes required in the production of aerospace bearing balls.

Nadcap is an internationally recognized accreditation program for special processes and products in the global aerospace and defense industry, administered by the U.S.-based nonprofit organization Performance Review Institute (PRI).
Achieving Nadcap accreditation requires exceptionally stringent quality control and audit standards.
The Cumming plant had already obtained Nadcap accreditation for Fluorescent Penetrant Inspection and Eddy Current Testing in 2024.

With the addition of the Surface Temper Etch certification required for aerospace bearing balls, the plant is now able to expand its product offerings to support a wider range of safety-critical aerospace applications.
This expansion will contribute to minimizing supply chain risk for customers.
The Aerospace and Defense segment has been identified as one of Tsubaki Nakashima's key growth areas in its Mid-Term Business Plan announced last year.

The new certificate represents an important step toward enhancing in-house manufacturing capabilities in the future and will further support the global expansion of production for Aerospace and Defense components.

Tsubaki Nakashima specializes in the manufacturing and sale of precision components for industrial applications, utilizing materials such as ceramics, steels, and plastics to produce more than 20,000 product types.
Product types include high-precision balls, rollers, large-scale blowers, and contract injection molding.
The company has over 90 years of expertise serving the automotive, industrial, aerospace, and medical industries.
Tsubaki Nakashima is listed under the prime market of the Tokyo Stock Exchange (securities code: 6464).
source_title: TN GEORGIA, INC. Obtains Nadcap Certification for Non-Destructive Testing in Aerospace Manufacturing | Tsubaki Nakashima

facts: Toyota Industries Group TACG-TICA plans to invest $44,415,543.00 to build out 206,961 square feet of new space in Pendergrass, Georgia.
The company plans to occupy the new space at 1000 Valentine Industrial Pkwy in Pendergrass on or about 4/1/2025.
TACG and TICA are members of the Toyota Industries Group and are among the world's leading manufacturers of automobile air conditioning compressors and component parts.
The company manufactures DC-AC inverters for automobile plug-in devices.
The manufacturing process covers operations from raw materials to final product.
TACG and TICA have grown to approximately 1,300 associates working together.
The company supplies its products to many of the world's top vehicle manufacturers.
Haru Ito serves as President.
source_title: Toyota Industries Group TACG-TICA to spend $44,415,543.00 to occupy 206,961 square feet of space in Pendergrass Georgia. - intelligence360

entity_name: Toyota-Denso Automotive Compressor Georgia (TACG) and Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America (TICA)
canonical_name: Toyota-Denso Automotive Compressor Georgia (TACG) and Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America (TICA)
facts: TACG and TICA are members of the Toyota Industries Group.
TACG and TICA manufacture and supply vehicle air conditioning compressors for more than half of the North American auto industry.
TACG and TICA assemble inverters that supply electronics to power plug-in devices used in vehicles.
The company distributes its energy-efficient products throughout North America.
The manufacturing campus is situated on 150 acres off Valentine Industrial Parkway in Pendergrass, Ga.
TACG and TICA are pioneers in automotive air compressor technology, with their compressors having become the industry standard.
Their compressors are used in over half of all cars, trucks, and vans manufactured in North America.
Well-known automotive brands relying on TACG and TICA compressors include Ford, Honda, BMW, Lexus, Subaru, Fiat-Chrysler, and Toyota Motor vehicles.
The facilities are located in Northeast Georgia, 60 miles north of Atlanta.
The Toyota Industries Group operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities with cutting-edge technology that adheres to Toyota manufacturing’s high standards of quality and efficiency.
TACG was established in 2004 and specializes in the manufacturing of automobile air conditioning compressors.
TICA was built adjacent to TACG in 2012 to meet increasing demand, expanding production capacity by manufacturing component parts used in TACG’s AC compressors.
In November 2020, TACG and TICA expanded their production capabilities to include DC-AC Inverters.
The DC-AC inverters enable the use of electronic devices such as cellphones, laptops, job-site tools, and small appliances in vehicles.
Environmental responsibility is at the core of TACG and TICA’s values, with a dedication to finding innovative solutions to environmental challenges.
Through research and development, TACG and TICA are providing eco-friendly automotive solutions for the automotive manufacturing industry.
Hiroto Ikeno serves as the President of TACG & TICA in Georgia.
TACG and TICA are among the leading makers of vehicle air compressors and DC-AC Inverters for the North American market.
The company rewards its team with the area’s top wages and company benefits, plus special incentives for creativity, ingenuity, and efficiency in its plants.
Associates participate in special celebrations, get-togethers at work, volunteering outside the workplace, and giving back to the local community.
source_title: Toyota Industries TICA-TACG Pendergrass, Ga Auto Manufacturing

facts: Between July 1 and October 31 of fiscal year 2021, Georgia increased economic development investments by 56% and jobs by 45% compared to the same period last year.
Since the start of fiscal year 2021 on July 1, Georgia secured $4.31 billion in expansions and new project investments, creating 11,833 jobs.
Eighty-three percent of the investments came outside the 10-county metro Atlanta region.
The 32 new projects located during October 2020 constitute a 10% increase over October 2019.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that the numbers prove their focus on protecting lives and livelihoods in Georgia is working, emphasizing laser-focus on creating new jobs and securing new investments across every area of the state despite a global pandemic.
Sixty percent of the jobs announced since July 1 were created from new project locations.
Georgia’s existing businesses accounted for 80% of investments in the state as they selected Georgia as the best location for continued growth.
The top job-creating industries included food and beverage processing, manufacturing, and logistics and distribution.
BandD Foods announced in October that they would open their eastern U.S. headquarters in Americus, Georgia.

Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America (TICA) announced they would expand their production facility in Pendergrass to meet growing demand in the electric vehicle market for their onboard power electronics.

On October 14, Hooker Furniture announced that their Home Meridian International division would open a new distribution facility in Liberty County’s Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified Tradeport East Business Center.
The Hooker Furniture investment totals $23.5 million and will deliver 50 new jobs to the area.
Foreign direct investment accounted for 23% of jobs created in fiscal year 2021 through October 31.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson met with businesses and prospective Georgia investors in Italy and Germany in early October.
Tomato sauce producer La Regina Atlantica announced they would create 250 jobs and invest $20 million in a new food processing facility in Alma, Georgia.
Kysor Warren Epta announced they would invest $27 million to expand their commercial refrigeration operations and create 200 jobs in Columbus.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia’s investments in international offices and partnerships continue to pay off in the form of jobs and opportunities for Georgians, and expressed pride in Georgia’s growing reputation as a solid location for business.
In September, Georgia was named “Top State for Doing Business” for a record-breaking seven years by Area Development magazine.

Georgia ranked No. 1 in 10 of the 13 ranked categories determined by site selectors who help businesses choose locations for long-term success, and placed in the top five in the other three categories.

links.context: View other state-announced economic development projects

source_title: Georgia's strong economic opening to FY21 continues

facts: Trenton Pressing is a metal stamping, welding, and assembly company located in Trenton, Georgia.
The company operates with 220 employees.
It supplies a number of large automotive OEMs.
It also supplies customers in other industries, such as the recreational vehicle industry.
In 2020, current owners purchased the company with the intent to modernize, streamline, and make it more profitable while maintaining jobs for the existing workforce.
Hans Pfunder serves as COO of Trenton Pressing.
John Zegers is the Northwest Georgia region manager for Georgia Manufacturing Extension Partnership (GaMEP) at Georgia Tech.
Pfunder explained plans to implement new technology to improve efficiency and communication.
Zegers connected Trenton Pressing with Sean Madhavaraman, a GaMEP technology project manager.
Madhavaraman identified an opportunity to connect legacy machinery to a Manufacturing Execution System (MES) using sensors and other Industry 4.0 technologies.
The company utilized various types of stamping presses, robotic welding cells, and other machinery that required real-time monitoring for output, efficiency, and downtime.
Machinery inherited from previous owners lacked real-time monitoring capabilities.
Pfunder worked with Madhavaraman to create a roadmap to connect the machinery and train employees on using the new data.
Engineering and maintenance teams installed new monitoring equipment and utilized software to allow real-time data tracking.
The collected data was used to predict equipment failure before it occurred.
This capability allowed the maintenance team to implement preventative maintenance and reduce downtime.
The data was also used to optimize production planning and improve employee experience.
Pfunder stated that employees like knowing how they are performing and how their work contributes to overall company success.
Using the new technology improved employee work/life balance by eliminating unexpected schedule changes and weekend shifts, according to Pfunder.
Operational improvements allowed the company to ship substantially more units.
The improvements freed up additional capacity for the company to take on new customers.
Marco Linner serves as director of business development at Trenton Pressing.
During a check-in visit, Linner discussed an idea to diversify their business by taking on more complex, lower-volume projects that could develop into long-term partnerships.
Within a week, Zegers learned that Plotmaster Systems, an agricultural equipment company in Sandersville, Georgia, was looking for a contract manufacturer.
Plotmaster had been working on product design with Bob Wray, a GaMEP technology project manager.
Plotmaster requested help sourcing a manufacturing partner with the right capabilities to start production quickly.
Zegers connected Trenton Pressing and Plotmaster Systems.
Linner met with Plotmaster and confirmed the product was a good business fit.
The product is now in production at Trenton Pressing.
The first units were delivered just three months after initial introduction, ahead of schedule.
Both companies hope to produce more products together in the future.
By working with GaMEP, Trenton Pressing implemented an MES and used collected data to improve OEE by nearly 40 percentage points in one year.
The company increased units shipped from its largest production line by 50%.
Shifts were reduced from two full shifts per day to one and a quarter.
Trenton Pressing connected with a new strategic customer and began producing products together within three months.
The company invested $12 Million in a new 100,000 sq. ft. facility to support future growth plans.
Pfunder stated that process transparency helped by GaMEP allows them to calculate ROI much better.
Pfunder noted they now have a world-class OEE, enabling them to service customers better and take on new projects.
Linner stated that GaMEP helped them become a better manufacturer and offer their capabilities to others.
Linner expressed pride in manufacturing in the USA and helping other innovative companies produce products locally.
source_title: Trenton Pressing Success Story | GaMEP at Georgia Tech

Mike Pfunder serves as the company's president and chief executive.
source_title: Auto parts supplier investing $10 million, adding factory workers in Trenton, Georgia, turnaround | Chattanooga Times Free Press

facts: Trenton Pressing produces a wide range of products through high-quality presses, world-class assembly, and other manufacturing technologies.
The company's team of engineers can help designs come to life or assist in making products more efficient, stronger, and sustainable.
They stamp out metal components using 7 automatic presses ranging from 200 to 660T.
Their team operates 4 hand presses ranging from 110 to 250T to produce high-quality products.
They have more than 20 fully- and semi-automated weld cells for metal fastening.
They employ a skilled team for manual welding processes.
Precision coating is often the next step in the process following pressing, depending on specifications.
They partner with multiple nearby finishing providers to guarantee the best application and cost for products.
After coating, their expert team provides assembly services for many of their products.

Engineering services include manufacturing engineering, design for manufacturability, product design assistance, FEA analysis, die design, robotic weld and assembly cell design, check fixture design, process poke yoke and camera inspection, and a full suite of CAD packages.
They assemble a wide range of different components.
Their assembly team conducts automated inspections to ensure parts are fully functional.

They are experts in metal fastening including GMAW welding (robotic and manual), resistance welding (spot and projection), assembling multi-component mechanisms, fastener torquing, DC tools, latch assemblies, riveting, tox joints, clinch fasteners, tapes, felts and grease, and automated inspection.
Quality services include Coordinate Measuring Machine (CMM) Layout, Quality Inspection Lab, Destruct Lab with Cut and Etch, Safe Launch, and End-of-Line (EOL) Checking.
Their quality management systems are certified to meet internationally recognized standards.
Trenton Pressing LLC has undergone a TISAX assessment conducted by an accredited audit provider, with results available on the ENX portal for authorized partners.
Their Quality Management System is certified as being in conformity with IATF 16949 by Intertek.
The IATF 16949 certification demonstrates a commitment to continual improvement, defect prevention, and reduction of variation and waste in the automotive supply chain.
This certification ensures consistent product quality, reliable delivery performance, commitment to customer satisfaction, and alignment with global industry standards.
source_title: Products + Services — Trenton Pressing

The building will serve as the company's hub for its assembly business.
Trenton Pressing described the new space as state-of-the-art and poised to take operations to a whole new level.

facts: QuantumScape is a decade-old startup that has been in stealth while developing a solid-state, lithium-metal battery for electric vehicles.
The company was founded by serial entrepreneur Jagdeep Singh and Stanford scientists Fritz Prinz and Tim Holme.
QuantumScape has raised $1.5 billion in capital through funding from Volkswagen and public funding via a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC).
The company is trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol QS, closing at $42.50 per share.
Early investors include Breakthrough Energy Ventures, formed by Bill Gates, John Doerr, and Vinod Khosla, as well as Lightspeed Ventures.
Tesla’s former CTO JB Straubel sits on QuantumScape’s board alongside John Doerr.
The battery eliminates the liquid electrolyte and traditional anode found in lithium-ion batteries, using a unique solid ceramic separator instead.
It uses an industry-standard mixed-metal cathode but creates a thin layer of lithium-metal anode when charged and discharged.
QuantumScape describes the chemical composition and manufacturing process of its separator as a trade secret.
A car using QuantumScape batteries could charge to 80 percent capacity in just 15 minutes, compared to potentially hour-plus charging times for current EVs.
Vehicles equipped with these batteries could achieve an 80 percent longer range than those using today’s lithium-ion batteries.
The battery technology is described as more compact, more energy-dense, safer to operate, and boasting longer lifespans.
QuantumScape and Volkswagen have created a 50/50 joint venture to produce the first battery factory for this technology.
The company must still create a commercial-scale factory and undergo a year of pilot testing with automakers like Volkswagen.
CEO Jagdeep Singh stated that it will be 2024 before cars with these batteries are seen on the road.
QuantumScape is competing against lithium-ion batteries, which are expected to drop in cost and increase in energy density over the next four years.

The company revealed its performance data during a virtual panel featuring battery experts including Nobel Prize winner Stan Wittingham and Paul Albertus, former head of the US DOE ARPA-E IONCS solid-state battery program.
source_title: A new EV battery shows big promise | Trellis
