source_title: Governor McAuliffe Announces $38.1 Million Volvo Group Investment in Pulaski County - Virginia's New River Valley

facts: Vanguard Truck Centers has completed its Volvo Trucks Certified Electric Vehicle (EV) Dealer program at five locations.
The new certified locations are in Phoenix, Arizona; Atlanta, Georgia (Forest Park); Charlotte, North Carolina; and Austin and Houston, Texas.
These locations provide comprehensive sales and service support for customers’ electromobility journeys.
With Vanguard's five additions, Volvo Trucks has expanded its Certified EV Dealer network into three additional states: Arizona, Georgia, and North Carolina.
Volvo's total certified dealership locations now number 50 across 24 U.S. states and three Canadian provinces in North America.
Peter Voorhoeve serves as president of Volvo Trucks North America.
Cost remains a significant barrier to electrification, particularly at scale.
Volvo Financial Services (VFS) introduced new financing options for customers and dealers to support the transition to zero-emission solutions.
Vanguard utilized a VFS dealer financing program to install onsite charging stations and dedicated EV service bays at its locations.
The financing also covered diagnostic tools, personal protective equipment for technicians working with high-voltage systems, and parts inventory for the VNR Electric model.
VFS recently introduced new financing options for fleets to support charging infrastructure installation costs, as well as service and maintenance plans.
Christopher Roback is vice president of sales and customer experience at Volvo Financial Services.
VFS now offers holistic solutions that combine the entire EV ecosystem into a single payment to distribute transition costs instead of requiring upfront capital.
Vanguard Truck Centers was founded in 1989.
Vanguard operates 26 modern facilities across Texas, Arizona, Missouri, Illinois, South Carolina, North Carolina, and Georgia.
Vanguard has trained 10 technicians, with two at each of the five new locations, to perform routine battery-electric truck maintenance and repairs.
The first five locations are now staffed with certified EV sales and service representatives to support growing fleet adoption of zero-emission vehicles.
Vanguard plans to continually evaluate each location for additional training and certification opportunities as market demand for zero-tailpipe emission solutions grows.
John Thomas is executive vice president of new truck sales at Vanguard Truck Centers.
Customers are submitting daily inquiries about electric vehicles, including those who have never purchased a Volvo Truck before but are interested in the VNR Electric model.
Volvo Trucks North America is headquartered in Greensboro, North Carolina.
It is one of the leading heavy-duty truck manufacturers in North America.
Uptime Services are delivered by a network of nearly 400 authorized dealers across North America and the 24/7 Volvo Trucks Uptime Center.
Every Volvo truck is assembled at the Volvo Trucks New River Valley manufacturing facility in Dublin, Virginia.
The Dublin facility meets ISO 9001 standards for quality and ISO 14001 standards for environmental care.
The Dublin facility holds dual ISO 50001/Superior Energy Performance certification at the platinum level for sustained excellence in energy management.
Volvo Trucks North America offers a full range of diesel, alternative-fuel, and all-electric vehicles as part of its complete transport solutions.
Volvo Trucks provides medium- and heavy-duty trucks globally through a dealer network with 2,200 service points in about 130 countries.
Volvo trucks are assembled in 12 countries across the globe.
In 2025 approximately 120,000 Volvo trucks were delivered worldwide.
Volvo Trucks is part of the Volvo Group, which also provides complete solutions for financing and service.
source_title: Volvo Trucks Expands Certified EV Dealer Network into Arizona, Georgia and North Carolina as Vanguard Truck Centers Adds Five Locations

canonical_name: Volvo Trucks New River Valley Plant
facts: Volvo Trucks North America has started production of its new regional hauler, the all-new Volvo VNR, at its New River Valley assembly plant in Dublin, Virginia.
Production began less than a year after Volvo unveiled the new regional-haul VNR.
The all-new Volvo VNR was unveiled in March 2025, with order books opening six months later.
The all-new Volvo VNR is 90% new compared to the legacy model.

Peter Voorhoeve, president of Volvo Trucks North America, stated the VNR is purpose-built for navigating congested city streets, distribution centers, and urban routes where visibility, precision, and agility are critical.
The first units rolled off the assembly line in February 2026.
First units will be delivered to Clayton, a national single-family homebuilder.
The all-new Volvo VNR is assembled at Volvo Trucks’ New River Valley Plant, where all Volvo trucks for the North American market are built.
The Virginia facility is Volvo’s largest manufacturing site globally and plays a central role in its U.S. operations.
Volvo recently invested $400 million in major upgrades at the plant.
Upgrades include a new 350,000-square-foot facility for cab welding along with upgraded paint and material flow systems.
The all-new Volvo VNR offers better forward visibility than the previous model, enabled by a sloped hood design and a larger panoramic windshield.
It incorporates safety innovations such as integrated side curtain airbags.
The new VNR delivers up to a 7.5% improvement in fuel economy compared with the legacy model.
Fuel economy improvements are driven by enhanced aerodynamics, advanced powertrain features, and idle-shutdown systems.
It features a tighter turning radius that makes it well-suited for navigating congested city streets, distribution centers, and urban routes.
The start of production of the Volvo VNR follows the launch of Volvo Trucks’ all-new long-haul Volvo VNL.
The all-new Volvo VNR delivers up to a 7.5% increase in fuel efficiency compared to the legacy model.
It offers a tighter turning radius for improved maneuverability in regional and urban environments.
Available with the Volvo Active Safety Platform with Volvo Active Driver Assist Plus.
Available with a Camera Monitor System option that provides superior visibility compared to traditional mirrors.
Provides multiple axle options, including Adaptive Loading, Electronically Controlled Suspension, and Global Rear Air Suspension.
Available in a variety of Class 8 configurations, including 4x2, 6x2, and 6x4 tractors, as well as straight trucks with multiple body types.
Built on a 24-volt electrical architecture to support advanced safety and connectivity features.
Engine ratings include the VGT engine, ranging from 405-455 HP and 1450-1850 lb-ft of torque.
The D13 Turbo Compound engine uses a waste heat recovery system to capture lost energy.
Two additional low-NOx engine ratings are available for the D13.
source_title: Volvo Starts Factory Production of All-New VNR Regional Truck | Heavy Duty Trucking

facts: Wabash announced it has strengthened its presence in the Southeast with three strategic moves in the Atlanta region.
The company opened a new Parts & Service center in the Atlanta area that began operations on September 18.
The facility provides parts, service, and truck body upfitting services.
Wabash describes the facility's location as strategic within the Southeast’s trucking corridor.
The center will offer Wabash’s Ready-to-Mount truck bodies for dry freight and platform applications.

Dave Hill, vice president of Parts & Services at Wabash, stated that the Atlanta expansions mean faster turnaround, more coverage, and better reliability for fleets operating across Georgia, Alabama, South Carolina, and Tennessee.
The Atlanta service center joins existing Wabash facilities in California, Florida, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Texas, with additional locations planned.
Fleetco, which has worked with Wabash for over 14 years, will now represent the company for trailer sales in the greater Atlanta market.
The company’s Preferred Partner Network grew with the addition of three CS Truck and Trailer locations in Georgia.
These three Georgia locations provide authorized third-party dealership services for maintenance and Wabash Genuine Parts.
Wabash designs and manufactures products including dry and refrigerated trailers, truck bodies, platform trailers, and tank trailers.
Wabash announced a new partnership with Global Tank to expand its tank trailer network across eight new states, including Kansas and Missouri.
Trailer orders increased 18% year-over-year in August, though analysts cautioned against overinterpreting the data.
source_title: Wabash expands southeast presence with new Atlanta service center By Investing.com

facts: Semi-trailer manufacturer Wabash National Corp. introduced its first line of dry and refrigerated truck bodies to meet growing demand for urban delivery services.
The new truck bodies are designed for Class 6, 7 and 8 chassis.

Brent Yeagy, group president of commercial trailer products at Wabash National in Lafayette, Indiana, stated the company is very conscious of a shift happening in the trucking industry fueled by re-urbanization and e-commerce trends.
Wabash stated its mission is to help customers be successful and support them as they work to meet final-mile delivery needs with the new truck body line.
The new truck bodies will be displayed at the International Foodservice Distributors Association conference Oct. 27-28 in Phoenix.
Production of truck bodies and other related equipment will take place at a dedicated facility in Lafayette and will ramp up over the next 18 months.

Wabash is a leading manufacturer of dry and refrigerated trailers, platform and bulk tank trailers, intermodal equipment, aircraft refueling equipment, structural composite panels and trailer aerodynamic devices.
source_title: Trailer Maker Wabash National Introduces First Truck Bodies - TT

facts: Wabash National Corp (WNC) has long been a bellwether for the North American transportation and logistics sector.
WNC's Q2 2025 consolidated revenue was $459 million, falling short of expectations.
WNC reported a razor-thin gross margin of 9% in Q2 2025.
WNC's adjusted EBITDA for Q2 2025 was just $16 million, representing 3.6% of sales.
WNC posted an adjusted net loss of $6.1 million, or $0.15 per share, in Q2 2025.
The Transportation Solutions segment generates 87% of WNC's revenue.
The Transportation Solutions segment saw a sequential decline in average sales prices due to a shift toward lower-margin drive-in units.
WNC revised its 2025 revenue guidance down to $1.6 billion, a $100 million reduction from earlier forecasts.
WNC's backlog stands at only $1 billion, down from $1.5 billion in early 2024.
WNC slashed its 2025 capital expenditure plans to $30–40 million, a 30% reduction from initial projections.
Inflationary pressures and supply chain bottlenecks are persisting for WNC.
WNC faced a $10.4 million hit from share repurchases in Q2 2025.
A lingering 2019 motor vehicle accident case could trigger additional costs for WNC.
Inflation-driven pricing adjustments for 2026 orders may offer some relief but risk eroding customer relationships.
WNC's Upfit business segment is on track to surpass 2,000 units in 2025.
WNC's Preferred Parts Network (PPN) is expanding to 110+ locations.
The Parts and Services segment projects 20% revenue growth in the second half of 2025.
WNC's liquidity was $312 million as of June 30, 2025.
WNC's Q3 guidance forecasts $390–430 million in revenue and a loss of $0.20–0.30 per share.
source_title: Wabash National Corp: A Cautionary Tale of Eroding Fundamentals and Escalating Risks

facts: Wabash (NYSE: WNC) is expanding its Wabash Parts and Preferred Partner Network to make genuine trailer and truck body parts more accessible.
The company's network of authorized dealers serves as the backbone of its aftermarket support, providing expert service, maintenance, and Wabash Genuine Parts to keep fleets operating efficiently.
Wabash introduced 14 new locations to its Preferred Partner Network, expanding the availability of Wabash Genuine Parts in areas not currently served by Wabash van trailer dealers.
Blaine Brothers of Blaine, MN joined the network as a new member.
Blaine Brothers of Clearwater, MN joined the network as a new member.
Blaine Brothers Truckaline of Columbus, MN joined the network as a new member.
Blaine Brothers of Scanlon, MN joined the network as a new member.
Blaine Brothers of St. Paul, MN joined the network as a new member.
Blaine Brothers of Baldwin, WI joined the network as a new member.
Great Western Trailer of Albuquerque, NM joined the network as a new member.
Great Western Trailer of Oklahoma City, OK joined the network as a new member.
Great Western Trailer of Tulsa, OK joined the network as a new member.
Great Western Trailer of Houston, TX joined the network as a new member.
North American Trailer of Inver Grove Heights, MN joined the network as a new member.
North American Trailer of Stanley, ND joined the network as a new member.
North American Trailer of Chippewa Falls, WI joined the network as a new member.
U.S. Trailer Parts & Supply of Chicago, IL joined the network as a new member.
Wabash Parts was created in 2022 to unify and expand Wabash’s tech-enabled parts distribution capabilities across all of its Transportation Solutions product lines.
The distribution channel covers van trailers, platform trailers, tank trailers, and truck bodies while ensuring immediate scalability.
The single distribution channel utilizes Wabash’s extensive network of equipment dealers’ service capabilities.
The channel leverages the infrastructure of industry-leading partners in national wholesale distribution for aftermarket heavy-duty truck and trailer parts.
The network operates multiple distribution centers across the United States and Canada.
Dan Millar serves as the managing director of Wabash Parts.
Customers can purchase Wabash Genuine Parts through authorized Wabash dealers or preferred partners.
Wabash is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana.

Wabash designs, manufactures, and services dry freight and refrigerated trailers, flatbed trailers, tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, structural composite panels and products, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade processing equipment.
source_title: Wabash Expands Parts and Services Distribution Network, Strengthens Aftermarket Support in Seven States

facts: Wabash is announcing a significant expansion of its network in the southeast.

Dave Hill, vice president of parts and services at Wabash, stated that these moves reflect how Wabash is building an extensive partner ecosystem that puts parts, service and support closer to customers.
The Atlanta expansions aim to provide faster turnaround, more coverage and better reliability for fleets operating across Georgia, Alabama, South Carolina and Tennessee.
Wabash is strengthening customer access and service in the greater Atlanta region with three strategic growth moves.
One move is opening a new Parts & Service center.
Another move is expanding its dealer relationship with Fleetco.
The third move is adding CS Truck and Trailer to its Preferred Partner Network.
The new Atlanta-area Parts and Services center is strategically located at the heart of the Southeast’s trucking corridor.
The Atlanta site provides faster access to parts, service and truck body upfitting.
The Atlanta site offers Wabash’s Ready-to-Mount (RTM) truck bodies for dry freight and platform applications with rapid turnaround times for in-stock chassis.
The Atlanta site joins Wabash service centers in California, Florida, Ohio, Pennsylvania and Texas, with additional locations on the way.
Fleetco, a long-standing Wabash dealer, will now represent Wabash in the greater Atlanta market with trailer sales.
This expansion broadens Wabash’s dealer footprint in the Southeast, giving customers additional access to new and used equipment through a trusted partner.
Bill Tirrill, president of Fleetco, stated they are proud to grow their existing partnership with Wabash in the greater Atlanta market.

With more than 14 years of experience working closely with Wabash, this expansion allows Fleetco to expand its sales force across the region and provide clients immediate access to new and used trailers.
Fleetco will improve the expertise and commitment clients expect from the Fleetco/Wabash team to create more opportunities to serve clients.
Wabash is adding three CS Truck and Trailer locations to its Preferred Partner Network in Georgia.
This addition brings expert service, maintenance and Wabash Genuine Parts to more customers through authorized third-party dealerships.
The Preferred Partner Network model is central to Wabash’s strategy of making high-quality parts more accessible through a seamless national network.
Dave Hill noted that expanding the dealer and partner network is critical to delivering the speed and service customers expect as their customer base grows.
Atlanta serves as an example of executing this strategy by combining service centers, dealer partners, and the Preferred Partner Network to increase coverage and elevate the customer experience.
source_title: Wabash expands southeast footprint - NewsBreak

facts: Key executives from global commercial vehicle supplier Meritor Inc. and trailer manufacturer Wabash National Corp. discussed industry evolution and revolution at American Trucking Associations’ Technology & Maintenance annual meeting in late February in Atlanta.
John Nelligan, Meritor’s vice president of global sales and service, stated the company is spending more on research and development than ever before to evolve current products and develop new ones.
Nelligan emphasized balancing near-term customer needs with future innovation to avoid losing today's customers or risking the future.

During its fiscal 2020 first quarter ended Dec. 31, Meritor reported introducing two electric powertrains, one electric suspension, and seven core products focused on fuel efficiency and reduced maintenance.
Meritor’s axles are currently 4% more efficient compared with 10 years ago, with a half-percent gain in axle efficiency worth $500 in fuel savings.
Meritor will introduce a lighter, single-piston air disc brake designed to compete more effectively with drum brakes in linehaul operations and reduce pad changes over the component's life.
Nelligan envisions electrification taking hold in intracity distribution, day cabs for shorter distance regional distribution, and port drayage applications.

He expressed optimism about longer-range shifts including oil companies funding electric-vehicle charging stations and electric batteries that can eventually be composted safely after their useful life.
Hydrogen is expected to power fuel cells on electric heavy-duty trucks in the near future.
Meritor announced the new Drivetrain On Demand program, which boosts uptime with 24-hour shipping for replacement driveshaft and differential assemblies.
Nikola Corp. announced in 2018 that Meritor would supply an advanced Class 8 independent suspension.
Nikola intends to bring hydrogen commercial vehicles with necessary fueling infrastructure to market in 2023, along with heavy-duty, battery-electric trucks in 2021.
Nelligan noted that increased industry investment will lead to faster breakthroughs and change.
Wabash National Corp. is focusing on meeting demand for diverse trailer applications used to deliver freight and consumer goods.
Dustin Smith, group president of commercial trailer products for Wabash, stated that customers trust Wabash to invest the right resources to find solutions for moving freight.

Wabash showcased a medium-duty Isuzu NRR model truck with a refrigerated truck body on the TMC show floor, potentially supporting e-commerce and urban deliveries, marking a new business area for the company a year prior.
Wabash’s DuraPlate HD dry van now features DuraPlate cell core technology, where composite panels reduce trailer weight by 300 pounds per 53-foot trailer.
Trailer orders slid in 2019 compared to the all-time record level in 2018 and started slow in 2020 at 15,000 units.
Smith noted that regarding electrification, many companies are scrambling to introduce the right technology.

Wabash displayed a 53-foot trailer with a Carrier Corp. refrigeration unit that operates without a typical diesel engine, instead using a module feeding electricity from under-trailer batteries to perform as if plugged in at a freight terminal.
Solar panels from eNow assist with charging the trailer's batteries during daylight hours, while shore power handles battery recharging at night.
The trailer’s molded structural composite walls increased thermal efficiency, reducing the number of required batteries.
Smith stated the electrified refrigerated trailer would go into service shortly after the show.
source_title: Meritor, Wabash Stay Focused on Now and When - TT

facts: Wabash National Corp. has announced a 10-year agreement with Hydro, a leader in the global aluminum and energy industry.

Under the agreement, Hydro will be a key supplier for Wabash National’s demand for dry van trailers, refrigerated trailers with molded structural composite (MSC) technology, flatbed trailers, and truck bodies for the next 10 years through 2031.
The agreement also covers requirements of new products Wabash National may release through 2031.
The enhanced supply aligns with Wabash National’s recent announcement of additional dry van capacity set to come online at the beginning of 2023.
Wabash National is planning future capacity for MSC Technology products.

Wabash National and Hydro have entered a mutually beneficial business relationship aligned on a shared long-term vision of delivering sustainability to customers through continued collaboration and innovation.

Richard Mansilla, vice president of global supply chain at Wabash National, stated the company selected Hydro as a strategic supplier partner because they share the same values and vision for where the industry is headed.
The partnership will help address Wabash National’s near-term aluminum supply needs to aid in increasing production of dry van trailers, refrigerated trailers, and truck bodies with MSC Technology.
In the longer term, Wabash National plans to work more closely with Hydro on innovation and footprint optimization for the benefit of customers, employees, and shareholders.
Hydro has operations in 40 countries and has supplied Wabash National with aluminum extrusions for over 12 years.
Hydro is a three-time Wabash National supplier award winner and was named Supplier of the Year in 2020.

Jerrod Hoeft, Hydro’s vice president of national accounts for transportation, stated that Hydro supports industries through innovative products and solutions and views Wabash National as a visionary leadership partner.
source_title: Wabash inks long-term deal aluminum supplier Hydro | Trailer Body Builders

facts: Wabash is a leader in transportation solutions and connected solutions for the transportation, logistics and distribution industries.
President and Chief Executive Officer Brent Yeagy will speak at ACT Research’s OUTLOOK Seminar 68 on February 22, 2023, in Columbus, Indiana.
Yeagy's presentation is titled “Understanding the Dynamic Changes within Logistics that Are Altering the Future of the Trailer and Truck Body Markets.”
The presentation will discuss how changing supply chain dynamics, the rise of e-commerce, and a growing desire for sustainable business models are impacting the outlook for transportation equipment.

Yeagy stated, “We can no longer look at the past to predict the future of the trailer and truck body markets,” emphasizing the need for new perspectives to address customer demands as traditional models become less relevant.
Yeagy noted that Wabash is focused on pragmatic solutions with business partners to solve real problems, adding that “the engine is becoming considerably less relevant.”
ACT Research’s seminars are held twice each year for clients and the larger transportation, logistics and distribution industry.

Seminar attendees include industry executives and decision-makers representing C-suite and upper management of OEMs, Tier 1 & 2 suppliers, autonomous and electric new entrants, fleets, dealers and financial institutions.
OUTLOOK Seminar 68 centers on the theme “Driving Decarbonization: The Future of Commercial Vehicles.”
Wabash is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana.

Wabash designs, manufactures, and services a diverse range of products including dry freight and refrigerated trailers, flatbed trailers, tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, structural composite panels and products, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade processing equipment.
source_title: Wabash to Speak on Dynamic Changes in Logistics at ACT Research Seminar | WNC Stock News

facts: Wabash National Corporation is an American industrial manufacturing company specializing in the design and production of semi-trailers, truck bodies, liquid transportation systems, and other engineered products for the commercial transportation sector.
Founded in 1985 and headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana, the company employs approximately 6,000 people and reported trailing twelve-month revenue of $1.64 billion as of September 30, 2025.
Wabash National focuses on integrating physical and digital technologies to deliver end-to-end solutions that enhance supply chain efficiency in transportation, logistics, and infrastructure markets.
The company holds nearly 300 global patents and pioneered advancements such as aerodynamic components and sustainable refrigeration systems.
It became the first semi-trailer manufacturer in the region to achieve certifications in both quality management and environmental standards.

In recent years, Wabash National has expanded its offerings to include electric vehicle solutions and digital platforms for fleet management, aligning with industry shifts toward sustainability and connectivity.

As of 2025, the company emphasizes lightweight composite technologies that support electric vehicle (EV) solutions by reducing trailer and body weight to extend range and efficiency for battery-electric trucks in commercial fleets.

In September 2025, Wabash National opened a new parts and service center in Atlanta, Georgia, along with expanding its Fleetco dealer network in the Southeast to strengthen customer access and support.
DuraPlate composite panels incorporate 15-30% post-consumer resin and are fully recyclable at the end of their lifecycle, minimizing waste in the transportation sector.
EcoNex Technology is a proprietary molded composite material designed for improved thermal efficiency, reduced weight, and lower emissions in refrigerated trailers.

In August 2022, the company committed $20 million to upgrade its 53-acre Little Falls, Minnesota facility, adding over 200 jobs by the end of 2023 and establishing it as the primary hub for EcoNex Technology.
During the early 2020s, Wabash National prioritized domestic sourcing, achieving 95% U.S.-based materials to mitigate volatility from supply chain disruptions and material shortages.

In February 2025, Wabash acquired TrailerHawk.ai, a provider of advanced cargo security technology, to integrate AI-driven monitoring into its trailers-as-a-service offerings and enhance supply chain security.
In April 2025, the company partnered with Echo Global Logistics to expand its drop trailer business, improving fleet flexibility for logistics customers.

Wabash National's dry freight vans feature patented DuraPlate composite panels that reduce overall trailer weight by up to 300 pounds compared to traditional steel panels and offer a service life of 15-20+ years with minimal repairs.
In December 2024, Wabash announced the 2026 model year DuraPlate Dry Van, incorporating Cell Core technology for enhanced durability and the same 300-pound weight reduction.
Ongoing R&D explores bio-based resins and energy-harvesting integrations, including collaborations with academic institutions to test composites that recapture vibrational energy for auxiliary power.
The company holds numerous U.S. patents covering composite floor structures, joint configurations, punching methods for panels, reinforced preforms, and honeycomb sandwich composites.
Brent L. Yeagy serves as President and Chief Executive Officer of Wabash National as of 2025, having held the position since June 2018.
In 2025, the company reported Q3 net sales of $382 million (a 17.8% decline year-over-year) and net income of $40 million for the quarter, while revising full-year revenue guidance to $1.5 billion.
Wabash National's stock (NYSE: WNC) declined over 60% in the year leading to late 2025, closing around $7.80 per share in November 2025.
The company differentiates itself through proprietary DuraPlate composite panels and lightweight technologies that align with the growing adoption of EVs in transportation and logistics.
source_title: Wabash National — Grokipedia

facts: NORICAN GROUP announced the expansion of operations at its LaGrange, Georgia Technology Hub.
The facility has been operated as the Wheelabrator Group Americas headquarters since 1987.
The facility offers manufacturing, inventory, and logistics capabilities.
The planned expansion will move all Norican North America business operations into the LaGrange technology hub.
The consolidated businesses include Wheelabrator, DISA, StrikoWestofen, and Italpresse Gauss.
The company will ramp up current resources to meet commercial operations and administration requirements for all lines of business.
Inside sales and customer support activities will join inventory and logistics centralized in LaGrange.
The expansion will transfer the current operations of DISA from Oswego, Illinois to LaGrange.
The expansion will transfer the current operations of StrikoWestofen and Italpresse Gauss from Holland, Michigan to LaGrange.
The announcement was published in 2020.

facts: NORICAN GROUP NORTH AMERICAN, INC. is a federal contractor registered under UEI RX68TFJ6MN65 and CAGE code 1UB46.
The company has been awarded $10,429,385 across 122 federal contracts.
Primary work spans All Other Industrial Machinery Manufacturing, Military Armored Vehicle, Tank, and Tank Component Manufacturing, and Unknown NAICS.
Top awarding agencies include the Department of Defense and the Department of Homeland Security.

Wheelabrator Group Inc. specializes in the precision manufacturing and supply of specialized industrial machinery components and systems for federal agencies, with a focus on high-reliability equipment for mission-critical applications.
Core capabilities include the design and fabrication of blast machinery, spinner hanger systems, and custom bearings for industrial paint booths.
The company demonstrates deep expertise in mechanical assembly, surface treatment systems, and heavy-duty machinery integration.

Wheelabrator Group excels in producing custom-engineered parts that meet stringent operational demands, particularly in environments requiring durability, corrosion resistance, and precise mechanical tolerances.
The contractor provides urgent repair solutions and mission-essential equipment to support time-sensitive federal requirements.

Wheelabrator Group has a consistent track record working with the Department of Homeland Security and the Department of Defense, providing specialized machinery for infrastructure maintenance, facility decontamination, and industrial processing systems.
Work for DHS includes urgent repairs to IPF Miami equipment and paint booth components.
DoD contracts involve the manufacture of blast machines critical to surface preparation and material conditioning.
The contractor's primary industry focus lies in miscellaneous general-purpose machinery manufacturing (NAICS 333998 and 333999) and industrial machinery manufacturing (333248).
Wheelabrator Group is positioned as a niche supplier of tailored mechanical systems rather than mass-produced equipment.

The company serves vertical markets requiring custom, non-standardized components for industrial and security applications, including facility maintenance, paint systems, and surface treatment infrastructure.

As a small business structured as a 2L entity based in LaGrange, Georgia, Wheelabrator Group operates without federal certifications but maintains a focused presence in the defense and homeland security supply chain.
Its geographic location supports regional logistics for federal installations in the southeastern U.S.
Wheelabrator Group has a reputation for agile, precision manufacturing in response to urgent operational needs.
source_title: NORICAN GROUP NORTH AMERICAN, INC. | CLEATUS

facts: Wheelabrator Group will celebrate its twenty years in operation at its LaGrange, Ga. facility in September.
The LaGrange location opened on Sept. 21, 1987.
The facility is the home of Wheelabrator Plus, the aftermarket-sales division that provides a full range of services, replacement parts and training.
LaGrange is also home to the Wheelabrator Group North American Research and Development Center.
The site houses a one-of-a-kind Customer Demonstration and Testing Facility.

The Equipment Modernization Program (EMP) Team focuses on providing equipment upgrades that are effective solutions for customers’ requested improvements in productivity and to reduce the costs associated with the operation and maintenance of their existing blasting equipment.
A core group of employees at this location moved to LaGrange from Mishawaka, Ind., in 1987 to start up the facility.
The LaGrange site is located southwest of Atlanta on I-85.

Wheelabrator Vice President North American Supply Chain Pierre Tanguay stated the location was chosen because of its proximity to a major interstate and Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport in Atlanta.
The City of LaGrange worked closely with Wheelabrator to train the local workforce.
The LaGrange facility now employs 130 people, with more than 20 additional employees working in field sales and service.
The LaGrange facility will celebrate its 20th anniversary with a luncheon and employee recognition this fall.
Wheelabrator will honor another historical milestone in 2008 as the company celebrates its 100th anniversary.
The company was founded in 1908 in Ohio as the Homogeneous Sand Mixing Machine Company.
The company developed the Wheelabrator® airless blast cleaning wheel in 1932, a simple mechanical unit that used controlled centrifugal force instead of compressed air for abrasive blasting.
The Wheelabrator trademark became part of the corporate name spanning the decades from the 1940’s to the present day.
Wheelabrator Group is the global leader in providing surface preparation and finishing solutions.
Wheelabrator Group works closely with customers to deliver the best solutions for their specific requirements using a broad array of technologies, products, services and technical know-how.
The company's strategically located and electronically linked offices allow Wheelabrator Group to provide unrivaled customer service and support worldwide.
source_title: Press Release / Wheelabrator LaGrange Facility Announces Twenty Years in Business - Heavy Machinery / Handling Solutions - Flammer Relations, Inc. | Denim Marketing | PRZOOM

facts: WIKA USA is located in Lawrenceville, GA.
WIKA announced a new FlexWindow™ option for select pressure gauges.
The FlexWindow™ offers a versatile alternative to the traditional pressure gauge vent plug.
Liquid-filled gauges are excellent solutions for applications with vibration, pulsation, shock, exposure to the elements, and other adverse conditions.
Traditional liquid-filled gauges are susceptible to internal pressure build-up, requiring pressure compensation via a manually operated vent valve.
Traditional liquid-filled gauges also have a large air bubble that can impair operators’ ability to accurately read the gauge.
Hardy Orzikowski, Senior Product Manager at WIKA, stated that these challenges led to the development of the new FlexWindow™ option for select WIKA pressure gauges.
WIKA’s patented FlexWindow compensates for pressure build-up due to changes in ambient temperature.
The innovative new gauge window is made with liquid silicone rubber (LSR).
The FlexWindow™ offers several advantages over pressure gauges with the traditional polycarbonate window and vent plug.
The new option is currently available on the 2-1/2” version of models 213.53, 233.53, 233.54, and 233.55.
WIKA plans to expand availability of the FlexWindow™ option in the future.
For 75 years, WIKA has continuously advanced instrumentation for pressure, temperature, level, and flow measurement.
WIKA leverages decades of experience to develop a broad selection of stock and custom instrumentation as well as engineering services to support plant safety, productivity, and profitability.
WIKA is a global leader in lean manufacturing with manufacturing locations around the globe, which enables flexibility and the ability to meet high delivery demands.
source_title: WIKA USA Announces Exciting New Product Feature - WIKA USA

facts: WIKA Instrument Corporation's metro Atlanta headquarters and manufacturing facility was chosen by the nationally syndicated cable television program "How It's Made" to be featured on a segment demonstrating how pressure gauges are made.
The program airs Friday, January 16 at 9 p.m. (EST) on Discovery Communication's Science Channel and Science Channel HD.
WIKA is the leading U.S. supplier of industrial pressure gauge, sensor and temperature instrumentation.

Klaus Gross, WIKA chief operating officer, stated that "How It's Made" selected WIKA as the standard bearer of the mechanical pressure industry by choosing their state-of-the-art manufacturing facility to feature the processes by which pressure gauges are manufactured.
For over 60 years, WIKA Instrument Corporation has continued to globally advance pressure gauge, sensor and temperature instrumentation technology and applications.

In the United States, WIKA's operation utilizes state-of-the-art manufacturing technology and highly automated proprietary production equipment in its nearly quarter-million-square-foot facility in the metro Atlanta suburb of Lawrenceville, Ga.
As the industry leader in lean manufacturing, WIKA offers a wide variety of stock and customized instrumentation solutions for critical applications that are often distributed within days.

The WIKA customer service department recently earned the Operational Excellence Award for Leadership from one of North America's leading distributors of industrial maintenance, repair and operation replacement parts.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group is investing $7.59 billion in Metaplant America (HMGMA) in Georgia.
The HMGMA plant is estimated to generate 8,500 jobs directly.
Hyundai is building a $5 billion EV battery cell plant with SK ON in Georgia.
The SK On battery cell plant is expected to employ 3,500 workers.
A study by the Center for Automotive Research estimated that Hyundai’s investments in Georgia will create about 40,000 jobs and result in $4.6 billion in individual earnings per year.
Hyundai Motor Group was second in EV sales last year in the United States, behind Tesla and ahead of Ford and General Motors.
Hyundai's focus on affordable and compact electric vehicles helped boost its EV sales in the United States.
HMGMA could help Hyundai retain its spot as the second top-selling EV automaker in the United States.
The Georgia-based plant would make Hyundai’s EVs more affordable by allowing them to qualify for the Inflation Reduction Act’s full $7,500 tax credits.
HMGMA-produced electric vehicles will gain IRA points for being assembled in the United States and using locally produced battery cells from SK On.
Hyundai Motor Group announced that the official start of production at Metaplant America has been moved up to the fourth quarter of 2024.
The automaker previously estimated that the start of production at HMGMA would occur in the first quarter of 2025.
Hyundai plans to hold a grand opening celebration by the first quarter of next year.

Oscar Kwon, HMGMA President and CEO, stated: “2024 is going to be a great year for HMGMA. This is the largest economic development project in Georgia’s history, and we have been moving at a rapid speed. Completing a project of this scale on this timeline has been a challenge, but our team has delivered amazing results.”

Oscar Kwon also stated: “We owe a lot of this to the hard work of our Meta Pros and the continued support and cooperation of our state and local partners, including the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Joint Development Authority.”
Hyundai made the production timeline announcement during an event celebrating its positive impact on the State of Georgia.
During the event, the Georgia General Assembly passed a Resolution making February 26, 2024 Hyundai Day.

Governor Brian Kemp stated: “For decades, Hyundai Motor Group has been an incredible partner to our state, and we knew exactly what kind of historic benefits a project like this would bring to hardworking Georgians when we first began talks with HMG.”
source_title: Hyundai Metaplant America to boost its EV sales in the US

The Blank Slate vehicle includes essential features, with optional wraps and accessories being entirely customizable by the buyer.
Customers can configure the vehicle as a two-seat pickup, a five-seat SUV, or a Fastback.

The vehicle is designed to be easy to maintain, easy to fix, and easy to spec for exact business needs.

facts: The State of Georgia is in the middle of a building boom, driven mainly by construction of new plants, massive expansions and upgrades to existing facilities and ports, and a large-scale, multi-million-dollar expansion of the network of highways, bridges and other infrastructure.
The National Statistics Office of Georgia reported that the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) from construction in the Peach State has been rising and the growth shows no signs of abating.
In recent weeks, four separate projects worth nearly $1 billion have been announced that will create thousands of construction jobs.
Market data firm Statista reports the state’s industry is expected to generate US$40.11 billion in GDP from construction of buildings and $34.55 billion from trade contractors in 2024.

A survey by the Associated General Contractors of America conducted earlier this year found most contractors were optimistic about raising the dollar value of investments in various types of projects and anticipated an increase in their headcount.
First Quality Baby Products is investing $418 million to expand its footprint and create 60 new jobs in Macon-Bibb County, Ga., south of Atlanta and near the state’s geographic centre.
First Quality has been in Macon since 2008, with expanded facilities located next to the company’s current location at 2108 Avondale Mill Rd.
The company and its affiliates manufacture baby diapers and hygiene, paper and packaging products for health care, retail and commercial channels.

Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority chairman Robby Fountain said the organization has worked with First Quality to facilitate its growth and the company’s decision to expand its operations in Macon is testament to the “strength and attractiveness” of the local business environment.
Lee Kum Kee Sauce Group, a global name in Asian sauces and condiments, is building a new, $288-million plant in LaGrange, Ga., that will create 267 jobs.
The facility will be located at the Georgia International Business Park in LaGrange and serves as the company’s second production base in the United States.
The Lee Kum Kee plant will feature intelligent factory solutions, digitalization, product innovation and R&D capabilities, and a consumer engagement centre.

Georgia Department of Economic Development commissioner Pat Wilson said international companies are finding success because of planning like the business park, which since opening just five years ago is now home to more than 41 international companies representing 18 countries.
Syensqo, a global science leader in materials and chemical solutions, has started construction on a new, $178-million, battery-grade manufacturing facility along Clanton Road in Augusta.
The company has an existing facility in Augusta that supports 800 jobs and will create approximately 100 new full-time manufacturing jobs in Richmond County with the new plant.
Products made at the Syensqo plant will support Georgia’s growing electric vehicle (EV) manufacturing ecosystem and provide material for more than five million EV batteries per year.
The sprawling plant will be the largest polyvinylidene fluoride production facility in North America.
German-based Ritz Instrument Transformers is investing $28 million in a new facility at Burke County Industrial Park in Waynesboro, Ga., that will employ 130 people.
The company makes instrument transformers for the utility and manufacturing sectors.

Ritz USA CEO and general manager Scott Flowers said the U.S. electrical grid is undergoing rapid expansion and transformation as the utility industry reacts to and prepares for growth driven by adoption of electric vehicles, new data centres needed to power AI, continued addition of renewable generation, and an increase in the U.S. manufacturing base.
The investment marks the single largest to date for the Ritz Group, with the availability of a well-trained workforce being a factor in choosing Waynesboro.
Operations at the Ritz facility are expected to begin in late 2025.

Global food and agriculture leader Cargill is establishing a new office hub in Atlanta that will create 400 tech jobs and accelerate the digital transformation for the food and agriculture industries.

Cargill chief information and digital officer Jennifer Hartsock said Atlanta’s rich pool of technical talent was a deciding factor in the company’s choice, with employees helping to create digital solutions, harness analytics and artificial intelligence, and drive technical innovation to make the food system more sustainable and accessible.
Hyundai is at work on a $5.5-billion electric vehicle and battery plant in Ellabell, Ga., that will employ 8,500 workers once up and running.

More than $4.5 billion in infrastructure projects are planned for Georgia ports and intermodal infrastructure over the next decade to enable the Georgia Ports Authority to double berth capacity and increase yard and rail capacity.
Georgia is pumping $1.5 billion into transportation infrastructure around cities and counties to help complete key transportation projects and kickstart new strategic projects.

Improvements in the months ahead will include roads and bridges, more than doubling the state’s funding to cities and counties for transportation infrastructure, increasing funding for general aviation airports, resurfacing more state highways, and kicking off new projects.
source_title: Georgia’s building boom driven by new plants, upgrades and a massive highway expansion

Funding facilitates the growth of innovative firms.
Funding is supporting a modernization project and the centralization of Woodbridge’s global R&D facility in Ontario.
Woodbridge has been innovating foam and technology solutions for over 40 years.
The company operates 67 facilities across the world.
The company has worked with the majority of key automotive OEMs.
Nearly 60% of all vehicles have their products and solutions integrated into them.
Woodbridge applied for and was successfully awarded $20M in Canadian government funding from the Strategic Innovation Fund (SIF).

The company will create a Centre of Excellence to centralize its global research and development initiatives.
R&D activities include advanced materials and product design of molded foam products like armrests and car seats.
By modernizing the manufacturing plants and building the R&D centre, Woodbridge will be better equipped to meet market demands for products.
The company will expand its collaboration with high schools and post-secondary institutions.

facts: The global seat foam market size reached USD 13.7 billion in 2024.
The market is projected to advance at a CAGR of 5.2% from 2025 to 2033, reaching a forecasted value of USD 21.4 billion by 2033.
Growth is primarily driven by the rising adoption of advanced seating solutions and the increasing production of vehicles, including electric vehicles.
The automotive sector remains the largest application area, accounting for more than 45% of global seat foam consumption in 2024.

Automakers are increasingly incorporating lightweight and energy-absorbing advanced foam materials to enhance fuel efficiency, occupant protection, crashworthiness, weight reduction, and emission standards.
The integration of smart seating systems and the rise of electric vehicles are contributing to the evolution of seat foam formulations.
Manufacturers are investing in research and development to deliver products that meet stringent regulatory and performance standards.
Polyurethane foam remains the dominant product type due to its excellent resilience, flexibility, cost-effectiveness, and ability to provide consistent support.
Memory foam has witnessed substantial growth driven by consumer awareness of ergonomic seating benefits and the rising prevalence of musculoskeletal disorders.
Gel foam is gaining traction for its superior cooling properties and efficient temperature regulation capabilities.
Latex foam is valued for its natural origin, hypoallergenic properties, durability, and adoption as an eco-friendly seating solution.
Bio-based foams derived from renewable resources like soy and castor oil are gaining popularity among environmentally conscious consumers and manufacturers.
Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) constitute the largest end-user share, driving demand for high-quality, reliable foam materials that meet strict performance and safety standards.
The aftermarket segment caters to replacement and upgrade needs, fueled by vehicle refurbishment trends and do-it-yourself culture.
Direct sales remain the preferred distribution channel for large OEMs requiring bulk quantities and customized specifications.
Distributors and wholesalers serve as critical intermediaries providing inventory management, technical support, and logistics to SMEs and retailers.
Online retail has emerged as a rapidly growing distribution channel driven by e-commerce platforms and changing consumer purchasing behaviors.
The Asia Pacific region dominates the global seat foam market, capturing approximately 46% of total market value in 2024, equivalent to USD 6.3 billion.
North America and Europe represented significant markets with sizes of USD 3.1 billion and USD 2.7 billion respectively in 2024.
Latin America and Middle East & Africa markets were valued at USD 1.0 billion and USD 0.6 billion respectively in 2024, offering promising growth opportunities.
Key competitive factors include product quality, price, customization capabilities, sustainability initiatives, and customer service.
Mergers, acquisitions, joint ventures, and strategic alliances are common strategies employed by leading players to expand product portfolios and enter new markets.

Major companies operating in the global seat foam market include Recticel Group, The Woodbridge Group, FoamPartner Group, Adient plc, Lear Corporation, Johnson Controls International plc, Bridgestone Corporation, Carpenter Co., Saint-Gobain Performance Plastics, and INOAC Corporation.
Adient plc and Lear Corporation are major suppliers of automotive seating systems, integrating advanced foam technologies to deliver superior comfort, safety, and performance.
The market faces challenges including raw material price volatility, supply chain disruptions, and stringent environmental regulations regarding chemical emissions and foam production.
Sustainability is a key trend, with manufacturers increasingly adopting bio-based and recyclable foams, green chemistry, and eco-friendly production processes.
source_title: Seat Foam Market Research Report 2033

facts: Woodbridge's scientists developed a process to integrate biogenic carbon (biocarbon) in its purest form into the company's high-performing polyurethane seating foam chemistry.
The new product, TrimVisible™ BIO, enables a cradle-to-gate reduction in carbon dioxide (CO2) footprint for seating foam without compromising material properties, performance and comfort.
This technology ultimately contributes to the reduction in CO2 footprint for the lifetime of the vehicle.
The original TrimVisible™ was recognized for its black color and released as a molded seat foam solution that provided a craftsmanship improvement and lower total seat system cost.
TrimVisible™ pioneered the conversion of the molded seat foam market to black, and this global trend continues today.
TrimVisible™ LE was the second generation offering that established a benchmark for low Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) and low odor performance in the industry.
With the introduction of the company's third-generation, TrimVisible™ BIO, Woodbridge builds upon its market-leading family of TrimVisible™ products.
TrimVisible™ BIO uniquely delivers lightweight performance and carbon-neutral impact, meeting the needs of the evolving automotive market and electric vehicle transition.
Woodbridge has partnered with a certified sustainable managed forest and developed a proprietary process to convert residue from lumber processing into a high-purity form of biocarbon.
The Woodbridge process sequesters the biocarbon by effectively locking CO2 in a form that can be used to engineer solutions that significantly reduce the CO2 footprint of products.

Woodbridge scientists have formulated chemistry to displace conventional urethane chemicals with biocarbon to manufacture TrimVisible™ BIO, a patent-pending technology that offsets the company's Scope 1 CO2 manufacturing emissions to net-zero.

Mike Irwin, Vice President, Advanced Product and Chemical Technology, stated that adding carbon-neutral foam products helps reduce global warming potential and enables support for customers reaching supply chain carbon reduction goals while displacing fossil fuel-based chemicals with a completely sustainable alternative.
Woodbridge is developing a comprehensive line of products based on sustainable technologies beyond TrimVisible™ BIO.

The company is pursuing near- and long-term research and development (R&D) projects to advance circularity in the automotive supply chain as well as to increase environmentally friendly and bio-derived solutions to mitigate global warming potential and eliminate dependence on non-renewable technologies.

John Zianis, President and CEO, stated that Woodbridge is a world-leading seating foam manufacturer focused on bringing climate-neutral, renewable, and sustainable technologies as a key part of its forward-looking product strategy.

Through R&D activities, Woodbridge continues finding ways to succeed in replacing fossil-fuel-derived materials with environmentally friendly and sustainable solutions to improve its future competitive position.
Woodbridge has corporate headquarters in Mississauga, Ontario, Canada, and automotive headquarters in Troy, Michigan, USA.
The company employs more than 7,500 teammates in over 50 locations across 10 countries.
Woodbridge offers material technologies for applications in automotive, commercial, recreational, packaging, healthcare and building products.
The company offers a full complement of services that includes consulting, technical support, chemical R&D and accredited laboratory testing.
source_title:
Woodbridge Develops CO2 Neutral Automotive Seat Foam |
                                    Newswire

entity_name: Georgia State Tax Credits and Incentives
canonical_name: Georgia State Tax Credits and Incentive Programs
facts: The state of Georgia offers tax credits and incentives for businesses that want to expand, relocate, or start operations in the Peach State.

The Georgia Jobs Tax Credit (GAJTC) rewards job creation within the state by providing $750 - $5,250 per new full-time job created each year for up to five years, depending on location and eligibility requirements.
Georgia counties and census tracts are ranked as economic tiers based on unemployment rate, per capita income, and percentage of residents whose incomes are below the poverty level.
Businesses located in Tier 1 counties, military zones, opportunity zones, or less developed census tracts can use GAJTC to offset income tax or reduce payroll withholding obligations.
Companies may be eligible for GAJTC if they create new full-time jobs in Georgia that are at least 35 hours a week, offer health insurance, and pay more than the lowest county average wage.
GAJTC can offset 50% to 100% of state income tax liability with a 10-year carry-forward period.
All industries are eligible for GAJTC in the 40 least-developed counties and other specifically designated zones.

Beyond designated zones, employers generating jobs must operate in one of eight qualified industries: Manufacturing, Tourism, Processing, Telecommunications, Broadcasting, Warehousing and distribution, Elderly and disability services, or Research and development.
The GAJTC county credit amount ranges from $750 to $4,000.
Companies located in Tier 1 counties and specially designated areas can apply the base job tax credit against employee withholding taxes after corporate income tax has been eliminated.

Georgia's Quality Jobs Tax Credit (GQJTC) provides a significant tax break for eligible companies across all industries if created jobs pay at least 10% above the average county wage and are considered high-paying jobs.
GQJTC provides a 5-year credit for each qualifying job added, provided the jobs are maintained.
GQJTC can be used to offset up to 100% of a company's total Georgia income tax liability.
GQJTC can be applied against employee withholding after the income tax benefit has been exhausted.
A one-year look back is available for GQJTC claims.
Unused GQJTC credits can be carried forward for up to 10 years.
GQJTC cannot be combined with the Georgia investment tax credit for the same project or used for the same jobs claimed under GAJTC, but may be used along with the Georgia retraining tax credit.
The GQJTC equals $2,500 to $5,000 per job every year for five years, based on the increase in average jobs over the previous year's average.
To qualify for GQJTC, a company must create at least 50 net new qualified quality jobs over a period of two years, exceeding the previous 12-month period's average.
Acquiring employees through a merger does not count toward the GQJTC credit.
For rural counties only, ten (10) new Tier 1 county quality jobs and 25 Tier 2 county new quality jobs must be created to qualify for GQJTC.
GQJTC requires paying at least 110% of the average wage in the county where the jobs were created.

Businesses that have claimed QJTC previously must create at least 50 net new qualified quality jobs over a 24-month period and make at least a $2.5 million investment in qualified investment property to start a new period of eligibility.
The Georgia retraining tax credit (GARTC) enables businesses to offset their investment in employee training for new equipment, new technology, or competitive positioning.

Any business that files a Georgia income tax return is eligible for GARTC if training programs are designed to enhance quality and productivity, teach certain software technologies, and are approved by the technical college system of Georgia.
Eligible GARTC expenses include costs of instructors and teaching materials, employee wages during training, and reasonable travel expenses.
GARTC provides a tax credit of 50 percent of direct training expenses.
GARTC provides up to $500 credit per full-time employee per training program.
The annual maximum for the GARTC amounts to $1,250 per employee.
Unused GARTC credits can be carried forward for ten years.
The investment tax credit applies to employers making an investment in certain types of manufacturing processes or equipment or providing telecommunications services.
Depending on the county, the investment tax credit ranges from 1% to 5% of qualified investment expenses.
To qualify for the investment tax credit, a company must have operated a manufacturing or telecommunications facility in Georgia for at least three years and make a minimum $100,000 investment.
Qualifying businesses in rural counties that meet certain criteria can utilize the investment tax credit against payroll withholding.
Eligible expenses for the investment tax credit include land acquisitions, improvements, buildings, machinery, and equipment.
Companies in certain designated counties may also be able to apply excess investment tax credits to payroll withholding.

The Georgia research & development (R&D) tax credit applies to facilities engaged in or headquartered in manufacturing, warehousing and distribution, tourism, telecommunications, broadcasting, or companies engaged in qualified R&D activities.
The Georgia R&D tax credit equals 10% of qualified R&D expenditures over a base amount.
The value of the R&D credit is determined based on qualifying expenditures each year along with a gross receipts test.

Qualifying R&D expenditures include the purchase of materials and supplies, fees for Georgia-based contract work, and the portion of wages and salaries of personnel, direct management, and direct support dedicated to in-state R&D activities.
The port tax credit bonus applies if a company increases imports or exports through a Georgia port by 10% and qualifies for GAJTC or the investment tax credit.
To qualify for the port tax credit bonus, a company's base port traffic amount must equal at least 10 TEUs (twenty-foot equivalent units).
The required 10% increase in port traffic can be measured against the previous year or base year.
When paired with GAJTC, the port tax credit amounts to an additional $1,250 credit per new job per year for five years, as long as both the job and higher level of port traffic are maintained.
ADP offers SmartCompliance®, a solution combining 45+ years of tax credits expertise, dedicated specialists, and technology to help identify eligible tax credits for businesses.
source_title: Maximizing Your Benefits: An Overview of Georgia Tax Credits and Incentives | SPARK Blog | ADP

facts: Project Start Date is 01/12/2021.
Project End Date is 31/03/2023.
CRIBE Funding amount is $269,000.
Project Total Value is $538,000.
Consortium Members include Woodbridge Foam Corporation, Michigan State University (Prof. Mojgan Nejad), West Fraser, Fortum Bio2X, Sweetwater Energy Inc, and Ford Motor Company.
The project aims to increase the commercial readiness of lignin-containing polymers by scaling up the production of lignin-based polyurethane (PU) flexible foams to move toward carbon-negative cars.
The consortium targets achieving its goals by the end of May 2023.
The project involves formulating liquid lignin-polyol using technical lignins produced by Fortum Bio2X (organosolv wheat straw), Sweetwater (hydrolysis hardwood), and West Fraser (kraft softwood).
The formulated liquid lignin-polyol will be used to partially replace petroleum-based polyol in the formulation of PU flexible foam by Woodbridge Foam Corporation.
Woodbridge Foam Corporation will scale up production of lignin-containing PU flexible foams and test them in the manufacturing line of Ford Motor Company.
Project outcomes will be communicated via conferences, technical reports, journal papers, industry meetings, and social media.
source_title: Moving toward Carbon-Negative Automobiles: Incorporating Lignin in Automotive Parts on Commercial Scale : Centre for Research & Innovation in the Bio-economy

facts: Woory Industrial Company, Ltd., a Korea-based automotive parts manufacturer, recently announced it will establish its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
The company develops and produces HVAC auto components for all types of vehicles, including electric, internal combustion engine, eco-friendly, and hydrogen-fueled cars.
Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia is proud to be the first U.S. home of Woory Industrial as it moves closer to its goal of becoming the e-mobility capital.
Governor Kemp noted that being No. 1 for business means creating opportunity in all parts of the state and expressed excitement about the new partnership in Dublin-Laurens County.
The company’s new facility will be located at 404 Kellam Road in Dublin.

According to a press release by the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), the facility will produce electrical heaters, control units, and actuators for electric vehicles (EV) and internal combustion carmakers in North America.
Woory is making an $18 million investment for the new facility.
The investment is expected to create more than 130 new jobs in Dublin-Laurens County.
New operations at the facility are expected to begin towards the end of this year, according to GDEcD.
Jungwoo Kim, CEO of Woory Industrial Company, stated that over the past 10 years, Woory has successfully grown into a specialized EV component company through continuous innovation.
Jungwoo Kim added that Georgia will be a good opportunity for Woory to take another step forward as part of the vanguard of EV development.
Jungwoo Kim expressed confidence that Woory's experience and technology will contribute to the prosperity of the community and thanked the partnership of the State of Georgia and the City of Dublin.
Governor Brian P. Kemp welcomed Woory Industrial as the latest job creator helping Georgia become the e-mobility capital of the nation.

Governor Kemp noted that the emerging EV industry continues to bring historic levels of new jobs and investment to communities across the state, thanks to Georgia's highly-skilled workforce and reliable infrastructure network.
According to GDEcD, from July 1, 2022, to December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Woory Industrial Co., Ltd. will establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.

facts: Korean auto parts makers were historically regarded by stock investors as mere subcontractors with performance largely dependent on local carmakers.
Nearly all major global automakers and tech giants are going all in on electric vehicles, bringing Korean auto parts players into the spotlight due to their technology and capacity for EV components.
Analysts say Korean companies are well positioned in the global EV value chain as Hyundai Motor Co. and Kia Corp. are among the leading automakers shifting to EVs and future mobility.
Hyundai has set a goal of supplying 8-10% of the world’s EVs by 2025.
Hyundai plans to introduce more than 12 battery-powered electric cars built on the E-GMP platform.
Among major global carmakers, only four have EV-dedicated platforms ready to roll out battery-powered cars: General Motors Co., Volkswagen Group, Hyundai Motor, and China’s Geely.
Japan’s Toyota Motor Co. has developed its own EV platform architecture called e-TNGA but has yet to unveil an electric car based on it.
Global auto parts players previously outperformed Korean peers in combustion-engine components but were relatively late preparing for the EV era.
German parts makers benefited from gasoline and diesel car popularity in China, while Japanese auto parts makers focused on hybrid cars.
Korean auto parts makers have shifted their focus to EV components.
LG Chem Ltd., Samsung SDI Co., and SK Innovation Co. have supplied battery components to Volkswagen, which also procures other parts from Korean suppliers.
EV tires are supplied by Hankook Tire & Technology Co. and automotive compressors by Hanon Systems Co.
GM gets batteries for its own EV platform from LG Chem, motor converters and inverters from LG Electronics Inc., headlamps from SL Corp., and tires from Hankook Tire.
Automotive parts used in EVs are five times more expensive per unit compared to combustion-engine cars, according to Samsung Securities auto analyst Im Eun-young.

Stock investors can take advantage of expected gains by investing in suppliers advancing into the US EV market jointly with Hyundai and Kia, or buying shares of parts makers with business tie-ups with global automakers.
Hyundai and Kia are widely expected to provide the E-GMP platform to other carmakers and tech giants entering the electric car market.
Analysts expect Korean automakers to produce more EVs in the US than their Chinese rivals starting from 2022.
SL Corp. entered the US market by supplying headlamps to Hyundai and Kia there.
SL Corp. is forecast to see its US revenue rise 50% this year, with global sales estimated at 3 trillion won ($2.7 billion), up 20% from 2020.

Korean suppliers Hanon, Mando Corp., Woory Industrial Co., and Pyeong Hwa Automotive Co. count Chinese EV automakers NIO Co. and Xiaopeng Motors Co. as new clients, in addition to existing customers Tesla Inc. and Volkswagen.
Mando, Woory, and Pyeong Hwa also supply components to US EV startups Canoo Inc. and Rivian Co.
The price-to-earnings ratios (PER) of Mando and Hanon are hovering around a multiple of 30 times.
The PE multiple of Hyundai Mobis Co., an affiliate of Hyundai Motor, stands at 15 times, signaling further upside potential.
NH Investment & Securities analyst Cho Soo-hong named Hyundai Mobis one of their top picks among auto parts plays as Hyundai Motor Group’s E-GMP is set to take off soon.

The South Korea-based company’s new plant will be located near Statesboro and assemble automotive thermal solutions.
The company aims to open the site in May 2024, creating 160 jobs.
Another South Korea-based company, Woory Industrial Co., is also headed to the state, investing $18 million to establish its first manufacturing facility in the U.S.

The new plant will be located in Dublin and will produce electrical heaters, control units and actuators for EV and internal combustion automakers in North America, according to a June 7 announcement.
The site will create more than 130 jobs and operations are expected to commence in November.
Woory Industrial CEO Jungwoo Kim stated that the transition to electric vehicles is the future, and the U.S. EV market is the key market among all automotive markets.
Georgia will be a good opportunity for Woory to take another step forward as part of the vanguard of EV development, according to CEO Jungwoo Kim.

The two factories are following Hyundai to the state after the automaker announced a $5.5 billion manufacturing campus in Ellabell last year, which is the largest economic development project in Georgia’s history.
Georgia officials had predicted Hyundai’s Metaplant would attract approximately $1 billion to the state via offsite supplier investments.
The automaker’s suppliers have outpaced that estimate, with plans to invest nearly $2 billion in the state, according to officials in April.
More suppliers are still anticipated to be announced, according to Angela Hendrix, senior vice president of marketing and public relations of the Savannah Economic Development Authority.
Hyundai continues to expand its own investment in the state.
In late May, Hyundai and LG Energy Solution struck a joint venture deal to build a $4.3 billion battery cell plant, which will be adjacent to the automaker’s EV campus.
The two South Korea-based companies will each take a 50% stake in the venture and facility.
Hyundai will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant to be sent to its U.S. manufacturing sites.
source_title: Hyundai’s Georgia supplier network expands to 9 manufacturers | WardsAuto

facts: On June 7, 2023, Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Woory Industrial Co., Ltd. will establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
Woory Industrial Co., Ltd. is an automotive parts manufacturer.
The new facility will create more than 130 new jobs.
The project involves an estimated $18 million investment in the Dublin-Laurens area.

facts: AY Manufacturing Ltd. operates as the plant operations for Sunroof Assembly, Fuel Tank Band Paint and Assembly, and Small Parts Paint.
AY Manufacturing Ltd.'s estimated annual revenue is currently $17.2M per year.

facts: Samvardhana Motherson International Limited (SAMIL) via its 100% subsidiary SMRP B.V. (SAHN B.V.) has entered into an agreement to acquire 81% stake in Yachiyo’s 4W (Y4W) Business.

Motherson will form an 81%:19% strategic partnership with Honda Motor where both partners will drive the company towards a strong and sustainable future and be fully prepared for next-generation products and technologies.
The transaction emphasises Motherson’s commitment to be a globally preferred sustainable solutions provider for the automotive industry, according to the statement.
Yachiyo Industry Co., Ltd. (Listed Yachiyo) is a publicly listed (Tokyo Stock Exchange) subsidiary of Honda Motor Co., Ltd. (Honda Motor) which comprises 4W and 2W businesses.
As part of the overall transaction, the 2W business (housed under Goshi Giken) will be transferred to Honda Motor before Motherson acquires 81% stake in Yachiyo’s 4W business.

Yachiyo 4W business employs approx. 3,200 employees in its 13 manufacturing facilities and 3 R&D centres across 08 countries including the USA, Brazil, Mexico, China, Japan, Thailand, Indonesia and India.
Yachiyo 4W reported revenue of JPY ~116 Bn/ EUR 824 Mn1 and EBITDA of JPY 13 Bn/ EUR 94 Mn1 respectively for FY23.
The equity value of the business is JPY 23 Bn / EUR 145 Mn2 for a 100% stake.
Yachiyo 4W acts as a worldwide strategic supplier to Honda Motor with a fully aligned footprint, supporting substantially all of its sunroof and fuel tank requirements at each manufacturing location.
The strong relationship between Yachiyo and Honda Motor has been forged on the back of decades of excelling on Honda Motor’s strict technical and quality expectations.
Sunroof systems have high growth potential globally due to an increase in demand and are expected to see a surge in application across all car segments.
The transition to hybrid vehicles will continue to see the use of plastic fuel tanks.
The trend of fuel cell powertrains is also aligned with Type-IV hydrogen storage tanks under the advance stages of development.
Yachiyo 4W has developed multiple value-added next-generation products like lighter plastic tail gates and type-IV hydrogen storage tanks.
The close proximity to Honda Motor plants across the globe provides an opportunity to offer existing products of Motherson to Honda Motor globally, according to a statement.
Post successful closure of this transaction, Motherson will become a partner of choice for Honda Motor.
The transaction enables further diversification for Motherson in line with its 3CX10 strategy along with opportunities for the company to offer these products to other OEMs across the globe.
Vivek Chaand Sehgal, Chairman of Motherson, commented that this acquisition is an important landmark for Motherson as they are honoured to be handpicked by Honda San for a global partnership.

Sehgal stated they will give their best to live up to the trust bestowed upon them by Honda San and that these new product segments are in line with their increasing content per car strategy as well as their customer-centric approach.

Sehgal believes this business will be highly synergistic with Motherson’s existing portfolio, noting ample opportunities to grow this new business globally by leveraging global relationships with automotive OEMs.
source_title: Motherson to acquire 81% stake in Yachiyo Industry | hrnxt.com | Simple & actionable insights & ideas on people, work and workplaces.

facts: Yachiyo Manufacturing of America is an automotive parts manufacturing facility located in Carrollton, Georgia.

Shimizu North America managed the design and construction of this greenfield project in Carrollton, GA.

The facility was completed in 2014.
