facts: Honda recognized 56 North American suppliers during its annual Honda Supplier Conference in Columbus, Ohio, with awards for outstanding performance in supplying parts and materials in 2025.

Thirty-seven suppliers were honored for supporting Honda with parts and materials for mass production, and 19 suppliers were honored for supplying service parts that serve the needs of customers after they purchase a Honda or Acura product.

Paul Dentinger, senior vice president of the Purchasing & Supply Chain Center at Honda Development & Manufacturing of America, LLC, stated that Honda is focusing its automobile business on maximizing hybrid and gas-powered models while continuing to invest in its North American supplier network.
Honda purchased over $35 billion in parts for mass production operations from North American suppliers in 2025.
The purchased parts were used to produce more than 1.52 million Honda and Acura vehicles sold in North America in 2025.
Honda also purchased more than $1.78 billion in parts from Service Parts suppliers used by Honda and Acura dealers to serve customers in North America.
Mass production supplier awards were given in four categories: Excellence in Quality and Delivery; Value; Quality, Delivery & Value; and Sustainability.

Honda conducts business with approximately 700 OEM suppliers across North America that provide parts to the company's 13 plants nationwide producing Honda and Acura vehicles, engines, and transmissions.
Service parts awards reflect a supplier's combined performance in quality, cost, and on-time delivery.

Honda works with nearly 1,600 service parts suppliers in North America to provide service and accessory parts for more than 26 million Honda and Acura automobiles in operation in the United States, as well as for powersports and power equipment products.

Honda manages a national network of parts and distribution facilities that deliver high-quality service parts to customers through Honda and Acura dealers across the country and support global parts operations.
Astemo Rock Spring, Inc., located in Rock Spring, Georgia, was recognized as a 2025 Mass Production Supplier Award Winner.
Maxxis International - USA, located in Suwanee, Georgia, was recognized as a 2025 Mass Production Supplier Award Winner.
Honda has produced automobiles in North America for over 40 years, beginning in November 1982 at the Marysville Auto Plant in Ohio.
In 2025, nearly 99% of all Honda and Acura automobiles sold in the U.S. were made in North America.
Honda established manufacturing operations in North America in 1979 with motorcycle production in Marysville, Ohio.
Honda currently employs about 33,000 associates in North America who support manufacturing operations at 18 plants.
These 18 plants have an annual capacity to produce 1.6 million automobiles, nearly 2 million engines, 52,000 power equipment products, and about 250,000 powersports products.
The facilities also produce the HondaJet advanced light jet and GE Honda HF120 turbofan engines.
Cumulatively, Honda has invested more than $25 billion in its North American manufacturing capabilities, including more than $3.1 billion over the past five years alone.
The company works with over 750 original equipment suppliers in North America with cumulative parts purchases of over $631 billion since 1982.
source_title: Honda Recognizes 56 North American Suppliers with Awards for Operational Excellence

facts: Officials at Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. say they've opened a second assembly plant in Newnan.
The company will hire 100 employees to work on a new utility vehicle.
Yamaha has completed transferring all of its ATV production to the Coweta County plant south of Atlanta.
The plant now employs 1,250 workers.
In addition to the 100 to be hired, an additional 300 workers could be added in years to come.
The facility already produces Yamaha's Grizzly 700 FI all-terrain vehicle and the YFZ450R sport ATV.
The new plant will produce the Viking side-by-side vehicle and other models.
source_title: Yamaha Expands Production In Georgia | Georgia Public Broadcasting

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp welcomed the announcement that Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. has officially decided to move its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The company is relocating out of California following roughly 50 years of operation there.
The decision follows a meeting between Governor Kemp and Yamaha executives in October of last year at the Southeast U.S./Japan (SEUS-Japan) annual gathering in Tokyo.
Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. manufactures ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, and other motorized products.
The company already has a strong presence in Georgia, employing over 2,300 Georgians.
The relocation of the Yamaha Motor Corp. U.S. headquarters will begin this year (2026) and is expected to continue through 2028.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that they are honored and proud to welcome Yamaha’s American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business.
Governor Brian P. Kemp noted that the move is a loud and clear testament to what Georgia offers job creators from around the world.
Governor Brian P. Kemp invited other California-based companies looking for a better home to consider Georgia.
The Yamaha Motor Company currently employs thousands of people across various facilities in Georgia.
The 1.3-million-square-foot Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America factory in Newnan opened in 1986 and currently employs roughly 2,000 Georgians.
Yamaha relocated its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999.
The company expanded the Kennesaw facility with a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
In 2019, the company moved its motorsports operations to Georgia to benefit from close proximity to existing facilities.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Yamaha to Move HQ to Georgia - SelectCobb

facts: Yamaha Motor Corp., USA announced $30,000 in funding to All Kids Bike for six programs teaching kids in Georgia and California public schools' kindergarten PE classes how to ride bikes.
The grant is provided through the Yamaha Outdoor Access Initiative (OAI).
Yamaha employees from Marietta, Georgia, and Cypress, California corporate offices, plus YMMC personnel in Newnan, Georgia, volunteered to build and deliver bikes and helmets to local schools.
Steve Nessl, Yamaha's Motorsports Marketing Manager, stated that giving employees the opportunity to contribute time is meaningful and valuable.
The All Kids Bike program is now in 350 schools across 45 states, with another 50 schools in training to have the program by year-end.

Full program deliveries included 24 balance bikes, pedal conversion kits, helmets, and a teacher's bike to Elm Street Elementary in Newnan, Georgia, A.L. Burruss Elementary School in Marietta, Georgia, and Juliet Morris Elementary School in Cypress, California.
Three additional schools in Newnan, Marietta, and Cypress are guaranteed funding through the Yamaha grant for this year.
Bob Brown, Vice President of Finance and Operations Support at YMMC, highlighted that these are the first schools in Georgia to receive the All Kids Bike program.
Ryan McFarland, founder of All Kids Bike and CEO of Strider Sports International, Inc., stated he grew up on Yamaha and helped deliver bikes to Morris Elementary in Cypress.
YMMC facility in Newnan, Georgia, was founded in 1986 and produces golf cars, personal watercraft, Side-by-Side vehicles, and all-terrain vehicles (ATVs).
The YMMC factory holds ISO9001 Quality Management System and ISO14001 Environmental Management System certifications.
YMUS operates corporate offices in California and two in Georgia, with facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, and factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.
Yamaha OAI has contributed more than $4.5 million to nearly 400 projects nationwide over more than 12 years.

links.context: Submission guidelines and applications for grants

source_title: Yamaha Funds Six All Kids Bike Programs in Georgia and California

facts: Brian Kemp announced that Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. will relocate its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw, moving from California after roughly 50 years of operation there.
The relocation of the Yamaha Motor Corp. U.S.A. headquarters is expected to begin this year and continue through 2028.
Yamaha manufactures ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, and other motorized products.
The company already has a substantial presence in Newnan and employs more than 2,300 people across Georgia.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that welcoming Yamaha's American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business is a testament to what Georgia offers job creators from around the world.
The company operates a major manufacturing facility in Newnan known as the Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America plant.
The 1.3-million-square-foot Newnan plant opened in 1986 and employs about 2,000 workers.
Yamaha moved its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999.
The company expanded its Kennesaw location with a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
In 2019, the company relocated its motorsports operations to Georgia to be closer to its existing facilities.
Governor Kemp first discussed the potential relocation with Yamaha executives in October during the Southeast U.S./Japan annual gathering in Tokyo.
Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. was founded in 1955 and is headquartered in Iwata, Shizuoka, Japan.

The company produces motorcycles, marine products, power equipment, and robotics under its corporate mission of being a “Kando Creating Company,” focused on delivering new excitement and enriching lives around the world.

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation SMART Warehouse
facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) broke ground on a new “SMART” Warehouse in Newnan, Georgia.
The groundbreaking occurred yesterday relative to June 10, 2022.
This is the first-of-its-kind SMART Warehouse for Yamaha Motor Company worldwide.
The facility spans 200,000 square feet and utilizes state-of-the-art automated storage and retrieval systems.
The warehouse will reduce response time for fulfilling dealer orders for Yamaha WaveRunners, Golf Cars, ATVs, and Side-by-Sides by more than 50 percent.
Mike Chrzanowski, President and CEO of Yamaha Motor Corporation, stated the facility will improve operational efficiency and material flow velocity to factory assembly lines.
Demand for outdoor recreational products produced at this Georgia factory continues to exceed supply.
SMART stands for Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation.
The medium parts automated storage and retrieval system will retrieve and put away 240 containers per hour, compared to the current rate of 170 containers per hour.
The small part automated system will handle 2400 containers per hour, compared to the current capability of 550 containers per day.
Automatic conveyors and tuggers will move parts through the SMART center to the factories, enabling a 50 percent reduction in forklifts.
The significant investment underscores Yamaha’s long-term commitment to YMMC employees, the City of Newnan, and Coweta County.
The facility will bring higher paid and more skilled jobs into Coweta County, including robotics experts, software developers, and systems engineers.
The warehouse was designed by SSI SCHAEFER.
An observation platform will be included for local school students to learn about manufacturing and automated processes.
Yamaha expects to complete the SMART Warehouse in 2024.
Yamaha Motor Corporation, USA (YMUS) maintains corporate offices in California and two in Georgia, facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, and factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.

YMUS sells products including ATVs, Side-by-Side vehicles, WaveRunners, Golf Cars, motorcycles, snowmobiles, boats, outboard motors, and outdoor power equipment through a nationwide network of distributors and dealers.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation Breaks Ground on Innovative SMART Warehouse in Newnan, Georgia - Motor Sports NewsWire

facts: Japanese automaker Yamaha Motor Company and autoparts manufacturer Yanmar Company announced an alliance for the United States recreational off-highway vehicle (ROV) business.

The aim of this business alliance is to expand sales in the growing US ROV market by leveraging Yamaha’s technical capabilities and manufacturing know-how alongside Yanmar’s farmer-oriented dealer network in the US.
The companies will conclude a contract by the end of 2016 for the OEM supply of Yamaha’s multi-purpose VIKING and VIKING VI ROV models to Yanmar starting in December 2016.
Yanmar will sell these supplied products under its own brand name beginning in January 2017.

The two companies share values of creating and developing markets through Monozukuri (engineering, manufacturing, and marketing) and have previously collaborated on industrial-use unmanned helicopters, generators, and snow blowers, according to an official statement from the automobile manufacturer.
Demand for ROVs in the US market has been increasing in recent years, with applications ranging from practical work such as agriculture and dairy farming to leisure-oriented purposes.
Yamaha launched the VIKING, a 3-seater multi-purpose model, in 2013.
Yamaha launched the VIKING VI, a 6-seater model, in 2014.
The two ROV models supplied on an OEM basis will feature an exterior design refreshed to Yanmar specifications through new coloring and graphics.
The products will be sold under the Yanmar brand and model name as new gasoline engine-powered vehicles.
Manufacturing of these ROV models will be carried out in the state of Georgia by Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America.
source_title: Yamaha-Yanmar form alliance for US recreational off highway vehicle biz, ETAuto

canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. Newnan Facility
facts: The new line of Yamaha all-terrain vehicles will be produced at the company's Newnan, GA plant.
The introduction of the new product line will mean the initial hire of approximately 100 employees.
Viking, the newest of Yamaha's all-terrain vehicles, will go into production in late June or early July.
The introduction of the side-by-side Viking line will require Yamaha to open a second assembly plant at the Newnan facility.

Mike Martinez, vice president of Yamaha's ATV/SxS group, stated that the Viking is "the most comfortable and off-road capable vehicle in its class based in large part on feedback from farmers and ranchers who asked for a rugged, high-capacity utility vehicle that is also fun and comfortable to drive."

Martinez noted that the all-new Viking is "the only side-by-side to offer three-person seating capacity with other features that farmers and ranchers want, like electric power steering and enough cargo capacity to hold a fully-loaded pallet."
Approximately 70 percent of the all-terrain vehicles produced by the Japan-based Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. have a market in the United States.
Martinez stated that "Yamaha continues to ramp up U.S. production thanks to the ATV production transfer, the introduction of all-new products like the Viking and improving market conditions."
The Newnan Yamaha facility currently employs 1,250 people.
The addition of the Viking line will add 100 people to that total with the potential for the addition of 200 or more employees once production ramps up over the next two years.
Yamaha has invested $250 million in its Newnan operations in the past 12 years.
The facility is now Yamaha's worldwide hub for ATV/SxS production and distribution.
The company also has operations in California, Wisconsin, and Texas.
source_title: Yamaha To Expand ATV Production, Add 100 Jobs  | Dirt Toys Magazine

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America operates a facility in Newnan, GA.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America and Yamaha Golf-Car Company donated a new golf car to Piedmont Newnan Hospital.
The donated vehicle is designated for hospital volunteers and to provide a free shuttle service for patients arriving at and departing from the hospital.
Mike Robertson serves as the CEO of Piedmont Newnan Hospital.
The recent opening of Piedmont Medical Plaza II has increased patient volumes, creating a need for additional shuttle capacity.
Following this donation, the hospital now operates three golf cars.
The additional golf car enables the shuttle service to transport more individuals across the hospital campus without long wait times.

Piedmont's volunteer drivers provide shuttle services for patients or visitors with impaired mobility, health risks that prevent walking long distances, those accompanied by small children, or general visitors.
The hospital has implemented COVID-19 safety measures to ensure rider safety on the shuttle service.
Mike Chrzanowski holds the title of Chairman at YMMC (Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America).

Tom McDonald serves as the President of Yamaha Golf Car Company and emphasized that supporting the local community is a serious priority for Yamaha, specifically highlighting assistance for patients, visitors, and staff navigating the hospital parking complex.
This marks the second golf car Yamaha has donated to Piedmont Newnan since the hospital opened its new campus on Poplar Road in 2012.
Piedmont Newnan operates as a nonprofit community hospital where donations are vital for supporting their mission and directly benefiting patient health and wellness.
source_title: YAMAHA MOTOR MANUFACTURING AND YAMAHA GOLF-CAR COMPANY DONATE NEW GOLF CAR TO HELP PATIENTS OF PIEDMONT NEWNAN HOSPITAL | Golf Cart Resource

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America designs and assembles WaveRunners, ATVs, Side by Sides, and Golf Cars at its Newnan plant in Coweta County, Georgia.
The Newnan facility is one of Coweta County’s largest private employers.
Yamaha is currently expanding its team by hiring another 200 contract employees before the end of the year (2021).
Most jobs with Yamaha start as contracts at $16.50 per hour and are eligible to be converted to full-time hires in 30 days.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing in Newnan donated $10,000 to fund scholarships for over 300 students from Title I schools in Coweta County to attend a floating classroom program on West Point Lake.
The $10,000 donation specifically provides opportunities for 333 students to attend the three-hour field trip.
The donation was paid for through the company's recycling program at the Newnan facility.
Bob Brown, Vice President of Finance and Operations Support at Yamaha, stated the company aims to excite kids about science and invest in future generations of scientists and vehicle assemblers.
source_title: Yamaha donates $10K for kids to learn science on floating classroom | The Citizen

facts: DHL Supply Chain and Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) are progressing with studies, permitting, and filings that could lead to the construction of a new distribution facility on YMMC’s Newnan, Georgia campus.
The proposed facility is 784,700 square feet.
The project represents an estimated $60–90 million investment.
The facility would be solely dedicated to YMMC operations.
If approved, the new facility is expected to improve efficiency while also benefitting the community.
Currently, freight traffic flows between YMMC’s main plant and off-site distribution centers, contributing to congestion along Highway 34 and surrounding intersections.
Consolidating logistics operations on the YMMC campus could reduce truck traffic on local roads, ease congestion, improve safety, and come at no added cost to taxpayers.
Bob Brown serves as President of YMMC.
Bob Brown stated that the project is about strengthening the supply chain and being a good neighbor while carefully navigating studies and permitting.
A key potential benefit is a dedicated, world-class logistics hub to support Yamaha’s manufacturing operations.
Another benefit is reduced truck traffic on local roads by consolidating operations on-campus.
The site design will be thoughtful to maintain the character of the surrounding area.
The project would invest in roadway improvements such as new turn lanes and upgraded intersections to improve traffic flow and safety.
Sustainable features will include stormwater management systems, tree buffers, and pervious surfaces to reduce environmental impact.
Pending successful completion of studies and required approvals, construction could begin as early as 2026.
Operations could potentially start in the first quarter of 2028.
Dave Rumler serves as President of Automotive Engineering and Manufacturing at DHL Supply Chain North America.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Company (YMMC) employs more than 2,000 metro Atlanta residents to design and build recreational vehicles at its Newnan manufacturing facilities.
All of the world’s Yamaha golf cars, Side-by-Sides, as well as most of its WaveRunners and ATVs are manufactured at the plant in Coweta County, Georgia.

links.context: Information about Yamaha Motor Manufacturing and its role in the Georgia community

source_title: DHL Supply Chain to Build New Distribution Facility on Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Campus – Winters Media

canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) is based in Newnan, Georgia.
The company sponsored a recent Environmental, Health and Safety Day to highlight the importance of workplace safety and environmental impact for its team.

Employees and contractors at the facility received T-shirts with the slogan “Stop, Think, Act Revs Our Safety Culture” to remind them of the focus on preventing workplace injury and identifying hazards ahead of time.
Approximately 2,500 people work at the Newnan location.
Jason Broshear serves as the division manager of corporate planning and sustainability.
The company empowers frontline workers to identify safety hazards and develops solutions collectively with management to prevent injuries.
The “Stop, Think, Act” process instructs workers to stop if they see a hazard, think about how to fix it, and act by taking steps to correct it.

Daily operational focuses include increasing safety, health and environmental awareness; proactively preventing injuries; employee engagement and management support; prevention of illness; reduction of air emissions; reducing energy and water use; and lowering landfill waste.
The Newnan facility’s recycling program has a sizable ongoing impact by funding local environmental education, community programs, local charities, and college scholarships.
For an EHS Day appreciation event, the company used SCRIP from nearby Huddleston Elementary School in Peachtree City to purchase 2,700 gift cards to local restaurants for its workforce.
This initiative provided Huddleston Elementary School with a fundraising windfall of $5,000.
Gift cards and T-shirts were funded by proceeds from employee recycling efforts involving cardboard, metal, plastics, and other materials.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America designs and assembles WaveRunners, ATVs, Side by Sides, and Golf Cars at the Newnan plant.
The facility is one of Coweta County’s largest private employers.
The company is currently expanding its team by hiring another 100 contract employees.
Most jobs with Yamaha start at $18 per hour and are eligible to become full-time hires in 30 days.
source_title: Yamaha celebrates workplace safety and its environmental impact – Winters Media

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC), based in Newnan Georgia, broke ground June 9 on a new, advanced, and innovative “SMART” Warehouse.
The SMART Warehouse is the first of its kind for Yamaha Motor Company worldwide.
The facility uses state-of-the-art automated storage and retrieval systems.
The new warehouse will be 200,000-square-foot.
The facility will reduce response time for fulfilling dealer orders for Yamaha WaveRunners, Golf Cars, ATVs, and Side-by-Sides by more than 50 percent.
The automated system is described as a seven-story vending machine that uses high-speed automation to send parts immediately to build recreational vehicles.

Mike Chrzanowski, President and CEO of Yamaha Motor Corporation, stated the new warehouse will improve overall operational efficiency and material flow velocity to factory assembly lines to get product to dealers and customers faster.
Demand for all outdoor recreational products produced at this factory continues to exceed supply.
SMART is an acronym for Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation.
The medium parts automated storage and retrieval system will be capable of retrieving and putting away 240 containers per hour, compared to the current rate of 170 containers per hour.
The small part automated system will be capable of handling 2400 containers per hour, in comparison to the current capability of 550 containers per day.
The new systems will enable Yamaha to reduce forklifts by 50 percent using automatic conveyors and tuggers to move parts through the SMART center to the factories.
The significant investment underscores the company’s long-term commitment to YMCC employees as well as the City of Newnan and Coweta County.
The system will bring higher paid and more skilled jobs into Coweta County, including robotics experts, software developers, and systems engineers.
The warehouse was designed by SSI SCHAEFER.

facts: Yamaha Motor Corporation will install solar arrays at the company’s Southeastern Headquarters in Kennesaw, Ga. and Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) in Newnan, Ga. this summer.
The initiative supports Yamaha’s goal to reduce emissions produced as a direct result of business activities and achieve carbon neutrality within its manufacturing operations and facilities by 2035.
Yamaha expects conclusion of the solar installations by the end of 2024.
Yamaha will work with VeloSolar in Atlanta, Ga., for the installation of the solar array panels.
With more than a decade of experience designing, installing and maintaining commercial solar installations, VeloSolar is one of Georgia’s largest solar installation companies.
The Kennesaw solar array will supply about 60 percent of the facility’s electricity needs.
At the Newnan facility we anticipate avoiding roughly 13,600 tons of carbon dioxide over the life of the array – that’s equivalent to eliminating approximately 14,930,000 pounds of coal emissions.
These panels are in addition to the already existing solar arrays at YMMC.
The solar projects in Georgia also align with Yamaha’s participation in the DrawdownGeorgia Business Compact.

A statewide initiative facilitated by the Ray C. Anderson Center for Sustainable Business at the Georgia Institute of Technology (Georgia Tech), the Drawdown Georgia Business Compact is a business-focused collaborative initiative focused on galvanizing climate action in Georgia.

Mike Chrzanowski, President and CEO, Yamaha Motor Corporation, USA, stated that the installation represents a substantial step forward in reducing carbon emissions and that the company will continue to identify emission-reducing opportunities as it moves toward 2035.

According to a detailed NHTSA filing (Recall No. 25V769) dated Nov 14, 2025, a defect stems from a three-day period in July 2025 when automated crimping equipment was offline for maintenance, causing the backup manual process to produce inconsistent terminal connections.
The issue affects vehicles built between July 31 and October 24, 2025, creating a risk of increased injury in certain frontal crashes.
GMC's investigation shows that the defective wire harness, built by Yazaki North America, may interfere with the airbag's ability to receive deployment signals.
Although some vehicles produced during the period were corrected before leaving the plant, 245 SUVs and 109 pickups remain at risk.
GMC says owners may notice an airbag warning light, but not all vehicles will display one.
Dealers will install a jumper harness with properly crimped connectors at no cost, with owner notifications scheduled to begin December 22, 2025.
The issue was first flagged on August 21, 2025, when a loose wire was noticed during assembly of a Hummer EV SUV.
GM opened a formal investigation days later and traced the problem to a manual crimping procedure used during supplier machine downtime.
No field incidents or injuries have been reported, but GM opted to issue a safety recall after confirming inconsistent harness integrity.
The airbag defect adds to a broader pattern of scrutiny surrounding GM's safety campaigns and legal pressure over past recalls.
This latest recall will likely intensify conversations about quality control as GM scales its EV lineup.
Yazaki North America's competitors include Systems Control, Edison, Anord Control Systems, Bosch Mobility Solutions, Daimler Truck North America and 7 more.

EEDDS is a technology that reliably and accurately conveys the information and power needed for a vehicle.
EEDDS consists of EEDS, which optimally distributes and connects power and information inside the car, and HMI, which optimally conveys information inside and outside the car to the driver.
The fusion of EEDS and HMI creates EEDDS (Electrical/Electronic Distribution & Display System), connecting the inside of a car, a car to the outside, and a car to a person.

facts: The YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. National Manufacturing Center is located in Macon, Georgia.
The facility produces zippers and other fastening products.
The company installed a building automation system called TREND to monitor and control the facility’s electrical, water, and gas consumption within its plants.
The TREND system contains a dashboard with charts showing the facility’s current water, electricity, and gas consumption.

Dustin Miller, YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. senior manager of facilities engineering, stated that monitoring water usage at each plant and specific processes allowed them to eliminate wasted usage during down periods.
The system enabled the company to reduce the amount of water required to run certain processes.
The facility caught an excessive flow of water during a shutdown period caused by various equipment and valve failures using the monitoring system.
Through the use of TREND, the company reduced the number of air compressors used in one plant from four to two and a half based on measured air flow demand in cubic feet per minute.
Reducing the air compressors decreased the horsepower required to supply the plant and significantly reduced the cost to run them.
The TREND system is described as a continuous monitoring platform.
The facility operates an extensive recycling program with containers inside plants for plastic bottles, cans, white paper, and magazines.
Recycling containers outside the plants are used for scrap metal, cardboard, and brass.
The company participates in the annual Ocmulgee Alive! River Cleanup event every October to help clean up the Ocmulgee River in Macon, Georgia.

The clinic provides treatments for conditions ranging from simple aches and pains to complex issues like diabetes and asthma.

Founder and president Tadao Yoshida established a "cycle of goodness" philosophy emphasizing profit, societal enrichment, and worker happiness.
The Macon complex utilizes decentralized management and vertical integration of production lines to eliminate inter-plant shipping of unfinished goods.
A filament extrusion plant for zippers operates with only three workers per shift, maintained at that level for safety reasons.
Approximately 400 YKK employees from Japan have visited the Macon facility over the past decade to study its operations.
YKK replaces all machinery every five years as a standard operational practice.

facts: YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. announced the launch of its new Solutions Group ahead of the Industrial Fabrics Association International (IFAI) Expo.

The Solutions Group was created to help manufacturers struggling with issues such as time-consuming manual processes, high scrap rate and wasted material, inconsistent quality, high inventory, multiple suppliers, and rising production costs.
The group will provide consultation on new product development and manufacturing process improvement, custom machinery and assembly service.
YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. has been engaging in manufacturing process improvement for customers through its Field Technology Centers since 1978.
John Smith serves as president of YKK U.S.A. Inc.

President John Smith stated that the YKK brand is synonymous with quality and innovation, and that YKK has long been a trusted partner in providing high-quality products that solve customers’ problems.

President John Smith noted that eighty-six years of experience manufacturing zippers and other fastening products means YKK has an enormous amount of know-how regarding sewing operations, waste reduction and process improvement.
President John Smith asked those who have turned to YKK for quality products to now seek their expertise on improving efficiency and reducing scrap and inventory.

One of YKK’s fundamental behaviors is “listen to understand,” and consultations by YKK begin with a plant visit and listening session by a team of YKK engineers to understand the issues before solutions are offered.
YKK’s technical support network is located across the U.S., Mexico, the Caribbean, and Central America.
YKK Solutions Group customers can expect improved ergonomics for their employees and a more sustainable operation as waste and energy are reduced.

President John Smith stated that YKK believes in the “Cycle of Goodness,” that no one prospers without rendering benefit to others, and that YKK commits to using its ingenuity to provide creative solutions for customers to improve their bottom line.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that YKK AP America Inc. will invest up to $125 million in an expansion that will include a new manufacturing facility and additional machinery and equipment in Bibb County.
The new facility will create 100 new jobs in addition to the 250 jobs YKK AP currently supports in Macon, Georgia.
Governor Kemp stated that ensuring companies already operating in Georgia can grow and thrive is a priority alongside attracting new investments.
Governor Kemp noted YKK AP recognizes the advantages of doing business in middle Georgia and has chosen to expand its relationship with the Macon community through 100 new jobs.
YKK AP specializes in architectural products for commercial and residential buildings.
YKK AP entered the U.S. market in 1991 with a vertically integrated, commercial products factory in Dublin, Georgia.
YKK AP maintains its headquarters in Atlanta.
YKK AP and its Ontario-based subsidiary Erie Architectural Products employ over 1,200 employees in North America.
Nearly 900 of the company's employees are located within the State of Georgia.
Oliver Stepe, President of YKK AP America Inc., stated the company is excited to increase its investment in Macon, the hometown of their residential products business.

The new facility will expand the workforce, introduce further vertical integration of vinyl extrusion, glass, and window production, and allow for enhanced efficiencies to manufacture high-quality windows and doors for homebuilders and homeowners.

Oliver Stepe stated this investment builds toward a long-term vision to be the leader in the residential window and door market and positions the company to expand its business in and beyond the Southeast region.
YKK AP’s new state-of-the-art facility will be located at the Macon-Bibb I-75 Industrial Park, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certified site.
After completion, the company will relocate its operations and employees from its current site on Ocmulgee Boulevard to the new facility.
The Macon facility will become home to the company’s residential division, which manufactures vinyl windows and doors for builders and homeowners.

The facility will serve as a model plant for future North American manufacturing facilities, featuring new technologies, advanced sustainable manufacturing processes, and enhanced work environments for employees.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in early 2024.
The company is currently hiring for numerous positions.
Macon-Bibb County Mayor Lester M. Miller stated it is a great day when a long-running business partner decides to make another investment and bring an additional 100 jobs to the area.
Mayor Miller noted that using the new state-of-the-art facility as a blueprint for future facilities illustrates trust in Macon-Bibb County and Georgia as the best place to do business.
Senior Project Manager Candice Scott represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was completed in partnership with the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority and Georgia Power.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated YKK AP has a strong history of growth and success in Georgia, opening its first branch in Atlanta in 1986 and establishing its U.S. manufacturing presence in middle Georgia five years later.
Commissioner Wilson noted the company has since expanded its presence in Georgia and built valuable relationships with local communities.

Commissioner Wilson stated companies in the advanced manufacturing industry, like YKK AP, have played a key role in bringing jobs and opportunities to Georgia, creating the second most new jobs in the state last year.
To earn GRAD certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific certification requirements prior to a visit by a prospective business.
Completing GRAD certification with GDEcD is a proactive way for community planners and landowners to help catalyze economic growth and industrial development for their regions.
Through the GRAD program, Georgia has more than 60 industrial certified sites that are ready for fast-track industrial projects.

facts: CJ Foodville Corporation announced it will invest more than $47 million to build a new bakery and food processing facility in Gainesville.
A subsidiary of CJ Group, CJ Foodville is one of the largest food service and food processing companies in South Korea.
Governor Brian Kemp announced the investment on September 5, 2023.
The project will create an estimated 285 jobs in Hall County.
The company made its first overseas expansion and entered the U.S. market in 2004 with its TOUS les JOURS bakery café brand.
The TOUS les JOURS brand currently has four storefronts in Georgia.
The new facility will be located at the Gainesville Business Park northeast of Atlanta.
The facility is expected to be operational in 2025, according to the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
Once operational, the facility is expected to produce more than 19,000 tons of products per year at full capacity.
Tony Hunsoo Ahn, CEO of CJ Foodville USA, stated the decision was based on strong profitability to enhance production and supply capabilities for the expanding TOUS les JOURS business.
Ahn noted the company is committed to promoting the unique competitiveness of the K-Bakery, built upon excellent product quality in a diverse range of bread and cakes.
The ultimate goal is to establish the TOUS les JOURS brand as a globally cherished bakery brand that transcends borders.
South Korea was one of the top three international sources of job creation in Georgia during Fiscal Year 2023.

In August 2023, two other South Korean companies announced investment projects in Georgia: Duckshin Housing will invest more than $15 million for its first U.S. manufacturing presence in Athens, and Daesol Ausy will invest $72 million for a new manufacturing facility in Harris County.
Half of the United States’ top 100 food processing companies operate in Georgia.

Locations of new and expanding food processing companies ranked among the greatest number of new industrial projects during fiscal year 2022 and resulted in more than $923 million in investment in fiscal year 2023, according to GDEcD.

links.context: Georgia Department of Economic Development tweet about the investment announcement

source_title: South Korean Food Processing Company Announces $47 Million Investment in Georgia | The Star News Network

facts: Federal energy officials on Wednesday announced a record $26.5 billion loan to electric utilities in Georgia and Alabama.
The loan aims to save customers money as companies undertake expansion driven by demand from computer data centers.
$22.4 billion of the total loan will go to Georgia Power.
$4.1 billion of the total loan will go to Alabama Power.
Georgia Power and Alabama Power are subsidiaries of Atlanta-based Southern Company, one of the nation’s largest utilities.
The companies plan to use the funds to build new natural-gas fueled power plants, build new transmission lines, and upgrade existing power plants.
Energy Secretary Chris Wright said the loan will result in more than $7 billion in savings over decades from a lower, federally subsidized interest rate.
Chris Wright stated that Southern is focused on driving down costs and ensuring customers have access to affordable, reliable, and secure energy for decades to come.

Wright and President Donald Trump have frequently advocated for fossil fuel-friendly policies, including orders over the past nine months to keep some coal-fired plants open past planned retirement dates to ensure grid reliability.
Wright claims these orders have saved utility customers millions of dollars and helped keep lights on during last month’s winter storm.
Critics argue the orders are unnecessary and have raised electric bills as utilities operate older, more expensive plants.
Chris Womack, Southern’s chairman, president and CEO, said the loans will help lower the cost of grid investments to enhance reliability and resilience for customers.
The loan announcement comes amid scrutiny over rising utility bills, with electricity prices increasing faster than inflation in many states.
There is widespread opposition to new data centers for artificial intelligence.
Trump announced a “ratepayer protection pledge” against higher utility bills tied to AI during his State of the Union address on Tuesday.
Trump stated tech companies will provide their own power as they build data centers, though he provided no details and claimed prices will go down.
It is unclear whether any tech companies have signed pledges to build their own power plants.
Wright said on a call with reporters that “every name you know that’s developing a data center has been in dialogue with us.”
Wright cited “cooperation” from tech giants Microsoft, Google, and Meta, but did not specify any written agreements.

Federal officials have long provided utility loans, including $12 billion guaranteed by the first Trump administration and President Barack Obama’s administration for two costly nuclear reactors at Georgia’s Plant Vogtle, partially owned by Georgia Power.
Trump’s tax and budget bill last year reshaped the loan program to focus on increasing capacity to generate and transmit electricity.
Loan guarantees under President Joe Biden focused on green energy goals.
Gregory Beard, director of the newly renamed Office of Energy Dominance Financing, said cutting interest rates and discarding Biden’s policy will restore affordability.
The loan office will review individual projects to ensure financial viability.
Beard stated they will not build a plant or deploy capital until sure it is right for the local community and ratepayer.
These requirements do not appear to be laid out in loan agreements that Southern released on Wednesday.
Jennifer Whitfield, an attorney for the Southern Environmental Law Center who represented Georgia Power expansion opponents, said the loans will save money for Georgians but questioned their wisdom.
Whitfield called it a “bailout paid for by every taxpaying citizen of the United States.”
Any savings for customers must be approved by elected Public Service Commissions in Alabama and Georgia.
Commissioners last July approved a three-year rate freeze requested by Georgia Power.
Commissioners in Alabama approved a two-year rate freeze in December.
Company officials tout the freezes when utilities nationwide seek record increases, but opponents complain company-friendly regulators locked in high prices and profits.
Voters removed two Republican incumbents from the Georgia commission in November amid complaints about rising bills.
Commissioner Peter Hubbard, one of two new Democrats, unsuccessfully tried to roll back approval for Georgia Power’s expansion in recent weeks.
Hubbard stated that declining costs of solar, wind, and battery power could make new natural gas plants uneconomic over time.
Hubbard said the federal loan is “locking us into a costlier option” and is not meeting the moment of affordability.
source_title: Feds announce $26.5 billion loan for electric power expansion in Georgia and Alabama - WTOP News

facts: Adient West Point is located in West Point, Ga., roughly halfway between Atlanta and Montgomery.
The plant operates just down the road a couple miles from the Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) plant.
Adient serves as a Tier 1 supplier to Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia.
Adient's decision to operate in West Point mirrors the keiretsu tradition initiated by Japanese automakers and is now a standard best practice for automakers worldwide.
Parent company Adient plc is the world’s largest supplier of automotive seats, responsible for providing roughly one out of every three automotive seats.
Adient plc is a $17.4 billion company that spun off from Johnson Controls a couple years ago.
The parent company has well over 200 manufacturing plants worldwide.

Each plant follows the Adient Manufacturing System (AMS) approach to manufacturing, built on four foundations: customer focus, stable operations environment, zero tolerance for waste, and organizing around customer pull.
Adient West Point is the first U.S. just-in-time (JIT) plant to achieve AMS Level 3.
Achieving AMS Level 3 requires implementing and demonstrating evidence for 556 manufacturing objectives.
The plant’s continuous improvement initiatives include the use of Six Sigma, lean manufacturing and Kepner & Tregoe problem-solving.
The hourly workforce has been certified in “5 why” analysis to discover the root cause of any problem areas.
Adient West Point adopted High-Performance Teams (HPTs) and became the benchmark plant for HPT within all seating plants in the United States.
The West Point plant served as the pilot plant for HPTs for the company.
It took a year to prepare the workforce for the HPT effort, which included lessons in teambuilding, building trust and teaching the hourly team the plant metrics.
The HPT effort launched in 2017 with the full engagement of the hourly workers.
Under the HPT initiative, the plant achieved significant improvements in safety, quality, delivery (with no customer disruptions in the past two years) and cost.
To date, the company has achieved 526 days and counting with no OSHA recordable incidents, marking a new milestone for the plant.
KMMG recognized Adient West Point as its best supplier in safety.
KMMG asked Adient’s safety manager, Robert Brock, to train 29 other local suppliers in best practices in environmental health and safety (EHS).
Plant manager Tracy Breeding set a basic goal for the facility: “To be the best damn JIT plant in the Americas.”
Engineering manager Jason Martin has been at Adient and Johnson Controls for 22 years, noting that HPT was something new and Tracy’s enthusiasm for it was infectious.
Operations manager Steve Perdue stated that floor workers are keeping leaders accountable by asking for the information they need to do their jobs better.
Prior to HPTs, various departments at the plant functioned in silos, but now they work together as a team with improved communication.
HR manager JR Smith noted that the word on the street is that Adient is a good place to work, which makes it easier to recruit new hires and circumvent talent finding difficulties.
The plant's motto is “Safety first. Quality always. Excellence everyday.”

source_title: 2018 IW Best Plants Winner: Adient Takes a Back Seat to No One for High Performance | IndustryWeek

facts: Adient will invest $8 million to open a factory in Normal to assemble components for electric vehicle manufacturer Rivian.
Adient will create 75 full-time jobs at its new facility near Rivian’s manufacturing plant.
The state’s Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity is supporting the investment through its Reimagining Energy and Vehicles program, which promotes clean energy and advanced manufacturing.

Gov. JB Pritzker stated in a news release that more EV suppliers are calling Illinois home due to unmatched workforce, infrastructure and competitive incentives, helping grow the state's reputation as a global leader in the electric vehicle sector.
Gov. JB Pritzker noted that Adient’s new facility in Normal is a critical investment in the state’s growing EV ecosystem and supply chain.
At its new factory, Adient will produce complete front and rear seat assemblies for Rivian.
The company will repurpose an existing 85,000-square-foot warehouse south of College Avenue.
In May, Rivian announced an additional $120 million investment to build a new 1.2 million-square-foot supplier park across Highway 150 that will create nearly 100 more jobs.
That investment was supported by a $16 million incentive package from Illinois, which includes a REV tax incentive valued at $5 million over 20 years.
Last year, Rivian announced a $1.5 billion investment to build its R2 SUV model at its Normal factory.
The California-based automaker expects to create more than 550 full-time jobs within the next five years.
Rivian’s investment was supported by a 30-year incentive package from Illinois valued at $827 million.
Most of that funding is from REV tax credits that focus on workforce development.
Through REV, the automaker is eligible to receive tax benefits totaling nearly $634 million over the next three decades.
Rivian must retain at least 6,000 jobs.

Carlo Materazzo, Rivian’s vice president of manufacturing operations, said having suppliers like Adient located near their plant in Normal will be a key enabler when they increase production next year to build R2.
Materazzo added that integrating suppliers on site reduces costs and improves manufacturing efficiency.
Adient designs, develops and manufactures components and seating systems for major automakers.
The company is headquartered in Michigan and has more than 70,000 global employees and 200 manufacturing and assembly plants in 29 countries.

Normal Mayor Chris Koos said the addition of a manufacturing partner benefits Normal, Bloomington or McLean County, and all of central Illinois will benefit from job creation, a stronger economy and a foundation for future growth.
source_title: Automotive seat maker Adient to invest $8M in Normal factory near Rivian plant - Chicago Sun-Times

facts: Adient celebrated the expansion of its Greenfield facility with a ribbon-cutting ceremony attended by company, local, and regional officials on Wednesday, Nov. 2.
The company is opening a storage facility at the Greenfield plant as part of a $6 million investment.
According to Adient’s Director of Operations NA Foam Business Unit Todd Bruno, the new storage facility will hold more than two million pounds of chemical.
The chemical stored at the facility is used in manufacturing operations at all seven Adient locations in North America.
The need for the facility stems from a “significant weather event” that affected a chemical supplier in southern Texas, causing no deliveries for two weeks.
During the supply disruption, customer orders continued to arrive, forcing Adient to import the chemical by air charter from overseas at a cost of millions.
Company executive leadership challenged staff to develop a plan to make the chemical more accessible and avoid future supply issues.
Adient evaluated other North American locations for the facility but selected Greenfield due to its central location.
The Greenfield plant will be responsible for ordering, managing, and shipping the chemical to other Adient locations when needed.
Todd Bruno stated that the investment positions the Greenfield plant as a shipping site for all other plants and well for future opportunities.
Bruno noted that the organization could have placed the investment anywhere but chose Greenfield, stating it should make the plant team and community proud.
source_title: Adient celebrates expansion at Greenfield facility | The Highland County Press

facts: Adient plc is based in Plymouth, MI and is the world’s largest manufacturer of automotive seating, accounting for one-third of the global market and providing components for 25 million vehicles.
Adient was founded as a spin-off from Johnson Controls in 2016.
The company works with all major automakers and vehicle classes and operates some 200 assembly plants in 30 countries.
One of Adient's factories is a 125,000-square-foot plant located in West Point, GA.
The West Point facility assembles seats, door panels, and other interior parts for the Kia Sorento.
Construction of the West Point plant began in 2009 when Adient was still part of Johnson Controls.
Subassemblies for some 500 cars a day move through the Georgia facility.
The plant completes 55 jobs an hour with a goal to increase production to 60 jobs an hour.
Todd Emmons is an engineer in the automotive business unit for Adient and oversees operations at the West Point facility.
The Georgia plant operates on a just-in-time basis to deliver products exactly when the nearby Kia facility is ready.
Each workstation has only 84 inches of pick space.
The facility uses UNEX Manufacturing Flow Cell workstations and carton flow racks for line-side assembly.
Adient ordered 41 Flow Cells, which are assembled using UNEX instructions in less than a half an hour each.
Reconfiguring the Flow Cell racks is three times faster than reconfiguring other types of products.
The plant plans to add new car parts to the line for other types of vehicles soon.
John Maicki is president of DR Storage Systems and worked with Adient engineers to identify line-side assembly technology for the Georgia facility.
John Maicki first recommended Flow Cell racks to a customer in 2003 and has recommended them ever since.

facts: Adient and Autoliv co-developed innovative safety solutions to significantly enhance occupant protection in deeply reclined seating positions, commonly referred to as zero-gravity.
The combined safety solutions are now ready for mass production.
The solution combines Adient's new seating concept, Z-Guard, with Autoliv's advanced safety technologies.
Zero-gravity seating is gaining popularity in mid- to high-end vehicle models due to its ergonomic benefits.
Deeply reclined postures can compromise the effectiveness of conventional safety systems designed for standard upright seating, potentially leading to safety risks.
Adient's Z-Guard integrates advanced constraint and energy-absorbing technologies into a dynamic safety system built around Multi-Dimensional Collaborative Protection.
Z-Guard features an Active Cushion Collapse Mechanism that rapidly collapses the seat frame in a crash to absorb energy, adjust posture, reduce spinal impact, and protect vital body regions.
Z-Guard features an Adjustable Seat Belt Outlet that adapts to various passenger postures and body types to enhance comfort while maximizing precise restraint and minimizing shoulder slippage risks.

Autoliv technologies complement the system with an Integrated Seatbelt System for improved stability, a Dynamic Lumbar Retractor for controlled restraint force, a Pelvic Cushion Airbag to reduce pelvic impact, and a Head Side Airbag for enhanced side collision protection in reclined positions.
The combined systems safeguard critical areas including the head, neck, chest, and pelvis to deliver dynamic, full-process occupant protection.
Z-Guard enables deep integration with vehicle driver assistance systems tailored to OEM requirements and real-world scenarios.
Leveraging predictive signals, Z-Guard utilizes high-speed, motor-driven seat repositioning to adjust the occupant's posture before a collision to reduce injury risk.

If a vehicle cannot detect a crash in time or lacks active repositioning capabilities, Z-Guard provides robust protection through dual pretensioners, cushion buffering, and a collapse mechanism to prevent forward movement and submarining.

The seating concept is designed to meet the evolving demands of cockpit electronic architecture in the era of software-defined vehicles by seamlessly integrating safety features into the smart cockpit environment.
Adient is leading the commercialization of Z-Guard.
Z-Guard is scheduled for production in a high-volume model from a major global OEM.
Adient (NYSE: ADNT) is a global leader in automotive seating with around 70,000 employees in 29 countries.
Adient operates more than 200 manufacturing/assembly plants worldwide and produces and delivers automotive seating for all major OEMs.

Adient's expertise spans every step of the automotive seat-making process, from complete seating systems to individual components, taking products from research and design to engineering and manufacturing into millions of vehicles every year.
Adient directs readers to its website for more information.
source_title: Adient and Autoliv Unveil Breakthrough in Dynamic Seat Safety Solutions

facts: State and local leaders will gather on Wednesday, March 18, at the Georgia Freight Depot in Downtown Atlanta to discuss factors shaping Georgia’s economic future during the inaugural “State of Economic Development” event.
The event is hosted by the Georgia Chamber of Commerce.

The gathering will bring together business leaders, economic developers and policymakers to explore strategies, partnerships and investments driving the state’s continued economic growth and competitiveness.
The event is part of the Georgia Chamber’s ongoing “State of” series, which explores key issues shaping Georgia’s business climate.
Confirmed speakers include Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development Pat Wilson.
Georgia Chamber of Commerce President Chris Clark will speak at the event.
Chief Executive Officer of the Bulloch County Development Authority Benjy Thompson is a confirmed speaker.
Governor Brian Kemp will deliver video remarks at the event.
source_title: Georgia Chamber to host state economic event - Rough Draft Atlanta

facts: The Georgia Center of Innovation is a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
The Center launched new website assets following a record year of impact on Georgia businesses.

During fiscal year 2021, the Georgia Center of Innovation had more than 700 engagements with Georgia businesses and industry organizations, representing an increase of over 16% from the previous fiscal year.
The Center added a new area of expertise focused on Agricultural Technology (AgTech).
Teams at the Center represent some of the state’s leading industries, including manufacturing, logistics, IT, aerospace, and energy technology.
Staff with the Center are located strategically around Georgia to partner with businesses and provide expert advice and resource connections.
Services provided by the Center are complimentary and considered a concierge service provided by the state.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated the Center is proud to be the only state to provide this benefit, connecting small, large, and medium-sized businesses with solutions to source materials, offer guidance on implementing technology, and provide support for growth.

The Center expanded its IT team by establishing a partnership with Augusta University and the Georgia Cyber Center to create a shared position delivering on-the-ground support for companies in Augusta’s cybersecurity ecosystem.
The Center added support for Georgia’s growing digital media industry to its IT team.
The AgTech team supports Georgia’s No. 1 industry by focusing on precision agriculture, controlled environment agriculture, and food product innovation.
Experts at the Center consistently support GDEcD business recruitment efforts by providing critical insights and aiding in ecosystem development statewide.

Past assistance examples include helping Thomasville’s Sweet Grass Dairy reach new markets, assisting Skyfire with securing drone service contracts, and ensuring Carbice reached full-scale production for nanotech thermal tape.

During the COVID-19 pandemic, the Center led Georgia’s effort to meet PPE needs by identifying and coordinating businesses that manufacture, store, and distribute supplies, utilizing a COVID-19 Suppliers Interactive List and Map.
Executive Director David Nuckolls described the Center as an extra complimentary resource available to Georgia businesses that offers direct advice or connections to address business challenges.

GDEcD serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm, acting as the lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging industry expansion, locating markets for Georgia products, promoting tourism, and planning/mobilizing state resources for economic development.
source_title: GDEcD Georgia Center of Innovation Launches New Website Following Record Year - AllOnGeorgia

facts: Kemp highlighted Georgia's rising prominence in key industries such as electric mobility during his State of the State address.
Four of the largest economic development projects in state history were announced in less than 365 days.
Just those four projects alone will bring over 20,000 new jobs and over $17 billion in investment to rural communities across Georgia.
Those good-paying jobs are in fields that will define the next generation of manufacturing.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development announced that companies announced more than 17,000 new jobs totaling $13 billion in investment during the second half of 2022.
85% of those jobs and investments were located outside of metro Atlanta.

Kemp reiterated his budget proposals to address workforce housing and job training and recruitment, including fully funding the state's education formula, the post-secondary HOPE Scholarship, and a "rural workforce housing fund."
source_title: Kemp ushers in 'a new era' for Georgia in State of the State address | Georgia Public Broadcasting

facts: Archer Aviation has closed its business combination with Atlas Crest Investment.
Upon the completion of the transaction, Atlas Crest changed its name to Archer Aviation Incorporated and became a publicly traded company.
As its common stock and warrants commenced trading, Archer celebrated with the ceremonial ringing of the bell to open the New York Stock Exchange.
The business combination generated approximately $857.6 million of gross proceeds.
The gross proceeds include $600 million of proceeds from the PIPE.
The proceeds will help fund Archer's vision of bringing an eVTOL to market and launching an urban air mobility network.
Brett Adcock, co-founder of Archer, stated that completing the go-public transaction and having Archer trade on the New York Stock Exchange is a huge milestone for the business.

Michael Spellacy, CEO of Atlas Crest, stated that with the proceeds from the transaction, Archer is entering the public markets from a position of financial and competitive strength to accomplish its mission of revolutionizing the future of urban transportation.
Brett Adcock concluded that Archer's eVTOL aircraft can be the future of how people will travel in and around cities.
Morgan Stanley projects the urban air mobility market to be worth $1 trillion by 2040.
source_title: Archer rings the bell on the future with NYSE listing | Magazine | Business Air News

facts: Stellantis invests an additional $55 million in Archer following the achievement of its transition flight test milestone.
This latest investment builds on Stellantis’ series of open market purchases of 8.3 million shares of Archer’s stock in March of this year.
During 2023, Stellantis invested $110 million in Archer through a combination of open market stock purchases and investments under the companies’ strategic funding agreement.
Construction nears completion on Archer’s high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, GA, which is on track to be completed later this year.
The first phase of the build out is a ~350,000 square foot facility on an ~100 acre site designed to support production of up to 650 aircraft annually.
This facility would make it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aircraft industry.
Archer’s goal with this facility is to establish a factory that can support its planned commercial ramp up by leveraging Stellantis as its contract manufacturer.
Stellantis has been a strategic partner to Archer since 2020 through various collaboration initiatives, and as an investor since 2021.
During this time, Archer has leveraged Stellantis’ deep manufacturing, supply chain, and design expertise in connection with efforts to design, develop, and commercialize its eVTOL aircraft.
Archer’s Midnight is a piloted, four-passenger aircraft designed to perform rapid back-to-back flights with minimal charge time between flights.

Archer aims to transform urban travel by replacing 60–90 minute commutes by car with estimated 10–20 minute electric air taxi flights that are safe, sustainable, low noise, and cost-competitive with ground transportation.
Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares stated that the additional investment keeps them on course for a future where freedom of mobility extends beyond today’s roads.
Archer CEO Adam Goldstein highlighted the commitment by Stellantis to provide manufacturing expertise and capital needed to accelerate Archer’s business objectives.
source_title: Energy, Automobile, EV, Renewable News | Stellantis Invests Additional $55 Million In Archer Following Recent Flight Test Milestone

facts: Since the passage of the Inflation Reduction Act a year ago, Georgia ranks second in the nation for new clean energy projects.
Georgia attracted over $18.83 billion in investment, the third-highest in the country.
Covington will gain 1,000 new jobs from the Archer Aviation Electric Aircraft Manufacturing Facility.
Augusta will see 600 new jobs from the Solvay Specialty Polymers Battery Factory.
The Hyundai and LG Battery Plant will generate nearly 4,000 jobs in the Savannah area.
These Savannah battery plant jobs are thanks to Biden’s clean energy plan.
Federal investments solidify Georgia’s leadership in electric vehicles, while also promoting affordability and accessibility.
Republicans in Congress have fought to repeal these job creating measures and all the health benefits that come along with it.
Republicans in Congress pushed our government to the brink of shutdown, and the impact on Georgia families would be disastrous.

Lawmakers now have until mid-November to reach an agreement to keep the government open, and in turn, protect these new Georgia jobs and the vital air and water quality monitoring from the EPA that Georgia communities rely on to stay healthy.
Opposing these policies means opposing job creation, pollution reduction, safeguards for clean air and water, and lower utility costs for our neighbors.
Under the Gold Dome, I’m committed to clearing the path for clean energy jobs to expand workforce opportunities across Georgia.
This opportunity can spark an economic boom statewide, marking a historic moment where we prioritize both economic growth and environmental health.
Senator Tonya Anderson represents District 43, which includes Newton and Rockdale counties and a portion of DeKalb County.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. has raised $850 million in funding to support the rollout of its flying taxis.
The NYSE-listed company announced the deal on Thursday.
The transaction was structured as a direct offering in which Archer sold 85 million shares for $10 apiece.
In today’s trading session, its stock price dropped about 14% to exactly $10.
The deal marks Archer’s third nine-figure capital raise in under a year.
It secured $300 million in February from a consortium that included Blackrock and closed a $430 million investment three months earlier.
Following this week’s cash infusion, Archer’s total pro forma liquidity stands at about $2 billion.
Archer founder and Chief Executive Officer Adam Goldstein stated: “We now have the strongest balance sheet in the sector and the resources we need to execute both here in the U.S. and abroad.”
Santa Clara, California-based Archer is developing a flying taxi called Midnight that takes off like a helicopter and flies like a plane.
According to the company, it can take four passengers on up to 100-mile trips with a top speed of 150 miles per hour.
Midnight lands and takes off from specialized landing pads called vertiports.
The aircraft is powered by 12 electric rotors that can adjust their angle midflight to reduce drag.
Archer says they’re 100 times quieter than a helicopter, which makes Midnight suitable for use in urban areas.
The rotors draw power from six onboard battery packs optimized for quick charging, which reduces downtime between flights.
Midnight completed its first piloted test flight in late May.
According to Archer, the aircraft reached altitudes of more than 1,500 feet.
The successful test may have simplified the company’s latest capital raise by boosting investor confidence in its development roadmap.
Archer will use the funds to develop an “AI-based aviation software platform.”
In parallel, it plans to build the infrastructure necessary to support flying taxi networks based on Midnight.
The company has inked deal to establish such networks in the U.S., United Arab Emirates, India and South Korea.
Last year, Archer won a contract to provide air taxi services for the 2028 Summer Olympics in Los Angeles.
Midnight aircraft will ferry spectators and staff between sports venues in the city.
The company has also teamed up with United Airlines to launch multiple flying taxi routes in the U.S.

facts: Archer has facilities in Georgia and California where several Midnight aircraft are in various stages of completion.
Archer plans to expand its Midnight fleet and flight envelope throughout 2026 across its Georgia and California facilities.
The Georgia and California facilities will support Archer's planned Type Inspection Authorization (TIA) activities with the FAA as soon as this year.
Archer has submitted applications for the White House's eVTOL Integration Pilot Program (eIPP) across California, Florida, Texas, Georgia, and New York.
Archer plans to deploy additional piloted Midnight aircraft in American cities through the eIPP later this year.
President Trump signed an Executive Order titled 'Unleashing Drone Dominance' that established a White House mandate to accelerate air taxi deployments in American cities.
Archer is working directly with the DOT and FAA to help ensure the eIPP positions the U.S. to lead in the new air taxi category.
Archer's order book is in the billions, with seven of the world's largest airlines choosing to partner with Archer.
In Q4, Saudi Arabia's PIF partnered with Archer to deploy aircraft, starting with Red Sea Global.
Korean Air selected Archer as its exclusive partner.
A consortium with Japan Airlines was selected to support commercialization in both Osaka and Tokyo.
The FAA confirmed final acceptance of 100% of the 797 Means of Compliance for the Midnight aircraft, making Archer the first eVTOL manufacturer to achieve this milestone.
Completing the Means of Compliance unlocks Archer's ability to finalize remaining certification plans with the FAA, which are expected to be resolved in the coming quarters.
Type Inspection Authorization (TIA) work on the Midnight program is expected to begin as soon as this year.

Archer designed the Midnight aircraft to be as close to a Part 23 airplane as possible and vertically integrated only the powertrain and flight control software due to a lack of existing supply base, partnering with Tier 1 aerospace suppliers for the rest.
The Midnight aircraft requires only 17 issue papers for certification, with 13 being industry-standard and 4 unique to its design and battery pack.
Archer chose a four-blade aft propeller design for Midnight to reduce complexity, simplify certification, and maintain manufacturability with minimal performance impact.
The Midnight aircraft is designed to certify both vertical takeoff and landing (VTOL) and conventional takeoff and landing (CTOL), providing safety and operational flexibility advantages.

Archer's defense strategy centers on a partnership with Anduril to design an autonomous, hybrid-electric VTOL aircraft for dual use as a loyal wingman for defense and cargo/medevac for commercial customers.
Archer opened a new engineering hub in Bristol, UK, and has hired a team of 20+ local engineers.
In November, Archer announced its first third-party powertrain deal with Anduril and EDGE Group to power their Omen autonomous air vehicle.
President Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill allocated a historic $12.5 billion to modernize the U.S. air traffic control system.
Archer is working to build a full-stack autonomy solution for aviation and plans to unveil its first software product in this category later this year.
Archer acquired Hawthorne Airport to use as a testbed for autonomy technologies.
Archer partnered with Palantir to build the AI foundation for next-generation air traffic control, movement control, and route planning.
Archer is working with NVIDIA to integrate their IGX Thor platform into Midnight for real-time onboard computing for safety-critical autonomy applications.
Archer is working with SpaceX's Starlink to bring high-speed, low-latency connectivity to its aircraft.
Archer ended Q4 with approximately $2 billion in liquidity.
Adam Goldstein serves as the Founder & Chief Executive Officer of Archer.
source_title: Archer posts 2025 loss, ends year with $2.0B cash | ACHR 8-K Filing

facts: Archer Aviation received a certificate of occupancy for a high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, GA.

facts: Archer Aviation is an electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft developer transitioning from research and development toward commercial flight.
The company unveiled its “Launch Edition” program to highlight tangible steps towards bringing its Midnight aircraft to market.
Archer Aviation formed a strategic partnership with Palantir Technologies Inc. to infuse artificial intelligence into next-generation aviation.
The partnership establishes an exclusive collaboration focused on constructing an artificial intelligence foundation for the future of next-generation aviation technologies.

The alliance will initially leverage Palantir’s Foundry and Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) to significantly accelerate the scaling of Archer’s aircraft manufacturing capabilities at its cutting-edge facilities located in Georgia and Silicon Valley.

The application of Palantir’s platforms is intended to optimize and streamline Archer’s manufacturing processes, enhancing efficiency and output as the company progresses towards high-volume production of its Midnight eVTOL aircraft.
The partnership extends to a broader scope involving the joint development of next-generation software solutions designed to revolutionize critical aviation systems.
Archer and Palantir engineers are slated to work in parallel to create AI-powered software aimed at significant improvements across essential aviation functions.

These advancements include improved air traffic control for safer, more efficient airspace management, optimized ground operations to reduce congestion at vertiports and airports, and enhanced route planning for better flight paths and energy efficiency.
The formalization of the partnership took place at Palantir's AIPCon during a public signing ceremony.
Palantir co-founder and Chief Executive Officer Alex Karp and Archer founder and Chief Executive Officer Adam Goldstein participated in the public signing ceremony.
Following the unveiling of the AI-focused collaboration, shares of Archer Aviation experienced a notable intraday surge.
Market data from March 19, 2025, indicated Archer Aviation stock trading at $8.38, reflecting a gain of $0.47 per share, or around a 5% increase since the partnership announcement.
The price increase adds to a continued start of a possible rally that has seen Archer Aviation stock price up around 18% since rumors of this news started circulating on March 12, 2025.
Archer Aviation held a strong liquidity position with exceeding $1 billion in cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2024.
A $300 million capital raise in February 2025 strengthened the company's financial flexibility.
The substantial cash reserves support the Palantir partnership, Midnight aircraft commercialization, Archer Defense development, and manufacturing expansion at its ARC facility.

The collaboration with Palantir charts a smarter course for Archer, potentially accelerating its path to commercial success and solidifying its role in leading the AI-driven revolution of the aviation industry.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. announced on Dec 19, 2024, that it received the certificate of occupancy for its high-volume manufacturing facility, ARC, earlier this week.
ARC is connected to the Covington Municipal Airport in Georgia (Newton County).
Archer is working to complete the tooling load-in for the initial manufacturing line at ARC.
Production at ARC is planned to begin in early 2025, with a goal of ramping to two aircraft per month by the end of 2025.
Archer built ARC in close partnership with Stellantis to mass-produce Archer’s Midnight aircraft.
Stellantis is contributing capital, advanced manufacturing technology and expertise, and experienced personnel to scale the facility to 650 aircraft annually by 2030.

The Midnight aircraft is designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise, and safe alternative to ground transportation in congested cities around the world.
