facts: Yancey Engineered Solutions plans a Cordele facility.
The company is a metro Atlanta manufacturer.
The company is investing millions in a South Georgia facility.
The facility is expected to create hundreds of jobs.
The project will create 300 jobs.
The jobs are for a community recently hit by layoffs.
source_title: Yancey Engineered Solutions, plans Cordele facility, 300 jobs - Atlanta Business Chronicle

facts: Yancey Engineered Solutions, a division of Austell-based Yancey Bros., plans to invest $5.7 million into the shuttered Big Tex plant in Cordele.
The company will renovate the facility located at 502 South Midway Road to create a hub for power-generation packaging systems.
Company officials project the revamped factory could ultimately support up to 300 jobs over the next several years.
The project was formally announced on June 25 via a press release from the governor’s office.
Governor Brian Kemp stated the expansion will provide direct opportunity for hardworking Georgians in Crisp County and help drive further growth statewide.
Cordele Mayor Wesley Rainey and Crisp County officials welcomed the announcement, noting it could allow former Big Tex workers to return to the shop floor.
Yancey plans to hire welders, quality-control technicians, assembly workers, and drivers for the new facility.
The Cordele-Crisp Industrial Development Authority, Crisp County Power Commission, and Georgia Quick Start partnered to help secure the deal.
Yancey Engineered Solutions focuses on packaging, containerization, and sound-attenuated enclosures for generators.
The company's existing testing and service center in Griffin handles similar enclosure work to help the Cordele site ramp up smoothly.
Georgia Quick Start will provide workforce training as hiring scales up over the next several years.
Senior Regional Project Manager Christy Bozeman represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development on the competitive project.
Local leaders noted that reusing an existing industrial building cuts down on lead time and improves chances of bringing experienced manufacturing workers back.
Site work at the facility is expected to begin soon, according to Cordele-Crisp Industrial Development Authority officials.
Company and county leaders have not provided a firm opening date but stated hiring will roll out gradually as the building is refurbished.

links.context: Company careers page for job openings and training details

source_title: Shuttered Cordele Factory Roars Back To Life As Yancey Bets On 300

facts: Georgia’s second inland port will provide companies with a fast and economical route to the Port of Savannah.
source_title: Blue Ridge Connector could be Georgia economic development engine - Atlanta Business Chronicle

facts: Blue Ridge Manufacturing’s success story began in 1979 when 19-year-old Charles Rayside opened a one-man business in a rented warehouse building.
The initial operation was a small trailer business consisting of a few trailers, 4x4 truck accessories, and youthful optimism.
In 1984, the business expanded to a West Palm Beach, FL location on West Palm Beach’s main commercial thoroughfare for growing trailer sales and commercial truck equipment.
Five years later in 1989, the business moved into the truck body and equipment market.
From a one-man business in 1979, Rayside Truck and Trailer grew to 70 experienced employees, 7 acres of fenced property, a 40,000 square foot showroom/plant, and two locations.
In 2006, the company opened a new manufacturing plant in Blue Ridge, GA that is now known as Blue Ridge Manufacturing.
Charles Rayside aimed to manufacture better truck bodies based on customer input, 20 years of field testing, modern manufacturing processes, and quality workmanship.
Blue Ridge Manufacturing offers a full line of flatbed, stake, trash, dump, landscape & western truck bodies.

Charles Rayside acknowledged that with a strong Southern market, the business has grown beyond expectations and will continue providing for this expanding market with the same quality products and customer service.
source_title: Freedom - K.E. Rose Company LTD

facts: Bosch Group established a presence in North America in 1906.
As of Dec. 31, 2025, the Bosch Group employs around 38,000 associates in more than 100 locations in the North American region.
According to preliminary figures, Bosch generated consolidated sales of $18.7 billion in the U.S., Mexico and Canada in 2025.
Facts and figures section lists: Founded 1906, 41,000 associates, $17.4 billion consolidated sales, More than 100 locations.
As of September 2024, Paul Thomas serves as President and CEO of Bosch in North America and President of Bosch Mobility Americas.

In his role as president and CEO of Bosch in North America, Thomas works with business units and sales organizations across all four of the company’s business sectors: Mobility, Industrial Technology, Consumer Goods, and Energy and Building Technology.
Mr. Thomas leads technology, strategy, and sales development for the mobility sector for Bosch in the Americas region.
He also leads a four-person regional board tasked to advance mobility growth and opportunity in the Americas.
Mr. Thomas has more than 25 years of mobility experience and has held senior leadership positions in the areas of sales, production control, logistics, manufacturing and business unit management.
His global leadership experience while at Bosch includes several years abroad in Germany, as well as responsibilities in the areas of global product development and global commercial sales.
Mr. Thomas is on the MEMA Original Equipment Suppliers Board.
He has spoken at Automotive News Congress, the SAE Global Leadership Conference and CAR Management Briefing Seminars.
He prioritizes engagement in secondary education, including participation on the Mobility Advisory Council at Michigan State University.
He has previously served on the advisory board for PlanetM, the state of Michigan’s mobility incubator, and the United Way for Southeastern Michigan Campaign Cabinet.
Mr. Thomas earned a bachelor’s degree in materials and logistics management from Michigan State University, and an MBA from Wayne State University.
He completed an immersive management regimen at Carnegie Mellon University, with a distinct focus on innovation and the management of global teams.
source_title: Our company | Bosch in the USA

facts: Bosch announced an investment of around one billion US dollars over several years in the development and production of components for electromobility and automated driving in China.
The investment will fund a new centre for research, development and production in Suzhou.
The subsidiary Bosch Automotive Products will be responsible for building the facility at the existing Bosch site in Suzhou, west of Shanghai.
The new centre is titled “Bosch R&D and Manufacturing Base for New Energy Vehicles Core Components and Automated Driving”.
The facility will cover 300,000 square metres.
The centre will focus primarily on electrified drive products with the latest SiC power modules, integrated braking systems and solutions for automated driving.
The first phase of construction is scheduled to be completed by mid-2024.
The investment sum is also equivalent to around seven billion yuan or 950 million euros.
According to the German publication Handelsblatt, this is the second-largest single investment in Bosch's history after the chip factory in Dresden.

Stefan Hartung, Chairman of the Board of Bosch who also oversees Bosch’s China business on the board of management, stated that as a multinational enterprise, Bosch needs to make full use of China’s local R&D capability and production capacity.
Hartung added that through continuous development in China, Bosch will further expand its global competitiveness and create a solid foundation for the future of mobility.
Since Bosch Suzhou was founded in 1999, Bosch has continuously expanded its investments in the city and deepened cooperation with local partners.
According to Handelsblatt, 10,000 people are currently employed at four plants in Suzhou.
Bosch emphasizes that it is leading the transformation of the entire car industry in Suzhou into a modern sector.
Yudong Chen, president of Bosch China, stated the investment will enable the company to better respond to the fast-changing and demanding demand of the local market.
Bosch maintains a “local for local” development strategy to deepen roots in the local market and support China’s path to future electrified and intelligent mobility.
The Handelsblatt reported concerns about a threatening one-sided dependence on China, noting that silicon carbide power modules are particularly delicate.
Bosch is the only automotive supplier to build silicon carbide chips itself.
With Bosch’s power electronics, Chinese electric car manufacturers will become even more powerful and dominant in their home market.
German carmakers have been losing significant market share in the Chinese market, especially in electric cars.
Bosch recorded strong annual increases in revenues in electromobility, registering an order volume of more than ten billion euros in the e-mobility sector for the first time in 2021.
Figures for 2022 are not yet available.
In addition to drive solutions, Bosch sees a particularly lucrative market in hydrogen components.
In May 2022, Bosch announced its intention to enter the development of components for electrolysers.
Bosch has never entered the battery cell market.
source_title: Bosch to invest over $1 billion in e-mobility capacities in China - electrive.com

facts: GCC is preparing now to be the exclusive Bosch Rexroth Mobile Partner for six additional states: North Carolina, South Carolina, Virginia, West Virginia, Tennessee and all of Georgia.
Effective April 1, 2025, GCC will provide Bosch Rexroth products to NC, SC, VA, WV, GA, TN, FL, WA, OR, AK, PR and Northern ID.
GCC has long been a trusted partner in the hydraulics sector, specializing in providing technical expertise and tailored solutions to OEMs.
Businesses in the expanded states can now access GCC’s complete range of services, including system design, circuit optimization, and technical support, backed by Bosch Rexroth’s superior products.
GCC collaborates with OEMs to develop custom subassemblies that can meet unique project requirements.
Efficient inventory management minimizes downtime and maximizes productivity for GCC customers.
GCC’s aftermarket services ensure that equipment can stay operational for the long haul, covering installation to maintenance throughout its lifecycle.
The GCC sales team includes Certified Fluid Power Specialists, ensuring customers receive expert guidance.
GCC integrates the latest technologies to deliver smarter, more effective mobile solutions focused on safety and efficiency.
GCC promoted Jordan Pritt to Executive Vice President in April 2025, overseeing company expansion in the Southeast.
The partnership expansion enables customers across these territories to access world-class hydraulic and electrohydraulic expertise closer to home.
source_title: New Expansion: GCC, Exclusive Bosch Rexroth Mobile Partner | GCC

facts: Bandag signed an agreement with Tegeta Motors to build a state-of-the-art Tegeta Bandag Factory in Rustavi, Georgia.
The project marks an important step in expanding sustainable retreading solutions across the Caucasus region.
The facility is supported by an initial investment of USD 2 million.
The plant aims to produce up to 5,000 retreaded tyres annually.
Production capacity is expected to double within five years.
The facility will be the first Bandag retreading factory in the Caucasus.
Construction is scheduled to begin this year.
Completion of the facility is targeted for late 2026.
The project strengthens Tegeta Motors’ position as a leading provider of advanced automotive services in the region.

Jacques Fourie, President of Bridgestone Africa, Middle East Region, stated that the collaboration combines technical expertise and a deep commitment to sustainable mobility to advance high-quality retreading solutions in the Caucasus.
The facility will initially retread tyres for buses and trucks to meet international safety and performance standards.
This approach is expected to significantly reduce environmental impact and lower operational costs for corporate and commercial customers.
Representatives from Bridgestone and Bandag will provide construction oversight to ensure adherence to global quality standards.
The plant will be equipped with state-of-the-art Bandag machinery sourced from Europe and the United States.
The retreading process reinforces the casing of a used tyre with premium materials used in new tyre manufacturing.
Retreaded tyres cost 30-40% less than new tyres while delivering comparable performance.
Retreaded tyres provide a service life of up to five years.

Bandag’s retreading technology requires less raw material and energy, extends tyre life through multiple cycles, reduces waste generation, minimizes carbon emissions, and supports efficient use of natural resources.
Ekaterine Kavtaradze, CEO of Tegeta Motors, stated that the factory launch advances fleet solutions, promotes sustainable mobility, and strengthens their presence in the Caucasus region.
Tegeta Motors will integrate the retreading operations into its existing ecosystem, which includes tyre services, oil and battery operations, and recycling activities.
Bridgestone MEA continues to provide technical expertise and innovation support throughout the factory’s development.
source_title: Bandag to Build State-of-the-Art Retreading Factory in Georgia in Collaboration with Tegeta Motors and Bridgestone – Executive Bulletin

facts: Bridgestone Americas, Inc. is a subsidiary of Bridgestone Corporation (Japan), which holds 100% share capital.
The company primarily develops, manufactures, and markets tires under brands including Bridgestone, Firestone, Bandag, SureDrive, and Fuzion.
It produces tires for passenger vehicles, light trucks, commercial vehicles, agricultural vehicles, motorcycles, and off-road vehicles.
In January 2025, the company announced plans to initiate strategic initiatives to optimize its business footprint, improve competitiveness, and enhance U.S. operations.
As part of these January 2025 initiatives, the company announced the decision to close its Truck and Bus Radial Tire Plant in LaVergne, Tennessee, effective July 31, 2025.

Other January 2025 cost optimization initiatives include reducing plant capacity and workforce at its agriculture tire plant in Des Moines, Iowa, and implementing workforce reductions across corporate and sales units.

The company's tire product portfolio includes all-season tires, touring tires, run-flat tires, all-terrain tires, maximum traction tires, performance tires, summer tires, winter tires, sedan/minivan tires, utility vehicle tires, truck tires, bus tires, agriculture tires, and off-road heavy-duty tires.
The company also provides other solutions such as end-to-end fleet solutions and Airide air springs.
In 1988, Bridgestone Corporation purchased The Firestone Tire & Rubber Company.
In 1990, corporate headquarters for the merged companies moved from Chicago to Akron, Ohio, and Americas operations were consolidated as Bridgestone/Firestone, Inc.
In 1990, the company finished building a production facility in Warren County, Tennessee.
In 1992, corporate headquarters moved from Akron, Ohio to Nashville, Tennessee.
In 1994, the Warren County plant in Tennessee became the first U.S. tire plant to meet the ISO9000 quality standard.
In 1999, the company opened a passenger and light truck tire manufacturing facility in Aiken County, South Carolina.
In 2001, Bridgestone Corporation's Americas operations were reorganized under a holding company structure with Bridgestone Americas Holding, Inc. (BSAH).
In 2004, the Joliette plant in Quebec, Canada produced its 100-millionth tire.
In 2006, the Aiken County plant in South Carolina produced its 50-millionth tire.
In 2007, Bridgestone Firestone do Brasil Industria e Comercio Ltda inaugurated its second Brazilian facility in the state of Bahia.
In May 2007, Bridgestone Americas Holding, Inc. acquired Bridgestone Bandag, LLC.
In 2009, Bridgestone Americas Holding, Inc. was renamed to Bridgestone Americas, Inc. (BSAM).
In September 2021, the company completed the acquisition of Azuga Holdings Inc. (Azuga).
In June 2022, the company opened a new Advanced Tire Production Center (ATPC) in Akron, Ohio.
source_title: Bridgestone Americas, Inc. - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Bridgestone Americas, Inc. (BSAM) operates the Bandag plant in Griffin, GA.
The Bandag plant in Griffin has been in operation for over 45 years.
The facility produces tread rubber and retreading materials for Bandag dealers.
Bandag is dedicated to the research, development, and manufacturing of premium retread tires.
Bridgestone is hiring Production Technicians at the Griffin, GA location.
Starting pay for Production Technicians is $22.28/hour.
Automatic pay increases occur every 3 to 6 months, progressing to $27.32/hour in 24 months.
Tenure-based pay steps are available at 5 and 10 years.
Specialized roles offer earning potential up to $30.74/hour.
Shift premiums and perfect attendance incentives are available.
Employees are paid weekly.
Competitive medical, prescription, dental, and vision coverage begins on the first day of employment.
A 401k plan with company contribution is offered.
Paid time off (PTO) and 11 paid holidays are provided.
A teammate referral bonus of up to $750 per referral is available after employment.
Basic/supplemental life insurance and flexible health spending account options are included.
Free counseling, consultation services, wellbeing resources, and pet insurance through MetLife are offered.
Fertility and family building benefits are provided through a partnership with Kindbody.
Production Technician responsibilities include setting up, operating, maintaining, and troubleshooting manufacturing equipment.
Employees must move, pack, and sort raw materials or finished goods.
Workers monitor output quality to identify, discard, or re-manufacture faulty products.
Maintaining accurate daily production records is required.
The role requires heavy lifting of up to 70 lbs occasionally from heights of 3 to 5 feet.
The position involves medium physical effort, climbing stairs, and considerable standing.
Preferred qualifications include 2 years of employment stability and 1 year of manufacturing experience.
A High School diploma or GED is required prior to start.
Bridgestone Americas, Inc. is headquartered in Nashville, Tennessee.
BSAM operates alongside Bridgestone Europe, Middle East and Africa (BSEMEA) as the 'Bridgestone West' strategic region.
Both BSAM and BSEMEA are subsidiaries of Japan-headquartered Bridgestone Corporation.
The company develops, manufactures, and markets Bridgestone, Firestone, and associate brand products for broad customer needs.
Bridgestone is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer considering all individuals regardless of protected characteristics.
Form I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification must be completed at the start of employment.

facts: State officials will channel a $450,000 "building reuse" grant to subsidize the renovation of Bridgestone Bandag's facility on the west side of Oxford.
The N.C. Department of Commerce grant followed Bridgestone Bandag's pledge to invest $1.6 million in the renovation of its West Industry Drive facility and create 61 new jobs.
Bridgestone Bandag is a subsidiary of Bridgestone Corp., the Japanese tire and auto-parts maker.
Bandag specializes in retreading tires on a mass scale for the trucking industry.
The company is at least the third to receive state subsidies for relocation, expansion, or renovation in the West Industry Drive area in recent months.
The N.C. Department of Commerce pledged $5.7 million about a year ago for the development of a packing and cold-storage facility by Meel Corp.
In April, the Department of Commerce said it would help underwrite an expansion of the CertainTeed shingle plant.
Bandag's project qualified for aid because Granville County is classified by state officials as a "Tier 2" county.
Tier 1 counties are the 40 worst-off economically in North Carolina, while Tier 2 counties are the next 40.
Tier 3 counties comprise the remaining 20 and are mostly urban areas like Wake and Durham counties.
Granville County officials stated the building-reuse grant aims to bolster projects in Tier 1 and 2 counties.

Gov. Roy Cooper stated that "investments in rural North Carolina have a positive impact on our state's overall success," and that building-reuse grants help communities like Granville County create good jobs, improve residents' quality of life, and boost the local economy.
source_title: State chips in for Bridgestone-Bandag renovation

facts: Bridgestone officially merged its North American truck tire and retreading operations earlier this month.
Bridgestone publicly launched the new Bridgestone Bandag Tire Solutions (BBTS) business unit for 1,000 dealers and distributors gathered in New York for the truck tire group’s annual BizCon meeting.
Saul Solomon was introduced as the president of the newest Bridgestone Corp. business unit.
The new business unit has moved its headquarters to the former Bandag offices in Muscatine, Iowa.
Bridgestone purchased the privately held retread franchising business in mid-2007.
The goal of the new operating unit is to bring fleets tires, retreads and solutions into one integrated package.
Solomon stated the new operating unit has been structured “to be easy to do business with,” while acknowledging that “no one size fits all customers.”
Sales and marketing for the merged Bridgestone truck tire and retread business will remain in Bridgestone’s North American headquarters in Nashville.
Singh Alhuwalia, a Bridgestone veteran, now serves as president, sales & marketing for BBTS.
The company's brands – Bridgestone, Firestone, Dayton and Bandag – will continue to go to market under their current mix of channels.
Distribution channels include fleet sales, independent dealers, truckstops, and franchises for the Bandag retread line.
Bridgestone will remain the premium truck tire brand, with a support infrastructure for fleets to match that market position.
Firestone will continue to offer a less costly option.
The Dayton brand will compete on price with low-cost imported truck tires.
Bandag will retain its franchise structure.
source_title: Bridgestone launches new truck-tire business unit | FleetOwner

facts: Bridgestone and Bandag invested $400,000 in their retreading plant in Campana.
The investment aims to optimize the country's tire retreading service.
Funds were allocated to purchase state-of-the-art machinery.
This announcement is part of a $5 million investment plan announced by Bridgestone and Bandag in 2021.
The project will be implemented over the next five years.
Both companies aim for sustained growth in the tire retreading business with innovation as the core focus.
They offer a complete tire lifecycle and guarantee customer safety.
Bandag is one of Bridgestone's sustainable mobility solutions in the country.
The service maximizes tire lifespan responsibly, using 66% less petroleum, reducing CO2 emissions by 24%, and using 19% less water than manufacturing a new tire.
Bandag retread treads feature innovative designs adaptable to various terrains, offering the same performance as new tires while decreasing rolling resistance.
A main benefit is greater fuel efficiency and reduced total cost per kilometer for transport fleets.

facts: On October 9, 2019, Bert Brantley, Chief Operating Officer of the State of Georgia Department of Economic Development, led a high-ranking delegation to Carcoustics’ headquarters in Leverkusen, Germany.
The visit sought to recognize Carcoustics’ earlier announcement that it would make an investment in the single-digit million range and create 200 new jobs in Buford.
The visit also aimed to engage in a productive exchange with company executives to establish a close-knit network on the ground.
Today, 200 employees staff the facility in Buford in Gwinnett County, Georgia, with more positions expected to be filled in the near future.
Carcoustics provides acoustic and thermodynamic solutions for vehicles.
Experts from Carcoustics TechConsult, an in-house development center, demonstrated core competencies using various test setups during the visit.
The delegation toured Carcoustics’ production lines where advanced manufacturing processes were showcased.
Carcoustics is a medium-sized automotive supplier headquartered in Leverkusen, Germany, on an international course for growth.
Since 2017, the company has opened five new locations: Nováky (Slovakia), Wroclaw (Poland), Shenyang (China), Querétaro (Mexico), and Buford, Georgia (USA).
Carcoustics is already present in Howell, Michigan, and maintains a sales location in Troy, Michigan.
The Buford location allows the company to better serve the Greater Southeast region.
Lars Johannsen, Business Development Manager at Carcoustics, stated that proximity to customers in the south of the USA ensures optimum networking and avoids long transport routes.
Production for customers such as BMW and Daimler has already started successfully.
At the moment, the company faces challenges in the search for a suitable logistics partner and in human resources.
Dorie Phillips, Human Resources Manager for Carcoustics Buford, stated that in the wake of the general shortage of skilled workers, they are faced with the challenge of finding qualified employees.
Phillips noted the need to promote trade schools with the same passion as colleges to secure more talent for manufacturing positions.

facts: Club Car officially opened a new manufacturing facility in Appling, Ga., with a ribbon-cutting ceremony attended by elected officials and area business leaders.
Club Car CEO Mark Wagner and Georgia Governor Brian Kemp cut the ceremonial ribbon at the facility opening.
The new facility contains 105,000 square feet of flexible production and warehouse space.
The facility was opened to produce the fully electric, street-legal Club Car Urban for the United States market.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated that Club Car is bringing jobs into Georgia in the growing e-mobility sector and leading the way in compact utility EV manufacturing.
The production line can produce up to 20 vehicles per day.
There is currently one production line with 13 stations and room to add two more lines if needed.
The facility was opened to address expected increased demand in the U.S. for the Club Car Urban.
Club Car has committed nearly $8 million in capital investments into the facility over two years.
CEO Mark Wagner stated that their innovative vehicles are impacting commercial fleet electrification, mini-mobility, and last-mile delivery.
Club Car has been a Georgia-based company for more than sixty years.
The Club Car Urban is an electric, street-legal vehicle designed to navigate large campuses, last-mile delivery routes, municipalities, and hospitality spaces.
Prior to this facility's opening, customers had to order the Club Car Urban and wait for it to be built and shipped overseas from Poland.
Club Car was acquired by Platinum Equity from Ingersoll Rand in 2021.

facts: Club Car is a Georgia-based golf cart manufacturer.
Club Car has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Garia A/S, which has produced luxury golf and leisure vehicles since 2005.
The acquisition deal is expected to be finalized by the end of June.

In 2015, Garia launched its Garia Utility line of vehicles designed for work environments such as facility management and last-mile delivery.
Last November, Garia acquired Polish lightweight utility vehicle manufacturer Melex.
Both companies' utility products are mostly models that Club Car doesn't currently manufacture, according to a media release.
Club Car President Mark Wagner stated that the product lines complement each other nicely with very little overlap.

Mark Wagner called the Garia deal 'great news for our dealers and customers in Europe and the United States who will have access to a wider range of zero-emission, on and off-road vehicles at a greater variety of price points.'
Garia is a subsidiary of the Lars Larsen Group, which owns companies in the golf car, furniture, hotel, and restaurant sectors.

facts: Club Car has set the standard in golf carts, utility vehicles, and personal transportation for more than 60 years.

Every detail from design and fabrication to assembly reflects a focus on durability, comfort, and performance.

The plants serve both the original equipment and replacement channels.
The plants employ nearly 6,000 people.

facts: Continental AG's decision to spin off its Automotive division is set to culminate in a public listing on September 18, 2025.
The automotive industry shift is driven by electrification, software-defined vehicles, and supply chain reconfigurations.
In Q2 2025, Continental's Automotive division demonstrated an adjusted EBIT margin of 9.0%, far exceeding the full-year guidance of 2.5–4.0%.
Excluding the temporary boost from IFRS 5 accounting standards for spin-off assets, the Q2 2025 margin yielded a 4.0% margin, up from 2.9% in Q2 2024.
Margin expansion is a direct result of aggressive cost-reduction initiatives and strategic price adjustments.
The division is streamlining operations and focusing on high-margin technologies to shift from volume-driven growth to a model prioritizing profitability and operational efficiency.
The division's Q2 order intake was €5.7 billion, a 21% increase from sales of €4.7 billion in the same period.
Products such as satellite cameras, brake systems, and electronic control units (ECUs) accounted for over €3 billion in combined orders.
Demand reflects alignment with long-term industry trends, including autonomous driving and vehicle electrification.
A semiconductor joint venture was launched with Global Foundries to reduce reliance on external suppliers.
The JV accelerates the division's transition into a semiconductor solutions provider, a sector projected to grow at a 15% CAGR through 2030.
This vertical integration strategy enhances margins and future-proofs the division against supply chain disruptions.
In Q2 2025, the Continental Group allocated €654 million to R&D, representing 6.8% of sales.
R&D focus includes AI-driven vehicle electronics and the recently launched second-generation smart tachograph for trucks.
The smart tachograph is a direct response to EU regulatory updates, ensuring compliance while embedding Continental's software into critical vehicle systems.
The spin-off creates a standalone entity with a clear strategic identity as a technology-driven supplier focused on high-margin, software-centric solutions.
By separating from the industrial rubber business, the Automotive division can allocate capital more efficiently and pursue M&A opportunities tailored to its core competencies.
Continental's Automotive division is trading at a discount to its EV/EBITDA multiple compared to peers like Bosch and Aptiv, despite outperforming in key metrics.
source_title: Continental's Spin-Off of Automotive Division: A Strategic Catalyst for Long-Term Value Creation

facts: SK On has appointed an automotive sales expert from Continental as its new head of European operations, overseeing all sales activities in the region.

According to industry sources on September 17, SK On appointed Thomas Eller, a former Vice President at the German automotive parts company Continental, as its European representative earlier this month.
Thomas Eller is a sales expert in the automotive industry who worked at Continental for about 20 years from 2005 until May of this year, holding roles such as Global Head of Sales.
Prior to Continental, Thomas Eller worked at Motorola Solutions for approximately five years.

Based in Germany, where major automakers such as BMW and Mercedes-Benz are concentrated, he is expected to oversee local sales, expand new orders in the European market, and manage client relationships.
SK On aims to build a "customized cooperation system for local clients" and focus on expanding demand in the European market through the new European representative position.
The new structure minimizes time differences and physical distance compared to the previous approach where headquarters handled all client interactions, enabling faster and more efficient responses.
SK On currently operates three plants in Komarom and Ivancsa, Hungary.
Increasing the operational rate of these Hungarian plants through new orders to enhance profitability is considered a key task for SK On.
In February, SK On established a North American representative position to strengthen its local sales capabilities.
Rob Schnell, a sales expert with 30 years of experience in the automotive industry and an engineering background, is serving as the North American representative.
Rob Schnell has collaborated with global automakers such as Ford, General Motors (GM), and Stellantis.
source_title: SK On Appoints Former Continental Automotive Sales Expert as Head of European Operations - The Asia Business Daily

facts: Engineers from Continental Romania are working in international teams to develop Continental’s new virtual driving school technology.
Continental targets the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to give vehicle systems human understanding.

The German automotive producer presented at the CES Asia event a computer vision platform that uses Artificial Intelligence (AI), neural networks and machine learning to improve advanced sensor technologies.
The company’s fifth generation of its multi-function camera, which will begin series production in 2020, will use neural networks alongside traditional computer vision processes.

Depending on the available hardware, these can be scaled and refined, and using intelligent algorithms they improve the scene understanding involving the classification of the intentions and gestures of pedestrians.

Karl Haupt, head of the Advanced Driver Assistance System business unit at Continental, said “AI plays a large role in taking over human tasks. With AI software, the vehicle is able to interpret even complex and unforeseeable traffic situations – it’s no longer about what’s in front of me but about what could be in front of me.”
Artificial Intelligence opens up new possibilities for the computer vision platform, such as detecting people and interpreting their intentions and gestures.
Robert Thiel, head of Machine Learning in the Advanced Driver Assistance Systems business unit, said “The car should be so intelligent that it understands both its driver and its surroundings.”

Continental relies on AI in its product development departments to perform extremely complex tasks such as pedestrian detection to learn the concrete parametrization of this design from huge quantities of data.
This requires the creation of a system that combines and parametrizes the data input i.e. the millions of pixels in a camera image used for pedestrian detection.
The second step involves enabling this system to search every combination of the parameters that solves the problem, according to a press release.
Romanian teams develop software, electronics for Continental’s new 48V eBike system.
source_title: Romanian engineers work on Continental’s virtual driving school technology | Romania Insider

facts: OSHA issued Continental Tire the Americas LLC citations for two repeated violations, 16 serious violations, and five other than serious violations after an investigation that began in November 2021.

Inspectors alleged deficient safety procedures for energy control, combustible dust preventative engineering, housekeeping, personal protective equipment, emergency egress routes, and hazard communication.
Continental Tire the Americas LLC faces $341,866 in proposed penalties from OSHA.

In two separate incidents on consecutive days in November 2021, three workers at the Southern Illinois tire plant suffered severe injuries associated with combustible dust, flammable gas, unguarded moving equipment, and failure to control unintentional equipment energization during servicing.
On Nov. 13, 2021, OSHA received an employer-reported referral after an employee suffered amputation of three fingers while unjamming a machine.
The day after the Nov. 13 incident, one employee suffered severe burns requiring hospitalization and another suffered a concussion from an industrial rubber-compound mixer explosion.
OSHA Area Director Aaron Priddy stated that Continental Tire must review company safety procedures and employee training to ensure workers are safe on the job.
Continental Tire the Americas LLC is based in Fort Mill, South Carolina.
The company manufactures passenger, light truck, and commercial tires at facilities in Barnesville, Georgia; Mount Vernon and Sumter, South Carolina.
Continental Tire the Americas LLC is a subsidiary of the German-based tire and automotive component manufacturer Continental AG.
source_title: OSHA cites Continental Tire after investigating injuries of three workers | Mount Vernon | thesouthern.com

facts: Continental announced a leadership change in its replacement tire business in the Americas on March 18, 2025.
Dr. Jochen Etzel, President and CEO of Continental Tire the Americas, LLC, is retiring effective May 1, 2025.
Tansu Işık will succeed Dr. Jochen Etzel as the new head of Continental's Replacement Tire sector for the Americas.
Tansu Işık will report directly to Christian Koetz, a member of Continental's board of directors and head of Continental Tires.
Jochen Etzel has been the cornerstone of Continental's tire business in the Americas since 2011.

Etzel played a key role in unifying Continental's various tire activities in the region and strengthening their competitive position, contributing significantly to the company's overall growth and success.
Etzel began his career as a lawyer in Continental's corporate law department, later served as a financial director overseeing tire business development outside Europe, and led Continental's M&A team.
Christian Koetz stated that Etzel's impact on Continental's tire business was immeasurable and thanked him for his leadership and contributions.

Tansu Işık brings over 20 years of international experience in Continental's tire business across various markets and functions, including marketing, supply chain, strategy, and key account management.
Işık started his career as a business development specialist in Istanbul, Turkey.
After holding various roles within the company, Işık joined Continental Tires headquarters in Hanover, Germany, as pricing manager for the EMEA region.
Işık later moved to Shanghai, China, as director and then vice president of key account management (OE) for China and APAC.
From 2017, Işık worked as marketing manager for Continental's replacement tire business in EMEA.
He subsequently led the organization for strategic business development and global marketing at Continental Tires.
Currently, Işık is responsible for fleet and trailer offtake in the South region and channels for Continental's replacement tire business in EMEA.
In this role, he oversees commercial operations in several countries, leads Continental's truck tire activity in EMEA, and is responsible for retreading and digital solutions in the region.
Işık holds an Executive MBA from Koç University and training from INSEAD, Duke University, and Ashridge University.
Continental was founded in 1871 and generated sales of €39.7 billion in 2024, employing around 190,000 people in 55 countries.
The Tires group sector generated preliminary sales of €13.9 billion in fiscal 2024.
Continental's tire division employs more than 57,000 people worldwide and operates 20 production and 16 development sites globally.
Continental Tire the Americas is located in Fort Mill, South Carolina, United States.
Press contact for Continental Tires – The Americas is Allison Zora, Head of Brand and Public Relations, located at 1794 MacMillan Park Drive, Fort Mill, SC 29707.
source_title: Continental annonce un changement de direction dans son activité de pneus de remplacement dans les Amériques

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Daechang Seat Savannah Corp., a supplier specializing in automotive seat frames, will invest $72.5 million in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center, in close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), creating over 500 jobs.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant project alone reach at least nine counties and expressed excitement about adding Daechang Seat Savannah Corp. to the growing manufacturing base centered around Chatham County and the Savannah region.
Daechang Seat Corp. (DSC) was established in 1979 in Kyungju, South Korea, and has become one of the largest seat frame producers in the world.
Globally, DSC employs more than 5,000 people across seven countries.
The company’s production process includes fully automatic lines with arc welding and spot-welding machines.
Jinsuk Lee, CEO of Daechang Seat Savannah Corp., expressed appreciation to the State of Georgia, local communities, and the City of Savannah for their support.

Jinsuk Lee stated that DSC takes great pride in creating improved opportunities and employment prospects within Chatham County and the Savannah area, and is honored to maintain its partnership with the City of Savannah.
Jinsuk Lee noted that DSC’s primary objective is to drive growth through facility expansions to transform into a global entity.
Jinsuk Lee confirmed that DSC currently holds a prominent partnership with Hyundai Transys and Hyundai Mobis at HMGMA and hopes to collaborate with more projects in the future.
DSC’s new facility will be located at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The company will hire for assembly operators, equipment technicians, mechanical engineers, and electrical engineers through local job fairs and website postings.
Griff Lynch, Georgia Ports Authority President and CEO, noted that DSC is located only minutes from the Port of Savannah to take advantage of Georgia’s world-class connectivity.
Griff Lynch stated that the Georgia Ports Authority is focused on providing the best global gateway for U.S. supply chains and welcomed DSC as the latest job creator in Georgia’s automaking sector.

Trip Tollison, Savannah Economic Development Authority President and CEO, announced that Daechang Seat Savannah Corp. will be the third Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America supplier to locate in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center, bringing 500 new jobs and $72.5 million in capital investment to Chatham County.
Trip Tollison described Daechang Seat Savannah Corp. as a well-established global company and expressed excitement for them to grow and become part of the community.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Savannah Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that countless partners are helping transform Georgia into a center of the automotive and mobility industry and welcomed DSC to Savannah.
Pat Wilson highlighted that Savannah is an established global gateway and Chatham County has leveraged it to create a bustling community for living, working, and enjoying.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Daechang Seat Co. (DSC) has recently ventured into the electric vehicle sector and continues to expand its scope beyond automotive seat frames.
DSC anticipates greater opportunities for growth with the completion of new manufacturing facilities, which will provide larger installation spaces.
source_title: Automotive supplier Daechang Seat Savannah Corp. to create 500 jobs in Chatham County - SEDA

facts: More than 500 new jobs are coming soon to Chatham County.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced that Daechang Seat Corp. USA, a supplier specializing in automotive seat frames, will invest $72.5 million in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
Globally, DSC employs more than 5,000 people in seven countries.
DSC’s facility will be located at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The company will be hiring for assembly operators, equipment technicians, mechanical engineers, and electrical engineers through local job fairs and website postings.
Gov. Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant project reach at least nine counties.
Gov. Kemp noted that this announcement is a reminder of the impact projects like the Hyundai Metaplant have beyond their initial investment.
The company will be adding to Georgia's growing manufacturing base centered around Chatham County and the Savannah region.
source_title: Georgia Gov. Kemp announces more than 500 new jobs coming to area - WJCL

facts: Daehan Solution is an unfunded company based in Incheon, South Korea, founded in 1993.
The company operates as a manufacturer and supplier of auto components.

Daehan Solution manufactures engine covers, hood insulators, dash inner & outer panels, insulation tunnels, headliners, floor carpets, truck sides, luggage boards & boxes, undercover panels, fender insulators, rear package trays, trunk mats, and trunk rid for automobiles.
The company has 6,232 active competitors, including 51 funded and 696 that have exited.
Daehan Solution's top competitors include Uno Minda, Motherson Group, and Valeo.
As of April 30, 2026, the company has an employee count of 34.
Daehan Solution is associated with a legal entity named DAEHAN SOLUTION GEORGIA (CIN: 262735267) located in the United States, which is active.
DAEHAN SOLUTION GEORGIA was incorporated on December 31, 1999.
As of December 31, 2016, DAEHAN SOLUTION GEORGIA had an employee count of 284.
In November 2017, Alston completed a project for Daehan Solution.
Daehan Solution has not raised any funding rounds to date.
Daehan Solution has made no investments or acquisitions to date.
source_title: Daehan Solution - 2026 Company Profile & Competitors - Tracxn

facts: Daesol Ausys Georgia will establish a new manufacturing facility in West Point, Georgia.
The facility will create more than 140 new jobs.
The project involves $72 million in investment in Harris County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right and is excited that Daesol Ausys will join other innovators in Harris County.
Georgia’s automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians.
The state’s automotive industry will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state for future jobs.
Daesol Ausys was established in Korea in 2017.
The company specializes in designing and manufacturing motor and electric vehicle interior components and accessories.
It is a key supplier for Kia Georgia, Hyundai Motor Group, and General Motors.
Min Ho Kwon, CEO of Daesol Ausys, stated the company is committed to delivering world-class services that exceed clients’ requirements and expectations.
Min Ho Kwon expressed excitement about the project in the Northwest Harris Business Park and looking forward to continuing the partnership with the State of Georgia and Harris County.
Daesol Ausys’ new facility will produce luggage boards and luggage covers.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in December 2024.
The company will be hiring for positions in production, purchasing, quality control, maintenance, human resources, and management.
Robert Grant, Chairman of the Harris County Commission, stated that Daesol Ausys’ decision to locate in the Northwest Harris Business Park represents a commitment to Harris County.
West Point and Harris County have a wonderful relationship with Daesol Ausys’ sister companies Daehan Solutions and Daesol Materials.
Officials have been working closely with company officials to get this new venture in West Point.

Craig Greenhaw, Chairman of the Harris County Development Authority, extended a welcome to Daesol Ausys and looked forward to a long and mutually beneficial partnership with the newest corporate citizen.
Craig Greenhaw noted that West Point and Harris County continue to work well together, calling this another successful venture bringing jobs and capital investment into the region.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was executed in partnership with the Harris County Commission, Harris County Development Authority, City of West Point, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that economic development is a team sport driven by the local level and congratulated Harris County and community partners for helping bring Daesol Ausys to West Georgia.
Pat Wilson noted that the automotive industry is providing generational change in the region and across the state, and expressed excitement for the opportunities Daesol Ausys will provide.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain.
Georgia has created more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Daesol Ausys Georgia is a leading manufacturer of automotive components and a key supplier of luggage boards and luggage covers.
The company is committed to exceeding customer expectations with award-winning products and services through innovation and efficiency.
The company has a long history as one of the top suppliers to Kia and Hyundai worldwide.

facts: CJ Foodville Corporation announced it will invest more than $47 million to build a new bakery and food processing facility in Gainesville.
CJ Foodville is a subsidiary of CJ Group and one of the largest food service and food processing companies in South Korea.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced the investment on September 5, 2023.
The project will create an estimated 285 jobs in Hall County.
CJ Foodville made its first overseas expansion and entered the U.S. market in 2004 with its TOUS les JOURS bakery café brand.
The TOUS les JOURS brand currently operates four storefronts in Georgia.
The new facility will be located at the Gainesville Business Park northeast of Atlanta.
The facility is expected to become operational in 2025, according to the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
Once operational, the facility is expected to produce more than 19,000 tons of products per year at full capacity.
Tony Hunsoo Ahn, CEO of CJ Foodville USA, stated the decision was based on strong profitability to enhance production and supply capabilities for the expanding TOUS les JOURS business.
Tony Hunsoo Ahn stated the company is committed to promoting the unique competitiveness of the K-Bakery, which is built upon excellent product quality in a diverse range of bread and cakes.
Tony Hunsoo Ahn stated their ultimate goal is to establish the TOUS les JOURS brand as a globally cherished bakery brand that transcends borders and captivates palates worldwide.
South Korea was one of the top three international sources of job creation in Georgia during Fiscal Year 2023.
In August 2023, Duckshin Housing announced it would invest more than $15 million to establish its first U.S. manufacturing presence in Athens.
In August 2023, Daesol Ausy announced it will invest $72 million to establish a new manufacturing facility in Harris County.
Half of the United States’ top 100 food processing companies operate in Georgia, according to GDEcD.
New and expanding food processing company locations ranked among the greatest number of new industrial projects during fiscal year 2022.
These food processing investments resulted in more than $923 million in investment in fiscal year 2023, according to GDEcD.

facts: A South Korean automaker plans to build a $72 million plant in Georgia to make parts for electric vehicles.
This will be the third floor located in the same West Point commercial park for members of the company’s family group.
Daehan Solution Co. opened a million-euro factory in 2008 to manufacture roof cladding and other sound insulation parts for the area.
Daesol Material opened a $35 million factory there in 2017 to manufacture fiber upholstery for automobiles.
Daehan Solution now employs 350 full-time workers, while Daesol Material employs 50 workers.
Meghan Richardson serves as an economic developer for the city of West Point.
Daehan Solution also has a plant near Montgomery, Alabama, which manufactures Hyundai’s assembly plant.

The new facility is part of a wave of electric vehicle suppliers that announced factories in Georgia after Hyundai announced in 2022 that it would build a $5.5 billion plant to gather electric vehicles and batteries in Ellabell, Georgia, near Savannah.
The Hyundai plant in Ellabell is expected to start generating cars in 2025.
Suppliers have pledged to invest about $2.2 billion and hire 5,000 people.

Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp stated that the state’s auto industry employs more than 50,000 Georgian workers and will continue to grow as providers of electric mobility in each and every corner of the state for the jobs of the future.
Daesol Ausys is expected to start production in December 2024.
The company will pay between $15 and $20 an hour.
The state will pay for the exercise of personnel.
The state donated $500,000 last year to Harris County to clean up and level the site and build a road, a budget the county matched through sales tax collection.
The city of West Point and the county are offering 8 years of asset tax exemptions, worth an estimated $5.7 million.
Daesol Ausys could earn benefits of $875,000 in income tax credits, at a rate of $1,250 per job over five years, as long as employees earn at least $31,300 per year.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that DAS Corp., an automotive parts company, will establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter.
The facility will create 300 new jobs.
The facility will generate more than $35 million in investment in Candler County.
The initiative will involve collaborative indirect investments.

Suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant resulted in over $2 billion in investment last fiscal year alone, helping Georgia achieve a third straight year of record-breaking economic growth (Governor Brian Kemp).
Georgia's logistics assets include a reliable network of rail lines and highways that connect companies to key business partners statewide and globally (Governor Brian Kemp).
DAS was established in Korea in 1987.
DAS specializes in designing and manufacturing seating systems, safety seating components, and seating structures.
DAS is a key supplier for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) and Hyundai Motor Group.
The strategic location near prominent cities like Savannah and Macon facilitated convenient access and played a pivotal role in the decision-making process (Sean Kim, Chief Operating Officer at DAS).
Metter was deemed an ideal location in anticipation of its role as a global electric vehicle production hub due to its close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (Sean Kim).
The new DAS manufacturing facility is located in the Candler County Industrial Park in Metter.
The facility will primarily produce automotive seat structures.
Operations are projected to begin in the latter half of 2024.
Over the course of the next five years, DAS will be recruiting for assembly technicians.
DAS intends to establish collaborations with local employment services as part of its hiring process.

The City of Metter expects a transformative impact on the community through stimulating investment in housing, creating other business opportunities, and improving socioeconomic wellbeing (Metter Mayor Ed Boyd).
Candler County Chairman Glyn Thrift thanked DAS for choosing the county, expecting it to provide a boost to spur more economic development.
Georgia Ports Authority supports the growing automotive manufacturing presence in the state to drive economic activity and jobs (Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority).
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the GDEcD Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was executed in partnership with the Candler County Industrial Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Electric Cities of Georgia, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia is well-positioned for the arrival of Hyundai’s supplier network due to years of consistent investment in site preparation, workforce solutions, and connectivity.
DAS is another addition to Georgia’s automotive community (Pat Wilson).

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
DAS Corp. maintains a strong focus on innovation and quality to consistently provide premier automotive seating solutions tailored to evolving customer needs.
Strategic collaborations with Hyundai Motor Group and Kia Motors continue to drive progress within the constantly evolving automotive industry.
DAS Corp. garnered total revenue of approximately $900 million USD during fiscal year 2022.
source_title: Hyundai supplier DAS Corp. to Bring 300 Automotive Jobs to Metter  - Grice Connect

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development was honored for its contributions towards strong US-South Korea ties at The Korea Society’s 2023 Annual Dinner at the Plaza Hotel in New York City this September.

The department received the Van Fleet Award, previously given to heads of state such as former US President George W. Bush and former ROK President Kim Dae Jung for outstanding contributions to US-Korea relations.
Korea International Trade Association (KITA) was also recognized as an awardee at the event.

Korea Society President and CEO Thomas Byrne stated that both awardees play an integral part in enhancing trade and investment between the two countries, creating high-paying jobs in American communities and securing supply chains in critical technologies.
Georgia is one of the top destinations for Korean foreign direct investment, with $19.5 billion in greenfield investment in the state since 2003.
Many of the largest Korean investments in Georgia are related to automotives and EV batteries.

A Hyundai Motor Group-LG Energy Solutions joint venture in Bryan County will bring a total of $7.5 billion in investment into a battery facility and a new EV factory known as the ‘Metaplant,’ along with over 8,500 jobs in the next eight years.
Hyundai is partnering with SK on a joint venture to develop an EV battery plant in Georgia’s Bartow County with a $4-5 billion investment and an expected 3,500 new jobs.
Automotive supplier Daesol Ausys Georgia will build a new facility for manufacturing motor and EV components in Harris County, bringing a $72 million investment.
Automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea will establish a facility in Henry County with a projected $72 million in investment.
DAS Corp, a supplier for the Hyundai Metaplant, will invest $35 million in Candler County to establish a manufacturing facility.
These smaller Korean investments were all announced in 2023.
In September of 2023, Hyundai signed a memorandum of understanding with Georgia Tech to celebrate their collaborative partnership focused on sustainable development.
CJ Foodville, a division of CJ group, announced a $47 million investment in a baked goods factory in Hall County to support its bakery chain ‘TOUS les JOURs’ growing presence in the US.
The Korean American population in Georgia has continued to grow to about 73,000 according to US Census data as of 2021.
In Fall 2023, 1,910 South Korean students are projected to enroll in universities in Georgia.
There are 44 programs that US students from Georgia universities can choose from to study abroad in South Korea.

Jae Kim, President of the Southeast US Korean Chamber of Commerce and Senior Manager of Aprio, noted that a 'Pali Pali' (quick, quick) corporate culture from Koreans is making it into the American workforce when working together with the US.

Jae Kim added that beyond historically large monetary amounts coming into the US from Korea, there is a next level wave of exchanges including joint ventures, joint research and development, joint workforce development, and joint culture impacting both small and large businesses due to recent investments.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated in his keynote speech that the Georgia-Korea partnership foundations were laid nearly 40 years ago when Georgia first established an economic office in Korea.

Governor Kemp noted the partnership blossomed when Kia chose Georgia for its first US manufacturing plant in 2006 and will continue to grow over the next 10, 20, and 30 years as new opportunities bring further transformation.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development’s receipt of the Van Fleet Award serves as a testament to economic, cultural, and educational US-South Korea ties and shared prosperity to come.
source_title: Georgia- South Korea Investment Ties Celebrated at Korea Society Gala in NYC – Asia Matters for America

facts: Home Meridian International is investing $23.5 million in a Georgia expansion.
Governor Brian P. Kemp welcomed the company to Georgia and confirmed it will build a new distribution center in Liberty County.
The selected site for the expansion is Tradeport East Business Center, which holds a GRAD (Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development) rating.
The GRAD certification program is administered by the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) to help network planners and landowners catalyze economic expansion.
According to the GDEcD, more than 8,000 jobs have been created at GRAD sites.
Liberty County Development Authority President Allen Brown stated that HMI selected Tradeport East Business Center as the most productive location on the Georgia coast for its long-term growth.
In September, the Port of Savannah handled the largest shipment ever made on the east coast of the United States.
The Georgia Port Authority announced that August was its most productive month and that it was granted the prestige of being the country’s main port for U.S. exports.

Griff Lynch, Executive Director of the Georgia Port Authority, stated that Georgia’s unmatched connectivity via roads and railroads, combined with Savannah’s global port network, makes it ideal for logistics operations like IHM.

Hank Evans, Director of GDEcD’s Global Trade Division for Logistics, Energy, Agribusiness and Food Projects, collaborated with the Liberty County Development Authority, Georgia Port Authority, and Georgia Power on the project.

GDEcD commissioner Pat Wilson stated that HMI’s decision to expand in Georgia is supported by the state's logistics network, including its ports, road infrastructure, rail formula, and the world’s busiest airport.

SK Enmove offers ZIC lubricants, providing superior lubricant fluid solutions with premium synthetic engine oils.

SK Enmove proactively addresses global changes driven by climate and strives to advance sustainable business practices.

facts: The State of Georgia is producing record-breaking economic development and trade numbers in recent years despite a global pandemic that has stifled growth in other locations.

In April, Governor Brian Kemp announced that job creation and investments resulting from economic development projects in the state during the first nine months of the fiscal year had already surpassed fiscal year 2021 year-end totals.
In January and February 2022 alone, Georgia’s total trade with the world was $28.8 billion.
Georgia exports topped $6.9 billion in the first two months of 2022 and saw an increase of 9 percent compared to the same period in 2021.
According to Business Roundtable’s 2022 fact sheet, international trade supported 1,328,100 Georgia jobs, which was more than one in five jobs in 2019.
The study found that trade-related jobs grew four times faster than total employment from 1992 to 2019.
Prior to the pandemic, Georgia exported to 212 countries and territories, with Canada, Mexico and Germany ranking among the top markets for Georgia-made goods.
In 2019, 40 percent of Georgia’s goods exports went to Free Trade Agreement (FTA) partners, and Georgia exports to FTA partners increased by 42 percent between 2010 and 2019.
Canada is the largest export market for the state, and exports from Georgia to Canada have grown 37 percent in the last 20 years.
The state’s commercial partnership with Canada is reciprocal, as Canada sells more to Georgia than it does to all of Mexico.
GDEcD’s Canada team helps 100 Georgia small businesses a year export to Canada on average.

The Canada Office offers services including providing market reports, identifying potential partners or end users, and connecting potential exporters with business prospects interested in Georgia products and services.
Ludovic Ortuno is the Managing Director of Georgia’s Canada Office.
Pain Care Labs, an industry leader in noninvasive pain relief established in 2006, has been working with the GDEcD Trade team since 2009.
In 2016, Pain Care Labs connected with the Canada Office to plan its market entry strategy.

Over the last five years prior to the article's publication, the Canada team provided reports regarding medical device registration, established contacts with Health Canada authorities, and provided research to help Pain Care Labs understand classification rules and requirements to receive medical device certification.
After receiving its Medical Device License in Fall 2021, Pain Care Labs has exported its pain relief devices to hospitals, clinicians and consumers across its target market.
Fischer Connectors, a Swiss multinational corporation with North American headquarters in Georgia, first connected with the GDEcD Trade team and Canada Office in 2020.

GDEcD’s team helped Fischer Connectors better understand their industry ecosystem in Canada through an initial market assessment that included product opportunities, competition and potential barriers.
GDEcD’s curated list of target prospects and introductory meetings led to new sales for Fischer Connectors in the Canadian market.

The Southeast U.S.- Canadian Provinces (SEUS-CP) Alliance was established in 2007 as part of a shared commitment to promoting trade and commercial opportunities between Canada and the Southern states.
SEUS-CP hosts an annual conference that brings together economic development, business and innovation leaders from Alliance members for a robust B2B matchmaking program.

The Alliance includes Alabama, Georgia, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Tennessee from the U.S., as well as New Brunswick, Newfoundland & Labrador, Nova Scotia, Ontario, Prince Edward Island and Quebec from Canada.
The 2022 SEUS-CP Conference was scheduled to be held at the Savannah Convention Center from June 19-21.
Georgia has been represented in Canada since 1981.
GDEcD’s main office is located in Montréal, Québec.
In 2021, GDEcD estimates that total trade between Georgia and Canada was $11.16 billion, a 16% increase from the prior year.
Georgia exports to Canada increased by 17 percent compared to 2020, accounting for a total of $6.18 billion.
Top exports to Canada include motor vehicles, tractors, bulldozers, carpets, and civilian aircraft.
Automotive is a key industry in Georgia-Canada trade, and representatives from Canada have been outspoken in support for this major Georgia industry and the growing electric vehicle ecosystem.
Georgia leads the nation in the export of carpets, poultry and peanut products to Canada.

GDEcD’s nationally recognized International Trade team works in all industry sectors statewide and provides unbiased, solutions-focused trade consultations to Georgia companies seeking to expand their global presence.

facts: Denkai America and Home Chef are both adding new locations soon in Right to Work Georgia.
Altogether, these two companies will create 950 new jobs.
Denkai America is investing $430 million in order to locate headquarters in Right to Work Richmond County, Georgia.
Denkai America's investment will also create 250 new jobs.
Georgia officials state that selecting Denkai America strengthens the state's status as a leader in emerging electric mobility and existing aerospace, defense, and technology industries.
State officials emphasize they have been laser focused on bringing quality jobs to highly-trained, hardworking Georgians across the state.
Denkai America is expected to thrive in Georgia's top-ranking pro-business environment.

The State of Georgia is committed to harnessing widespread electric mobility adoption to ensure Georgians in today's automotive industry have access to future EV jobs at home, citing Denkai America's vital role in the EV ecosystem.
The Development Authority of Augusta, Georgia, welcomes Denkai America and its North American Headquarters to the Augusta Corporate Park.
Denkai America will bring high-skilled jobs to the growing list of companies that call Augusta and the Augusta Corporate Park home.
The Augusta Commission and Augusta Utilities Department provided support and hard work to make Denkai America's development happen.
Home Chef is creating 700 new jobs as a result of investing in a new location in Right to Work Douglasville, Georgia.
Douglas County officials express an honor in welcoming Home Chef and are committed to recruiting great companies and partnering with them to thrive in the community.
Home Chef's new production operations will be its first facility to incorporate a full service kitchen for cooking and batch production of ingredients for its meals.
The Home Chef facility will feature a 6,000 square-foot space handling the cooking of vegetables, starches, and grains, as well as in-house sauce production.
source_title: What Do Denkai America and Home Chef Have in Common? - NRTWC

facts: Denkai America is the only North American manufacturer of high-quality electrodeposited (ED) copper foil used in printed electronics.
Denkai America will locate its new manufacturing facility and North American Headquarters in Richmond County.
The company’s initial investment will be $150 million to establish an ED copper foil production facility.
The facility will focus on electric vehicle (EV) battery foil.
The project is located on a 115-acre site.
Denkai America has a goal to invest a total of $430 million over the next five years.
The project aims to create 250 new jobs over the next five years.

Governor Kemp stated that Georgia is proud to be selected as the home of Denkai America, further strengthening the state’s status as a leader in the emerging electric mobility and existing aerospace, defense, and technology industries.
Governor Kemp noted the state has been laser focused on bringing quality jobs to highly-trained Georgians across the state.
Governor Kemp expressed looking forward to seeing one-of-a-kind companies like Denkai thrive in Georgia’s top-ranking pro-business environment.
Denkai America’s materials have traditionally been used in printed circuit board (PCB) applications, primarily for the aerospace and defense industries.
The materials are now also used in lithium-ion batteries powering electric vehicles.
Denkai has been operating for more than 60 years.
Denkai has team members worldwide with operations in Japan and North America.
Heday Nakajima, Ph.D., Denkai Group President and CEO, stated the investment highlights their commitment to leading ED battery foil manufacturing in North America for many years to come.
Heday Nakajima expressed gratitude to the teams in Richmond County and the State of Georgia for making the project possible.
Nobu Masuda, Denkai America’s President and CEO, stated they are thrilled to expand their manufacturing presence into Augusta.
Nobu Masuda noted Augusta has the skilled and talented workforce needed for company growth.
Construction is expected to begin in September 2022 as the first part of a three-phase investment.
The facility will be fully operational by December 2024.
The company will be hiring for skilled industrial technicians, production managers, and engineers.
Steven Kendrick, Chairman of the Augusta Economic Development Authority, stated the authority is excited to welcome Denkai America and its North American Headquarters to the Augusta Corporate Park.
Steven Kendrick noted they will bring high-skilled jobs to the growing list of companies in Augusta and the Augusta Corporate Park.
Steven Kendrick thanked the Augusta Commission and the Augusta Utilities Department for their support and hard work.
Barton Lowrey, Global Commerce Director of Corporate Solutions and Cyber Security at the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), represented GDEcD on this competitive project.
The project was in partnership with the Augusta Economic Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Georgia Quick Start is the Technical College System of Georgia’s nationally ranked workforce training program.
Georgia Quick Start has provided specialized training tailored specifically to business needs for more than 40 years.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that as electric mobility adoption becomes widespread, the State of Georgia is committed to harnessing this generational opportunity to ensure Georgians in the automotive industry have access to future jobs at home.
Pat Wilson noted Denkai America plays a vital role in the EV ecosystem.
Pat Wilson expressed excitement to welcome the innovative company to Augusta and Richmond County.

Pat Wilson stated the investment is further evidence of Georgia’s commitment to building lasting international relationships driving economic partnerships across the state, including recruitment of the wider electric mobility value chain.
Georgia’s prime location, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and business-friendly climate has made it attractive for diverse industries focused on a sustainable future.
Georgia is cultivating a vertically integrated supply chain to help companies increase efficiencies by reducing reliance on imported materials.
EV-related projects have contributed more than $12.6 billion in investments to Georgia since 2020.
EV-related projects have contributed more than 17,800 new jobs to Georgia since 2020.
Augusta Corporate Park is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified park.
To earn GRAD program certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific certification requirements prior to a visit by a prospective business.
Completing the GRAD certification program with the Georgia Department of Economic Development helps catalyze economic growth and industrial development for regions.
Denkai America is a leader in the manufacture of high-quality electrodeposited copper foils for printed circuit board (PCB), industrial, and energy storage applications.
Backed by a global presence, Denkai America delivers application-specific copper foils required by technology-leading companies.
Katie Byrd is the Director of Communications for the Office of Governor Brian P. Kemp.
Andrew Isenhour is the Deputy Director of Communications for the Office of Governor Brian P. Kemp.
Marie Gordon is the Director of Communications for the Georgia Department of Economic Development.

facts: The Japanese manufacturer of copper foil used in printed electronics will build its North American headquarters in Georgia, as announced by Gov. Brian Kemp.
Denkai America is making an initial investment of $150 million on a 115-acre site in Richmond County.
Ultimately, the company will spend $430 million on a headquarters and manufacturing plant.
The project will create 250 jobs over the next five years.
Denkai America's products have traditionally been used in printed circuit board applications primarily for the aerospace and defense industries.
The company's copper foil is now also used in lithium-ion batteries powering electric vehicles.

Gov. Brian Kemp stated, "Georgia is proud to be selected as the home of Denkai America, further strengthening our state's status as a leader in the emerging electric mobility and existing aerospace, defense, and technology industries."

Gov. Brian Kemp added, "We have been laser focused on bringing quality jobs to highly trained, hardworking Georgians across the state, and we look forward to seeing one-of-a-kind companies like Denkai thrive in Georgia's top-ranking pro-business environment."

Nobu Masuda, president and CEO of Denkai America, stated, "We are thrilled to expand our manufacturing presence into Augusta, which has the skilled and talented work force our company needs for growth."

The facility will be fully operational by December 2024.

The state Department of Economic Development worked with the Augusta Economic Development Authority, Georgia Power, and the Technical College System of Georgia's Quick Start program to land the project.
Denkai America will be hiring skilled industrial technicians, production managers, and engineers.

facts: Taiwanese industrial giant LCY Group has made a USD 9.5 million loan to DENKAI America to expand its presence in the US.
The deal was made by LCY's tech division LCY Technology.
LCY Technology develops copper foil products, which it sells to domestic and foreign printed circuit board (PCB) and copper clad laminate (CCL) manufacturers.
DENKAI Group specializes in the research, development and production of copper foil for high-end lithium-ion batteries, circuit boards and 5G substrates.
DENKAI has two production bases located in Japan and the US.
The two companies hope that the loan and licence agreement will help expand their respective reach and customer portfolios.
The partnership aims to enhance research and development in rigid and flexible boards.
