facts: DENKAI America specializes in the production of copper foils, operating within the industrial manufacturing sector.
The company's main offerings include copper foils used in printed circuit boards, industrial applications, and energy storage systems.
These copper foils are produced through an electroplating process on titanium drums, and surface treatments to meet requirements.
DENKAI America was founded in 1976 and is based in Camden, South Carolina.
DENKAI America's headquarters is located at 29 Battleship Road Extension, Camden, South Carolina, 29020, United States.
DENKAI America is currently at the Loan stage and is alive.
DENKAI America has raised a total of $9.5M, with its last raise occurring two years ago.

DENKAI America is included in an Energy Storage expert collection containing 5,442 items focused on companies developing and manufacturing energy storage solutions such as lithium-ion batteries and solid-state batteries.
DENKAI America is identified as one of the leading players operating in the electrodeposited copper foils industry.
Copper foils are a key material in the production of lithium-ion batteries, which are used in electric vehicles for energy storage.
The high energy density and durability required for EV batteries rely on copper foils, particularly for anodes, making them indispensable in the EV manufacturing process.
The global electrodeposited copper foils market was worth US$ 9.5 Bn in 2022 and is expected to reach US$ 22.2 Bn by the year 2031 at a CAGR of 9.9% between 2023 and 2031.
North America generated the largest market value for electrodeposited copper foils in 2023 and is expected to maintain its dominance during the forecast period.
Investors of DENKAI America include LCY Chemical Group.
Competitors of DENKAI America include SK Nexilis and 4 more.

facts: Augusta Mayor Garnett Johnson serves on the Georgia Department of Economic Development Board of Directors.
A German copper recycler announced an almost $1 billion investment in the community that will create hundreds of good-paying jobs.

In November 2021, Hamburg-based Aurubis announced it would invest $340 million in a recycling and copper smelting facility to be built on 150 acres in Richmond County’s Augusta Corporate Park and hire 125 employees.
Roughly six months after breaking ground last June, the company announced that Georgia’s largest-ever single German investment was going to get even bigger.
In December, Aurubis announced a Phase II bringing their total investment to $690 million and 250 jobs.
The 150 acres are graded and two buildings are in the air for the Aurubis facility.
The first building at the Aurubis site is expected to be completed in the third quarter.
The first smelt at the Aurubis facility will be sometime in 2024.
The Aurubis smelting facility will produce copper from recycled cable, e-waste and other metal-bearing material.
Denkai America announced in July it will build its North American headquarters in Richmond County, making an initial investment of $150 million on a 115-acre site.
Denkai is a Japanese copper-foil manufacturer that will be located in the industrial park.
Ultimately, Denkai will invest $400 million in their new North American headquarters and manufacturing plant and create 250 jobs.

In October, Belgian plastics and chemicals firm Solvay was announced to get a $178.2 million grant from the U.S. Department of Energy for construction of a production line for polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) at its Augusta site.
PVDF is a polymer used as a binder and coating for the separator in lithium-ion batteries, semiconductors and other applications.
Cal Wray, president of the Augusta Economic Development Authority, says they worked $35 billion worth of investment opportunities in the Augusta area last year.
In the five years since his staff arrived, the Augusta EDA has announced just over $3 billion worth of investment.
Existing companies and manufacturers continue to invest in Augusta and Richmond County, with almost all announcing new jobs, including E-Z-Go and Starbucks.
E-Z-Go, owned by Textron, is a leading global manufacturer of golf cars, utility vehicles and personal transportation vehicles founded in Augusta in 1954.
E-Z-Go is one of Augusta’s oldest corporate headquarters and employs 1,700 people.
The Augusta area has one of the top 20 most diverse economies in the country.
The Augusta area is also in the top 20 in concentration of engineers in the country.
About 24,000 people are working in advanced manufacturing in the Augusta region according to Augusta Technical College President Jermaine Whirl.
Augusta Technical College was awarded money from the governor and legislature to build an 81,000-square-foot advanced manufacturing and engineering technology training center.
The college was awarded $2.7 million for design, $35 million for construction, and roughly $6.9 million for furniture, fixtures and equipment for the new training center.
The new advanced manufacturing center is located off I-20 in Columbia County.

The facility will house engineering technology programs including architectural, computer/electrical, mechanical, nuclear, machine tool/CNC, chemical technology, industrial automation, mechatronics/robotics, metrology and quality management, hydraulics/pneumatics, additive manufacturing/3D printing and welding programs.
The training center has a three year build-out and is expecting to have over 1,300 new students.
The facility will nearly double the number of graduates from Augusta Tech’s School of Aviation, Industrial and Engineering Technology, which will graduate 680 students at its summer commencement.
The building will increase corporate training efforts for companies including John Deere, Club Car and Starbucks to train more incumbent workers.
Manufacturing jobs in the region pay anywhere from $55,000 all the way up to six figures after two to three years on the job.

A partnership between Augusta Technical College, the city of Augusta, First Tee of Augusta and Augusta National Golf Club will result in the purchase of the Augusta Municipal Golf Course (The Patch) on Jan. 1, 2025.
The golf course purchase initiative was announced during Masters Week in April.
Augusta Tech’s golf management program and turf-grass management program will be relocated to The Patch.
Augusta National Golf Club has committed to updating current facilities at The Patch, including installing new greens and renovating the clubhouse.

Augusta Advantage Program creates a streamlined pathway for students to transfer from Augusta Technical College to Augusta University, offering accelerated admissions into eight pathways: business administration, communication, computer science, kinesiology, nursing, political science, psychology, and social work.
These transfer pathways were chosen based on significant workforce needs here and beyond according to Augusta University President Brooks Keel.
Augusta University has a partnership with Augusta Technical College since 2015 for a Cybersecurity to Information Technology Pathway.

Augusta University has an agreement with the Army Cyber Center of Excellence to offer several master’s degree programs to soldiers, including information security management and intelligence and security studies.
The Army moved its entire cyber command to Fort Gordon three years ago.
Augusta University worked with public and private partners to develop the Georgia Cyber Center to support the Army and businesses that support its mission.
The Medical College of Georgia (MCG) is the state’s only public medical school and is the eleventh largest medical school in the country.
A pending merger means Wellstar will take over the operations of the Augusta University Health System.
One potential benefit of the Wellstar merger is that med students may be able to practice in other parts of the state where Wellstar has facilities.
Augusta University had year-over-year enrollment growth of approximately 3% since 2015 and enrolled 9,800 students in the fall.
Augusta University was one of only eight universities in the University System of Georgia that had an increase in enrollment last fall.
source_title: Augusta | Richmond County: More than the Masters - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Georgia’s ports are a key factor for automakers, their suppliers, other manufacturers, and logistics operations deciding to bring jobs to the state.
Griff Lynch serves as President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority.
The Georgia Ports Authority's mission covers the entire state, extending beyond just Savannah or Atlanta.
In partnership with the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), the state announced 350 projects from potential port users in Fiscal Year 2023.
These FY2023 announcements will bring more than 33,000 manufacturing and logistics jobs and $21 billion in investment to 96 counties across the state.
The Georgia Ports Authority, GDEcD, and the Georgia Department of Transportation are working together to foster responsible growth that delivers good jobs while preserving quality of life.
The Georgia Ports Authority is investing $29 million in an overpass that will route trucks leaving its Ocean Terminal in Savannah directly onto U.S. 17, avoiding community streets.
According to the Global Commerce division of GDEcD, Georgia’s growing electric vehicle ecosystem has touched every corner of the state.
Growth in the state's EV ecosystem is led by announcements associated with the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, which have located in more than 10 counties across the state.
Commissioner Pat Wilson at GDEcD stated that companies, especially in e-mobility, are prioritizing speed-to-market solutions.
Investing in site development and obtaining “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” certification are ways communities can prepare for new opportunities.
Anovion Technologies, a synthetic graphite manufacturer, is creating 400 jobs for Southwest Georgia in Decatur County.

Anovion Executive Chairman Chip Dunn cited the state’s recruitment effort that swayed the company’s decision to locate in rural Georgia, including meetings with Gov. Brian Kemp and factors such as the GPA’s inland port system and access to waterway and land transport.
The GPA’s inland port system includes the Appalachian Regional Port in Crandall, Ga., and the Blue Ridge Connector rail yard now under construction near Gainesville, Ga.
The inland port system supports the movement of large, heavy products from centrally located communities to the state’s deepwater ports.
In the Savannah area alone, the inventory of industrial property totals more than 124 million square feet, with more than 14 million square feet under construction at the close of Calendar Year 2023.

Stephen Ezelle, a broker for industrial real estate firm Cushman & Wakefield, stated that the Savannah regional economy continues to experience expansion and strong absorption in the industrial sector despite a slowing economy across most U.S. markets.
The outlook for Savannah’s industrial market remains favorable thanks to continued growth at the Georgia Ports with conservative projections to eclipse 8 million TEUs of throughput by 2030.
Operational efficiency offered by the GPA and the shift in import cargo from the West Coast and NY/NJ are key drivers in the growth of Savannah’s industrial market.
Despite moderation of container trade nationally, Savannah is expected to continue balanced expansion thanks to the Hyundai announcement and the GPA’s growing national market share.
Ezelle predicts continued absorption of well-located light industrial warehouse space.
Savannah’s industrial sector ended 2023 with an overall vacancy rate of 7 percent.

Stacy Watson, director of economic and industrial development at the GPA, stated that being within 30 miles of the port is great but not required to be competitive in drayage, lease rates, and overall cost of doing business.

Locations 40 or 50 miles away from Savannah are an easy drive, attributed to the port’s location upriver of the Savannah population center and GDOT efforts making truck routes less congested and easily accessible.
Direct truck routes from the Port of Savannah connect to Interstates 95 and 16, while on-terminal service from two Class I railroads expedite cargo moving to and from the marine terminal.
Available properties in Bryan, Liberty and McIntosh counties as well as points west offer interstate and rail links to the port and to thriving inland markets.
Augusta is only a short drive from Savannah, and a majority of manufacturers in Augusta are port users.

Cal Wray, president of the Development Authority of Augusta, Ga., stated that Augusta-Richmond County provides an ideal location for port users due to available, affordable industrial land, a key base for manufacturers with over 350 already calling Augusta home, and easy rail and highway connections to the port.
Recent announcements in Augusta include Aurubis Richmond, a metal smelting operation bringing 250 jobs and $600 million in investment.
Recent announcements in Augusta include Denkai America, a copper film producer providing 250 jobs and $440 million in investment.
Recent announcements in Augusta include Purecycle Technologies, which recycles plastic into like-new pellets for reuse, bringing 100 jobs and $400 million in investment.
A stand-out industry ecosystem in Augusta is electric vehicle manufacturing, featuring EZGo Textron and Club Car along with their related suppliers in Richmond and Columbia counties.
Metals and chemical manufacturing are two other important industries in Augusta.
The Georgia Ports Authority supports the efficient operation of existing industries as well as local economic development efforts and is a key partner in ongoing recruitment of new industry.
The GPA serves as a great resource to existing manufacturers who have invested billions in the region already, many of which have announced significant further investments in the last five years.
source_title: Georgia Ports highlights site selection opportunities | AJOT.COM

The company provides parking available on-site.

facts: Eaton Corporation plc is a Dublin, Ireland-headquartered intelligent power management company founded in 1911.
The company provides energy-efficient solutions for electrical, hydraulic, and mechanical power across more than 180 countries.
Eaton operates through five key segments: Electrical Americas, Electrical Global, Aerospace, Vehicle, and eMobility.

Its electrical segments account for the vast majority of revenue through products such as circuit breakers, switchgear, power distribution equipment, and UPS systems serving data centers, utilities, industrial, and commercial markets.
Eaton's Aerospace segment supplies fuel, hydraulic, and pneumatic systems for commercial and military applications.
Its Vehicle and eMobility segments focus on drivetrain systems, emissions reduction technologies, and electrification solutions for both on-road and off-road vehicles.
The company has approximately 97,000 full-time employees.
Eaton reported revenues of $27.4 billion in 2025.
The company announced a definitive agreement to spin off its Mobility Group via a merger with Dana Incorporated in a Reverse Morris Trust transaction valued at over $10 billion.
The deal values the Mobility business at approximately $5.1 billion and includes a ~$1.1 billion cash distribution to Eaton.
The spin-off transaction is expected to close in Q1 2027.
Eaton completed the acquisition of Boyd Thermal in March, bolstering its 'grid to chip' data center cooling narrative.
The company published its 2025 Sustainability Report highlighting $2.1 billion in R&D investment.
Eaton's Electrical Americas segment reported 42% rolling average order growth in Q1.
The company reported record Q1 2026 revenue of $7.5 billion with a 22.7% segment margin.
Eaton raised its full-year organic growth guidance to 9%-11%.
Eaton announced a strategic partnership and investment with VoltServer to develop Digital Electricity solutions for longer-distance power delivery.
source_title: Eaton Corporation plc Stock Price: Quote, Forecast, Splits & News (ETN)

facts: Ended trading at $423.92 on Friday after reaching an intraday high of $432.34, its highest in the past year.
Shareholders approved all six proposals at the April 22 annual general meeting, including the re-election of 11 directors and retaining Ernst & Young LLP as auditor.
Shareholders approved executive compensation and authorized the board to issue shares under Irish law.
Shareholders backed waiving pre-emption rights and authorized overseas share buybacks.
First-quarter earnings will be reported on May 5, ahead of the bell on the New York Stock Exchange.
The company's first-quarter earnings call starts at 11 a.m. Eastern on May 5.
Eaton's Electrical Americas and Electrical Global units supply power distribution, electrical components, wiring devices, circuit protection, and power-quality gear.
The company operates aerospace, vehicle, and eMobility businesses.
VP and chief architect for data centers JP Buzzell described a future where data centers become AI-first, energy-aware ecosystems.
Certain AI clusters now consume more than 500 megawatts, compared to just 5 megawatts several years ago.
Eaton is partnering with Siemens Energy to pair on-site power generation with modular data center builds.
Eaton claims this partnership can slash deployment timelines by as much as two years depending on the project.
GE Vernova lifted its 2026 annual revenue and profit targets, citing rising demand for turbines and grid gear from data centers and grid upgrades.
GE Vernova's backlog climbed $13 billion to reach $163 billion.
In March, Eaton completed its acquisition of Boyd Thermal, bringing liquid-cooling systems for data centers and other sectors.
CEO Paulo Ruiz described Boyd's expertise as key for Eaton to offer grid to chip solutions covering everything from the power grid to server hardware.
In February, Eaton projected its 2026 adjusted profit would fall short of Wall Street estimates.
Big-name industrial buyers were holding off on orders due to trade restrictions and geopolitical jitters.

facts: On August 6, 2020, Governor Brian P. Kemp and the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced a strong start to the first month of fiscal year 2021 with new investment projects in Georgia.
In July, Georgia announced roughly $574 million in new investments and 3,629 new jobs created in nearly every region of the state.
Governor Kemp stated that jobs at every skill level were created across advanced manufacturing to software development in nearly every region of the state during July.
Governor Kemp thanked state and local economic development partners and companies establishing or expanding operations in Georgia during an unprecedented economic climate.
Existing companies in Georgia prior to July, including Frito-Lay, ThredUP, and Airman USA, created roughly 65% of the new jobs and represented 75% of the investments as they expanded operations.
German automotive parts manufacturer GEDIA was one of nine companies deciding to establish operations in Georgia for the first time.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson was traveling in Germany to meet with government officials, tourism, and trade partners when the announcement was made.
Commissioner Wilson's trip to Germany was supported by Georgia’s government partners in the Free State of Bavaria, a strategic partner region.
Commissioner Wilson met at GEDIA headquarters and visited other companies in the final stages of deciding to enter the U.S. market.
The investment data follows Atlanta being named the No. 1 Tech Hub in the United States by Business Facilities Magazine.
GDEcD announced that female-founded gaming software company Amebous Labs, founded by Georgia Tech graduate Annie Eaton, will create more new jobs opening a virtual reality studio in Atlanta.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that July's numbers provide hope and opportunity to counter economic uncertainties through jobs and investment.
Commissioner Wilson noted that Georgia remains the best state in the nation to do business for seven consecutive years despite fighting the spread of COVID-19.

The state announced that residential affordable-housing brand Common is investing $7 million to establish its second headquarters in Atlanta, delivering 274 career opportunities in architecture, interior design, software engineering, operations, and more.
source_title: GA: July saw $574 Million in new investments, 3,629 jobs

facts: Ecoplastic Corporation will invest about $205 million in a new Georgia facility.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Ecoplastic Corporation will create 456 new jobs at the new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County.
The company is an automotive parts manufacturer based in Korea, established in 1984.
Ecoplastic manufactures plastic automobile parts for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and other suppliers.
The company is a Tier-1 supplier for Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) and Kia.
Ecoplastic engages in the manufacturing of automotive interior and exterior plastic parts, including bumpers, consoles, trims, and plastic molds.
Han Shang, President and CEO of Ecoplastic, stated the company is honored to establish a production facility for electric vehicle parts in Bulloch County, Georgia.
The new facility will be located at 4822 Highway 301 South Register near Statesboro.
The plant will be capable of injection molding and painting.
The facility will produce exterior parts, bumpers and garnishes, interior parts, consoles, and trims for electric vehicles.
Ecoplastic expects to begin operations by October 2024.
The company will be hiring for HR, finance, parts development, quality control, facility maintenance, injection, and paint production positions.
Development Authority of Bulloch County Chair Billy Allen welcomed Ecoplastic to Bulloch County.
Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on the project.
The project is in partnership with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that Ecoplastic has decades of experience as a supplier for Kia and Hyundai.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry.
Since 2020, more than 30 EV-related projects have announced more than $21 billion in investment and announced more than 26,300 jobs in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground on October 25 on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility in neighboring Bryan County.
Offsite suppliers connected to Hyundai's project were predicted to invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around the state.
Ecoplastic Corporation was established in June 1984 as Apollo Ind Co., Ltd.
The company established its first Technical Research Center in 1987.
Ecoplastic was publicly listed on the Korean Stock Exchange in March 2000.
The company currently employs more than 770 employees.
Ecoplastic recorded sales revenue of more than $700 million in 2021.
The new facility will serve the Hyundai Metaplant from Statesboro, taking advantage of close proximity to Georgia's ports system.
source_title: Ecoplastic Corp. plans to invest in new GA facility

Ecoplastic Corporation will invest approximately $205 million in a new manufacturing facility.

Governor Brian Kemp welcomed Ecoplastic to the state's growing ecosystem of manufacturers, logistics professionals, and suppliers.

Ecoplastic will serve the Hyundai Metaplant from its new Statesboro location.

facts: The current offering involves the expansion of an existing advanced manufacturing facility operated by Ecoplastic America Corp in Bulloch County, Georgia.
The Project will consist of a new Phase II production plant adjacent to the already operational facility.
The Phase II plant is designed to meet growing demand for automotive components supplied to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) in Bryan County, GA.
The Project is structured with a senior, first-lien security interest in the land, building, and equipment.
The Project is further enhanced by repayment guarantees from publicly listed companies.
Following the Reform and Integrity Act (RIA) of 2022, Rural Area TEA projects now have a 20% visa set-aside.
Investors of the Project qualify for the reduced EB-5 investment threshold of $800,000.
Investors are provided with “Priority Processing”, reducing I-526E applications processing times to as few as 6 months.
The Project benefits from being an expansion of an already operational facility, rather than a greenfield development.

With existing infrastructure, workforce, and customer demand in place, the Phase II expansion is positioned to efficiently scale operations and support continued growth tied to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
The Project is expected to generate more than 2,200 total jobs upon completion.
The job creation provides a substantial buffer above the EB-5 job creation requirement.

SRC has completed multiple full cycles guiding investors through the initial capital investment, I-526 approvals, I-829 approvals and ultimately the full return of original capital over a long-standing relationship with Ajin USA.
SRC Ajin Fund VI LLC involves the construction and operation of an 83-acre auto parts manufacturing plant by Joon Georgia, Inc.
The Joon Georgia, Inc. plant supplies parts for HMGMA, Hyundai’s $7.59B mass production metaplant in Bryan County, GA.
SRC Ajin Fund VI LLC supported 99 investors and fully funded $79.2M.
There were 45+ approvals on adjudicated I-526 petitions filed between 2024 and 2025 for this fund.
The fund achieved the fastest I-526E adjudication under 3.5 months with Rural Priority Processing.
SRC Ajin Fund V LLC was a further expansion to support growing demand and was the beneficiary of a USCIS-approved project exemplar.
SRC Ajin Fund V LLC had a 100% approval rate on adjudicated I-526 petitions, with I-829 in progress.
SRC Ajin Fund V LLC returned 100% investment capital for investors that sustained the ‘at risk’ loan term.
SRC Ajin Fund IV LLC was established to support rising demand by expanding Ajin USA's current manufacturing plant using EB-5 capital through SRC.
SRC Ajin Fund IV LLC had a 100% approval rate on adjudicated I-526 petitions, with I-829 in progress.
SRC Ajin Fund IV LLC returned 100% investment capital for investors that sustained the ‘at risk’ loan term.
SRC Ajin Fund III LLC provided a final round of EB-5 funding to complete construction and operationalize a critical, Tier-1 auto parts manufacturing facility.
The Tier-1 facility supplies key body frame components to Kia and Hyundai Motors.
SRC Ajin Fund III LLC had a 100% approval rate on adjudicated I-526 and I-829 petitions.
SRC Ajin Fund III LLC returned 100% investment capital.
SRC Ajin Fund II LLC provided additional EB-5 funding to continue construction of a giant 330,000 square foot facility in Alabama.
SRC Ajin Fund II LLC had a 100% approval rate on adjudicated I-526 and I-829 petitions.
SRC Ajin Fund II LLC returned 100% investment capital.
SRC Ajin Fund I LLC was the first fund and start of a long-standing relationship between Ajin USA and SRC.
Ajin USA leveraged EB-5 funds to begin constructing a manufacturing facility in Chambers County, Alabama, USA, designated as a “Rural Area”.
SRC Ajin Fund I LLC had a 100% approval rate on adjudicated I-526 and I-829 petitions.
SRC Ajin Fund I LLC returned 100% investment capital.

facts: Revalyu Resources announced in December 2022 that it would locate a plant in Statesboro, Ga.
Revalyu Resources broke ground on Oct. 4 at the 43-acre site in the city's Gateway Regional Industrial Park.
The original planned investment of $50 million was increased to more than $200 million for the Statesboro plant.
The plant will be capable of recycling 200 million pounds of polyethylene terephthalate (PET) plastic each year.
Revalyu's Statesboro plant will recycle 100 percent post-consumer material at a rate of about 25 million bottles each day.
Construction of the facility's first phase is expected to be completed in the third quarter of 2025.
The first phase will employ 71 people, with another 50 employees added in Phase 2.
Jan van Kisfeld, managing director of Revalyu, stated that this is the company's first plant in the U.S. and a very important step for expansion.
Revalyu produces 100 percent recycled pellets that are equivalent in quality to conventional oil-based PET pellets.
The advanced recycling plant will serve existing and future U.S. customers directly from the U.S., enabling quicker transportation time, lower cost, and a smaller carbon footprint.
Revalyu has been supplying chemically recycled PET chips or pellets to manufacturers in the United States for more than six years.
The company's choice of the Statesboro/Bulloch County site followed three months of intensive search and serious negotiations with the Development Authority of Bulloch County (DABC).
Benjy Thompson, CEO of the DABC, noted that Revalyu is the first German-owned company to locate in Bulloch County.
Revalyu was founded in 2007 by Vivek Tandon and was originally called perPETual.
Vivek Tandon attended the Statesboro groundbreaking.
The company's existing processing plant is in India and is being expanded to recycle 240 metric tons of PET a day.
The new southeast Georgia plant will be able to process 100 metric tons of PET daily.
Revalyu's revolutionary process allows used PET plastic to be efficiently, profitably, and easily recycled again and again without degradation of quality.
The proposed Revalyu facility is one of five Bulloch County industrial plant site selections announced by the DABC in the past 20 months.
Together, the five companies have promised to eventually create more than 1,500 jobs at their respective sites.
Massachusetts-based Aspen Aerogels announced a $325 million plant to make aerogel insulation used in and around electric vehicle batteries.
Aspen Aerogels is constructing its 90-acre plant on a site in Bruce Yawn Commerce Park.
Ecoplastic America Corp., a supplier of injection-molded plastic automotive body parts to Hyundai Motor Group, intends to build a $205 million plant.
Ecoplastic America promises to create 456 jobs phased in over eight years at its facility south of Statesboro on a site owned by the DABC.
Site preparation for the Ecoplastic America factory is already under way.
Ajin USA, a supplier of metal auto body parts to Hyundai, committed to invest $317 million and eventually employ 630 people at its Joon Georgia plant.
The Ajin USA site encompasses 83 acres next door to the Aspen Aerogels site in Bruce Yawn Commerce Park.

Hanon Systems will build a $40 million manufacturing plant for producing automotive air control equipment, including air conditioners, heaters, cooling modules, compressors, radiators, fans, evaporators, and condensers.
Hanon Systems will be a tier-one supplier to the Hyundai Metaplant that is building EV cars northwest of Savannah.
The Hanon Systems facility will be constructed in Gateway Industrial Park and create 160 jobs.

facts: Fouts Bros. Inc. recently announced a local expansion in Milledgeville and Baldwin County that will bring new jobs and investment to the community.
The company is elite when it comes to manufacturing fire and fire rescue trucks.
When Scott Edens first came to Milledgeville, the company was building 150 trucks annually with a goal to double that number as soon as operations got underway.
President and CEO Scott Edens moved the company headquarters from Smyrna to Milledgeville a few years ago to continue growing the business founded by his grandfather, C.J. Fouts, in 1952.
The expansion includes $4 million in investment for this year and next.
The company is adding 96 jobs in 2023 with a forecast to add 70 more in 2024.
These employment figures will bring the company's total workforce up to nearly 500 employees, making it one of Baldwin County's top employers.
The company is currently using only about half of the space at its facility, which was the former home of Rheem.
Fouts Bros. has plans to build on the back 30 acres of the property due to business expansion.
Executive Director Jonathan Jackson shared the expansion details during a DAMBC board meeting on Monday morning.

Sharon Seymour, chairwoman of The Development Authority of Milledgeville and Baldwin County (DAMBC), stated the authority is glad Fouts Bros. chose their community as home and looks forward to working with them to provide good incomes for hundreds of employees.
Jonathan Jackson noted that the company's hiring has been a challenge, but they found the local community better suited to handle it than their previous location in Smyrna.
The facility site was previously classified as a Brownfield site requiring well monitoring and sub-surface ventilation due to Rheem's operations.
Through cooperation between Rheem and Fouts Bros., groundwater monitoring and underground ventilation requirements were eliminated, and the site was removed from the Georgia Hazardous Sites list.
Jackson described the environmental remediation as a textbook example of how Georgia’s Brownfield program works through the EPD, calling it a huge accomplishment.
Fouts Bros. is continuing to perform necessary environmental work at the site.
Employees at the facility are making good wages and doing well, according to Jackson.

The company was searching for a building and site that could work for its operations and ultimately chose Milledgeville and Baldwin County due to local leadership, economic development efforts, and community members.
source_title: Fouts Brothers to expand in Milledgeville and Baldwin County | The Union-Recorder

facts: Fouts Bros is a family-owned business established in 1952 with facilities located in Georgia and Texas.
Fouts Bros is recognized as one of the largest and most diverse commercial truck manufacturers in the United States.
The company operates Fouts Fire, a division dedicated to manufacturing high-quality fire trucks and emergency vehicles for fire departments and emergency responders.
Fouts Fire's mission focuses on ensuring the safety and efficiency of fire and rescue operations through reliable and expertly maintained equipment.
The company's core values include quality, efficiency, continuous improvement, safety, and teamwork.
The company conducts business with honesty and transparency while continuously seeking new ways to improve and evolve.
Fouts Bros fosters relationships with its community, supports local initiatives, and operates in an environmentally responsible manner to minimize its impact on the planet.
A Fire Truck Technician position is available in Milledgeville, Georgia, USA.
The Fire Truck Technician role involves performing regular maintenance, troubleshooting, and repairs on fire trucks and emergency vehicles to meet high-quality standards.
Responsibilities include conducting thorough inspections and testing of firefighting equipment and systems, as well as providing technical support and guidance to team members.

Required qualifications for the technician role include proven experience in fire truck or similar technical roles, strong knowledge of mechanical, electrical, and hydraulic systems, and the ability to interpret technical manuals and schematics.
Candidates must possess excellent problem-solving and communication skills, with certification or training in fire apparatus maintenance and repair considered a plus.
source_title: Fire Truck Technician @ Fouts Bros | Develop Milledgeville Job Board

facts: Fire Service Inc adds Fouts Fire to their list of trusted fire truck manufacturers.
Fire Service Inc currently sells E-ONE, KME, Ferrara (WI ONLY), and Fouts Fire.
Fouts Fire is family-owned and operated since 1952.
Fouts builds trucks that fight fires in every state in the continental U.S.
Fouts Fire is based out of a 700,000 square foot facility in Milledgeville, GA.
Fouts Fire operates a second facility in La Grange, TX.
Unlike other American fire apparatus manufacturers, Fouts Fire's business model is based on a standardized line of trucks honed and perfected over many years.
Fouts Fire's repeatable processes allow them to build more fire trucks for less money and deliver them quickly.
Fouts Fire has deep supplier partnerships cemented over seven decades of heavy-duty manufacturing and fabrication experience.
Fouts Fire's supply line is always full, enabling delivery even when competitors cannot deliver a truck until the year after next.
In 1952, CJ Fouts and his brother Jerry bought a Sinclair service station in Smyrna, GA, founding Fouts Bros Inc.
The brothers invested in auto and truck dealerships, commercial body manufacturing, real estate, and fire trucks.
Fouts Bros is now in the hands of the third generation of the family.
Fouts Fire is dedicated to offering the best value fire apparatus in the country.
Fouts Fire's product lineup includes industry-leading 3000 gallon tankers and the newest product, the Fouts FOUR.
Fouts Fire's production facilities have expanded and its engineering standards are cutting-edge.
Fouts Fire's core values are service, quality, and family first.
Seventy years after founding, Fouts Bros remains family-owned and family-operated.
source_title: Fire Service Inc Now Sells Fouts Fire. - Fire Service, Inc.

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy will make a $6.6 billion loan to Rivian Automotive to build a factory in Georgia.
The factory had stalled as the startup automaker struggled to become profitable.
It is unclear whether the administration can complete the loan before Donald Trump becomes president again in less than two months.
It is unclear whether the Trump administration might try to claw the money back.
Trump previously vowed to end federal electric vehicle tax credits, which are worth up to $7,500 for new zero-emission vehicles and $4,000 for used ones.
Trump later softened his stance as Tesla CEO Elon Musk became a supporter and adviser.
The R1S dual-motor electric sports utility vehicle (SUV) is on the pilot production line at company headquarters in Irvine, California.
Rivian went public and began producing large electric R1 SUVs, pickup trucks and delivery vans at a former Mitsubishi factory in Normal, Illinois, in 2021.
The California-based company announced it would build a second, larger, $5 billion plant about 40 miles east of Atlanta, near the town of Social Circle.
The R1 vehicles cost $70,000 or more.
The original plan was to produce R2 vehicles, a smaller SUV, in Georgia with lower price tags aimed at a mass market.
The first phase of Rivian’s Georgia factory was projected to make 200,000 vehicles a year.
A second phase was capable of another 200,000 vehicles a year.
Eventually, the plant was projected to employ 7,500 workers.
Rivian was unable to meet production and sales targets and rapidly burned through cash.
In March, the company said it would pause construction of the Georgia plant.
The company said it would begin assembling its R2 SUV in Illinois instead.
CEO RJ Scaringe said the move would allow Rivian to get the R2 to market more quickly, sometime in 2026.
The move would save $2.25 billion in capital spending.
German automaker Volkswagen AG said in June it would invest $5 billion in Rivian in a joint venture.
In the joint venture, Rivian would share software and electrical technology with Volkswagen.
The money eased Rivian’s cash crunch.
Tuesday’s announcement throws a lifeline to Rivian’s grander plans.
The company says its plans to make the R2 and the smaller R3 in Georgia are back on.
The money would come from the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Loan Program.
The program has $17.7 billion to provide low-cost loans to make fuel-efficient vehicles and components.
The program has focused mostly on loans to new battery factories for electric vehicles in recent years but also helped finance the initial production of the Tesla Model S and Nissan Leaf.
The program, created in 2007, requires a "reasonable prospect of repayment" of the loan.
Democratic U.S. Sen. Jon Ossoff has been a vocal supporter of electric vehicle and solar manufacturing in Georgia.
Ossoff hailed Tuesday’s announcement as "yet another historic federal investment in Georgia electric vehicle manufacturing."
Ossoff had asked Energy Secretary Jennifer Granholm to support the loan in July.
Ossoff said, "Our federal manufacturing incentives are driving economic development across the state of Georgia."
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp says his goal is to make Georgia a center of the electric vehicle industry.
Kemp has had a strained relationship with the Biden administration over its industrial policy.
Some studies have found Georgia has netted more electric vehicle investment than any other state.
Kemp has long claimed that manufacturers were picking Georgia before Biden’s signature climate law, the Inflation Reduction Act, was passed.
Garrison Douglas, a spokesperson for Kemp, said earlier this month that the governor wants Trump to prioritize "a market-based approach to economic growth."

Douglas stated that as the e-mobility space was already growing in Georgia before federal intervention, the governor remains vocally opposed to the Biden administration’s decision to pick winners and losers and impose counterproductive mandates that disadvantage Georgia-based auto manufacturers.
The loan to Rivian could rescue one of the Kemp administration’s signature economic development projects even as Biden leaves office.
That could put Rivian and Kemp in the position of defending the loan if Trump tries to quash it.
State and local governments offered Rivian an incentive package worth an estimated $1.5 billion in 2022.
The deadline for the company to complete its investment and hiring under that deal was extended to 2030.
Neighbors opposed to development of the Georgia site mounted legal challenges.
State and local governments were projected to spend more than $125 million to buy the nearly 2,000-acre (810-hectare) site, clear trees and grade land.
That work has been finished.
The state also has completed most of $50 million in roadwork that it pledged.
The pause at Rivian contrasts with rapid construction at Hyundai Motor Group’s $7.6 billion electric vehicle and battery complex near Savannah.
The plant in Ellabell, announced in 2022, could grow to 8,500 employees.
Hyundai said in October that it has begun production there.
source_title: Biden administration giving Rivian $6.6B loan to build Georgia factory | FOX 5 Atlanta

facts: A Hyundai factory under construction near Savannah, Georgia, was previously touted by former President Joe Biden and other politicians as a win for manufacturing jobs in the United States.
Homeland Security Investigations arrested 475 people who were in the country illegally, primarily from South Korea, at the under-construction battery plant.
Hyundai claimed that none of the detained individuals were "directly employed" by the company.
The incident brings renewed scrutiny on construction sites of large projects as the Trump administration continues to investigate illegal worksite practices.

Former President Joe Biden announced in May 2022 that the project involves more than $10 billion in new investment in American manufacturing, including a $5 billion commitment for advanced automotive technology and a $5.5 billion investment to open a new factory near Savannah, Georgia, which is going to create more than 8,000 new American jobs.
Biden stated that his administration is setting ambitious standards to cut pollution in cars and trucks and boost fuel economy standards for those continuing to operate on gasoline.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp touted the project in February in an X post praising the "momentum" of the project.
The effort is a partnership between Hyundai and LG Energy Solution to bolster EV development in the U.S.
HSI reported that some of the arrested workers were employed by subcontractors on the construction site, which has since been paused.
An EV manufacturing plant on the campus was not affected by the raid.
ICE and other law enforcement agencies were part of the operation at the site.
Hyundai stated that it prioritizes the safety and well-being of everyone working at the site and complies with all laws and regulations wherever it operates.
LG Energy Solution stated that it will fully cooperate with the relevant authorities.
Operations at worksites have become a noteworthy element of the Trump administration’s crackdown on illegal immigration.
A man wearing an HSI vest at the site on Thursday announced via video that he had a warrant and ordered construction to cease immediately, stating "We need all work to end on the site right now."
A DHS spokesperson described the operation as a "complex case" that required help from numerous federal agencies and the Georgia State Patrol.

facts: Fox Factory Holding Corp. is an American company headquartered in Duluth, Georgia, U.S.
The company was founded in 1977 in California.
Fox Factory is a public company.
The company reported revenue of US$1.464 billion in 2023 and $205,278,000 in 2022.
Fox Factory had 2,600 employees as of 2019.
The company is best known for its FOX brand of off-road racing suspension components.
Fox Factory produces suspension components for motorcycles, automobiles, all-terrain vehicles, side-by-sides, trophy trucks, snowmobiles, and mountain bikes.
In 2008, the company was bought by a private equity firm, Compass Diversified Holdings.
The company went public in 2013.
In 2014, Fox Factory acquired Sport Truck USA in Coldwater, MI, including brands BDS Suspension, Zone Offroad, and JKS Manufacturing.
In 2014, Fox Factory acquired Race Face and Easton in Vancouver, B.C., which manufacture and distribute high performance cycling components and apparel.
In 2015, Fox Factory acquired certain assets of Marzocchi dedicated to mountain bike suspension.
In 2017, Fox Factory acquired a majority share in Tuscany Motors, an Elkhart, IN vehicle upfitter focused on full-size trucks on Ford, Ram, and General Motors platforms.
In 2019, Fox Factory acquired RideTech in Jasper, IN, a manufacturer of traditional, coilover, and air suspension systems for muscle cars, trucks, and hot rods.
In 2020, Fox Factory agreed to acquire SCA Performance in Trussville, AL, a vehicle upfitter focused on full-size trucks on the Chevrolet, Ford, and RAM platforms.
In 2021, Fox Factory acquired Outside Van in Portland, OR, a custom converter of adventure vans on the Mercedes Sprinter platform.
In November 2023, Fox Factory acquired Marucci Sports in Baton Rouge, LA, a sports apparel manufacturer primarily known as a bat supplier to Major League Baseball, for $572 million.
In 2016, sales of mountain bike related equipment accounted for 56% of the company's total revenue.

As of January 2025, Fox Factory offers fork models (Fox 32, 34, 36, 38, and 40) with travel ranges from 40mm to 203mm depending on the model and damper options like Grip/Grip2, Fit GRIP/Fit4, and GripX/X2.
As of January 2025, Fox Factory offers rear shock models including Fox Float SL, Float, Float X, DHX, Float X2, and DHX2 for various use cases from cross country to gravity riding.
source_title: Fox Factory - Wikipedia

facts: Fox Factory selected Carroll Daniel to develop a new facility in Gainesville, Georgia.
The project consisted of a 350,000 SF manufacturing facility and a 50,000 SF office component located on a 30-acre site.
The facility houses 300 employees.
Fox Factory offices reside on the second level of the facility, providing views down into assembly areas to offer an integrated and collaborative experience.
The building includes a large cafeteria, locker rooms and all-hands meeting areas in addition to office space.
Construction occurred during the height of the COVID-19 pandemic.
Carroll Daniel navigated schedule changes and material shortages to keep the project on track and on-time.
After completion, the Fox Factory project received three awards for pushing the boundaries of possibility for design-build project design and construction.

The company designs and manufactures performance-defining ride dynamics products primarily for bicycles, on-road and off-road vehicles and trucks, side-by-side vehicles, all-terrain vehicles, snowmobiles, specialty vehicles and applications, motorcycles, and commercial trucks.
Fox Factory Holding Corp. is a direct supplier to leading powered vehicle OEMs.

facts: Fox Factory designs, engineers, manufactures, and markets suspension products.

The company provides performance-defining products such as suspension systems and components, cranks, wheels, cycling components, and other components for bicycles, snowmobiles, on-road and off-road vehicles, trucks, side-by-side vehicles, motorcycles, and other specialty vehicles.
Fox also provides products to original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
Fox Factory is headquartered in Duluth, Georgia with operations around the world including a large manufacturing facility in Gainesville, Georgia that produces shocks for off-road power vehicles.
The Fox Factory Facility in Gainesville, GA is a greenfield plant that started up in 2020 making high performance automotive and off-road vehicle shocks.
The facility is able to vertically integrate several critical parts into the assembly of shocks by machining and anodizing parts that were purchased from outside suppliers.
A prototype cell is running with a robot for loading two of the CNC machines in a machining cell.

Robotic loaded is needed to reduce labor and improve efficiencies but additional improvements to the machining cells are being implemented and need to be considered to determine the potential labor savings.
The project will focus on improving labor efficiency in a large machine shop starting with internal bodies for automotive shocks.
Planned improvements include simulating robotic loading of CNC lathes, elimination of inner body wrapping for rust prevention, and implementation of machine deburring.
Project goals include improving labor efficiency, improving machine utilization, and reducing changeover time.
The potential impact is potentially 1.5 million per year in direct labor efficiency.
source_title: Fox Factory 7505 | H. Milton Stewart School of Industrial and Systems Engineering

The company acquires complementary businesses to integrate engineering and manufacturing expertise beyond its core shock and suspension segment.

facts: Arizona-based Solarcycle announced plans to build a $344 million factory in northwest Georgia.
The new facility marks the company's first expansion into manufacturing new glass for solar panels from recycled materials.
Solarcycle will hire more than 600 workers at the Cedartown, Georgia site.
The Cedartown factory is scheduled to open in 2026.
Cedartown is located approximately 50 miles (80 kilometers) northwest of Atlanta.
Solarcycle was founded in 2022 and previously opened its first facility in Odessa, Texas.
The company opened a headquarters and research lab in Mesa, Arizona earlier this month (February 2024), hiring more than 100 people.
Solarcycle can extract materials worth 95% of a solar panel's value, including silver, silicon, copper, and aluminum.
The Georgia factory will produce enough glass annually to manufacture solar panels capable of generating 5 gigawatts of electricity.
The company plans to sell the manufactured glass to solar panel producers in the United States.
South Korean-owned Qcells, which manufactures solar panels in nearby Dalton, contracted Solarcycle to recycle decommissioned Qcells panels in the United States.
Solarcycle has similar recycling contracts with more than 40 other solar energy companies.
The company has raised tens of millions of dollars from private investors for expansion.
Solarcycle received $1.5 million from the U.S. Department of Energy to fund research and development.
The facility could qualify for $9 million in state income tax credits, calculated at $3,000 per job over five years.
To qualify for the state income tax credits, workers must earn at least $31,300 annually.
Cedartown and Polk County will provide property tax breaks to the company, though no specific estimate was provided.
Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Solarcycle approached state economic recruiters at a trade show.
Chris Thomas, president and CEO of the Development Authority of Polk County, confirmed the property tax breaks but did not provide an estimate.
Wilson stated that “Solarcycle provides a critical piece to the integrated solar supply chain we are building in Georgia.”
U.S. Sen. Raphael Warnock credited President Joe Biden's Inflation Reduction Act with spurring Solarcycle's investment in Georgia.
source_title: Company plans $344 million Georgia factory to make recycled glass for solar panels

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies serves multiple industries such as automotive, aerospace, medical devices, and bottling equipment.
The company opened a facility in Cleveland, Georgia, thirty years ago.
The Cleveland, Georgia location employs more than 500 people from the community and surrounding towns.

The Georgia Manufacturing Extension Partnership (GaMEP) at Georgia Tech reached out to Wyman Hare, production partner manager of Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies, to set up a benchmarking tour for its Advanced Manufacturing Consortium.
Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies is advanced in lean thinking and has many practices that consortium member companies can learn from.
Wyman Hare agreed to invite the consortium members into their plant but also requested to join the group himself.
Wyman Hare stated, “We may have won the Shingo Prize, but we, as a team, feel like we can always continue to learn from other companies in how to improve our operations.”
During an initial plant tour, Hare made a connection with an organization that was far along in its lean journey.

Hare arranged for twenty team members from the Cleveland facility and three other Freudenberg-NOK plants to tour a facility where they witnessed a communication method that made them rethink how they share information.

Wyman Hare said, “the moment we saw the hospital’s Lean Production Management System in place, we knew the changes we needed to make in our facility to have our information flow faster from the bottom to the top of the organization, so that we, as a management team, could be more responsive and communicative with our direct reports and then downward from there.”
Each morning, every department from the plant floor to human resources gathers for 20 minutes within their respective teams.
Three additional tiers of meetings occur after the initial gathering, each layer sharing information from the prior meeting until a final meeting takes place among Hare and his direct reports.
Within two hours, the entire organization understands daily priorities and helps resolve issues in a more timely and organized manner.
Over time, this communication method proved influential in breaking down silos, earning trust, and increasing plant-wide transparency.
Since the success at the Cleveland plant, Hare and Jennifer Watson, GROWTH manager, implemented this system division-wide, getting all 15,000 employees on the same communication flow.
Hare and his team are looking forward to connecting with nine other GaMEP consortium groups statewide outside of Northeast Georgia.
Wyman Hare stated that their experience with GaMEP and the Advanced Manufacturing Consortium has been extremely positive, serving as a great sounding board to make connections and learn from others.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies Success Story - GaMEP at Georgia Tech

facts: Freudenberg-NOK's LaGrange and Cleveland, GA facilities have received a quality award from Hino Motor Manufacturing USA Inc, a part of the Toyota group of companies.

Freudenberg-NOK is a maker of elastomeric seals and custom molded products.

The quality award is based on the number of parts per million defect as well as on-time delivery.
Parts per million (ppm) serves as a measurement of good to bad product in the industry.
Alban Silarbi oversees the LaGrange operation.
Freudenberg-NOK's Georgia facilities are ISO 9000, QS 9000/2000, TS 16949 and AS 9100 certified.

facts: Patent 12535139 covers a drive-line seal system with a housing bore, rotatable shaft, slinger with radially outwardly extending surface, annular retainer, and annular seal body featuring main seal lip, dust lip, and axial seal lip with raised pumping features; filed June 7, 2023, granted January 27, 2026, inventor Lloyd Wylie.

Patent 12442473 covers a seal for oval cross-section pipe sections using an oval tubular insert with flat wall and curved end sections, plus an elastomeric over-mold with raised seal beads of varying radial thickness; filed June 28, 2024, granted October 14, 2025, inventor Eduardo Hernandez-Vazquez.

Patent 12410866 covers a cassette seal arrangement for shaft and housing sealing featuring a sleeve on the shaft, retainer cartridge on the housing, seal member with main and exclusionary lips, and an electrically conductive non-woven element between them; filed June 7, 2023, granted September 9, 2025, inventors D. Anthony Manley, Darin Battles, Christopher Beller.

Patent 12404929 covers a tube seal assembly with a nipple and fluid passage, cylindrical member with flange, metal reinforcement, and over-molded seal body with locking feature and sealing bead; filed August 2, 2023, granted September 2, 2025, inventors Darin Battles, Michael Blake.

Patent 12281703 covers a radial shaft seal with outer cover cylindrical/radial portions, inner seal element metal case, dynamic oil seal lip engaging the outer cover, and static oil seal lip engaging an inner cover; filed January 10, 2024, granted April 22, 2025, inventor Keith Clark.

Publication 20250084257 covers a high-temperature resistant insulating barrier coating using insulating resin (high-consistency silicone rubber or epoxy), flame retardants (intumescent melamine powder/expandable graphite plus ammonium polyphosphate), and reinforcing fiber (aromatic polyamide); filed September 12, 2023, published March 13, 2025, inventors Ursula Oyer, Raymond L. Szparagowski, Paul J. Hochgesang.

Najafabadi, Darin Battles, Christopher Beller.

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies is one of the world’s largest producers of elastomeric seals and custom molded products.
The company helps automakers reduce emissions and improve fuel economy in three powertrain areas: gaskets, fluid power and special sealing solutions.

Brad Norton, senior vice president of sales and marketing at Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies, noted that ‘green’ vehicles were expected to garner significant attention at the North American International Auto Show.
Norton stated Freudenberg-NOK is a leading supplier of products helping automakers meet market demand for green vehicles.
The company is committed to developing innovative products for cars on the road today and for future concepts.
A common theme in many 2012 vehicles is fuel efficiency and lowered emissions.
Freudenberg Sealing Technologies’ new Low-Emission Sealing Solutions (LESS) package addresses automaker demands with reduced-friction sealing solutions that cut wear and power loss.
The LESS package contains innovative products that optimize the combustion process, support modern downsizing concepts and make start-stop systems possible.
According to Freudenberg, LESS contributes to a sustainable reduction in consumption and emissions while paving the way for better performance and enhanced driving pleasure.
LESS solutions now in production include friction-reduced dynamic seal ranges from the Elastomeric Energy-Saving Seal or the Power-Optimized PTFE Seal.
Freudenberg-NOK has developed two new piston seals that reduce friction and lower energy consumption in applications such as mechanical transmissions for the automotive industry.
The new piston seal products include the Torque Control Seal (TCS) and D-ring with PTFE enhancement.

Freudenberg-NOK’s gasket division offers a global EPDM Compound and gasket design ideal for use in coolant circuits using automotive coolants in engines, battery packs, power inverters or other applications using liquid thermal management.
This material is currently in use at four major automotive OEMs and one commercial vehicle OEM.
The company’s gasket division has become a major partner in the electric vehicle and hybrid electric vehicle market by sealing oversized large battery housings.

Freudenberg-NOK fluid power sealing applications innovate with new materials such as transmission seal rings, transmission thrust washers and plastic accumulator pistons to help customers save costs or improve fuel efficiency.
Freudenberg-NOK’s transmission seal rings are the primary source for PTFE seal rings in North America.
The company has developed substantially more durable materials and can engineer solutions for most seal, groove and bore requirements.
Quantum, PTFE, PEEK, Torlon and other specialty resins continue to push the boundaries of high value, engineering resins for automotive sealing applications.
Freudenberg-NOK has expanded its sealing expertise into metal replacement with thermoplastic designs.

The industry-recognized thermoplastic accumulator piston is chemical-resistant, cost-reducing, has high temperature with high load capabilities and extremely tight dimensional stability, resulting in increased system efficiency and reduced cost.

Norton stated that these solutions only scratch the surface of Freudenberg-NOK’s innovations and that the company will continue to solve customer needs in the automotive industry to meet current and future sealing requirements.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK Promoting New Materials, Green Technologies - AftermarketNews

facts: Truett McConnell University’s Hans Hut School of Business announced a regional initiative with Freudenberg-NOK in Cleveland, Ga.
Freudenberg Sealing Technologies is a leading market and technology partner for demanding applications, including sealing technology and electro-mobility.
Established in 1989, Freudenberg-NOK is the Americas joint venture between Freudenberg in Germany and NOK Corporation in Japan.
The company boasts 5,000 employees in North America and over 50,000 globally.
Freudenberg-NOK is one of the largest employers in White County, GA where Truett McConnell University resides.
As part of the new initiative, Truett McConnell will be a preferred online education provider for Freudenberg-NOK employees.
Initially, the partnership will focus on undergraduate and graduate degree programs in business with the opportunity to broaden degree programs and the languages in which they are offered.

Tiffani Carson, Human Resource Manager for Freudenberg-NOK stated the company is excited to partner with TMU, values growth for associates, and noted team members are looking forward to furthering their education while working through online, hybrid and onsite classes.

Dr. Emir Caner, President of TMU offered that Truett McConnell University is honored to partner with Freudenberg-NOK, the leading specialist in sealing technology globally, and combining innovators with their MBA program will see a new generation of business professionals.

Dr. Kimberly Maiocco, Dean of the Hans Hut School of Business added it is an honor to receive the trust of Freudenberg-NOK in supporting professional development of employees globally, noting Freudenberg values collaborative innovation and looking forward to a long relationship providing expanding academic opportunities through business programs.

Dr. Stacy Hall, Associate Professor of Business and Program Director of Strategic Partnerships for TMU’s Hans Hut School of Business said the partnership underscores the School of Business’ commitment to partner with leading companies around the globe to provide quality education, collaboration and employment opportunities for students.

Truett McConnell University is a private Christian liberal arts and sciences university located in Cleveland, Georgia, offering 50 different programs of study including baccalaureate and master’s degrees.
The University boasts a 10:1 students to faculty ratio while 100% of freshmen receive financial aid.
source_title: Truett McConnell School of Business helps Freudenberg-NOK employees with college finances - The Christian Index

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies announced it will support college students designing single-passenger vehicles for the 2015 SAE Supermileage Competition with custom seals and engineering resources.
Freudenberg-NOK will support the Supermileage event for a third year as both a Gold Sponsor and a component supplier.
The company engineered a new series of custom Energy Saving Seals (ESS) to fit the latest Briggs & Stratton engine used by students to power their Supermileage cars.
The ESS seals are part of the company’s Low Emission Sealing Solutions (LESS) portfolio and are custom-designed to fit the crankshaft on the small four-cycle engine.
Global automotive manufacturers are intensively focused on improving fuel economy and lowering vehicle emissions, mirroring the goals of the student vehicles.

Luis Lorenzo, senior vice president, Technology and Innovation at Freudenberg-NOK, stated that Supermileage is a prime example of what the auto industry is pursuing and expressed excitement about helping students achieve their goals.
In the previous year's competition, Freudenberg-NOK supplied ESS seals that reduced friction by 57 percent.
Because a newer engine model will be used in the 2015 competition, Freudenberg-NOK will design and produce new ESS seals to fit the updated single cylinder engine.

Lorenzo added that the company is actively pursuing trends like light weighting and fuel saving technologies, and Supermileage allows them to work on these major automotive industry challenges with up-and-coming engineers.

In addition to providing friction-reducing seals, Freudenberg-NOK will sponsor two students and their vehicle from the top-performing team that uses the seals at the Center for Automotive Research’s Management Briefing Seminars (CAR MBS) in August.

In the 2014 competition, the top team was Concordia University from Montreal, Quebec, Canada, which received the Endurance Award for achieving the highest average fuel economy with at least five complete runs.
Students will have the opportunity to participate in the CAR MBS Student Program with all expenses paid.

Alex Laroche, a Concordia University engineering student and 2014 CAR MBS participant, said Freudenberg-NOK gave them the opportunity to start a relationship with auto industry leaders and build their network, and noted that publicity from an Autoline.tv interview was appreciated by their university and sponsors.
SAE International’s Supermileage Competition takes place June 4-5 at Eaton Corp.’s proving grounds in Marshall, Mich.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK Renews Support For Collegiate Fuel Economy Competition

facts: Freudenberg-NOK is an American joint venture partnership between Germany's Freudenberg & Company and Japan's NOK Corporation.
The company is one of the world's largest producers of elastomeric seals and custom molded products.
Its product portfolio includes bonded piston seals, brakes, fluid power products, gaskets, O-rings, radial shaft seals, and vibration control devices.
These products are sold to the aerospace, automotive sealing, health care, appliance, industrial equipment, and chemical processing markets.
Products are distributed under the brand names Flexitech, TransTec, Omega, Simrit, and Vibracoustic.
Freudenberg-NOK employs nearly 6,000 associates across 39 facilities in the United States, Canada, Mexico, and Brazil.
The company operates one facility in LaGrange, Georgia.
The LaGrange facility is located at 1618 Lukken Industrial Dr W, Lagrange, GA 30240.
The facility is situated near the intersection of Lukken Industrial Dr W and Airport Pkwy.
Freudenberg-NOK was founded in 1989 and is headquartered in Plymouth, Michigan.

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies introduced two new lubricants for the installation of static seals: FN Lube and FN Lube Plus.
The products were developed with support from Klueber Lubrication, which is part of the Freudenberg Group.
The lubricants are aimed primarily at applications involving O-rings.
Lubrication plays an important role during O-ring installation to prevent twisting, damage, or incorrect positioning caused by excessive friction.
FN Lube and FN Lube Plus were developed to reduce installation friction while remaining compatible with different elastomer materials.
Freudenberg-NOK tested the lubricants for compatibility with elastomers, specifically examining the retention of tensile strength and elongation.
The new products are intended for static sealing applications where O-rings must remain in place during installation without being stressed by excessive friction or unsuitable lubricants.
The collaboration between Freudenberg-NOK and Klueber Lubrication combines sealing technology knowledge with specialist lubricant experience.
The resulting product is an installation aid that becomes part of the overall sealing system rather than a separate component.
FN Lube and FN Lube Plus are available through Freudenberg-NOK’s North American distribution network.
The products have been integrated into the company’s digital catalogue and PartnerHUB platform.
Freudenberg-NOK aims to support customers in achieving more reliable installation of O-rings and other static seals.
Consistent installation during series production can help prevent damage, rejects, and later sealing problems.
source_title: FN Lube and FN Lube Plus for O-ring installation

facts: Dr. Mohsen Sohi is retiring on June 30, 2025, and will be succeeded by Claus Möhlenkamp as Chairman of the Management Board and CEO of Freudenberg SE on July 1, 2025.
Claus Möhlenkamp will simultaneously join the Management Board of Freudenberg SE and Freudenberg & Co. Kommanditgesellschaft on January 1, 2025, serving as Deputy Chairman.
Dr. Mohsen Sohi is American and held leadership positions in U.S. companies in the aerospace, automotive, and electronics industries for a decade and a half before joining Freudenberg.
In 2003, Sohi was named President and CEO of the Freudenberg-NOK General Partnership in Plymouth, Michigan, USA.
The Freudenberg-NOK General Partnership is active in the sealing business and is owned by Freudenberg and its longtime Japanese partner NOK.
Sohi strategically realigned the joint venture and expanded activities into new sectors including medical technology and the oil and gas industry.
In 2010, Sohi was appointed to Freudenberg Group’s Management Board in Weinheim as a board member.
Sohi was named Chief Executive of Freudenberg & Co. Kommanditgesellschaft and the newly formed Freudenberg SE in 2012.
Over the past 10 years, the Freudenberg Group has more than doubled its revenue and profits.
The company has increased its research and development investments each year by more than 10 percent to more than 600 million euros annually.
Claus Möhlenkamp has worked in various roles at Freudenberg for more than 30 years.
As CEO of Freudenberg’s largest business group, Freudenberg Sealing Technologies, Möhlenkamp played an important role in the company's development.
Möhlenkamp joined Freudenberg Sealing and Vibration Control Technology as a key account manager for Mercedes-Benz in 1994.
In 2001, Möhlenkamp assumed management responsibilities at the Freudenberg-NOK General Partnership in the United States.
By 2005, he returned to Europe and joined the Management Board of Freudenberg Sealing and Vibration Control Technologies.
In 2007, Möhlenkamp was named CEO of Freudenberg Sealing and Vibration Control Technologies.
In 2011, Möhlenkamp took over global business responsibility for the newly formed Freudenberg Sealing Technologies following the merger of European and American sealing activities.
Under Möhlenkamp’s leadership, Freudenberg Sealing Technologies played a major role in shaping the progress and transformation of the auto industry.
Numerous Freudenberg innovations aim to increase the efficiency of internal combustion engines and sustainably reduce their emissions.
For many years, Freudenberg Sealing Technologies has supported the trend to electric mobility with intelligent solutions.
Freudenberg Sealing Technologies made the transition from a component manufacturer to a system manufacturer in the fuel cell and battery fields, which are key technologies for the future.
Since 2022, Freudenberg has conducted its fuel cell and battery business through the newly formed business group Freudenberg e-Power Systems.
Möhlenkamp is a member of the Freudenberg Executive Council, which takes Business Group views into account at the Group level.
Mathias Thielen serves as Chairman of the Board of Partners for Freudenberg.
The company's sustainability strategy outlines a step-by-step path to climate neutrality by 2045.
Freudenberg & Co. KG is headquartered at Hoehnerweg 2-4, 69469 Weinheim, Germany.
source_title: Change at the helm of the Freudenberg Group

facts: Governor Brian Kemp visited GEDIA Automotive Group in Attendorn, Germany, last Friday with a high-ranking delegation.
The delegation was led by Commissioner Pat Wilson and included representatives of the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
GEDIA is a leading automotive supplier with approximately 4,700 employees worldwide.
GEDIA has operated a state-of-the-art thermoforming plant in Dalton, Georgia since 2021.
GEDIA employs approximately 700 people in North America, with about 65 at the Dalton site.

Even before construction of the plant began, the company maintained close cooperation with the local government in Georgia, which is considered a key success factor in the smooth development of the site.
The visit to Attendorn was intended to further strengthen the excellent relations and emphasize the importance of transatlantic cooperation.
The delegation received a comprehensive tour of the Attendorn thermoforming plant, which is comparable to the site in Dalton, Georgia, in many respects.
Particularly noteworthy was the fact that the tour and the explanations of the individual processes were carried out by plant employees.
This gave Governor Kemp the opportunity to talk directly to the employees who work with the modern facilities on a daily basis.
The Governor was able to ask specific questions about the processes and thus gained a deep insight into the practical work.
Construction of the plant in Dalton, which was carried out under extremely difficult conditions, was a particular success.
Construction began during the Covid-19 pandemic, a time characterized by uncertainties, material shortages and travel restrictions.
On top of that, the company had to overcome a major cyber attack shortly before.
Despite these challenges, GEDIA was able to complete the plant on schedule without delays.
Helmut Hinkel, CEO of the GEDIA Group, stated: “The political environment is changing rapidly, and to remain successful, we need to orient ourselves well and strengthen the right networks,”
The visit was capped off with an evening reception at the historic Schnellenberg Castle.
In this special setting, the participants deepened their conversations and discussed the future development of GEDIA in the USA.
Governor Kemp emphasized: “The relationship between GEDIA and Georgia is a prime example of a successful international partnership,”
Helmut Hinkel agreed: “We are proud of the trust and support we receive from the Georgia government. This is the basis for our success and future growth.”
With the opportunity to triple production capacity in Dalton on the already developed site, GEDIA is optimistic about the future.
The discussions during the visit made it clear how committed and business-friendly the federal state of Georgia is.
Governor Kemp emphasized his openness and determination to attract new investors to the state and to actively support companies.
The comprehensive support that companies like GEDIA receive from the local government throughout the entire investment and development phase is particularly impressive.
Both sides are confident about a long-term and successful cooperation.
source_title: Governor Kemp visits GEDIA in Attendorn: A sign of successful transatlantic cooperation | GEDIA Automotive Group

facts: AAMCO Franchise announced a partnership with its sister company, Global Powertrain Systems (GPS).
The partnership gives AAMCO franchisees immediate access to a vertically integrated supply chain with preferred pricing on the brand’s core transmission category.

Brian O'Donnell, senior vice president of franchise sales at AAMCO Transmission Inc., stated that Global Powertrain Systems allows franchisees to control the cost, quality and speed of delivery on their highest ticket item, transmissions.
The partnership has driven greater efficiencies for the brand.
It provides an opportunity to focus on growing the Total Car Care business and capture more market share in the general automotive repair industry.

Sherri Underwood, co-owner of an AAMCO service center in Levittown, Pa., noted that the partnership makes the business model very simple to operate and easier to run a successful business with less support.
Benefits of the partnership include a better warranty for customers.
The partnership saves salary costs that would otherwise be paid to a builder.
It reduces challenges associated with maintaining a specialized labor workforce.
