facts: The client is a global leader in powertrain technology.
The company is headquartered in the European Union.
The company was built on the industrial expertise and legacy of one of the world’s leading automotive manufacturers.
The company operates manufacturing plants and warehouses across multiple continents.
The company spans design, production, and distribution of powertrain systems.
The group maintains a strong culture of safety, health, and continuous improvement embedded across every level of operations.
Its Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) policies target zero work-related illnesses and accidents.
Dedicated teams are focused on injury prevention and ongoing improvement of workplace conditions.
The client had developed a custom ergonomics standard tailored specifically to their work domain.
The custom ergonomics standard was a rigorous framework that went beyond generic assessment methods.
The process for executing ergonomic assessments remained manual prior to the new platform.
Manual assessments were largely based on Excel spreadsheets and in-person observation by trained experts.
Slow and resource-intensive execution created operational bottlenecks.
Each manual evaluation took approximately 120 minutes per task.
Hundreds of workstations existed across multiple countries.
The assessment backlog grew faster than experts could clear it.
Ergonomics specialists needed to travel to each facility for on-site observation.
Expert travel generated approximately $10,000 per expert per year in travel costs.
Travel dependency limited site coverage.
With EHS teams stretched across safety audits, incident investigations, and compliance, ergonomic assessments were often the first activity deferred.
Deferred assessments left risk unquantified.
The client selected ErgoEdge to transform their ergonomics program from a manual, Excel-driven process to an AI-powered digital platform.
A critical requirement was the ability to digitize and run their proprietary ergonomics standard alongside established open methods.
ErgoEdge digitized the client’s proprietary scoring methodology directly into the platform.
The custom standard runs alongside RULA, REBA, and NIOSH in a single unified environment.
No separate tools or parallel spreadsheets are required.
Frontline supervisors record workers using standard smartphones and tablets.
No wearable sensors, depth cameras, or additional hardware are required.
Videos are uploaded to a secure cloud for AI processing.
Computer vision processes each video frame-by-frame.
The system estimates 3D body joint positions and calculates risk scores.
Comprehensive reports include posture simulation and recommendations.
Experts no longer need to travel for assessments.
Local teams capture video on-site while specialists review findings remotely from anywhere.
The solution was deployed across 5 countries.
Assessments are now 10X faster, taking roughly 10 minutes per task compared to the previous ~120 minutes.
Ergonomic risk assessment transformed from a bottleneck into a routine activity.
EHS teams assess three to four times more workstations, including previously unassessed lower-priority stations and remote sites.
Remote assessment eliminated expert travel, recovering significant travel budget (~$10K saved per expert per year).
Faster assessments, eliminated travel, and reduced consultant dependency substantially lowered per-assessment cost.
Ergonomics became economically scalable.
The digital platform enabled real-time demonstrations and interactive training for new EHS team members.
New assessors can begin conducting assessments independently after a short onboarding session, replacing weeks of shadowing senior ergonomists.
Most AI ergonomics platforms only support standard methods like RULA, REBA, and NIOSH.
This implementation is distinguished by digitizing the client’s proprietary ergonomics standard developed specifically for their work domain and refined over years of internal expertise.
The custom methodology was built directly into the platform alongside established open standards.

A quote states: “ErgoEdge enabled our team to cover three to four times more workstations while reducing each assessment from two hours to just ten minutes. We can now run both our custom standard and established methods on a single platform.”
source_title: Global Powertrain Leader Enables 10X Assessments Using…

entity_name: Frost & Sullivan Global Powertrain Outlook 2020 Study
canonical_name: Frost & Sullivan Global Powertrain Outlook 2020 Study
facts: Frost & Sullivan released the "Global Powertrain Outlook, 2020" study examining global supply and demand trends for efficient powertrains.
Research Analyst Naga Karthik Voruganti stated that growth opportunities in key regional markets will vary considerably.
Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) are banking on the success of full hybrids, which have complied with stricter regulations and demonstrated growing customer confidence.
Engine downsizing will continue while highly efficient Gasoline Direct Injection (GDI) engines witness increased adoption.
Integration of Gasoline Particulate Filters (GPFs) and three-way Catalytic Converters (3WCs) is expected to increase substantially in 2020 as OEMs seek WLTP certification for gasoline vehicles.

Mild-hybrid powertrains and standardization of exhaust after-treatment technologies, including Selective Catalytic Reduction (SCR) and coated GPF, are highlighted as major trends impacting the powertrain industry in 2020.
Hybrid vehicle sales in India recorded a 75 percent growth from 2018.
The rollout of Bharat Stage VI emission norms is expected to negatively impact diesel vehicle prices, causing them to rise.

In the United States, 1.3 million electric and hybrid vehicles are expected to sell in 2020 alone, representing a 4.7 percent growth driven by Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs) and mild and full hybrids.
European electric vehicle (xEV) sales are expected to grow by 5.3 percent, with EVs projected to register a 27.5 percent growth despite moderate Covid-19 impacts.
Latin America countries saw a spike in EV sales driven by hybrids, with EVs and hybrids expected to hold a 1.2 percent market share in 2020.
Despite overall vehicle sales degrowth in China, the EV share is expected to grow from 4.9 percent of sales in 2019 to 5.6 percent in 2020.
In South Korea, mild hybrid electric vehicles (MHEV) in the 48V segment enjoyed a 283 percent growth with sales quadrupling in 2019 following the end of a temporary tax cut in August.
Car sales in Indonesia are expected to register a degrowth of 17.3 percent in 2020 due to Covid-19 impacting the global supply chain.

Japanese brand new passenger car sales increased by 1.9 percent compared to foreign brand sales declining by 3.3 percent, alongside a general trend of vehicle sharing and fading appeal of cars among younger Japanese consumers.

Global automotive sales are predicted to decline by 14.9 percent due to Covid-19 by the end of 2020 as manufacturing facilities and supply chains were hit by worldwide lockdowns, though consumer purchase trends are projected to remain unaffected.
In advanced markets like Europe, demand for diesel engines is expected to decline by 30 percent by the end of 2020 as WLTP commences.
Global EV sales are projected to increase by 3.4 percent, inducing demand for gasoline-hybrid and fully-electric powertrains.
source_title: Global Powertrain Scenario - Auto Components India

facts: Glovis Georgia is building a new facility in West Point.
Residents voiced concerns regarding the new facility.
The West Point City Council discussed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between the city and Glovis Georgia, LLC at its meeting on Tuesday.
source_title: Glovis Georgia building new facility in West Point, resident...

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced on December 18, 2014, that Hyundai Glovis Georgia will relocate its parts consolidation center to West Point.
The relocation will create up to 190 jobs over four years.
The company will develop a new, state-of-the-art 310,080-square-foot sub-assembly and sequencing facility in the Northwest Harris Business Park.
The facility will be used to assemble and distribute components to meet the strict demands and timetables of its suppliers.
Hyundai Glovis Georgia Chief Operating Officer Hong Kee Rhee expressed appreciation for support from West Point, Harris County, and the state of Georgia.
Yoonie Kim, senior project manager on the international investment team at the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), assisted Hyundai Glovis Georgia on behalf of the state.
The City of West Point Development Authority and Georgia Electric Membership Corp also assisted with the project.
Transwestern represented Hyundai Glovis Georgia during the site selection process.
Harris County Commission Chairman Harry Lange welcomed Hyundai Glovis Georgia as a new corporate citizen and noted its commitment to Harris County.
Grand opening for the facility is anticipated in the fall of 2015.

GDEcD Commissioner Chris Carr highlighted Georgia's competitive business climate, solid network of suppliers, direct access to leading original equipment manufacturers, and interconnected transportation infrastructure as key factors for companies like Hyundai Glovis Georgia.
Hyundai Glovis designs logistics strategies tailored to customers' environments and utilizes advanced information systems and logistics technologies.
The company provides total logistics services in domestic and international markets, shipping business operations, and logistics consulting.
source_title: Hyundai Glovis Georgia to create 190 jobs in Harris County – Sowegalive

facts: Glovis America has announced plans to deploy 21 units of the Hyundai Xcient hydrogen fuel-cell trucks to support operations at the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) electric vehicle manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The fuel-cell trucks will transport vehicle parts from HMGMA suppliers to the factory, marking a significant step towards sustainable logistics.
The Xcient hydrogen fuel-cell trucks are part of the Class 8 heavy-duty category and will make up more than one-third of Glovis America’s truck fleet at HMGMA.
Each truck is equipped with two 90 kW hydrogen fuel cell systems, providing a total output of 180 kW.
The trucks are paired with a 350 kW electric motor and offer a range of over 450 miles per charge.
A mobile hydrogen refueling station will be established at the Georgia Megasite to support the trucks.
Plans exist for a permanent hydrogen production and refueling station to be announced in the future.
Oscar Kwon, CEO of HMGMA, stated that HMGMA is committed to creating sustainable transportation solutions.
The Hyundai Xcient hydrogen fuel-cell trucks release zero emissions and offer a cleaner alternative to traditional freight logistics methods used at other manufacturing facilities.
This initiative highlights Hyundai’s ongoing commitment to integrating sustainable solutions in its operations, especially in the supply chain of its manufacturing facilities.
The introduction of hydrogen-powered logistics is expected to play a crucial role in reducing emissions and enhancing the green credentials of Hyundai’s operations in the U.S.
source_title: Hyundai Glovis to Deploy 21 Hydrogen Fuel-Cell Trucks for Metaplant America Logistics

facts: Haering Precision USA LP is located at 50 Anton Haering Street, Lavonia, GA 30553.
The company participated in Hart College and Career Academy's Industry Signing Day for the second consecutive year.
The Industry Signing Day event highlights students who have chosen to enter the workforce after graduation directly or through an apprenticeship or further training program.
The company states that industry participation in schools is key to ensuring the future workforce pipeline for the manufacturing industry.
During March and April, the company focused on connecting with local schools and community partners.
Partner organizations include Hart County Schools, Franklin County High School, Banks County High School, Elbert County High School, Stephens County High School, and North Georgia Technical College.

An employee is traveling to Germany to start an International Training Program.

Jeremiah stated that “the apprenticeship program gave me a career and opportunities I never would have had.”
Jeremiah is now continuing his career with the company in the Production Maintenance department.

National Apprenticeship Week is a nationwide recognition for employers, educators, state agencies, and others to showcase how apprenticeships improve and expand career pathways while helping employers drive economic growth across all industries.
The company recently welcomed members from the Hart County Board of Education, Board of Commissioners, City Council, and the Hart Chamber to its facility.

The company hosted Franklin County Schools 11th graders for their annual Life Skills Day.

The company maintains a continued partnership with Franklin County High School.

facts: German auto parts maker Haring Precision USA is planning a grand opening on November 4 to show off their plant in the Gateway 2 Industrial Park to local business and government leaders.
According to Dr. Jurgen Haring, owner, and president of the Haring Group, the November event will show off their 150,000 sq foot production space containing 40 acres of state-of-the-art machinery.
In 2014, former Gov. Nathan Deal announced Haring’s plan to locate its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Hartwell, creating 800 jobs by 2025 and investing $54 million into the project.
The new facility manufactures fuel injection parts and other precision components for the automotive industry.
Some of the newly created positions include extensive training, as well as learning German, at the company’s headquarters in Germany.
Founded in 1961, Häring has steadily grown to become one of the leading partners of the automotive industry.
Today, at its three state-of-the-art production locations in Germany, Poland, and China, the company manufactures precision components and subassemblies to satisfy the demands of its customers.
Haring is one of more than 120 automotive manufacturers that have invested more than $5 billion in the state of Georgia.
source_title: Haring Schedules Open House for New U.S. Hartwell Plant - 92.1 WLHR

facts: Haering Precision USA LP was named Impact Business of the Year 2024.
The award recognizes companies with more than 50 employees that make a significant economic and social impact through job creation, corporate responsibility, and community engagement.
Dustin Chambers, Commercial Plant Manager at Haering Precision, stated that receiving the Impact Business of the Year award reflects the team’s dedication to making a difference.
Haering Precision emphasizes investing in people, developing talent, investing in future generations, and supporting initiatives that contribute to the well-being of Hart County and beyond.
The award was presented on February 21, 2025, during the Hart County Chamber of Commerce’s Annual Awards Banquet.

Dwayne Dye, former Director of Economic Development for the Hart County Industrial Building Authority, highlighted the company's commitment to workforce development and the creation of long-term career opportunities in his speech.
Dwayne Dye noted that the potential for a long-term positive impact on local students, apprentices, the workforce, and the Northeast Georgia region is only the beginning.
Haering Precision remains committed to education, innovation, and workforce development in the future.
The company's goal is to ensure sustainable growth for both its employees and the entire community.

facts: HEXPOL develops, manufactures, and sells polymer compounds and engineered products.
HEXPOL operates in two business areas: HEXPOL Compounding and HEXPOL Engineered Products.

The HEXPOL Compounding business area offers rubber, thermoplastic elastomer, and thermoplastic compounds for automotive and engineering, construction, consumer and medical technology, cable and water treatment, energy, oil and gas, and general industries.
The HEXPOL Engineered Products business area provides gaskets for plate heat exchangers, seals, as well as polyurethane, plastic, and rubber wheels for forklifts and electric trucks.
source_title: HEXPOL Company Profile - Office Locations, Competitors, Revenue, Financials, Employees, Key People, Subsidiaries | Craft.co

facts: HEXPOL Group's rubber compounding arm announced an investment in a devulcanization line in Europe.
The investment enables HEXPOL Compounding in Europe to produce several thousand tonnes of compounds with devulcanized rubber content per year.
The new extruder will be installed at the HEXPOL Compounding site in the Czech Republic in late 2024.
The devulcanization process uses mechanical forces like shear, compression, and temperature to break down crosslinking bonds of cured rubber with a specific extruder, adding no additional chemicals.
Devulcanized material retains the polymer, carbon black or other fillers, and plasticizer of the original compound, creating a raw material base for new rubber parts.
HEXPOL Compounding will provide devulcanization services to other European HEXPOL Compounding sites to serve a broad customer base.
The company adjusts compound recipes based on devulcanized rubber and other renewable raw materials to achieve the same performance as compounds based on standard raw materials.

Carsten Rüter, President of HEXPOL Rubber Compounding Europe & Asia, stated HEXPOL's target is to reprocess cured rubber from post-industrial sources for typical rubber applications in the automotive or building and construction industry.

Ralph Wolkener, President HEXPOL Compounding Europe & Asia, said customers are becoming business partners by collecting and sorting production side products until HEXPOL transforms them into devulcanized raw materials.
Norbert Niemand, Managing Director at HEXPOL Lesina, confirmed adjusting compound recipes based on devulcanized rubber is part of HEXPOL's service.
source_title: HEXPOL Compounding Invests in Devulcanization, Supports Transition to Circular Economy - Wire & Cable India

facts: HEXPOL is a world-leading polymers group with strong global positions in advanced polymer compounds (Compounding), gaskets for plate heat exchangers (Gaskets), and plastic and rubber materials for truck and castor wheel applications (Wheels).

Customers are primarily systems suppliers to the global automotive and engineering industry, construction industry, the energy sector, medical equipment manufacturers and OEM manufacturers of plate heat exchangers and forklifts.
The Group is organised in two business areas, HEXPOL Compounding and HEXPOL Engineered Products.
The HEXPOL Group has approximately 5,100 employees in fourteen countries.
America showed overall a continued strong development during the fourth quarter of 2022.
Development for HEXPOL Engineered Products was very strong during the fourth quarter of 2022, driven by increased demand for energy saving products.
source_title: Year-end report 2022 - HEXPOL

facts: HEXPOL Americas announces the closure of its production site in Kennedale, Texas by the end of Q2, 2025.
All business from the Kennedale production site will be relocated to other North American facilities.
HEXPOL Americas will work closely with customers to ensure a seamless transition for the Kennedale closure.
Costs associated with the closure of the Kennedale production site will be accounted for in Q4 2024.
The Kennedale closure transaction will be reported separately in Q4 2024.
HEXPOL Americas divested its production site in Muscle Shoals, Alabama effective immediately to private investment firm Karo Ventures LLC.
HEXPOL Americas will work closely with Karo Ventures LLC to ensure a seamless transition for associates, customers, and suppliers at the Muscle Shoals site.
The Muscle Shoals production site manufactured products outside of HEXPOL’s core business.
The Muscle Shoals site had an annual turnover of some 7 MUSD.
The Muscle Shoals site had a profitability level below that of the HEXPOL Group.
The Muscle Shoals divestment transaction will be reported separately in Q4 2024, with minimal impact on the net result.
Gary Moore, President HEXPOL Compounding Americas, stated both decisions are not a reflection of the skills or commitment of the Kennedale and Muscle Shoals workforces.
Gary Moore stated the decisions are necessary to maintain competitiveness and focus on core business to improve supply chain support for customers.
Klas Dahlberg, President and CEO of HEXPOL Group, stated the decisions align with their business model to focus on core business, optimize efficiency, and support customers effectively.
source_title: Hexpol announces the closure of two facilities in the U.S. - Rubber World - The Technical Service Magazine for the Rubber Industry

facts: Hexpol Group is a Swedish compounding group that consolidates production in California into one plant.
Hexpol Group’s first quarter results came in lower than last year, due in part to slow demand from the automotive and construction industries.
For the three months to 31 March, Hexpol Group posted a 4% year-on-year decline in earnings (EBIT) to SEK905 million (€18.4 million) on 11% lower sales of SEK5.3 billion.
Sales were negatively affected by a SEK5 million currency impact, while the acquisition of Star ThermoPlastics helped improve sales by 1%.
The Swedish group’s biggest operation Hexpol Compounding reported a 12% decline in sales to SEK4.9 billion.
Sales to automotive-related customers were sequentially “stable” but were “down slightly” compared to first quarter 2023.
Hexpol linked the decline to “a lower production rate in the automotive industry in the markets where we are active.”
A “similar picture” emerged from sales to customers in the building & construction sector, though with “significantly lower” first quarter sales compared to a year ago.
Sales to consumer-related end customer segments also fell year-on-year, driven by generally lower demand.
Segment earnings decreased by 4% during the quarter to SEK837 billion, but operating margin improved from 15.5% to 16.9% on “better product- and price-mix”.
At Hexpol Engineered Products, segment sales during the first quarter came in on par with those of the corresponding period in 2023.
The operations in America and Asia “developed positively” during the quarter, without further details.
Hexpol Engineered Products also benefited from increased demand from forklift truck applications, though gaskets and other product areas showed slightly lower sales.
Earnings at Hexpol Engineered Products dropped by nearly 7% to SEK68 million, with margins declining from 19.6% to 18.2%.
Group-wide, Hexpol increased sales in Asia by 5% year-on-year, while revenue in Americas and Europe fell 13% and 11% respectively.
Acting chief executive and CFO Peter Rosen commented that demand from customers remained on the same level as the second half of 2023, while sales prices were stable.
Another part of Hexpol's business model is to “continuously assess” production efficiency and cost levels.
Hexpol decided to consolidate its operations in California by closing one of its two factories there and transferring production to the remaining unit.
The consolidation work is progressing according to plan and is expected to be completed after the summer of 2024.
Uncertainty regarding inflation, interest rates, Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, and the situation in the Middle East ‘remained high’.
Hexpol expects its strong customer focus and geographical closeness to customers to enable it to deal with disruptions and further strengthen its market position.
source_title: Hexpol results dip on slow automotive, construction demand | European Rubber Journal

facts: Two more automobile parts suppliers have joined the wave of companies flocking to Georgia to service Hyundai Motor Group’s future Metaplant near Savannah and the expansion to Kia’s West Point factory.
Hwashin, a Korean car part and frame maker, announced Tuesday a $176 million facility in Dublin.
The Hwashin facility is located roughly 100 miles northwest of Hyundai’s future $7.6 billion electric vehicle factory.
On the same day, Korean car seat and arm rest supplier Hyundai Industrial Co. announced a $24 million project near Newnan.
The Hyundai Industrial Co. project will provide parts to manufacturing plants for both Hyundai and Kia.

Robyn Sincere, president of the West Point Chamber of Commerce, stated that while the suppliers still have plenty of work to do before beginning production at their new West Point locations, they are excited about the opportunity to grow jobs in Georgia for Hyundai’s future EV plant.
The announcement comes as Hyundai Motor Group announced a $1.52 billion investment in its future Savannah EV plant in February 2023.
George Kim, president of Hyundai Motor America, said, “We are excited to welcome Hwashin and Hyundai Industrial Co. to Georgia.”
source_title: 2 more Hyundai suppliers announce manufacturing plants in Georgia

facts: Hyundai Industrial intends to invest over $24 million in a new manufacturing plant in Coweta County, Georgia, near Newnan.
The investment is expected to create 100 new jobs.
Governor Kemp affirmed that this project will continue to boost the auto industry and overall state economy.
A partnership with auto giants Kia and Hyundai has drawn many suppliers resulting in job creation across Georgia.
Governor Kemp stated: 'Partners like Kia and Hyundai are attracting a large network of suppliers, creating jobs and opportunity for hardworking Georgians across the entire state.'
Suppliers have pledged nearly 6,000 jobs for Georgia since last year's announcement about the Hyundai Metaplant.
These supplier jobs especially benefit communities outside major urban areas.
Hyundai Industrial is a Korean firm established in 1978 with headquarters in Ulsan.
The company specializes in producing car seats, armrests, and headrests for some of the world's leading auto brands.
Hyundai Industrial expressed excitement about contributing to the e-mobility transition in Georgia and fostering mutually beneficial growth with Coweta County.
Coweta County Board of Commissioners Chairman John Reidelbach welcomed Hyundai Industrial Co., Ltd. to Coweta County.
Chairman Reidelbach stated: 'We are thrilled to welcome Hyundai Industrial Co., Ltd. to Coweta County.'
Reidelbach highlighted that access to I-85 will make transport seamless to nearby automobile plants.
Georgia fosters industry practices, collaboration, and innovation, putting it at the forefront of the automotive and mobility industry.
Georgia has invested heavily across the EV supply chain, resulting in over $25.1 billion in investments and 29,000 job additions since 2020.
Automotive sector jobs went up by 324 percent in fiscal year 2023 compared to fiscal year 2021.
Suppliers flocking to Georgia's mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) contributed over $2 billion in investments.
source_title: Governor Kemp announces Hyundai Industrial investment of $24 million in Georgia, creating 100 new jobs - Peach Tree Times

facts: A creator of seats, arm rests and set out rests toward Kia and Hyundai plans to contribute $24 million and recruit 100 individuals in Coweta Province.
Gov. Brian Kemp announed the plan on Nov. 1.
Hyundai Modern Co. Ltd. is the most recent in march of providers coming to Georgia.
It intends to serve both the extending Kia plant in West Point and Hyundai’s Metaplant under development close to Savannah.

Georgia Division of Financial Improvement Chief Pat Wilson said that with Kia in West Georgia and Hyundai on the eastern coast, suppliers like Hyundai Industrial can locate almost anywhere in the state and still be center of at least two major manufacturers.
Situated in Ulsan, South Korea, Hyundai Modern will enter the U.S. through an auxiliary, HDI America Inc., which has bought a current structure at 83 Amlajack Way close to Highway 85.
The parent organization was laid out in 1978 and works two plants in Korea and two in China.
It has 174 all out representatives and yearly incomes of 264 billion Korean won, or about $200 million, as per its site.
Group Pioneer Woosuck Kim said the organization is “excited to be part of the e-mobility transition happening in Georgia.”
source_title: Investing $24 million and hiring 100 people in Coweta County, a Korean auto supplier - The Open News

facts: Hyundai Industrial Co., Ltd. will invest more than $24 million in a new manufacturing facility near Newnan in Coweta County.
The investment will create 100 new jobs.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that partners like Kia and Hyundai are attracting a large network of suppliers, creating jobs and opportunity for hardworking Georgians across the entire state.

According to Governor Kemp, these suppliers have committed to bringing over 5,300 jobs to Georgia, mostly to communities outside of large urban areas, since last year’s Hyundai Metaplant announcement.
Hyundai Industrial was established in 1978 and is headquartered in Ulsan, Korea.
The company specializes in manufacturing car seats, arm rests, and head rests for the Hyundai and Kia supply chain.

Mr. Woosuck Kim, Team Leader at HDI America Inc., stated that Hyundai Industrial has more than four decades of experience providing comfortable and safe experiences for drivers and passengers with some of the world’s biggest automotive brands.

Mr. Kim added that the company is excited to be part of the e-mobility transition happening in Georgia and looks forward to working with Coweta County to create mutually beneficial growth for the community and future employees.
Hyundai Industrial has purchased an existing building at 83 Amlajack Way in Coweta County.
The purchased building will be retrofitted to produce arm and head rests for OEMs, including Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Hyundai Industrial will focus on filling manufacturing production roles.
Coweta County Board of Commissioners Chairman John Reidelbach stated they are thrilled to welcome Hyundai Industrial Co., Ltd. to Coweta County.
Chairman Reidelbach noted that Hyundai joins the county's thriving manufacturing industries and will have close proximity to I-85, making transport to nearby automobile plants effortless.
Chairman Reidelbach expressed excitement for the opportunities Hyundai will bring as workforce opportunities continue to increase within the community.

Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Coweta County Development Authority and Georgia EMC.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that with Kia in West Georgia and Hyundai on the eastern coast, suppliers like Hyundai Industrial can locate almost anywhere in the state and still be center of at least two major manufacturers.
Commissioner Wilson noted that companies are able to select a community that’s the right fit for them, and vice versa.

Commissioner Wilson described the relationship between Coweta County and Hyundai Industrial as another classic example of Georgia’s partnership approach to economic development, matching a community with an existing asset and plan to a company whose goals aligned.

Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry for over a century.
As the electric vehicle market continues to grow, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25.1 billion in investments and 29,000 jobs since 2020.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to fiscal year 2021.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Hyundai Industry Co., Ltd. aims to manufacture automotive car seats that provide comfort and play a critical role in safety.

Hyundai Industrial creates safer and more comfortable experiences through developing cutting-edge products based on talent and advanced technology while striving for mutually beneficial growth for its employees and the community.

facts: Hyundai announced it will continue expanding its facility in southeast Georgia with a $2.7 billion investment over the next three years.
The expansion will create 3,000 new jobs.
The company's adjacent battery plant construction site was raided this month by federal immigration agents.
The raid led to the detention and deportation of more than 300 South Koreans.
Hyundai's announcement indicates the raid will not deter their plans to invest in the Bryan County site.
The opening of the battery plant has been delayed due to the federal raid.
Didi Caldwell is president and CEO of Global Location Strategies and one of the world's most authoritative site selection and incentive negotiations experts.
Gov. Brian Kemp congratulated Hyundai on the announcement, writing on social media that it reinforces Hyundai's commitment to this project and the state.

Savannah Mayor Van Johnson wrote on social media that the project will bring new opportunities to the workforce, bolster infrastructure, and strengthen Savannah's position as a hub for advanced manufacturing.
The investment announcement comes weeks after a mass immigration raid at the site that rounded up hundreds of workers.
The raid caused diplomatic tension between the U.S. and South Korea.
The raid delayed completion of a Hyundai LG Energy Solutions battery factory.
South Korea's president said companies from his country might be "more hesitant" to invest in the U.S. if they fear difficulty related to immigration.
Georgia has key economic ties to Korean firms including Hyundai, SK Battery, and Qcells.
Didi Caldwell stated that the raid undermines trust in the U.S. administration having Korean interests at heart, which is critical for investment decisions.
Overseas firms sometimes send workers to the U.S. under various short-term visas, often resulting in overstays and an improperly documented workforce.
There is a need for specialized technicians and engineers experienced in these operations to transfer institutional knowledge to the U.S., as companies aim to replicate successful models exactly.
Georgia is a right-to-work state, which tends to foster more friendly labor management relations.
Georgia has a very strong technical college system and a state incentive program called Quick Start to help companies identify, recruit, and train workers.
About 40% of construction workers in the United States are immigrants with various levels of documentation.
Fear of ICE detention is exacerbating labor shortages, causing documented and undocumented workers to miss work or flee due to raid rumors.
Companies investing in the U.S. prioritize certainty, as uncertainty related to immigration, tariffs, and monetary/fiscal policy acts as an enemy of investment.
Scenario planning for investments has grown by an order of magnitude compared to the past.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated he does not expect the Hyundai raid to deter companies from locating in Georgia.
Kemp spoke to reporters on Tuesday at the groundbreaking for the Rivian Electric Vehicle Plant east of Atlanta.
Kemp reported having good conversations with current and prospective companies in Georgia, as well as with the White House regarding visa issues.
Former President Donald Trump campaigned on cutting red tape and improving efficiency regarding visa processing.
source_title: Georgia Today: Hyundai Facility Expansion; Push to End Income Tax; Expand Access to Higher Education | Georgia Public Broadcasting

Governor Brian Kemp stated that partners like Kia and Hyundai are attracting a large network of suppliers, creating jobs and opportunity for Georgians across the state.
Suppliers have committed to bringing over 5,300 jobs to Georgia, mostly to communities outside of large urban areas, since last year's Hyundai Metaplant announcement.

facts: Governor Brian Kemp and GDEcD announced sustained momentum for business recruitment and expansions in Fiscal Year 2024.
Between July 1, 2023, and June 30, 2024, GDEcD’s Global Commerce team supported 429 facility expansions and new locations statewide.
These efforts resulted in more than $20.3 billion in investment and the commitment of 26,900 new private sector jobs statewide.
83% of FY2024 expansions and new locations are directed to communities outside the 10-county Atlanta region, representing over 18,300 new jobs and $12.5 billion in investment.
Manufacturing, including the aerospace, automotive, and e-mobility sectors, accounted for over 50% of expansions or new locations in FY2024.
Kia Georgia added the EV9 to its lineup at its facility in Troup County.

Governor and First Lady Kemp led economic development missions to Korea and Switzerland focusing on strengthening relationships with Hyundai Motor Group and its supplier network.
Georgia landed more than 8,100 new jobs and $5.9 billion in investments from international companies expanding or locating in the U.S. market during FY2024.
The State of Georgia celebrated over 50 years of continuous representation in Europe and Japan, which remain among the top sources of foreign direct investment.
Georgia will mark 40 years of continuous representation in Korea in the coming fiscal year, earning the James A. Van Fleet Award from the Korea Society.
Markets where Georgia maintains business and trade representation accounted for 29% of total new jobs and investments in FY2024.

Business Facilities magazine ranked Georgia as the No. 1 state for EV investments.
Business Facilities magazine ranked Georgia as a top 10 state for food processing, cybersecurity, aerospace and defense, and AI growth hubs.

Lt. Governor Burt Jones emphasized that workforce development through the Technical College System of Georgia, University System of Georgia, College and Career Academies, and private-public partnerships is critical to supporting incoming businesses.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns highlighted historic investments in education, workforce development, and infrastructure as key drivers of economic success.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that Georgia's strong partnerships and diverse industry landscape provide major advantages for businesses.
GDEcD Commissioner Kristi Brigman stated that Georgia is exploring new heights in mobility and technology following a decade of excellence in project milestones.

facts: Troup County comprises 446 square miles in west-central Georgia.
The county is located about 45 minutes south of Atlanta, about 45 minutes to the east of Auburn, Alabama, and about 45 minutes north of Columbus, Georgia.
Troup County has a population of about 70,000.
The local economy shifted from textile manufacturing to the automotive industry, advanced manufacturing, and tourism around 2007-08.
Kia established operations in La Grange around 2007-08.
Kia's North American headquarters is located in Troup County.
The facility produces the Kia Telluride, the upcoming EV9 electric SUV, the Kia Sportage, the K5 (formerly the Optima), and the Kia Sorrento.
Kia employs about 3,000 people in Troup County.
Subsidiary suppliers around Kia include Hyundai and Sewon.
Approximately 13-15,000 employees are directly related to Kia across the county's supplier network.
Troup County serves as the international headquarters for Duracell.
County officials state that the majority of batteries utilized throughout the world are manufactured in Troup County.
The county is expanding its water and sewer infrastructure to support economic development and industrial growth.
Troup County has invested heavily over the past ten years to maintain high-quality roads and corridors for company access.
A CSX and Georgia Ports Authority inland port project is being developed to transport goods from Kia, Duracell, and other suppliers to the Port of Savannah and beyond.
The county hosts three higher education institutions: LaGrange College, Point University, and West Georgia Technical College.
Hyundai Transys Georgia Seating System LLC operates in West Point, Georgia, supplying premium seating solutions to Hyundai, Kia, Lucid, and Rivian.
Hyundai Transys focuses on ergonomic design, cutting-edge technology, and environmental responsibility for automotive seating components.

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America LLC (HMGMA)
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America LLC
facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America LLC (HMGMA) was opened in Ellabell, Georgia by executive chairman Chung Euisun.
The HMGMA project represents Hyundai Motor Group's largest US investment and the largest economic development project in Georgia's history.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated at the opening that the company is “not just cutting ribbons anymore. It’s breaking new ground.”
Hyundai Motor Group completed construction of an $8 billion electric vehicle plant in Georgia with an annual production capacity of 300,000 vehicles.
When combined with its two other US plants, the Georgia facility will allow Hyundai to manufacture at least 1 million cars annually.
Days before the opening, executive chairman Chung visited the White House at President Donald Trump's request to unveil a $21 billion investment package.
The $21 billion package includes the HMGMA expansion and a new vertically integrated electric steel mill built by Hyundai Steel Co. in Louisiana.
Hyundai aims to control its supply chain "from molten metal to mobility" on US soil through these investments.

Hyundai chairman stated during the inauguration ceremony: “With the rich history of craftsmanship and manufacturing in this community, together with the talented workforce at HMGMA, we are building the future of mobility with America, in America.”

Event attendees included Georgia Gov. Brian P. Kemp, US Representative Buddy Carter, Hyundai Motor Group Vice Chair Chang Jae-hoon, Hyundai Motor CEO José Muñoz, Kia CEO Song Ho-sung, and HMGMA employees referred to as “Meta Pros.”
The plant opening coincides with President Trump's announcement of a 25% tariff on imported vehicles taking effect on April 3.
Analysts say Hyundai is preemptively scaling up US-based manufacturing to offset the negative impact of auto tariffs and improve price competitiveness.
Hyundai Motor Group sold 1.71 million vehicles in the US last year, ranking fourth behind General Motors (2.69 million), Toyota (2.33 million), and Ford (2.07 million).
With the Georgia plant, Hyundai aims to overtake Ford and break into America’s automotive Big Three.
Globally, Hyundai ranks third in vehicle sales behind Toyota and Volkswagen.
The HMGMA facility spans 11.8 million square meters, roughly four times the size of Seoul’s Yeouido financial district.
The plant will be ramped up to produce 500,000 units a year within the next few years.

Combined with Hyundai's Alabama factory (360,000 unit capacity) and Kia's Georgia plant in West Point (340,000 unit capacity), the group is poised to build up to 1.2 million vehicles annually in the US.
This combined US production capacity will be equivalent to 70% of Hyundai's 2024 sales in the market.
In October 2024, less than two years after breaking ground, the first electric vehicle (a Hyundai IONIQ 5) rolled off HMGMA’s assembly line.
The Hyundai IONIQ 9 three-row electric SUV is now in production at HMGMA and will be joined in 2026 by the first Kia model built at the facility.
In addition to EVs for Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia, the plant is equipped to produce hybrid vehicles.
Kia CEO Song stated that Kia will account for roughly 40% of HMGMA’s production, mostly hybrids.
All HMGMA processes including order collection, procurement, logistics, and production are optimized using AI and real-time data.
The facility integrates cutting-edge robotics and vision systems to enhance automation, including automated guided vehicles for logistics.
Boston Dynamics’ robot dog Spot is used for exterior quality inspection in the weld shop, and parking robots operate in the assembly shop.
Automation in HMGMA’s assembly line is nearly double that of Hyundai's existing facilities.
A solar parking lot at the site generates 5.2 megawatts of energy, accounting for roughly 5% of Metaplant’s total power consumption.
Hyundai's investment in Georgia related to HMGMA and joint battery ventures with LG Energy Solution Ltd. and SK On Co. totals $12.6 billion.
The project includes a total of 8,500 jobs at HMGMA by 2031, according to Hyundai.

Total investments in Georgia are expected to create nearly 40,000 direct and indirect jobs and generate $4.6 billion in individual earnings every year, according to the Center for Automotive Research.
Under the announced $21 billion US investment plan through 2028, $9 billion will be used to expand HMGMA facilities.
$6 billion will be allocated to enhance parts and logistics business and establish an advanced steel mill in the US with an annual production capacity of 2.7 million tons.
The new steel mill will supply high-quality automotive steel plates to HMGMA when commercial production begins in 2029.
An additional $6 billion will drive innovation and expand strategic partnerships with US companies in autonomous driving, robotics, AI, and advanced air mobility (AAM).
Through the $21 billion commitment, Hyundai expects to create 14,000 new direct full-time jobs in the US by 2028.
The overall economic impact is expected to generate more than 100,000 direct and indirect job opportunities across related industries.
source_title: Hyundai’s new American dream comes with new plant, more spending - KED Global

facts: South Korea-based automotive component supplier Hyundai Mobis has introduced new battery cooling technology using pulsating heat pipes to prevent EV batteries from overheating during ultra-fast charging.
The new insulation material is known as a pulsating heat pipe (PHP) and is made of aluminum alloy and coolant.
Heat pipes are also used in devices like computer CPUs and smartphones, where the metal tube-shaped thermal conductors improve heat transfer efficiency and serve as high-heat-dissipation materials.
Battery systems (BSA) are usually made by adding battery management systems (BMS), cooling fans, and other electronics to multiple battery modules (BMA).
The BMA generates electrical energy and consists of stacked battery cells, making it essential to optimize the cooling structure to prevent overheating.
Hyundai Mobis's system differs from traditional thermal management by placing pulsating heat pipes between battery cells to rapidly transfer heat to cooling blocks.

This placement ensures stable temperature control at the module level and is expected to significantly reduce electric vehicle charging time by effectively withstanding heat during ultra-fast charging.
Pulsating heat pipes work by distributing heat through the vibration and circulation of coolant, maintaining efficiency with minimal gravity impact even in fast-moving vehicles.
The PHPs have over ten times the heat transfer capacity of standard aluminum and swiftly dissipate heat from overheated battery cells to the vehicle's exterior.
Hyundai Mobis will employ a molding process to build the system, enabling large-scale continuous production and streamlining the PHP manufacturing process.
The new molding process aims to lower production costs for the thermal management system.
To ease installation on vehicle batteries, the company produced PHPs with a thickness of just 0.8 mm.
The 0.8 mm PHPs are significantly thinner than standard heat pipes, which are around 6 mm thick, offering a larger surface area to support wider EV adoption.
The company states its approach directly addresses two key consumer concerns: charging time and safety, both crucial for widespread electric vehicle adoption.
Hyundai Mobis is also known for producing various components for electrification, brakes, chassis and suspension, steering, airbags, lighting, and automotive electronics.
Last year, Hyundai Mobis introduced its e-Corner System, which allows all four wheels to turn 90 degrees.
The e-Corner System helps cars perform parallel parking in tight spaces by rotating front wheels inward and rear wheels outward.
The e-Corner System supports a 360-degree idle turn and a 180-degree maneuver to reverse from a dead end, offering improved maneuverability for parking and navigating narrow areas.
source_title: Hyundai Mobis tackles EV battery overheating with ‘pulsating heat pipes’

facts: Hyundai is investing an additional $5 billion to ramp up production of vehicles in the US.
The new funding brings Hyundai's total investment in the US to $26 billion.
The investment will be deployed over the next three years (2025–2028) to boost production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis vehicles.
Hyundai is building a new state-of-the-art robotics facility and steel plant in Louisiana.
Hyundai is also building a new robotics innovation hub to design, manufacture, and deploy vehicles.
The new robotics facility is expected to create about 25,000 jobs in the US over the next four years.
The robotics facility will have an annual production capacity of 30,000 units.
In the first half of 2025, Hyundai and Kia sold more vehicles than in any first half since entering the US market nearly 40 years ago.
Hyundai and Kia cumulatively sold a combined 1.5 million "eco-friendly" vehicles in the US this week (August 2025).
The Hyundai Tucson Hybrid and Kia Niro Hybrid are the brands' top-selling eco-friendly cars in the US.
The all-electric Hyundai IONIQ 5 remains one of the top-selling EVs in the US and is the brand's fourth most popular eco-friendly vehicle.

facts: Hyundai initially failed to qualify for the federal EV tax credit.
Hyundai is intensifying efforts to regain eligibility by ramping up U.S. production and sourcing more EV battery components domestically.
Hyundai’s new Metaplant in Georgia is central to its strategy of manufacturing U.S.-made EVs.
The automaker initially expected American-made vehicles, including the 2025 IONIQ 5 and IONIQ 9, to qualify for the federal tax credit.
Hyundai’s EVs have been excluded from the federal tax credit since the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) was enacted in 2022.
The Department of Energy (DOE) released its list of EVs eligible for the federal tax credit in mid-January.
Most of Hyundai’s EV portfolio was notably absent from the DOE's eligibility list.
Hyundai’s luxury subsidiary brand, Genesis, also did not meet the requirements to qualify for the credit.
The only Hyundai Motor Group vehicles that made the cut on the DOE list were the 2025 Kia EV9 and EV6.
The specific reasons for Hyundai’s exclusion have not been officially confirmed.
It is widely believed that Hyundai’s exclusion stems from the IRA’s new battery-sourcing rules.
These IRA regulations require a higher percentage of battery components to be sourced from the U.S. or its trade partner countries.
Hyundai plans to increase U.S. production by mass-producing the 2025 IONIQ 5 at its Georgia-based Metaplant to regain eligibility.
SK Battery America (SKBA), the U.S. subsidiary of SK On, will produce batteries for both Hyundai and Kia EVs.
This battery production strategy further aligns with IRA requirements.
Until Hyundai’s vehicles become eligible for the tax credit, the automaker will continue passing the $7,500 credit on to consumers through leasing options.
Potential changes in federal policy, such as President Donald Trump’s proposed plan to scrap EV incentives, could disrupt Hyundai’s strategy and plans.

facts: Hyundai TRANSYS Georgia Seating System LLC will invest $9 million to expand its production operations in West Point, Georgia.

Hyundai TRANSYS's Georgia manufacturing center produces seats and seat foam pads for the Hyundai Santa Fe, KIA Sorento, electric vehicles, and other car models.
The company also produces automobile parts including transmissions and axles for Hyundai vehicles.

facts: Hyundai Transys, an automotive parts producing unit under Hyundai Motor Group, announced it will invest $9 million in expanding its seating manufacturing plant in Georgia, the United States.

Previously, the company announced a $240 million investment plan for expansion of its powertrain plant in Georgia in December last year.

facts: Textron, Inc. is a multi-industry company that leverages its global network of aircraft, defense, industrial, and finance businesses to provide customers with innovative solutions and services.
The company operates under brands such as Bell, Cessna, Beechcraft, Pipistrel, Jacobsen, Kautex, Lycoming, E-Z-GO, Arctic Cat, and Textron Systems.
Textron started as a small New England business in 1923 and has grown into a $12.9 billion company with 34,000 employees.
The company serves customers in industries including aerospace and defense, specialized vehicles, turf care, and automotive systems.
Product development pipelines include the evolution of aircraft, rotorcraft, armored vehicles, electrical vehicles, and automotive systems.
As part of its Archive 2025 initiative, Textron established a new set of 5-year sustainability goals focused on greenhouse gas emissions, energy use, water use, and waste generation.
Textron aims to reduce greenhouse gas emission intensity by 20% by the end of 2025 compared to its 2019 baseline year.
Textron aims to reduce energy use intensity by 10% by the end of 2025 compared to its 2019 baseline year.
Textron aims to reduce water use intensity by 10% by the end of 2025 compared to its 2019 baseline year.
Textron aims to reduce waste generation intensity by 10% by the end of 2025 compared to its 2019 baseline year.
Textron is classified under NAICS code 336, Transportation Equipment Manufacturing.
Textron pledged to the Better Plants Challenge in December 2019.
source_title: Textron, Inc. | Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Vehicle Technologies Office (VTO) partners with the National Park Service (NPS) through the Clean Cities National Parks Initiative.

The initiative supports transportation projects that educate park visitors on the benefits of shifting to affordable, domestic alternative fuels, advanced vehicles, and fuel-saving technologies and strategies.

Since 2010, VTO and Clean Cities and Communities coalitions have partnered with NPS on 35 projects to put alternative fuel and fuel-efficient vehicles on the road, reduce vehicle idling, and improve vehicle efficiency.
These actions support and coincide with NPS's efforts to reduce vehicle congestion, air pollution, and resource degradation, and to preserve the long-term quality of the parks.
source_title: Clean Cities and Communities: National Parks Initiative

facts: Mando Corporation was selected as General Motors' (GM) "Supplier of the Year 2021".
The 30th edition of the Supplier of the Year Awards ceremony took place on March 17 (local time) in Phoenix, Arizona, United States.
134 suppliers from 16 countries, including Mando America Corporation, participated in the awards and received their prizes.
The award recognizes exceptional suppliers in the top 1% tier, selected from approximately 20,000 suppliers worldwide.
Mando has been recognized as GM's best supplier eight times since 2003, including this award.

Supplier selection was based on company performance metrics such as launch, supply chain, and quality, along with qualitative evaluation scores for innovation and engineering, communication and transparency, and safety.
Mando achieved the highest score in the cultural priorities category.
Mando also won the "Launch Excellence Award" at the 29th special award ceremony for delivering electric parking brakes according to the supply schedule despite a 165 km/h hurricane in September 2020.

Shilpan Amin, GM's Vice President of International Purchasing, stated that this year's event was special due to the program's 30th anniversary and provided an opportunity to recognize supplier perseverance during one of the industry's most difficult years.

Amin noted that these leading suppliers demonstrated resilience and strengthened their commitment to sustainability and innovation, with GM and its suppliers ready to build a better future through strong relationships and collaboration.

Mando CEO Cho Seong-hyeon stated he was very happy and encouraged to receive the 30th award, adding that they will overcome greater difficulties through joint efforts of leaders and employees worldwide, including union members, despite an unclear market situation.
Mando Corporation (KS 204320) is a leading global supplier of auto parts specializing in hybrid electric vehicle solutions and autonomous driving.
The company received an A rating in MSCI environmental, social, and governance assessments in 2021.
Mando was founded in 1962 with its headquarters located in Seoul, South Korea.

The company provides advanced driving systems including braking, steering, suspension, propulsion, and power solutions, as well as software solutions for automakers and new mobility service providers.

Mando's technologies span three fields: integrated vehicle safety, autonomous driving, and electric mobility, which provide vehicles with intuitive detection, decision-making, and intelligent movement capabilities.
Mando serves more than 60 clients from over 50 sites worldwide, encompassing local and global automakers in all major regions.
source_title: Mando Corporation, sélectionné comme « fournisseur de l'année 2021 » par General Motors (GM)

facts: Mercedes-Benz will move up to 500 corporate, technical and high-tech roles to its existing “1MB” facility in Sandy Springs.
Mercedes-Benz will move up to 500 corporate, technical and high-tech roles to a new, multi-million dollar Research & Development hub in Metro Atlanta.
The announcement establishes Atlanta as Mercedes-Benz’s headquarters in North America.
This centralizes and unites key corporate functions driving the success of the U.S. market.
Mercedes-Benz will establish Atlanta as its headquarters in North America by moving additional corporate roles to the “1MB” facility in Sandy Springs, Ga. from Farmington Hills, Mich.
The company is making a multi-million dollar investment in a state-of-the-art Research & Development hub in Metro Atlanta for technical teams from various U.S. locations.
The move of up to 500 roles further expands Mercedes-Benz’s longstanding presence in the Atlanta area.
Jason Hoff, CEO, Mercedes-Benz North America, thanked the State of Georgia for its support in deepening Mercedes-Benz’s roots in the Atlanta area.
The move is expected to be completed by August 2026.
Mercedes-Benz opened its 1MB facility in 2018.
Currently, there are approximately 800 Mercedes-Benz employees in Atlanta.
With today’s announcement, up to 500 additional roles will move to Georgia.
Brian Kemp, Governor of the State of Georgia, stated that Georgia continues to lead the way in the future of mobility and technical innovation, attracting world-class companies like Mercedes-Benz.
Mercedes-Benz is co-locating its Research & Development and customer lifecycle-focused activities in the U.S.
Financial services teams and corporate functions based in Farmington Hills will be co-located with current sales teams in Sandy Springs.
Technical teams from various U.S. locations will move to a new Research & Development hub near Sandy Springs.
The strategic investment in the Atlanta region will allow the company to leverage the rich engineering talent pool around institutions like Georgia Institute of Technology.
The company aims to create new opportunities to collaborate with the region’s thriving startup ecosystem and established tech sectors.
Mercedes-Benz announced it will bring production of the GLC SUV to the company’s Tuscaloosa, Ala. facility.
The U.S. is a vital market for Mercedes-Benz, with sales of 374,100 vehicles, both passenger cars and vans, last year.
The company is responsible for 11,100 direct jobs in the U.S., of which around 8,000 are in manufacturing.
There are approximately 112,000 jobs across Mercedes-Benz’s business suppliers and service providers.
Mercedes-Benz has a heritage dating back to 1888 in Long Island City, NY.
As a major local investor, Mercedes-Benz has committed over $10 billion to production, infrastructure, R&D, design, and the dealer network.
This includes $7+ billion in its Tuscaloosa, Ala., site and $560+ million in its Charleston, S.C., plant.
The company supports more than 163,000 jobs across the country, including 11,100 direct jobs (of which around 8,000 are in manufacturing) and 28,000 dealership employees.
Its nationwide presence currently spans 385 dealer partners and 24 corporate locations in 13 states.
More than 4.5 million vehicles have left the Tuscaloosa plant over the past 28 years.
Roughly sixty percent of annual output from Tuscaloosa is exported.
The United States is the second-largest global market for Mercedes-Benz Sprinter vans.
Since 2006, Mercedes-Benz Vans, LLC (MBV Charleston) has assembled more than 400,000 vans for delivery across North America.
Mercedes-Benz works with around 400 suppliers in the United States.
The company is sourcing local demand for steel and aluminum almost exclusively in the United States.
The Tuscaloosa operation is the global hub for its SUVs (GLE, GLS, GLE Coupe, and Mercedes-Maybach GLS as well as the EQS SUV, EQE SUV, and the Mercedes-Maybach EQS SUV).
Mercedes-Benz is making electric vehicle (EV) infrastructure investments of approximately $1.2 billion to expand a high-speed charging network in North America over the next six to seven years.
The company launched its first EV charging location in Sandy Springs, Ga. in November 2023.
In 2024, Mercedes-Benz sold 324,500 passenger cars and 49,600 vans in the U.S.
The United States is the second-largest global market for Mercedes-Benz cars and vans.
Mercedes-Benz currently supports over 700,000 finance and lease customers in the United States.
The company maintains a portfolio of over $40 billion.
The company has filed nearly 100 technology patents from the U.S.
Mercedes-Benz introduced DRIVE PILOT, the first SAE-Level 3 system for conditionally automated driving approved for Nevada and California.
The company actively supported communities with nearly 40,000 hours of company-supported volunteer work in 2024 alone.
Mercedes-Benz AG is part of the Mercedes-Benz Group AG with a total of around 175,000 employees worldwide.
Ola Källenius is Chairman of the Board of Management of Mercedes-Benz AG.
The company focuses on development, production and sales of passenger cars, vans and vehicle-related services.
It aspires to be the leader in electric mobility and vehicle software.
Product portfolio includes Mercedes-AMG, Mercedes-Maybach, G-Class (all-electric models), and smart brand.
In 2024 it sold around 2.4 million passenger cars and vans globally.
It has more than 30 production sites on four continents.
It is constructing and extending its global battery production network on three continents.
