facts: Mercedes is establishing a new Research & Development hub outside Atlanta.
The automaker is consolidating its U.S. workforce, with 500 jobs moving to Georgia.
The consolidation and hub establishment are expected to enable the company to be faster and more agile.
The new R&D hub represents a multi-million dollar investment in Georgia.
Georgia is home to Mercedes' U.S. headquarters and the Mercedes-Benz Stadium.
Mercedes opened its 1MB facility in Sandy Springs seven years ago.
The 1MB facility currently houses around 800 employees.
Up to 500 additional employees are coming to Georgia, with arrivals expected by August 2026.
Mercedes is co-locating its U.S. R&D and customer activities to “enhance its focus on both product excellence and customer experience.”
Financial services teams and corporate functions currently based in Farmington Hills, Michigan, will move to Sandy Springs.
Technical teams from across the United States will also relocate to the new R&D hub near Sandy Springs.
Mercedes stated the location will allow it to “leverage the rich engineering talent pool around institutions like Georgia Institute of Technology.”
Mercedes stated the location will allow it to “create new opportunities to collaborate with the region’s thriving startup ecosystem and established tech sectors.”
Mercedes-Benz North America CEO Jason Hoff said the move “strengthens our position for continued growth and reinforces our established commitment to the U.S. market.”
Jason Hoff added that bringing teams closer together will enable them to be more agile, increase speed to market, and ensure the best customer experience.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp praised the move, stating Georgia continues to lead in mobility and attract world-class companies like Mercedes-Benz.
Governor Brian Kemp noted they are excited that a job creator with close ties to Georgia is doubling down on its presence in the state.
Hyundai Motor Group's Metaplant America recently began production outside Savannah.
Rivian’s factory is advancing with a loan of up to $6.57 billion from the Biden Administration.
Mercedes called the United States a “vital market” that generated over 374,000 sales last year.
Mercedes employs around 11,100 people in America.
A majority of Mercedes' U.S. employees build vehicles at its plant in Tuscaloosa, Alabama.
Mercedes recently announced plans to add the GLC model to its Tuscaloosa, Alabama plant.
The Tuscaloosa plant currently produces the GLE, GLE Coupe, GLS, EQE SUV, and EQS SUV.
source_title: Mercedes Invests In Georgia For Its Next Big Thing | Carscoops

facts: German luxury automaker Mercedes-Benz signed a multimillion-dollar deal aimed at further cementing its relationship with Atlanta, home of its new U.S. headquarters.
Mercedes-Benz bought the naming rights to the still-under construction $1.4 billion stadium that will be home to the NFL’s Atlanta Falcons and MLS’s Atlanta United.
Steve Cannon, CEO of Mercedes-Benz USA, stated that buying the naming rights to the new Falcons stadium is the largest marketing deal in the company’s history.
Officials were mum about the cost to Mercedes-Benz for the near-record 27-year naming rights at Monday’s press conference.

Donna Boland, manager of corporate communications for Mercedes-Benz USA LLC, confirmed that since Cannon has been with the company for 24 years, he can be trusted that this is the biggest marketing initiative they have undertaken.
As a matter of policy, Mercedes-Benz does not disclose marketing or sponsorship costs for this deal or others.
Experts contacted by PolitiFact estimated the annual value of the naming rights ranged from $8 million to $20 million.

Don Muret of Sports Business Journal put the price of the Atlanta naming rights at $12 million a year, essentially double what Mercedes-Benz paid in 2011 for the naming rights to the Superdome for 10 years.
Andrew Zimbalist, professor of economics at Smith College, valued the deal at $8 million to $10 million a year, or minimally $216 million for 27 years.

Joel Maxcy, associate professor at Drexel University, projected the highest value at $20 million a year, citing national and regional television exposure and stadium visibility to commuters and travelers.
Victor Matheson, professor of economics at College of Holy Cross, expected the naming rights went for about $11 million.
Mercedes-Benz previously had naming rights in New Orleans costing $60 million.
The company also has its names on arenas in Shanghai, China, and Stuttgart, Germany.
Sports Business Journal reported Mercedes-Benz signed the Shanghai deal in 2009 for 10 years at $6 million a year, totaling $60 million.
The automaker spends about $340 million a year in the U.S. on advertising across all media, according to Kantar Media.

Parent company Daimler estimated U.S. ad spending of $561.9 million in 2014, primarily for its Mercedes-Benz business, while also marketing trucks and buses under brands including Freightliner, Thomas Built Buses, Western Star, and Fuso.
Bradley Johnson, director of data analytics for Advertising Age, noted that the Atlanta stadium deal will likely account for just a small part of the automaker’s annual marketing budget.
source_title: PolitiFact | Mercedes-Benz on target with superlative about Atlanta stadium rights

facts: Mercedes-Benz USA recently announced it would move its headquarters from Montvale, NJ to the Atlanta area.
The relocation will bring at least 800 jobs to Georgia.
Large incentive packages dominated the discussion for other recent automotive headquarters moves, including Subaru’s commitment to Camden, NJ and Toyota’s planned move to Plano, TX.
Mercedes-Benz USA cited locating closer to the strong and growing customer base in the southeast as a key factor supporting its location choice.
Additional factors cited by Mercedes-Benz USA included talent and quality of life, cost savings, and business efficiency.

CEO Steve Cannon stated that incentives are just a small piece of the picture when making a 50-year decision, noting that "a pile of incentives in Year One, Two or Three over a 50-year decision doesn’t make a gigantic impact."

The company's strategy emphasizes a long-term outlook, growth especially among younger, millennial buyers, emphasis on customer service and digital platforms, constant focus on efficiency, and a recent pattern of consolidating regional operations.
Economic developers aware of the company’s non-cash, non-incentive factors would be better prepared to present their communities in a light more appealing than simply offering a “pile of incentives.”
Many articles cited New Jersey’s tax climate and high costs as the reason behind Mercedes-Benz USA's move.
Georgia has lower taxes than New Jersey, but the Tax Foundation ranks the state 32nd in the country for tax climate for business.
Last summer, the New Jersey Assembly approved a bill related to manufacturer-dealership regulations that angered New Jersey-based car companies.
Jaguar Land Rover stated that if the NJ bill was enacted, “we would be actively looking at whether there is a more business-friendly environment in a neighboring state.”
The CEOs of other car companies based in New Jersey all signed a letter opposing the bill, which did not become law.
Focusing economic development efforts exclusively on incentives and taxes will mean missed opportunities and lost business.
source_title: 16442557-3-things-the-mercedes-hq-move-tells-us-about-incentives - Smart Incentives

facts: Michelin tread technologies are designed to deliver maximum traction, longer tire life, and consistent performance across challenging road and weather conditions.
From advanced tread patterns to innovative siping designs, Michelin tread solutions help fleets improve safety, efficiency, and return on investment.
The exclusive B² technology from Michelin makes the bead area more robust to increase the service life of tires in mines, quarries, construction, and handling operations.
Tires with Diamond Tread Pattern technology feature a wide metal bead wire that increases the area in contact with the rim.
The enhanced strength of the bead area results in fewer rotations on the rim.
With the Michelin diamond tread pattern, 44%1 of the tread is always in contact with hard surfaces, while with a bar tread pattern, only 29%1 of the tread is always in contact.
The larger contact area provides exceptional traction because two sections of lug are always in contact with the surface.
Even pressure distribution across the tire surface helps maintain stability on various terrains, resulting in enhanced robustness and wear resistance.
Matrix Siping technology features an innovative interlocking 3-dimensional matrix siping with zig-zag walls that lock together when in contact with the road.
Sipes provide hundreds of biting edges to improve wet and snow traction.
Interlocking sipes add strength and stability normally associated with solid tread blocks to improve wear for longer-lasting performance.
Michelin-patented Regenion technology uses 3D metal printing techniques to provide solid grip over the tire’s lifetime in all conditions.
As the tire wears, new grooves emerge in Regenion designs.
The compact and rigid tread pattern of Regenion provides higher mileage, more consistent wear, and lower fuel consumption.
In 2018, two Michelin engineers behind Regenion technology won the European Inventor Award in the industry category from the European Patent Office (EPO).
New grooves opening as the tread wears provide long-lasting control in all weather conditions throughout the tire's life.
The rigid tread pattern leads to more consistent wear and longer mileage.
Compact tread designs lower rolling resistance, helping to reduce fuel consumption.
Stubble Shield technology includes an anti-stubble strip, oriented lugs that deflect stubble, and rubber components that prolong the life of agricultural tires.
MICHELIN tires with Stubble Shield technology are identified with a dedicated pictogram.
Tires with Stubble Shield technology provide extra protection against stubble damage and support tire lifespan.
Agricultural tires resistant to damage from residual stubble reduce downtime caused by punctures, allowing confident operation on any terrain.
Michelin’s tread innovations are engineered for traction, durability, and efficiency in every condition.
Tread patterns determine how the tire grips the road, with Michelin’s Diamond Tread Pattern increasing surface area contact to improve traction on hard surfaces.
Specialized siping enhances grip in wet or snowy conditions while helping to maintain tire flexibility in cold temperatures.
Regenion designs feature tread grooves that evolve as the tire wears, maintaining grip over the lifespan for longer-lasting traction and better fuel efficiency without sacrificing mileage.

Different tread designs are tailored for specific uses: Regenion is ideal for long-haul trucks, Matrix Siping is perfect for mixed climates, Diamond Tread Pattern excels on hard surfaces, and Stubble Shield is designed for agricultural tires.
source_title: Michelin Tread Technologies

facts: Michelin announced the full deployment of TreadEye, a proprietary technology developed to enhance the retread process and deliver measurable value to fleets.
After a period of phased implementation, TreadEye is now available across the Michelin Retread Technologies franchise network in the United States.
TreadEye is an industry-leading tread depth assessment tool that helps fleets maximize their tire investment—because every 32nd of an inch counts.
The system accurately and consistently collects 1,200 measurement points, providing precise insights into tread wear and casing condition.

Nate Kirian, Vice President of Sales and Distribution, Commercial Business Lines for Michelin North America, Inc., stated that TreadEye is a game-changer for retread operations and fleets served, highlighting how Michelin continues to innovate to directly benefit customers.

Key benefits of TreadEye include identification of pull points by fleet location; maximization of rubber on the road by avoiding early pulls; helps in prevention of casing damage, helps reduce DOT violations, and downtime from late pulls; and improved efficiency in the tire removal process, directly impacting a fleet’s bottom line.

links.context: Learn more about Michelin Retread Technologies

source_title: MICHELIN ANNOUNCES FULL ROLLOUT OF TREADEYE TECHNOLOGY ACROSS U.S. RETREAD NETWORK :: Michelin North America, Inc.

facts: Michelin Retread Technologies (MRT) is increasing its production capacity, adding a new press at its Covington GA retreading operation.
The new press is capable of producing one million new treads per year.

The new MRT press has been designed to produce new high-technology treads and to take advantage of Michelin's advanced co-extrusion technique, a process where two different rubber compounds are combined to ensure longer life and cooler casing temperatures, maximizing the life of the casing.
Michelin is currently the only manufacturer in the world offering co-extruded treads to its retread customers.
The new press also will allow the company to expand its offering of wider treads for Michelin's X One wide-single tire.
Expansion of the Covington tread pressing plant is the first major expansion since the facility opened in 1999.
The new retreading press along with other future additions is expected to double the volume of the facility.
source_title: Michelin installs new retread press at plant | Trailer Body Builders

facts: Michelin is a 130-year-old company known for premium tires with longer wear and solid performance.
The company has focused on innovating tire compounds, renewable and sustainable materials, tire design, and wearability.
Michelin aims to produce tires that are completely recyclable and 3D printable.
Some estimates attribute more than 70% of microplastics found in water to tire dust.
A few years ago, the company introduced the CrossClimate2 tire designed to go 60,000 miles.
The CrossClimate2 features a novel chevron-style tire tread and new compounds that are softer and more pliable yet longer lasting even on hot summer roads.
CrossClimate2 tires are designed to perform as well on wet pavement as dry roads.
Longer wearability from CrossClimate2 is intended to reduce pollution by shedding less tire dust and microplastics into water and reducing worn tires sent to landfills.
Following the CrossClimate2, Michelin introduced the X-Ice Snow+ tire.
The X-Ice Snow+ breaks apart longer tread rows into an interlocking lattice-design tread pattern to create more sipes that absorb snow and slush or disperse water.
The new tread pattern and compounds for the X-Ice Snow+ are designed to deliver better fuel economy and better range for EV drivers.
Winter tires like the X-Ice Snow+ can add capability to rear wheel drive cars, allowing small sports cars like a Porsche 911 to drive on snowy, slushy roads.
Michelin used "piano acoustic tuning" to reduce noise from the X-Ice Snow+ tires, utilizing the directional tread of the lattice design to allow stickiness while reducing noise.

Drivers typically swap all-season tires for winter tires in the Fall and change them back in the Spring, but putting them on too early or leaving them on too long can destroy a set of tires or reduce the tread life.
Michelin says the tread life on the X-Ice tires should last longer, giving owners more time to make the switch in warm weather and potentially more winters to drive on them.
The X-Ice tires feature a chevron-angled lattice pattern with an distinct and elegant look.
The X-Ice tires do not go on sale until the Spring.
Michelin expects competitors and copy-cats to follow its lead in tire tread design and performance specs.
source_title: Michelin’s New Tire Designed For  Better Performance On-Road—And Snow

facts: Michelin announces the full deployment of TreadEye, a proprietary technology developed to enhance the retread process and deliver measurable value to fleets.
After phased implementation, TreadEye is now available across the Michelin Retread Technologies franchise network in the United States.
TreadEye is an industry-leading tread depth assessment tool that helps fleets maximize their tire investment because every 32nd of an inch counts.
The system accurately and consistently collects 1,200 measurement points to provide precise insights into tread wear and casing condition.

“TreadEye is a game-changer for our retread operations and for the fleets we serve,” said Nate Kirian, Vice President of Sales and Distribution, Commercial Business Lines for Michelin North America, Inc.
Key benefits of TreadEye include identification of pull points by fleet location.
The technology helps maximize rubber on the road by avoiding early pulls.
TreadEye helps prevent casing damage, reduces DOT violations, and minimizes downtime from late pulls.
The system improves efficiency in the tire removal process, directly impacting a fleet’s bottom line.

entity_name: Martinez v. Mobis Alabama, LLC Settlement
canonical_name: Martinez v. Mobis Alabama, LLC et al.
facts: A purported class action lawsuit was filed against Mobis Alabama, LLC d/b/a Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, Inc., GB2G, Inc. d/b/a Allswell, SPJ Connect, Inc., Total Employee Solution Support, LLC (TESS), and Youngjin Lee.
The lawsuit alleges the defendants misrepresented job terms offered to TN visa holders and violated state and federal statutes regarding recruiting, employment, and workplace practices.
Affected workers were recruited by TESS, SPJ, or Allswell and assigned to work at Kia Georgia’s or Mobis’s West Point, Georgia locations.
The relevant period for affected workers who received wages from Allswell is August 11, 2018 through the present.
Eligible class members may receive an approximate settlement payment of between $4,100.00 and $16,800.00.
The defendants disagree on whether their recruiting and employment practices were proper but agreed to resolve the dispute through a settlement.
The U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Georgia (Newnan Division) granted final approval of the settlement on May 29, 2026.
Class members have legal options to remain in the settlement class, exclude themselves from the agreement, object to it in writing, or attend a hearing.
source_title: Home - Martinez v. Mobis Alabama LLC et al.

facts: On November 13, 2023, MOBIS North America Electrified Powertrain, LLC received the Georgia Economic Developers Association (GEDA) Deal of the Year Award in the Mid-Size Community category.
The GEDA award recognizes outstanding achievement in locating or expanding new businesses in Georgia that meet community economic development goals and involve significant community collaboration.

MOBIS was recognized alongside community leaders from the Development Authority of Bryan County (DABC), City of Richmond Hill, Bryan County, Raydient Places, and the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).

GEDA is a non-profit association of professionals and volunteers involved in economic development, organized to increase effectiveness, encourage cooperation and information exchange, upgrade professional skills, and foster closer working relationships among Georgia economic development practitioners.

Jon Seagraves, DABC Chairman, stated that recruiting MOBIS to Belfast Commerce Park at Heartwood was a team effort and thanked Mr. Park and Mr. Lee with MOBIS for being instrumental in the company's decision to choose Bryan County.
Hyundai MOBIS is a globally recognized South Korean automotive parts maker founded as Hyundai Precision & Industries Corporation in 1977.
The company forms the "parts and services" arm for South Korean automakers Hyundai Motor Company, Genesis Motors, and Kia Motors.
MOBIS's business scope encompasses key automotive parts, specifically e-powertrain systems including Battery Systems and Power Electric Systems.
MOBIS produces three major modules: Chassis Modules, Cockpit Modules, and Front-end Modules, along with aftermarket sales and service.
The Development Authority of Bryan County (DABC) has a mission to recruit and retain industry in Bryan County to create jobs and investments.
Since 2019, DABC has assisted with the location of projects representing over 5,000 new jobs and over $1.7 billion in capital investment.
The Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) project represents 8,500 jobs and $7.59 billion in capital investment, making it the largest economic development project in Georgia state history.
The HMGMA project was recruited to Bryan County by the Savannah Harbor I-16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, of which Bryan County is a member.
source_title: MOBIS North America Electrified Powertrain, LLC: Named Georgia Developers Association’s 2023 Deal of the Year for Mid-Size Communities - Richmond Hill Lifestyle

facts: A half dozen landowners filed a lawsuit that might have halted development of the massive Rivian auto plant near Social Circle.
Lawyers hired by Georgia and a local development authority asked a Morgan County judge to make the landowners pay for taxpayer dollars spent defending the project, arguing the lawsuit was frivolous.
The state had sought $338,000 from the landowners for legal costs in the Morgan County lawsuit.

A Morgan County judge ruled against the state, determining the suit was an earnest attempt to clarify if Georgia officials had authority to waive local land use rules for a private development project on public land.
The court's decision means the landowners do not have to pay the state’s $338,000 legal costs in the Morgan County lawsuit.

The state bypassed agricultural zoning for the 2,000-acre property by purchasing it, then leasing it to the Joint Development Authority for Morgan and nearby counties, which in turn leased it to Rivian.
Nearby landowners sued asking whether a for-profit corporation that makes automobiles on public land was a “governmental purpose” that would trigger legal immunity.
The state has filed a similar motion in a related Fulton County case, seeking to recover another $203,000 in legal costs.
The state could push forward with its motion for legal costs in the Fulton County case and could appeal the Morgan County decision.

A spokeswoman for the Georgia Department of Economic Development did not answer questions about the state’s next legal steps but issued a joint statement with the local development authority about the Rivian project.

The joint statement said Rivian will soon break ground on its plant construction, marking the beginning of a generational project that will bring long-term economic opportunity to the region and the state.
The joint statement added that courts have consistently sided with the state and the local authority, and this ruling does not change the status of the Rivian project.
Landowners’ attorney John Christy said it would have been financially devastating for the landowners to pay the legal costs.
Christy thinks the judge issued a well-reasoned order that will discourage an appeal by the state and lead to dismissal of the Fulton County demand for legal costs.
Christy stated, “I think that any appeal would be frivolous,” and noted the standard on appeal would be very high.
Morgan County Superior Court Judge Stephen Bradley wrote in an 11-page order filed Friday that the state’s claim about a frivolous lawsuit was undermined by its own actions.
Judge Bradley noted the state voluntarily joined the case as a defendant and hired a large team of lawyers, signaling an expectation for a much more pitched battle.
A lawyer for the local development authority displayed apparent misgivings about whether the state’s sovereign immunity would transfer to the Rivian project.
The authority’s lawyer explained in court that she was withdrawing applications for a change in local restrictions because it would have required 34 public hearings.
Judge Bradley interpreted the withdrawal of applications as a tacit admission that the law governing the matter was unclear.
Bradley wrote that neither suit filed by the plaintiffs was based on bad faith or short of justiciable issue, calling it an unprecedented case.

The ruling highlights new and unanswered questions of law concerning governmental land use regulations and public immunity, specifically focusing on sovereign immunity to shield a private, for-profit corporation.
Bradley wrote that the government’s handling of the property transaction seems clearly designed to circumvent resistance from local voices opposing the Rivian Project.
source_title: Landowners near Rivian plant defeat state demand for legal costs

facts: Murata Electronics North America Inc. has named a new chief executive.
The new chief executive is the first non-Japanese person to hold the position since the company arrived in Georgia in the 1970s.
David Kirk will become president, CEO and a director of Murata Electronics North America Inc. on April 1.
source_title: Murata Names First Non-Japanese CEO for North America - Global Atlanta

facts: Murata Electronics North America Inc. is located in Smyrna, Ga., and is repositioning itself in the passives market by building stronger relationships with its OEM and distribution partners.
The company aims to dominate the component count of a typical cellular phone or other wireless device by getting involved early in its customers' product-design cycles.
Tom Kuhl, director of sales, stated, “We're evolving from a supplier of commodity products to becoming a high-end design partner.”
Murata is a supplier of ceramic capacitors, microwave components, and crystal oscillators.
Sales for the supplier surged over the past two years, reaching $425 million in 1998 and climbing to $590 million last year as a result of the wireless and telecom boom.
Parent company Murata Manufacturing Co. Ltd., Kyoto, Japan, had sales in 1998 and 1999 of $3 billion and $4 billion, respectively.
The company is looking to increase sales by 35% this year.
To achieve growth, the company is increasingly finding itself in meetings with OEMs, contract electronics manufacturers, and distributors during the initial stages of an end-product's design.
This strategy involves identifying OEM design requirements earlier and developing products to meet those demands.
In the wireless and telecom markets, there is a push for increasingly smaller end-use products such as cookie-thin cell phones, PDAs, and pagers.
The movement toward component miniaturization is driving much of Murata's product-development efforts.
Murata is about to bring to market high-frequency chip inductors that have a 0201 footprint, which is about the size of pepper.
The company is currently using thin-film, wire-wound, and monolithic designs on its chip inductors.
A typical cell phone contains 25 to 30 inductors.
Murata is developing smaller, surface-mount crystal oscillator products that offer a longer life span.

John Switalski, product manager of crystal oscillators, noted that technology is moving away from through-hole packages to surface-mount devices that feature digital tuning as well as inverted crystals.
Sales for these types of products have jumped from nearly $4 million more than a year ago to almost $10 million this year.
Crystal oscillator sales are expected to climb to $20 million next year, according to Switalski.
Nearly 7% of Murata's revenue has been earmarked for R&D.
The company plans to raise capacity by 40% over the next year or so.
Manufacturing of 0402-size components and high-capacitance MLCCs will take the lion's share of the investments.
source_title: Murata revs production to meet wireless boom - EDN

facts: Murata Electronics North America announced the launch of its LFB182G45BG2D280 balanced output matching filter.
The filter is aimed at a broad range of 2.4 GHz wireless applications using ZigBee and Bluetooth low energy technologies.

The new filter has been designed specifically for use with wireless connectivity system-on-chips (SoCs) from Texas Instruments Incorporated (TI), the CC253x and CC254x for ZigBee and Bluetooth technology networking respectively.
Murata's solution measures just 1.6 x 0.8 mm.
The filter incorporates all the necessary RF circuitry of a matching network, balun and filter within a single package.
Scott Klettke, Marketing at Murata Electronics North America, stated that the launch demonstrates continual success in delivering advanced products to emerging markets.
Klettke noted that the level of integration achieved is unprecedented and that it replaces nine discrete 0402 components, speaking to ease of use and time to market savings when using a TI solution.
Sample pricing for Murata's output filter is $.25 per piece.
The approximate lead-time for the filter is two to four weeks.
Murata Electronics North America is a wholly owned subsidiary of Murata Manufacturing Co., Ltd., whose global headquarters are in Kyoto, Japan.
Murata Manufacturing was established in 1944 and is a worldwide leader in research, design, manufacture and sale of ceramic based passive electronic components and power supply modules.
Murata has annual revenues in excess of $7B dollars.

Murata's products are found in applications including consumer, mobile communications, computers and networking equipment, automotive electronics, advanced metering equipment, digital home electronics, lighting and illumination, and lifestyle healthcare devices.
source_title: Murata Launches World's Smallest 2.4 GHz Balanced Output Filter

facts: Murata is showcasing intelligent system technologies for mobility, wellness, and connectivity at CES 2026.
Smart mobility demonstrations feature embedded RFID tire tag and sensing, gyro compass, headlight leveling using MEMS, and motion tracing demonstration using low frequency magnetic fields.
Embedded connectivity solutions utilize advanced Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and Ultra-Wideband (UWB) modules to deliver precise location detection and coverage across vast areas.

Connectivity demos highlight Wi-Fi Mesh for range extension, Wi-Fi HaLow TM (Type 2HK/Type 2HL Module), Type 2NR Module (Nordic nRF54L15 based), UWB Smart Lock, indoor location tracking with Bluetooth Channel Sounding, and satellite position tracking.

Health-centric sensing solutions on display include a molecular analysis platform for 15-minute DNA/RNA analysis, Vios Monitoring System for continuous hospital-grade telemetry and remote ECG, and an application example of the 3D substrate Multilayer LCP Product with piezoelectric film sensor PicoleafTM (Smart Ring Demo).

Ultrasonic technology showcase includes an ultrasonic cleaning device to clear optics for AD/ADAS in any weather, ultrasonic tracking powered by pMUT for low-power 3D/6DoF spatial tracking for XR, and airborne ultrasound transmission metamaterial for bi-directional ultrasound through plastic/metal.
At the time of this announcement, some exhibit products are concept products or under development, with specifications and appearance subject to change without notice.
Murata Manufacturing Co., Ltd. is a worldwide leader in the design, manufacture, and sale of ceramic-based passive electronic components and 2NRs, communication modules and power supply modules.
The company is committed to the development of advanced electronic materials and leading edge, multi-functional, high-density modules.
Murata has employees and manufacturing facilities throughout the world.
Media contact for inquiries is Amy Talley, General Manager of Corporate Communications.

facts: Novelis Inc. is an American industrial aluminum smelting company headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, United States.
The company produces rolled aluminum and operates as an aluminum recycler.
Novelis supplies aluminum to sectors including beverage cans, automotive, aerospace, consumer electronics, construction, foil, and packaging.
Novelis has been an independent subsidiary of Indian multinational aluminum and copper manufacturing company Hindalco Industries since 2007.
Hindalco Industries is a subsidiary of the Aditya Birla Group.
The company was incorporated in 2004 after being spun off from Alcan, a Canadian mining and aluminum manufacturer now part of Rio Tinto Alcan.
In 2007, Hindalco Industries acquired Novelis for $6 billion.
In May 2024, Novelis filed its registration statement for a proposed IPO.
Novelis withdrew its IPO plans in October 2024.
In March 2025, Novelis announced the closure of its operation in Fairmont, West Virginia, at a 100-year-old production plant.
Novelis is the world's largest producer of rolled aluminum sheet.
The company operates in 11 countries and divides its operations into four regions: North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Regional headquarters are located in Atlanta, Zurich, Seoul, and São Paulo.
Novelis also has operations in the United Kingdom, Germany, Italy, France, Canada, China, Malaysia, and Vietnam.
The company invested $100 million in its first manufacturing facility in China, which opened in 2014.
Novelis announced a $400 million capital investment for an expansion in Asia.
Novelis announced a $300 million capital investment for an expansion in Brazil.
Novelis announced a $200 million capital investment for an expansion in North America.
In July 2014, Reynolds Consumer Products Inc., a subsidiary of the Rank Group of New Zealand, purchased Novelis's North American consumer products division for CAD $33.75 million.
Novelis recycles more than 70 billion used beverage cans each year.
In May 2022, Novelis announced plans to invest $2.5 billion in a new, low-carbon aluminum plant in Bay Minette, Alabama.
Once complete, the Bay Minette facility will be the first fully integrated aluminum mill built in 40 years.
On September 16, 2025, the Oswego, New York facility suffered a three-alarm fire in the "hot mill" section that brought down part of the factory's roof.
Novelis produces aluminum structures and components for transportation products in automotive, automotive heat exchanger, and mass transportation applications.
On April 15, 2020, Novelis acquired Aleris Corporation for $2.8 billion, entering the high-end aerospace segment.
In 2012, Novelis received the Georgia Governor's International Award for establishing its Global Research & Technology Center in Kennesaw, Georgia.
Steven Fisher serves as President and CEO of Novelis.
As of 2025, Novelis reported revenue of US$17.1 billion and an additional financial figure of US$683 million.
As of 2025, Novelis reported total assets of US$16.51 billion and total equity of US$4.51 billion.
As of 2025, Novelis employed 13,250 people.
In 2013, Novelis was named metals company of the year, and CEO Phil Martens was named CEO of the year at the Platts Global Metals Awards in London.
In 2011, Novelis received an Innovation Award from BMW for lightweight vehicle construction.
source_title: Novelis - Wikipedia

facts: Novelis Inc. holds a Production and Transformation member class with the Aluminium Stewardship Initiative since January 2015.
Novelis Inc. is headquartered in Georgia, United States.
Novelis Inc. is the world's largest recycler of aluminum.
Novelis partners with customers in the aerospace, automotive, beverage can, and specialties industries to deliver lightweight aluminum solutions across North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Novelis Inc. is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group based in Mumbai, India.
Aluminium Norf GmbH (Alunorf) in Neuss, Germany is a joint venture owned by Speira and Novelis Inc to produce aluminum coils.
Novelis operates a Greensboro, Georgia manufacturing site that produces aluminum sheet ingot through shredding, decoating, melting, and casting.

Novelis supplies rolled aluminum sheet products to the beverage packaging industry from its Warren, Ohio manufacturing site.

Novelis operates an R&D Center & Testing Laboratory at its Zhenjiang, China site.
Novelis performs cold mill rolling, coil slitting, and coil finishing/annealing at its Kingston, Ontario facility.
Novelis performs hot milling, pre-treatment, and finishing of automotive aluminum products at its Guthrie, Kentucky facility.
Novelis performs remelting, recycling, casting, hot mill rolling, cold mill rolling, and coil finishing at its Oswego Works Mill in New York.
Novelis produces aluminum sheet ingot at its Berea, Kentucky site through shredding, decoating, melting, and casting.

facts: Novelis Inc. announced its second quarter results of FY2021-22 on Wednesday, November 3.
The company revealed net income of $237 million compared to a net loss of $37 million in Q2 FY2021.
Net income from continuing operations stood at $239 million versus $144 million a year ago.
Net sales improved 38 per cent year-on-year in 2QFY22 from $3 billion to $4.1 billion.
Sales improvement was primarily driven by a 5 per cent increase in shipments and higher average aluminum prices.
Flat rolled products shipments increased from 923 kilotonnes in Q2 FY21 to 968 kilotonnes in Q2 FY22.
The shipment increase was buoyed by strong demand for sustainable aluminum beverage packaging and specialty products.
Adjusted EBITDA gained 22 per cent to $553 million in Q2 FY22 from $455 million during the same period last year.
The EBITDA gain was attributable to higher volume and favourable metal benefits.
Novelis achieved a record Adjusted EBITDA per tonne shipped of $571 in Q2 FY2022, compared to $493 in the prior year.
President and CEO Steve Fisher stated that the company's diverse portfolio of high-recycled-content products allowed them to capture strong market demand and deliver another record quarter of EBITDA.

Steve Fisher noted that while navigating near-term supply chain challenges, the company is actively seeking and implementing new investment opportunities to capture growing customer demand for sustainable, low-carbon aluminum solutions.
Novelis announced plans to invest $500 million in growth capital projects.
The investment plans include a $375 million investment to expand rolling and recycling capabilities in Zhenjiang, China.
Year-to-date free cash flow from continuing operations was $158 million compared to the prior year of $169 million.

Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer Devinder Ahuja stated that since announcing Novelis' long-term capital allocation framework earlier this year, the company reduced gross debt, improved net leverage to below its targeted 2.5x level, and returned $100 million of capital to its common shareholder.

Devinder Ahuja added that the company maintained adequate liquidity to effectively manage working capital needs while remaining strongly positioned to reinvest capital in new capacity and capabilities.
source_title: Novelis Inc. earns net income of $237 million in 2QFY22; Adjusted EBITDA improves 22% to $553 million; Aluminium Extrusion, Profiles, Price, Scrap, Recycling, Section

facts: Novelis Inc. announced the completion of its acquisition by Hindalco Industries Limited.
The all-cash transaction values Novelis at approximately US$6.0 billion, including debt.
Novelis shareholders will receive US$44.93 in cash for each outstanding common share.
Novelis shareholders approved the transaction at a special meeting on May 10.
Hindalco is based in Mumbai, India, and serves as the flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group.
The Aditya Birla Group reports annual revenue of US$14 billion and a market capitalization in excess of US$23 billion.
Novelis will operate as a subsidiary of Hindalco following the acquisition.
Martha Brooks, Novelis' chief operating officer, stated the arrangement created significant value for shareholders while providing new opportunities for the combined company's future.
Kumar Mangalam Birla, chairman of Hindalco, described aluminum as a core business with enormous growth potential and viewed the acquisition as a step toward becoming a premium metals major.

Debnarayan Bhattacharya, managing director of Hindalco, highlighted geographical market and product synergies, noting Novelis is the global leader in value-added, high-end aluminum rolled products and aluminum can recycling.
The transaction was accomplished by way of a statutory plan of arrangement under Canadian law.
Hindalco, through its wholly-owned subsidiary AV Metals Inc., acquired 75,415,536 common shares of Novelis, representing 100 percent of the issued and outstanding common shares.
Immediately after closing, AV Metals Inc. transferred the common shares to its wholly-owned subsidiary AV Aluminum Inc.
Novelis' stock has ceased trading on the New York Stock Exchange.
De-listing on the New York Stock Exchange and the Toronto Stock Exchange is expected to occur shortly.
Novelis is the global leader in aluminum rolled products and aluminum can recycling.
The Company operates in 11 countries, has approximately 12,900 employees, and reported revenue of $9.8 billion in 2006.
Novelis provides customers with a regional supply of technologically sophisticated rolled aluminum products throughout Asia, Europe, North America and South America.
The Company supplies aluminum sheet and foil to the automotive and transportation, beverage and food packaging, construction and industrial, and printing markets.
source_title: Hindalco Industries Completes Acquisition of Novelis Inc. :: Novelis Inc.

facts: Novelis is a global leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Novelis has 13,250 team members working across 29 facilities in North & South America, Europe and Asia.
The aluminum recycling process uses only 5% of the energy it takes to produce primary aluminum, lowering carbon emissions by as much as 95%.
Novelis launched its company-wide vision, Novelis 3x30, in October 2024.
Novelis aims to increase average recycled content from 63% in FY24 to 75% by FY30.
Novelis aims to become the lowest emissions flat rolled products (FRP) aluminum provider with less than 3 tonnes of CO₂e per tonne of FRP shipped by the end of 2030.
Novelis operates a 40.6-acre used beverage can recycling facility in Greensboro, Ga.
The Greensboro, Ga. facility was Novelis's first stand-alone used beverage can recycling facility and is responsible for pioneering the majority of its recycling technologies.
Novelis established the Novelis Innovation Hub at Georgia Tech in 2019 to collaborate with students on aluminum research and development.
The collaboration with Georgia Tech focuses on creating advanced alloy technologies, lightweighting solutions, and closed-loop recycling systems that reduce waste and improve material efficiency.
Novelis is the official recycling partner of Mercedes-Benz Stadium.
As the official recycling partner of Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Novelis collects aluminum cans at Recycle for Good bins throughout the facility.
Novelis has leveraged its stadium recycling efforts to fund and build six Habitat for Humanity homes on Atlanta's historic Westside.
Novelis has an annual goal of collecting 120,000 pounds of aluminum, a value equal to the cost of one Habitat home.

facts: Novelis Inc. is the world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Novelis will invest approximately $130 million to upgrade its operations in Oswego.
The investment aims to meet growing customer demand for sustainable, aluminum flat rolled products.
The project increases hot mill capacity by 124,000 metric tons at the plant.
The Oswego plant serves the can, automotive, and specialty products markets.
The project enhances finishing capabilities for automotive sheet.
The investment includes upgrades to the plant’s hot reversing mill motors and drive train.
The investment includes upgrades to the plant’s hot finishing mill coolant systems.
The investment includes an enhancement of batch annealing capabilities.
The project aligns with Novelis’ sustainability goal to reduce energy use by 10% by 2026.
These projects will begin in spring 2022.
These projects are expected to be completed in 2024.
The investment follows the expansion of automotive finishing capabilities and recycling capacity at the Oswego plant over the last decade.

Novelis aluminum supports safe, sustainable, and cost-effective lightweighting of vehicles and commercial trucks.
Lightweighting with Novelis aluminum results in better performance, agility, increased fuel economy, and reduced carbon emissions.

facts: Novelis Inc. is a leading sustainable aluminium solutions provider and the world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Novelis announced an investment of approximately $90 million to increase recycling capacity for used beverage cans (UBCs) at its plant in Latchford, UK.
The project will increase the facility's recycling capacity for UBCs by 85 kilotonnes per year.
This expansion equals a growth of more than 100%.
Emilio Braghi serves as executive vice president of Novelis and president, Novelis Europe.
Novelis has the highest ambition to maximize its recycled content across its product range among all recycling players in the European market.
Globally, Novelis had an average of 63% recycled content in its products in fiscal year 2024.
The investment includes the construction of a new dross house.
The investment includes three new bag houses.
The investment includes the installation of state-of-the-art shredding, sorting, de-coating, and melting technologies.
These technologies will enable the plant to recycle a larger volume and new types of aluminium scrap.
These technologies will increase operational efficiency.
The expansion of recycling capacity and implementation of advanced technologies will result in an annual CO2e reduction of more than 350,000 tonnes for Novelis Europe.
The project is expected to begin commissioning in December 2026.
Once complete, the facility will be able to recycle 100% of UBCs collected under the future UK deposit return scheme.
This initiative will create a local, fully circular system that avoids the need to export scrap from the UK.
Allan Sweeney serves as plant manager of Novelis Latchford.
The investment in Latchford strengthens Novelis's position in the UK market as the largest recycler of aluminum.
Technological developments will allow the recycling of all types of UBC scrap.

facts: Reported financial results for the first quarter of its 2023 fiscal year, noting net income and net sales increased while shipments decreased slightly.
President and CEO Steve Fisher stated the company delivered record financial performance in the first quarter following last fiscal year's record performance.
Net income attributable to common shareholders increased 28 percent compared with a record $307 million in the previous year.
Net income from continuing operations increased 1 percent to $307 million.

Excluding special items, net income from continuing operations increased 18 percent compared with the previous year, largely due to higher underlying adjusted EBITDA, unrealized derivative gains, and a lower tax provision in the current year, partially offset by a tax litigation gain benefiting prior year net income.

Net sales increased 32 percent to $5.1 billion for the first quarter of fiscal year 2023 compared with $3.9 billion in the prior-year period, primarily driven by higher average aluminum prices and local market premiums.
Total flat-rolled product shipments were 962,000 metric tons, 1 percent lower than prior-year shipments of 973,000 metric tons, mainly owing to supply chain constraints.

Adjusted EBITDA increased 1 percent to a record $561 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2023 compared with $555 million in the prior-year period, which included a $47 million gain related to a favorable decision in a Brazilian tax litigation.

The underlying increase in adjusted EBITDA is primarily from higher product pricing, including some higher cost pass-through to customers, favorable product mix on improved automotive and aerospace shipments, and lower metal costs arising from improved recycling performance, partially offset by high-cost inflation and unfavorable foreign exchange translation.

Adjusted free cash flow from continuing operations was an outflow of $72 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2023, higher than the $30 million the previous year, primarily because of a less favorable metal price lag.
The company reported a net leverage ratio of 2.2x at the end of the first quarter of fiscal year 2023 compared with 2.5x in the prior year period.

Executive vice president and chief financial officer Devinder Ahuja stated that in a strong demand but capacity-constrained environment, the company continues to focus on delivering high-quality, high-recycled-content products to customers while optimizing operations and portfolio.
The company is vigorously allocating capital to grow alongside its customers, with more than $4.5 billion of investment opportunities on its horizon.
Earlier this year, Novelis broke ground on a $365 million investment in a highly advanced recycling center in Guthrie, Kentucky.
The Guthrie, Kentucky recycling center will be able to cast 240,000 tons of sheet ingot for its automotive customers per year.
Novelis announced a strategic partnership with Sortera Alloys Inc. of Fort Wayne, Indiana, designed to help the company increase its use of prime and obsolete scrap.
The company maintains a strong total liquidity position of $2.4 billion as of June 30.
source_title: Novelis sees growth, constraints in Q1 financial report - Recycling Today

facts: Novelis Inc. is a leading sustainable aluminum solutions provider and the world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Novelis reported a net loss attributable to common shareholders of $160 million for the third quarter of fiscal year 2026, compared to a net income of $110 million in the prior year.
The Q3 FY2026 net loss was significantly impacted by fires at the Oswego, US plant that occurred in September and November.
Production interruptions at the Oswego facility caused rolled product shipments to be approximately 72 kilotonnes lower than expected.
The Oswego production disruptions resulted in an estimated negative pre-tax $54 million impact on Adjusted EBITDA and Net loss.
Novelis recorded an additional $327 million in pre-tax losses directly related to the Oswego fires.
Adjusted EBITDA for Q3 FY2026 was $348 million, representing a 5% year-over-year decrease.
The decline in Adjusted EBITDA was impacted by an estimated negative $54 million from the Oswego fires and $34 million from tariffs.
Rolled product shipments totaled 809 kilotonnes in Q3 FY2026, down 11% year-over-year.
Adjusted EBITDA per tonne shipped reached $430 in Q3 FY2026, an increase of 6% year-over-year.
Novelis is recovering from the Oswego production disruption and anticipates restarting the hot mill in late Q2 calendar year 2026.
Novelis received a $750 million equity contribution from its common shareholder in December.
Steve Fisher, president and CEO of Novelis Inc., stated that underlying performance remains strong despite short-term capacity constraints due to the Oswego disruption.

The company's resilient business model is supported by strategic investments in new capacity, effective cost management initiatives, and favorable market conditions, particularly in beverage packaging which is its largest product end market.
Cost efficiencies and favorable recycling benefits contributed to a 6% year-over-year increase in Adjusted EBITDA per tonne to $430 in the third quarter.
Net sales for Q3 FY2026 increased 3% versus the prior year period to $4.2 billion, primarily driven by higher average aluminum prices.
Higher net sales were partially offset by an 11% decrease in total rolled product shipments to 809 kilotonnes.
Lower shipments to the automotive, beverage packaging, and specialties markets were primarily driven by the estimated 72 kilotonne negative shipment impact from the Oswego production disruption.
Higher aerospace shipments partially offset the lower shipments to other end markets.

The decrease in net income was also due to unrealized losses on derivatives in the current year compared to gains in the prior year, partially offset by favorable metal price lag from rising average local market aluminum premiums.
Adjusted EBITDA per tonne increased despite absorbing tariffs and the impact of the Oswego fires, highlighting the robustness of the core business according to management.
Net cash used in operating activities was an outflow of $90 million for the first nine months of fiscal year 2026, compared to a net cash inflow of $263 million in the prior year period.
The operating cash flow decrease was largely related to impacts from the Oswego fires.
Adjusted free cash flow was an outflow of $1,641 million for the first nine months of fiscal year 2026, compared to an outflow of $915 million in the prior year period.
The adjusted free cash flow decrease was impacted by an estimated negative $485 million related to the Oswego fires.
Total capital expenditures increased by 34% to $1,577 million for the first nine months of fiscal year 2026.
Capital expenditures were mainly allocated to strategic investments in new capacity under construction.
A notable strategic investment is a greenfield rolling and recycling plant located in Bay Minette, Alabama.
source_title: Novelis Inc. 10-Q Quarterly Report February 2026

facts: Governor Kemp’s office announced on Thursday morning, June 22, that auto parts manufacturer NVH Korea will be locating in Locust Grove.
The company produces battery parts for electric vehicles.
The announcement represents Henry County’s first addition in EV manufacturing.
Electric vehicle production is a growing sector of new manufacturing in Georgia.
Multiple new vehicle plants are coming to the state.
NVH Korea will invest $72 million dollars and create over 160 new jobs in Locust Grove.
The facility will be located at Gardner Logistics Park off Colvin Drive.
The facility expects to begin operations in the second quarter of 2024.
The new facility will supply battery parts for electric vehicles in the United States for manufacturers such as KIA and Hyundai Motor Group.
The facility will produce EV battery system components that protect, connect and sense the performance of battery cells.
NVH Korea is an automotive parts manufacturer based in the City of Ulsan and was established in 1984.
The company specializes in automotive noise, vibration, and heat-control parts.
The company has current production facilities in Korea, Europe, and India.
The company operates a manufacturing facility in Columbus that produces floor mats, cargo mats, and cargo trays.

Chairman and CEO Ja Kyum Koo stated that Georgia was chosen as the company’s first EV battery component manufacturing site and that cooperation between state agencies and company staff helped guide the project.

Chairman and CEO Ja Kyum Koo said the project is the first step in contributing to the electrification of the mobility industry and expressed a desire to join the community of Locust Grove and Henry County.
The announcement by NVH Korea is the latest in a series of electric vehicle manufacturing projects coming to Georgia.
Since 2020, Georgia has attracted more than $22.7 billion in investments and created over 28,400 new jobs in the EV sector.
Two new vehicle plants headlining recent announcements are Rivian near Social Circle and Hyundai near Savannah.
Each of the Rivian and Hyundai plants represents several billion dollars in investment and thousands of jobs.
Several new battery plants are also building in various parts of the state.
source_title: Auto Parts Manufacturer NVH Korea to Locate in Locust Grove

facts: Automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea Inc. is investing $72 million to establish a new Locust Grove, Ga., EV battery component facility.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group came to Bryan County in October 2020 to build a $5.5 billion electric vehicle and battery manufacturing plant.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced that automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea will soon have a new facility in Locust Grove.
The new facility has the ability to create over 160 jobs in Georgia.
The new facility will bring an investment of $72M.
This investment is part of Gov. Brian Kemp’s plan to promote Georgia to international companies in order to create more jobs.
Gov. Brian Kemp said: “Thanks to our reliable infrastructure network connecting partners all over the world and a great workforce, job creators can find success in every community in our state.”

Gov. Brian Kemp said: “The incredible growth of the e-mobility and battery industries in Georgia is a testament to our ability to support large suppliers like NVH Korea. We’re proud to welcome them as the latest partner in making Georgia the epicenter of the industrial revolution of our time.”
NVH Korea is an automotive parts manufacturer that specializes in automotive noise, vibration, and heat-control parts.
In Georgia, NVH Korea operates a manufacturing facility that produces floor mats, cargo mats, and cargo trays.
The new facility will be located at Gardner Logistics Park on Colvin Drive in Locust Grove.
The new facility will produce battery parts for electric vehicles (EVs) for Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, and Hyundai Motor Group.
NVH Korea will begin to operate the new facility in the second quarter of 2024.
Mayor Robert Price of the City of Locust Grove said: “NVH’s investment will increase our manufacturing jobs base and make us part of Georgia’s electric vehicle ecosystem.”
Mayor Robert Price said: “We look forward to a successful partnership with NVH and the opportunities this investment creates.”
Officials say this new facility will help Georgia become a larger part in the electronic vehicles industry as the market continues to grow.
The hope is to see a significant increase in jobs for Georgia residents following this construction.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp and First Lady Marty Kemp announced they are leading a delegation of state officials on an international mission to the Republic of Korea.
The mission focuses on reinforcing strategic economic development partnerships.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that the Republic of Korea was the very first country he visited when he became governor due to incredible partners located there.
Governor Kemp noted that the trip aims to reinforce friendships bringing historic investments and jobs to communities across Georgia.
Governor Kemp highlighted that Georgia’s connections to Korea go back decades and will further strengthen relationships having a generational impact on the state being No. 1 for business.
The state delegation consists of President Pro Tem John Kennedy, Representative Soo Hong, and Representative Lehman Franklin.
The delegation includes representatives from the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), Georgia Environmental Protection Division, and Georgia Ports Authority.
Governor and Mrs. Kemp and the delegation will conduct meetings with existing industries operating or under construction in Georgia.
Industries to be met include Hyundai Motor Group, Kia, CJ Foodville, LG Group, SK Group, and Hanwha Qcells.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that meeting with leadership of existing industries at their headquarters provides a unique opportunity to learn about industry perspectives and future directions.
Commissioner Wilson emphasized that keeping lines of communication open helps stay ahead of industry shifts and positions Georgia at the forefront of new opportunities.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson said, "Connectivity leads to business, and relationships build the foundations of mutual progress."
The State of Georgia has maintained continuous representation in Korea since 1985.
Korea’s Consular Office with jurisdiction across the Southeast, Puerto Rico, and U.S. Virgin Islands has been located in Georgia since 1976.
Korea has been one of the top sources of Foreign Direct Investment and international job creation in Georgia for the last three years.
In fiscal year 2023 alone, Korean companies announced more than $10 billion in investments and the creation of 12,605 jobs across the state.
Total trade between the State of Georgia and Korea totaled over $15.8 billion in calendar year 2023.
Trade with Korea accounted for nearly 10 percent of the state’s total trade in calendar year 2023.

facts: Apollo Funds entered into a definitive agreement to acquire a majority stake in Panasonic Automotive Systems Corporation from Panasonic Holdings Corporation.

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems India (PASI) offers the development, manufacture, and sale of automotive components and parts for four-wheelers and two-wheelers.

Panasonic is transforming the in-car experience by harnessing new technologies including Connected Devices, Head-Up Displays, Wireless Chargers, and 360° Camera Solutions.
These new technologies are designed to be simple to use, safe, durable, and cost-effective.
PASI is currently catering to the top OEMs of the automotive industry through its wide range of products and solutions.
PASI aims to expand its product gamut to serve more customers in the automotive sector.

facts: At CES 2022, Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America announced a collaboration with Totem USA to bring Totem’s first UL-certified eBike to the U.S. market, “Zen Rider.”
In 2019, Panasonic received the industry’s first e-Bike safety standard certification from UL (UL2849).
The Totem USA eBike “Zen Rider” is powered by a Panasonic motor and battery system designed as an affordable, family-friendly option to meet growing eBike market demands.

Research from NPD indicated a 240% growth rate for electric bicycles in the 12 months leading up to July 2021, with sustained interest expected after pandemic-era consumer spending on cycling and sporting equipment.

Derek Arciniaga, senior manager of business development at Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America, stated that the UL-certified Panasonic system is intended to expand mobility transformation in the United States.
The Zen Rider features a low step-through mounting design, making its motor/battery system especially suited for urban areas.
Panasonic’s second UL 2849 certified motor & battery eBike system includes a 2-Axle motor + battery configuration.
The system provides 75 Nm of max torque and a 404 Wh battery.
The eBike offers pedal assistance up to 15mph, aligning with city/local ordinances that have 15mph speed limitations.
Totem USA, in collaboration with Panasonic, is prepared to design, develop, and produce custom eBike offerings for companies interested in UL certified eBikes.
The Panasonic drive system is suitable for two-wheel and three-wheel city, touring, or cargo eBike variants.

Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is a division company of Panasonic Corporation of North America and a leading global supplier of automotive infotainment and connectivity system solutions.
Panasonic Automotive is headquartered in Peachtree City, Georgia, with sales, marketing, and engineering operations in Farmington Hills, Michigan.
Totem USA is located in McKinney, Texas, and is a division of the Golden Wheel Group, which is the 4th largest manufacturer in the world of bicycles and eBikes.
Panasonic Corporation of North America is headquartered in Newark, NJ, and is the principal North American subsidiary of Osaka, Japan-based Panasonic Corporation.
Panasonic was named one of Interbrand’s Top 100 Best Global Brands of 2021.

facts: The Perkins manufacturing facility in Griffin, Georgia supplies engines to the construction, agriculture, material handling, and power markets in North America.
The facility was established in 2004.
It produces the compact and powerful 400 series engines with an annual capacity of 50,000 units.
The Griffin manufacturing facility utilizes a single-piece flow production system that electronically tracks every engine from assembly through heat testing to painting and final customer coating.
Jeff Rohr serves as the Plant Manager for the Griffin facility.
The 400 series engines produced in Griffin are available in 2, 3, and 4 cylinder configurations with a seamless power range of 10.2-49.2 kW (13.7-66 hp) for the North American market.
Production plans include the new 404F-E22T/TA common rail direct injection engine, which generates 36-50 kW (48-67 hp) of power.
Engine assembly occurs on crawler tracks and features electronic screens that provide operators with build data specific to each engine.
The facility uses electronic torque control tools to ensure every fastener is tightened precisely according to technical specifications during assembly.
After assembly, engines undergo comprehensive heat testing on one of ten test benches located within the same soundproof enclosure.
Following testing, engines are painted, coated, subjected to 100% inspection, and then shipped directly to customers.
The Griffin facility minimizes lead times and delivery times by supplying engines directly from the factory to North American customers.
Engine quality follows the Caterpillar Production System (CPS), ensuring efficient processes, tested and validated parts, and strict quality control across all Perkins manufacturing facilities.
The workforce consists of a trained US team focused on the North American market.

facts: Peterson Spring learned of a connection to springs that were at the base of the huge smoke stacks of Detroit Edison’s “Seven Sisters” power generating plant across from Belle Isle on the Detroit River.
The Seven Sisters was a landmark for generations until it was imploded in the summer of 1996 after 81 years.
Salvaged from the rubble were five mighty, hand-forged, hot-coiled springs made by August Peterson.
Bob Umpfenbach, at one time an employee of Peterson Spring on Telegraph Road in Southfield, Michigan, made contact with the demolition crew and asked them to save some of Peterson’s springs.
According to Bob, there were three sizes of springs, and he was promised samples of each size.
Unfortunately, someone scrapped the others by mistake, leaving Peterson Spring only the smaller ones.
At present, these are on display at the Peterson Spring Home Office in Southfield.

Bud Peterson recalled that as a child, his father would tell him and his sister, Thelma, that the springs at the base of the giant stacks of the power plant were handmade by their Grandfather, August.
The compression springs August made were necessary in order to compensate for the expansion and contraction caused by temperature changes in the stacks.
There were actually two or three compression springs in each unit, one nestled inside the other, for a combined weight of 51.25 pounds.
To make these springs, August took a bar of steel and got the ends red hot in a forge (which is a charcoal fire with bellows underneath), and then tapered the ends with hammer blows.
After this was done, he heated the whole bar red hot in a furnace, took it out, and coiled it over an arbor on a big, heavy coiler he built himself.
He was making these springs for the Seven Sisters Plant in 1914 when his first granddaughter, my sister Thelma, was born.
The Detroit landmark was dubbed “Seven Sisters” because of its seven 325-foot-high stacks.
It was officially retired in 1983 and imploded shortly after 9 o’clock in the morning on August 10th, 1996.
When it was built, the plant required: 9,000 piles, 23,000 yards of concrete, and nearly 1,300 tons of reinforcing.
The initial boiler was so large the Detroit Edison Chairman Alex Dow hosted a luncheon inside it in 1914.
Each stack weighed about 1,286,000 pounds, and each was wide enough to allow two lanes of traffic.

The DTE power plant was not completely demolished, the two other stacks, named Two Brothers, of the Conners Creek Power Plant are still operational, sitting on the Detroit River near Chrysler’s Jefferson North Plant.
Today, Peterson Spring carries on the family tradition of spring manufacturing through the precision coiling of compression springs, extension springs, torsion springs and die springs.
The company offers a technical array of services from grinding to prototyping to sub-assemblies.
This global supplier presents expertise in many industries including agriculture, industrial, automotive and motorsport.
source_title: Peterson Spring Recovers Century Old Springs

facts: Pirelli's US plant is located in Rome, Georgia, approximately 70 miles northwest of Atlanta.

Pirelli tires are manufactured to be precisely the same in every facility worldwide and are fitted as standard equipment on many European car models.
The Rome plant produces approximately 450,000 pieces (gross) per year.
Products include premium tires for cars, light trucks, and SUVs, ranging in sizes from 17 inches up to 30 inches.

facts: On Thursday, May 7th, 2026, Pirelli announced it will start production of connected tires featuring Cyber™ Tyre technology at its Rome, Georgia plant.

The Rome, Georgia plant is already dedicated to the most technologically advanced products for the U.S. market in the High-Value segment and Motorsport, and will be further enhanced by the new production line.

Cyber™ Tyre is described as the world's first hardware-and-software system capable of collecting data and information from sensors embedded in tires and processing them through Pirelli's proprietary software and algorithms.

The technology communicates in real time with the vehicle's electronics to enable new functionalities integrated with driving and control systems, enhancing the driving experience, increasing safety levels, and supporting connected infrastructure.

The announcement coincided with Pirelli's participation in the SelectUSA Investment Summit, a flagship event promoted by the U.S. Department of Commerce, where Cyber™ Tyre was showcased as a core innovation shaping smart mobility.
At the summit, Cyber™ Tyre was showcased at the Georgia State booth, highlighting Pirelli's industrial footprint in the state, where it has been established for more than 20 years.

Claudio Zanardo, CEO of Pirelli North America, stated that the start of Cyber™ Tyre production in the Rome, Georgia plant is a significant milestone for Pirelli in the country and reflects its commitment to bringing advanced technologies closer to the market while strengthening its industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
Pirelli is finalizing the introduction of the latest version of the MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) production process at the Rome, Georgia facility.
The MIRS process will be exclusive to the Georgia factory and represents the most advanced manufacturing process for high-end, premium production within the entire Pirelli Group.
This highly digitalized system enables a direct link between product design and industrial application while increasing productivity, flexibility, and quality.

The development lays the groundwork for growth in Pirelli's production capacity in Rome, which is an integral part of the Group's industrial development plans and strengthens its presence in the United States, one of its most important strategic markets.
The Rome, Georgia plant specializes in high-value tire production for the North American market and hosts a dedicated R&D center to strengthen technological development and product innovation.

The facility is recognized for responsible sourcing practices, including the use of FSC® (Forest Stewardship Council®)-certified natural rubber, underscoring the company's broader environmental commitment across its supply chain.
source_title: Pirelli Will Start Cyber Tyre Production in Georgia - Rome CEO

facts: Pirelli announced its new manufacturing line for connected tires will be established in Georgia, USA.
The decision is part of the company’s broader strategy to localize high-tech tire production to meet rising demand in North America.
The facility is expected to produce Pirelli’s latest line of connected tires, including the Pirelli P Zero Elect and Cinturato P7 All Season.
These tires are tailored for electric vehicles (EVs) and premium sedans.
The tires feature embedded sensors that communicate with the vehicle’s onboard systems to provide continuous feedback for optimizing handling and energy efficiency.
Georgia has emerged as a hub for automotive manufacturing due to its strategic location, skilled workforce, and favorable economic incentives.
Pirelli’s choice underscores the importance of producing high-technology tires domestically to meet North American automakers’ standards.
Automakers pushing toward electrification and smart vehicle features include Tesla, Ford, and General Motors.
The move is expected to create dozens of jobs and stimulate local economies.
The project faced unexpected hurdles from the United States government, particularly concerns raised by the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS).
CFIUS almost blocked the project over fears related to Chinese influence and technology transfer.
Concerns included worries that the embedded sensors and connectivity systems could involve Chinese technology firms or be vulnerable to espionage.
The U.S. government expressed concerns about reliance on Chinese-made components or software compromising national security.
Broader geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and China around trade and technology heightened scrutiny on foreign investments in critical infrastructure.
After negotiations and reassurances from Pirelli regarding intellectual property protections, the project was allowed to proceed.
The U.S. government imposed certain restrictions and oversight measures on the project.
The manufacturing facility remains on track to open.
Major competitors like Michelin, Continental, and Goodyear are also investing heavily in sensor-integrated tires.
Michelin is developing the Uptis (Unique Puncture-proof Tire System), which focuses on maintenance reduction and sustainability.
Continental offers ContiConnect, which provides tire monitoring for commercial fleets.
Goodyear developed the Oxygene concept, an innovative design for airless, sustainable tires with embedded sensors.
Tesla Model S and Model 3 already support tire pressure monitoring systems (TPMS).
The industry’s collective move toward connectivity reflects a broader push for safer, more efficient, and sustainable mobility.
Pirelli supplies tires for Formula 1, World Superbike, and GT racing, which influences its consumer products.
Widespread adoption of connected tires in mainstream models could take 3-5 years, depending on technological maturity, regulatory environment, and automaker integration.
The article synthesizes publicly available information as of October 2023.

facts: Executives stated on a 2024 financial results call that Pirelli could potentially expand its tire production capacity in the US to offset the impact of the escalating North American trade war.

To meet US market demand, Pirelli imports more than 50% of its US supply from Mexico and about 40% from Brazil and Europe.

facts: Pirelli operates a manufacturing and research complex in Rome, Ga.
The facility relies exclusively on production from its modular integrated robotized system, or MIRS.
Pirelli's automation strategy focuses on making smaller batches of a large number of products to keep mixed production runs profitable.
The system eliminates the need to keep expensive raw materials and finished tires on hand.
Small production runs can be as few as four tires at a time.
Gaetano Mannino serves as president of Pirelli Tire North America.
Mannino stated that they make tires for ultra-big bling-bling wheels in small numbers, noting it has become like a fashion business where being first with a hot design leads the market.
The facility produces specialized tires, including sets of gigantic tires for Hummers.
A traditional tire factory may sprawl up to a half-mile in length and can be set up to produce popular models in batches of 1,000 or more.
Switching molds and setting up machinery for a different model in a traditional factory can take workers eight hours or more.
A MIRS production unit consists of a dozen robots feeding a group of rubber-extruding and ply-laying machines.
The system fabricates tires around metal drums that the robot's arms grip powerfully.
The robot arms have sophisticated wrists that let them hand off the tire-in-progress to the extruding machinery at all sorts of angles as strips of rubber and reinforcing material are built up.
One MIRS system can simultaneously build 12 different tire models.
A bar code on each drum tells the equipment which model of tire it is about to make, and the control software supplies the appropriate recipe for the machinery.
At the end of the process, robots load the unfinished tires into molds that will emboss the tread pattern and sidewall lettering in a 25-minute steam-curing process.
Robots ensure precise, repeatable movements to build tires with everything in the right place without overlapping rubber strips or joints.
MIRS ensures every tire is well balanced and quiet running, matching a level of consistency human labor cannot achieve.
Pirelli engineers originally considered trucking the robotic system to racetracks to quickly make and test racing tires.

About two years ago, a general from the Pentagon contacted Mannino about taking a MIRS module to Iraq, but it was rejected because the plants must be very clean and cannot operate in desert conditions.
source_title: Robotic assembly lines help drive tire innovation / Small batches, greater variety have changed profit calculus
