PFS issued an auto Asset Backed Securities (ABS) offering in the USA with a principal amount of $870.4 million dollars.
This transaction marked the second bond issuance for PFS in 2025.
PFS is an indirect, wholly owned subsidiary of German luxury car maker Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG (“Porsche AG”).
The securities maintained a ‘AAA’ rating from rating agencies and achieved competitive pricing.
The transaction was divided into five tranches, including a floating rate tranche.
The ABS offering was backed by a pool of auto contracts financing Porsche vehicles.
RBC Capital Markets led the transaction, with BofA Securities, Scotiabank, Société Générale, and Truist Securities serving as book runners.

The deal reached subscription levels in each tranche to achieve an upsize from a growing investor base comprised of investment funds, asset managers of financial institutions, trusts, banks, and corporates.

“Despite launching during one of the busiest weeks in the 2025 ABS primary market, PFAST 2025-1 received solid investor support,” said Tobias Hausladen, Treasurer & Chief Financial Officer at Porsche Financial Services, Inc.

“We greatly appreciate the confidence investors continue to place in Porsche Financial Services. The outcome reflects the strength of our credit platform and the consistency of our performance, even in challenging market environments.”
PFS provides leasing and financing products for Porsche in the United States.
PFS was founded in 1991.
In 2012, PFS expanded its North America operations to become the captive finance provider for Bentley, Lamborghini, and Bugatti under the Volkswagen Group.
source_title: Porsche Financial Services, Inc. Issues Prime Auto Transaction in the U.S. ABS Market

facts: Porsche’s U.S. home base in Atlanta is officially much bigger and twistier.
Porsche Cars North America will open its second, 1.3-mile handling circuit now called the “West Track” (designed by Tilke) to the public on Saturday.
The new driver development course features greater elevation changes and a G-force-inducing carousel section compared to the original track.
The PCNA campus and Experience Center—one of two in the U.S. and 10 around the world—has undergone a $50-million expansion.
Both courses will operate separately, but the new course can be linked with the existing one, the South Track, for almost three miles of varied driving experiences.
The facility is positioned at the northeast corner of the Atlanta airport.
The 33-acre addition is adjacent to the 27-acre Porsche headquarters that opened in 2015.
Porsche says the Atlanta experience center has logged nearly 400,000 guest visits to date.
Porsche's growth joins a bevy of developer investment in Hapeville that city leaders hope will more firmly establish the city as a live-work-play alternative around ITP Atlanta.
Corners of the new course were inspired by the Nürburgring carousel turn, the Laguna Seca corkscrew (with a 30-foot rise), and the Tail of the Dragon road in the Smoky Mountains.
Other features include a dynamic zone for slaloms, a low friction circle, and a new ice hill.
Prices for one and ½ hour sessions at the West Track start at $600 for time behind the wheel of the base Taycan, Porsche’s first foray into electric vehicles.
The priciest experience allows guests to spend that same amount of time driving a 911 Turbo S and a 911 GT3—both exotic models with more than 500 horsepower—for $1,275.
The original track next door offers experiences with Boxster and Cayman models from $450 for 90 minutes.
source_title: First look: Porsche's exhilarating Atlanta headquarters expansion | Urbanize Atlanta

PCNA has been headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia since 1998.

PCNA is the exclusive U.S. importer of the Porsche 911, 718 Boxster, 718 Cayman, Macan, Cayenne, Panamera and Taycan.
The Taycan electric vehicle is among the models imported by PCNA.
The newly launched Porsche Parking Plus app includes a filter for parking facility features that specifically lists "electric vehicle charging."
PCNA is home to the first Porsche Experience Center in North America featuring a module-based 1.6 mile driver development track, business center, and fine dining restaurant, 356.
Kjell Gruner serves as President & CEO of PCNA, where he is responsible for both the United States and Canada.
Kjell Gruner also heads the executive leadership team of Porsche Digital, Inc., the sports car manufacturer’s digital subsidiary in the U.S.

facts: Porsche Cars North America sold a record 76,167 new vehicles in the U.S. in 2024.
The 2024 sales figure represents a one percent increase over the previous sales record set in 2023.
Timo Resch serves as the President and CEO of PCNA.
PCNA introduced unprecedented new ICE, Hybrid, and BEV models including the new 911, Panamera, Taycan, and Macan.
The 911 model set an all-time sales record with 14,128 retail sales in 2024.
The 911's 2024 sales represent a 20.8 percent increase compared to 2023.
The 718 model sold 5,698 units in 2024.
The 718's 2024 sales represent a 25.9 percent increase compared to 2023.
The 911 and 718 models combined accounted for 26 percent of all Porsche vehicles sold in the U.S. in 2024.
The Cayenne model achieved record sales with 22,432 units sold in 2024.
The Cayenne's 2024 sales represent a 9.6 percent increase over 2023.
Porsche Approved Certified Pre-Owned (CPO) sales totaled 43,249 vehicles in 2024.
CPO sales in 2024 represent a 12 percent increase compared to the prior year.
The Taycan model saw a 37.3 percent decrease in sales in 2024.
Taycan sales dropped from 7,570 units in 2023 to 4,747 units in 2024.
Panamera sales decreased by 5.3 percent in 2024, dropping from 4,205 units to 3,982 units.
Macan sales decreased by 6.6 percent in 2024, dropping from 26,947 units to 25,180 units.
Global EV sales are slowing down, contributing to the decline in Taycan sales.
Porsche is introducing a new EV Macan model.
An EV Cayenne line is expected from Porsche.
An EV 718 line is expected from Porsche.
A local Porsche dealer offered a brand-new Macan 4 EV for 10 percent off MSRP.
Porsche CFO Lutz Meschke stated the company is considering hybrid drive or combustion engines for originally planned all-electric vehicles.
Porsche is currently making conceptual decisions regarding powertrain options for its future vehicles.
Porsche confirmed it will stick with the combustion engine for much longer than originally planned.

facts: PCNA retailed 75,415 new vehicles in the U.S. in 2023, setting a new record and representing a 7.6 percent increase versus the previous record set in 2022.
Timo Resch serves as President and CEO of PCNA.
Porsche ranked number one among luxury brands in the 2023 J.D. Power U.S. Sales Satisfaction Index Study.
The 911 model sold 11,692 units in 2023, an increase of 14.6 percent versus 2022, marking the first time since 2007 that PCNA sold more than 11,000 911 models in a single year.
The Macan set a new annual record with 26,947 deliveries in 2023, up 13.8 percent versus last year against a previous high of 24,716 set in 2021.
Taycan sales grew by 4.1 percent to 7,570 cars in 2023, representing 10 percent of PCNA sales that year.
The 718 model line was the fastest-growing in 2023 with an increase of nearly 30 percent to total 4,526 cars.
Sales of the third-generation Cayenne drove past 20,000 vehicles for only the second time in PCNA history during 2023.
Porsche Approved Certified Pre-Owned (CPO) sales in the U.S. totaled a record 38,788 cars, a 17.7 percent year-over-year increase.

The same Atlanta campus also houses the U.S. headquarters of Porsche Classic.

PCNA operates a second Porsche Experience Center near Los Angeles with an eight-module educational track totaling 4.1 miles, a business center, Restaurant 917, and the headquarters of Porsche Motorsport North America.

facts: German luxury automaker Porsche is celebrating the 10th anniversary of its Atlanta experience center this summer (2025).
The investment combines test tracks with Porsche's North American headquarters.
It is one of the largest investments made in Atlanta’s Southside since the turn of the century.
The project revitalized the site of the shuttered Ford Motor Co. plant near Hapeville.
For roughly 60 years, airplane drone near the world’s busiest airport was countered by sounds of automobiles coming off assembly lines at the Ford plant.
In 2006, factory lines at Ford Motor Co.’s plant near Hapeville went quiet.
Alan Hallman, Atlanta’s mayor since 2003, called the Ford factory’s shuttering “an economically devastating event.”
Mayor Hallman stated that Porsche's investment “transformed that area” and “woke that area up again after Ford’s departure.”
The Porsche Experience Center is located in Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport / I-57, Atlanta, Georgia.
The project represents a $1.2 billion investment announced in 2015.
The site employs more than 1,000 employees.
The facility serves as Porsche's North American headquarters and includes a unique experience center combining test tracks and driving.
Mayor Hallman referred to the project as “a third crown jewel” for Atlanta.
The investment is expected to serve as a major economic driver for the Southside area.
The site includes a test track and driving experiences for customers and employees.
source_title: Porsche's Atlanta experience center reshaped Southside over 10 years

facts: The PCNA campus underwent a $50 million expansion, completed in late 2023.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp visited the Porsche Cars North America headquarters at One Porsche Drive during an all-staff meeting.

During the visit, Governor Kemp and PCNA President and CEO Timo Resch discussed Porsche’s new cars, electrification, Porsche’s investment in Georgia, and a new solar array installed at the Porsche Experience Center in Atlanta.
First Lady Marty Kemp accompanied Governor Kemp on the visit to One Porsche Drive.
Governor Kemp last visited a Porsche site in January 2020 when he toured the factory in Zuffenhausen.
Porsche Cars North America headquarters has been located in Atlanta since 1998.
PCNA will celebrate its 40th anniversary in the United States in September.

Mercedes Benz and Hyundai operate automobile plants in Alabama, contributing to high demand for auto supply industry jobs in Northwest Georgia.

RealTruck operates as part of Truck Hero Inc., which is located in Ann Arbor.
RealTruck announced its first-ever advisory team composed of seven elite professional athletes who are also investors in the company.

The athlete investors include Dak Prescott (Dallas Cowboys), George Kittle (San Francisco 49ers), C.J. McCollum (New Orleans Pelicans), Jason Kelce (Philadelphia Eagles), Dustin Brown (Los Angeles Kings), Byron Jones (Miami Dolphins), and Beau Allen (Super Bowl LII Champion).
The athletes joined RealTruck through their November 2021 investments made in partnership with Patricof Co, a specialized private investment and advisory platform for professional athletes.
Dak Prescott is a two-time Pro Bowl Quarterback who serves on the advisory team.
George Kittle is a three-time Pro Bowl tight end who serves on the advisory team.
The athlete investors will engage in quarterly meetings with RealTruck’s top executives.

The advisory team will have behind-the-scenes access to company operations to provide insights on positioning products to enthusiast consumers and expanding brand reach into mainstream sports and lifestyle markets.
RealTruck sells more than 1 million curated truck parts and accessories.

RealTruck.com is based in Ocala, Fla.

Truck Hero emphasizes market-leading functionality, engineering, quality, and design in its products.

facts: RealTruck is the premier manufacturer and digital destination of accessories for truck, Jeep®, Bronco® and off-road enthusiasts around the world.
The company is globally headquartered in Ann Arbor, Michigan.
RealTruck employs over 6,000 associates operating from more than 35 facilities across four continents.
The company's product portfolio includes the Husky Liners total vehicle protection brand and boasts over 1,000 patents and pending applications.
RealTruck utilizes an omni-channel retail approach delivering a seamless consumer experience online, as well as through its 12,000+ dealer network and automotive (OEM) partnerships.
RealTruck operates manufacturing and distribution facilities in Lawrenceville, GA.
RealTruck operates manufacturing and distribution facilities in Pendergrass, GA.
Carl-Martin E. Lindahl serves as CEO and Board Director of RealTruck since November 2022, following leadership roles at SVP Worldwide, KitchenAid, and the Whirlpool Corporation.

Carl-Martin E. Lindahl is a former McKinsey & Company partner, serves on the board of the Fiskars Group, is originally from Sweden, holds an M.Sc. from Chalmers University of Technology, and attended UC Berkeley’s Haas School of Business.

Baljinder Singh serves as Chief Financial Officer overseeing finance, procurement, and strategic growth initiatives, having joined in August 2023 after serving as CFO of Wahl Clipper and holding senior roles at SVP Worldwide, Serta Simmons Bedding, Grainger, and Motorola.
Baljinder Singh holds an MBA from Roosevelt University and a BS in Accountancy from Northern Illinois University.

Tammy Bohen serves as Chief Human Resources Officer leading HR, Environmental, and Health & Safety functions since March 2023, bringing over two decades of HR leadership experience from companies like SVP Worldwide, Whirlpool, and Johnson & Johnson.
Tammy Bohen holds a Master’s degree in Human Resources and a Bachelor's in Recreation Management.

Eric Dale serves as Chief Legal Officer and EVP of Government Affairs and Corporate Transactions, joining in April 2023 after senior roles at Balance Point Capital and Nielsen, and serves as Vice Chairman of Bankwell Financial Group.
Eric Dale holds a BA from Clark University and a JD from New York Law School.
Tony Ambroza serves as Chief Growth Officer leading brand transformation since 2021, previously driving major growth at Carhartt and holding marketing leadership roles at Under Armour and Nike.

Eric Firer serves as Chief Information Officer since 2026, leading technology strategy to drive accelerated growth, reinforce security, and elevate the customer experience, having previously served as CIO, North America at both Kellanova and Bacardi.

Antonio Galvao Costa serves as Senior Vice President of Supply Chain and Customer Service, leading global supply chain, planning, distribution and customer service teams, having joined in 2026 after prior leadership roles in supply chain at Tenneco/DRiV.

Ken Hossler serves as Senior Vice President of Manufacturing Operations, joining in January 2023 after holding manufacturing leadership roles at SVP Worldwide, Peloton Interactive, and Whirlpool Corporation.

Matt Grau serves as Head of M&A and Investor Relations since 2021 after serving as VP of Finance, previously working in investment banking at Jefferies and BB&T Capital Markets.

facts: On Oct. 17, 2023, RealTruck, Inc. acquired Mountain Top, the leading designer and manufacturer of truck bed accessories outside North America.
The acquisition accelerates RealTruck’s international expansion and complements its recent acquisition of Mexico-based brand Go Rhino.
Mountain Top supplies major original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and sells truck accessories throughout Europe, Asia, Australia, and the U.S.
Mountain Top has state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Denmark, Thailand, and the U.S.
Mountain Top is the only European truck bed cover supplier of scale certified by the International Automotive Task Force (IATF).
Mountain Top’s flagship product line is the premium retractable truck bed cover that comes in both electronic and manual versions.
In August 2023, RealTruck acquired IDDEA (Industria Diseñadora De Autopartes, S.A. De C.V.), which goes to market under the Go Rhino brand.
Go Rhino holds 40 issued and pending patents for innovative premium products developed and manufactured for truck, Jeep, and SUV owners.
RealTruck’s acquisition of Mountain Top further expands RealTruck’s global footprint and supply base in the OEM-focused European truck bed accessories market.
RealTruck is headquartered in Ann Arbor, Mich., with over 5,000 associates across 47 locations throughout North America.
RealTruck holds more than 630 patents and sells products through more than 12,000 dealer locations and automotive (OEM) partnerships.

facts: RealTruck is America’s leading truck accessory brand.
RealTruck released the “Built By American Innovation (A.I.)” campaign to celebrate America's 250th anniversary.
The campaign highlights people and hands-on work across the U.S., including mechanics, welders, farmers, builders, and makers.
As part of the campaign, RealTruck profiles truck owners and builders through documentary-style films featuring real-world product testing.

Our approach to innovation is grounded in solving real-world problems. We think ahead, so the products they rely on feel intuitive, dependable, and ready for whatever the job demands.”

facts: RealTruck Inc. is a global aftermarket product and accessory brand for truck, Jeep, Bronco, and off-road enthusiasts.
RealTruck is returning for its 16th year at the SEMA Show in Las Vegas, Nevada from November 4-8.
RealTruck will occupy booth #50027 to showcase 17 in-booth vehicle builds and its latest product portfolio.
The company is highlighting celebrity build collaborations with automotive design icon Chip Foose, global adventurist Bear Grylls, and basketball player Dwyane Wade.
RealTruck is showcasing a one-of-a-kind build in collaboration with Makita U.S.A. featuring workplace utility on four wheels.

RealTruck will host two press conferences: the first on Tuesday, November 5 at 11:30 a.m. PST to unveil the Chip Foose collaboration, and the second on Thursday, November 7 at 11:30 a.m. PST featuring Bear Grylls and his two customized vehicles.

RealTruck Vice President of Marketing Lee Riser stated that SEMA is the company's biggest event of the year, providing an ideal platform to engage customers and reinforce leadership in the aftermarket industry for trucks, Jeeps, Broncos, and off-road enthusiasts.
Some of the showcased builds will be auctioned in the coming months to support special charity organizations.
RealTruck is collaborating with automotive designer Chip Foose on a one-of-a-kind F-150 build unveiled via live stream press conference on Tuesday, November 5 at 11:30 a.m. PST.
The Chip Foose F-150 will be auctioned at Barrett-Jackson in Scottsdale, Arizona in January 2025, with all proceeds benefiting The Progeria Research Foundation.
RealTruck announced an integrated partnership with Bear Grylls and his inaugural “Adventure Garage with Bear Grylls” show airing globally in 2025.
As part of the Bear Grylls partnership, RealTruck customized a 2021 Ford F-150 and 2024 Toyota Tacoma as permanent fixtures on the show.
RealTruck products will be seamlessly integrated into each episode of the Bear Grylls show for extreme adventures.
RealTruck is collaborating with basketball legend Dwyane Wade on a customized one-of-a-kind F-250 build revealed at the show and auctioned at Barrett-Jackson in Palm Beach, Florida in April 2025.
Proceeds from the Dwyane Wade F-250 auction will benefit his non-profit organization Social Change Fund United, supporting underrepresented communities of color.

RealTruck collaborated with Makita U.S.A., Inc. on a construction-focused Ford F-150 build featuring Husky Liners Wheel Well Guards, Universal Mud Guards, Low Profile Vent Visors, Go Rhino XVenture Gear Tool Wrench Rolls, Recovery Bag, Large Hard Case, AMP Research Bed Step, A.R.E. Double Drop Down HD Aluminum Kit, and a custom Makita wrap with an exclusive first look at their new modular storage solution.
RealTruck announced four new products: TruXedo Pro X15 TS, BAKFlip MX4 TS, Sport Bar, and Go Rhino Hitch Based Swing-A-Way Tire Carrier.

The TruXedo Pro X15 TS is a soft truck bed cover with versatile T-slot channels in the rails for expanding cargo-carrying abilities, featuring 15-degree side rails that sit flush with bed rails when closed.

The BAKFlip MX4 TS is a top-selling truck bed cover constructed from alloy materials including heavy-duty aluminum top panels and side rails, with a textured automotive-graded finish and integrated T-slot rails compatible with racks and carriers.

The RealTruck Sport Bar features a dual-tube design in single or double front tube options with a detachable front bar, constructed from high-strength 2-1/2” OD steel tubing with a textured black powder coat finish and molded side panel inserts with 3D-effect venting.
The Sport Bar includes a versatile light multi-mount system compatible with factory track systems, most aftermarket track-style bed covers, clamp-to-truck bed caps, and most retractable bed covers.

RealTruck’s Go Rhino Hitch Based Swing-A-Way Tire Carrier is engineered to install into any universal 2-inch receiver hitch, built from high strength 3/16-inch-thick steel with black geomet-coated hardware, featuring a fully adjustable wheel mount accommodating tire combinations up to 37-inch diameter and 200 pounds weight capacity.
RealTruck products are categorized under Restyling & Car Care Accessories, Trucks, SUV's & Off-Road, and Wheels & Accessories at the show.
source_title: RealTruck Inc. Returns to SEMA Showcasing Multiple Celebrity Builds Only RealTruck Can Bring to Life; Several Partnership Collaborations; New Product Launches | semashow.com

facts: Sewon America is a subsidiary of Korean-based Sewon Precision Industry Co.
Sewon America will create 740 new jobs in Rincon.

This project is the largest known private investment in the City of Rincon to date.
The announcement fulfills the commitment to create jobs and investment in all four Savannah Harbor Interstate-16 Joint Development Authority (JDA) counties.
The JDA commitments are a result of Hyundai’s Metaplant location in Bryan County.
Hyundai’s Metaplant is the largest economic development project in state history.
Suppliers for the Metaplant have now committed over $1.8 billion in investment.
Supplier investments surpass initial estimates by at least $800 million.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that his first international trip as governor to Korea included congratulating Chairman Kim on recent expansion and discussing future collaboration.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns, who represents part of Effingham County, helped secure the project and engage Sewon America executives.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones commended Governor Brian Kemp, Sewon America, and stakeholders on the historic economic development announcement for the City of Rincon.
Sewon America operates one of the largest automobile body parts manufacturing plants in North America.
The company opened a manufacturing facility in LaGrange in 2008.
The LaGrange facility has since doubled in size.
Following completion of the Rincon facility, Sewon is expected to support 1,600 jobs in Georgia.
Vice Chairman Kim stated that Sewon America was founded 15 years ago in west Georgia and is building another facility in east Georgia.
The growth is supported by Governor Kemp, the Georgia Department of Economic Development, and Effingham County.
Sewon’s new facility will be located at the Grande View industrial park in Rincon.
The new manufacturing location will produce electric vehicle (EV) body parts for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs), including Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in 2025.

Rincon Mayor Ken Lee welcomed Sewon America to the community and commended the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority for bringing the largest employment and private investment project in the city’s history.

Chairman of the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority (IDA) Troy Smith stated that Sewon selected Grande View industrial park for significant investment to allow current and future generations to have fulfilling, well-paying careers without leaving Effingham County.
Executive Director of the Georgia Ports Authority Griff Lynch called the announcement terrific news for Effingham County and the State of Georgia.
Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC for this project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Sewon has integrated into Georgia’s automotive community through local colleges and Georgia Quick Start.
Sewon supports Hyundai Motor Group’s operations from Effingham County, which has the skilled workforce, infrastructure, and collaborative spirit to support growth.
On October 25, 2022, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry.
Since 2020, more than 35 EV-related projects have announced more than $21 billion in investment and 26,700 jobs in Georgia.
Sewon America was established in 2008 in LaGrange as a Georgia company.
Sewon primarily operates in the Motor Vehicles and Car Bodies industry within the Transportation Equipment sector, producing stamped body components.
Sewon Group is celebrated for high-quality automotive parts.
source_title: Sewon America Announces 740 New Jobs in Effingham County - Expansion Solutions

facts: Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp announced a “Georgia Made” program to encourage manufacturers to promote the state on their products.
Kemp announced the program Wednesday at the state Capitol.
Representatives of 11 companies, whose factories Kemp visited during a statewide tour last fall, were present at the announcement.
The program parallels the existing Georgia Grown certification that promotes agricultural products.
source_title: Kemp Launches ‘Georgia Made’ Logo For In-State Manufacturers – WABE

facts: Effingham County is preparing for an influx of new residents, businesses, and industry due to multiple massive economic development announcements over the past year.
Infrastructure improvements, quality-of-life amenities, and expanded healthcare services are underway across Effingham County and its cities of Springfield, Rincon, and Guyton.
Last summer (relative to April 2023), Hyundai Motor Group announced its first fully dedicated electric vehicle (EV) and battery manufacturing facility in Georgia, located in Bryan County.
Effingham County is part of the four-county Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority.
Brandt Herndon serves as CEO of the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority (IDA).
The Hyundai project represents a $5.54 billion investment, making it the largest economic development project in Georgia state history.
The Hyundai project will deliver approximately 8,100 new jobs to Georgia’s coastal region.
Sewon America, a subsidiary of Korean-based Sewon Precision Industry Co., announced 740 new jobs and a $300 million facility for auto body parts manufacturing in Rincon.
The Sewon investment is the largest known private investment in the City of Rincon.
Sewon is one of many suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant that have collectively committed over $1.8 billion in investment.
The Effingham County IDA is developing Grande View, a 450-acre park located off Old Augusta Road, where the new Hyundai supplier will be situated.
The Georgia International Trade Center offers up to 7.2 million square feet of light industrial and manufacturing warehouse space as part of a $500 million development.
The trade center park spans 1,150 acres and is almost fully built out, leaving only 100 acres of marketable property.
Tenants at the Georgia International Trade Center include Shaw Industries, Pon.Bike (Pacific Cycle), Serena & Lily, Lowe’s, DeWell Group, Dukal, and Sunland Logistics, all newcomers to Effingham.
County Manager Tim Callanan arrived approximately four years ago as growth in the area began to explode.
The county set aside specific areas for industrial development a decade ago, which allowed logistics companies to locate there a few years later.
Effingham now contains about a half-dozen million-square-foot warehouses due to logistics company locations near the port.
Logistics operations diversified Effingham's property tax digest and provide revenue for road capacity, parks, and water/sewer expansion.
The county benefits from Transportation Special Purpose Local Sales Tax (TSPLOST) dollars and has completed about 80% of its planned projects.
TSPLOST funding covered repaving efforts, including $5 million of the county’s $20 million share of the $52 million Effingham Parkway.
The Effingham Parkway is a north-south corridor extending from central Effingham into Chatham County, scheduled for completion in 2025 to provide traffic relief.
Plans include constructing nine roundabouts to improve traffic flow.
Unlike neighboring counties announcing mega-developments of 10,000 or 13,000 housing units, Effingham maintains steady housing growth.
The county issues about 600 new home construction permits annually, a sustainable level expected to increase due to proximity to the Hyundai plant.
To prepare for population growth, Effingham County is expanding its wastewater treatment plant to triple its current capacity.
Effingham is extending water lines westward toward the Ogeechee River and south of Guyton to the Chatham County border to meet demand from developers near the Hyundai plant.
SPLOST funds will finance approximately $25 million in parks projects over the next two years, including renovations for all existing parks and acquisition of land for new ones.
Rincon, which has the county’s largest population, is doubling the capacity of its wastewater treatment plant as part of major infrastructure preparations.
City Manager Jonathan Lynn states that the Sewon announcement brings jobs and residents needed to attract major retailers like Target, Chick-fil-A, Hobby Lobby, or TJ Maxx.
Guyton City Manager Meketa Hendricks Brown is surveying her town of 2,300 residents on recreation, housing, and transportation needs to prepare for the Hyundai plant's impact.
Guyton is educating staff and residents on Korean culture to welcome new residents and will launch a website with city services translated into Korean.
Guyton is working to accommodate additional sewer capacity in anticipation of residential housing growth within the next 18-24 months.
Springfield, the county seat, is experiencing increased residential and commercial growth compared to previous years.
Springfield officials are considering additional downtown parking and converting an old ballpark into greenspace with a playground, walking trails, and new bathrooms.

Effingham Health System (EHS) has undergone major transformation over the past five years under President and CEO Fran Witt, who holds a doctorate of nursing practice and serves on the American Hospital Association’s Rural Health Services Committee.
EHS established new centers for cardiology, orthopedics, and cancer care within the past five years to align with growth and expansion.
EHS implemented Oracle Cerner, an integrated electronic health record and financial ecosystem across its patient care locations.
EHS plans to expand its emergency room and open a new Effingham Pediatric Center to serve families expected to migrate to the county over the next few years.
EHS received 2022 Small Hospital of the Year honors from the Georgia Alliance of Community Hospitals and employs over 470 people.
Andrew Cripps arrived four years ago as head of the Effingham Chamber of Commerce, noting that strong growth was already underway before the Hyundai plant announcement accelerated it.
The chamber is stimulating commercial and industrial growth in Effingham to provide local job opportunities and reduce commutes to Savannah for residents.
The chamber urges a balanced approach to commercial and residential growth to prevent the tax burden from falling fully on residential taxpayers.
Effingham Forward is a chamber initiative educating the community on how balanced growth provides tax benefits, jobs, opportunity, and reduces road infrastructure burdens for commuters.

The Georgia Salzburger Society was established in 1925 as a genealogical and archaeological organization to recognize the history of Salzburgers who arrived in Georgia in March 1734 after fleeing religious persecution in Austria led by Baron von Firmian.

Early Salzburgers built brush arbors, pavilions, and shacks on property called Ebenezer before constructing the Jerusalem Lutheran Church using clay and straw bricks, with work widely believed to have been done by women and children whose fingerprints remain visible.
The brick church survived Revolutionary War and Civil War military occupations and has hosted continuous worship services since its completion.

The society celebrates Salzburgers' history on Landing Day every March and maintains the Loest Research Library containing colonial-era primary sources like birth/marriage records, diaries, journals, and maps.
The Georgia Salzburger Society reports several thousand members globally, with approximately 500 active participants.
source_title: Effingham County: Disciplined Growth - Georgia Trend Magazine

The Company will increase production of door frames in the U.S. from the fall of 2013.
