facts: Aspen Aerogels' Aerogel Technology Platform optimizes the performance and safety of electric vehicles and energy infrastructure assets.

Global organizations have utilized Aspen’s engineering expertise and advanced aerogel technology for more than 20 years to help solve challenges in resource efficiency, electrification, and sustainability.
Aspen's advanced insulation products now help to promote the adoption of electric vehicles, enhance energy efficiency and support the availability of cleaner fuels in traditional energy markets.
The Aerogel Technology Platform is built on two decades of R&D investment.
Aspen maintains a million square feet install base for its aerogel solutions.
Solutions from Aspen are used by 25 of the largest refineries.
Aspen Aerogels is deeply committed to producing quality insulation materials and making continuous improvements for all its customers across a range of applications and industries.
Automated analyses of critical process parameters and production characterization within the onsite quality laboratory ensure control of process and product.
Aspen Aerogels is ISO 9001:2015 certified (certificate No. USA/QMS/000222) for its Northborough, MA and East Providence, RI facilities.

The scope of the ISO certification includes all major business processes including design, manufacturing, fabrication, quality, supply chain, research and development, administration, sales, and marketing.
All Aspen suppliers are required to be ISO 9001 certified.
Aspen cascades additional requirements, as appropriate, to its suppliers to ensure quality systems are intact and customers are protected.
One of Aspen Aerogels' manufacturing facilities has officially achieved IATF 16949 certification according to the automotive-specific quality management standard.
With the new certification, Aspen is well positioned as a reliable, committed, and quality-oriented automotive supplier for its varied automotive customer base.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels | A Technology Leader in Sustainability

facts: Aspen Aerogels selected a 90-acre parcel in the Southeastern United States as the site for its second aerogel manufacturing plant (Plant 2).
The company is working closely with state and local officials in the Southeast to finalize zoning approvals and incentive arrangements for Plant 2.
Aspen anticipates making a joint public announcement of the Plant 2 site selection with the governor's office in the near future, subject to board approval.
Plant 2 will be designed to provide more than twice the capacity of Aspen's existing East Providence plant.
Phase 1 of Plant 2 is planned to become operational during the second half of 2023.
Aspen is expediting work on Plant 2 to capitalize on accelerating demand for its PyroThin thermal barriers used in EV batteries.
Aspen is developing carbon aerogel technology for low-cost, high-performance, silicon-rich anodes in lithium-ion batteries under its Aspen Battery Materials (ABM) division.
Nearly $10 million was invested in 2021 in scientists, engineers, and facilities to advance ABM's technical milestones for energy density and cycle life.

Aspen is delivering production parts for PyroThin thermal barriers to a major Asia-based automotive OEM for its first all-electric vehicle (sold in Asian, European, and North American markets) starting in Q4 2021.
The Asian EV OEM has partnered with another large Asian EV OEM that will use the same battery modules powered by Aspen's materials.
Aspen is also delivering production parts for PyroThin thermal barriers to a major U.S. automotive OEM during Q4 2021.
The transition from prototype to production parts signifies thermal barrier components destined for Q4 and Q1 commercial EV launches.
Aspen's expanded role with EV OEMs now includes broader battery performance and mechanical management solutions beyond just mitigating thermal runaway, resulting in increased content per vehicle.
Koch Strategic Platforms made a $75 million investment in Aspen to strengthen its balance sheet and leverage the Koch network for scaling operations.
Aspen engaged Koch Project Solutions to support Plant 2 engineering and construction, including providing onsite project leadership during the build phase.
Aspen is considering establishing fence-line production of critical raw materials near Plant 2 with principal suppliers to shorten the supply chain and reduce costs.
Aspen plans to establish a fabrication facility for PyroThin thermal barriers likely in Mexico, targeting mid-2022 establishment and early 2023 fully automated operations.

Aspen's revised full-year 2021 outlook includes $6 million to $7 million in PyroThin thermal barrier revenue, up by $5 million from prior estimates due to expanded scope for EV battery thermal management and safety.
Aspen targets doubling revenue every 24 months through the decade, driven by accelerating EV megatrend demand for its aerogel technology platform.
The company is strategically increasing investment in technical, operational, commercial, and R&D teams to support its thermal barrier business and carbon aerogel battery materials opportunity.
Aspen's multi-year capital expenditure projections will include funding for Plant 2, Mexican fabrication operations, and other assets required to support EV business opportunities.

facts: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. (NYSE: ASPN) announced an underwritten public offering of 4,250,000 shares of its common stock on October 21, 2024.
The company expects to grant underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 637,500 shares at the public offering price.
Aspen intends to use net proceeds from the offering for working capital, capital expenditures, and general corporate purposes.
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC and Morgan Stanley are acting as joint book-running managers for the offering.
The offering is being made pursuant to a shelf registration statement on Form S-3 filed with the SEC on October 21, 2024.

Aspen's carbon aerogel initiative, Aspen Battery Materials, seeks to increase the performance of lithium-ion battery cells to enable EV manufacturers to extend driving range and reduce the cost of EVs.
Aspen's PyroThin® products enable solutions to thermal runaway challenges within the electric vehicle ("EV") market.
The company's strategy is to partner with world-class industry leaders to leverage its Aerogel Technology Platform® into additional high-value markets.
Aspen is headquartered in Northborough, Massachusetts.
source_title: ASPEN AEROGELS, INC. ANNOUNCES PUBLIC OFFERING OF 4,250,000 SHARES OF COMMON STOCK PR Newswire

facts: Aspen Aerogels reported $37.9 million in revenue for fiscal Q1 2026 (period ended March 31, 2026), down 8% sequentially from fiscal Q4 2025.
Revenue breakdown included $21.6 million from the Energy Industrial segment and $16.3 million from the thermal barrier segment.
GAAP net loss was negative $23.7 million, compared to a negative $72.9 million in fiscal Q4 2025.
Adjusted EBITDA was negative $12.7 million, representing a 29% sequential improvement from negative $18 million in fiscal Q4 2025 despite lower revenue.
Company-wide gross margin was 11%, with segment gross margins of 15% for Energy Industrial and 6% for thermal barrier.

A temporary operational disruption occurred at the East Providence facility due to an explosion, causing a plant shutdown and near-term cost pressures from repairs, expedited freight, and elevated inventory across facilities.
Aspen Aerogels received $37.6 million in claim proceeds from General Motors (GM).
Of the GM claim proceeds, $3.5 million was recognized as revenue in fiscal Q1 2026, with $4.9 million per quarter to be recognized through 2027 under ASC 606.
The company ended fiscal Q1 2026 with $175.6 million in cash and cash equivalents, up from $158.6 million at year-end 2025, supported by GM proceeds and an $8 million working capital benefit.
The term loan balance stood at $86 million, with a sole covenant requiring cash to equal at least 100% of the term loan balance after fiscal Q1 2026.
Fiscal Q1 2026 capital expenditures totaled $1 million, with full-year guidance remaining under $10 million for CapEx and $26 million in scheduled debt payments.
Fiscal Q2 2026 revenue is expected between $40 million and $48 million, representing a 5% to 28% sequential increase.
Q2 adjusted EBITDA guidance ranges from negative $10 million to negative $4 million, pending production and supply outcomes.
Management expects approximately 20% segment growth in Energy Industrial for 2026, driven by subsea, LNG, and maintenance turnaround opportunities.
European thermal barrier revenue tripled year over year for the quarter, with management forecasting $10 million to $15 million in European thermal barrier revenue for 2026.
General Motors EV market share averaged 14.1%, with Cadillac EV sales accounting for over 30% of segment sales in fiscal Q1 2026.

Management cited lower GM production relative to sales, resulting in destocking, and anticipates GM will align production rates more closely with sales volumes consistent with a demand-driven approach.
PyroThin is Aspen Aerogels' proprietary thermal barrier designed for lithium-ion battery fire protection in electric vehicles and energy storage systems.
Across European awards, Aspen supports programs incorporating battery cells from a diversified global supply base, including European, Korean, Japanese, and leading Chinese manufacturers.
The staged restart of the East Providence facility is planned for May, conditional on safety reviews and agency coordination.
The sale of Plant 2 assets is expected in fiscal Q4 2026 and will be applied directly for debt reduction.
Financial framework from restructuring enables management to reduce breakeven revenue levels from $330 million in 2024 to $175 million by the end of 2027.
Q2 guidance assumes GM production at an annualized rate of approximately 55,000 to 65,000 vehicles in the quarter.
The current IHS forecast has GM producing nearly 100,000 vehicles for 2026, pointing to more production weighted to the second half of the year.
Q2 cash outflows are projected between $20 million and $30 million, including $12 million of CapEx and scheduled debt payments.
Management reiterated that sequential improvement for each quarter in 2026 is expected, targeting adjusted EBITDA breakeven at $50 million in quarterly revenue during the second half.
Expenses were impacted by one-time items, including a $2.2 million property tax charge and approximately $1 million of charges related to nonrecurring professional services in fiscal Q1 2026.
GM has maintained its full line of EV nameplates and stated it remains dedicated to its long-term EV success, including in the Cadillac division where EV sales represented over 30% in Q1 2026.
Battery electric vehicles now account for more than 20% of new vehicle registrations in Europe, supporting early stages of production ramp-up among OEMs with whom Aspen has design awards.
Aspen is advancing growth opportunities in battery energy storage systems, engaging in qualifications and commercial discussions with developers serving grid infrastructure and data centers.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels (ASPN) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript - AOL

Aspen plans to build a 500,000-square-foot advanced manufacturing facility situated on a 90-acre parcel in southeast Georgia.

The company plans to begin hiring personnel in the second half of 2022 across skilled manufacturing operators, advanced process engineers, quality and maintenance technicians, and professional support roles.

Billy Allen, vice chair of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, welcomed Aspen Aerogels to the Statesboro-Bulloch County community and highlighted the region's commitment to innovation in the electric mobility ecosystem.
Georgia Southern University and Ogeechee Technical College are expected to provide critical workforce support to help Aspen flourish.
Assistant Director Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development's Global Commerce division on this project.
The project is being pursued in partnership with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson welcomed Aspen Aerogels' innovative solutions for the electric vehicle and energy storage markets to Georgia and expressed confidence in Georgia employees collaborating with Aspen's scientists and engineers.

Southern Gateway Commerce Park is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) program-certified park, requiring due diligence and specific certification requirements prior to prospective business visits.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels to open manufacturing facility in Bulloch County

facts: Aspen Aerogels announced a PyroThin® design award from a large EU battery manufacturer to supply a next generation vehicle platform of a major EU luxury sports car brand.
The EU battery supplier will be the manufacturing partner for the next generation vehicle platform.
The vehicle platform is expected to underpin multiple nameplates and has an expected start of production in 2025.
Award represents Aspen’s sixth overall OEM win.
Aspen President and CEO Donald R. Young stated the company is excited to add another OEM to its growing list of customers as they prepare to launch their next generation EV lineup.
Mr. Young added that the award and the depth of the quote and development pipeline with additional OEM customers further validates PyroThin® as the go-to EV thermal barrier solution.
Aspen believes it will add more awards to its roster during 2024 to drive further diversification in its PyroThin® customer base for 2025 and beyond.

CFO Ricardo C. Rodriguez commented that the profitable Energy Industrial segment, combined with a broad set of blue-chip PyroThin® customers, positions Aspen to continue supporting the long-term EV demand ramp.

Aspen Battery Materials is the Company's carbon aerogel initiative that seeks to increase the performance of lithium-ion battery cells to enable EV manufacturers to extend driving range and reduce EV costs.
Aspen has a planned second manufacturing plant in Georgia ("Plant II").
Plant II has an extended construction and commissioning timeframe.
Aspen is managing the construction of Plant II to align with expectations of demand from EV customers.
Forward-looking statements include risks regarding the cost of capital projects, including Plant II.
Risks include the potential inability to continue construction of Plant II at a cost consistent with estimates and aligned with expectations of EV customer demand.
EV thermal barrier customers have the right to cancel contracts with Aspen at any time and without penalty.
Aspen faces risks related to costs, expenses, or investments incurred in excess of projections used to develop pricing under contracts with EV thermal barrier customers.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. - Aspen Aerogels Announces Thermal Barrier Award on Major Luxury Sports Car and Reiterates 2024 Financial Outlook

facts: Aspen Aerogels' PyroThin Thermal Barrier business addresses the U.S. EV market, which remains in reset mode with market share settling at approximately 5%-6%.
GM’s monthly EV market share averaged 14.1% this year, suggesting a sales rate over 100,000 EVs in 2026.
GM produced EVs in Q1 and April at levels below current sales volume, resulting in lower finished vehicle inventory levels.

GM has maintained its full line of EV nameplates and remains dedicated to long-term EV success, including in its Cadillac division where EV sales represented 28% of total sales in 2025 and over 30% in Q1 2026.

In Europe, battery electric vehicles now account for more than 20% of new vehicle registrations, with stronger structural drivers supporting early production ramp-up among OEMs with whom Aspen has design awards.
EU Thermal Barrier revenue in Q1 increased more than threefold versus the prior year quarter, and 2026 revenue is expected in the range of $10 million-$15 million.
European thermal barrier programs incorporate battery cells from a diversified global supply base including European, Korean, Japanese, and leading Chinese manufacturers.
Aspen Aerogels is advancing new growth opportunities in battery energy storage systems for grid infrastructure, data centers, and other high reliability applications.
Q2 2026 guidance assumes GM production at an annualized rate of approximately 55,000-65,000 vehicles in the quarter.
IHS Markit forecast projects GM will produce nearly 100,000 vehicles for 2026, pointing to more production weighted to the second half of the year.
Aspen Aerogels received $37.6 million in claim proceeds from GM in Q1, which is recognized as revenue ratably through the end of 2027 with approximately $4.9 million per quarter thereafter.

An operational disruption occurred on April 8 at Aspen Aerogels' Aerogel manufacturing facility in East Providence involving an explosion in a high-temperature oven, causing plant damage and temporary cessation of operations.
Staged restart of East Providence operations is expected to begin in May, subject to continued progress in mechanical, operational, and safety reviews.

facts: Beaver Manufacturing Company is headquartered at 12 Ed Needham Drive in Mansfield, Georgia 30055.
The company provides engineered fiber solutions and high-performance technical fibers for vehicles, including hoses, power transmission belts, air springs, couplings, and tires.
Beaver transforms technical yarns such as aramid, polyester, polyvinyl alcohol, rayon, nylon, and glass into advanced high-tech components that are ultra-strong, resilient, and adaptable.

The company's engineered fibers are used for reinforcement, strength, and performance in hoses, power transmission belts, mechanical rubber goods, and fiber optic cables across markets including agricultural, aerospace, chemical, construction, energy, equipment, food and beverage, information technology, marine, general industrial, and transportation.
Beaver offers high-performance fibers designed to withstand extreme conditions for umbilical, subsea, bundle, hydraulic, fracking, and drilling hoses used in onshore and offshore marine applications.
The company claims to offer more types of industrial fiber than any other single supplier, with an emphasis on sustainability.
Beaver Manufacturing Company operates additional facilities in Tepeji del Río, Hidalgo, Mexico, and Mühlhausen/Thüringen, Germany.
source_title: Beaver Manufacturing

facts: Beaver Manufacturing Company, Inc. (BMC) is a North American supplier of technical fibre reinforcements for hoses and other industrial applications.
BMC announced plans to expand its global presence by acquiring Intercord Thüringen GmbH of Germany.
Intercord Thüringen GmbH is a producer of high-quality technical fibres.
The acquisition brings together two technologies to increase the company’s customer base worldwide.
The partnership enables BMC to add RFL treating technology to its product line.
The acquisition adds BMC's third production plant across the globe.
The manufacturing facility is located in Mühlhausen Thüringen, Germany.
This new German facility rounds out BMC’s worldwide footprint alongside existing plants in the US and Mexico.

Michael Dubin, Sr. Vice President & Chief Operating Officer of Beaver Manufacturing Company, Inc., stated that the acquisition makes a significant expansion into the European market and enters the tyre and belt industry.
The German plant will continue to produce RFL treated yarns and cords, as well as hose products incorporating Beaverloc treatments.
For more than 20 years, Intercord’s technology has produced cords for tyres, hoses and transmission belts in the automotive and industrial industries.

Ramazan Yasbay, Managing Director of Intercord (who will continue as Managing Director of the German facility for BMC), stated that Beaver Manufacturing advanced the U.S. hose yarn industry with environmentally friendly treated yarn solutions and can now do the same in Germany.
Adding their technology and product line to Intercord's will increase the level of innovation in this industry throughout Europe and the Americas.
Despite a sluggish economy across the globe during the pandemic, BMC executives say this partnership will continue its growth and increase production.
Only two years after opening its manufacturing site in Mexico, BMC promises job creation into 2021.
source_title: Beaver Manufacturing expands with acquisition of Intercord Thüringen

facts: Blue Bird Corporation unveiled a new state-of-the-art paint facility at its Fort Valley, Georgia, assembly plant during a ribbon-cutting ceremony.
The new paint facility is 60,000-square-foot and utilizes the latest robotic technology.
Buses roll into the paint facility on a conveyor, meeting programmed robots that spray each bus per engineering specifications.
Painted buses pass through ovens to set the paint before proceeding through multiple inspection bays and mounting to a chassis.
The facility operates as an automotive-grade paint facility with a full clean-room environment that blocks out debris.
The facility features a zero-to-landfill design where all paint overspray is captured, dried, and sent to a power generation plant to be used as fuel.
Blue Bird has made a significant investment to build and operate the new facility.

Trey Jenkins, senior vice president of operations at Blue Bird Corporation, stated the new paint facility can paint a bus three times faster than manual methods with a higher paint transfer rate and consistent coverage.

Jenkins noted that the technology will dramatically reduce the time it takes to paint a bus, allowing the company to meet customer needs at a faster rate and increase efficiencies within production lines.
Phil Horlock, president and CEO of Blue Bird Corporation, stated the facility illustrates Blue Bird’s commitment to technological innovation that drives quality, value and efficiency.
Horlock added excitement over the positive impact this investment will have for customers and the specialized jobs it will bring to the local community.
Buses have recently started to use the painting process at the new facility, which will be fully operational later in 2019.
Blue Bird (NASDAQ: BLBD) is the leading independent designer and manufacturer of school buses with more than 550,000 buses sold since its formation in 1927.
Approximately 180,000 Blue Bird buses are in operation today.
Blue Bird distinguishes itself by a singular focus on the design, engineering, manufacture, and sale of school buses and related parts.
It is the only manufacturer of chassis and body production specifically designed for school bus applications.
Blue Bird is the market leader in alternative fuel applications with its propane-powered, electric and compressed natural gas-powered school buses.
Blue Bird manufactures school buses at two facilities in Fort Valley, Georgia.
The Micro Bird joint venture operates a manufacturing facility in Drummondville, Quebec, Canada.
Service and after-market parts are distributed from Blue Bird’s parts distribution center located in Delaware, Ohio.
source_title: Blue Bird Unveils New State-of-the-Art Paint Facility - School Transportation News

facts: Workers at Blue Bird Corp.’s two factories and warehouse in Fort Valley will begin voting Thursday on whether they want to be represented by a labor union.
More than 1,400 employees at the company’s facilities in Fort Valley will vote through Friday on whether to unionize under the United Steelworkers.
The United Steelworkers union represents more than 850,000 workers nationwide in a variety of industries.
Blue Bird workers are seeking higher pay, a more regular schedule, and better vacation and sick time benefits from the publicly held Macon-based company.
Blue Bird has long been the largest employer in nearby Peach County.
The unionization vote represents an opportunity for organized labor to make gains on the stony soil of the Deep South.
source_title: Workers at Georgia school bus maker Blue Bird begin voting on whether to unionize – WABE

facts: Blue Bird Corporation in Macon received an $80 million grant to convert a prior manufacturing site in Fort Valley into a "state-of-the-art" electric school bus production site.
The grant funding comes from the U.S. Department of Energy.
The project will bring over 400 new manufacturing jobs to Middle Georgia.
The conversion will take place at an approximately 600,000-square-foot facility.
The federal grant is part of the U.S. administration's Domestic Automotive Manufacturing Conversion Grants program.

The grants program represents a nearly $2 billion investment to convert long-standing automotive facilities to manufacturing electric vehicles, retain good-paying manufacturing jobs and bolster domestic supply chains.
Blue Bird is one of 11 firms selected for the federal grant program.
Phil Horlock serves as CEO of Blue Bird Corporation.
Horlock stated that the company is delighted the DOE supported its conversion project over many other submitted applications.
Horlock noted the grant will enable Blue Bird to reinvest in middle Georgia on a site that previously manufactured high-end motorhomes.
Horlock added the grant will allow Blue Bird to expand its workforce and invest in a community integral to its history and continued success.
Horlock indicated the company is looking forward to fully participating in the next phase of the negotiation process with the DOE.
The facility will increase production of Type-D school buses to meet rising nationwide demand for clean student transportation.
Type-D school buses require low maintenance costs, seat up to 87 passengers, and run up to 120 miles on a single charge.
Blue Bird is recognized as the leader in electric school buses with nearly 2,000 zero-emission vehicles currently on the road.
The new Fort Valley facility will allow Blue Bird to retain and expand its existing workforce of nearly 2,000 employees.
U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm commented that losing jobs to foreign competition and a changing industry is very hard for manufacturing communities.
Granholm stated that even as competitors invest heavily in electric vehicles, these grants ensure the automotive industry stays competitive in supporting communities and workforces.
source_title: Blue Bird in Georgia to expand EV bus production with $80m grant, create over 400 jobs

facts: Blue Bird Corporation specializes in the design, engineering, production, and distribution of school buses and related components.
The company serves customers across the United States, Canada, and international markets.

Blue Bird primarily makes money by selling completed school buses and bus body/chassis configurations to customers (typically school districts and private fleet operators) through its dealer/distributor channel.

Blue Bird's revenue from bus sales has shown a robust upward trajectory, driven by strategic pricing and increased demand, particularly in electric vehicles (EVs), which now represent a growing share of sales.
The company’s focus on EVs and manufacturing investments underscores its commitment to long-term growth, positioning it well for future expansion in the sustainable transport sector.
Earnings call date was May 06, 2026 (Q2-2026).
Next earnings date is Aug 12, 2026.

Management reported record Q2 adjusted EBITDA, record free cash flow and liquidity, raised full-year and long-term outlooks, closed a strategic MicroBird acquisition, and secured an $80 million DOE MES grant for a new plant.

Challenges discussed were modest and largely manageable in the near term—slight YoY revenue/unit shortfalls driven by production days, lower Q2 EV deliveries (albeit with a strong EV backlog), tariff/policy volatility, near-term cost pressures, and production capacity constraints until the new plant comes online.
Company beat guidance on all metrics for the 14th consecutive quarter; Q2 and first-half results exceeded prior guidance across revenue, adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow.
Consolidated Q2 revenue was $353 million, about $6 million (≈1.7%) below prior year.
Unit sales for the first half were 4,283, down 142 units YoY—impacted in part by fewer production days this quarter.
Pre-acquisition fiscal 2026 guidance increased revenue to $1.515–$1.565 billion and adj. EBITDA to $230 million (range $220–$240M) with an EV sales outlook of ~900 units for the year.
Following the April 1 MicroBird consolidation, the company now guides consolidated revenue to $1.725–$1.775 billion (Q3 $500M; Q4 $560M) and adj. EBITDA to $245M (range $235–$255M).
The company targets roughly a 14% full-year margin with adjusted free cash flow targeted at $100–$125M (about 50% of EBITDA after an extraordinary $25M CapEx).
Key metrics noted on the call include a ~3,600‑unit backlog (including ~900+ EVs/912 cited).
Q2 adjusted EBITDA was $50.8M and record Q2 free cash flow was $40M.
Record liquidity stands at $418M (cash $276M).
There was $5M of debt reduction year‑over‑year.
A $100M buyback program is in place ($5M executed, $90M remaining).
The company reconfirmed an $80M DOE MES grant toward a >$300M new plant (Type C capacity 9,000 buses/yr, production start CYQ4‑2028).
Updated medium- and long-term targets include mid-term adj. EBITDA ~ $275M; long-term revenue ~ $2.5B and adj. EBITDA $325–$375M+.
TTM Total Revenue is 1.49B, Gross Profit is 314.22M, EBITDA is 199.14M, and Net Income is 133.01M.
Balance sheet shows Total Assets of 689.36M, Cash of 275.89M, and Total Debt of 87.98M.
TTM Free Cash Flow is 183.80M and Operating Cash Flow is 206.37M.
Blue Bird disclosed 41 risk factors in its most recent earnings report, with the most risks reported in the "Production" category.
On May 12, 2026, Blue Bird Body Company agreed to purchase group annuity contracts from Pacific Life to transfer future benefit obligations and administration for 2,044 participants.

On May 6, 2026, Blue Bird reported fiscal 2026 second-quarter results showing net sales of $352.6 million, down 1.7% year-on-year due to fewer production days, while GAAP net income rose to $29.3 million and adjusted EBITDA reached a record second quarter.

Consistent, high-quality cash generation (OCF $206M, FCF $184M, FCF ~0.96x net income) and record liquidity provide durable financial flexibility that supports debt reduction, a $100M buyback, and funding of strategic capex or M&A without immediate reliance on external financing.
A steep reported TTM revenue decline introduces persistent uncertainty around demand timing and shipment recognition, which can compress operating leverage and complicate planning.
source_title: Blue Bird Corporation (BLBD) AI Stock Analysis | Smart Price Targets & Insights - TipRanks.com

facts: Georgia Senator Raphael Warnock visited Blue Bird Corporation in Fort Valley, Ga. as part of a statewide tour promoting infrastructure and clean energy funding.
During the visit, Blue Bird Corporation unveiled the test drive of an electric bus with zero emissions.
Senator Raphael Warnock is a co-sponsor of the Clean Commute for Kids Act to provide further investments for clean transportation.
The Clean Commute for Kids Act reauthorizes through FY2025 the Clean School Bus Program.

The Clean School Bus Program authorizes the Environmental Protection Agency to award grants for up to 60% of the replacement costs of acquiring clean school buses and charging and fueling infrastructure.
Senator Warnock stated that increasing clean transportation services in Georgia requires help from both the federal government and partnerships with private investors.
As part of his statewide tour, Senator Warnock is also discussing economic recovery from the pandemic with Georgians.
source_title: Senator Warnock tours Blue Bird Corporation in Fort Valley, views electric school bus

facts: For more than 65 years, Bonnell Aluminum has built a reputation as one of North America's leading manufacturers of custom aluminum extrusions.
The company made recent sizable investments to broaden its market and product reach.
Bonnell Aluminum provides customers with some of the most comprehensive range of capabilities in aluminum extrusions, fabrication and finishing services in the industry.
The company operates four strategically located manufacturing facilities.

Bonnell Aluminum and its subsidiary, AACOA (Division of Bonnell Aluminum), serve many of the nation's leading and most respected manufacturing companies in the Building and Construction, Automotive, and Specialty Markets.
Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation (NYSE:TG), a global company specializing in the manufacturing of plastic films and aluminum extrusions.
The company maintains its mailing address at P.O. Box 428 Newnan GA 30264.

facts: Tredegar Corp.’s subsidiary Bonnell Aluminum plans to expand and upgrade its anodizing capacity to serve the commercial-architectural construction market.
The company expects to invest approximately $5 million to expand and modernize the anodizing line at its Carthage, TN, facility.
The upgraded line is planned to be fully operational in the second quarter of 2015.
The expansion will increase the plant’s anodizing capacity by an additional 35%.
Ira Endres, Vice President Sales and Marketing, commented that the upgrade will increase capacity to better service customers’ anodizing requirements while enhancing quality and consistency.
Brook Hamilton, President of Bonnell Aluminum, added that the company’s 2012 acquisition of AACOA increased its expertise in anodizing processes and technology.
The project will benefit from the added knowledge from the AACOA acquisition and raise the bar for anodizing quality and consistency in the commercial-architectural construction market.

Bonnell Aluminum is pleased with the U.S. Department of Commerce’s final ruling to institute anti-dumping and countervailing duties on unfairly traded imports of aluminum extrusions from 14 countries.
The company manufactures custom-fabricated and finished aluminum extrusions.
Bonnell Aluminum serves the building and construction, automotive and transportation, consumer durables, machinery and equipment, and electrical and distribution markets.
Branded product lines include TSLOTS by Bonnell Aluminum and Futura Transitions by Bonnell Aluminum.
The company has manufacturing facilities located in Newnan GA, Carthage TN, Niles MI, Elkhart IN, and Clearfield UT.
Additional details regarding the Commerce Department's ruling were issued by The U.S. Aluminum Extruders Coalition on September 27, 2024.

facts: Bonnell Aluminum has its headquarters in Newnan, Georgia.
The company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Carthage, Tennessee; Niles, Michigan; and Elkhart, Indiana.
Bonnell Aluminum employs a team of more than 1,300 people.
The company specializes in the production of mill finish, anodized, painted, fabricated, and thermal-barrier assemblies of customized aluminum extrusions.
Product offerings include applications up to 16-inch diameter circles with mirror-like surface finishing.
Aluminum extrusions are used in construction, pleasure crafts, heat sinks for L.E.D. lighting, photovoltaic panels, automotive engineering, walkways, windows, and shower enclosures.
The company's work is visible at the Georgia Aquarium, Luxor’s pyramid-styled hotel & casino in Las Vegas, and the Philadelphia Eagles football stadium.
William Bonnell acquired his first extrusion press in 1939, making his firm the first in the industry to have in-house extrusion capability.
Scientists working with the U.S. Government in the Manhattan Project turned to William Bonnell for help extruding radioactive uranium to develop what became the world’s first atomic bomb.
Bonnell was the originator of more than 500 shapes which are still in use today.
In 1955, the William L. Bonnell Company emerged after buying out patents and trademarks affiliated with Trimedge.
The company expanded plant operations in Georgia under William Bonnell's leadership until his death in 1960.
In 1965, the company became a wholly owned subsidiary of Ethyl Corporation.
Ethyl Corporation opened the Tennessee facility in 1969 and later acquired an aluminum window fabricator formerly known as Capitol Products.
By the late 80s, Ethyl consolidated its aluminum, plastics, and energy holdings into Tredegar Corporation, where Bonnell Aluminum functions as a core business unit.
Duncan Crowdis serves as President of Bonnell Aluminum and leads an experienced group of executives.

During a period when Chinese imports captured up to 20% of America's market due to heavy government subsidies, Bonnell joined other extruders to file petitions with the U.S. Department of Commerce citing unfair trade practices.
Working with support from the U.S. Trade Commission, the team successfully secured an ultimate decision by the Department of Commerce to apply duties to extruded product imports from China.
As a result of these duties, Chinese imports have been reduced to a trickle compared to 2009 levels.
Following the recent economic downturn and diminished construction rates, Bonnell strategically diversified by acquiring Indiana-based extruder AACOA.
The company is pursuing opportunities in the automotive industry driven by a push for vehicle light-weighting to meet Federal fuel efficiency guidelines implemented by 2025.
Aluminum is utilized in automotive applications because it is one-third the weight of steel but offers appropriate strength for many applications.
Transportation will provide a significant future direction for the extrusion industry, including Bonnell Aluminum.
Bonnell Aluminum works in conjunction with the Aluminum Extruder’s Council to educate designers on the benefits and opportunities of using aluminum extrusions.
The company's leadership emphasizes securing long-term client relationships and retaining long-serving employees through a culture of high integrity and trust.
Bonnell Aluminum's vision is to act like a small, regional provider that genuinely wants customer business while leveraging its capabilities as a large extruder.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum | Business World Magazine

facts: The European Union-funded EU4Environment programme, with technical support of the United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO), hosted a national conference on Resource Efficient and Cleaner Production (RECP) in Georgia at Biltmore Hotel.
This year’s event marked an important milestone in the Action’s activities dedicated to Circular Economy (CE) and New Growth Opportunities in the country.
The Government of Georgia recognises the opportunities of the green economy and its potential for sustainable economic growth, especially in terms of job creation and poverty reduction.
Small- and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) represent 99.7% of Georgian enterprises.
SMEs are becoming central in the green and CE policymaking within various government institutions.
National strategic documents and legal frameworks increasingly provide incentives for green actions within SMEs.
Mr. Romeo Mikautadze serves as Deputy Minister of Economy and Sustainable Development of Georgia.
Between 2020-2023, within the EU-funded EU4Environment programme with UNIDO technical assistance, eleven professionals and 44 companies were assessed and trained using the RECP methodology.
The training covered 17 RECP Clubs members and 27 Demonstration Companies.
Overall, 115 RECP measures were recommended, which could potentially yield annual savings worth 2,173,268 EUR.
So far, 26 RECP measures have been implemented, expecting savings of 557,650 EUR per year.
Mr. Cătălin Gherman serves as Deputy Head of Cooperation of the Delegation of the European Union to Georgia.
EU4Environment is part of the EU’s efforts to open new opportunities for people and the economy due to twin green and digital transitions.
The event gathered over 100 participants in a hybrid format, centred around Georgia’s SMEs through six specialised sessions.

Stakeholders discussed practical means to base Georgia’s growth agenda on the CE, assessed the country’s integration of CE practices in the past decade, and rejuvenated the policy and regulatory framework for green growth.

International experiences from EU4Business and others, paired with national experiences on resource efficiency, value chains, and regulatory frameworks, were discussed regarding green skills and directing investment towards efficient use of resources.
Mr. Solomon Pavliashvili serves as Deputy Minister of Environmental Protection and Agriculture.
Georgia has been making significant progress toward the establishment of the CE in recent years.
The “Assessment of the circularity level of the Georgian Economy” policy document was created with assistance from the Swedish Government.
This document identifies 14 key economic sectors with the greatest potential for waste recycling and defines circularity goals and target indicators for national CE policy.
The document also identifies sectoral opportunities and develops policy options and recommendations.
This provides the framework for elaborating a national CE roadmap that will determine the pathway for Georgia to a circular transformation.
The national CE roadmap will also serve as the basis for developing the CE strategy.

In the past decade, the EU with UNIDO technical assistance built the capacity of Georgian SMEs through RECP Demonstration Projects and pilot exercises on Industrial Waste Mapping (IWM) and Product Environmental Footprint (PEF).
The IWM pilot exercise was carried out in the regions of Rustavi and Zestaponi, involving 723 enterprises employing 7,000 workers.
The PEF pilot trained 33 experts and initiated an in-depth exercise for five companies to assess selected products.
This work will contribute to promoting the potential benefits of the PEF methodology for Georgian enterprises interested in the EU Single Market for Green Products.
It will also assist Georgia on its path to EU membership, which requires harmonisation of the country’s legal system with the EU acquis.
Ms. Tatiana Chernyavskaya serves as EU4Environment Project Manager at UNIDO.
Resource efficiency is an essential component of CE and part of UNIDO’s mandate to accelerate and promote inclusive and sustainable industrial development, a key contributor to SDG 9.

The EU-funded “European Union for Environment” (EU4Environment) programme aims to help the EU’s Eastern Partnership countries preserve their natural capital and increase people’s environmental well-being by supporting environment-related action, demonstrating and unlocking opportunities for greener growth, and setting mechanisms to manage environmental risks and impacts better.
The Action’s implementation period is 2019-2024.

facts: The automotive industry in Georgia represents $2.8 billion in gross state product.
Since 1909, when the first automobile was assembled in the state, Kia Motors Manufacturing located there a hundred years later.
In the last five years prior to publication, Georgia located 97 automotive companies creating 7,376 jobs.

The state is home to more than 300 automotive-related facilities and six headquarters, including Cox Automotive, Genuine Parts Co., Mercedes-Benz USA LLC, Novelis, Exide Technologies, and Porsche Cars North America Inc.
In January 2018, Groupe PSA, the French manufacturer of Peugeot and Citroën and the second-largest carmaker in Europe, announced it will open its North American headquarters in Atlanta.
Automotive suppliers view Georgia as central to supplying manufacturing plants for BMW in South Carolina, Mercedes-Benz in Alabama, and Volkswagen in Tennessee.
Toyota, Honda, and Hyundai have additional manufacturing operations in Alabama, while Volvo and Mercedes-Benz are located in South Carolina.
Honda Precision Parts of Georgia builds transmissions in Tallapoosa.
TD Automotive makes air conditioning compressors in Pendergrass.
HL-A Co. (Honda) produces locks and related door accessories in Bremen.
IMS Gear manufactures electronic brake and steering systems in Gainesville.
voestal-pine Automotive Components forges fenders in White.
Kumho Tire fabricates tires in Macon.
Hitachi Automotive Systems is Georgia's largest metal equipment and components manufacturer, located in Monroe, and employs 1,200 people.
Hitachi produces valve timing controls, drive shafts, and electric power steering for customers including Ford, GM, Honda, Hyundai, Nissan, Subaru, and Aston Martin.
Hitachi celebrated its 20th anniversary in Walton County in July 2017.
Hitachi operates over 590,000 square feet of manufacturing space on 70 acres with annual revenue exceeding $500 million.
Hitachi credits Georgia's pro-business environment, transportation network, favorable tax strategy, lower land costs, and the Quick Start job training program for its success.
Hitachi is the largest employer in Walton County and works with the local school system to provide career paths for non-college-bound students.
In 2016, Hitachi received the "Community Impact Award" from the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
West Point is home to Kia Motors Manufacturing, which transformed the former textile town into a job hub.
Kia's suppliers in the area include Daesol Material Georgia LLC, Hyundai Dymos, and Sewon America.
In December 2017, Daesol completed construction of a new 120,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in Northwest Harris Business Park, creating 110 jobs with a $35 million investment.
Daesol manufactures automotive fiber padding at the Harris County facility.
In 2014, Hyundai Dymos began manufacturing seats for Kia Sorentos at its West Point plant, creating 350 jobs with a $35 million investment.
In 2008, Sewon America located operations in LaGrange, investing $300 million and growing its workforce from 360 in 2009 to 750.
Kia operates on 2,200 acres, employs more than 3,000 workers, and produced its one-millionth vehicle at the West Point facility in 2017.
Since Kia's arrival in 2009, more than 40 new businesses including restaurants and automotive suppliers have opened in the county.
The city of West Point's population swells to 10,000 people a day due to Kia and its suppliers, compared to a 2016 resident population of 3,766.
Georgia boasts more than 300,000 skilled production workers and a central location with access to international airports, ports, rail lines, and interstates.
Georgia's culture of innovation dates back to 1935 when Asa Phillips invented a ventshade that became standard equipment on Volkswagen Beetles for 25 years.
The state is home to two innovation centers and companies exploring connected cars, autonomous vehicle technologies, advanced diagnostics, and fuel efficiencies.
Companies in Georgia's auto tech space include AT&T Drive Studio, Car360, Elan Technology, Verizon Connect, and Wheego Technologies.
GM and Panasonic Automotive operate innovation centers in Atlanta and Peachtree City, respectively.
GM announced plans for its Atlanta innovation center in 2012, starting with 1,000 employees and growing significantly since.
Georgia's talent pool has spawned startups like Wheego, which develops self-driving/autonomous cars using artificial intelligence and machine learning.

Another Georgia Tech-spawned startup is Parkmobile, which helps users find parking spaces and processed more than 50 million transactions in North America in 2017 before being acquired by BMW in January 2018.
Fort Valley has been home to Blue Bird Corp., one of Georgia's two automotive manufacturers, since 1927.
Blue Bird employs about 2,400 people (nearly all in Georgia) and posted $991 million in revenue for fiscal year 2017.
In January 2018, Blue Bird moved its corporate headquarters to Macon to make room for a plant expansion.
In July 2018, Blue Bird unveiled two new electric-powered school buses following a $4.4 million Department of Energy grant awarded in December 2016.
Blue Bird built its first electric-powered bus in 1994 for a Southern California demonstration project and slated production of the latest iteration for 2018.
Blue Bird launched its propane-powered school bus in 2012, delivering its 10,000th propane bus in 2017, with school districts saving $2,000 to $3,000 per bus annually compared to diesel.
A school bus rolls off Blue Bird's Fort Valley production line every 13 minutes.
The Ray is an 18-mile stretch of I-85 serving as a testing ground for new transportation, safety, and energy generation technologies with a goal of net zero environmental impact.
The Visitor Information Center in West Point within The Ray hosts Georgia's first solar-powered PV4EV charging station and a tire safety check station that texts drivers their tire pressure.
The visitor center features a 7,000-square-foot pollinator garden for honeybees, monarch butterflies, and other species.
Adjacent to the visitor center is a 50-square-meter solar-paved highway using photovoltaic techniques to generate electricity while allowing traffic.
The Ray's right-of-way is farmed with Kernza plants that enrich soil, retain water, and sequester carbon, alongside bioswales to capture particulate pollutants from runoff.
The Ray is scheduled for complete repaving in 2019, with future iterations potentially featuring rubber roads made with recycled tires to reduce noise and increase durability.
source_title: Auto Evolution - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) delivered its first battery-electric Freightliner eM2 trucks.
The Freightliner eM2 is manufactured at DTNA’s plant in Portland, Oregon.
PITT OHIO, a transportation, warehouse, and logistics services company, is the first to deploy the eM2.
Two Freightliner eM2 Class 7 trucks were delivered to PITT OHIO for integration into its fleet.
The delivered trucks have 26-foot-long box bodies.
The trucks will operate out of PITT OHIO’s Cleveland terminal.
The deployment supports PITT OHIO’s Less-Than-Truckload (LTL) freight shipping business.
David Carson serves as senior vice president of sales and marketing at Daimler Truck North America.
DTNA is dedicated to providing innovative, safe and reliable solutions that adapt to the changing needs of its customers.
The delivery of the eM2 demonstrates DTNA’s ongoing commitment to its growing portfolio of zero-emissions vehicles.
The eM2 builds on the success of the conventional M2 and extends efficiency and versatility into the zero-emission vehicle space.
DTNA’s electric lineup includes the Jouley, an all-electric bus from Thomas Built Bus available since 2020.
DTNA offers the FCCC MT50e walk-in van chassis for final mile delivery, available since 2021.
The Class 8 Freightliner eCascadia has been available since 2022.
DTNA added the eM2 to its lineup in 2023.
The eM2 has a typical range of 180 miles for Class 6 and 250 miles for Class 7.
DTNA states the eM2 is ideal for pick-up and delivery customers who require an adaptable electric solution.
Vocational innovation vehicles were introduced last year and are in use with customers.
Fyda Freightliner, a DTNA Elite Support certified dealership with seven locations serving the Midwest, supported the eM2 sales process and delivery.
Fyda Freightliner has supported PITT OHIO for the past 20 years.
source_title: DTNA delivers Freightliner eM2 trucks to PITT OHIO

facts: Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) has announced plans for a substantial investment in a new electric vehicle engineering facility at its Swan Island headquarters in Portland, Oregon, totaling more than $40 million.
The new 110,000-square-foot engineering facility aims to consolidate multiple research projects, focusing on both battery electric and hydrogen-fuel cell electric vehicles, into a single location.
This strategic investment is projected to create approximately 150 new, high-wage jobs in the area.

John O’Leary, President and CEO of DTNA, stated that the company appreciates the City and State’s support of its expansion plans to further promote the development and proper testing of advanced technology and looks forward to collaborating on training and employing a future-ready workforce.
The new facility serves as an extension of DTNA’s “Electric Island” and will expedite the testing and advancement of charging equipment for commercial vehicles.
DTNA previously collaborated with Portland General Electric (PGE) in 2021 to establish a pioneering charging station for electric trucks.
Daimler will allocate $3 million toward a new Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE) training center situated adjacent to Electric Island.
The EVSE training center will serve as a training hub for the DTNA dealer network and a resource center for community stakeholders interested in the clean transportation industry.
Of the $3 million allocated for the training center, $1.5 million will be financed through a forgivable loan from Prosper Portland, the city’s economic development agency.
Daimler’s presence in Portland dates back to 1942 with the founding of the Freightliner brand, which Daimler acquired in 1981.
The establishment of DTNA’s North American trucks headquarters in Portland underscores the company’s long-standing commitment to the city.
source_title: Daimler Truck North America to Invest Over $40 Million in New Electric Vehicle Engineering Facility in Portland

facts: The Southern Workers Assembly expressed full solidarity with 7,000 Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) workers and UAW members in North Carolina, Tennessee, and Georgia engaged in a contract fight for record gains.
The workers' current UAW master contract with DTNA expires at midnight on April 26.
The UAW stated that if a new contract meeting workers' demands is not settled by the April 26 expiration, workers will go on strike.
In March, UAW workers at DTNA voted by a margin of 96 percent to authorize a strike if necessary.
Workers in the DTNA plants are prepared to take decisive action to win justice during the contract negotiations.
UAW workers at DTNA manufacture Freightliner and Western Star heavy trucks and school buses for Thomas Built Buses.

The DTNA contract fight is organized under the slogan “Record profits mean record contracts” and follows the historic “Stand Up” strike last fall by auto workers at plants owned by Ford, GM, and Stellantis.
Daimler Truck, the parent company of DTNA, is the largest commercial vehicle manufacturer in the world.
Since 2018, Daimler has seen a 90 percent increase in its profits.
In 2023 alone, Daimler Truck’s profits increased by 39 percent, reaching nearly $6 billion.
In March, CEO Martin Daum referred to company profits as “red hot” and asserted that “leftover money certainly belongs” to Daimler Truck shareholders.
The company plans to increase stock dividends by 46 percent.
Over the same period, the cost of basic living expenses such as groceries, gas, childcare, housing, and healthcare increased by upwards of 20 percent in some cases.
Many DTNA workers have been forced to take second jobs to make ends meet despite the company's high profitability.
Workers are demanding significant wage increases, cost of living adjustments, job security protections, improved retirement benefits, and an equalization of pay scales across all DTNA plants.
Auto workers across the South are initiating an unprecedented, coordinated organizing drive at 13 nonunion auto manufacturers in the region.
Workers at the Volkswagen plant in Chattanooga, Tennessee are set to vote to join the UAW on April 17-19.
Workers at the Mercedes-Benz plant in Vance, Alabama have recently filed for a UAW election after reaching supermajority support.
Organizing drives are going public at other auto plants across the Southeastern region.
The UAW is building solidarity with DTNA workers in Mexico and Brazil to counter corporate strategies that threaten to move jobs to Mexico to suppress wage demands.

UAW Region 9A Director Brandon Mancilla stated in a Labor Notes op-ed that the UAW is fully dedicated to building solidarity with workers in Mexico across auto, heavy truck manufacturing, and other sectors.

UAW Region 9A Director Brandon Mancilla wrote in his op-ed that “The best resistance to Donald Trump is to unite our working-class struggles…The UAW, and the labor movement as a whole, face an existential question: are we going to consent to our decline, or do what it takes to end the international race to the bottom?”
The auto industry is poised to transition production increasingly toward battery and electric vehicle (EV) manufacturing in the coming years, facilitated by billions in government subsidies.
Nearly two-thirds of the new EV sector jobs are expected to come to the U.S. South, particularly Georgia, North Carolina, and South Carolina.
Billions of dollars in capital investments across other manufacturing sectors are also flowing into the U.S. South.

The United States is now the cheapest country globally to produce electric vehicles, with companies choosing the Southeastern region to leverage its historically anti-labor political environment and socioeconomic challenges.
Workers across multiple sectors, including public schools, municipal services, and industry, are organizing to change the future of the Southeastern region.
A decisive victory for DTNA workers is viewed as critical to building working-class power and turning the tide against corporate anti-labor strategies in the South.
source_title: The South Stands Up! Solidarity with DTNA workers and auto workers on the move! - Southern Workers Assembly

facts: Daimler Truck North America recently announced two investments at its headquarters complex on Swan Island in Portland, Oregon: a new electric vehicle supply equipment training center near Electric Island and a new engineering facility at its campus.

The addition to Electric Island represents the next phase of testing and development of charging infrastructure for commercial vehicles since the first-of-its-kind public charging station was built in partnership with Portland General Electric and opened in 2021.

The anticipated project for the new training center will cost $3 million, with half of the costs funded through a forgivable loan from Prosper Portland, the City of Portland’s economic development agency.
The new training center will be used by DTNA dealers and as a resource center for community stakeholders interested in clean transportation.

John O’Leary, president and CEO of DTNA, stated that Daimler Truck North America appreciates the city and state’s support of its expansion plans to promote the development and proper testing of advanced technology and looks forward to collaborating on training and employing a future-ready workforce.

The new DTNA engineering facility will be approximately 110,000 square feet in size and will consolidate testing activities under one roof with a focus on research and development for emerging propulsion technologies, such as battery-electric and hydrogen-fueled vehicles.

The anticipated project for the new engineering facility will cost $40 million and will be partially funded by the State of Oregon with a $700,000 forgivable loan from the state’s Strategic Reserve fund.

Business Oregon Director Sophorn Cheang stated that Oregon is the location of choice for businesses leading clean technology innovation in the U.S., and that the partnership to support DTNA’s investment adds to the region’s competitiveness and will provide opportunities for a skilled workforce in manufacturing and engineering.
source_title: Daimler Truck North America invests $43M in clean transportation tech in Portland, Oregon | FleetOwner

facts: Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) has a decades-long track record of market leadership in the commercial vehicle industry.
John O’Leary serves as President and CEO of DTNA, steering the company through transformation, innovation, and challenges.
DTNA employs a dual-brand strategy focused on Freightliner and Western Star.
The Freightliner brand is recognized for on-highway and medium-duty vocational applications.
In a good year, DTNA sells around 200,000 vehicles in North America across a wide variety of customers.
Major US fleets such as Ryder, Penske, Schneider, and XPO predominantly run Freightliners.
DTNA expanded into the vocational market by investing in Western Star to build credibility in the heavy-duty vocational segment.
Western Star had a strong legacy in Canada and is now an emerging player in the heavy-duty vocational segment.
DTNA remains flexible and prepares scenarios to pivot quickly when there is more certainty regarding emissions policy or the economic outlook.
The company focuses on running a lean, highly efficient, and profitable operation to weather economic and regulatory uncertainty.
DTNA expects that once US clarity is gained on economic and emissions policy, it will be well-positioned to help grow the economy and accelerate out of uncertainty.
Many industry participants are currently waiting on the sidelines due to a lack of clear policy direction.
DTNA maintains strong products, excellent dealer representation, and loyal customers in its B2B business model.
Relationships and trust with key customers are crucial for DTNA's success regardless of external factors like tariffs or emissions regulations.
source_title: ITOY - Daimler Truck Capital Market Day 2025 | Interview with John O’Leary

facts: Daimler Truck North America renewed its Gold Level Partnership with the Women In Trucking Association on April 15, 2024.
Daimler Truck North America has been a partner of the Women In Trucking Association since 2012.
The partnership supports the mission to encourage the employment of women in the trucking industry, promote their accomplishments, and minimize obstacles they face.
Daimler Truck North America sponsors the annual Influential Woman in Trucking Award recognition program that honors talented, resourceful, and high-performing female leaders in the industry.
Kary Schaefer, Chief Engineer of Product Engineering at Daimler Truck North America, serves as chair on the Women In Trucking Association board of directors.

Kary Schaefer stated that Daimler Truck North America recognizes the value of innovation and creativity that comes from a strong culture of diverse backgrounds and perspectives due to rapid transformation in the truck industry.

Kary Schaefer noted the partnership allows Daimler Truck North America and its employees an opportunity to shape the future of the industry and workforce through the mission of the Women In Trucking Association.

Jennifer Hedrick, president and CEO of the Women In Trucking Association, stated appreciation for Daimler Truck North America's ongoing support and commitment to increasing gender diversity in transportation.
Jennifer Hedrick highlighted Kary Schaefer's leadership as chair of the Women In Trucking Association for over the past year.

The Women In Trucking Association was founded in 2007 and serves as a resource for more than 8,000 corporate and individual members located in the United States, Canada, Mexico, Japan, Australia, Sweden, South Africa, and New Zealand.

The Women In Trucking Association released the 2023 WIT Index, an official barometer to benchmark and measure the percentage of women who make up critical roles in transportation each year, with more than 300 company participants.
The Women In Trucking Association awarded a deserving member a 2018 Volvo VNL670 in collaboration with Arrow Truck Sales, Inc.

The Women In Trucking Association honored over 300 leaders in transportation through several programs and hosted more than 1,900 registered attendees at the 2023 Accelerate! Conference and Exhibition.

Gold Level Partners supporting the Women In Trucking Association include Arrow Truck Sales, Bridgestone Americas, Daimler Truck North America, FedEx Freight, Great Dane, J.B. Hunt Transport, Michelin North America, Navistar International, Inc., PACCAR, Penske Transportation Solutions, Ryder System, Walmart, and WM.
Daimler Truck North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Oregon.

Daimler Truck North America designs, engineers, manufactures, and markets medium- and heavy-duty trucks, school buses, vehicle chassis, and their associated technologies and components under the Freightliner, Western Star, Thomas Built Buses, Freightliner Custom Chassis Corp, and Detroit brands.
Daimler Truck North America is a subsidiary of Daimler Truck Holding AG (DTG), one of the world's leading commercial vehicle manufacturers.
source_title: Women In Trucking Association Announces Continued Gold Partnership with Daimler Truck North America

facts: Daimler Truck AG started production of the Freightliner eM2 at their subsidiary Daimler Truck North America (DTNA).
The battery electric medium-duty truck is designed for pick-up and delivery applications.
Rakesh Aneja, head of eMobility at DTNA, stated that the start of production marks another important milestone on their journey to a zero-emissions product line.
Rakesh Aneja added that customer feedback reinforces confidence that the Freightliner eM2 will meet and exceed expectations.
The Freightliner eM2 is being manufactured at DTNA’s truck manufacturing plant in Portland, Oregon.

First production units in the U.S. will be delivered to Ferguson, Hogan Truck Leasing, Inc., Penske Truck Leasing, PITT OHIO, Ryder System, Inc., TITAN Freight Systems and Velocity Truck Rental & Leasing.
In Canada, the first units will go to Brossard Leasing and Day & Ross.

The eM2 is built on the Freightliner M2 106 Plus platform and follows the introduction of the battery electric Freightliner eCascadia in 2022, extending CO2-neutral transportation to the medium-duty segment.
The vehicle features a proprietary, fully integrated, battery-electric Detroit ePowertrain.
Its design places electric motors and a 2-speed transmission directly on the drive axles, resulting in minimized energy cost per mile.
The class 6 single-motor model provides up to 190 continuous horsepower, a 194 kWh battery, and a typical range of 180 miles (290 kilometers) on a single charge.
The class 7 dual-motor variant offers up to 255 continuous horsepower, supported by a 291 kWh battery, providing a typical range of 250 miles (400 kilometers) on a single charge.
The eM2 features proprietary battery side impact protection and the Detroit Assurance suite of safety systems.

Safety systems include Active Brake Assist 5 (ABA5), Tailgate Warning, Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) down to 0 mph, Lane Departure Warning, Intelligent High-Beams, Automatic Wipers/Headlamps, and Side Guard Assist.

links.context: Read more: Daimler enters medium-duty EV race with Rizon brand

source_title: Daimler starts BEV Freightliner eM2 production | Trailer Body Builders

facts: Thomas Built Buses (TBB), a division of Daimler Truck Specialty Vehicles (DTSV), gave an update on the expansion of its Type A Minotour production capacity at its legacy manufacturing plant located at 1408 Courtesy Road in High Point, N.C.

The expansion builds on Thomas Built’s legacy in High Point, home to the company’s original facility dating back to 1916, where it first produced streetcars before evolving to build Type C, Type D and now Type A buses.
The expansion reflects Thomas Built’s streamlined production strategy following the relocation of Type D production to the Saf-T-Liner plant earlier in 2025.
Work on the Type A expansion began in late 2024 and is expected to be completed by 2026.
T.J. Reed, president and CEO of DTSV, stated that investing in the legacy facility honors more than a century of craftsmanship in High Point while continuing to modernize for the future.
The expansion includes a series of modernization efforts aimed at improving workflow, enhancing safety and supporting long-term sustainability.
The project will increase production capacity by more than 50 percent, strengthening Thomas Built's operational efficiency and supporting continued growth in North Carolina.
Dedicated space for Minotour production has nearly tripled, now encompassing roughly 72,000 square feet of the building.

Jeff Allen, senior vice president of operations and specialty vehicles at Daimler Truck North America (DTNA), stated that the expansion preserves the company's people, community, and commitment to building safe, reliable buses for students everywhere.
DTNA aims to strengthen its operations in North Carolina while ensuring it can serve more customers with greater efficiency, sustainability and innovation.
Thomas Built Buses was founded in 1916 and is headquartered in High Point, North Carolina.

Thomas Built Buses is a subsidiary of Daimler Truck North America LLC, which designs, engineers, manufactures and markets medium- and heavy-duty trucks, school buses, vehicle chassis and their associated technologies and components under the Freightliner, Western Star, Thomas Built Buses, Freightliner Custom Chassis Corp and Detroit brands.
Thomas Built Buses and Freightliner Custom Chassis Corp together form Daimler Truck Specialty Vehicles.
Daimler Truck North America is a subsidiary of Daimler Truck, one of the world’s leading commercial vehicle manufacturers.
source_title: Thomas Built Buses expands Type A Minotour production capacity at plant in High Point | Daimler Truck North America

facts: Daimler Truck North America has produced big rigs in Portland since the 1940s and is garnering attention for work on trucks that run on alternative fuels and operate autonomously.

In the 1930s, Leland James, president of Consolidated Freightways, asked manufacturers to use aluminum rather than steel to make trucks lighter for mountainous Pacific Northwest routes; when they refused, he established the company that became Freightliner.
The Freightliner manufacturing facility in Portland opened in 1947.
In 1981, Freightliner was bought by Daimler-Benz AG, followed by acquisitions of Thomas Built Buses and Western Star Trucks.
By 1992, Freightliner had become the nation’s leading producer of heavy-duty trucks.
In 2004, Freightliner opened a full-scale wind tunnel in Portland, the first in North America designed to test full-size commercial vehicles.
In 2008, Freightliner assumed the name Daimler Truck North America, which was renamed again in 2021 following a spin-off from German industrial conglomerate Daimler AG.

Daimler Truck North America continues to produce more medium- and heavy-duty vehicles in North America than any other company, with headquarters, testing, and key manufacturing functions remaining in Oregon.
Most of the company’s Oregon activity happens on Portland’s Swan Island, including a LEED Platinum-certified headquarters on the banks of the Willamette River.
The Western Star facility is unique in that it also manufactures Daimler Truck’s electric vehicles alongside its specialized heavy-haul and off-highway trucks.
Production of the heavy-duty Freightliner eCascadia began last year.
Production of the company’s electric box truck, the eM2, will begin by the end of this year, according to spokesman Fred Ligouri.
Demand for battery-powered big rigs is currently constrained by limited recharging infrastructure.

Battery-powered trucks are well-suited to specific applications such as local pickup and delivery as well as drayage, which involves the delivery of container trucks from ports to nearby repackaging centers.
Battery power works well for school buses because they have a very defined duty cycle that includes a mid-day pause perfect for recharging.
About 400 electric buses manufactured by Daimler Truck’s South Carolina-based Thomas Built Bus line are now on the road.
Daimler Truck opened “Electric Island” in 2021, a charging station for battery-powered commercial vehicles located across the street from its headquarters.
The Electric Island facility operates as a partnership with Portland General Electric and is open to the public.
All of the company’s reliability and durability testing takes place at its High Desert Proving Grounds, an 87-acre facility located about 120 miles southeast of headquarters in Madras.
The facility features a 3.5-mile test track that allows the company to simulate a vehicle’s full life cycle in only six months.
Daimler Truck uses the proving grounds to test new technology away from busy roads and has tested autonomous trucks there, which the company hopes to produce at scale by the end of the decade.
Daimler Truck acquired Virginia-based Torc Robotics in 2019 to advance its autonomous technology capabilities.
The company partnered with Alphabet subsidiary Waymo, a leading developer of automated driver technology, in 2020.
The company employs about 3,000 people in Oregon and southwest Washington.
Design and development work orchestrated from and tested in Oregon continues the pursuit of efficiency that began back in the 1940s.
The company’s Cascadia on-highway truck has become 35% more efficient since its introduction in 2007.
Daimler Truck aims to produce only vehicles that are CO2-neutral in driving operation for its core markets by 2039.
source_title: Daimler Truck North America Pursues Efficiency, Zero Emissions - Oregon Business & Industry

facts: Incorporated in 1968, DeKalb Tool & Die, Inc. specializes in the design, construction, and repair of a wide range of metalworking tools.
The company produces progressive dies, transfer dies, draw dies or single hit tooling.
Mechanical presses, hydraulic presses, and double action presses from 60 to 1800 tons are available at the facility.
The facility has coil feed capability up to 60 inches wide for producing stamped metal parts.
The company's engineering staff handles CAD design for tooling projects.
DeKalb Tool & Die delivers critical tooling requirements on one of its company trucks.
DeKalb Tool & Die was recently awarded Blue Bird Corporation’s Global Supplier of the Year award.
The manufacturing facility is located at 2220 Stephens St., Tucker, Georgia, United States ZIP: 30084.
The Tucker, Georgia location is convenient to both the Atlanta International Airport and the Atlanta Perimeter Highway.
The company offers personal tours of its tooling and manufacturing facility for visitors in the Atlanta area.
DeKalb Tool & Die provides services including designing and building new tooling, modification and repair of existing tooling, or emergency metal stamping.

facts: DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee, Inc. (DMTN) is located in Maryville, Tennessee.

DMTN focuses on the manufacture of inverters, instrument clusters, electronics (engine, safety, and device products), and body electronics (keyless entry, air conditioning control units, tire pressure monitoring systems and body control modules) for its global customers.
The Maryville campus comprises more than 2.6 million square feet of manufacturing, warehouse and administrative floor space in 15 buildings on 175 acres of land.
DMTN was established in 1988.
The facility's main business field manufactures electrification and safety components, instrument clusters, and automotive electronic products.
DMTN is located at 1720 Robert C Jackson Drive, Maryville, TN 37801 US.
Chuntao Ye serves as President of DMTN.
DENSO holds 100% ownership of DMTN.
DMTN maintains ISO 14001:2015 Advanced Waste Management Systems certification (Certificate Number: E00030).
DMTN maintains IATF 16949:2016 certification with DQS Certificate Numbers DMTN-101: 10000650, DMTN-201: 10000652, DMTN-202: 10000651, and DMTN-203/204: 10003548.
