links.context: Gainesville-Hall County Economic Development Council website

source_title: Gainesville-Hall Metro Area #1 in Best Performing Cities - Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce

facts: Hanon Systems offers a family of eCompressors ranging from compact 33cc units to high-output 45cc models.
The eCompressor family delivers industry-leading acoustic performance and efficiency across a wide voltage range.
The compressors are designed to handle advanced refrigerants including R744 and R290.
These compressors provide the durability, control, and performance needed for today’s electrified and heat pump systems.
The modular design for 33cc and 45cc eCompressors enables full-range manufacturing on a single line to cut costs and accelerate production.
The modular design supports voltage applications up to 800V.
The high-performance, high-durability 45cc eCompressor offers high speed operation to support system approaches like vapor injection or hot gas bypass systems.
The R1234yf/R290 eCompressor is designed for EV and Hybrid vehicle platforms.
The R744 eCompressor features a durable scroll design controlled by an integrated electric motor and sophisticated electronic inverter.
The R744 eCompressor provides efficient cooling for conventional, heat pump and multi-loop cooling systems.

To accommodate the R744 system’s unique requirements, the R744 eCompressor features minimized component clearances, an improved casting process producing low porosity and high strength, a backpressure control valve for scroll stability, and a modular inverter with a single ePin interface.
The Rotary Suction Compressor features a fixed displacement swashplate design that draws refrigerant through the center shaft.
The Rotary Suction Compressor delivers better fuel economy by creating less suction loss than a traditional suction valve.
The Rotary Suction Compressor is offered in a wide range of displacements, with features designed to improve coefficient of performance (COP) and reliability.
The Variable Swashplate Compressor varies its duty cycle and the amount of refrigerant flow to offer improved power consumption and fuel economy.

The Variable Swashplate Compressor is available in a range of displacements and clutched or clutchless designs, with enhanced design features to improve efficiency, durability and noise, vibration and harshness (NVH).
The Vapor Injection eCompressor allows two-stage compression within a single compressor.
Optional liquid gas separator (LGS) or economizer and electronic expansion valves (EXVs) are modularized for efficient options in the Vapor Injection eCompressor.
The Vapor Injection eCompressor improves driving range and battery cooling capability by providing a higher coefficient of performance (COP).
The R1234yf/R290 eCompressor is designed for EV and Hybrid vehicle platforms.
The R744 eCompressor is designed for EV and Hybrid vehicle platforms.
The Rotary Suction Compressor is designed for ICE vehicle platforms.
The Variable Swashplate Compressor is designed for ICE vehicle platforms.
The Vapor Injection eCompressor is designed for EV and Hybrid vehicle platforms.
source_title: Compressors | Hanon Systems

facts: Hanon Systems is a leading global provider of automotive thermal and energy management solutions.
The company inaugurated two new establishments in Hungary: a greenfield production facility in Pécs and a building expansion in Réstätg.
The new manufacturing facility in Pécs is located in Baranya county.
The Pécs facility increases production capacity for fluid transport products.
The Pécs facility strengthens the company’s manufacturing footprint in Europe.
The Pécs facility provides 22,464 square meters of manufacturing space.
The Pécs facility accommodates equipment including forming, brazing, welding and bending, assembly lines and testing for automotive air conditioning (A/C) lines.
The Pécs site offers the opportunity for further expansion to address future business needs.
The Pécs site currently employs more than 300 employees.
The building expansion in Réstätg is located in Nógrád county.
The Réstätg expansion adds an additional 4,500 square meters to the existing site.
The Réstätg expansion supports additional capacity of aluminium die castings for compressor operations in Europe.
The Réstätg building is equipped with modern machinery for horizontal die casting.
There are currently more than 150 employees at the Réstätg site.
Min Sung, Hanon Systems President and CEO, said the company is pleased to grow in Hungary.
Min Sung stated that the locations for their investment program were carefully chosen as an important hub in efforts to support the industry’s migration toward e-mobility.

Min Sung noted that the company continues to explore bringing new and innovative technologies to market and implementing best practices that bring a positive impact to communities, employees, customers and the planet.
source_title: Hanon Systems expands its manufacturing capability in Hungary | Cooling India Monthly Business Magazine on the HVACR Business | Green HVAC industry | Heating, Ventilation, Air conditioning and Refrigeration News Magazine Updates, Articles, Publications on HVACR Business Industry | HVACR Business Magazine

facts: Hanon Systems is a leading global automotive thermal management supplier and a subsidiary of Hankook & Company Group.
Hanon Systems announced it will participate in the AAPEX 2025 exhibition in Las Vegas, USA, from November 4 to 6.
This marks the company's first joint exhibition with Hankook & Company Group affiliates.
Joint participation by three affiliates — Hankook Tire, Hanon Systems, and Hankook & Company's ES Division — presents integrated solutions for electrified and sustainable mobility.
The group is expanding its innovation and technological leadership into the aftermarket to strengthen global competitiveness.
AAPEX (Automotive Aftermarket Products Expo) is recognized as North America's leading automotive aftermarket exhibition and the largest industry event in the Western Hemisphere.

Through this platform, Hanon Systems aims to showcase its latest advancements in thermal management solutions, reinforcing its position as a global leader in automotive innovation that supports both OEMs and the aftermarket.
Participation with Hankook & Company group affiliates serves to strengthen brand awareness and foster strategic partnerships across North and Latin American markets.
Hanon Systems Vice Chairman and CEO Soo-Il Lee will attend the exhibition in person to meet with global customers and partners, discussing opportunities for future collaboration.
The company plans to develop tailored solutions aligned with customer needs to expand global business partnerships based on these engagements.

The Hanon Systems exhibition area will feature six themed zones: HVAC systems, compressors, coolant and refrigerant modules, heat exchangers, electronic & fluid pressure products, and PACE award-winning technologies.

At the booth entrance, a vehicle mock-up will demonstrate the Group's comprehensive integrated solutions, incorporating Hanon Systems' 4th-generation heat pump system, Hankook Tire's iON evo formula E edition, and Hankook & Company's ES Division AGM battery.
Hanon Systems' 4th-generation heat pump system was first applied to the Kia EV3.
The heat pump system is the world's first to feature a parallel heat-source recovery structure that simultaneously utilizes ambient air and waste heat from the motor and battery.
This innovation design enhances cabin comfort and battery thermal efficiency, contributing to extended driving range for electric vehicles (EVs).
An advanced controller further optimizes performance by integrating hardware and software to efficiently manage the vehicle's thermal system.
The booth will showcase a range of eco-friendly EV components utilizing natural refrigerants, including Hanon Systems' R744-based electric compressor and R290-based refrigerant modules.
The R744 electric compressor is supplied to the Volkswagen Group's MEB platform.
The R744 electric compressor achieved a major production milestone of one million units in September 2025.
In the PACE Award zone, visitors can see the company's award-winning visible-light LED photocatalyst HVAC technology.
This technology is designed to enhance in-vehicle air quality through advanced sterilization and deodorization.

The innovation delivers 98.5% sterilization and 97.5% deodorization efficiency, while generating zero electromagnetic interference and offering semi-permanent durability compared with conventional ionizers.

Vice Chairman and CEO Soo-Il Lee stated that although APPEX traditionally focuses on the aftermarket, it is recognized as a meeting point for innovation and collaboration across the entire mobility industry.
Through the exhibition, Hanon Systems will highlight technologies that shape the future of sustainable mobility and demonstrate the strength of Hankook & Company Group synergy.
All visitors are invited to experience the latest technologies and integrated mobility solutions at booth A30021, level 2, Venetian Expo during APPEX 2025.
Hanon Systems was founded in 1986.
In January 2025, Hanon Systems became a subsidiary of Hankook & Company Group.
Its offering includes a wide range of solutions in the areas of heating, ventilation and air conditioning, powertrain cooling, compressor, fluid transport, and electronics and fluid pressure.
The company currently operates 50 manufacturing sites and three regional innovation centers.
Hanon Systems employs more than 20,000 people across 21 countries.
source_title: Hanon Systems Joins Hankook and Company Group to Showcase Thermal Management Technologies at AAPEX 2025

facts: Hanon Systems USA LLC is a leading supplier of advanced automotive thermal and energy management solutions.
The company serves global OEMs with innovative technologies for electric, hybrid, and conventional vehicles.
Hanon Systems USA LLC has recently focused on expanding its portfolio for electric and hybrid vehicles.
The company invests in advanced thermal management technologies.
Hanon Systems USA LLC strengthens global partnerships.
Hanon Systems USA LLC was integrated into Hankook & Company Group as a core subsidiary in 2025.
The company makes strategic efforts to expand market presence and reach new customer segments.
Hanon Systems USA LLC implements advanced technologies to improve service delivery and customer experience.
The company delivers comprehensive thermal and energy management solutions for the automotive industry, including advanced HVAC, powertrain cooling, and fluid pressure technologies.
The company’s portfolio supports electric, hybrid, and internal combustion engine vehicles.
Hanon Systems USA LLC leverages global R&D and manufacturing capabilities to meet the evolving needs of automotive OEMs.
Its operations are backed by a legacy of innovation and strategic growth.
The company is positioned as a trusted partner in vehicle climate and energy systems.
Hanon Systems USA LLC is located in Novi, Michigan, USA.
The company was founded in 1986.
The page lists the identifier 018880.KS.
source_title: Hanon Systems USA LLC | Automotive Thermal Management Sol...

facts: Tennessee has committed to give $13 million in incentives to suppliers for Ford’s new electric truck factory at BlueOval City.
Magna Seating will receive $3 million for its factory at BlueOval City.
Cosma International's factory at the BlueOval City site in Haywood County will receive $7.5 million in incentives.
A third manufacturing facility in Lawrence County will receive $2.5 million in incentives.
The incentive package is set to be approved at a meeting of the State Funding Board on Monday.
Hanon Systems USA will receive $4.5 million in incentives for its operations in Loudon City.
EnChem America will receive $2 million in incentives for its operations in Haywood County.
McNeilus Truck and Manufacturing will receive $1.3 million in incentives for its Murfreesboro facility.
Additional incentives of $1 million and $404,000 for unnamed companies will be announced at the State Funding Board meeting.
Magna has planned to spend $790 million to build an 800,000-square-foot frame and battery enclosures facility and a 140,000-square-foot seating facility at BlueOval City.
The Tennessee Legislature previously approved $884 million in incentives for Ford at the BlueOval City facility during a special session in October 2021.
Magna is expected to employ 1,300 workers across its three facilities, with 750 at the battery enclosure plant and 300 at the seating plant.
The Magna plants are expected to be ready for production in 2025.
The components produced by these suppliers will be used on the Ford F-150 Lightning trucks manufactured at BlueOval City.
Ford is expected to produce 500,000 electric trucks per year at full production capacity at the site.
Hanon Systems is a South Korean company that plans to build a $170 million manufacturing facility for its thermal supplies for electric vehicles.
These Hanon thermal supplies include components for heating ventilation and air conditioning, powertrain cooling, compressors, fluid transport, and electronics and fluid pressure.
source_title: Tennessee To Give Ford Electric Truck Supplier $13M In Incentives | Tennessee Conservative

facts: HELLA Automotive Mexico Irapuato has been producing for four years as of September 2018 and is positioned as one of the most advanced plants in the manufacturing of lighting components worldwide.
The German company settled in Irapuato, Guanajuato, with an investment of high double digit in million dollars.
This facility became the fifth productive plant launched by HELLA in Mexico.

It is one of the largest plants from Grupo Hella and aims to contribute to the global vision of being a number one company at the forefront for manufacturing lighting, headlights, tail lights, and interior lighting.
Harpak Mozaffari Gilani, director of Planta Hella Irapuato, chose the area due to proximity with assemblers and sector supply, and detected potential in human capital.
The plant has important lighting development projects for vehicles, and customers are satisfied by its location and layout, considering it a benchmark in Grupo Hella.
Developing a new product requested by a customer takes up to four years before market launch.
Car manufacturers present new vehicle concepts or lighting ideas, or ask Hella to present development proposals.
The development process involves detailed quoting, proposals, a development phase, tooling manufacturing, and an assembly line setup.
Each light undergoes a validation and quality process where changes are made or manufacturing starts depending on development complexity.
The plant currently operates 20 production lines.
Manufacturing capability reaches 13,000 daily units.
The facility generates about 6.5 million tail lights and headlights per year.
Little more than half of the annual output is exported to the United States.
Automotive supply in Mexico has improved over recent years, with companies becoming increasingly independent of raw material imports.
Improved local supply reduces order waiting time compared to importing components from other countries, which takes a month.
Local suppliers deliver components in a couple of hours, providing a logistical advantage.
Raw material granulated plastic continues to be imported, creating an opportunity for a local company to provide it.
The Irapuato plant employs more than 2,200 collaborators.
A 30 percent increase in personnel was projected for May 2019.
The automotive industry experiences continuous personnel rotation of 6.5 percent.
Hella seeks to retain talent by generating a better labor environment and including families in recreational activities.
The plant operates a Training Center with an approximate capacity of 100 people for theoretical and practical training on assembly processes.
Management aims to foster the idea of Familia Hella, recognizing personnel effort and results while involving families to transfer company values.
Total property land covers 120,000 m2.
Total construction area is 55,000 m2.
Start of operations was in 2014.
source_title: Made in Mexico HELLA lighting up the Mexican and from Abroad Automotive Industry

facts: Hitachi Astemo installed India’s first ground-mounted solar power plant of 3 megawatts (MW) at its Jalgaon manufacturing plant.
The 3 MW solar power plant will be built in an area of 43,301 sqm.
The ground-mounted solar power plant will consist of 7,128 ground-mounted solar panels and 10 inverters.
Hitachi Astemo is known for the development, manufacture, sale, and service of automotive and transportation components.
The solar power plant will embark on a new journey in the field of sustainable energy in India.
Hitachi is working to reduce its carbon emissions and become carbon neutral by 2050.
With this plant, the company would be able to eliminate close to 4,000 tons of carbon dioxide emissions each year.
Eliminating this amount of CO2 will be equal to planting around 150,000 trees.
The installation of the 3-megawatt solar power plant is just the beginning of the company’s plan.
The company plans to commission an additional 1.5-megawatt solar power plant by March 2023.
At its Jalgaon plant, the company produces brake systems for 3-wheelers and 4-wheelers.
The Jalgaon plant manufacturing includes disc brakes and drum brakes.
The Jalgaon plant is also engaged in foundry operations.
Hitachi Astemo is a subsidiary of the Japanese firm Hitachi, established in 2021.
The company is constantly working to provide efficient modifications in the automobile industry.
Recently, Hitachi Astemo along with Hitachi developed thin-type inverter technology for EVs.
The developed thin-type inverter technology makes the machine more compact as well as energy efficient.
source_title: Hitachi Astemo planted its first solar power plant in India

facts: Japanese automotive firm Astemo is investing £100m in its Horwich factory.
The investment could create more than 200 skilled jobs.
The Tokyo-based firm makes electronic and powertrain systems for vehicles.
Astemo will create a new production line in Horwich to manufacture inverters for electric vehicles.
The expansion is backed by the UK Government and follows a “significant” contract win.
Astemo states it will be the only company in the UK with that specific EV inverter production capability.
The move puts Greater Manchester at the heart of the country’s push towards electric vehicles.
The expansion is expected to create around 220 new high-skilled manufacturing jobs.
The announcement follows a trade mission to Japan by Greater Manchester leaders.
Metro mayor Andy Burnham and city council leader Bev Craig met with Astemo executive officer Mathieu Devillard at the company’s Tokyo HQ.
Andy Burnham called the investment a huge vote of confidence in Bolton and Greater Manchester.
Burnham stated the project puts the city-region at the forefront of the electric vehicle revolution, creating high-quality, long-term jobs and opportunities in advanced manufacturing.
Burnham noted that during the trade mission to Japan, Astemo's long-term commitment to Greater Manchester was clear and they will have full backing as they look to grow over the coming years.
Burnham stated international engagement has made Greater Manchester the number one UK destination for foreign direct investment outside London.

Burnham added that Japanese companies like Astemo have found a genuine partner in the city-region and its growing reputation for innovation is increasingly making it the natural choice for international businesses who want to grow.
Bev Craig said the new investment demonstrates the confidence international businesses have in Greater Manchester's economy and the real economic value created by long-term engagement with Japan.
Craig noted that Greater Manchester's diverse, resilient economy is worth approximately £80 billion annually, providing a foundation for Japanese companies to establish and expand UK operations.
Craig mentioned looking forward to celebrating the depth of cultural and trading partnership with Japan during Japan Week in Manchester in September this year.
Bolton Council’s leader, Coun Nick Peel, called the news fantastic for Bolton, which will create hundreds of jobs in Horwich and ensure a new production line for EV inverters.
Peel stated pioneering companies like Astemo are investing in their people and technologies for the long-term and making a home in Bolton.
Peel noted that investments like this create a renewed sense of excitement and confidence in the borough that will attract further investment.
Business and Trade Secretary Jonathan Reynolds called the news "a huge win for Bolton and Greater Manchester".
Reynolds added the investment helps build a competitive zero-emission vehicle supply chain in the UK.

Reynolds stated security for working people is their number one priority, and by backing the world-leading auto sector they are creating conditions for increased investment, bringing growth, jobs, and opportunities to every part of the UK.
source_title: Greater Manchester 'at forefront of EV revolution' after Japanese firm creates 200 jobs with £100m investment - Manchester Evening News

facts: Hitachi Astemo was created in 2021 from the merger of Hitachi Automotive Systems with three other Japan-based suppliers: Nissin Kogyo, Keihin, and Showa.
The newly formed company focuses on safety, electrification, comfort, and sustainability.

The strategy combines the manufacturing and technical expertise of Nissin and Chassis Brakes in brake systems, Keihin’s powertrain business, and Showa’s suspension and steering business with the core businesses of Hitachi Automotive Systems.
Tim Clark serves as CEO and president of Hitachi Astemo Americas.
The "Astemo" portion of the company name stands for Advanced Sustainable Technologies for Mobility.
Plans include expanding capabilities in powertrain technology, chassis systems, and advanced driver-assistance features.
The merger reshapes traditional Japanese supplier family groupings, as founding companies come from the Honda family while Hitachi was previously part of the Nissan family.
40% of Hitachi Astemo’s business is with Honda.
The remaining business comes from Nissan, General Motors, and Ford.
As part of Hitachi Ltd., the company possesses technical depth, software capabilities, advanced manufacturing skills, and global scale.
The goal is to expand business with other carmakers, with Honda's percentage expected to decline as new business is acquired.
Annual sales are expected to increase to $14.8 billion in fiscal 2025 with an EBITDA margin of 15%.
Revenue from the electrification business is expected to reach $3 billion in fiscal 2025 and more than double current levels by 2030.
The company produces inverters, which convert electricity derived from batteries to alternating current that drives electric motors, as well as electric motors themselves.
Hitachi Astemo has considerable experience building components necessary for electric powertrains amid an industry debate over whether automakers should make or buy these parts.
Growth plans will draw on the R&D of Hitachi Ltd. in electrification, digital systems, and software.
Hitachi Ltd.’s digital expertise aligns with auto industry efforts to deliver over-the-air updates to vehicles.
John Nunneley serves as senior vice president of Hitachi Astemo Americas.
Nunneley states the company will not give up on internal combustion engines, anticipating a long life for them but less technical development.
Current targets include the U.S. reaching a 67% BEV penetration of new light-vehicle sales by 2032 and zeroing out new ICE vehicle sales by 2040.
A significant number of ICE vehicles will remain on the road after 2040, requiring replacement parts.
Over the next decade-plus, hybrid ICE and EV systems are expected to increase, providing another source of business.
The company can supply finished modules and complete systems, which are in demand from many large and small OEMs.
Hitachi Astemo is a significant supplier to the motorcycle sector due to its long association with Honda.
The company has substantial business in hydraulic suspensions, foundation brakes, calipers, and throttle bodies.
The company recently expanded its U.S. manufacturing capacity with a $100 million, 752,000-ft. (69,863-sq.-m) expansion of its Berea, KY plant.
This expansion is designed to specifically support the growth of BEV-related business.
To increase brand exposure, the company bought into a sponsorship in the IndyCar series with Team Penske and in motorcycle racing.

facts: Hitachi Astemo Aftermarket BU China region attended Automechanika shanghai organized from December 2 to 5.
The total exhibition area spans 350,000 square meters with a record high of 6,763 exhibitors, marking a 20% increase from the previous session.
Hitachi Astemo celebrated the 20th anniversary of Automechanika shanghai alongside its brands HITACHI, TOKICO, NISSIN, KEIHIN, and SHOWA at Booth E50 in Hall 2.1.
The exhibition theme was "Green, Innovation, Diversity," highlighting Hitachi Astemo's commitment to green and new energy mobility solutions.
Hitachi Astemo displayed aftermarket product solutions in powertrain systems, intelligent chassis systems, steering systems, and braking systems, highlighting Honda's original auto parts.
For the first time, Hitachi Astemo introduced motorcycle modified shock absorbers and braking systems at the event.
Several new product lines were showcased to reinforce localized product development in China.
Traditional star products displayed include high-pressure fuel pumps, ignition coils, airflow sensors, and direct injectors.

Focus areas included engine and chassis systems such as fuel pumps, oxygen sensors, ABS wheel speed sensors, VTC, OCV, EGR valves, brake discs, brake calipers, brake master cylinders, brake fluid, and shock absorbers.
Nearly 20 product lines were exhibited, covering powertrain, braking, and suspension systems.
Hitachi Astemo acts as a Tier 1 supplier of fuel injectors, with all products being original equipment (OE) that provide rapid and precise fuel injection using high-speed electromagnets and pivots.

All brake disc products undergo rigorous bench testing to ensure stable performance under extreme conditions, meeting OE dimensional and tolerance standards with quality equal to or exceeding original factory requirements.
Hitachi Astemo has been supplying shock absorbers to major OEMs since 1934 and equips one in every five vehicles worldwide.
The company operates 13 shock absorber production facilities across seven countries, making it one of the world's largest shock absorber suppliers.
All shock absorber products undergo stringent durability tests and 100% quality inspections to ensure stable and reliable performance.
The motorcycle product portfolio highlighted included brake pads, calipers, front master cylinders, and high-performance front and rear shock absorbers.
The exhibition concluded with the signing of the Hitachi Astemo engine oil contract project.
A FY25 Honda distributor agreement was continued with Guangzhou Yishan Enterprise.
An outstanding Honda after-sales product distributor was recognized for FY24.
Honda key product training was completed during the event.
Ms. Teresa Li delivered a presentation titled "Adhere to the path of sustainable development in the involution" at the Sustainable Development Forum on December 5.
The four-day exhibition concluded as a resounding success.

entity_name: LG Energy Solution and Hyundai Motor Group Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
canonical_name: LG Energy Solution and Hyundai Motor Group Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
facts: LG Energy Solution (LGES) and Hyundai Motor Group announced an EV battery cell manufacturing joint venture in the U.S. on May 26, 2023.
The two companies signed a memorandum of understanding to produce EV batteries in the U.S. and accelerate electrification efforts in North America.
The signing ceremony occurred at LGES headquarters in Seoul with Youngsoo Kwon, CEO of LG Energy Solution, and Jaehoon Chang, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, in attendance.
Youngsoo Kwon, CEO of LG Energy Solution stated, “Two strong leaders in the auto and battery industries have joined hands, and together we are ready to drive the EV transition in America.”

Youngsoo Kwon added, “By further advancing our product competitiveness and global operational expertise, LG Energy Solution will commit our best efforts to offering the ultimate sustainable energy solutions to our customers.”

Jaehoon Chang, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company stated, “Hyundai Motor Group is focusing on its electrification efforts to secure a leadership position in the global auto industry. We will create a strong foundation to lead the global EV transition through establishing a new EV battery cell plant with LG Energy Solution, a leading global battery producer and long-time partner.”
LGES and Hyundai Motor Group will each hold a 50 percent stake in the joint venture.
The joint venture involves a total investment amount of over USD 4.3 billion (KRW 5.7 trillion).
The new battery cell manufacturing facility will be located in Bryan County, Savannah, Georgia.
The site is adjacent to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, which was under construction at the time of the announcement.
The joint venture will have an annual production capacity of 30 GWh.
This capacity will support the production of 300,000 EV units annually.
Construction for the facility is scheduled to begin in the second half of 2023.
The joint venture plans to start battery production at the end of 2025 at the earliest.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the new plant.
Hyundai Mobis will supply the assembled battery packs to Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
The facility aims to create a stable regional battery supply and enable Hyundai Motor Group to respond quickly to soaring U.S. EV demand.
With this joint venture, LGES will have seven battery plants operating or under construction in the U.S.
LGES is concentrating most of its resources on expanding U.S. production capacity to expedite the clean energy transition in the country.
LGES and Hyundai Motor Group have a long-standing electrification partnership, having previously supplied EV batteries for the Elantra Hybrid, Kona Electric, and IONIQ 6 dedicated EV.
The Hyundai Elantra Hybrid, an LPi hybrid vehicle introduced in 2009, was Hyundai Motor Group’s first electrified model.
In 2021, LGES and Hyundai Motor Group began construction of a battery cell joint venture in Indonesia, scheduled to start production in the first half of 2024.
This marks the second battery manufacturing joint venture between LGES and Hyundai Motor Group as they aim to strengthen their long-time partnership in electrification efforts.
source_title: LG Energy Solution and Hyundai Motor Group to Establish Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture in the U.S. – LG Energy Solution

facts: The South Korean government has secured the release of about 300 nationals detained following a raid by US authorities on an electric vehicle battery joint venture between Hyundai Motor Co. and LG Energy Solution Ltd. in Georgia.
Kang Hoon-sik, Seoul’s presidential chief of staff, told senior ruling party officials on Monday that negotiations with the US government on their release have concluded.
Kang stated, “Our government, business associations and relevant companies worked as one to secure their release.”
The detained Korean workers will return home on a chartered plane under a “voluntary departure” process, avoiding deportation records and future entry bans.

Korea’s Foreign Minister Cho Hyun is scheduled to travel to Washington later on Monday to finalize administrative procedures with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, clearing the way for repatriation flights as early as Wednesday.
Nearly 500 employees, including 300 Koreans (mostly LG Energy staff, subcontractors and engineers), were taken into custody last Thursday by agents from ICE, HSI, DEA, and Georgia state troopers.
The detained employees are alleged to have worked on the site without proper work permits, having entered the US under ESTA or short-term B1 business visas.
The $4.3 billion joint venture battery facility is among the largest projects in Georgia and was expected to create 8,500 jobs.
The facility was scheduled for completion later this year to supply battery cells to Hyundai’s nearby EV factory, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America LLC (HMGMA).
Construction work on the site was suspended following the raid.
The incident has thrown Korea’s flagship investment projects in the US into disarray.
At least 22 other factory sites involving Korean business groups in autos, shipbuilding, steel, and electrical equipment have been nearly halted.
Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. are heavily investing in US chip facilities.
Samsung SDI Co. and SK On Co. are building battery plants with their partners GM and Ford, respectively.
Several of these Korean-invested sites rely on rotating teams of Korean engineers.

There are growing concerns that major Korean investment projects in the US could be affected, including the MASGA (Make American Shipbuilding Great Again) program, a joint US-Korea shipbuilding project.
Hyundai Steel Co. is constructing a US steel mill.
Power equipment manufacturers HD Hyundai Electric Co., Hyosung Heavy Industries Co., and LS Electric Co. are expanding local factories.
These projects require the deployment of hundreds of employees from headquarters and partner companies.
Korean companies with US interests have frozen travel plans and recalled staff already in the US over fears of further raids.

A company executive in Seoul stated, “Korean workers are being treated like criminals for building factories that Washington itself lobbied for,” adding, “If this continues, investment in the US could be reconsidered.”
The incident underscores a structural weakness in Korea Inc.’s overseas expansion model.
With H-1B and L-1 work visas in short supply, Korean firms have routinely rotated engineers through 90-day ESTA entries or short-term B-1 visas to meet tight construction schedules.

Industry officials in Seoul warn that unless visa arrangements for skilled Korean employees are settled through bilateral talks, investment timetables worth more than 140 trillion won ($101 billion) across multiple US states will face serious delays and cost overruns.
The ICE raid risks inflaming tensions between Washington and Seoul, a key ally and major investor in the US.
Political pundits said ICE adopted aggressive enforcement quotas under President Donald Trump’s pledge to deport 1 million undocumented migrants annually.

According to DHS, the US government deported nearly 200,000 people during the first seven months of the Trump administration, which is the highest number in a decade but short of Trump's campaign goal.
The New York Times reported that collateral arrests not originally the goal are on the rise.
Analysts said the Georgia raid reflects partisan fault lines in Washington.
The Hyundai-LG joint venture project was announced during former President Joe Biden’s 2022 visit to Seoul and championed by him as a pillar of his clean-energy agenda.
Trump has sought to roll back EV subsidies and has been skeptical of such plants, according to analysts.
A Korean industry analyst stated, “There is an unavoidable political dimension here,” noting that the project was once celebrated as a symbol of the alliance but has now become collateral damage.
source_title: Korea’s major US investment projects halted as detained LG Energy workers set for release - KED Global

facts: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp visited Seoul this week to meet Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution executives following a September immigration raid that disrupted construction at their joint battery plant.
Federal agents detained more than 300 Korean workers at the Ellabell facility, delaying completion by two to three months from its 2024 target date.
The companies are developing a $4.3 billion battery factory and a $10.7 billion electric vehicle complex that will generate approximately 40,000 positions.
Kemp likely offered expanded tax benefits and administrative assistance to maintain investment levels after the enforcement action raised concerns about project timelines.
LG Energy Solution has resumed limited business travel to American facilities with essential staff holding short-term visas.
The governor discussed potential manufacturing visa programs with President Donald Trump to support foreign factory operations.
Korean firms committed over $10 billion to Georgia investments in 2023, creating 12,605 positions while bilateral trade reached $15.8 billion.
Authorities released 316 Korean nationals on September 12 after the raid.
source_title: Georgia governor meets Hyundai, LG Energy to address immigration raid fallout - TGM Business

facts: LG Energy Solution estimated its operating profit was 601 billion won (S$547 million) during the July to September period, up from 448 billion won in profit a year earlier.
US consumers rushed to buy electric vehicles to take advantage of government incentives that expired on Sep 30.
Analysts said the company's profit may have been boosted by the surge in US EV sales ahead of the end of a US$7,500 federal tax credit in September.

LG Energy Solution warned in July of a further slowdown in EV demand by early next year due to the elimination of the tax credits as well as US import tariffs imposed by US President Donald Trump’s administration.
The company would have made a 236 billion won operating profit excluding US battery manufacturing incentives it received under the US Inflation Reduction Act, according to a regulatory filing.
LG Energy Solution has tried to boost production of batteries for energy storage systems (ESS) to offset sluggish EV demand and improve profits in the second half of this year.
The company signed a US$4.3 billion deal to supply Tesla with ESS batteries, as the US automaker looks to reduce reliance on Chinese imports due to tariffs.
LG Energy Solution suffered a setback in September with its joint battery factory with Hyundai Motor in the United States.
Construction at the Georgia facility was suspended in the wake of an immigration raid in early September that led to the arrest of hundreds of South Korean workers.
Hyundai Motor CEO Jose Munoz said last month that the factory was facing a minimum startup delay of two to three months after the raid.
LG Energy Solution said early this month that it would gradually resume US business trips for its employees and subcontractors.
The Trump administration agreed to allow South Koreans to work on equipment at US sites under existing temporary visas.

facts: Oklahoma City-based Hall Capital and Tulsa-based Argonaut Private Equity jointly acquired Pontiac, Michigan-based JAC Products, Inc.
The sale became effective on Oct. 28, 2016, with terms not disclosed.
JAC Products is the leading global designer and manufacturer of roof racks, cargo management systems, assist steps, and related products for automobiles.
JAC Products operates factories in Michigan, Georgia, Portugal, and China.
JAC Products currently employs more than 1,500 employees globally.
The existing management team, including Chief Executive Officer Mike Wood, will remain with the company to help drive strategic growth plans.
Strategic growth plans include diversifying its product mix and expanding globally into new markets.
CEO Mike Wood stated: “JAC is in a major growth phase and this new ownership is committed to the implementation of our strategic plan.”
The JAC acquisition will be part of Hall Capital Partners Fund II, which was launched in 2011 with $92 million in committed capital.

Fred J. Hall, Chairman & CEO of Hall Capital, stated: “The JAC Products acquisition by our Fund II brings to Hall Capital a solid, mature company that is well poised for growth and operating in an industry in which we have generations of experience.”
Argonaut Private Equity will add JAC Products to its third fund.
Steve Mitchell, CEO of Argonaut Private Equity, stated: “This is an exciting and significant acquisition for Fund III.”
Hall Capital is a family-owned private investment company with offices in Oklahoma City, Nashville, Chicago, and Dallas.
Hall Capital's roots extend back to legendary automotive pioneer Fred Jones’ first enterprise in 1920.
Hall Capital is led by Fred Jones’ grandsons: Fred Hall, Kirkland Hall, and Brooks “Boots” Hall.
Hall Capital focuses on private equity funds and investments in the real estate, automotive, and oil & gas sectors.
Argonaut Private Equity was founded in 2002 and is based in Tulsa.
Argonaut Private Equity manages investments across multiple asset classes with over $3 billion of capital deployed in direct investments.
Argonaut has made over 100 investments and fosters a global network to discover and partner with best-in-class management teams.
JAC Products was founded in 1967.
JAC Products supplies OEM manufacturers worldwide with engineering, sales, manufacturing, and partnership locations in North America, Europe, China, Japan, and South America.
source_title: Hall Capital & Argonaut Private Equity Acquire Michigan-Based JAC Products – Hall Capital

facts: Joon Georgia will invest $317 million to produce parts in Bulloch County.
The supplier plans to hire 630 workers at a new factory in southeast Georgia.
The new factory will be located roughly 30 miles (50 kilometers) west of the site where Hyundai executives broke ground on its new EV plant two weeks prior to the announcement.
Joon Georgia is expected to open its factory near Statesboro in mid-2024.
The company will supply Hyundai Motor Group's first U.S. electric vehicle plant, which is under construction nearby.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated that Joon Georgia is "the first of many" suppliers expected to come to Georgia to support the $5.5 billion Hyundai plant in Bryan County.
Hyundai plans to open its Georgia plant in 2025, with production capacity of up to 300,000 electric vehicles per year.
Joon Georgia is a subsidiary of Ajin USA, which supplies parts to other Hyundai plants.

Joon Georgia already operates an existing facility in Cusseta, Alabama, near the Georgia border that manufactures parts for Hyundai's plant in Montgomery, Alabama, and Kia's auto plant in West Point, Georgia.
State and local officials in Georgia secured tax breaks and incentives worth $1.8 billion to lure Hyundai, marking the state's largest economic development deal.
Gov. Brian Kemp's office announced the investment details in a news release.
Pat Wilson, commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, called the announcement "a landmark moment as we drive Georgia's automotive industry into the future."
In addition to Hyundai hiring 8,100 workers, suppliers are expected to create thousands of additional jobs in the state.
source_title: Hyundai Ev Plant: Supplier to hire 630 workers near Hyundai's EV plant in Georgia, ETAuto
