facts: Kumho Tire operates a tire manufacturing plant in Macon, Georgia.

In May 2019, an administrative law judge with the National Labor Relations Board found that Kumho Tire had engaged in 'pervasive' illegal conduct during a unionization campaign at the company's tire manufacturing plant in Macon, Georgia.
At least 12 managers, including the company's CEO, issued illegal threats to lay off employees or close the plant if it unionized.
The company illegally interrogated employees and created an impression of surveillance during the unionization campaign at the Macon, Georgia facility.
The NLRB required Kumho Tire to read a notice to all workers at the Macon, Georgia plant outlining how the company violated their rights.
Kumho Tire supplies OEM tires for vehicle manufacturers including Audi, BMW, Chrysler, Fiat, General Motors, Genesis, Hyundai, Kia, Mercedes-Benz, Nissan, and Volkswagen.
In July 2018, Chinese company Doublestar finalized a deal to acquire a 45 percent stake and control of Kumho Tire from the Kumho Asiana Group for $607 million.
Kumho Tire manufactures a full range of tires under the Kumho, Marshal, and Zetum brands.
The company operates three research and development centers, with the largest in Gwangju, South Korea, and additional centers in Akron, Ohio, and Birmingham, England.
In October 2024, Kumho Tire won the grand prize at the '2024 Global Top Excellent Environmental Technology Awards' for developing low-wear-low-carbon tires to reduce fine dust and carbon dioxide.
Kumho Tire was previously operated as a business unit of the Kumho Asiana Group.
Kumho Tire exports tires worldwide and maintains a global network of sales organizations.
source_title: Kumho Tire - Wikipedia

facts: South Korean tire manufacturer Kumho Tire USA Inc. will sell bonds to help finance the construction of its one million-square-foot facility in Macon, Ga.
The Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority agreed to issue bonds for up to $600 million for the plant.
It was up to Kumho to decide when to put those bonds up for sale, according to the Macon Telegraph.
The tiremaker will sell up to $300 million of the total bonds approved to finance the construction and equipping of the facility, according to media reports.
Bank of America and Merrill Lynch will underwrite the bonds and sell them to investors.

Kevin Brown, the authority’s attorney, told the Telegraph that the authority is a conduit for the bond transaction, relieving itself, the city-county government and the school board from the responsibility of financing.
source_title: Kumho Tire USA Inc. Will Finance Georgia Plant By Selling Bonds

facts: Established in 1960 and headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
Manufactures tires and distributes tire products globally.
Operates within the industries of Manufacturing, Tires & Rubber, Automobile Parts Stores, and Retail.
Similar companies in its sector include Maxxis International–USA, Cooper Tire & Rubber, Titan International, and Kenda.
source_title: Kumho Tire Email verification & Email checker | NeverBounce

facts: Kumho Tire Co., Inc. was founded in 1960 in Gwangju, South Korea, by entrepreneur Pak In-chon as a workshop producing 20 tires per day.
The company is currently among the top 20 global tire manufacturers with revenue exceeding $2.27 billion.
Kumho's country of origin is South Korea, combining Japanese discipline, American technology, and a philosophy of rapid improvement.
Three plants in South Korea are located in Pyeongtaek, Gokseong, and Gwangju, producing high-tech models for the premium segment.
Since 2018, 45% of Kumho's shares have been controlled by the Chinese group Doublestar.
The brand operates eight production facilities across four countries: South Korea, China, Vietnam, and the USA.

Production locations include Pyeongtaek, Gokseong, and Gwangju in South Korea for premium lines, R&D, and aviation tires; Tianjin, Nanjing, and Changchun in China for the mass segment and Asian export; Binh Duong in Vietnam for eco-lines and cost reduction; and Macon, Georgia, USA for the North American market and localization.
The Macon, Georgia plant was launched in 2016 to serve the North American market, reducing delivery times for local dealers.
The country of manufacture depends on the specific batch and is indicated on the tire sidewall.
There are no assembly facilities for Kumho within the Russian Federation.

An official representative operates in Moscow at Krasnopresnenskaya Naberezhnaya, 12, coordinating import, EAEU certification, warranty service, dealer relations, marketing support, and staff training.
After 2022, the brand adapted supplies to the Russian market through parallel imports and official partners.
Authenticity can be verified by checking DOT or E1 markings on the sidewall.
The company operates five technological centers in Gwangju, Akron, Frankfurt, Tianjin, and Seoul, investing millions of dollars annually in new compounds and constructions.
Key innovations include ESCOT technology for contact patch optimization, XRP construction for reinforced sidewalls, and 3D siping for wet road grip.
Mercedes-Benz, BMW, Volkswagen, Hyundai, and Kia use Kumho tires in the original equipment of certain models.
Independent tests show Ecsta PS91 and WinterCraft WP52 models consistently rank in the top 10 by ADAC and Autobild.
Passenger car tire recommendations include Ecowing ES31 for low rolling resistance, Solus HA32+ for all-season urban use, Ecsta PS91 for dry asphalt grip, and Ecsta HS52 for sporty handling.
SUV/crossover tires include Crugen HP71 (all-season) and Road Venture AT52 (off-road).
Winter solutions include WinterCraft Ice WI31 (studded) and WinterCraft WP52 (friction/non-stud).
Optimal tire age is no more than 3 years from production, indicated by four digits in an oval on the sidewall.
Chinese plants operate under unified corporate standards with quality control centralized in Gwangju.
Winter lines are tested in Ivalo, Finland above the Arctic Circle, and all-season models are adapted for temperature swings from -35°C to +40°C.
Doublestar's investments enabled equipment modernization and expanded R&D, confirmed by ESG ratings and EcoVadis Gold awards.
By 2028, a first European plant is planned in Opole, Poland, with $587 million in investments to reduce logistics costs and speed up deliveries to Eastern Europe, including Russia.
Green technology development aims for eco-friendly materials to reach 44.5% of production by 2026.
Ecowing-marked models demonstrate a 3-5% reduction in fuel consumption due to optimized rubber compound.
Tire lifespan with proper operation and maintenance is 40,000–60,000 km.
Warranty terms depend on the model and region, coordinated by the Moscow office.
source_title: Шины Кумхо: страна производитель и секреты качества 🚗

facts: Kumho Tire U.S.A. is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
Kumho Tire U.S.A. serves as the US sales, marketing, product development, and distribution arm of Kumho Tire Co. Inc.
Kumho Tire is one of the world's largest tire manufacturers with more than 60 years of history.
The company sponsors KUMHO driver Jack Shanahan in the Formula DRIFT PRO Championship.
Driver Jack Shanahan placed third at Round One of the Formula DRIFT PRO Championship on the Streets of Long Beach.
Shanahan competed using KUMHO Ecsta V730 tires in an E82 BMW constructed in 16 days.
Ed Cho serves as the CEO of Kumho Tire U.S.A.
In 2025, Jack Shanahan debuted in the Formula DRIFT PRO Championship, finished sixth overall, and won Rookie of the Year.
For the 2026 season, Shanahan targets podium finishes and championship success.
source_title: KUMHO TIRE'S JACK SHANAHAN SECURES THIRD AT FORMULA DRIFT SEASON OPENER

facts: Kumho Tire USA Inc. is launching a grand touring all-weather tire, the Solus HA32.
The company said the Solus HA32 was designed to provide outstanding traction in winter conditions for passenger cars, CUVs and SUVs.
The announcement originated from Atlanta.
source_title: Kumho set to launch Solus all-weather passenger tire - Tire Business

facts: Korean Development Bank appointed Kim Jong-Ho as the new chairman of Kumho Tire, effective December 1, 2017.
Kim Jong-Ho previously held the title of CEO for Kumho Tire from 2008 to 2012 and successfully led the company through significant challenges during the global recession and recovery period.
Chairman Kim holds extensive tire industry experience from various international leadership positions across global divisions in Singapore, the Middle East, Australia, and the U.S.
During the early stages of the company’s growth, Jong-Ho’s focus on innovation and technology across all businesses contributed to substantial global expansion.

Chairman Kim stated: “Our primary focus is to truly listen to the needs of our customers and implement a solution-based strategy and react to market changes with optimal agility. It is time for us to make disciplined and effective changes to better address and manage market demands”.
Han Yong-seong, a former executive from Woori Bank, was selected as vice president of Kumho Tire.
Han Yong-seong previously served as chief financial officer of Taihan Electric Wire from 2010 to 2014, overseeing the restructuring of the company’s subsidiaries.
The appointments of Kim and Han will be finalized during a shareholders’ meeting on December 1, 2017.
Kumho Tire was founded in 1960 and has more than 50 years of history immersed in an innovative approach to product development, market expansion, technical progression, and environmental awareness.
Kumho Tire is positioned as one of the world’s top tire manufacturers and the world’s 9th largest global tire company.
Kumho Tire products are distributed in 160 countries.
Kumho Tire USA is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
Kumho Tire USA serves as the U.S. sales, marketing, product development, and distribution arm of Kumho Tire Co. Inc.
source_title: Kim Jong-Ho Appointed Chairman of Kumho Tire | Kumho Tire USA

facts: Automotive parts supplier Kyungshin America Corp. (KAC) signed a 136,000-square-foot lease at Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub (SGIH) in Georgia.
The KAC facility is scheduled to open in February 2024.
The facility will import and distribute automotive components to Hyundai Motor Group’s new electric vehicle plant.
Hyundai Motor Group’s new electric vehicle plant represents a $7.5 billion investment.
Hyundai Motor Group’s new electric vehicle plant is located near the Port of Savannah and serves the Southeast region.
Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub (SGIH) comprises a total of 2,600 acres.
SGIH is a public-private partnership between the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority (ECIDA) and Broe Real Estate Group.
Broe Real Estate Group operates as the real estate affiliate of The Broe Group.
source_title: Automotive Parts Supplier Signs 136,000 SF Lease at 2,600-Acre Logistics Park in Savannah - REBusinessOnline

facts: Kyungshin Holdings is a South Korea-based manufacturer of electronic components for the automotive industry.
The company announced an investment of $45m (58.59bn won) to establish three plants in Gomez Palacio, a city in northern Mexico.
The project will create 2,200 jobs.
The new plants will manufacture battery modules for electric cars.
The new plants will also manufacture electronic cards and harnesses.
Kyungshin Holdings is based in Incheon.
The company already has existing facilities in Gomez Palacio, located in the state of Durango, Mexico.
Kyungshin Holdings maintains international operations in Cambodia, China, Honduras, India, Indonesia, Mexico, Poland, Serbia, Turkey, the US and Vietnam.
The company was founded in essence in 1974 as KyungShin Industrial.
The company has operated under the name Kyungshin Holdings since 2016 after splitting from its parent company.
Gomez Palacio is a city of around 350,000 inhabitants located on the eastern Durango border with the state of Coahuila.
This announcement marks the second major investment the company has announced in the space of a few weeks.
In July 2023, Linamar Corp, a Canada-based advanced manufacturing company, stated it would invest $60m (C$79.14m) to expand its operations in Gomez Palacio.
Linamar Corp's expansion involves adding specialised equipment for the production of electric vehicles.
Linamar Corp's expansion will create 200 jobs.
source_title: Kyungshin to create 2,200 jobs in Mexico - Investment Monitor

facts: The sale of Savannah Gateway 1G was announced on October 31, 2024.
JLL Capital Markets facilitated the $34.1 million sale of 1001 Gateway Parkway, a Class A distribution center in Rincon, Georgia, also known as Savannah Gateway 1G.
JLL represented the seller, Broe Real Estate Group, the multi-billion-dollar real estate developer of the 2,600-acre Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub.
An affiliate of LXP Industrial Trust acquired the property.
The 204,800-square-foot facility is fully leased to two tenants: Kyungshin America Corporation and Weida Freight.
Kyungshin America Corporation is a tier 1 Hyundai supplier and a subsidiary of Kyungshin Corporation.
The center features tilt-wall construction, a 32-foot clear height, an 185-foot truck court depth, 38 dock-high doors, two drive-ins, 31 stalls, 25 levelers, and 133 auto spaces.
The property is located in the Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub, 11.9 miles from the Garden City terminal at the Port of Savannah.
The site offers easy access to Interstate 95 and Interstate 16.
The industrial park provides dual-rail service from CSX and Norfolk Southern.
The upcoming Effingham Parkway is slated for completion by September 2025, which will enhance the property's accessibility.
The facility will be positioned as the closest dual-access facility to the port in the Highway 21 Corridor upon the parkway's completion.
JLL Investment Sales and Advisory team led the transaction with Senior Managing Directors Britton Burdette, Matt Wirth, and Dennis Mitchell, along with Senior Director Jim Freeman.
Senior Managing Director Britton Burdette stated that the transaction highlights the growing importance of the Savannah market in the national industrial landscape.

Burdette noted that as port activity continues to increase, well-positioned facilities like 1001 Gateway Parkway are becoming increasingly valuable to investors and tenants seeking to optimize supply chain operations.
source_title: Savannah Gateway 1G sells for $34.1M in booming industrial market

facts: Lear Corporation is a public manufacturing company formerly known as American Metal Products.
Founded in Detroit, Michigan in 1917, originally manufacturing tubular, welded and stamped assemblies for aircraft and automobile industries.
Headquarters located in the United States.
Operates 257 locations across 39 countries as of 2017.
Serves an international area served.
Key executives include Raymond E. Scott (President, CEO, and Director) and Terry Larkin (Executive Vice President, Business Development and General Counsel).
Provides automotive seating and electrical systems to original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
Reported revenue of US$23.3 billion in 2024.
Reported US$888 million and US$507 million in additional 2024 financial metrics.
Holds total assets of US$14.0 billion and total equity of US$4.45 billion as of 2024.
Employs 173,700 people as of 2024.
Organized into Seating and Electrical divisions.
Ranked #148 on the Fortune 500 list in 2018 and #147 in 2019.
Grew during the 1980s and 1990s through acquisitions to supply complete interior automotive systems (seating, electrical, flooring, interior trim, instrument panels) to OEM auto companies.
On March 16, 1999, announced the acquisition of United Technologies Automotive for $2.3 billion.

Completed the acquisition of United Technologies Automotive on May 4, 1999; the subsidiary produced dashboards, electrical distribution systems, motors, air-flow parts, interior door panels and switches.
Became the first large corporation to achieve ISO/TS 16949:1999 worldwide across 280 global facilities, managed by Bill Cooper (Global Quality Systems Senior Quality Manager).
On April 5, 2004, announced a $220 million acquisition of Grote & Hartmann, a Wuppertal, Germany-based maker of electrical components.
Completed the Grote & Hartmann transaction on July 6, 2004.
By late 2005, most OEM auto companies stopped purchasing total interiors from single companies, making Lear's interior systems division unprofitable and prompting a divestiture search.

In early 2007, transferred its former North American Interior Systems Division to International Automotive Components Group (IAC), a joint venture of Lear, WL Ross & Co., and Franklin Mutual Advisers.
The IAC transfer deal involved 26 manufacturing plants and two Chinese joint ventures, with Lear contributing $27 million in cash for a 25 percent interest plus warrants for an additional 7 percent.

In 2007, Lear's board agreed to a $2.3 billion takeover offer from American Real Estate Partners (an affiliate of Carl Icahn), which included a $100 million fee for Icahn if another offer was accepted; the deal was later voted down by shareholders.
On July 2, 2009, Bloomberg News reported Lear planned to file for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after reaching an agreement with secured lenders and bondholders.
Announced emergence from bankruptcy on November 9, 2009.
On August 10, 2011, announced that CFO Matt Simoncini was elected CEO and president, effective September 1, 2011.

In 2012, won the Automotive News PACE Award for its Solid State Smart Junction Box (S3JB), which features integrated solid state fuse technology eliminating relays, a non-driver-compartment accessible package reducing warranty returns, smart software logic for fail-safe operation, and a patented thermal single state board design.

In May 2012, acquired automotive and specialty fabrics company Guilford Mills for $257 million; Guilford produces fabrics for water filtration, window covering, performance apparel, medical and industrial applications.
In August 2014, announced the acquisition of automotive leather supplier Eagle Ottawa LLC for $850 million.
In May 2015, announced plans to open a new automotive plant in Gostivar, Republic of North Macedonia in summer 2015, marking Lear's first facility in North Macedonia.
In February 2017, signed a definitive agreement to acquire Grupo Antolin's automotive seating business for €286 million on a cash and debt-free basis, funded with cash on hand.
Served as a backer for the Jaguar Racing Team in the 2001 Formula One World Championship and currently supports the Formula E Competition.
In November 2022, acquired Novi-based supplier InTouch Automation, which provides Industry 4.0 technologies and automated testing equipment used in automotive seat production.
source_title: Lear Corporation - Wikipedia

facts: Penske Logistics, LG Electronics and Lear Corporation will collectively make $158.8-million capital investments and create 1,130 jobs.
The projects are among a range of projects approved by the Michigan Strategic Fund (MSF) that incentivized total capital investment of $595 million and 1,500 new jobs.

Jeff Mason, CEO of the Michigan Economic Development Corporation (MEDC), stated that the new investments underscore Michigan’s expanding economy and appeal for logistics capacity, automotive industry, and autonomous vehicle potential.

Penske Logistics will receive a $2.5-million Michigan Business Development Program performance-based grant as part of its $98.6-million investment into a 606,000-square-foot storage and distribution center.
The Penske Logistics project will create 403 jobs in Romulus.
Penske Logistics operates more than 360 locations across North America, South America, Europe, and Asia, offering warehousing, transportation management, lead logistics, and freight brokerage.
Doug Studley, director of operations at Penske Logistics, credited the MEDC, city of Romulus, InSite Real Estate, and Bonner Advisory Group for making the project possible.
The MSF grant played a vital role in attracting Penske Logistics to Michigan over Indiana.

Wayne County Executive Warren C. Evans noted that the project aligns with the Aerotropolis, a seven-community, two-county public-private partnership focused on corporate expansion around Detroit Metropolitan Airport and Willow Run Airport.
LG Electronics Inc. will establish a 250,000-square-foot factory for advanced electric vehicle (EV) components in Hazel Park.
The LG Electronics plant will produce power electronics subassemblies for EVs starting in 2018.
LG Electronics' $25-million private capital investment is supported by a $2.9-million Michigan Business Development Program grant.

Ken Chang, LG Electronics USA senior vice president and head of the LG Vehicle Components North American Business Center, stated that producing world-class EV subassemblies in the U.S. is a key pillar of their strategy to partner with U.S. automakers.
The LG project will create at least 292 jobs, including 137 factory workers in Hazel Park and 155 engineers and technicians at the expanded LG R&D Center in Troy.
LG Electronics USA operates four business units: Home Entertainment, Mobile Communications, Home Appliance & Air Solutions, and Vehicle Components.
The Vehicle Components unit collaborates with General Motors on the Chevrolet Bolt EV.

Oakland County Executive L. Brooks Patterson highlighted LG's continued growth in Oakland County and praised the partnership between the county, MEDC, Troy, and Hazel Park for facilitating business expansions.
Southfield-based Lear Corporation will construct a 160,000-square-foot building at the location of the former administration building at Buick City, north of downtown Flint.
The MSF board approved a $4.35-million Michigan Business Development Program performance-based grant to support Lear’s $29.3-million capital investment.
Lear's project includes the subassembly and final assembly of automotive seats and will create 435 new jobs in the state.
Ray Scott, Lear executive vice president and president of Seating Operations, stated the expansion reflects a positive business outlook and contributes to Flint's revitalization.
The city of Flint offered a tax abatement to reduce overall project costs for Lear.
Michigan was selected over competing sites in the United States and Mexico for Lear's investment.
Lear aims to begin construction at the site in the fall with operations starting next spring, marking the return of auto manufacturing to the location since 1999.
Flint Mayor Karen Weaver stated the project will provide hundreds of hard-working Flint residents with good-paying jobs within the city limits and boost the local economy.
Tim Herman, CEO of the Flint & Genesee Chamber of Commerce, noted that Lear's presence continues the region's rich automotive history and pledged chamber support for the company's success.
The Michigan Economic Development Corporation (MEDC) serves as the state’s marketing arm and lead advocate for business development, job awareness, and community talent development.
source_title: MI: Penske Logistics, LG Electronics, Lear Corp. Invest $158M, Hiring 1,130 | Trade and Industry Development

facts: The new plant will build seats for just-in-time delivery to the nearby General Motors Flint Assembly plant, as well as the General Motors Fort Wayne plant in Indiana.
Ray Scott, Lear President and CEO, stated: “Flint is the definition of a community-driven city, fuelled by dedicated people who are committed to making things happen.”
Lear’s Flint seating plant is the first major automotive supplier manufacturing facility constructed in Flint in more than 30 years.
The new plant’s conveyor system utilises Lear’s proprietary LPS (Lear Production System), which monitors all key elements of the seat.
Waste and recycling efforts will ensure the facility is a zero waste to landfill.
Additional elements that will further contribute to the plant’s environmental footprint include energy-efficient lighting and an area dedicated for green space covering 30% of the whole site.
source_title: Lear Corp opens U.S. seating plant - International Leather Maker

facts: Lehigh Technologies is a leading specialty chemicals company that produces high-performance micronized rubber powders (MRP), a sustainable raw material that reduces costs by up to 50%.
Lehigh's cryogenic turbo mill technology converts end-of-life rubber into MRP used in a wide range of markets.
With a state-of-the-art applications and development center, Lehigh technical experts collaborate with customers to optimize products for each application.
With its comprehensive compounding, formulation and testing capabilities, Lehigh's Application & Development Center collaborates with customers to help them optimize products for individual needs.
Lehigh has been recognized as a World Economic Forum Technology Pioneer (2010) and a Bloomberg New Energy Pioneer (2015).
The company has also received several industry awards including Sustainable Technology of the Year by Tire Tech Expo (2014).
Headquartered in Tucker, Georgia, Lehigh has capacity of over 140MM pounds/year - the largest known capacity in the industry.
Lehigh Technologies is privately held.
Lehigh Technologies produces highly engineered, versatile raw materials called micronized rubber powders (MRP) that can replace oil- and rubber-based feedstocks in a wide range of applications.
MRP delivers product performance, lower production costs and reduced waste.
By transforming waste materials into high-value products, Lehigh provides a sustainable solution to the important environmental problem of end-of-life tires and other post-industrial rubber material.
The company has two main product lines: PolyDyne™ and MicroDyne™, which range from 830 microns to 50 microns in size.
All of these products are manufactured from end-of-life tire materials and post-industrial rubber using proprietary cryogenic turbo mill technology.
Lehigh also produces a number of innovative new compounds, including Enduraprene™, EkoDyne™, Rheopave™ and Zenoflex™.
The company's high quality, micron-scale MRPs are suitable for a number of high-performance consumer and industrial applications, including tires, plastics, asphalt and construction materials.
MRPs serve as a lower-cost, sustainable alternative to virgin rubber and oil-based materials, providing a boost to the bottom line.

Lehigh's customers include the largest tire companies in the world, leading flooring manufacturers and companies at the forefront in the construction materials, asphalt modification and coatings segments.

The company operates a best-in-class Application and Development Center (ADC) staffed with a team of experienced industry professionals and experienced scientists who leverage their in-depth understanding of how MRPs function to help customers develop and optimize tailored formulations using MRP products.
Lehigh has designed the MRP product ranges to deliver significant value across a variety of markets while reducing the environmental impact of end-of-life tires.

Across different markets and geographies, Lehigh’s MRP serves global markets totaling more than $10 billion in revenue, including asphalt, construction materials, industrial rubber, plastics, polyurethanes and tires.
The company's chemists and engineers are constantly exploring new applications and markets where MRP can provide compelling benefits.
Lehigh Technologies was established in 2003.
The company employs 200 people.
Alan Barton serves as President of Lehigh Technologies.
Lehigh's address is 120 Royal Woods Court SW Tucker Georgia United States ZIP: 30084.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies, Inc - Georgia,Tucker

facts: Lehigh Technologies Inc., a sustainable materials company, strengthened its state-of-the-art research and development capabilities by investing significantly in new mixing and analytical equipment for its Application and Development Center in Atlanta, Georgia.
The expansion enables Lehigh to drive further adoption of its sustainable micronized rubber powder material in green tire applications.

Alan Barton, CEO of Lehigh Technologies with headquarters in Tucker, Georgia, northeast of Atlanta, stated that the global green tire market is expected to grow significantly over the next five years.
Lehigh has positioned itself to support this growth by establishing best-in-class research and development facilities and a strong technical team.
These latest expansions are an important part of that strategy and will reinforce peer-level technical relationships with major tire companies.
Along with expansion of the technology team the company said, Lehigh has made significant investments in advanced silica-silane mixing and dynamic mechanical analysis capabilities.

The new internal mixing unit was custom-built to Lehigh specifications and includes precision speed and temperature control as well as capability for mixing high viscosity silica-silane-SBR compounds.
The new DMA is a Metravib model DMA+ 1000.

The capabilities of this state-of-the-art unit include measurement of predictive parameters critical to green tire performance such as rolling resistance, traction, and handling under a wide range of conditions.
Tom Rosenmayer, Lehigh's Vice President of Technology, stated that having advanced mixing and DMA testing capabilities is critical to the development of functional materials based on MRP.
Among its peers, Lehigh is unique in having this capability in-house, enabling us to work more efficiently with our customers to develop innovative solutions to meet their needs.
MRPs lower costs, increase the sustainability profile of end products and deliver performance without sacrificing the reliability and performance offered by traditional raw materials.
Lehigh's customer base already includes five of the top ten tire companies in the world.
More than 250 million tires have been manufactured using its MRP technology, company officials said.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies Inc. Upgrades Its Atlanta, Georgia, Application and Development Center | www.inknowvation.com

facts: Michelin announced it has acquired Lehigh Technologies, a specialty materials company that uses patented cryogenic turbo mill technology to transform rubber from end-of-life tires and industrial goods into materials for new tires and other products.
The acquisition reduces the amount of raw materials initially needed, such as elastomers and fillers from oil- and rubber-based sources.

Pete Selleck, chairman and president of Michelin North America, stated that the company is always looking for ways to achieve safer and more sustainable mobility by using high-technology recycled materials without compromising safety or performance while consuming less finite natural resources.

Christophe Rahier, senior vice president of strategic planning materials for the Michelin Group, said the acquisition demonstrates Michelin’s strategic intent to extend its materials expertise beyond tires and foster advanced rubber recycled materials in tire and non-tire industries.
Lehigh Technologies is based in Tucker, Georgia, near Atlanta.
Lehigh Technologies employs about 100 people.
The firm produces highly engineered, versatile raw materials called micronized rubber powder (MRP).

MRP is a low-cost, high-performance sustainable material that can be used as a substitute for other oil- and rubber-based materials used in manufacturing tires, plastics, asphalt and construction materials.
Lehigh’s customers include some of the largest tire companies in the world, as well as companies in construction materials, asphalt modification and other markets.

Alan Barton, CEO of Lehigh Technologies, stated that the deal provides an incredible opportunity to continue growth with the full backing of Michelin to expand capabilities, execute a global growth strategy and pursue new market opportunities.
Michelin completed the acquisition on Oct. 13.
source_title: Michelin Acquires Specialty Materials Company Lehigh Technologies

facts: Lehigh Technologies Inc. announced that Jim MacMaster, former executive vice president and chief operating officer at Yokohama Tire Corp. North America, has joined its board of directors.
Lehigh also appointed Adel Halasa, a polymer scientist formerly with Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co., to its advisory board.
The company has been continuing to drive adoption of its micronized rubber powder (MRP) material in high-value applications.
Lehigh is now expanding its presence in Asia and Europe.
CEO Alan Barton stated that having an executive with Jim MacMaster's level of experience in a key market segment will help deliver the compelling value proposition of MRP to the tire industry.
MacMaster began his career with Yokohama in 1981 in sales and rose through multiple commercial roles to become vice president of the company’s North American Commercial Tire Division.
In 2001, MacMaster was named executive vice president at Yokohama.
As a new Advisory Board member, Adel Halasa will provide guidance to Lehigh’s technical team.
Halasa was a research and development fellow at Goodyear from 1983 to 2009.
He has since served as an adjunct professor at the University of Akron.
Halasa has been listed on more than 300 patents.
He was recognized in 1997 with the Charles Goodyear Medal for his work developing anionic solution polymerization of styrene-butadiene rubber (SBR).

Tom Rosenmayer, vice president of technology at Lehigh Technologies, stated that the company's technology roadmap for the tire industry is based upon driving higher loading levels of MRP and developing MRPs with chemical reactivity.
Halasa’s depth of experience and knowledge of polymers and their structure-property relationships will help Lehigh develop new products faster as a key part of its growth strategy.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies Adds Industry Vets to its Ranks - Tire Review Magazine

facts: Michelin revealed during its Movin’On 2018 event an ambitious plan to ensure that by 2048, all of its tyres will be manufactured using 80 percent sustainable materials and 100 percent of all tyres will be recycled.
According to the World Business Council for Sustainable Development (WBCSD), an estimated 1 billion end-of-life tyres (ELT) will be generated worldwide in 2018, representing around 25 million tonnes.
Currently, the global recovery rate for tyres is 70 percent with 50 percent of the material recycled and used in products such as rubber used in sports surfaces, and 20 percent is energy recovery.

Michelin's tyres are currently made using 28 percent sustainable materials – 26 percent bio-sourced materials such as natural rubber, sunflower oil and limonene and 2 percent recycled materials such as steel or recycled powdered tyres.
Michelin is investing in “high technology recycling” to increase sustainable material content to 80 percent and produce tyres that are 100 percent recycled for future vehicles.
Michelin proposes to develop partnerships and identify new ways to recycle tyres or find new outlets for recycled tyres.
The route to this target will be achieved by research programmes into bio-sourced materials like Biobutterfly and working with high-level partners on advanced technologies and materials.
Michelin acquired Lehigh Technologies Inc., a US-based specialty chemical company and a specialist in high technology micro powders derived from recycled tyres.

Lehigh is now part of the High Technology Materials Business Unit of Michelin and is the leading producer of Micronized Rubber Powders (MRP), a sustainable raw material that reduces feedstock costs by up to 50 percent while delivering performance across markets.

MRP replaces oil- and rubber-based feedstocks in industrial and consumer applications including high-performance tyres, plastics, consumer goods, coatings, sealants, construction materials and asphalt.
Lehigh technical experts collaborate with customers to optimize products for each application.
Lehigh operates the world’s largest MRP manufacturing plant in Tucker, Georgia, with an annual production capacity of 54 000 tonnes.

Lehigh’s state-of-the-art Application & Development Center is also located in Tucker and serves as an innovation hub where Michelin conducts research and formulates MRPs in collaboration with its customers.
Lehigh Spain, a joint venture with Hera Holding, is based in Barcelona.
The first Lehigh plant outside of the US, located in Murrillo del Fruto, is under construction and will begin operations in the summer of 2018.

Christophe Rahier, Director of the High Technology Materials Business Line at Michelin, stated the acquisition demonstrates Michelin’s strategic determination to capitalize on expertise in high-tech materials beyond tyres by promoting innovative recycled tyre materials in non-pneumatic industrial sectors.

Michelin revealed its VISION concept at Movin’On last year: an airless tyre made of bio-sourced and recycled products using advanced materials and 3D printing technologies for tread renewal, making it 100 percent recyclable.

The VISION concept features a connected ecosystem providing services and advice to the driver, a bio-degradable tread renewable with a 3D printer, and a mobility solution that reduces the environmental footprint of car journeys.

Achieving the 80 percent sustainable materials and 100 percent recycling targets will save Michelin an estimated 33 million barrels of oil per annum or 54 TWh, equivalent to about one month’s total energy consumption in France.
source_title: Michelin reveals plan to increase bio-based components and recycling in tyres | Bioenergy International

facts: Michelin has acquired US scrap tire and rubber recycling company Lehigh Technologies.
The acquisition was announced by Michelin North America on 18 Oct.
Terms of the deal were not disclosed.
Lehigh is based in Tucker, near Atlanta, Georgia.
Lehigh has developed a patented cryogenic turbo mill technology to transform rubber from end-of-life tires and industrial goods into materials for new tires and other products.
The 'micronised' powder (MRP) end-product is offered as a low-cost, sustainable substitute for conventional oil- and rubber-based materials.
Applications for MRP encompass tires, plastics, asphalt and construction materials.
Customers include some of the world’s largest tire companies.
Michelin stated it is 'always looking for ways to achieve more sustainable mobility, including by using high-technology recycled materials, without compromising safety or other performances.'

Christophe Rahier, senior VP of strategic planning materials for Michelin Group, said the acquisition demonstrates Michelin’s strategic intent to foster the use of advanced rubber recycled materials in the tire and non-tire industries.
Alan Barton, CEO of Lehigh, stated the acquisition provides Lehigh with an 'incredible opportunity' to continue its growth.
Barton added that with Michelin's backing, Lehigh can expand capabilities, execute its global growth strategy, and pursue new market opportunities.
Lehigh claims its US facility in Tucker is the world’s largest MRP manufacturing plant.
The Tucker facility has an annual production capacity of 140 million pounds (around 64 kilotonnes).
Lehigh also set up a factory in Murillo el Fruto, northern Spain to serve European markets.
The Spanish facility was originally due for start-up in 2016 and then early 2017, though there has been no recent announcement about its status.
The Spanish facility is equipped with a 10,000-tonne a year capacity cryogenic turbo mill to produce MRP from end-of-life tires, buffings and post-industrial scrap.
source_title: Michelin acquires Lehigh Technologies | European Rubber Journal

facts: German-based LINDE + WIEMANN GmbH KG will establish a U.S.-based assembly operation with associated national sales and distribution activities in Hart County, Georgia.
The company's first facility will initially employ approximately 200 people.
The project represents a capital investment of more than $35 million.
Georgia Gov. Nathan Deal stated that the decision to locate in Hart County sends a strong message about Georgia's business-friendly climate.
Gov. Nathan Deal noted that the announcement indicates Northeast Georgia as a hub for manufacturing products vital to major automotive industry leaders worldwide.
GDEcD Commissioner Chris Carr stated they worked with local partners in Hart County to build a strong environment for companies like LINDE + WIEMANN.
GDEcD Commissioner Chris Carr said the state will continue working hard to attract top-tier business in the future.
LINDE + WIEMANN is a privately-held, mid-sized German company specializing in the manufacturing and assembly of engineered steel systems mounted in motor vehicles.
The company's products include instrument panel assemblies, front end and door structure assemblies, and structural body-in-white assemblies.
LINDE + WIEMANN will locate on a 29-acre site in the Gateway II Industrial Park in Hart County, Georgia.
The company will construct a 60,000 square foot facility and a 3,500 square foot head office building in the initial phases of the project.
From the proposed facility, LINDE + WIEMANN will supply customers including BMW in Greer, South Carolina, and Daimler in Tuscaloosa, Alabama.
CEO Ulrich Schoof stated that LINDE + WIEMANN performed an extensive site search over several months to identify the right location.
As a Tier One supplier to BMW and other automotive OEMs, LINDE + WIEMANN's quality products depend on the quality of the local workforce.
Hart County offered LINDE + WIEMANN access to qualified labor, attractive logistics, reasonable operating costs, quality of life, room for expansion, and supportive local leadership.
The company was delighted with the support from the State of Georgia and welcomed the assistance of Georgia's German-speaking project manager.
LINDE + WIEMANN will develop the Hart County site as a premier hub for its North American business.
LINDE + WIEMANN expects to create jobs and wealth in the Georgia Piedmont region as it grows its business.
The new U.S.-based assembly and stamping operation will put LINDE + WIEMANN in a strategic position to expand its client base in the North American market.
The company presently has production operations in Germany, Spain, the Czech Republic, South Africa, Turkey, and China.
Hart County Board of Commission Chairman Jimmy Carey welcomed LINDE + WIEMANN to the community and expressed hope for a long, mutually beneficial relationship.

Chairman Jimmy Carey stated that decisions by world-class advanced manufacturing operations to locate in Hart County reinforce local efforts to provide an educated, skilled workforce and continue long-term development.
Hart County Industrial Building Authority Chairman Bill Leard welcomed LINDE + WIEMANN and highlighted the excellent career opportunities for area citizens.
GDEcD Director of International Investment Nico Wijnberg represented the Global Commerce division throughout the project.
The project involved collaboration with the Hart County Industrial Building Authority, the Hart County Board of Commissioners, and Kevin Lovelace of Georgia Power.
source_title: LINDE + WIEMANN to create 200 new jobs, will invest more than $35M in Hart County - Georgia Ports Authority

facts: Linde + Wiemann SE & Co. KG (also referred to as Linde & Wiemann) is a subsidiary company headquartered in HE, DE.
The company operates in the industry of Automobile and Light Duty Motor Vehicle Manufacturing.
Linde & Wiemann provides automotive engineering and manufactures car components.
The company's technical research fields include Automotive engineering.
Linde & Wiemann holds a total of 56 patents.
Linde & Wiemann has one office located in SE and one other location.
Key executives at the company include Michael Macht.
Competitors of Linde & Wiemann include Compagnie Plastic Omnium and Montaplast GmbH.

Ltd., Polestar Performance AB, GKN Walterscheid GmbH, Wuhu Shengli Technology Co., Ltd., and Anhui Sheng.

The company's patent portfolio innovation word cloud highlights technical focus areas including motor vehicle, battery box, structural component, battery cell, electric vehicle, vehicle chassis, laser welding, automotive structural component, battery case, hot forming tool, torsion beam axle, battery compartment, and frame reinforcement element.

High related markets for the company include Automotive and Transport, specifically Automotive Parts and the Automotive Front-end Module Market, with mentions of Compagnie Plastic Omnium SA and Montaplast GmbH.
A subsidiary of Linde & Wiemann is Accra Teknik AB, which focuses on mining engineering and natural resource management and holds 123 patents.

facts: Linde + Wiemann RSA is inviting applications for a one-year Graduate Development Programme.
The application closing date is 16th February 2025.
Five (5) graduate positions are available across the Plant, Quality, Maintenance, and Manufacturing Engineering departments.
The programme is designed to transform graduates into high-performing professionals through work exposure and on-the-job training at the East London Plant.
Graduate trainees will be monitored throughout the 12-month period and receive mentorship and coaching from relevant Department Managers.

Eligibility requires a completed degree or diploma-level qualification, one to two years of relevant work experience (advantageous), computer literacy, strong problem-solving abilities, and effective communication skills.
Only applications including relevant qualifications, experience, and supporting documentation will be considered.
Linde + Wiemann supports Employment Equity principles and advertises the position both internally and externally.
Applicants who do not hear back within 14 days of the closing date should consider their application unsuccessful.

The Plant Department is seeking an Industrial Engineer Graduate in Training to assist with day-to-day activities, requiring a National Diploma in Industrial Engineering, one to two years of experience (preferably in an Automotive Environment), Office proficiency, and advantageous AutoCAD & SolidWorks knowledge.

The Quality Department is seeking a Quality Graduate in Training for receiving inspections, quality alerts, G8Ds, scrap analysis, supplier claims, performance reviews, and audits, requiring a National Diploma in Mechanical Engineering and Office proficiency.

Two Manufacturing Engineering Graduates in Training are needed to provide technical and administrative support, requiring a National Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree in Industrial or Mechanical Engineering and Microsoft Office proficiency.

The Maintenance Department (Assembly Plant) is seeking a Maintenance Graduate in Training for planned and corrective machinery maintenance, requiring a National Diploma in Electrical or Mechanical Engineering, ability to use workshop tools and electrical testers, engineering drawing interpretation skills, and Office proficiency.

Working conditions require frequent walking or standing, hand use, visual inspections, occasionally lifting up to 15kgs, working at heights or in confined spaces, driving forklifts or boom lifts, and occasional extended hours.
The work environment may involve exposure to chemicals, noise, dust, temperature changes, and cramped areas.
Linde + Wiemann RSA is located at Ikhala Road, BW3 Building, Zone 1A, Sunnyridge, East London 5201.
source_title: Linde + Wiemann RSA, East London – Graduate Development Programme Opportunity - Latest Learnerships, Internships & Jobs In South Africa 2026.

facts: JBM Group is a $1.5bn conglomerate with business interests in auto systems & assemblies.
JBM Group has announced that it has acquired a major shareholding in Germany-based Linde-Wiemann GmbH KG.
Linde-Wiemann is a leading manufacturer of complex structural components & assemblies to automotive OEMs worldwide.

The acquisition brings synergies for JBM Group in the form of new products, global customers, and new German technologies to expand its global footprint and add strategic value to its Indian operations.
Linde-Wiemann is headquartered in Dillenburg, Germany.
Linde-Wiemann specializes in proprietary technologies in hot forming, tubular assemblies, and the usage of alternate materials such as aluminium for lightweighting in auto systems.
Linde-Wiemann operates 17 manufacturing plants across 8 countries: Germany, US, China, Spain, Turkey, Czech Republic, Hungary, and South Africa.
Linde-Wiemann has an employee strength of over 2300 plus.
Linde-Wiemann brings over 70 years of experience serving global auto OEMs including BMW, Volkswagen, Audi, Skoda, Porsche, Daimler, Ford, and PSA.
JBM will gain access to Linde-Wiemann's strengths in Electric Vehicle components & systems and battery structure & systems.
Nishant Arya, Executive Director of JBM Group, stated the acquisition is a strategic fit perfectly in sync with their existing scope of operations & solutions.
Nishant Arya noted that both entities will bring forward an impeccable combination of frugal engineering & world class technology.
Nishant Arya indicated they will leverage Linde-Wiemann's experience in Europe coupled with JBM's expertise in the auto component domain.

JBM and Linde-Wiemann will together focus on developing new products that contribute towards improving vehicle safety and performance through lightweighting, increasing product quality and minimizing time to market for OEMs.
Nishant Arya takes over additional charges as Chairman of Linde-Wiemann.
Ulrich Schoof, Chief Executive Officer of Linde-Wiemann, described JBM as a highly complementary partner regarding customers and technologies in the global automotive market.
Ulrich Schoof stated that the use of joint potentials is an essential motivation for the new partnership.
Through the new business model, Linde-Wiemann will be able to unlock substantial growth by the adaptation of product competencies in the area of chassis and suspension.
Linde-Wiemann will extend its holistic system understanding in the field of e-mobility through exchange with JBM's electrical bus division.

Ravi Sardana, EVP of ICICI Securities Ltd, the financial advisor to JBM Group, said the investment brings together two leading players with strong technology and product portfolio, complementary footprint, and matching ambitions for growth.
The financing of the entire transaction has been done by ICICI Bank Limited.
Linde-Wiemann's stamping capabilities are reflected by machines with pressing capacities up to 3000 tonnes.
Linde-Wiemann brings complex progressive dies and transfer presses along with hydraulic press lines being used for forming.
The core competence of Linde-Wiemann's in-house tool shop includes extensive knowledge in ultra-high-strength steels and the integration of complex feeding units.
Linde-Wiemann's current product portfolio includes critical auto systems such as cross car beams, bumpers, rocker panels, and tubular assemblies.
source_title: JBM acquires major shareholding in Linde-Wiemann - The Machinist

facts: Listed auto-components maker JBM Group has acquired a majority stake in Linde-Wiemann, a German manufacturer and supplier of structural components and assemblies to automobile companies globally.
JBM Group announced the acquisition via a stock exchange filing on Monday.
The transaction was financed by ICICI Bank.
Financial details of the deal were not disclosed.
Linde-Wiemann specializes in hot forming, tubular assemblies, and the use of alternative materials such as aluminium to create lightweight auto systems.
Linde-Wiemann operates 17 manufacturing plants across Germany, the US, China, Spain, Turkey, Czech Republic, Hungary, and South Africa.
Linde-Wiemann employs more than 2,300 people.
The acquisition will help JBM access new products, increase its number of global customers, and add to its competitiveness in India.
JBM will benefit from Linde-Wiemann’s strengths in electric vehicle components and battery structure and systems.
Linde-Wiemann’s clients include Volkswagen, Audi, Skoda, Porsche, Daimler, and Ford.

Nishant Arya, executive director of JBM Group, stated that the combined entity will focus on developing new products to improve vehicle safety and performance through light-weighting, increasing product quality and minimizing time to market for OEMs.
Arya noted the acquisition opens opportunities in technology, highlighting Linde-Wiemann's strength in alternate technology and work on global platforms for Volkswagen, General Motors, PSA, and Ford.
Arya claimed the deal will make JBM an automatic choice for those OEMs.
Linde-Wiemann possesses EV components and battery technology that JBM hopes to utilize in its buses.

JBM manufactures safety-critical items including chassis and suspension systems, axles, twist beams, exhaust systems, air tanks, and fuel tanks for passenger cars, two-wheelers, three-wheelers, commercial vehicles, and farm and construction equipment.
source_title: JBM acquires majority stake in German auto firm Linde-Wiemann

facts: Shares of Magna International fell more than 10% on Thursday after the Canadian auto part supplier missed on earnings and lowered its full-year profit forecast.
The share decline was attributed to high program costs and the impact of a slowdown in China.
Magna reported first-quarter earnings of $1.63 per share on revenue of $10.59 billion, falling short of analyst expectations for $1.71 per share on sales of $10.7 billion.
Total sales fell 2% year over year, which Magna noted compares well to total global light-vehicle production decreasing 7%.
Reversing the impact of currency conversions and stripping out revenue from recent divestitures, revenue was up 3% from the first quarter of 2018.
Magna now expects full-year net income to come in between $1.9 billion and $2.1 billion, down from earlier guidance for $2.1 billion to $2.3 billion.
The company expects total 2019 light-vehicle production of 16.7 million units in North America, down from its earlier forecast for 17 million units.

CEO Don Walker cited lower overall vehicle production, margin pressure in the power & vision segment related to higher program engineering and other costs, and a Getrag joint venture in China due to lower than previously anticipated sales as factors weighing on full-year results.
Magna has long been seen as a best-of-breed auto supplier, with shares more than tripling in value over the past 10 years.
The company has a broad portfolio of business, a wide range of customers, and has been aggressively moving into new areas including autonomous driving.

CEO Don Walker stated that despite current issues, the company remains confident in its ability to outpace production in its markets, generate strong free cash flow, and create value for shareholders.
Magna offers a 2.83% dividend yield.
source_title: Why Magna International Shares Are Falling on Thursday | The Motley Fool

facts: Magna International, Inc. posted a Technician, Maintenance position at its Carrollton, Georgia facility.
The role operates under the Decostar brand within the Magna Exteriors portfolio.

Magna Exteriors produces access systems such as liftgates, exterior trim, modular systems, front-end modules including fascia, active aerodynamic systems, and lightweight structural components for automotive, commercial truck, and industrial markets.
The maintenance technician works a 3rd shift from 6pm to 6:20am on a 7 days on, 7 days off schedule (Thursday to Thursday).
Key duties include repairing and maintaining equipment and facilities to meet uptime goals and inspecting machinery for defects per a preventative maintenance program.
Technicians must order replacement parts and fluids through the Maintenance Manager/Supervisor and determine reasons for equipment failures to advise managers on corrective actions.
Employees are required to fabricate equipment components using welders, milling machines, lathes, and other machine tools as needed.
The role requires troubleshooting and repairing electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, and mechanical systems, and assisting in tooling and mode changes per the production schedule.
Workers must execute lockout/tagout procedures on molding department equipment, maintain a computerized maintenance management system (CMMS), and uphold high departmental cleanliness standards.
Minimum qualifications require a High School Diploma or G.E.D., with an Associate degree in a technical field preferred.
Candidates must possess a minimum of three years of maintenance experience in Molding, Paint, or Assembly backgrounds.
Workers are required to be Forklift, Aerial Lift, and First Aid certified.

Magna International operates as an equal opportunity employer, hiring based on experience and qualifications regardless of color, ancestry, religion, gender, origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, or gender identity.
The job posting explicitly states that this position has expired.
source_title: Technician, Maintenance at Magna International, Inc. in Carrollton, Georgia | Job Openings for DisABLED Veterans

facts: Magna is one of the world's largest suppliers in the automotive space and operates as a global mobility technology company.
The company has over 65 years of expertise and utilizes an ecosystem of interconnected products combined with complete vehicle expertise to advance mobility.
The Magna Government Affairs Department focuses on identifying and maximizing economic incentives to support the company's business objectives.
The Senior Compliance & Economic Development Analyst - Tax Focus manages and optimizes economic incentive programs, ensures compliance, and implements tax-saving initiatives.

The role collaborates closely with the Executive Director of Government Affairs, the Magna Government Affairs Compliance team, and internal stakeholders across corporate functions, groups, and divisions.
The position requires expertise in tax policy, financial analysis, and regulatory compliance to drive positive economic development outcomes.
The analyst collaborates across Magna's global operations to ensure accurate reporting, regulatory compliance, and the effective execution of economic incentive programs.
The role involves evaluating property valuations, identifying tax savings opportunities, and ensuring adherence to government contracts and incentive agreements.
Essential duties include managing and ensuring compliance with economic incentive agreements, tax credit programs, and contractual obligations.
The analyst reviews property valuations and assessments to identify property tax savings opportunities and develops strategies to optimize those savings.
The role serves as a liaison with government agencies, tax authorities, and external partners to address tax-related issues and ensure compliance with evolving regulations.
Responsibilities include researching, analyzing, and interpreting financial and tax data to support strategic decision-making and risk mitigation.
The analyst enhances reporting processes for transparency and accountability while maintaining accurate records within the Government Affairs CRM database.
The position works closely with internal finance, legal, HR, and corporate compliance teams to align economic incentive projects with business objectives.
The role monitors unclaimed property claims for accurate documentation and reporting, and identifies or recovers unclaimed funds owed to Magna.
The analyst provides subject matter expertise and guidance on tax policies, compliance best practices, and regulatory changes.
Project management duties include overseeing project budgets, preparing summaries of tax savings opportunities, and providing leadership in executing compliance-related initiatives.
The position requires a minimum of 3+ years of experience in state and local tax compliance, economic incentives, regulatory affairs, or corporate finance.
Candidates must possess strong knowledge of tax credits, property tax assessment processes, and compliance requirements.
A Bachelor's degree in finance, economics, business administration, public policy, or a related field is required.
An advanced degree (MBA, JD, or master's in taxation) or certification in tax/accounting-related disciplines is preferred.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint is required.
The work environment is a normal office setting requiring regular sitting or standing, with occasional travel of approximately 25% to support incentive negotiations and compliance reviews.
The role supports Magna's long-term economic strategy by leveraging financial incentives, ensuring compliance, and supporting the company's global expansion and operational excellence.
The company discourages email applications to comply with GDPR requirements and local Data Privacy Law.
The job posting indicates that the position has expired.
source_title: Senior Analyst, Compliance & Economic Development - Tax Focus at Magna International, Inc. in Troy, Michigan | Job Openings for DisABLED Veterans

facts: GDEcD International Offices are located worldwide in Argentina, Brazil, Canada, Chile, Colombia, Europe, Israel, Japan, Korea, Mexico, Peru, Southeast Asia, the United Kingdom, and Ireland.
The GDEcD facilitates international trades, attracts foreign investment, and strengthens state-level international relations.
Gov. Brian Kemp and Commissioner Pat Wilson frequently go on trade missions, intertwining business and diplomacy by meeting with companies and representatives abroad.
Gov. Brian Kemp and Commissioner Pat Wilson most recently visited Belgium and the United Kingdom in February 2026.
The governor and commissioner host delegates and company executives for trade talks, such as the Belgian delegation with Princess Astrid of Belgium visiting Georgia in June 2022.
The top five exports from Georgia in 2025 were civilian aircraft parts, computers and hardware, motor vehicles, network communications equipment, and medical devices.

Many large international companies are located in Georgia, including Hapag-Lloyd, Hyundai Motor Group, KIA Motors, La Regina Atlantica, Mercedes-Benz USA, Porsche USA, Q CELLS, SK On, TEKLAS, and more.
Atlanta alone partners with 17 sister cities, including Brussels, Nuremberg, Tbilisi, Lagos, Rio de Janeiro, Toulouse, and more.
In 1967, former Mayor Ivan Allen Jr. forged Atlanta’s first sister city relationship with Salzburg, Austria.

The University of Georgia, Georgia Institute of Technology, and Emory University coordinate study abroad programs across the world, notably with the UGA Campus in Cortona, Italy, and the GT Europe campus in Metz, France.
Athens, GA, and Cortona are sister cities.
More than 8,000 UGA students have attended the Cortona program.
More than 13,000 students have attended the GT Europe campus since its founding in 1990.
Atlanta has over 70 consular or trade offices and over 30 chambers of commerce.
The International School of Atlanta opened in 1984 to offer diplomats and other international families International Baccalaureate programs in multiple languages.
Defense companies are booming industries in Georgia.
Data centers and Artificial Intelligence are becoming increasingly important topics in Georgia, especially considering the state’s attractive energy prices.
The automotive industry has been one of the major sectors in Georgia.
There have been several changes within the automotive industry, with the push for and then the pullback of electric vehicles while balancing the continued demand for combustion vehicles.
Georgia has invested in renewable energy and sustainability research, but its companies still mainly produce traditional cars and vehicles.
It is challenging for US and international companies to keep up with the prices and technology in China, which are some of the most competitive in the field.
Initiatives to increase robotics and automation in Georgia include RoboGeorgia and the Institute for Robotics and Intelligent Machines (IRIM) at GT.
These programs aim to increase automation by helping companies connect with suppliers, technology, and information to advance competitiveness.
Changes in US tariff policy have led international companies to express concern that trade relation uncertainty will negatively impact their earnings reports.
Hyundai has invested billions into Georgia and opened many job opportunities across the state.
In September 2025, Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers arrested approximately 475 people from the Hyundai plant in Georgia.
There was significant backlash from South Korean officials advocating for the rights of their citizens following the ICE arrests.
The event fractured the relationship between Korea and the United States, causing tension over the safety of international workers in the US and the relationship between Seoul and Washington, D.C.
The situation threatened Georgia’s over fifty years of close relations with Korea.

Despite changing political and trade situations, many companies remain interested in expanding to Georgia because of right-to-work policies that make it easier for manufacturers to manage their local workforce.

Maintaining strong trade, investment, and diplomatic ties between Georgia and international partners is highlighted as important for continuing long-standing partnerships and benefiting from the US market.
source_title: Georgia International – Georgia Political Review

facts: Since its founding more than 50 years ago, Maxxis International has been dedicated to making tires riders and drivers can trust for quality and performance.
Founded in Taiwan, Maxxis got its start by making bicycle tires, and soon expanded its offerings to include tires for auto, light truck, ATV, motorcycle, trailer, and more.
Today, Maxxis distributes its products in more than 180 countries.
The company has operations in Asia, North America, South America, Europe, Africa, and Australia, employing more than 30,000 people.
Maxxis’ ability to serve customers around the world is rooted in its manufacturing capacity, which includes facilities in Taiwan, China, Thailand, Vietnam, and India.
Maxxis opened its first distribution facility in North America in 1985.
The company has more than half a million square feet of warehouse space across the U.S., located in Atlanta, Los Angeles, Dallas/Fort Worth, and Indianapolis.
Using the latest technology, Maxxis engineers at R&D centers in Asia, Europe, and North America work to ensure that the company’s products are cutting edge.
Maxxis also has the largest tire proving ground in China.
The company solicits feedback and assistance from its line-up of sponsored riders, who report on tires’ performance in the harshest, most demanding conditions.
source_title: About Maxxis Tire Company - MAXXIS US

facts: The area landed a new $46.3 million state-of-the-art automobile manufacturing facility.
The facility is located in rural Bibb County, near the Woodstock community.
The 150,000 sq ft facility is located inside the Scott G. Davis Industrial Park near Interstate 20/59.
Steve Jordan, MöllerTech’s North American president and CEO, stated that “Bibb County is the perfect location for our new state-of-the-art facility.”
The facility will be the flagship manufacturing plant for the company.
The plant joins two other U.S. Mollertech facilities – one in Elberton, Georgia, and one in Shelby Township, Michigan.
The plant will produce interior parts for the next generation of Mercedes-Bens SUV’s.
Mollertech expects to employ 222 workers at the new plant.
The company is currently hiring for Quality, Maintenance, Production Planning, and Production Operatives roles.
Mercedes is currently building a $248.2 million Global Logistics Center and after-sales North American hub in Bibb County.
The new Mercedes facility will create jobs for nearly 700 people.
The existing Mercedes-Benz plant is located just 11 miles away in Vance, Alabama.
Once the new Mercedes facility opens in Bibb County, the Birmingham 7 county region will be home to 16 German based companies that employ almost 6,700 people.

Lee Smith, 2018 Chairman for the Birmingham Business Alliance and East Region Executive for BBVA Compass, stated that “Jobs can transform communities and we’ve already begun to see that happen in Bibb County with the addition of MöllerTech, followed by Mercedes-Benz.”
Mollertech is a branch of German-based MollerGroup, one of the oldest manufacturing companies in Germany.
The family-owned company employs over 1,800 people worldwide.
Mollertech currently develops and produces interior parts for the automotive industry for customers including Audi, BMW, Daimler, GM, Honda, Porsche, Rolls-Royce, and Toyota.
source_title: German based automobile company opens manufacturing plant in Birmingham area | Bham Now

facts: German automotive supplier MöllerTech held a ribbon-cutting ceremony to officially open its new facility in Woodstock, Alabama.
The 151,313 s.f. facility produces interior parts for automakers such as Mercedes-Benz.
The plant's operational timeline was accelerated by eight months.
Gunther Schmidt, CEO of MöllerGroup (MöllerTech’s parent company), stated the new state-of-the-art plant has been a tremendous effort for everyone involved.
Steve Jordan, president of MöllerTech USA LLC, noted they just opened the building and plan to be there for many years to come.
MöllerTech selected Gray Construction to design and build its advanced manufacturing facility, which will serve as a flagship U.S. operation.
The facility is located just six miles from the Vance, Alabama campus for Mercedes-Benz.
The plant will specifically manufacture parts for Mercedes-Benz’s next-generation SUVs.
Stephen Gray, president and CEO of Gray Construction, commented on the partnership and the company's significant impact on Alabama and the auto industry.
The plant currently employs approximately 50 people.
MöllerTech expects to have 222 team members once the facility reaches full capacity in 2019.
Founded nearly 300 years ago, parent company MöllerGroup is a family-owned business with 1,800 employees worldwide.
MöllerTech is recognized worldwide for the development and production of plastic parts in the automotive industry, as well as surfacing, flocking, leather covering, gluing, and stitching services.

Gray Construction is a nationally-ranked engineering, architecture and construction firm with nearly 60 years of experience in manufacturing, automotive, food and beverage, distribution and retail facility construction.

Gray Construction currently has several large projects underway or recently completed, including facilities for Mercedes-Benz in Vance, Ala., MöllerTech USA in Woodstock, Ala., Fritz Winter North America LP in Franklin, Ky., Volvo Car US Operations Inc. in Ridgeville, S.C., and Sofidel in Circleville, Ohio.

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development announced Nifco KTW America Corporation with the 2020 Automotive Supplier of the Year GEAR Award.
Nifco KTW America Corporation has over 200 people (200+ people).
The GEAR (Georgia Emerging Automotive Recognition) Awards are designed to recognize forward movement in Georgia’s automotive and mobility industry through innovation and emerging technologies.
Nifco KTW America Corporation specializes in the production of interior and exterior plastic products as well as hi-gloss interior painted décor trim.

facts: Overall sales for FY ended Mar. 31, 2016 were 265,683 million JPY, representing a 17.9% year-over-year increase.
Operating income for FY ended Mar. 31, 2016 was 27,574 million JPY, up 31.5% from the previous fiscal year.
Ordinary income for FY ended Mar. 31, 2016 was 26,374 million JPY, a 27.9% increase year-over-year.
Profit for the year attributable to owners of the parent reached 17,742 million JPY in FY ended Mar. 31, 2016, up 37.5% year-over-year.
Composite plastic molded products sales totaled 239,185 million JPY for FY ended Mar. 31, 2016, an 18.5% increase.
Operating income for composite plastic molded products was 29,591 million JPY, up 29.4% year-over-year.
Nifco's mid-term business plan (Nifco Innovation Plan 2020) targets overall sales of 330.0 billion yen and operating income of 38.0 billion yen by FY ending Mar. 2021.
The company plans to invest JPY 3,300 million for R&D activities during the fiscal year ending March 2017.
Overall capital expenditure for FY ended Mar. 31, 2016 was 21,488 million JPY.
Nifco Inc.'s German subsidiary, Nifco KTW GmbH, established a new company in Georgia, USA named Nifco KTW America Corporation.
The new Georgia-based subsidiary will manufacture and sell plastic parts for automobiles.
The facility is capitalized at USD 200 million (approximately JPY 2.4 billion).
Capitalization funding is provided 90% by Nifco KTW and 10% by Nifco America.
The new company will supply its products to a German automaker's North American facility.
Planned capital investment for the new Georgia plant is JPY 3,088 million, with an investment timeline from May 2015 to May 2016.
Volkswagen AG nominated Nifco KTS GmbH as one of 55 strategic partners across 61 fields of competence under its "Volkswagen Future Automotive Supply Tracks" (FAST) initiative.
Nifco KTS GmbH was specifically designated for kinematic components.
The nomination was announced via a press release on May 23, 2016.
Nifco acquired German automotive plastic parts supplier KTW GmbH & Co. KG and its group companies to convert them into a fully owned subsidiary.
The KTW Group manufactures and sells automotive plastic parts such as instrument panels and door trims, with its sole plant located in Bavaria.
Prior to acquisition, sales to BMW accounted for more than half of KTW's total revenue.
Nifco aims to enhance its European business by expanding relationships with German automakers using its global sales network.
The company consolidated its automotive resin parts manufacturing subsidiaries in Germany under its fully owned subsidiary, Nifco Germany GmbH, effective December 31, 2014.

R&D activities are conducted mainly at the Nifco Technology Development Centre, with consolidated Yokosuka and Nagoya functions relocated to a building next to the Yokosuka Research Park in FY ending March 2015.

A testing facility and explosion-proof testing facility near the home office and R&D center were scheduled for completion during FY ending March 2017 to verify powertrain and fuel-system product capabilities.
The company is developing energy-saving automotive products that switch base materials from metal to plastic to save weight and improve fuel efficiency.
Nifco finished developing new fuel-system parts compliant with U.S. evaporative hydrocarbon emission regulations.
The company developed related products for collision-reduction brake parts and obstacle-detection sensors for safety applications.

Nifco spent JPY 6,876 million in capital investments for FY ended Mar. 31, 2016 on establishing a new headquarters office in Yokosuka, the Yamagata Plant, die manufacturing facilities, and fuel-system product equipment.
Nifco America Corporation spent JPY 1,273 million to install facilities for fuel-system products and forming machines.
Nifco (HK Ltd.) spent JPY 2,301 million mainly on constructing a new production plant.
Nifco (Yancheng) Co., Ltd. in China spent JPY 1,736 million mainly on die facilities and forming machines.
A total of JPY 6,285 million was spent in the Asian region for facilities and equipment during FY ended Mar. 31, 2016.
source_title: Nifco Inc. Business Report FY ended Mar. 2016 - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: The Nifco group is invested in designing, developing and manufacturing quality components for the global Automotive Industry.
Products range from the interior of a vehicle to the exterior, inclusive of the engine.
Exterior components range from corner covers to wheel arch liners.
The company incorporates material and fastener know-how into larger, multi functional components.
Nifco supplies Powertrain components to the Global Automotive Industry with full capabilities in design, development, manufacturing and testing.
Kinematics trim assemblies feature controlled movement incorporating specialist Motion Control Devices.
Nifco has specialist business units dedicated to fuel and safety critical parts.
The company designs, develops and manufactures quality interior trim solutions from single mouldings to complex assemblies.
In 1979, Nifco invented a small gear damper for Sharp cassette players and now supplies dampers as part of complex functional assemblies.
Nifco produces valves which control the fuel in vehicles.
The company manufactures car seat sides and door handles.
Nifco originated as 'Nippon Industrial Fastener Corp' and fasteners remain at the heart of its operations.
The company is built on values called the Nifco Spirit, fostered at inception and passed down through generations.

facts: Georgia was named the top state in the country for doing business for the fifth consecutive year by Area Development, a leading corporate site selection and facility planning publication.
Georgia ranked first among all states for workforce development programs.
Georgia ranked first among all states for having a cooperative and responsive state government.
Governor Nathan Deal announced the ranking alongside Area Development Publisher Dennis J. Shea.
The Stephens County Development Authority has been highly successful in attracting major international corporations to establish operations in the county.
Stephens County Development Authority Executive Director Tim Martin attributes the county's success to its attractive workforce development program.
The Stephens County workforce development program operates in conjunction with North Georgia Technical College to produce a job-ready labor force.
Tim Martin stated that finding employees is the number one consideration for many potential employers.
The authority utilizes the Currahee Campus and maintains partnerships with existing industry to support workforce needs.
Tim Martin highlighted the role of the local school system in ensuring the workforce meets employer requirements.
Companies such as GEM Industries and Nifco KTW America continue to grow and expand their businesses in Stephens County.
Toccoa-Stephens County has become a favored location for filmmakers, hosting five major movies over several years.
The Toccoa-Stephens County development authority works closely with national site developers and project managers to secure new businesses for the state.
The authority maintains close relationships with project managers selling the State of Georgia to ensure they have viable locations to offer.
The authority actively uses various social media platforms to promote its business climate and opportunities.
Area Development’s 2018 Top States for Doing Business rankings were based on a survey of site consultants regarding location and facility planning.
Survey categories included the overall cost of doing business, corporate tax environment, business incentive programs, competitive labor environment, and access to capital and project funding.

During fiscal year 2018, the Georgia Department of Economic Development Global Commerce Division helped to create more than 27,000 new jobs through a record-breaking 419 expansion or relocation projects.
The 419 projects in fiscal 2018 amounted to $5.56 billion in investments across Georgia communities.
