facts: Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc. (NAMI) and Business Development Center (MI) are part of the Nisshinbo Group.
The Nisshinbo Group consists of 59 subsidiaries and 40 affiliates worldwide.
Nisshinbo was established in 1907, when it was producing yarns and threads.
In the 1940s, Nisshinbo was called upon to manufacture automotive friction materials and developed a process using woven fabric embedded with friction material.
Nisshinbo brakes and friction materials have evolved to serve diverse applications in the global transportation market.
Nisshinbo maintains a library of more than one thousand different recipes, each specific to customer applications.
North American operations are dedicated facilities serving OEM transportation manufacturers in the NAFTA market.
NAC is located in Sterling Heights, Michigan, and provides team management, engineering, prototyping, and manufacturing capabilities.
NAMI is Nisshinbo's state-of-the-art facility located in Covington, Georgia.

facts: Indian River Transport Company announced plans to build a new trucking terminal in Cordele, Crisp County, Georgia.
The new facility will have frontage along Interstate 75 at Exit 102.
The Cordele terminal will include offices, truck bays, washout facilities, and an asphalt pad of about eight acres for trailer storage.
Construction should begin in mid-2019.
Once completed, Indian River anticipates hiring approximately 125 people.
About 100 of the new hires will be commercial truck drivers.
Indian River is a privately-owned food grade tank carrier providing transportation services throughout the United States.
The company offers import/export services between the US, Canada and Mexico.
Much of its liquid freight includes milk, various juices, and other beverages.

facts: As of January 3, 2022, Nivel Parts & Manufacturing Co., LLC appointed Brett Hankey as Chief Executive Officer.
Brett Hankey takes over the position from Brent Moore who has been CEO since 2018.
During Brent's tenure, Nivel made significant organizational investments and profitability improvements to create a foundation for continued growth.
Brent Moore continues to support and guide the business as the newly appointed Vice Chairman, Board of Directors for Nivel.
In this new role, Brent will leverage his 20+ years of experience to provide a unique perspective across a wide range of governance and strategic objectives.

Brett Hankey joins Nivel from Distribution International where he served as Senior Vice President and General Manager responsible for the Sales and Operations team across the eastern half of the country.
Throughout his career, Brett gained extensive commercial, operational, and strategic leadership experience across a variety of distribution and manufacturing industries.
Founded in 1968, Nivel is a global manufacturer, distributor, and seller of aftermarket parts and accessories for niche vehicles and heavy-duty equipment.
It is a trusted business partner to thousands of dealers, distributors, and consumers in the US and international markets.
Nivel owns the most valuable brands in the aftermarket niche vehicle parts and accessories categories: MadJax, Red Dot, GTW, Jake's, Reliance, Seizmik and Highlifter.

facts: Nivel is the industry leader in aftermarket solutions for personal transportation vehicles (PTV).
Nivel announced the acquisition of Xtreme Golf Cart Mats.
Xtreme Golf Cart Mats is the premier provider of high-quality, custom-fit golf cart floor mats.
Every mat is designed and engineered in America, ensuring a precision fit that protects vehicle flooring from debris, water, and wear.
Donnie Jouppi serves as Chief Executive Officer of Nivel.

Donnie Jouppi stated they were attracted to Xtreme Golf Cart Mats for their unwavering dedication to excellence, their “gold standard” approach to product design, and speed to market for new fitments.
Adding Xtreme’s American-engineered golf floor mats to its catalog allows Nivel to offer customers a level of interior protection and aesthetic appeal that is unmatched in the industry.
Nivel plans to leverage its extensive distribution network to make Xtreme products more accessible.
Customers can expect continued innovation through collaborative engineering focused on expanding the fitment catalog for new vehicle models.
Customers can expect premium quality with maintenance of the rigorous manufacturing standards customers have come to rely on.
Customers will be able to order Xtreme products through Nivel at the same time as their other parts, accessories, and Bolt batteries.
The acquisition marks a milestone in Nivel’s aggressive growth strategy focusing on brands that prioritize quality and American-led design.
Brandon Burkey serves as CEO of Xtreme Golf Cart Mats.

Brandon Burkey stated that every mat started with the question of whether he would put it in his own cart, which built Xtreme from the ground up into the best-fitting, longest-lasting mat in the industry.
Brandon Burkey noted Nivel has the network and resources to put that product in front of every golf cart owner in the country.
For over 59 years Nivel has been redefining the Personal Transportation Vehicle (PTV), Off Road Vehicle (ORV), Heavy Duty (HD), and Automotive experience with innovation, style, and functionality.
Nivel manufactures today’s best brands in these industries, many right here in the U.S.
source_title: Nivel Parts & Manufacturing Expands Premium Accessory Portfolio with Acquisition of Xtreme Golf Cart Mats - Golf Carting Magazine

facts: The City of Mableton was incorporated for the first time as a city in November following a voter referendum and approval from state legislators and Gov. Brian Kemp.
Gov. Brian Kemp signed the bill to create the new City of Mableton in May 2022, which passed with a 134-2 vote in the House and a 55-0 vote in the Senate.
The incorporation process took approximately six years from initial planning to voter approval.
Mableton now has a population of approximately 80,000 residents, making it the largest city in Cobb County.
The newly incorporated city covers almost 36 square miles.
Mableton is building its own departments for planning, zoning, code enforcement, parks, and sanitation.
Public safety services, including police and fire, remain under the jurisdiction of Cobb County.
The city charter provided two years to complete service transitions, with a target date of June 1, 2025.
By June 1, 2025, Mableton aims to have at least three to four key services fully transitioned to city management.
Initial working space for the new city administration was established at the Riverside Epicenter.
The $33-million Riverside Epicenter is located adjacent to Six Flags and down the street from the future Veterans Village site.
Tunnels to Towers is partnering with elected officials and Home Depot to develop a new facility in Mableton.
The Tunnels to Towers project will offer safe and secure housing to more than 90 Atlanta veterans from across metro Atlanta.
The future Veterans Village will be located on the former Wingate Hotel site.
The Veterans Village will feature livable apartments equipped with a bedroom, living room, kitchen, and bathroom.
Veterans at the new facility will have access to a community gym, business center, cafeteria, commercial kitchen space, and 24/7 support services.
Mayor Michael Owens is leading a vision of revitalizing major corridors including Veterans Memorial Highway, Mableton Parkway, and Riverside Drive to attract new businesses and bolster existing ones.
The Mableton 2045 Comprehensive Plan serves as a blueprint for sustainable growth over the next two decades.
The comprehensive plan covers land use planning, transportation, housing, economic development, parks and recreation, environmental sustainability, and community facilities and services.
In the next five years, Mableton plans to further develop commercial districts, enhance public transportation options, and invest in sustainable development projects.
Over 10 years, Mableton's vision includes becoming a regional hub for innovation and culture with thriving business sectors, green spaces, and robust community engagement.
source_title: Get To Know The New Mableton - Cobb In Focus

facts: India has reportedly allowed four Chinese power equipment manufacturers with factories in the country to participate in government tenders for critical power projects.
The four companies are TBEA Energy, Nanjing Electric India, New Northeast Electric India and Taikai Electric (India).
The exemption allowing these companies to participate in tenders is valid for two years and should not be treated as a precedent for other companies.
India's power ministry sought the exemption in January for entities with manufacturing units in India involved in critical power projects.
The exemption comes as India accelerates the expansion of its transmission network to support rising electricity demand and renewable energy additions.
Previously, amid bilateral tension, New Delhi required Chinese bidders to register with a government panel and secure political and security clearances before competing for any state contract.
In January, India was examining broader relaxations on Chinese bidders for government contracts, amid easing of border tensions.
The latest exemption does not suggest that India has abandoned its broader restrictions on Chinese companies.
In March, India would offer faster approval for investments involving minority Chinese stakes across a wider range of sectors, including electronics, batteries, rare-earth magnets and processing.
The faster investment approval applies provided Chinese ownership remains below 10 percent and majority control stays with Indian residents.
Security concerns related to Chinese investments remain and proposals will continue to undergo scrutiny.
Excluding Chinese supply chains could increase costs, delay delivery and reduce the efficiency of infrastructure expansion against the backdrop of India's rising infrastructure and energy needs.
India's coal-fired power output in June rose to its highest level since November 2023, as rising electricity demand kept pressure on the country's power supply system.
In the first five months of this year, bilateral trade jumped more than 20 percent year-on-year, showing that economic complementarity remains strong despite lingering political frictions.
Chinese Minister of Commerce Wang Wentao met Indian Minister of Commerce and Industry Piyush Goyal on the sidelines of the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference in Yaoundé, Cameroon.

China expressed willingness to work with India to make economic and trade cooperation a "ballast stone" in bilateral relations and welcomed India's participation in events such as the "Shared Market: Export to China" series to expand its exports to China.
In April, India's PHD Chamber of Commerce and Industry (PHDCCI) renewable energy and EV delegation visited Shanghai and held B2B meetings with Chinese companies.
Stronger India-China partnerships in renewable energy and EV sectors could help accelerate innovation and reduce technology costs.
source_title: India’s selective opening to Chinese power firms reflects pragmatic adjustment to energy needs, but more open approach needed: expert - Global Times

facts: Pak-Lite, Inc. was founded in Atlanta, Georgia in 1972 by Frank Pritchard as a manufacturer of protective packaging products.
Pak-Lite is a leading North American converter/manufacturer of US-sourced foams, rubber, nonwovens, foils, films, and specialty materials.
The company operates manufacturing facilities in Georgia (GA), North Carolina (NC), and Taiwan.
Pak-Lite employs over 300 dedicated professionals.
Manufacturing capabilities include extrusion lamination to foams and rubber substrates.
The company combines materials into composite form and supplies die-cut parts, components, roll stock, or sheeting.
Finished parts often include pressure-sensitive adhesives and release liners.
Additional capabilities include material selection, prototyping, skiving/slitting, rotary/flatbed die cutting, kiss cutting, spooling, precision parts manufacturing, and assembly.
Pak-Lite holds ISO 9001:2015 and IATF 16949 certifications.
The company provides converting services for foams, rubbers, and nonwoven fabrics for OEMs and the Tier 1 automotive industry.
Pak-Lite assists customers in designing exact components to meet demanding specifications.
The company has extensive experience with 3M Adhesives and Avery Adhesives.
Pak-Lite sources top foam, rubber, polymer, packaging, nonwoven, fiber, and film suppliers worldwide.
The customer base includes many of the top OEM and industrial companies in the world.
source_title: Foam Manufacturer | Pak-Lite Inc.

canonical_name: Radar-Transparent Protective Coatings Market Research Report 2033
facts: Global radar-transparent protective coatings market size reached USD 1.12 billion in 2024.
Market projected to grow at a CAGR of 7.8% from 2025 to 2033, reaching a forecasted market size of USD 2.23 billion by 2033.
Growth driven by increasing demand in defense and aerospace sectors, heightened investments in stealth technology, and proliferation of advanced radar systems.
Key growth driver is escalating demand for stealth and low-observable technologies in aerospace and defense to minimize radar cross-sections for aircraft, drones, and naval vessels.
Integration of radar-transparent coatings into next-generation unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and advanced missile systems is catalyzing market growth.

Rapid technological advancements in material science have enabled development of novel polymer-based, ceramic-based, and composite materials offering superior durability, thermal stability, and chemical resistance while remaining transparent to radar frequencies.
Continuous innovation in spray coating, dip coating, and vapor deposition technologies has improved efficiency, scalability, and cost-effectiveness of applying these coatings.
Increasing emphasis on sustainability and regulatory compliance is prompting manufacturers to develop eco-friendly, low-VOC, and non-toxic alternatives for commercial and industrial sectors.
North America dominates the radar-transparent protective coatings market, accounting for the largest share in 2024 with a market size of USD 420 million.
Europe is the second-largest regional market with a market size of USD 310 million in 2024 and a projected CAGR of 7.2% through 2033.
Asia Pacific region is experiencing the fastest growth with a market size of USD 250 million in 2024 and a projected CAGR of 9.1% through 2033.
Latin America and Middle East & Africa markets had sizes of USD 80 million and USD 60 million respectively in 2024, poised for growth via infrastructure development.
Polymer-based coatings hold significant market share due to lightweight properties, flexibility, and excellent radar transparency, widely used in aerospace and defense.
Ceramic-based coatings are gaining traction in high-temperature and harsh environment applications like aerospace engines and missile guidance systems due to superior thermal stability and hardness.
Composite coatings represent a rapidly expanding segment by combining advantageous properties of polymers, ceramics, and other materials for tailored mechanical strength and environmental resistance.

Other material types include advanced glass, fluoropolymers, specialty resins, graphene-based coatings, and nano-engineered films selected for niche applications requiring unique optical or dielectric properties.

Aerospace & defense segment dominates the application landscape, accounting for over 45% of market share in 2024, driven by need for radar stealth and operational reliability in military aircraft, drones, and naval vessels.

Automotive industry is emerging as a significant growth area for radar-transparent protective coatings, particularly with the rise of autonomous vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS).

Modern vehicles increasingly rely on radar and LiDAR sensors for navigation and collision avoidance, requiring coatings that protect sensors from environmental damage without interfering with signal transmission.
Integration of these coatings into smart vehicles and electric cars is expected to accelerate as automotive manufacturers prioritize sensor reliability and vehicle safety.

Electronics sector uses radar-transparent coatings to shield sensitive components in radar systems, communication devices, and high-frequency antennas amid proliferation of 5G infrastructure and IoT devices.
Telecommunications industry utilizes these coatings to protect antennas and base stations from environmental exposure and corrosion during global rollout of 5G and future 6G networks.
Spray coating technology is widely favored for its versatility, scalability, and ability to achieve uniform thickness over complex geometries in aerospace and automotive industries.
Dip coating is prevalent for smaller components and batch processing, offering excellent coverage and adhesion for electronic parts and sensors.
Vapor deposition techniques (CVD and PVD) are employed for high-performance coatings demanding exceptional purity and uniformity in advanced electronics and aerospace components.
Military sector dominates end-user demand, accounting for more than 50% of total demand in 2024, driven by imperative to maintain stealth and protect sensitive equipment.

Commercial sector growth fueled by integration of radar/sensor tech in aviation, automotive, telecom, and consumer electronics, with airlines adopting coatings to enhance safety and reduce maintenance costs.

Industrial end-users including manufacturers of high-frequency antennas and industrial automation systems adopt coatings to protect components from environmental exposure and electromagnetic interference.

Market opportunities include advancements in nanostructured materials, eco-friendly coatings, expansion into emerging markets, and integration with smart technologies like self-healing and anti-fouling properties.
Key challenges include high material costs, complex manufacturing processes, stringent regulatory requirements, need for rigorous testing/certification, and intellectual property concerns.
Major companies in the market include PPG Industries, AkzoNobel, Hempel, Axalta Coating Systems, and Sherwin-Williams.
PPG Industries is renowned for advanced aerospace coatings and strong R&D capabilities.
AkzoNobel is a global leader in sustainable and high-performance coating solutions.
Hempel and Axalta Coating Systems are key suppliers to defense and industrial sectors offering radar-transparent and specialty coatings.
Sherwin-Williams is a prominent player with an extensive product portfolio and global reach.
The market is highly dynamic with frequent new product launches, mergers and acquisitions, and collaborative research initiatives aimed at developing next-generation coatings.
New players and startups are entering the market with disruptive innovations in nanomaterials, smart coatings, and eco-friendly formulations to challenge established competitors.
source_title: Radar-Transparent Protective Coatings Market Research Report 2033

facts: The State of Georgia has become a center of international business, with Atlanta and Savannah serving as primary global gateways for people and products through air, sea, and rail.
Decades of strategic decisions by state leaders are delivering generational investments that continue to keep Georgia at the forefront of global trends and stretching into new markets.
Fifty years ago in 1973, then-Governor Jimmy Carter began to establish Georgia’s physical presence abroad.
Coryn Marsik serves as Director of International Investment at the Georgia Department of Economic Development.

Coryn Marsik stated that Georgia having international representation for over 50 years has helped give its economy remarkable resiliency, and that these offices recruit diverse investments to create jobs for Georgians and help companies reach new global markets.
The State of Georgia’s first international office was established in Tokyo in 1973.
Japanese companies continue to be the top international employer in Georgia to this day.
Active Japan-Georgia organizations include the Japan-America Society of Georgia and the Japan External Trade Organization.
Georgia is a charter member of the Southeast U.S.-Japan Alliance.
The State of Georgia’s Europe Office was established shortly after the 1973 Tokyo office opening, with professionals serving the European market from Munich, Germany.
Germany is one of Georgia’s top investor nations and has a robust mobility industry.
In 2020, Governor Brian Kemp and a State of Georgia delegation met with automotive companies headquartered in Germany to discuss electrification and other alternative fuels.
Following the 2020 trip, Georgia set a new goal to identify opportunities at every step of the supply chain.
The mobility and battery supply chain have announced more than $25.5 billion in investments and an estimated 30,700 new jobs over the next few years.
European-based companies enhancing Georgia’s mobility ecosystem include GEDIA Automotive, Aurubis AG, and Heliox.
Governor Brian Kemp served on a panel at the 2023 World Economic Forum in Davos-Klosters, Switzerland, sharing that one can count on stability and a great economy and business environment in Georgia.
Ellen Kraft serves as Managing Director of the Europe Office.

Ellen Kraft stated that the European market is full of opportunity for attracting innovative companies, helping Georgia businesses connect with new customers, and establishing new ties, noting the expansion of relationships with partners like the new North Rhine-Westphalia (NRW) office in Atlanta.
International businesses operating in Georgia tap into university-backed research, innovative ecosystems, and solutions-oriented partnerships.
Commissioner Pat Wilson explains how Georgia is reaping the seeds sown decades ago by visionary leaders.

facts: Rivian Automotive leaders are confident as the company starts long-delayed work on a $5 billion facility in Georgia.
The money-losing California-based company breaks ground Tuesday east of Atlanta.
President Donald Trump’s successful push to roll back electric vehicle tax credits impacts buyers starting Sept. 30, who will no longer qualify for savings of up to $7,500 per car.
Rivian Chief Policy Officer Alan Hoffman said the company believes it can sell electric vehicles not for environmental or tax incentive reasons, but because they're superior.
Hoffman stated: “We did not build this company based upon federal tax incentives.”
Hoffman added: “And we’re going to prove that we’re going to be successful in the future.”
The Georgia plant was first announced in 2021.
The Georgia plant is key to Rivian reaching profitability.
Rivian currently makes the high-end R1T pickup truck and the R1S sport utility vehicle in Normal, Illinois.
Rivian also makes delivery vans for Amazon and others at its Illinois facility.
Rivian's truck prices start at $71,000.
The Illinois plant will begin making smaller R2 SUVs next year, with prices starting at $45,000.
An expanded Illinois plant will be able to assemble 215,000 vehicles yearly.
If the R2 is a hit and Rivian successfully produces an even smaller R3, it will need more capacity.
The company has said the Georgia operation will be able to make 200,000 vehicles yearly starting in 2028.
Rivian plans another 200,000 in capacity in phase two at the Georgia plant.
This additional volume would spread fixed costs over many more vehicles.
The projections represent a big leap from the 40,000 to 46,000 vehicles Rivian expects to deliver this year.
Deliveries are down from 52,000 last year.
The company is limiting production now in part to launch 2026 models.
Alex Oyler, North American director of auto research firm SBD Automotive, said: “For Rivian, it’s do-or-die time.”
Oyler noted that Tesla showed the key to profitability is scale.
Oyler stated you can’t scale if your cheapest vehicle is $70,000.
Oyler added they need that plant online to achieve a level of scale of R2 and ultimately R3.
Sales growth for electric vehicles in the United States is slowing, rising only 1.5% in 2025's first half, according to Cox Automotive.
Tesla accounted for almost 45% of U.S. electric vehicle sales in that period, according to Cox.
Tesla is losing market share as others gain: General Motors' slice of American EV sales has climbed to 13%.
Rivian had a 3% share in the first half of the year, behind Tesla and six traditional automakers.
Excluding Tesla, Rivian is the most successful of the startup automakers.
The company initially tapped a largely unfilled niche: demand for electric pickups and SUVs.
Competition now includes Ford’s F-150 Lightning and the electric Chevrolet Silverado.
After an initial public offering in 2021, Rivian shares have fallen by more than 80%.
Automaker shares overall have outpaced the broader stock market.
Rivian lost $1.66 billion in 2025’s first half.
Some automakers’ ardor for electric vehicles is cooling.
Stellantis last week canceled Ram’s electric truck program.
Ford has delayed production at a new Tennessee plant.
General Motors abandoned plans to build electric vehicles at a suburban Detroit plant.
Sam Fiorani, a vice president at AutoForecast Solutions, said: “With all the competition out there in this market and the slowing growth of EVs, it does not play in Rivian’s favor.”
Fiorani added: “However, there still is an EV market out there.”
Georgia has pledged $1.5 billion of incentives to Rivian in exchange for 7,500 company jobs paying at least $56,000 a year on average.
Rivian can’t benefit from most incentives unless it meets employment goals.
The state is already spending $175 million to buy and grade land and improve roads.
Georgia Republican Gov. Brian Kemp has said he wants to make Georgia “the electric mobility capital of America.”
Kemp acknowledges Rivian faces bumps but remains confident the company can fulfill its promises.
Tesla has thousands of employees in California and Texas.
Two separate EV makers that hoped to assemble vehicles in a former GM plant in Lordstown, Ohio, went bankrupt.
Georgia’s Hyundai complex near Savannah is faring better, with production underway.
A battery plant at the Hyundai complex has been delayed by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement arresting 475 people on site, including more than 300 South Koreans.
Rivian was supposed to be making trucks by now at the 2,000-acre (800-hectare) site near Social Circle, about 45 miles (70 kilometers) east of Atlanta.
As the company burned through cash in 2024, it paused construction.
German automaker Volkswagen agreed to invest $5.8 billion in Rivian in exchange for software and electrical technology.
Then-President Joe Biden's administration in November agreed to loan Rivian $6.6 billion to build the Georgia plant.
Despite the Trump administration's hostility toward EVs, Hoffman said Rivian hopes the U.S. Department of Energy will distribute the loan money.
Hoffman argued the loan will boost domestic manufacturing.
Some neighbors still oppose the plant, saying it is an inappropriate neighbor to farms and will pollute the groundwater.
Eddie Clay, who lives less than a mile away, said: “I planned on dying and retiring on the front porch and the biggest project in Georgia has to go next door to me, of all places in the country?”
Clay says his well water turned mud-choked after excavation at the Rivian site.
There are other challenges for Rivian, including tariffs costing $2,000 per vehicle.
The Trump administration ending a tax-credit program will cost the company $140 million in revenue this year.
Long-term threats include low-priced, cutting-edge Chinese EVs.
Hoffman says Rivian is “in this for the long haul.”
Hoffman stated: “We think that we can compete with anyone out there and that once given the opportunity, we’re going to excel.”
source_title: It's 'do or die' for electric vehicle maker Rivian as it breaks ground on a $5 billion plant | Value The Markets

facts: Rivian has decided to build its planned second US plant to the east of Atlanta, in the state of Georgia.
Once completed, the plant should be able to produce up to 400,000 electric vehicles per year.
Construction is scheduled to begin in the summer of 2022 and production is to start in 2024.
The plant is to be located in Morgan and Walton counties.
The company says there are plans for a “carbon-conscious campus.”
In the summer of 2021, there were still rumours that Rivian was planning combined vehicle and battery cell production.
Rumours regarding the location being Mesa, Arizona or Fort Worth, Texas proved to be wrong.
The company wants to invest up to five billion dollars (around 4.4 billion euros) in the project in Georgia.
Investments will flow into buildings, production facilities, and site development.
Environmental compatibility, the company’s own renewable energy generation, and green spaces are also to play a role.
The 2,000-acre site will also include “abundant natural space.”
The site was chosen because of its advantageous logistic position, the availability of clean energy, and the number of available potential employees.
Rivian plans to employ up to 7,500 people on site.
Rivian will develop community engagement and workforce training programs in the new location area, similar to its plant in Normal, Illinois.

Rivian Chief People Officer Helen Russell stated: “We’re happy to partner with Georgia on our new manufacturing site, which will build our next generation of products that are important for scaling our business.”
In summer 2021, the company secured a number of vehicle names without giving details on what vehicle types these will be.
Helen Russell stated: “We’re confident that, alongside Illinois, Georgia is the place for Rivian to continue to scale and thrive.”
Rivian announced an expansion for its current sole plant in Normal, Illinois.
The electric automaker received approval for a 623,000 square-foot expansion, bringing its total space to more than four million square feet.
That would be an expansion of nearly 58,000 square feet to just over 370,000 square feet.
The additional space at the Illinois plant would be used for warehouse and manufacturing capacity.
Rivian went public in November 2021.
Rivian confirmed the Illinois expansion in its first-ever post-IPO shareholder report.
Production capacity at the first plant would be increased from 150,000 to 200,000 vehicles annually.
The first R1T vehicles have already been delivered.
In total, there are around 71,000 pre-orders for the R1 alone.
Rivian has not yet revealed more on its planned European plant.
A site in the UK south of Bristol and the Netherlands is in running for the European plant location.
Rivian might be taking over VDL Nedcar’s existing site in the Netherlands.
These plans appear to have pushed out US startup Canoo.
Canoo had already placed a contract manufacturing facility at VDL Nedcar, but announced it would not have its Lifestyle Vehicle built there after all.
This is because Rivian might not only use the Dutch production plant VDL NedCar for its own production but take it over completely.
As of an update on 03 May 2022, Rivian can count on subsidies and tax breaks totalling $1.5 billion (€1.43 billion) for its second US plant in Georgia.
The plant is expected to employ around 7,500 people and is scheduled to open at the end of 2024.

Under the agreement with the state of Georgia, Rivian has until the end of 2028 to reach the 7,500 jobs target and the $5 billion investment target to receive the full amount of incentives and tax savings.
source_title: Rivian reveals location of second US plant - electrive.com

facts: Validation vehicle production for the R2 model is scheduled for the first half of 2026, marking the final step before series production.
Pre-production cars for the R2 are already manufactured at Rivian's Illinois facility for final certification and crash testing.
CEO RJ Scaringe commented on social media platform X that the company is thrilled to begin deliveries to customers soon.
Hopes indicate the R2 launch could take place early in the first half of 2026.
The R2 is positioned as Rivian's breakthrough model, comparable to the Tesla Model 3 with higher production volume and lower costs.
Rivian's premium vehicle models are the R1T and R1S.
The compact and affordable electric SUV R2 was unveiled in spring 2024.
The R2 has a starting price of around $45,000 (approximately €38,700), with more expensive variants expected to launch first.
Initially, the R2 will be produced at Rivian's existing plant in Normal.
Construction of a new Rivian plant in Georgia has been postponed, with work only beginning in September 2025.
Postponing the Georgia plant construction is expected to save the company $2.25 billion in capital required for the R2 market launch.
Expanded capacity at the Normal facility could produce up to 155,000 R2 units per year.
Once the postponed Georgia production becomes operational, annual capacity could increase by an additional 400,000 units.
Rivian delivered just over 42,000 vehicles in 2025, representing an 18 percent decline compared to the previous year.
Vehicle delivery drops were more pronounced in the fourth quarter of 2025 due to the expiration of the US tax credit for electric vehicles at the end of Q3.
Volkswagen holds a current stake of 12.3 percent in Rivian, making it the company's second-largest shareholder, with plans to increase its involvement further.
The joint venture between Rivian and Volkswagen for developing vehicle architectures and software systems contributed around $214 million to Rivian's revenue in the third quarter of 2025.
source_title: Rivian’s factory activities point to imminent R2 launch - electrive.com

facts: Tesla was previously the undisputed leader in the EV space while competitors tried to catch up.
BYD is now the world's largest producer of battery-electric and plug-in hybrid vehicles (BEVs and PHEVs).
Rivian is carving out a niche in the premium electric truck and SUV market.
In 2025, BYD sold 4.6 million BEVs and PHEVs, representing a 7.7% increase from the previous year.
BYD vertically integrates by manufacturing many of its own batteries, semiconductors, electric motors, and power electronics.
This vertical integration provides BYD with greater cost control and the ability to respond quickly to demand changes.
BYD is expanding aggressively outside China by opening new manufacturing facilities and increasing exports to Europe, Latin America, and Southeast Asia.
Nearly 16.4 million vehicles were sold in the U.S. last year, making it the world's second-largest automotive market.
Rivian generated its first full-year gross profit in 2025 while narrowing annual losses and improving manufacturing efficiency.
Rivian's revenue reached approximately $5.38 billion in 2025.
Rivian is preparing to launch its lower-priced R2 SUV, which is expected to become the company's highest-volume product.
Rivian expects to deliver between 62,000 and 67,000 vehicles in 2026.
Tesla operates more than 80,000 Superchargers worldwide, running one of the world's largest fast-charging networks.
Tesla's energy generation and storage business is a meaningful revenue contributor, with Megapack battery deployments growing as utilities invest in grid-scale storage.
Tesla is investing heavily in autonomous driving, robotics, artificial intelligence, and manufacturing automation.
Tesla generated $6.2 billion in free cash flow during 2025.
Tesla ended Q1 2026 with more than $44 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments.
Tesla continues to produce more than 1.6 million vehicles annually.
Tesla's margins have compressed due to intensifying pricing competition, and its delivery growth has slowed.
Tesla remains profitable while many EV competitors continue to burn cash.
The EV industry focus has shifted from vehicle volume to building the strongest ecosystem around vehicles.
source_title: Tesla, BYD, or Rivian: Which Stock Currently Has the EV Crown? | The Motley Fool

facts: Rivian Automotive, Inc. is an automotive technology company, which is engaged in developing and manufacturing category-defining electric vehicles (EVs) as well as vertically integrated technologies and services.
The Company's R1 platform consists of two vehicles: the R1T, a two-row five-passenger pickup truck, and the R1S, a three-row seven-passenger sport utility vehicle (SUV).
In the commercial market, the Company offers a Rivian Commercial Vehicle (RCV) platform.
Robert Scaringe serves as Chairman of the Board of Directors, Chief Executive Officer, Founder.

facts: Rivian Automotive is tentatively considering putting a manufacturing plant east of Atlanta in the area of Morgan, Newton and Walton counties, according to the Atlanta Journal-Constitution.
A Rivian spokesperson stated that involvement in these processes does not indicate a final decision.
Rivian is headquartered in Irvine, California.
Rivian has a plant in central Illinois.
Rivian has been searching the country for a second location in order to expand.
An initial public offering last week estimated Rivian’s value to be around $100 billion.
Ford, Amazon, and Cox Enterprises are among its investors.
South Korea’s Kia Corporation has been building gasoline-powered cars and trucks in Georgia for 11 years.
State officials are keen on enticing more automobile producers with tax breaks and other incentives.
source_title: NEWS BRIEF: Electric vehicle factory coming to Georgia? | CL

facts: The state of Georgia and local governments will give Rivian Automotive $1.5 billion of incentives to build a 7,500-job, $5 billion electric vehicle plant east of Atlanta.
Documents outlining the incentive package were signed on Monday.
Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated the package reflects the largest single industrial announcement in Georgia history.
Rivian pledged to reach full investment and job targets by the end of 2028.
The 7,500 jobs will pay an average of $56,000 a year plus benefits.
The state hopes Rivian will anchor an entire electric vehicle industry in Georgia.
Wilson stated it is absolutely appropriate because the project is creating more jobs.
Greg LeRoy, executive director of Good Jobs First, called it by far the largest incentive package Georgia has ever offered to a company.
LeRoy noted it is also the largest incentive package any American state has ever given to an auto plant.
LeRoy stated this is very significant and the biggest auto assembly subsidy package in US history.
Rivian is based in Irvine, California.
Rivian is a startup manufacturer of electric trucks and commercial delivery vans.
The company challenges established automakers like Ford and General Motors as well as electric vehicle leader Tesla.
Rivian is already producing vehicles at its facility in Normal, Illinois.
Rivian hopes to break ground on the Georgia plant as early as summer 2022.
Rivian plans to begin production in Georgia in 2024.
The Georgia plant aims to produce 400,000 vehicles a year.
A company statement said the long-term economic partnership promises to deliver value to Rivian, the people of Georgia and their kids’ kids’ kids.
The project faces fierce local opposition from residents concerned about spoiling their rural quality of life.
The plant site covers 2,000 acres.
The site is located between Social Circle and Rutledge, approximately 45 miles east of downtown Atlanta.
The state took over planning and zoning for the project after opponents overwhelmed Morgan County officials.
Residents voiced concerns about possible well-water contamination, light pollution, and disruption of wildlife and farmland.
A released site plan shows Rivian shifting the plant away from wetlands and agreeing to limit light pollution in response to concerns.
Opposition has become entangled in politics with former U.S. Sen. David Perdue challenging Governor Kemp in the May 24 Republican primary.
Perdue emphasized the role of liberal billionaire George Soros in his opposition.
Soros bought $2 billion worth of Rivian shares around the time the company chose Georgia but owns only 2% of the company.
There is no evidence that Soros influenced the plant location decision.
Perdue stated to think about how many small businesses in Georgia could be helped for this kind of money instead of padding George Soros’ pockets.
Perdue also said Kemp gave away the farm to a woke corporation for something locals do not want, leaving hardworking Georgians footing the bill.
Governor Kemp reiterated his backing for Rivian during a Sunday debate.
Kemp stated he supports 7,500 great-paying jobs going to rural Georgia to an automobile manufacturing facility and will always be for that.
Local governments agreed to $700 million in property tax breaks for the project.
Rivian plans to make more than $300 million in payments in lieu of taxes over 25 years beginning in 2023.
The state will spend $200 million to buy the site, grade it, build road improvements including a new Interstate 20 interchange, and extend utilities.
Georgia will spend $62.5 million to build a dedicated training center for the plant.
Georgia projected spending $27 million on providing job training.
The incentive package includes a $200 million income tax credit calculated at $5,250 per job over five years.
If Rivian does not owe that much state corporate income tax, Georgia will give personal income taxes collected from workers instead.
Sales tax exemptions are estimated to save Rivian $175 million on machinery and $105 million on construction materials.
Kia received more than $450 million in incentives for its plant in West Point, southwest of Atlanta.
Georgia promised SK Innovation $300 million in incentives for a $2.6 billion, 2,600-worker battery plant northeast of Atlanta.
Wilson stated the state has strong protections to claw back money if Rivian falls below 80% of its promised investment or employment.
The state touts a projected $420 million annual payroll from the plant.
An analysis shows there will be 8,000 more jobs created elsewhere in the state with a total economic impact of more than $7 billion.
LeRoy stated the incentives will cost $200,000 per job and that state and local governments will never collect enough increased taxes to cover it.
LeRoy stated the state can never break even on the subsidies.
LeRoy stated there is no way the average worker in this place is going to pay $200,000 more in state and local taxes.
Rivian currently plans two consumer models: the R1T pickup with a base price of $67,500 and the R1S SUV with a base price of $70,000.
Amazon owns 18% of Rivian.
Amazon has ordered 100,000 delivery vans from Rivian, launching the company into commercial vehicles.
Rivian raised $11.9 billion through a stock offering on Nov. 10 to finance the new plant.
Rivian shares have fallen 70% since the stock offering as investors worry about production delays.
source_title: Georgia sets record-breaking incentives for Rivian's electric vehicle plant | FOX 5 Atlanta

facts: The US start-up Rivian is backed by Amazon and Ford among others.
Rivian wants to sell its electric vehicles in Europe from the beginning of 2022.
The company confirmed the European sales launch in an email to a potential European customer.
No further details on the European launch have been given so far.
The confirmation email was reported in the Rivian Owners Forum, among other places.
Rumors suggest the company was looking for a factory location allegedly in the United Kingdom, Hungary, or Germany.
Job advertisements, such as a senior analyst for delivery planning in the EMEA region, pointed to European ambitions.
The exact timing, distribution network, and models for the European launch remain unclear from the customer email, with early 2022 being the only rough timeline.
According to earlier rumors, Rivian could initially only bring the R1S electric SUV to Europe.
The somewhat larger R1T pickup truck is to be offered only in North America for the time being.
Allegedly, the company is planning to develop slightly smaller series with a focus on Europe and China.
Not much is known about the planned smaller series yet.
Rivian is planning to start deliveries of the Launch Edition of its R1T electric pickup in the US this month.
In March, Rivian presented ambitious plans for its own charging networks in the USA and Canada.
It is not yet known whether such charging networks are also planned for Europe.

facts: Rivian produced 13,992 electric vehicles from April to June (Q2).
Q2 production was 4,597 more than in the first quarter.
Rivian increased deliveries from 7,946 to 12,640 vehicles in Q2.
Production increased by 33 percent and deliveries increased by 37 percent compared to the previous quarter.
In Q2 2022, 4,401 Rivian EVs were built at the Normal plant in Illinois.
In Q2 2022, 4,467 Rivian EVs were delivered.
Rivian stated that the new figures remain in line with the company’s expectations.
Rivian is on track to reach its forecast of 50,000 vehicles per year.
Rivian will announce its Q2 financial results on August 8.
To meet the 50,000 vehicle annual target, Rivian must produce an average of 12,500 cars per quarter.
Rivian built 9,395 vehicles in the first quarter, which was below the 12,500 quarterly average needed for the annual target.
The annual target should be well within reach as a young carmaker is expected to increase its production rate during the year.
In early March, Rivian announced a recall of over 12,700 units of the R1S and R1T due to a passenger airbag deployment issue.
The recalled units covered all production until September 15, 2022.
In mid-April, Rivian recalled 5,030 units of the R1S electric SUV.
The reversing light on the recalled R1S vehicles did not comply with specifications and had to be replaced.
Rivian lost billions of dollars in 2022 and must cut costs.
Reports indicated further job cuts at the beginning of February.
Rivian plans to install the Tesla charging port (NACS) in its future electric cars starting from 2025.
The R1 model series and the upcoming R2 platform will feature the NACS charging system from 2025.
Until 2025, Rivian vehicles can use the Tesla Supercharger network with an adapter that the company plans to provide starting in 2024.
When Rivian vehicles have a Tesla charging port from 2025, an adapter will be offered to enable charging at CCS stations.
source_title: Rivian builds nearly 14,000 EVs in Q2 - electrive.com

facts: Sejong Georgia LLC will invest $27.8 million in a LaGrange facility.
The investment will bring 250 jobs to the LaGrange area.
The manufacturer will supply muffler and exhaust systems to the Kia Motors plant under construction in West Point.
Georgia Governor Sonny Perdue stated, "With a great supplier like Sejong on board, Georgia is continuing to build on the success of landing Kia."
The Wulsan, Korea-based company secured a 23-acre site in the LaGrange Industrial Park on Lukken Industrial Drive West.
The city purchased the former Federal Mogul facility, which closed in 2004, and will lease the space to Sejong.
Renovations on the 108,000-square-foot building, funded in part by state grants, are scheduled to begin in July 2008.
Production at the new facility is planned to begin in October 2009.
The city is offering the company a 7-year tax phase-in based on the investment and job creation.
The incentives package includes reduced rent on the building for the first 60 months and an option to buy the site after that time.
City officials and Sejong Georgia President H.S. Kim signed a memorandum of understanding regarding the site.
Diethard Lindner, chairman of the Development Authority of LaGrange, stated, "Having visited Sejong's facilities in Korea and Alabama, I am certain that they will be an excellent community partner."
This facility will be Sejong's second U.S. plant.
The company currently operates a plant in Fort Deposit, Ala., to supply the Hyundai Motors plant in Montgomery.
Other recent Kia supplier announcements for the area include Dongwon Autopart Technology of Korea building a metal-stamping plant in Meriwether County that will add 300 jobs.
Hyundai Mobis announced plans in August to build a plant in West Point.
Pretty Products, DaeLim USA, and Sumika Polymer Compounds America Inc. also recently made announcements regarding supplier locations in the area.

facts: Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Seohan Auto Georgia will create at least 180 new jobs.
Seohan Auto Georgia will invest more than $72 million in a new manufacturing facility.
The new manufacturing facility is located in Liberty County, Georgia.
Seohan Auto Georgia is a developer and manufacturer of automobile parts.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that the Hyundai Metaplant serves as an economic driver for the region, creating jobs in communities like Liberty County.
Governor Brian Kemp welcomed Seohan Auto Georgia as the latest supplier investing in Georgia's fast-emerging EV sector.
Seohan Auto Georgia Corporation operates as a Tier-1 automotive parts supplier for Hyundai Motor Group America.
Seohan Auto Georgia Corporation is headquartered in South Korea.
The company operates production facilities globally, including multiple locations in the United States.
CEO Jung Kee Koo cited sustained EV market growth as driving accelerated changes in the automotive industry.
Jung Kee Koo stated that Seohan Auto Georgia believes Georgia will become the center of the EV industry and a new frontier for the company's future.
Jung Kee Koo expressed pleasure in collaborating with Georgia on the company's expansion.
Jung Kee Koo noted Seohan's history of creating innovative solutions and its determination to advance in the EV industry through its Liberty County project.
Jung Kee Koo expressed confidence that Seohan's experience and technological capabilities will benefit the local community.
Jung Kee Koo thanked Georgia and Liberty County for their continuous support and trust during the project process.
Seohan's new Georgia facility is scheduled to begin production in late 2024.
The facility will be located at the Dunwoody Court Site within the Tradeport East Business Center.
The Tradeport East Business Center is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified site.
The new facility will manufacture H-shafts, axles, and brake systems for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
One of the primary customers for the new facility's output is Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.

Liberty County Development Authority Chairman Allen Brown described Seohan Auto Georgia and its global affiliates as responsible, thorough, and progressive organizations advancing innovative technology.
Allen Brown expressed pleasure at Liberty County being selected as Seohan's location and looked forward to supporting the advanced manufacturing project.
GDEcD Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the state's Global Commerce team on the project.
The project involved partnerships with the Liberty County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that the transition to e-mobility and sustainable technology creates opportunities for forward-looking companies and states.
Pat Wilson noted that automotive industry innovation is advancing rapidly, requiring companies to keep up with new technology and market demand.
Pat Wilson highlighted that communities investing in speed-to-market solutions, such as Liberty County's Tradeport East Business Center, give Georgia a competitive advantage.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility in neighboring Bryan County on October 25.
Offsite suppliers connected to the Hyundai Metaplant project were initially predicted to invest approximately $1 billion across Georgia.
Total announced projects in the region have now exceeded the initial $1 billion supplier investment prediction by nearly $1 billion.
Georgia is positioning itself as a hub for the electric mobility industry by leveraging its existing automotive industry assets.
Since 2020, over 35 EV-related projects in Georgia have announced more than $21 billion in total investment.
These projects have collectively announced the creation of 27,800 jobs in Georgia since 2020.

facts: Georgia totaled record international trade of more than $196 billion in 2022 while racing to become a global electric vehicle hub.

Sewon America announced on Feb. 21 that it is bringing 740 jobs to the town of Rincon (30 minutes northeast of Savannah) with a $300 million plant.
The Sewon America plant will make chassis pieces for electric vehicles, especially those built at Hyundai Motor Group’s Metaplot America in Bryan County.
Seoyon E-Hwa announced on Feb. 1 that it is bringing 740 direct and indirect jobs to Savannah with a $76 million plant.
Seoyon E-Hwa is an American subsidiary of a South Korean company with an existing facility supplying the Kia factory in LaGrange.
The Seoyon E-Hwa plant will make interior items such as door and tailgate trims for Metaplant America.
Currently, 500 of the Seoyon jobs are described as permanent.

The advent of the Sewon facility in Effingham County means that all four counties under the economic development authority for the Savannah region—Bryan, Bulloch, Chatham, and Effingham—have manufacturing projects for Hyundai suppliers.
These Hyundai supplier projects represent $1.8 billion in investments across the four counties.
Construction on the Hyundai Metaplot plant began in early January.
Chatham County Economic Development Authority President and CEO Trip Tollison says the facility’s capacity is expanding from 300,000 to 500,000 vehicles per year.
The capacity increase reflects rising EV demand.
SK Battery America operates two battery plants for Ford and Volkswagen EVs in Commerce.
SK originally committed to hiring 2,600 workers by the end of 2024, but met that goal last year.
SK intends to expand to 3,000 employees this year.

Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson says, “Today’s success is the result of a decades-long vision shared by Georgia leaders since the first international trade offices were opened 50 years ago.”

entity_name: Inflation Reduction Act Energy Tax Credits
facts: A Georgia Republican who has endorsed former President Donald Trump is concerned about the potential repeal of the Inflation Reduction Act, which has brought billions of dollars of clean energy investment to Georgia.

18 House Republicans sent a letter to House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) emphasizing the importance of maintaining energy tax credits in the Inflation Reduction Act if Trump were to be elected in November.
Trump has said he would repeal the Inflation Reduction Act on his first day in office and has called electric vehicle mandates the “death of the U.S. auto industry.”
The act has put more than $352 billion into clean energy across 47 states.
In Georgia, $24 billion has been invested and 30,661 jobs have been created, according to Climate Power, a climate communication organization.
Georgia leads the U.S. in clean energy job creation, according to Climate Power.
Rep. Buddy Carter, of Georgia’s 1st District encompassing Georgia’s coast and inland to Waycross, was one of the 17 co-signers of the letter.
His district has received $7.6 billion, more than a third of the state’s clean energy manufacturing investment.

Rep. Buddy Carter stated: “We must find a balance between rolling back the worst portions of the (Inflation Reduction Act) and ensuring that companies have the stability they need to continue investing in the United States.”
Three major projects in Southeast Georgia include the Hyundai EV Metaplant and LG Metafactory, worth $7.5 billion and bringing 8,500 jobs.

The letter warns that a full repeal of the Inflation Reduction Act would create a worst-case scenario where billions of taxpayer dollars would be spent with next to nothing in return.

facts: Seoyon E-HWA is a global auto parts supplier that manufactures both interior and exterior components.
Seoyon E-HWA will create 740 new direct and indirect jobs.
Seoyon E-HWA will invest almost $76 million in a new manufacturing facility in Chatham County.
Governor Brian Kemp announced the largest economic development project in Georgia history last year.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia is working to ensure its state is the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that projects like this will continue to choose Georgia as the No. 1 state for business and benefit communities in just about every zip code of Georgia.

Seoyon E-Hwa operates 32 corporations in North America, South America, Europe, and Asia.

Seoyon’s LaGrange location will continue to support 630 full time jobs.

Chairman Mr. Ryu Yang-seok committed that Seoyon E-Hwa will cooperate continuously with the local government.
Seoyon’s new facility will be located at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified site.
The new manufacturing facility will produce automotive interior parts such as door trims, headlining, and tailgate trims.
Production is expected to begin in October 2024.
More than two-thirds, or approximately 500, of the announced jobs will be permanent roles in human resources, development, quality, and production personnel.
Savannah Economic Development Authority President and CEO Trip Tollison expressed delight with the announcement that Seoyon E-HWA will be locating in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
Trip Tollison noted the site ideally positions them in close proximity to the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, the Port of Savannah, Interstates I-16 and I-95, and more.

Georgia Ports Authority Executive Director Griff Lynch stated that Seoyon is a valued customer using the Port of Savannah to supply its manufacturing facility in West Georgia.
Griff Lynch expressed excitement to help support Seoyon’s growth in this new location as the Hyundai announcement continues to have positive ripple effects throughout the regional economy.
Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was completed in partnership with the Savannah Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that with its second Georgia facility, Seoyon is committing to nearly doubling the number of jobs it supports in the state.

Pat Wilson stated that Hyundai’s new EV facility is drawing an experienced and dedicated supplier network to the region, adding to the state’s industry expertise and shaping a larger skilled workforce.

facts: SKF announced a strategic investment, together with Stephen Industries and Chalmers Ventures, in startup Anferra AB to tackle hazardous steel grinding sludge.
Grinding sludge is a finely powdered mixture of metal particles, abrasives, and contaminated grinding emulsion generated during metal grinding operations.
Globally, around 12 million tonnes of grinding sludge are produced each year, with most non-recycled volumes handled through incineration or landfill.
Anferra's innovative process converts steel grinding sludge into ferric chloride, a common chemical agent used for wastewater and water treatment.
Another by-product of the process is hydrogen gas, which can be further used as an energy carrier contributing to green energy solutions.
The technology enables a potential net climate benefit of negative 470 kg CO₂-equivalents per tonne of sludge compared to conventional disposal.
This climate benefit equals driving an electric car for 7,500 km per tonne of sludge processed.
The process achieves up to 90% iron recovery with significantly lower energy demand.
The technology has the potential to shift grinding sludge from being treated as hazardous waste towards becoming a secondary raw material.
This shift aligns with EU efforts to harmonise waste regulations and build a stronger market for secondary raw materials.
Mikael Krook, Director of SKF Ventures, stated that Anferra's approach represents a smarter way forward for circularity and decarbonization in the steel and bearing industry.
This investment is the first initiative coming out of SKF Ventures, SKF's initiative to accelerate new innovations.
Ebba Adolfsson, CEO and Co-founder of Anferra, expressed happiness at deepening collaboration with SKF to accelerate industrial implementation globally.
Stephen Industries brings expertise in scaling high-impact deeptech and greentech ventures.
SKF contributes industrial application knowledge, waste stream volume, and decades of experience in sustainability leadership.
Chalmers Ventures provides venture building capabilities and close connection to the research ecosystem for commercialization.
SKF applies circularity principles across its operations, including remanufacturing bearings and circular oil use via RecondOil.
Other SKF initiatives include exploring recycled steel solutions with ultra-low emissions and investigating hydrometallurgical methods to minimize resource loss.
SKF aims to accelerate the transition to a net-zero, more circular industrial ecosystem.
Since 1907, SKF has produced bearings, seals, lubrication systems, condition monitoring solutions, and services to reduce friction and save energy.
SKF is represented in approximately 130 countries with around 17,000 distributor locations worldwide.
SKF's annual sales in 2025 were SEK 91,583 million, with 37,271 employees.
source_title: SKF invests in startup Anferra to transform industry’s toughest waste problems into circular resources - SKF

facts: The Georgia Transportation Alliance is an affiliate of the Georgia Chamber of Commerce.
Joseph Santoro was selected as the new Executive Director of the Georgia Transportation Alliance.
Joseph Santoro starts his new position on December 6.
Joseph Santoro comes to the Georgia Transportation Alliance after a successful four-year tenure with the Council for Quality Growth.
Prior to joining the Georgia Transportation Alliance, Joseph Santoro worked as the Director of Policy & Government Affairs for the Council for Quality Growth.

Over the last four years, Joseph Santoro worked on local, regional, and state policies related to infrastructure planning, legislative initiatives, regulatory issues, land use, transportation, and environmental planning impacting the development and growth industry.
Joseph Santoro has over 10 years of government affairs experience working for membership-based organizations in metro Atlanta evaluating local, state, and federal policy priorities.
Joseph Santoro sets forth recommended pathways to successfully advocate on behalf of Board leadership and membership.
Joseph Santoro earned a B.S. in Public Policy from the Georgia Institute of Technology.
Joseph Santoro has been a Georgia Chamber Government Affairs Committee member since 2014 through various roles.
Joseph Santoro is a 2023 graduate of the Atlanta Regional Commission Regional Leadership Institute.
Joseph Santoro and his wife Kayla live in the City of Atlanta where they recently purchased their first home.
Nick Juliano is Partner at Impact Public Affairs and Chair of the Georgia Transportation Alliance.
Nick Juliano stated that Georgia is at a critical moment for its transportation industry, particularly as it relates to freight and logistics.
Chris Clark is CCE, President and CEO of the Georgia Chamber of Commerce.

Chris Clark noted that Joseph Santoro’s broad experience working with statewide transportation stakeholders and his trusted economic development insight make him the ideal fit to serve as Executive Director of the Georgia Transportation Alliance.

Chris Clark highlighted that with extensive surface transportation networks, two world-class ports, and one of the largest railroad systems in the country, Georgia’s transportation and logistics industry is an essential driver of record-breaking economic success.

The Georgia Transportation Alliance is the state’s only organization focused solely on identifying and promoting long-term transportation solutions specifically connected to economic development and job creation.
The Georgia Transportation Alliance works closely with the Georgia Chamber of Commerce, the Georgia Department of Transportation, and other key stakeholders in Georgia and Washington, D.C.
The goal of the Georgia Transportation Alliance is ensuring an efficient and safe statewide multi-modal transportation system.
