facts: Suhner Manufacturing Inc. has announced the availability of standard and custom flexible shafts for rotary power transmission on various medical, orthopedic, endoscopic and laparoscopic instruments.
In minimally invasive procedures, torsion stable rotary flexible shafts are vital for providing rotary motion on the surgical tool at the focal point of the work being performed.
Suhner flexible shafts offer torsional strength and minimal deflection with a uniformly smooth rotational behavior to ensure proper torsion and tactile feel on the distal end for the surgeon.
These rotary flexible shafts are available with options such as passivation, electropolishing and Teflon coating.
They are provided as cleaned and assembled product to suit the stringent applications of various medical procedures.
Suhner medical grade flexible shafts provide precisely controlled rotary power transmission in a limited space.
On one current application, a Suhner flexible shaft measuring only 0.024 inches in diameter is in use for endoscopic surgeries.

Other applications include endovascular aneurysm/aortic repair (EVAR) and thoracic endovascular (TEVAR) surgery, as well as stapling devices, orthopedic drills, surgical screwdrivers and laparoscopic devices.
Suhner rotary flexible shafts for medical instruments are available in clockwise, counterclockwise and bi-directional motion designs.
Shafts are made from Type 302, 304, 316 and 17-7 stainless steel, as well as high-carbon steel, depending on the application.
Suhner engineering works with OEMs to devise functional solutions for specific instrument, tool and device applications.
Medical grade rotary flexible shafts are available in various diameters from 0.024 to 0.500 inches.
Suhner Manufacturing Inc. is part of the Suhner corporation.
The company was founded in 1976 as a flexible shaft manufacturer to provide for the automobile sector.
The Rome, Ga.-based company operates a production facility with a floor space of 18,300 meters squared.
The facility supplies electrical power tools and abrasives, automation and cutting units.
source_title: Suhner Offers Small Diameter Medical Grade Flexible Shafts | Medical Product Outsourcing

facts: TDK E13 EM series gate drive transformers are designed for automotive 500 V battery systems, enabling reliable isolation in traction inverters and compact DC/DC converters.
TDK offers a Gas-Filled Contactor component for application in the electric vehicle market, solar array installations, and energy stand alone storage systems.
Product training modules provide information on battery charging and discharging circuit architectures within hybrid electric and electric vehicle applications.
source_title: TDK Distributor | DigiKey

facts: TE Connectivity is the world's leading manufacturer of the most innovative and reliable connectivity and sensors solutions on the market today.

TE Connectivity offers an unmatched portfolio of solutions for applications across a wide range of industries such as automotive, industrial equipment, data communication systems, aerospace, defense, medical, oil and gas, energy and consumer electronics.

TE Connectivity's product range includes Connectors, Automotive Connectors, Circular Connectors, D Sub Connectors, RF Connectors, Board-To-Board Connectors, Terminals, Terminal Blocks, Cable Assemblies, Relays, Antennas, Contactors, Potentiometers, Switches, and much more.
source_title: TE CONNECTIVITY distribütör |  Farnell  Türkiye

facts: Georgia leaders joined Delta and LATAM representatives on a trade mission to Chile and Peru to highlight the essential role the two airlines’ networks play in strengthening economic ties between Georgia and South America.
The Delta-LATAM joint venture partnership launched in late 2022, aiming to better connect North and South America and grow trade and tourism relations between the two continents.
Chile and Peru serve as key hubs for unlocking progress for both areas.
Since its initial launch, the alliance has more than doubled capacity from Delta’s Atlanta hub.
The alliance has nearly doubled capacity to LATAM’s South American hubs, unlocking connections and opportunities for customers in more than 300 destinations.
Last fall, the Joint Venture celebrated its one-year anniversary, which brought about 6 additional routes, 15,000 flights and transported more than 3 million customers approximately 56 million miles.
Total trade between the state of Georgia and Chile and Peru was $3.2 billion last year.

Leaders with the Georgia Department of Economic Development, Delta Air Lines and LATAM recently attended a trade mission to South America that spotlighted the essential role the airlines’ robust travel networks play in maintaining Georgia’s position as a global economic powerhouse.

Mary Waters, Chief Administrative Officer for the Georgia Department of Economic Development, stated that conversations with companies during the mission indicate that Georgia’s continued role as a global gateway will only expand for South American markets such as Chile and Peru.

Mary Waters noted that Georgia has a lot to offer Latin American businesses and invited any companies interested in doing business in Georgia to connect with offices in Brazil, Chile, Colombia, or Peru.
The alliance continues to aid in international trade relations, connecting Georgian, Chilean and Peruvian companies alike, particularly within the food and beverage industry.
Chile’s thriving food processing and agriculture sectors strongly align with Georgia’s No. 1 industry – agriculture.

Georgia’s position as a top state for total refrigerated capacity enables the movement of billions of dollars of food, agriculture, and even pharmaceutical products through access points made simpler through this Joint Venture’s efforts.

Alex Antilla, Delta’s Vice President for Latin America, stated that as the joint venture continues to grow, they are thrilled to witness its tremendous impact on enhancing connectivity between North and South America by expanding networks, improving customer experiences, and fostering economic growth.
Delta and LATAM expect to continue expanding on this partnership in the coming months, solidifying the growing trade and travel connection between these two thriving continents.
source_title: Creating connectivity: Georgia leaders spotlight economic opportunities created through Delta-LATAM joint venture – ALA Noticias

facts: Georgia offers HOV and HOT lane exemptions for EVs displaying proper alternative fuel license plates, regardless of passenger count; qualified applicants must prove full registration fee payment, and this exemption expires September 30, 2025.
Georgia Power offers a Time-Of-Use (TOU) rate for residential customers who own an EV and have a smart meter capable of separately measuring EV charger usage.
Georgia Power offers a $200 rebate for residential customers purchasing and installing a Level 2 EV charger; this rebate expires December 31, 2024.

Carroll Electric Membership Cooperative offers a "Drive Free for a Year" program providing a $30 monthly energy credit during the first year of new EV ownership, totaling up to $360 in annual energy savings.
Carroll Electric Membership Cooperative offers a $250 rebate for purchasing and installing a qualified EV charging station.
Snapping Shoals EMC offers a $30 bill credit for 12 months upon purchasing or leasing an electric vehicle, totaling up to $360 in annual energy savings.
Snapping Shoals EMC offers a $250 rebate for purchasing and installing a qualified EV charging station; the charger must be UL-rated, and NEMA 14-50 adapters do not qualify.
Tri-County EMC offers a $30 bill credit for 12 months if an electric vehicle is purchased after January 1, 2020, totaling up to $360 in annual energy savings.
Tri-County EMC offers a $250 rebate for purchasing and installing a NRTL certified Level 2 (240V) EV charging station.
Cobb EMC offers a $250 incentive for purchasing and installing an ENERGY STAR certified level 2 EV charging station.
Cobb EMC offers a Time-of-Use (TOU) rate for residential customers; charging during super off-peak hours with monthly usage below 400 kWh incurs no charges.
Coweta-Fayette EMC offers a $100 rebate for installing a Level 2 (240 volt) NRTL/UL approved electric vehicle charging station.
Coweta-Fayette EMC offers a Time-of-Use (TOU) rate for residential customers.
Diverse Power offers a $50 rebate for each NEMA 14-30 or 14-50 outlet installed to help customers create an EV-ready home.

GreyStone Power Corporation offers a $250 rebate for residential members purchasing and hardwiring a Level 2, UL rated charger at their home; limit is one rebate per household, and NEMA 14-50 adapters & portable chargers do not qualify.
GreyStone Power Corporation offers a Time-of-Use (TOU) rate for residential customers.
Jackson EMC offers a $250 rebate for purchasing a residential Level 2 EV charger.
Jackson EMC offers a Time-of-Use (TOU) rate for residential customers.

Sawnee EMC offers a $200 rebate for installing a residential Level 2 EV charger; qualification requires participation in one of Sawnee EMC's TOU, PEV, or CPPR rate plans for at least one year on the account where the charger is installed, and the charger must be UL or ETL listed.
Sumter EMC offers a $250 rebate for installing a residential Level 2 EV charging station.
City of LaGrange offers a $250 rebate for installing a residential Level 2 EV charging station.
Coastal Electric Cooperative offers a Time-of-Use (TOU) rate for residential customers.
Flint Energies offers a Time-of-Use (TOU) rate for residential customers.
The U.S. federal government offers new EV buyers a tax credit of up to $7,500 depending on the car’s specs and buyer’s income.
Pre-owned EV buyers may qualify for a federal tax credit of up to $4,000.
Homeowners in low-income or rural areas can receive up to $1,000 for installing an EV charger.
All-electric vehicles purchased new in 2023 or after may be eligible for a nonrefundable federal income tax credit of up to $7,500; excess credit cannot be applied to future tax years.
Federal tax credit availability depends on the vehicle's MSRP, final assembly location, battery component/critical minerals sourcing, and taxpayer’s modified adjusted gross income (AGI).

Pre-owned all-electric vehicles purchased in 2023 or after may be eligible for a federal income tax credit equal to 30% of the sale price up to a maximum of $4,000; the credit is nonrefundable and excess cannot be applied to future years.
To qualify for the pre-owned EV credit, the vehicle must be purchased from a dealer, have a sale price of $25,000 or less, and have a model year two years prior to the current calendar year.
Individuals in low-income communities and non-urban areas who purchase qualified residential EV chargers after January 1, 2023, may receive a federal tax credit of up to $1,000.
Qualification for the federal EV charger tax credit requires individuals to be located in low-income community census tracts or non-urban census tracts as defined in IRS Notice 2024-20.
source_title: 2026 Georgia EV Incentives Guide: Rebates, Tax Credits, Grants & More | Green Cars Compare

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group and SK On
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and SK On
facts: Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) and SK On have selected a site in Bartow County, Georgia, for a new electric vehicle (EV) battery manufacturing facility.
The new facility will supply Hyundai Motor Group’s plants in the U.S.
The companies will invest approximately $4-5 billion in the plant.
The project is subject to execution of relevant agreements and final board decisions by HMG and SK On.
The new facility will be located at Bartow Centre, a zoned manufacturing and industrial site located on Highway 411.
The companies are aiming to begin operations in 2025.
source_title: Hyundai Announces New Georgia Battery Manufacturing Plant | THE SHOP

facts: Ascend Elements is a Massachusetts-based battery recycling startup that opened a plant in Covington, Georgia in late March.
The Covington facility is claimed to be the largest electric-vehicle battery recycling facility in North America.
The plant can process 30,000 metric tons of input each year.
This capacity equates to breaking down the battery packs from 70,000 electric vehicles annually, according to Ascend CEO Mike O’Kronley.
The facility breaks down old batteries and preps the most valuable materials inside to be processed and turned into new batteries.
Currently, 80 to 90 percent of what goes into the Covington facility is scrap materials from battery factories.
SK Battery America’s plant in Commerce, Georgia supplies scrap materials to the Covington facility.
Ascend also accepts used consumer electronics from battery-collection programs such as Call2Recycle.
The company pays other companies for their old batteries to keep them from going into landfills.
These deals are mostly with EV or battery makers that have high volumes of spent batteries to get rid of.
Covington sits in the emerging “Battery Belt,” a swath of new battery factories and electric-vehicle plants opening up across the Midwest, Carolinas, Georgia, Tennessee, and Kentucky.
The location was influenced by nearby battery factories sending their scrap down the road for pre-processing.
Scrap and used batteries go through mechanical shredding and sieving to produce “black mass.”
Ascend extracts lithium carbonate from the black mass, while the remaining mass contains materials such as graphite, nickel, cobalt, and manganese.
Ascend currently sells most of these extracted substances to the market.
The company also converts some black mass into cathode precursor and cathode active materials at its Massachusetts R&D center.
Ascend is building a second commercial-scale facility in Hopkinsville, Kentucky.
The Kentucky plant will take outputs from Covington and convert them into cathode precursor and cathode active material for new battery manufacturing.

The Hopkinsville plant is valued at $1 billion and received $480 million in grant funding from the Department of Energy as part of the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law’s investment in domestic supply for critical materials.
The Kentucky location will introduce a technique dubbed “hydro to cathode” for efficiently extracting cathode materials from black mass.

CEO Mike O’Kronley stated that recycling plants will need to be within about an hour’s drive of every new battery gigafactory because EV batteries are classified as Class 9 hazardous materials and should not be long-distance shipped.
Ascend is raising money to build more plants due to the surging popularity of EVs producing more worn-out batteries than current recyclers can process.

Redwood Materials, another battery recycling startup founded by Tesla co-founder JB Straubel, has a Nevada facility processing 40,000 metric tons of input annually, equivalent to around 100,000 battery packs.
Redwood Materials is building a forthcoming $3.5 billion recycling facility in South Carolina.
Redwood Materials won a $2 billion conditional loan from the DOE for its Nevada plant to make new batteries from recycled materials.
Canada-based Li-Cycle received a $375 million conditional DOE loan for its own facility to process lithium carbonate from a network of recycling plants.
Cirba Solutions is expanding a battery-recycling plant in Ohio.
Investment in lithium-ion recycling soared in the last few years to match spiking demand for battery materials, as developing new mines in the U.S. remains extremely hard.
The federal government is lavishing funds on new battery recycling plants, and revamped EV tax credits call for increasing shares of domestically sourced batteries and battery materials.
These facilities tie into the Biden administration’s goal to make the U.S. more capable of supplying itself with batteries pivotal to electrifying transportation and decarbonizing the grid.
source_title: One of the biggest battery recycling plants in the US is up and running | Grist

facts: Hanon Systems will create a new manufacturing facility at Gateway Regional Industrial Park in Bulloch County, Georgia.
Prep work has already started for this plant.
Hanon Systems will employ 160 workers for its $40 million plant.
The company will manufacture heater and AC components for Hyundai’s electric vehicles that will be built at the plant along Interstate 16 in Bryan County.
They’re locating in the Gateway Industry Park that already houses Briggs and Stratton, Walmart Distribution, and more.
Benjy Thompson stated that locating Hanon inside Gateway Industrial Park closer to the city of Statesboro will make it easier for some people who live in the city to get to a job at Hanon.
Thompson says Bulloch County has now announced more than 4,000 jobs associated with the Hyundai plant since its announcement last May.
source_title: Hanon Systems creating 160 jobs for $40 million Hyundai meta plant in Bulloch Co.

facts: Reliance New Energy Battery Limited, a subsidiary of Reliance Industries Limited, secured a 10 gigawatt-hour (GWh) capacity under the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage.
The Ministry of Heavy Industries (MHI) signed a Programme Agreement with the company on February 17, 2025.
The PLI ACC Scheme was approved by the Indian government in May 2021 with a financial outlay of ₹18,100 crore.
The scheme aims to establish a total manufacturing capacity of 50 GWh for advanced chemistry cells essential for electric vehicles and renewable energy storage.
The scheme is technology-agnostic, allowing companies to use different technologies to set up battery manufacturing plants.
The first phase of the scheme in March 2022 awarded 30 GWh capacity to three companies, with agreements signed in July 2022.
With this new allocation to Reliance, the total awarded capacity under the scheme now stands at 40 GWh.
In January 2024, MHI invited bids for the remaining 20 GWh capacity under the scheme.

Seven companies submitted proposals for a total of 70 GWh: ACME Cleantech Solutions, Amara Raja Advanced Cell Technologies, Anvi Power Industries, JSW Neo Energy, Lucas TVS, Reliance Industries, and Waaree Energies.
After evaluation, Reliance emerged as the successful bidder for 10 GWh capacity, while the remaining bidders were placed on a waiting list.
The company has been investing in clean energy and battery storage solutions as part of its broader renewable energy strategy.
The Indian government announced exemptions on 35 additional capital goods related to EV battery manufacturing in the Union Budget for FY2025-26.
Only 10 GWh capacity remains to be allocated under the scheme as India moves closer to achieving its 50 GWh target.
source_title: Reliance Secures 10 GWh Battery Capacity Under PLI Scheme

facts: Cornell University researchers developed a battery recycling method called Direct Electrode-to-Electrode Regeneration (DEER) to recover critical minerals in lithium EV batteries.
The DEER electrochemical bath process restores spent lithium EV batteries to 95 percent of their original power capacity.
The method can cut recycling manufacturing costs by 56 percent compared to traditional approaches.

Vibha Kalra, the Fred H. Rhodes Professor of Chemical Engineering at Cornell's Duffield College of Engineering, stated that the process repairs batteries as-is without shredding or powdering them and puts them back into new batteries.
The dissolution in the chemical solution helps the battery recover its capacity, showing 95 percent recovery and shortening the circularity loop.
Standard industry recycling typically destroys dead cells using extreme-heat furnaces or grinds them into "black mass" drenched in harsh, corrosive acids.
When lithium-ion batteries die, a thick, crusty layer of solid electrolyte interphase builds up inside the cell, blocking energy flow even though the materials remain intact.

Cornell's DEER process involves opening the battery casing, extracting intact electrodes, and submerging them in 1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone to dissolve the insulating buildup while preserving internal structures.
The DEER method cuts down air pollution and slashes industrial water consumption compared to conventional recycling.
The United States currently possesses very few domestic reserves of critical minerals required for modern batteries and depends mostly on complex, foreign supply chains.
The US lacks the massive infrastructure needed to refine raw materials or rebuild crushed battery powder from scratch, causing domestic recycling to lag.
By keeping battery components intact, the DEER method eliminates the need for expensive overseas refabrication and allows the entire recycling process to happen locally, cheaply, and quickly.
The technique currently successfully treats batteries at a 70-80 percent state of health, which represents the typical retirement threshold for electric vehicles.
Researchers plan to test the DEER method on larger, industrial-scale batteries and adapt the process to combat other forms of wear, such as permanent lithium loss.
The study findings were published in the journal Energy and Environmental Science on June 9.
source_title: Spent lithium EV batteries get 95% power back with new chemical bath

canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America LLC
facts: South Korea’s Hyundai Motor broke ground on Hyundai Motor Group’s Metaplant America (HMGMA) LLC’s new manufacturing plant at the Bryan County Mega site in Georgia on October 25.
The USD5.54 billion investment by the company and its affiliate suppliers was announced five months ago.
Plans include producing a diverse range of innovative Hyundai, Genesis and Kia electric vehicles (EVs) and a new battery manufacturing facility.
HMGMA’s new smart factory is expected to begin commercial production in the first half of 2025.

Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung stated: “Today, our EVs are recognized as best in class, and with this partnership, we are determined to be the global leader in electrification, safety, quality, and sustainability. With the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, we will continue to evolve beyond an automaker to the world’s leading mobility solutions provider.”
The Group has a global target to sell 3.23 million full electric vehicles annually by 2030.
To realize this goal, the Group plans to establish a global EV production network that will ensure a stable supply of EVs around the globe.
With additional EV and battery production capabilities in the U.S., the Group aims to maintain its position as one of the top three EV providers in the U.S.
The Group plans to lead the EV market not just in sales, but also in terms of design, technologies, and mobility solutions optimized for the electric mobility era.
The investment is in line with the U.S. government’s roadmap to accelerate electrification, as global OEMs are announcing plans to expand their local EV production in the U.S.
Last year, the U.S. government announced a new initiative under which sales of zero-emission vehicles should account for at least half of OEM’s new vehicle sales by 2030.
The new plant will boast a highly connected, automated, and flexible manufacturing system, which organically connects all elements of the EV ecosystem.

The Georgia facility will become an intelligent manufacturing plant where all processes of production—order collection, procurement, logistics and production—will be optimized utilizing artificial intelligence (AI) and data.
The innovative manufacturing system will also help create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers.

As part of the Group’s commitment to sustainability, the plant will primarily rely on renewable energy sources to power the facility and use emission-reduction technologies to meet the RE100 requirements.
HMGMA will create more than 8,100 jobs over the next few years as the company aims to establish a stable supply chain for EV batteries and other EV components in the U.S. market.
Approximately USD1 billion is anticipated to be invested by non-affiliated HMGMA suppliers in connection with the project in other locations.

The celebration included a morning groundbreaking on site, followed by a future technology expo at Enmarket Arena in Savannah, where the company highlighted “advanced technology and extraordinary human achievement” with a wide array of exhibits for hundreds of attendees from the community in attendance.
Chung, along with Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp, and company officials, and state and local leaders, presided over the groundbreaking.
source_title: Hyundai Motor breaks ground on USD5.5 billion EV plant in U.S. - F&L Asia

facts: Hyundai Motor Company announced last year that it will make its XCIENT hydrogen fuel cell tractor available in the U.S. market in 2024 and is delivering on that promise with three weeks left in the year.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), in cooperation with 3PL Glovis America, will deploy 21 XCIENT heavy-duty hydrogen fuel-cell electric trucks for clean logistics operations.
HMGMA is located in Bryan County, Georgia, and is Hyundai Motor Group's first dedicated electric vehicle mass-production plant.
The trucks will transport vehicle parts from HMGMA suppliers across the region to the Megasite daily.
During the initial rollout, XCIENT trucks will transport parts between suppliers and the on-site Consolidation Center before eventually expanding to a broader network of suppliers.
The 21 XCIENT vehicles will represent more than one-third of the Glovis America truck fleet at HMGMA.

Hyundai Motor Group earlier this year announced HTWO, its new hydrogen value chain business brand encompassing the group's businesses and affiliates that enable each stage of the hydrogen value chain, from production and storage to transportation and utilization.

Jim Park, senior vice president of commercial vehicle and hydrogen business development for Hyundai Motor North America, stated that HMGMA's deployment of XCIENT for logistics and operations is one of the main initiatives of HTWO.
The deployment is delivering on Hyundai's strategy and vision to showcase a closed-loop hydrogen logistics ecosystem and improve the environmental sustainability of manufacturing operations.
HTWO Logistics, a joint venture between Hyundai Motor Company and Glovis America, will also install a mobile hydrogen refueling station at the Megasite for refueling.
A hydrogen production and refueling station is being developed for the Megasite.
source_title: Hyundai XCIENT fuel cell trucks hit the road in the U.S. | Commercial Carrier Journal

facts: On Aug. 5, SELC filed a petition on behalf of Concerned Citizens of Cook County with the Georgia Office of State Administrative Hearings to appeal an air quality permit for Spectrum's wood pellet facility in Adel, GA.
The proposed facility is located adjacent to a predominantly Black and Hispanic neighborhood in west Adel.
Wood pellet plants release air particles that create air quality problems and are linked to serious health issues, like asthma and heart disease.
EPD’s issuance of the permit violates the Clean Air Act and threatens the health and welfare of local residents who have voiced concerns.
EPD’s decision circumvents EPA non-discrimination goals by allowing construction before testing.
In 2021, EPD issued a permit to Renewable Biomass Group for a facility slated to produce 450,000 metric tons per year of industrial wood pellets.

Dr. Treva Gear, founding member of 4C, stated: 'Our community deserves better. Economic progress should not equate to our Black, Hispanic, and impoverished neighborhoods being treated as a sacrifice zone for repeated environmental injustices.'
Chandra Taylor-Sawyer, leader of SELC’s Environmental Justice Initiative, stated: 'EPD has a golden opportunity to do right by those living near Spectrum’s proposed plant and revoke the permit.'
The petition was amended on September 7, resulting in a stay until the Georgia Office of State Administrative Hearings details next steps.
On Jan. 28, 2021, EPD denied environmental groups' request to comment on a draft permit for Renewable Biomass Group's new wood pellet plant in Adel.
In Oct. 2021, Spectrum submitted a permit application for a facility with Phase 1 capacity of 600,000 tons per year and Phase 2 capacity of 1,320,000 tons per year.
At full capacity, Spectrum would become the largest wood pellet manufacturing facility in the world.
On Nov. 19, 2021, 4C and 14 other public interest organizations submitted comments opposing the application due to incomplete information.
In Dec. 2021, Spectrum submitted a revised permit application.
On Mar. 28, 2022, EPD solicited public comments on Spectrum's revised draft permit.
On May 2, 2022, 4C and 13 other public interest organizations submitted comments citing insufficient air pollution controls.
On Jul. 8, 2022, the permit was approved for two phases of construction and operation without notifying the 14 commenting groups or providing a hearing deadline.
Concerned Citizens of Cook County (4C) is a frontline community organization based in Adel, GA focused on equity and environmental justice.

links.context: Concerned Citizens of Cook County website

source_title: Local group opposes permit for one of the world’s largest wood pellet plants in predominantly Black and Hispanic neighborhood - Southern Environmental Law Center

facts: Ascend Elements, a Massachusetts-based battery recycling startup, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection.
The company had raised more than $500 million prior to its bankruptcy filing.
A critical Department of Energy grant that Ascend Elements was counting on to fund its commercial-scale operations was canceled.
Lithium carbonate prices peaked above $80,000 per ton in late 2022 but now trade around $12,000.
Nickel and cobalt battery material prices followed a similar downward trajectory over the past two years.
Honda was a strategic investor in Ascend Elements.
South Korean battery giant SK On was a strategic investor in Ascend Elements.
Ascend Elements developed a proprietary "Hydro-to-Cathode" recycling process.
Construction had begun on Ascend Elements' planned battery recycling facility in Hopkinsville, Kentucky.
The Hopkinsville facility site spans 140 acres.
The Hopkinsville plant was projected to process enough batteries annually to recover materials for 250,000 electric vehicles.
The Hopkinsville project was supposed to create 400 jobs in the region.
Local officials in Hopkinsville celebrated Ascend Elements' arrival as a clean energy investment that would anchor their economic future.
The Inflation Reduction Act provided billions for domestic battery manufacturing, though fund deployment has been slower and more selective than anticipated.
Ascend Elements' recycling process could recover up to 98% of critical materials from spent lithium-ion batteries.
Testing showed that Ascend Elements' recycled cathode material performed identically to virgin material.
Global EV sales growth slowed from the breakneck pace seen between 2020 and 2022.
China has flooded global markets with cheap battery materials, leveraging its control of processing capacity to undercut Western competitors.
Battery giants CATL and LG Energy Solution might see value in acquiring Ascend Elements' technology and partial facilities at bankruptcy prices.
Redwood Materials, founded by former Tesla CTO JB Straubel, has raised over $2 billion and is better capitalized to weather the battery recycling downturn.
Li-Cycle had to dramatically scale back expansion plans in 2023 due to its own financial challenges.
The battery recycling sector is currently overcrowded relative to current demand for recycled materials.
Ascend Elements' bankruptcy filing will give the company breathing room to restructure debt and potentially find new investors or acquirers.
source_title: Battery Recycler Ascend Elements Files for Bankruptcy | The Tech Buzz

canonical_name: Department of Energy Manufacturing Grant Cancellations
facts: The Department of Energy confirmed it is canceling $720 million worth of manufacturing awards.
The grant cuts affect companies that manufacture battery materials, recycle lithium-ion batteries, and produce super-insulating windows.
All grant money was authorized by Congress as part of the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law passed in 2021.
The bulk of the grant money was awarded in 2023 and 2024.
Energy Secretary Chris Wright has been reviewing contracts made during the Biden administration.
DOE spokesperson Ben Dietderich stated that the canceled projects “missed milestones” and “did not adequately advance the nation’s energy needs.”

Ascend Elements was awarded $316 million in October 2022 toward a $1 billion facility in Kentucky to refine recycling technology for manufacturing waste and end-of-life batteries into lithium-ion battery materials.
Federal government records show that $206 million has already been disbursed to Ascend Elements.
Ascend Elements stated it is proceeding with its plans using alternative funding sources to cover any shortfall from the grant cancellation.
Anovion was awarded $117 million to reshore technology for producing synthetic graphite for lithium-ion battery anodes.
According to Benchmark Mineral Intelligence, Chinese suppliers control 75% of the synthetic graphite supply chain and produce 97% of all synthetic graphite anodes.
Anovion’s plant is slated to be built in Alabama, with only $13.8 million disbursed to date according to a federal database.
LuxWall was awarded $31.7 million in November 2023 by the DOE to build a factory on the site of an old coal plant near Detroit for manufacturing building-insulating windows.
Only $1 million has been sent to LuxWall according to government records, though the company opened the first phase of its factory in August 2024.
The canceled grants were intended to help startups transition from technology development to commercial deployment by bridging the “valley of death” financing gap.

Government grants like these were designed to encourage private investors to contribute capital, with successful first-of-a-kind factories serving as templates to bolster the country’s manufacturing base.
source_title: Trump DOE confirms it’s canceling over $700M in manufacturing grants - battery material companies include Ascend Elements and Anovion - Batteries News

facts: The NEVI program provides $7.5 billion to build the backbone of America’s EV charging network along highways and in communities.
Five billion dollars of funding is allocated across all 50 states and Puerto Rico to build charging along highways.
Two and one-half billion dollars is available as competitive grants to build out charging in communities and to address gaps in highway charging.
NEVI was created through the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law in 2021.
The program operates on a five-year timeline, with the page noting it is currently in its third year.
EV chargers funded by NEVI are already operational in eight states.
Deploying EV charging infrastructure requires contracting, planning, design and engineering, permitting, procurement of equipment, and installation.
A custom map tracks announced and energized NEVI stations funded through the $5 billion formula program, excluding sites supported by competitive funding.
source_title: NEVI EV Charger Tracker - Plug In America

canonical_name: Georgia Department of Transportation National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Program
facts: The Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT) is implementing the National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) program in the state.
This program aims to establish a network of electric vehicle charging stations along designated corridors.
Round 1 had an open period from July 25, 2023, to November 27, 2023.
A Request for Qualified Companies for Round 2 was open through July 1, 2024.
The Round 2 Request for Proposals (RFP) opening date is July 1, 2024.
The Round 2 RFP closing date is September 30, 2024.
The rebate program is currently out of funding.
Round 2 Request for Proposals is open from July 1, 2024, to September 30, 2024.
The program covers state-wide geography.
The rebate type is custom.
Rebate amounts cover up to 80% of project cost.
There are 33 charging sites selected through a competitive bid process.
Chargers must be located within one travel mile of designated EV corridors.
Charging stations must be spaced no more than 50 miles apart.
Charger ports must be compatible with all electric vehicles sold in the United States.
Projects must provide information on publicly available electric vehicle charging infrastructure.
All projects must follow Buy America standards.
Each proposal must include at least four 150kW capable DC Fast Chargers with CCS ports.
Proposals must meet all federal NEVI guidelines.
A map of eligible locations is available through the GDOT NEVI Program Map.
The rebate program officially closed on October 7, 2024.
The Round 2 RFP opened on July 9, 2024, with a closing date of September 30, 2024.
The Request for Qualified Companies for Round 2 opened on May 9, 2024.
The GA NEVI Program is part of a larger national initiative to create robust electric vehicle charging infrastructure across the United States.
By strategically placing charging stations along key corridors, the program aims to reduce range anxiety and promote the adoption of electric vehicles.
Applicants are required to carefully review all federal NEVI guidelines and Georgia-specific requirements before submitting proposals.
The competitive bidding process ensures that the most qualified and cost-effective projects will be selected to create a comprehensive charging network across the state.
source_title: GA NEVI Program from Georgia DOT

facts: A Georgia judge rejected an agreement that would have provided a huge property tax break to Rivian Automotive.
The ruling clouds the upstart electric truck maker’s plans to build a plant east of Atlanta.
Morgan County Superior Court Judge Brenda Trammell rejected a routine request by a local government to validate a bond agreement.
The judge ruled that the development authority hadn’t proved that the $5 billion plant, projected to hire 7,500 people, was “sound, reasonable and feasible” as required under state law.
Trammell ruled that Rivian should be required to pay regular property taxes because of its level of control over property it would be leasing from the development authority.
Rivian declined to comment on the ruling.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development and a local four-county joint development authority that recruited Rivian expressed disappointment and respectfully disagreed with Judge Trammell’s decision.
They stated they aren’t giving up on their plans and are considering an appeal.
The groups said they remain undeterred in efforts to bring high-paying, American manufacturing jobs to Georgia and are currently assessing all legal options.
The Irvine, California-based electric vehicle manufacturer announced last year that it would build the facility on a 2,000-acre (800-hectare) site in Morgan and Walton counties.
The site is located about 45 miles (70 kilometers) east of Atlanta along Interstate 20.
Rivian plans to produce up to 400,000 vehicles a year at the facility.
Rivian also has a plant in Normal, Illinois.
The company had hoped to break ground as early as this summer and begin production in 2024.
By maintaining ownership of the property and leasing it to Rivian, local governments would have exempted Rivian from a projected $700 million in property taxes over 25 years.
Rivian agreed to make $300 million in payments in lieu of taxes during that period.
The property tax break is a key part of the $1.2 billion in tax breaks and incentives that Georgia and local officials offered for Rivian to build a plant in the state.
The long-used maneuver circumvents a ban in Georgia’s state constitution on giving “gratuities” to companies or individuals.
If Trammell’s order requiring normal property taxes is upheld, it could call other big tax breaks into question and keep officials from using the tool in the future.
The state plans to spend $200 million to buy the site and prepare it.
Rivian could claim a projected $200 million income tax credit.
Rivian could claim $280 million in sales tax breaks on machinery and construction materials.
The state plans to spend $90 million to build a job training center and train workers.

The judge found persuasive the arguments of a group of local residents who oppose development of the plant, saying it will spoil their quality of life in a rural area that Atlanta’s sprawl is now encroaching upon.

JoEllen Artz, president of opposition group Morgan Land, Sky & Water Preservation, said it was very fulfilling that local citizens were able to band together to do research and bring a legal team on board to deliver fantastic results.
Artz and other opponents intervened in the lawsuit to question the appropriateness of the tax break deal proposed by the four-county joint development authority.
The company has encountered difficulties in ramping up production in Illinois.
Rivian’s once soaring stock price has tumbled with some key investors dumping shares.
Trammell wrote that local and state officials appeared not to have considered the higher costs of services that local governments would incur.
The judge questioned whether Rivian had the money to complete the project.
Trammell wrote that “Rivian’s cash reserves are quickly drying up, thus casting serious doubt on whether it will be able to commence, let alone complete, the project.”
source_title: Rivian plant in Georgia loses property tax break after judge's ruling | Fortune

facts: Rivian Automotive announced it is pausing construction of its $5 billion electric truck manufacturing plant in Georgia to speed production and save money.
California-based Rivian had planned to start building its new R2 midsize SUVs at the Georgia site located east of Atlanta.
State and local governments offered an incentive package as large as $1.5 billion, one of the largest ever offered for an American auto plant.
CEO RJ Scaringe stated that production of the R2 will instead begin at Rivian's existing plant in Normal, Illinois.
The shift in production location would allow Rivian to get the R2 to market more quickly, sometime in 2026.
The move would save $2.25 billion in capital spending for now, according to a company news release.
Rivian has been burning through accumulated cash quickly while failing to meet production targets at its Illinois plant.
CEO RJ Scaringe stated that the Georgia site remains core to scaling across the R2, R3, and R3X vehicles and expressed appreciation for the partnership there.
Rivian did not provide a timetable for restarting work on the Georgia plant, stating only that resuming construction is expected later.
Rivian’s share price closed at $12.51, up 13%, after announcing new R3 and R3X crossover models alongside the R2.

On its first day of trading in 2021, Rivian shares closed at $100.73, giving the company a total stock market valuation of almost $86 billion, which was bigger than Ford and slightly lower than General Motors at the time.
The site near Social Circle was expected to eventually hire 7,500 workers and produce up to 200,000 vehicles upon completion of its first phase later this year.
A second planned phase would boost capacity for an additional 200,000 vehicles per year by 2030.
State and local governments were projected to spend more than $125 million to purchase the nearly 2,000-acre (810-hectare) site near Social Circle, clear trees, and grade land.
The state has finished purchasing and preparing the site, turning it over to Rivian.
The state has also completed most of a pledged $50 million in roadwork.
Signs for Rivian Parkway at a new traffic signal on U.S. 278 were removed on Thursday.
Rivian currently produces its R1T pickup truck, R1S SUV, and electric delivery vans for commercial users at its Illinois factory.
The R1 vehicles currently cost $70,000 or more.
The original plan was to produce R2 vehicles in Georgia with lower price tags aimed at the mass market.
The first phase of Rivian’s Georgia factory is supposed to make 200,000 vehicles a year, with a second phase capable of making another 200,000 a year.
The company stated last year that the first phase would start production this year.
State and local governments offered Rivian an incentive package worth an estimated $1.5 billion.
The deadline for Rivian to complete its investment and hiring under the incentive deal was extended to 2030.
Rivian cited a slowed pace of capital investment and legal challenges mounted by neighbors opposing development as reasons for the pause.
If the plant is not ultimately built, it would dent Governor Brian Kemp’s goal of making Georgia a center of the electric vehicle industry.
The construction pause contrasts with rapid construction at Hyundai Motor Group’s $7.6 billion electric vehicle and battery complex near Savannah.
Hyundai’s plant in Ellabell, announced in 2022, could grow to 8,500 employees.
Hyundai recently stated it now aims to begin production later this year instead of in 2025.
Governor Kemp’s office declined comment and referred reporters to the Georgia Department of Economic Development.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development stated that Rivian has restated its commitment to Georgia and that state and local economic development officials remain in steady communication with Rivian regarding its manufacturing plans.
JoEllen Artz of Morgan Land, Sky & Water Preservation, a group backing legal battles opposing the Rivian project, said she hopes the pause means the plant will never be built.
Artz stated she is happy with at least a pause in construction.
Since the state cut down trees and compacted a building pad for the plant, drainage problems have worsened on the site, causing water to stand after heavy rains.
Artz argued that the state and county should have consulted with local residents and studied the site before pushing through plans for Rivian.
Artz questioned whether electric vehicle demand justifies the plant and said Kemp should have pulled the plug.
Artz stated there were many times Kemp could have backed out gracefully and admitted the location was not suitable.
source_title: Automaker Rivian pauses construction of its $5 billion electric truck plant in Georgia

facts: Rivian announced it has expanded its expectations for how many electric vehicles it will deliver to customers this year.
The California-based automaker is in the process of building an EV factory an hour east of Atlanta.
Rivian increased its 2026 delivery outlook by 3,000 vehicles.
Rivian's updated delivery guidance for the year is 65,000 to 70,000 vehicles.
The increase in delivery guidance was prompted by a busier-than-anticipated second quarter bolstered by the launch of a new midsize SUV called R2.
Initial R2s are being built at the company’s sole plant in Illinois.
Rivian plans to expand production capacity at its future Georgia plant.
The success of the R2 is critical to Rivian’s future.
The company is securing $5.1 billion in tax incentives for its commitment to build a plant in the Georgia I-20 corridor.
Rivian is facing a looming chip shortage that could disrupt its supply chain and production.
source_title: Despite looming chip shortage, Rivian increases delivery outlook after R2 launch

facts: SK Battery America operates a manufacturing facility in Commerce, Georgia.
The South Korean battery supplier laid off 958 employees at its Commerce plant.
The layoffs represent about 37% of the workforce at the $2.6 billion plant that opened in 2022.
The facility will continue operating with roughly 1,600 workers.
The plant was a key supplier for the Ford F-150 Lightning.
Ford canceled the fully electric version of the pickup in favor of an extended-range alternative.
Ford CEO Jim Farley warned late last year that EV demand could slow due to the removal of EV credits and other factors.
EV demand is growing but far more slowly than manufacturers once projected.
Changing federal policy and the removal of consumer EV incentives have made the market environment less predictable for battery makers and automakers.
Consumer enthusiasm for EVs is softening compared with aggressive expectations set earlier in the decade.
Range anxiety, charging infrastructure limitations, and higher upfront costs remain key concerns for buyers.
EVs accounted for roughly 8% of U.S. new vehicle sales in 2025, essentially unchanged from the previous year.
Slower demand directly affects production volumes for suppliers like SK Battery America.
Battery plants were built to support aggressive EV rollouts that many automakers have now delayed.
Parts of the supply chain are temporarily oversized for current demand.
Some manufacturers have reduced their raw material commitments amid revised EV production plans.
Ford recently scaled back a lithium supply agreement.
Several major automakers have collectively written off more than $55 billion after overestimating near-term EV demand.
The write-downs reflect canceled projects, delayed factories, and investments that will take longer than expected to pay off.
A number of planned EV models ranging from mainstream crossovers to luxury performance models have been scrapped entirely before reaching production.
Companies are redirecting resources toward hybrids and plug-in hybrids as electric vehicles disappear from product roadmaps.
Some analysts argue the industry is transitioning into a more realistic growth phase after years of overly optimistic projections.
The market is adjusting to realities of consumer hesitation, infrastructure limitations, and financial challenges associated with scaling new technologies.
Rising geopolitical tensions and the possibility of disruptions to global oil supply have raised concerns about future fuel prices.
If gasoline costs spike again, electric vehicles could regain momentum faster than expected.

links.context: Add Autoblog as a Preferred Source

source_title: EV Boom Hits Reality as Battery Plant Cuts Nearly 1,000 Jobs - AOL

facts: Governor Brian Kemp and First Lady Marty Kemp joined President and Global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and President and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America José Muñoz to celebrate Hyundai Motor Group’s historic impact on Georgia and provide an update on Metaplant America progress.
The Metaplant America facility will officially start vehicle production in the fourth quarter of this year.
Governor Brian Kemp stated Hyundai Motor Group has been an incredible partner to Georgia for decades and highlighted the historic benefits the project brings to Georgians.
Governor Kemp noted Hyundai helps recruit businesses, gives back to communities, and partners with world-class colleges and universities to connect Georgians with opportunities.

Hyundai and Genesis electric vehicles on display included the IONIQ 5, IONIQ 6, GV60, Electrified GV70, and the hydrogen-powered XCIENT Fuel Cell Truck used for logistics between Hyundai’s manufacturing facilities and suppliers.
Governor Kemp officially declared February 26 as Hyundai Day throughout the state of Georgia.
The Georgia General Assembly passed a Resolution commending Hyundai Motor Group’s dedication to the State of Georgia and recognizing February 26 as Hyundai Day at the State Capitol.
José Muñoz stated Hyundai’s investments in Georgia will create tens of thousands of great paying American jobs and bring tens of billions of dollars in economic impact for decades to come.
Since the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America project was announced in May 2022, LG Energy Solution was named as the joint venture partner in the manufacture of battery components.
An additional expansion of the LG Energy Solution battery facility was announced.
Hyundai and SK On are building an EV battery manufacturing facility in Bartow County that will supply Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. plants.
Since the initial announcement of HMGMA’s location, Hyundai Motor Group’s planned investment in Georgia has reached more than $12.6 billion.
The project brings with it the creation of 12,000 direct, long-term jobs.

Lieutenant Governor Burt Jones and House Speaker Jon Burns joined legislative leaders, GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson, local elected officials from the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, and company representatives at the celebration.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones applauded Governor Brian Kemp, state agencies, and the University System of Georgia (especially Georgia Tech) for their partnership with Hyundai.
Lt. Governor Jones stated partnerships foster a thriving environment to grow Georgia's businesses and workforce.
Up to 300,000 Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia EVs are expected to be produced annually at the Metaplant.
17 HMGMA suppliers have announced projects across 10 Georgia counties spanning beyond southeast Georgia.
Further supplier announcements are expected.
Supplier facilities are expected to create more than 6,700 new jobs and more than $2.5 billion in investment.
Supplier facilities will be operational prior to the opening of HMGMA.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns credited Governor Kemp and the General Assembly for sound policy direction supporting Hyundai's Southeast Georgia investment.
Speaker Burns stated Hyundai's Metaplant will have a generational impact as a force multiplier for investments in education, healthcare, housing, and transportation.
The nearly 3,000-acre Metaplant site has four building exteriors complete with outfitting for internal operations underway.
The Metaplant’s architecture incorporates technologically advanced elements plus design features representing the Savannah area, Hyundai’s brand identity, nature, and clean energy.
HMGMA is hiring full-time employees, known as Meta Pros, at a rapid pace for training on machinery, office/clerical work, and other roles.
HMGMA currently has 566 full-time employees.
Approximately 75 percent of the Meta Pro workforce is from the Greater Savannah area.
An additional 37 employees are from Georgia but outside the local area.
Georgia Quick Start broke ground on its customized training center at the Metaplant site on February 8.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Georgians will be trained in high-skill careers as Meta Pros at one of the most advanced vehicle assembly and battery plants in the world.
HMGMA partnered with local technical colleges to prepare students for entry-level employment at the Metaplant.

Electrical Vehicle Professional Technical Certificate of Credit (EVP TCC) training is available at Coastal Pines Technical College’s Baxley and Waycross campuses, Southeastern Technical College’s Vidalia campus, Savannah Technical College, and Ogeechee Technical College.
TCSG Commissioner Greg Dozier stated technical colleges are providing comprehensive training programs tailored to the growing EV industry in Georgia.

In September 2023, Georgia Institute of Technology (Georgia Tech) and Hyundai Motor Company signed a memorandum of understanding to collaborate on research for sustainable mobility, including developing a hydrogen economy, workforce development, and smart city initiatives.
U.S. News & World Report ranked Georgia Tech as the top public university and No. 3 nationally in energy and fuel research in February 2024.

Georgia Tech President Ángel Cabrera stated the strategic partnership with Hyundai is one of their most significant corporate relationships and aims to advance science for emerging technologies and produce the next-generation workforce.
Since 1998, Hyundai has directed over $6 million towards local Georgia communities through philanthropic programs and partnerships.
Hyundai is promoting STEM programs in local K-12 schools.
The company hosts educational programs on alternative energy sources.
Hyundai supports local organizations dedicated to affordable homeownership.
The company focuses on environmental stewardship while championing the regional community.

Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority’s Trip Tollison stated Hyundai has given more than $1 million to regional nonprofits and formed key partnerships with local educational institutions.
Trip Tollison cited a commitment of $7.59 billion in capital investment in Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Trip Tollison stated the project will create 8,500 jobs.
More than 550 Meta Pros have already been hired, with 75 percent coming from the Savannah region.
Beginning with mass production, the electricity used to power the Metaplant will come from 100 percent clean power sources.
This aligns with Hyundai’s commitment to carbon neutrality by 2045.
A 41-acre park will be featured in front of the plant, including trails, running tracks, environmental art displays, sports fields, and other amenities for employee use.
source_title: State Leaders Celebrate Hyundai Motor Group’s Historic Impact on Georgia - Athens CEO

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) is a multi-billion-dollar electric vehicle and battery manufacturing complex located in Bryan County.
The HMGMA facility is approximately 20 miles from downtown Savannah and roughly equidistant from Richmond Hill.
The project represents a multi-billion-dollar capital investment by Hyundai Motor Group.
Associated supplier facilities and component manufacturers are locating within the broader Joint Development Authority footprint, which spans Bryan, Bulloch, Chatham, and Effingham Counties.
The development is generating thousands of jobs, hundreds of supplier and contractor positions, and a sustained relocation wave that is reshaping the rental economy across Richmond Hill and Pooler.
Direct Hyundai employees and contractors are relocating from Hyundai's existing U.S. and international operations to the area.
Relocating direct Hyundai employees and contractors typically require 60-to-180-day furnished housing while they buy or build permanent homes in the area.
Supplier-company personnel, including engineers, plant management, and skilled trades from dozens of supplier and component manufacturers, are making 30-to-90-day project assignments.

Construction and ramp-up contractors working on the ongoing facility build-out, supplier facility construction, and broader infrastructure projects are typically on 30- to 60-day assignments tied to specific project phases.
Temporary management and consulting personnel embedded in operations during ramp-up phases are typically staying for 14-to-45 days, often midweek-only with weekend departures home.
Pre-relocation house-hunters and exploratory visitors are flying in for one-to-two-week stays to scout neighborhoods, schools, and properties before relocating.
The pre-relocation house-hunter segment is growing as the workforce build-out continues.
The Metaplant-related demand is structurally less seasonal and less weekend-concentrated than leisure travel.
Metaplant-related workers are willing to pay rates that compete with or exceed leisure-traveler pricing benchmarks for the same property.
A two-bedroom Richmond Hill townhome grossing $35,000 per year on leisure-vacation bookings can gross $40,000 to $55,000 per year on a properly-positioned extended-stay mix tied to the Metaplant.
The HMGMA development is described as the single largest economic event in coastal Georgia in decades.
The Bryan County Development Authority and the Savannah Joint Development Authority publish updates on Metaplant-related employment, supplier location announcements, and broader economic development.
Track these sources for shifts in workforce size, supplier ramp-up, and project-phase changes that affect demand patterns.
The Bryan County Chamber of Commerce and the Richmond Hill Chamber serve as additional sources for local business and relocation context.
source_title: How to Market a Short-Term Rental in Richmond Hill, GA

facts: GDEcD's Global Commerce team claims involvement in 429 facility expansions and new locations during Fiscal Year 2024.
These facility expansions and new locations translate into an investment north of $20 billion across Georgia.
The investments created approximately 26,900 new private sector jobs statewide.
A substantial 83% of the expansions and new ventures are located beyond the 10-county Atlanta area.
Rural areas outside Atlanta generated over 18,300 jobs and more than $12.5 billion in investment.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated: “For years, we've worked closely with local and state partners all across Georgia to bring historic job creation to our communities, especially in rural areas.”
Metro Atlanta cemented its status as a logistic and business heavyweight with nearly $7.8 billion in investments.
New state locations were tied to investments reaching $12.8 billion.
Expansions brought forth 9,800 new jobs, according to Lt. Governor Burt Jones.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones emphasized the importance of a skilled workforce to support business growth and praised educational institutions and public-private partnerships for preparing the workforce.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns reflected on the legislature's collaboration with Gov. Kemp to sustain Georgia’s economic stride.

Jon Burns stated: "From cutting taxes and burdensome regulations to making historic investments in our education system, workforce development and infrastructure, we have delivered real results for the people of Georgia—and we will continue to do just that in the months and years ahead."
Manufacturing took the lead among industry sectors receiving boosts in FY2024.
Kia Georgia launched the EV9 vehicle model during FY2024.
Companies making investments in Georgia include Toyota, StandardAero, and Gerresheimer.
Investments topped $1.3 billion in the food processing and agribusiness sector.
International companies Yakult and CJ Foodville opted for Georgia to prop up their East Coast operations.
The technology and corporate hub sphere experienced growth, adding at least 4,400 new jobs.
Gov. Kemp and the state conducted economic development missions to Korea and Switzerland to tighten bonds with key investors.
GDEcD received a Gold Shovel award from Area Development magazine.
GDEcD received a top ranking for EV investments in Business Facilities magazine.
source_title: Georgia Celebrates Fiscal Year 2024 Business Boom, $20 Billion

facts: Investment promotion approvals in Thailand's EV industry reached 198 projects worth more than 137 billion baht as of May 2026.
Approved projects cover the full EV ecosystem, including electric vehicle production, batteries, key components, charging stations, and battery-swapping facilities.
Narit Therdsteerasukdi serves as secretary-general of the Board of Investment (BOI) and secretary of the National Electric Vehicle Policy Committee (EV Board).
The battery electric vehicle (BEV) segment accounts for 39.5 billion baht across 18 approved projects.
Hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs) represent 29.9 billion baht across seven approved projects.
Plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs) account for 9.429 billion baht across seven approved projects.
Other EV categories, including electric buses and electric motorcycles, represent 3.1 billion baht across 18 approved projects.
Investment in batteries and energy storage systems totals 33.5 billion baht across 57 approved projects.
Key EV components, including traction motors, battery management systems, and EV power control systems, account for 12.5 billion baht across 49 approved projects.
Charging stations and battery-swapping projects account for 9.788 billion baht across 42 approved projects.
Approved charging projects will support the installation of more than 22,900 charging outlets nationwide, including more than 10,000 fast chargers.
Most BEV manufacturers granted BOI promotion over the past three to four years have already started production in Thailand.
Mercedes-Benz was among the first BEV manufacturers to start production in Thailand under BOI promotion.
Other early BEV manufacturers include Great Wall Motor, SAIC Motor-CP, BYD, AION Automobile, Changan, and EV Primus.
BMW, Hyundai Mobility, and Omoda & Jaecoo were the most recent BEV manufacturers to start production in Thailand in 2026.
The BEV manufacturers granted BOI promotion collectively employ more than 16,000 Thai workers.
The BOI has introduced measures to promote joint ventures between Thai operators and foreign companies to help domestic parts makers adapt to technological change and enter global supply chains.
The BOI has stepped up activities encouraging the use of locally made parts, including Subcon Thailand and Sourcing Day.
Over the past two years, the BOI organized 18 business-matching events connecting Thai parts manufacturers with EV makers.
These business-matching activities have generated more than 1,200 business matches involving more than 800 capable Thai suppliers.
The BOI expects the business matches to create more than 60 billion baht in local parts procurement.
These matches are expected to open the way for Thai suppliers to enter next-generation production lines and grow alongside a new wave of EV investment.
The transition to electric vehicles presents Thailand with an important opportunity to become a center for the region’s EV supply chain and ecosystem.
Thailand aims to serve as a platform for international cooperation, enabling established and new manufacturers from different countries to build stable business bases and expand in the country.
The strategy is intended to create opportunities for Thai people through employment, skills development, and the upgrading of local enterprises into new supply chains.
Thailand’s automotive development strategy has progressed through successive phases, from one-tonne pickup trucks and internal combustion engine vehicles to globally competitive eco-cars.
The latest phase of Thailand’s automotive development strategy focuses on clean-energy and intelligent vehicles, particularly the development of production bases for various types of EVs.
The BOI has adjusted its investment promotion measures to cover all vehicle technologies, allowing manufacturers across the industry to invest and grow in Thailand.

The BOI's approach aims to build a fully connected future automotive supply chain covering vehicle makers, parts manufacturers, batteries, intelligent systems, software, research and development centers, and testing facilities.
The broader goal of Thailand's EV strategy is to increase the role of Thai enterprises in the global supply chain and support sustainable industrial growth.
The BOI's approach has helped Thailand maintain its position as the region’s strongest automotive production base and one of the leading bases globally.
In 2025, all types of electric vehicles together accounted for more than 44% of new vehicle registrations in Thailand.
Five years earlier, electric vehicles accounted for just 3% of new vehicle registrations in Thailand.
In 2025, HEVs held the largest EV share at 21.8%, followed by BEVs at 19.6% and PHEVs at 2.9%.
Figures indicate that different EV technologies can advance together, with hybrids expected to play an important role during the transition toward electric mobility.
source_title: Thailand EV investment tops 137bn baht as BOI targets ASEAN hub

facts: Tesla recently launched production of a mass-market vehicle.
Volvo promised to phase out gasoline-only engines by 2019.
Federal government offers a tax credit of up to $7,500 each for the first 200,000 electric or plug-in hybrid cars a manufacturer sells.

State tax credits, subsidies for recharging infrastructure, relief from gasoline taxes, preferential lanes and parking spots, and government fleet purchases help taxpayers pay for every electric car on the road.
In Georgia, electric vehicle sales plummeted 80% the month after a $5,000 tax credit was repealed.
When Hong Kong slashed a tax break worth roughly $55,000 for a Tesla in April, its sales ground to a halt.
From 2005 to 2010, some hybrid buyers enjoyed a $3,500 tax credit.
Hybrid sales peaked at 487,000, or 3.1% of total vehicles, in 2013, according to Edmunds.com, when gasoline averaged $3.51 a gallon.
A surge in oil supply from fracking caused gasoline prices to plummet to $2.36 a gallon, and hybrids’ market share dropped to just 2.1%.
Bloomberg predicted electric cars would become “price competitive” with ICE cars in eight years without subsidies.

Economists Thomas Covert, Michael Greenstone, and Christopher Knittel estimate that at the current battery cost of $270 per kwh, oil would have to cost more than $300 a barrel (in 2020 dollars) to make electric and gasoline equally attractive.
If battery costs fall to $100, as Tesla Founder Elon Musk has targeted, oil would have to average $90 for EVs to be equally attractive.

Kevin Book of ClearView Energy Partners notes that optimists “overlook the compensating effect of incumbent technology,” citing the decline in natural gas prices from hydraulic fracking and internal combustion engine efficiency rising 2% a year.
ClearView says that in an optimistic scenario where battery costs fall 10% a year starting now and gasoline begins at $5 a gallon, electric vehicles will be competitive in five years.
If battery costs fall just 5% a year and gasoline starts at $2.25, it will take more than 20 years for electric vehicles to become competitive.
source_title: Electric Cars Are the Future? Not So Fast - AutoSpies Auto News

facts: Hyundai Motor Company collaborated with Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd. to verify Private 5G (P-5G) RedCap technology for smart manufacturing.
The P-5G RedCap technology enables fast, seamless communication with lower power consumption and cost.
The technology will be showcased at MWC25 Barcelona from March 3rd to 6th.
Hyundai Ulsan Plant and Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) are pioneering P-5G in mass production.
Hyundai Motor plans to expand the technology to its new EV-dedicated plant in Ulsan, which is scheduled to open in 2026.
Hyundai Motor was the first Korean company to implement P-5G in mass production.
Hyundai Motor is the industry’s first in the mobility manufacturing sector to verify P-5G RedCap technology.
Jae Min Lee, Vice President and Head of E-FOREST Center of Hyundai Motor and Kia, stated that Hyundai will continue to accelerate the commercialization of smart manufacturing solutions.

Simon Lee, Vice President and Head of B2B·B2G Business Development at Samsung Electronics Networks Business, said the collaboration represents how industry leaders can drive business innovation by merging expertise.
Since January 2025, Hyundai Motor has completed testing of Samsung’s P-5G solutions including radio, core, and management systems with in-house vehicle inspection equipment at Samsung’s Suwon campus.
Hyundai developed a custom vehicle inspection device to verify the network’s operational capabilities.

P-5G is a dedicated onsite system installed using a specific frequency band that ensures no external interference and seamless data transmission for centralized control of industrial robots and devices.
The collaboration verified P-5G RedCap technology, which offers simplified device configurations, compact infrastructure, and reduced bandwidth usage.
RedCap technology results in lower power consumption, cost efficiency, and improved performance over traditional Wi-Fi in speed, data processing, connection stability, and low latency.
Previously limited to high-performance equipment, RedCap now enables high-speed wireless communication for vehicle inspection equipment, small tools, cameras, and tablets.

Hyundai Motor integrated RedCap enabled Qualcomm’s Snapdragon® X35 5G Modem-RF System chipsets into its Diagnostic Scan (D Scan) system for efficient vehicle inspection data transmission via Samsung’s 5G network.
Samsung’s P-5G solution reduces power consumption and more than doubles uplink capacity for real-time IoT data transmission, featuring an integrated management system for automation.
Hyundai established a P-5G testbed in Korea in late 2022 before applying the technology in mass production at its Ulsan plant in October 2024.
At the Ulsan plant, Hyundai deployed dozens of automated guided vehicles using the P-5G technology.
At Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), Hyundai adapted its P-5G to meet local regulations, enabling the efficient operation of over 200 autonomous mobile robots.
Implementation at plants in Korea and the US has significantly reduced communication disruptions and downtime.
Hyundai developed a patented dual wireless communication solution combining 5G networks with Wi-Fi, leading to zero communication-related downtime since deployment.
Hyundai plans further expansions of P-5G technology to key plants beyond Ulsan and HMGMA.
source_title: Hyundai Motor and Samsung Unveil Breakthrough Private 5G RedCap Technology for Smart Manufacturing - HMGMA
